PN CN114870909-A
TI Regenerating catalytic cracking spent catalyst,            comprises primary oxygen-poor regenerating catalyst            to-be-generated through first regenerator and            performing oxygen-enriched regeneration through second            regenerator.
AU YANG X
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG Y
   WU L
   YAN H
   MENG F
   WANG L
   SUN S
AE SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2022A5214H
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating catalytic cracking spent                catalyst, comprises primary oxygen-poor                regenerating catalyst to-be-generated through first                regenerator and performing oxygen-enriched                regeneration through second regenerator, where the                oxygen-poor regeneration is to control the molar                ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide is 2-5:1 and the                oxygen-enriched regeneration is to control the                molar ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide is 1:3-5,                when the catalytic cracking device is in the                starting stage, mixing the high-purity carbon                dioxide and high-purity oxygen, respectively                introducing into the first regenerator and the                second regenerator to burn and regenerate,                recycling the regenerated flue gas output by the                second regenerator to the first regenerator, and                when the catalytic cracking device is in stable                operation stage, the regenerated flue gas output by                the second regenerator completely replaces the                high-purity carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating                catalytic cracking spent catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes the coking regeneration of                the regenerating agent, and the carbon monoxide                content in the flue gas generated in the first                regenerator can reach more than 30%, which                significantly reduces the cost of carbon monoxide                separation and purification.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of                the process of a method for regenerating catalytic                cracking spent catalyst (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC H04-B02; H04-F04; H04-F02B; J04-E09; J04-E05; J04-E04
IP B01J-038/04; B01J-038/12

PN KR2022069683-A
TI Combustion apparatus for supplying exhaust gas generated by combustion operation using oxygen and supercritical carbon dioxide to turbine, has dilution region in which exhaust gas is introduced to dilute incomplete combustion gas contained in exhaust gas.
AU JANG S
   LEE S M
   KANG S W
   DOHYOUNGROG
   PARKKYUSANG
   PARK H Y
   BHANG M H
   JINYU-IN
   PARK Y C
   GEUM G
   SUH S
   OH J H
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
   HANWHA POWER SYSTEMS CO LTD (HANW-C)
   KOREA MIDLAND POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
   KOREA WESTERN POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
   KOREA SOUTH POWER CO LTD (KOSP-C)
   KOREA EAST WEST POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
   UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
   UNIV SOGANG RES & BUSINESS DEV FOUND (UYSO-Non-standard)
GA 202273997A
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a combustion region (110) in which oxygen and circulating gas i.e. supercritical carbon dioxide, passing through a turbine (200) are burned to generate a flame. A dilution region (121) dilutes incomplete combustion gas contained in the exhaust gas, and cools the temperature of exhaust gas to operating temperature of the turbine. The dilution gas is introduced into the dilution area to block the chemical reaction. Multiple dilution chambers are provided with multiple coupling holes that are coupled to a diluting gas supply line. The incomplete combustion gas is carbon monoxide, and the dilution gas is a gas in which methyl hydroperoxide and carbon dioxide are mixed.
   USE - Combustion apparatus for supplying exhaust gas generated by combustion operation using oxygen and supercritical carbon dioxide to a turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The removal of incomplete combustion gas is maximized by changing the ratio of the dilution gas supplied to the combustion chambers according to the flow rate of the exhaust gas. The oxidation reaction of carbon monoxide is promoted.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the gas turbine combustion apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Combustion apparatus (100)
   Combustion region (110)
   Dilution region (121)
   Turbine (200)

PT P
PN TW202218607-A
TI Sparkling water maker comprises main engine provided with gas cylinder installation port, water tank, cooling tank, first water pump, pressure tank, cooler, exhaust pipe, water outlet, and third switch valve.
AU SONG F
AE JIANGMEN YIKEMAITE ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202291567W
AB    NOVELTY - The invention discloses a sparkling water making machine, which comprises a host, a water tank, a cooling tank, a pressure tank, a cooler, an exhaust pipe, a water outlet and a water outlet. When in use, first pump water from the water tank into the cooling tank for cooling, then pump it into the pressure tank, then the first on-off valve opens to connect the carbon dioxide cylinder with the pressure tank, so that the high-pressure carbon dioxide gas is pressed into the pressure tank It is mixed with water to form sparkling water, then the first on-off valve is disconnected to form a sealed space in the pressure tank, and the second on-off valve is opened to allow excess carbon dioxide gas to be discharged from the exhaust pipe so that the air pressure in the pressure tank can reach the outside After that, the third on-off valve is opened, and the bubble water flows smoothly along the water outlet and through the water outlet by gravity. Compared with the prior art, under the same conditions, more carbon dioxide gas can be retained in the water to form carbonic acid. The produced sparkling water is of higher quality.
DC P28 (Kitchen, sanitary equipment (A47J, K, L).)
MC P28-A01
IP A47J-043/12; A47J-043/27; B01F-003/04

PN CN114085393-A
TI Preparation of thermally crosslinked polymer            separation membrane used for separating mixed gas of            carbon dioxide and methane or hydrogen and methane, by            dissolving polyimide in solvent, subjecting to            film-forming treatment to obtain membrane, and passing            argon gas to carry out crosslinking reaction.
AU JIN J
   WANG Z
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202235978P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of thermally crosslinked polymer                separation membrane involves reacting                benzylmethyl-containing diamine monomer and                dianhydride monomer in polar organic solvent (A)                under the action of a catalyst to obtain polyimide,                dissolving polyimide in polar organic solvent (B)                to obtain 3-40 wt.% film-forming solution,                subjecting to film-forming treatment to obtain flat                homogeneous membrane or hollow fiber membrane as                intermediate membrane, and passing argon gas or                mixed purging gas of nitrogen and oxygen into the                intermediate membrane to carry out thermal                oxidation crosslinking reaction.
   USE - The method is used for the preparation of                thermally crosslinked polymer separation membrane                used in preparing plasticization-resistant gas                separation membrane for separating mixed gas e.g.                mixed gas of carbon dioxide and methane or hydrogen                and methane, and small molecule separation membrane                or swelling-resistant nanofiltration membrane for                organic liquid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts thermal oxidation                crosslinking reaction and prepares stable                covalently crosslinked membrane with excellent                plasticization resistance even under high pressure                carbon dioxide feed condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the thermally crosslinked polymer separation                membrane in separation of mixed gas such as mixed                gas of carbon dioxide and methane or hydrogen and                methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The                  benzylmethyl-containing diamine monomer is of                  formula: NH2-R'-NH2. The dianhydride monomer is                  of formula (I). The polar organic solvent (A) is                  N,N-dimethylformamide and/or N-methylpyrrolidone.                  The polar organic solvent is one or more of                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  chloroform and tetrahydrofuran.R' = a group chosen from 22 groups e.g.                  groups of formulae (a)-(e); and R = group chosen                  from 17 groups e.g. groups of formulae                  (f)-(j).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: When intermediate                  membrane is flat homogeneous membrane, the                  film-forming treatment is carried out by coating                  film-forming solution on the surface of the                  substrate, volatilizing the solvent to obtain a                  homogeneous film, and drying to obtain the flat                  homogeneous membrane. When intermediate membrane                  is hollow fiber membrane, the film-forming                  treatment is carried out by filtering the                  film-forming solution through mesh, degassing at                  15-50degrees Celsius and spinning the degassed                  solution into hollow fiber membrane.Preferred Property: When mixed gas contains                  methane and carbon dioxide, the polymer                  separation membrane has carbon dioxide                  permeability coefficient of 100 Barrer, carbon                  dioxide plasticizing point of more than 50 atm,                  and methane and carbon dioxide selectivity                  coefficient of more than 50. When mixed gas                  contains methane and hydrogen, the polymer                  separation membrane has hydrogen permeability                  coefficient of 200-600 Barrer, and methane and                  hydrogen selectivity coefficient of more than                  100.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC A05-J01A; A02-A00A; A08-S02; A10-D03; A11-C02; A11-B04; A12-S06A; A11-B15C; A12-S05E; A12-S05L; A12-W11A; A12-W14; E11-Q01B; E11-W; E10-J02D1; E31-A02C; E31-N05C; J01-E03F; J01-E03E

AU WANG Y
AE SHAANXI GINYU TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C81233
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of hardness of wastewater involves transporting a raw liquid from a raw liquid pool through a raw liquid pump to a mixer for mixing with a carbon dioxide waste gas, feeding the mixed solution into a dissolved gas reactor, fully reacting the carbon dioxide waste gas with the calcium ions in wastewater, flowing into a buffer tank through an outlet of the dissolved gas reactor and sending to a high-level cyclone sedimentation tank by a buffer pump, solid-liquid separating fully-reacted treated mixed liquid in an automatic backwash surface filter to obtain a qualified clear liquid at the outlet of the filter, entering the qualified clear liquid into a clear liquid tank, and discharging the slag slurry produced by the high-level cyclone sedimentation tank and automatic backwash surface filter into a slag slurry tank at regular intervals.
   USE - Removal of hardness of wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses pressurized dissolved gas reactor to better dissolve carbon dioxide gas into the liquid, which realizes effective and full mixing of gas and liquid, improves the reaction efficiency, and reduces the waste caused by gas overflow.

AU REN W

   LI M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021A42359
AB    NOVELTY - A bifunctional catalyst is double-component catalytic system comprising heteronuclear double-metal complex and co-catalyst quat. ammonium salt. The heteronuclear double-metal complex comprises zinc-aluminum heteronuclear double-metal complex (I) and zinc-cobalt heteronuclear double-metal complex (II).
   USE - Bifunctional catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low pressure and mild reaction temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A bifunctional catalyst is double-component catalytic system comprising heteronuclear double-metal complex and co-catalyst quat. ammonium salt. The heteronuclear double-metal complex comprises zinc-aluminum heteronuclear double-metal complex of formula (I) and zinc-cobalt heteronuclear double-metal complex of formula (II). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing cyclic carbonate by bifunctional catalyst using outer loop reactor comprising reacting carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw materials using bifunctional catalyst and outer loop reactor at 50-150 degrees C at pressure of 0.4-3.0 MPa. Reaction involves adding cyclic carbonate containing bifunctional catalyst in outer loop reactor, heating starting material to reaction temperature by heat exchanger, introducing carbon dioxide to reaction system, introducing epoxy alkane and carbon dioxide into outer loop reactor, keeping reaction pressure, continuously reacting to total consumption of epoxy alkane, cooling and discharging pressure to transfer material to flash tank, removing carbon dioxide, and vacuum distilling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The compound (I) is prepared by:(A) mixing sodium tert-butoxide and 2-aminocyclohexanol monohydrochloride with molar ratio of 3:1 under inert gas protection, dissolving and stirring using organic solvent for 10-60 minutes, slowly adding 2-chloromethylpyridine hydrochloride with molar ratio of 1:1.1 with 2-aminocyclohexanol monohydrochloride, continuously stirring and reacting at 0-50 degrees C for 2-24 hours, adding organic solvent into reaction system for diluting and filtering, washing filter cake, collecting filtrate, filtering to obtain clear yellow liquid, vacuumizing to remove solvent to obtain crude product, and separating and purifying by column chromatography to obtain light yellow liquid as ligand 1;(B) mixing 3-formyl-4-hydroxyphenyl acetate derivative of formula (1) with molar ratio of 1:1 with ligand 1 under inert gas protection, dissolving by organic solvent, adding molecular sieve, stirring to 0-110 degrees C for 1-24 hours, stopping reaction, filtering, washing filter cake by organic solvent, vacuum distilling filtrate to obtain crude product, and using column separation method to obtain product as ligand 2;(C) mixing 3,5-di-tert-butyl salicylaldehyde, hexamethylenediamine and ligand 2 with molar ratio of 1:1:1 under inert gas protection, dissolving with organic solvent, adding molecular sieve, stirring to 0-110 degrees C for 1-24 hours, monitoring reaction by thin-layer chromatography until raw material point disappears or does not change, filtering, washing filter cake with organic solvent, vacuum distilling filter liquid to remove solvent to obtain crude product, and using column separation method to obtain solid product as ligand 3;(D) dissolving ligand 3 in organic solvent under inert gas protection, slowly dropping diethyl aluminum chloride solution, reacting to - 20-80 degrees C for 2-24 hours, vacuumizing to remove organic solvent to obtain crude product, and using column separation method to obtain ligand 4; and(E) dissolving ligand 4 in organic solvent under inert gas protection, slowly adding zinc acetate, reacting to - 20-80 degrees C for 1 -24 hours, filtering reaction liquid to remove insoluble substance, vacuumizing to remove solvent to obtain crude product, and re-crystallizing using methylene dichloride/n-hexane.The compound (II) is prepared by preparing ligand 3, dissolving ligand 3 in organic solvent under inert gas protection, slowly dropping anhydrous cobalt acetate solution, reacting at - 20-80 degrees C for 1-24 hours, vacuumizing to remove solvent to obtain crude product, using column separation method to obtain ligand 5, dissolving ligand 5 and silver nitrate in organic solvent under inert gas protection, light stirring for 2-24 hours, filtering to remove insoluble substance, vacuum drying, re-crystallizing using methylene dichloride/n-hexane to obtain ligand 6, dissolving ligand 6 in organic solvent under inert gas protection, adding zinc acetate, reacting to - 20-80 degrees C for 1-24 hours, filtering reaction liquid to remove insoluble substance, vacuumizing to remove solvent to obtain crude product, and re-crystallizing using methylene dichloride/n-hexane. Preferred Components: The inert gas is nitrogen, helium gas or argon gas. The molar ratio of compound (I) in catalyst to co-catalyst tetrabutyl ammonium bromide is 1:1-1000. The mass ratio of epoxy alkane to catalyst is 50-20000:1. The molar ratio of alkylene oxide to carbon dioxide is 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, methylene dichloride, toluene, n-hexane, methanol, ethanol or acetone and other organic solvent. The epoxy alkane is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide or epoxycyclohexane.

GA 202164566E
AB    NOVELTY - Material comprises: a porous carbon sphere                material diameter of 10 nm and 1 microm, with a                surface area of at least 600 m2/g, and a total pore                volume of at least 0.15 cm3/g, where 100% of pores                of the porous material have diameters of less than                1 nm as measured from carbon dioxide sorption                isotherms using the density functional theory (DFT)                method, where the porous material has an oxygen                content of less than 12 wt.% as measured by X-ray                photoelectron spectroscopy, and where the porous                material has a carbon dioxide adsorption capacity                of more than 1 mmol/g at 25degrees Celsius.
   USE - The material is useful for carbon dioxide                adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: cannot host water molecules to                appreciable extent showing its water resistibility                and outstanding advantages over unstable adsorbents                in humid conditions such as some reported zeolites                and MOFs; and determines best carbon spheres,                synthesized carbon spheres also demonstrated good                cyclic stability, easy regeneration, favorable                selectivity, and rapid sorption kinetics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                forming the porous carbon sphere material,                comprising (a) heating an aromatic poly-carboxylic                acid or anhydride precursor in the first heated                zone of a reactor at a temperature sufficient to                volatilize the precursor, (b) Flowing an inert                carrier gas to transport the volatilized precursor                into a second heated zone of the reactor, (c)                heating the precursor vapors in the second zone of                a reactor at a temperature of 700-900degrees                Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the material, where                  more than 50% of pores of the porous material                  have diameters of less than 0.5 nm as measured                  from carbon dioxide sorption isotherms using the                  DFT method. The porous material has an oxygen                  content of less than 5.3 wt.% as measured by                  X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. The material                  with a surface area of at least 800 m2/g. The                  material with a total pore volume of at least                  0.19 cm3/g. The porous material has a carbon                  dioxide adsorption capacity of more than 4 mmol/g                  at 0degrees Celsius. The aromatic poly-carboxylic                  acid or anhydride precursor is pyromellitic acid,                  pyromellitic anhydride or pyromellitic                  dianhydride. The precursor is heated to a                  temperature of 400-450degrees Celsius at 1                  atmosphere pressure. The inert carrier gas is                  argon or nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - 1 g Pyromellitic acid were placed in a ceramic                boat and inserted into the zone of tubular furnace                under argon (200 ml/min) and at atmospheric                pressure. The temperature of zone was raised to                700degrees Celsius. with a heating rate of 1degrees                Celsius, When the desired temperature was reached,                the temperature of zone was also increased to                450degrees Celsius and left for 30 minutes. Then,                the furnace was cooled down to room temperature                under argon, and the pure carbon spheres were                collected from the quartz tube and used without any                further process.

AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (S1) obtaining the relationship between the mass fraction of carbon in the RH refined molten steel and the corresponding total integral number of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, where the relationship is A = ax (C)-bx 10-4, i.e. the value of a is 210-305, and the value of b is 1.5-6.5, (S2) performing real-time monitoring of the total integral number of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas during the RH refining process, and (S3) determining the carbon content in the RH refining molten steel according to the relational expression in step (S1) and the total integral number of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in step (S2).

The photocatalyst is copper-2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid (DHTP) prepared from copper ions and 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid. The copper-2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid is nano-sheet material having an amorphous structure.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: has large number of active sites, promoting effect on carbon dioxide catalytic performance and reduced band gap; accelerates electron transport effect; inhibits electron-hole recombination; and improves utilization rate of sunlight and thus improves carbon monoxide production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing photocatalyst, comprising (i) mixing N,N-dimethylformamide, anhydrous ethanol and deionized water to obtain mixed solution 1, (ii) adding 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid (DHTP) into mixed solution 1 and ultrasonically dissolving to obtain mixed solution 2, (iii) adding soluble copper salt into mixed solution 2 and ultrasonically dissolving to obtain mixed solution 3, (iv) injecting triethylamine into mixed solution 3 and shaking to obtain brown gel solution, (v) subjecting brown gel solution to ultrasonic treatment to promote chemical reaction of substances in brown gel solution to obtain suspended granular copper-2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid containing post-reaction solution, and (vi) extracting copper-2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid from post-reaction solution to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst comprises copper ions and 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid in a molar ratio of 1:1. The soluble copper salt is copper(II) chloride. The amount of soluble copper salt is equal to that of 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid. Preferred Method: In steps (ii), (iii) and (v), the ultrasonic power is 40 kHz and the ultrasonic time is 8 hours. In step (iv), the shaking time is 5 minutes. In step (vi), the extraction of copper-2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid from post-reaction solution comprises centrifuging reacted solution to obtain precipitate, washing obtained precipitate and drying washed precipitate to obtain final product. The centrifugal rotating speed is 8000 revolutions/minute. In the method, the washing is carried out using acetone and deionized water, and the drying is vacuum freeze-drying, where the vacuum degree is 11 Pa and the freezing temperature is -48 degrees C. The method comprises The method comprises (i) adding 32 ml N,N-dimethylformamide, 2 ml anhydrous ethanol and 2 ml deionized water into a 50 ml glass bottle to obtain mixed solution 1, (ii) adding 0.75 mmol 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid (DHTP) into mixed solution 1 and ultrasonically dissolving 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid to obtain mixed solution 2, (iii) adding 0.75 mmol copper(II) chloride dihydrate into mixed solution 2 and ultrasonically dissolving copper(II) chloride dehydrate to obtain mixed solution 3, (iv) adding 0.8 ml triethylamine into mixed solution 3 and shaking for 5 minutes to obtain brown gel solution, (v) subjecting brown gel solution to ultrasonic treatment for 8 hours through an ultrasonic device to promote chemical reaction of substances in brown gel solution to obtain suspended granular copper-2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid containing post-reaction solution, and (vi) centrifuging post-reaction solution, taking precipitate, sequentially washing using acetone and deionized water and vacuum freeze-drying to obtain final product.

PN CN111410224-A
TI Improving utilization rate of carbon resources in phosphate tailings involves mixing phosphogypsum and ammonia, mixing with high magnesium phosphorus tailings, acidolysis, carbonizing, suction filtration to obtain e.g. calcium carbonate.
AU ZHANG H
   PAN Y
   WU H
   ZHANG J
   PAN Z
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 202067923E
AB    NOVELTY - Improving the utilization rate of carbon resources in phosphate tailings comprises (1) stirring and mixing phosphogypsum with aqueous ammonia to obtain a phosphate rock paste-aqueous ammonia mixed suspension, and optionally dividing phosphogypsum-ammonia mixed suspension into two parts, and (2) mixing high magnesium phosphorus tailings with water, adding hydrochloric acid, carrying out acidolysis reaction, where the carbon dioxide generated during the acidolysis reaction is passed into one of the two phosphogypsum-ammonia mixed suspensions and the other of the two phosphogypsum-ammonia mixed suspensions in sequence, carrying out suction filtration after a period of carbonization reaction to obtain calcium carbonate solid and ammonium sulfate solution, and the carbon dioxide that has not been absorbed by the mixed suspension is introduced into ammonia solution.
   USE - The method is useful for improving utilization rate of carbon resources in phosphate tailings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the conversion rate of the reaction of carbon dioxide and phosphogypsum-ammonia mixed suspension in tailings acid hydrolysis; adopts a pre-reaction method to absorb and store unreacted carbon dioxide, thus improving the carbon resources in the tailings; combines tailings and phosphogypsum to process the carbon resources in the tailings into calcium carbonate products, which reduces the waste of resources, reduces environmental pollution, and increases the additional tailings value; and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1): the solid-liquid ratio of phosphogypsum to ammonia is 1-2; and the phosphogypsum-ammonia mixed suspension is divided into two parts equally. In the step (2), the concentration of hydrochloric acid is 8.80-12.07 mol/l, and the mass ratio of the hydrochloric acid to the high-magnesium phosphorus tailings is 1.5-2.25:1. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of the acidolysis reaction in the step (2) is 20-60 degrees C, and the reaction time is 20-60 minutes. The carbonization reaction in the step (2) is carried out at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

TI Hydrogenation reactor for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate, has main pipeline that is connected with material inlet, and branch pipeline connected with premixing chamber connected to carbon dioxide intake pipe.
AU TAN Y
   LI L
AE DAYU XURI MINING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DAYU-Non-standard)
GA 201851674A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a method for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate by hydrogenation reaction kettle, comprising a kettle body, the kettle body is provided with a material inlet, a material inlet for connected with a mother liquid pipeline; the bottom of the kettle body is connected with a gas-liquid mixing chamber, the gas-liquid mixing chamber comprises a transverse emulsification chamber and emulsification chamber connected with premixing chamber, inner wall of the emulsification chamber provided with a fixed blade, emulsification chamber and collapsible is set with horizontal rotating shafts; the rotating shaft part of the emulsification chamber is set crossly movable blade and the fixed blade. the rotating shaft in the mixing chamber part is equipped with propeller; the rotating shaft is connected with a motor; the mother liquor pipeline provided with a shunt valve, shunt valve is connected with a main pipeline and branch pipeline; the main pipeline is connected with the material inlet, the branch pipeline is connected with the premixing, the premixing chamber is connected with a carbon dioxide inlet pipe. Hydrogenation reaction kettle of the invention can improve the mixing effect of the carbon dioxide gas and the mother liquor, improves utilization rate of carbon dioxide.

PN US2018015444-A1; WO2018013263-A1; CA3030838-A1; EP3509742-A1; EP3509742-A4; EP3509742-B1
TI Supported heterogeneous catalyst material used to            catalyze reverse water-gas shift reaction for selective            formation of carbon monoxide, comprises gamma-alumina            as support material and alkali-metal doped molybdenum            carbide as active material.
AU POROSOFF M
   WILLAUER H D
AE US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
   US SEC NAVY (USNA-C)
   US SEC NAVY (USNA-C)
   US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
GA 201805288H
AB    NOVELTY - Supported heterogeneous catalyst material                comprises: a support material comprising gamma                -alumina; and an active material comprising                alkali-metal doped molybdenum carbide.
   USE - The supported heterogeneous catalyst material                is useful for catalyzing RWGS reaction, or in                carbon dioxide hydrogenation via RWGS reaction for                the selective formation of carbon monoxide (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported heterogeneous catalyst material                provides carbon dioxide hydrogenation via the RWGS                reaction which achieves a carbon monoxide yield of                greater than or equal to 12%, and achieves a carbon                monoxide selectivity of 90% or greater                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) making the catalyst, comprising (a)                co-impregnating molybdenum and alkali-metal                precursors onto a gamma -alumina support, and (b)                carburizing the dried and calcined gamma -alumina                support to form A1-molybdenum carbide/ gamma                -alumina, where the alkali metal is potassium,                sodium or lithium, drying and calcining impregnated                gamma -alumina support and A1 is potassium, sodium                or lithium; and
   (2) carbon dioxide hydrogenation via RWGS                reaction for the selective formation of carbon                monoxide, comprising the step (a) as above per se,                drying and calcining impregnated gamma -alumina                support, the step (b) as above per se, and reacting                the A1-molybdenum carbide/ gamma -alumina with                carbon dioxide and hydrogen to form carbon                monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali-metal                  component of the active material comprises at                  least one alkali-metal precursor in elemental                  form or in the form of oxides, where the metals                  are potassium, sodium and/or lithium. The                  molybdenum component of the active material                  comprises at least one molybdenum precursor in                  the form of carbides, oxycarbides, oxides and/or                  elemental molybdenum. Preferred Method: The                  loading of molybdenum is 1-70%. The loading of                  the alkali metal is 0.1-15%. The carburization is                  performed at a temperature of 400-1000 degrees C.                  The reaction is performed: while applying                  external heat; at a temperature of 250-1000                  degrees C; at a pressure of 0-350 psig; while                  flowing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen gas, over                  the A1-molybdenum carbide/ gamma -alumina                  catalyst material; and while applying external                  heat and flowing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                  gas, over the A1-molybdenum carbide/ gamma                  -alumina catalyst material.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Mixture useful as liquid sorbent for methanol or methanol and water in methanol synthesis, comprises e.g. component in form of salt formed from bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide anion, and cation in form of quaternary ammonium cation.
AU ALBERT J
   BALDAUF M
   REICHERT J
   STARK K
   TREMEL A
   WASSERSCHEID P
   MELTZER K
   TREMMEL A
AE UNIV ERLANGEN-NUERNBERG (UYEL-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 201873380X
AB    NOVELTY - Mixture comprises a component (a) in the form of at least one salt formed from bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide anion (I), and a cation in the form of quaternary ammonium cation (II), phosphonium cation (III), imidazolium cation (IV), pyridinium cation (V), pyrazolium cation (VI) or triazolium cation (VII), a component (b) made of at least one of the following components: (b1) a salt which is formed from one of the anions (PO4)3-, (HPO4)2-, (H2PO4)-, (SO4)2- , (HSO4)-, (NO3), (NO2)- or Cl- and at least three of cations mentioned in the component (a); (b2) a salt formed from at least one bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide anion and lithium, potassium, cesium, magnesium, calcium, barium, nickel, cobalt, iron, scandium, lanthanum, zinc, gallium, cerium or aluminum cation; and/or (b3) a zwitterionic compound formed from one of the cations in the component (a).
   USE - The mixture is useful as a liquid sorbent for methanol or methanol and water in methanol synthesis using carbon monoxide and hydrogen, carbon dioxide and hydrogen or a mixture of hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide as synthesis products (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mixture comprises a component (a) in the form of at least one salt formed from bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide anion of formula (I), and a cation in the form of quaternary ammonium cation of formula ((NR1R2R3R)+) (II), phosphonium cation of formula ((PR1R2R3R)+) (III), imidazolium cation of formula (IV), a pyridinium cation of formula (V), pyrazolium cation of formula (VI) or triazolium cation of formula (VII), a component (b) made of at least one of the following components: (b1) a salt which is formed from one of the anions (PO4)3-, (HPO4)2-, (H2PO4)-, (SO4)2- , (HSO4)-, (NO3)-, (NO2)- or Cl- and at least three of the cations mentioned in the component (a), where the number of cations corresponds to the absolute amount of the charge number of the respective anion; (b2) a salt formed from at least one bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide anion and lithium, potassium, cesium, magnesium, calcium, barium, nickel, cobalt, iron, scandium, lanthanum, zinc, gallium, cerium or aluminum cation, where the number of bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide anions corresponds to the absolute value of the charge number of the respective metal cation; and/or (b3) a zwitterionic compound formed from one of the cations mentioned in the component (a), in which one of R, R1, R2 or R3 is -(CH2)z -SO3- group where z is 1-10.
   R1-R3 = H, linear or branched, partially saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic alkyl having 1-20C, where up to 6H are optionally substituted by OH groups, heteroaryl and/or heteroaryl-(1-6C)-alkyl having 3-8C, where at least one heteroatom including N, O and S in the heteroaryl, aryl and/or aryl-(1-6C)-alkyl having 6-20C in the aryl, where the alkyl is linear or branched, partially saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic, and at least one hydrogen on the heteroaryl is optionally substituted with at least one linear or branched, partially saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic-(1-6C)-alkyl and/or halo, or -(-(CH2)x -O-)y-CH3;
   x = 2-5;
   y = 1-20; and
   R = linear or branched, partially saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic alkyl having 1-20C, where up to 6H are optionally substituted by OH groups, heteroaryl and/or heteroaryl-(1-6C)-alkyl having 3-8C, where at least one heteroatom including N, O and S in the heteroaryl, aryl and/or aryl-(1-6C)-alkyl having 6-20C in the aryl, where the alkyl is linear or branched, partially saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic, and at least one hydrogen on the heteroaryl is optionally substituted with at least one linear or branched, partially saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic-(1-6C)-alkyl and/or halo, or -(-(CH2)x -O-)y-CH3.
   Provided that at least one hydrogen is optionally substituted on the imidazole ring, pyridine ring, pyrazole ring or triazole ring with 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 1-6C aminoalkyl, 5-12C aryl or (5-12C)-aryl-(1-6C)-alkyl, where the alkyl, alkoxy and aminoalkyl groups are linear or branched, partiality saturated, aliphatic or alicyclic and the aryl has 6-20C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for performing a methanol synthesis using carbon monoxide and hydrogen, carbon dioxide and hydrogen or a mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen as synthesis products in a reactor at 100-300 degrees C and a synthesis gas pressure of 50-300 bar in the presence of a catalyst, comprising providing a liquid mixture in the reactor, reacting carbon monoxide and hydrogen, carbon dioxide and hydrogen or a mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the synthesis product methanol or the synthesis products methanol and water in the reactor at 100-300 degrees C and a synthesis gas pressure of 50-300 bar, performing sorption of at least a portion of at least one synthesis product from at least one synthesis product-containing gas phase in the liquid mixture, and continuously or discontinuously discharging the liquid mixture from the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is copper-zinc oxide on aluminum oxide, which is arranged in the synthesis gas phase of the reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixture comprises 1-99 wt.%, preferably 1-80 wt.% the component (b). The mixture is liquid at 79 degrees C, preferably 19 degrees C. The mixture comprises (i) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (ii) methyltrioctylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (iii) trihexyltetradecylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (iv) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and cesium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (v) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and tributyl-4-sulfonyl-1-butanophosphonium, (vi) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and tris (tetrabutylphosphonium)phosphate, (vii) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, tris(tetrabutylphosphonium)phosphate and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (viii) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and bis(tetrabutylphosphonium)sulfate, (ix) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, bis(tetrabutylphosphonium)sulfate and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (x) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, tetrakis(hydroxymethyl)phosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, (xi) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, tributylhydroxymethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, or (xii) tributylmethylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, tributyl-2,3-dihydroxypropylphosphonium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide and lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide. The mass ratio of the first specified component to the second specified component in binary mixture is 4:1-7:3. The mass ratio of the first specified component to the second specified component to the third specified component in ternary mixture is 4:1:4, 4:3:1 and 4:3:3. The synthesis is gas carbon dioxide and hydrogen. Preferred Method: The synthesis product containing gas phase is introduced by a gas inlet stirrer in the mixture. The liquid mixture discharged from the reactor is expanded and at least methanol which at least partly leaves the mixture is recovered.The mixture is returned to the reactor after the expansion of the liquid mixture and at least partial escape of the synthesis product methanol or the synthesis products methanol and water from the mixture. At least portions of the synthesis products methanol and water are absorbed into the liquid mixture, in the reactor. The synthesis products methanol and water at least partially emerge from the mixture, after discharging the mixture from the reactor and depressurizing the mixture. At least partially leaked synthesis product methanol is recovered. The synthesis product methanol is returned to the reactor after relaxing the liquid mixture and at least partially leakage of the synthesis products from the mixture. The method comprises removing heat from the mixture prior to recycling to the reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No preparation method is given.

PN US2019329176-A1; US11400410-B2
TI Aqueous composition used to capture carbon dioxide            and process carbon dioxide comprises nucleophilic            compound e.g. sterically unhindered primary amine            moiety, Bronsted base e.g. basic nitrogen moiety,            water-soluble organic solvent and water.
AU LU Y
   DU Y
   YE Q
AE UNIV ILLINOIS FOUND (UNII-C)
GA 2019900733
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous composition comprises: (a) at least                one nucleophilic compound, where the nucleophilic                compound comprises a sterically unhindered primary                amine moiety and/or a sterically unhindered                secondary amine moiety; (b) at least one Bronsted                base, where the Bronsted base comprises a basic                nitrogen moiety and the Bronsted base does not                comprise either a sterically unhindered primary                amine moiety or a sterically unhindered secondary                amine moiety; (c) 5-50 wt.% a water-soluble organic                solvent; and (d) 5-40 wt.% water, where the                combination of the nucleophilic compound, the                Bronsted base, the organic solvent, and the water                components of the composition are at least 80%                miscible within a temperature of 20-80 degrees                C.
   USE - The composition is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide and processing carbon dioxide (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is significantly reduce both                the energy use and equipment cost for carbon                dioxide separation and compression compared with                not only the benchmark MEA process but also the                common biphasic solvent-based processes reported by                others.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a biphasic composition comprising (a) a                carbamate compound comprising a carbamate moiety of                at least one of a primary amine and a secondary                amine, (b) a Bronsted base, where the Bronsted base                is in the form of its conjugate acid, the Bronsted                base does not form a carbamate moiety with carbon                dioxide, (c) 5-50 wt.% a water-soluble organic                solvent and (d) 5-40 wt.% water, the biphasic                composition has a carbon dioxide-rich phase and a                carbon dioxide-lean phase, the rich phase comprises                the highest wt.% of water, the highest wt.% of the                carbamate compound, and the highest wt.% of the                conjugate acid and the lean phase comprises the                highest wt.% of the organic solvent;
   (2) capturing carbon dioxide comprising                contacting carbon dioxide gas with the composition,                where (a) the nucleophilic compound and carbon                dioxide gas form a carbamate moiety via the                nitrogen atom of the primary or secondary amine                moiety to provide a carbamate compound, (b) the                Bronsted base forms a conjugate acid where the                dissolution of carbon dioxide gas is facilitated,                (c) the water in the aqueous composition becomes                enriched with the carbamate compound and (d) the                aqueous composition partitions to form a biphasic                composition that has a carbon dioxide-rich phase                and a carbon dioxide-lean phase, and the rich phase                comprises the highest wt.% water, the highest wt.%                weight of the carbamate compound, and the highest                wt.% of the conjugate acid and the lean phase                comprises the highest wt.% of the organic solvent,                thus capturing carbon dioxide; and
   (3) processing carbon dioxide comprising (a)                contacting carbon dioxide gas and the composition                in at least one absorption columns to form a                biphasic composition comprising a carbamate                compound, a carbon dioxide-rich phase, and a carbon                dioxide-lean phase, (b) at least partially                separating the carbon dioxide-rich phase from the                carbon dioxide-lean phase in at least one                liquid-liquid phase separation (LLPS) units, where                the at least partially separated carbon                dioxide-rich phase is dispatched from the one or                more LLPS units, (c) feeding the carbon                dioxide-rich phase to at least one thermal                strippers and heating the carbon dioxide-rich phase                to a temperature that releases an amine compound                and molecular carbon dioxide from the carbamate                compound, where (i) the carbon dioxide-rich phase                is optionally preheated, the preheated carbon                dioxide-rich phase is fed to the middle or lower                portion of the one or more thermal strippers, or                (ii) the carbon dioxide-rich phase is not                preheated, the not preheated carbon dioxide-rich                phase is fed to the top portion of the thermal                strippers, (d) returning the amine compound to the                absorption columns, and (e) compressing the                molecular carbon dioxide with at least one                compressor into liquid carbon dioxide, thus                processing carbon dioxide from a gas to a                liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition is a                  homogeneous mixture within the temperature of                  20-80 degrees C, the combination of the                  nucleophilic compound, the Bronsted base, the                  organic solvent and the water components of the                  composition are completely miscible within the                  temperature of 20-80 degrees C, the boiling                  points of the nucleophilic compound, Bronsted                  base, and organic solvent are each at least 140                  degrees C and the vapor pressures of the                  nucleophilic compound, Bronsted base, and organic                  solvent are of less than or equal to 100 Pa at 20                  degrees C. The composition comprises 10-60 wt.%                  the Bronsted base and 10-40 wt.% the nucleophilic                  compound, where the nucleophilic compound does                  not comprise a tertiary amine moiety. The                  Bronsted base comprises a nitrogen heterocycle, a                  tertiary amine moiety, or a sterically hindered                  secondary amine moiety where the sterically                  hindered secondary amine moiety does not form a                  carbamate moiety with carbon dioxide. The                  nucleophilic compound or the Bronsted base has a                  total of 1-6 nitrogen atoms and the pKa of at                  least one nitrogen atom of the Bronsted base is                  7-15. The Bronsted base comprises an imidazole, a                  piperazine, a morpholine, a piperidine, a                  guanidine, amino ether, a non-alcoholic tertiary                  amine and/or a non-alcoholic sterically hindered                  amine and the molecular weight of the Bronsted                  base is of 60-250 Daltons. The thermal strippers                  comprise a second LLPS unit, a second carbon                  dioxide-lean phase is at least partially                  separated from a second carbon dioxide-rich phase                  in the second LLPS unit, and the at least                  partially separated second carbon dioxide-lean                  phase is dispatched from the second LLPS                  unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Bronsted base is                  2-ethylimidazole, 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole,                  1,4-bis(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine,                  4-(2-hydroxyethyl)morpholine,                  4-hydroxy-1-methylpiperidine,                  1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine,                  bis(2-(N,N-dimethylamino)ethyl) ether,                  N,N,N',N'',N''-pentamethyldiethylenetriamine,                  N,N,N',N'',N''-pentamethyldipropylenetriamine,                  triethylenediamine,                  3,3'-iminobis(N,N-dimethylpropylamine),                  di-sec-butylamine, N-propyl-sec-butylamine and/or                  2-(2-(dimethylamino)ethoxy)ethanol. The                  nucleophilic compound comprises a piperazine, a                  morpholine, amino ether, a non-alcoholic                  polyamine and/or a non-alcoholic monoamine and                  the molecular weight of the nucleophilic compound                  is of 60-250 Daltons. The nucleophilic compound                  is piperazine, 2-methyl-piperazine,                  1-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine,                  1-(2-aminoethyl)piperazine,                  4-(2-aminoethyl)morpholine, bis(2-aminoethyl)                  ether, bis(3-aminopropyl)amine,                  1,3-diaminopropane,                  3,3'-diamino-N-methyldipropylamine,                  3-(aminomethyl)pyridine,                  1-(3-aminopropyl)imidazole, hexamethyleneimine                  and/or N-methylcyclohexylamine. The nucleophilic                  compound comprises at least two secondary amine                  moieties or the nucleophilic compound comprises a                  secondary amine moiety that is a ring-member of a                  heterocycle. The organic solvent comprises an                  ether, ester, acetal, aldehyde, ketone, glyme,                  lactam, sulfolane and/or urea, where the organic                  solvent has a pKa no greater than 7 and is inert                  to carbon dioxide and the viscosity of the                  composition is less than or equal to 10 cP at 20                  degrees C. The organic solvent comprises                  N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone,                  1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone, triethylene                  glycol dimethyl ether (triglyme) and/or                  tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether (tetraglyme).                  The organic solvent comprises glyme, the                  composition comprises 0.5-3 mole equivalents of                  carbamate moieties per liter of the biphasic                  composition, the carbon dioxide-rich phase                  comprises 0.5-6.5 mole equivalents of carbamate                  moieties per liter of the carbon dioxide-rich                  phase, where when the composition is in contact                  with a gas comprising 96 vol.% oxygen and 4 vol.%                  carbon dioxide, and at least one metal catalyst,                  and the composition oxidatively degrades at a                  loss-rate of less than 10 wt.% in 10 days at 50                  degrees C. The organic solvent comprises                  tetraglyme, the Bronsted base is                  2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole, and the nucleophilic                  compound is piperazine and/or 2-methylpiperazine,                  where the composition is 2-4-fold less corrosive                  to carbon-steel than a 30 wt.% monoethanolamine                  (MEA) aqueous solution when the composition and                  MEA are under absorption conditions at 40 degrees                  C or desorption conditions at 120-150 degrees                  C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   SHIMADA Y
   HIROSHI O
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022C5211R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus (100)                comprises recovery device (1) for recovering carbon                dioxide; an electrochemical reaction device (2) for                electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide; and a pH                adjuster (52). The recovery device includes an                absorption unit (12) in which an anode side                electrolytic solution composed of a strong alkaline                aqueous solution and carbon dioxide gas are brought                into contact with each other so that the carbon                dioxide is dissolved and absorbed in the anode side                electrolytic solution, and a concentration unit                (13) that concentrates carbon dioxide. The                electrochemical reaction device includes an anode                (22), a cathode (21), an anion exchange membrane                (23) set between anode and cathode, a liquid flow                path set between the anode and the anion exchange                membrane, and a liquid flow path provided between                the cathode and the anion exchange membrane.
   USE - Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide treatment apparatus enables                to improve energy efficiency, is highly effective                in reducing loss of carbon dioxide, increases                amount of ethylene generated by reducing carbon                dioxide in the electrochemical reaction device and                increases the number of carbon atoms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus (100)                comprises a recovery device (1) for recovering                carbon dioxide; an electrochemical reaction device                (2) for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide;                and a pH adjuster (52). The recovery device                includes an absorption unit (12) in which an anode                side electrolytic solution composed of a strong                alkaline aqueous solution and carbon dioxide gas                are brought into contact with each other so that                the carbon dioxide is dissolved and absorbed in the                anode side electrolytic solution, and a                concentration unit (13) that concentrates carbon                dioxide. The electrochemical reaction device                includes an anode (22), a cathode (21), an anion                exchange membrane (23) provided between the anode                and the cathode, a liquid flow path provided                between the anode and the anion exchange membrane                and through which the anode side electrolytic                solution that has absorbed the carbon dioxide in                the absorption unit flows, and a liquid flow path                provided between the cathode and the anion exchange                membrane and through which a cathode side                electrolytic solution composed of a strong alkaline                aqueous solution of which a pH has been adjusted by                the pH adjuster flows. The pH of the cathode side                electrolytic solution is higher than that of the                anode side electrolytic solution, and the carbon                dioxide gas is supplied from the concentration unit                to a gas flow path on a side of the cathode                opposite to the anode, and the carbon dioxide gas                is reduced at the cathode.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: i. a                carbon dioxide treatment method; ii. a method for                producing a carbon compound.

AU MA Z

   CHEN J
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 202166928G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid involves continuously introducing reaction gas into reactor, so that the mixture in the reactor undergoes catalytic oxidation reaction at 150-200 degrees C under pressure of 1-10 MPa. The mixture comprises raw material, organic acid, halogen and metal catalyst. The raw material is 5-hydroxymethylfurfural or its derivative. The reaction gas comprises carbon dioxide and oxygen, in the mol ratio of more than 0.3:1.
   USE - Preparation of 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of controlling ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen in the reaction gas at 200 degrees C, effectively inhibiting occurrence of 5-hydroxymethyl furfural peroxidation, increasing selectivity of 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid, improving yield to more than 93.8%, inhibiting solvent combustion, and improving safety of the reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen in the reaction gas is 0.5-5:1. The mixture further comprises 1-20 wt.%, preferably 2-10 wt.% water. The metal catalyst comprises 0.05-2.5 wt.% of at least one metal element chosen from cobalt, manganese, iron, zirconium, cerium, selenium, copper, vanadium, ruthenium and nickel, preferably cobalt and/or cerium element or cobalt and manganese element, in the ratio of 1:3-4. The amount of metal element in the metal catalyst is 1.5-1.7 wt.%. The mol ratio of the metal element and halogen in the metal catalyst is 1-20:1, preferably 3-5:1. Preferred Components: The halogen comprises chlorine, bromine, fluorine or iodine. The halogen is present in the mixture in the form of hydrogen halide, sodium halide, ammonium halide or potassium halide. The reaction gas is composed of carbon dioxide and at least one of gases, such as oxygen, air or oxygen-enriched gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid is monocarboxylic acid, preferably acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid or valeric acid. The raw material is preferably 5-hydroxymethyl furfural ether derivative and/or 5-hydroxymethyl furfural ester derivative. Preferred Composition: The added amount of the raw material is 10-30 wt.%.

AU DENG Y
   HUANG Z
   FU X
AE HUBEI DUORUI PHARM CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021064293
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing injection involves taking water for                injection in liquid mixing tank, stirring to cool                down, weighing sodium bicarbonate, slowly adding it                to liquid mixing tank, after mixing, adding water                to prepare a medicinal solution, and continuously                introducing carbon dioxide into the medicinal                solution, adjusting the pH value, filtering liquid                medicine by a filter and then filling, injecting                carbon dioxide into the sterilizer, sterilizing at                115-121 degrees C for 15-30 minutes, cooling, and                exiting the sterilizer after temperature reaches a                specified temperature, followed by manual                inspection, vacuum packing with high-barrier bags                that block carbon dioxide and water vapor                transmission, and controling the vacuum degree to                -38 to -90 kpa.
   USE - Method for preparing injection.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Preparing injection                  involves taking 100 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate                  and 200-300 pts. wt. water, 0.5-1.2 pts. wt.                  sodium tartrate. Preferred                  Processes: The filter is a ceramic filter, and                  filter membrane on the inner wall of ceramic                  filter is a filter membrane coated with an ion                  adsorption layer, and preparation method of the                  filter membrane coated with an ion adsorption                  layer involves mixing 100 pts. wt. chitosan,                  20-30 pts. wt. polymethyl methacrylate, 15-20                  pts. wt. natural rubber latex, and 5-10 pts. wt.                  2-tert-butylcyclohexanol acetate, adding                  crosslinking agent and catalyst to react 5-10                  pts. wt. amylcinnamyl alcohol at 60-80 degrees C                  to form a viscous white viscous liquid, spreading                  the white viscous liquid onto the filter membrane                  and drying and solidifying to form a coating,                  covering filter membrane with ion adsorption                  layer.

TI Preparing a high-hardness anti-friction and corrosion resistant titanium-carbon-x-oxygen-y coating using carbon dioxide comprising e.g. polishing a substrate, carrying out ultrasonic cleaning and drying under nitrogen to obtain dried sample.
AU ZHANG B
   WANG Z
   ZHANG J
   JIA Q
   MU Z
   QIANG L
   GAO K
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202041352F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a high-hardness anti-friction and corrosion resistant titanium-carbon-x-oxygen-y (Ti-C-x-O-y)coating using carbon dioxide comprising polishing the substrate, carrying out ultrasonic cleaning and drying under nitrogen to obtain a dried sample, transferring to a vacuum chamber and evacuating at 9.8x 104Pa using pulse negative bias for argon gas ion bombardment under a mixed atmosphere of argon and carbon dioxide gas, using the titanium target for DC pulse magnetron sputtering deposition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a high-hardness anti-friction and corrosion resistant titanium-carbon-x-oxygen-y coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical and consumes low energy, and the obtained product has high hardness, low friction coefficient and excellent corrosion resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base material is single crystal silicon (100), stainless steel, bearing steel and gear steel. Preferred Method: The ultrasonic cleaning is by using acetone, absolute ethanol and distilled water in sequence for 5-15 minutes. The condition of ion bombardment by the pressure in the cavity is 5.0 Pa, the pulse negative bias strength is -1000V and the duty cycle is 60%, and the frequency is 60KHz and the bombardment time is 30 minutes. The mixed atmosphere of argon gas and carbon dioxide gas by the volume flow rate of argon gas is 50 sccm, and the volume flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10 sccm. The condition of the DC pulse magnetron sputtering deposition by the air pressure is 1.0 Pa, the pulse bias voltage is -100V and the duty cycle is 60%, the target current is 0.65-1.25A, the pulse duty cycle is 60%, and the deposition time is 60 minutes.

PN CN110332818-A
TI Method for improving combustion performance of cement rotary kiln pulverized coal under oxygen/carbon dioxide atmosphere, involves using coal powder, and burning.
AU LIU Q
   WANG F
   ZHANG Y
   ZHU H
   LIU Z
   YANG L
AE SHANGHAI YUANHAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2019867788
AB    NOVELTY - A cement rotary kiln pulverized coal combustion performance improving method involves using coal powder having an average particle size of 50-60 microns, when burning, where the mixing percentage of the ultrafine-grade pulverized coal at 20 mu m is 10-30%, the mixed mass percentage of the medium-grained coal powder of 20-60 mu m is 10-40%, and the mixed mass percentage of the large-grained coal powder of 60 mu m is 20-50%.
   USE - Method for improving combustion performance of cement rotary kiln pulverized coal under oxygen/carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving combustion performance of cement rotary kiln pulverized coal with ensured dioxide concentration in the dioxide/carbon dioxide, and can improve the self-combustion characteristics of the pulverized coal, and suppresses the formation of nitrogen oxide in the pulverized coal combustion process.

PN CN109704898-A; CN109704898-B
TI Production of olefin involves reacting synthesis            gas stream in reaction zone, separating obtained            product stream, and converting obtained carbon            dioxide-containing stream and hydrogen-containing            stream into crude olefin stream.
AU HU S
   YANG W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP SINOPEC (SNPC-C)
GA 201942309F
AB    NOVELTY - Production of olefin involves reacting (a)                synthesis gas stream in a reaction zone, obtaining                a product stream, separating (b) the product stream                into a carbon dioxide-containing stream (S1) and a                decarburization stream in a decarburization zone,                separating (c) the decarbonization stream into a                hydrogen-containing stream (S2) and a                dehydrogenation stream in a dehydrogenation zone,                separating (d) dehydrogenation stream into a carbon                monoxide-containing stream (S3) and a mixed                hydrocarbon stream through a carbon sequestration                zone, and converting (e) a portion of the stream                (S1) and a portion of the stream (S2) into a crude                olefin stream in a conversion zone. The process (c)                and process (d) can interchanged.
   USE - Production of olefin from synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of olefin with                high carbon dioxide byproduct utilization rate, and                yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Bicarbonate ions are                  formed in the decarburization zone. Carbon                  monoxide and/or hydrogen are separated from the                  dehydrogenation zone and carbon sequestration                  zone, by pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process                  or a membrane separation process.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN CN109663790-A; CN109663790-B
TI Concrete stirring lorry returning plant residual cleaning method, involves controlling pressure in reaction kettle, and cleaning return plant of concrete agitating lorry tank using cleaning solution for cleaning out excess.
AU CANG D
   HOU G

   YAO H
   JI W
   LI C

AE YANCHENG GUOTAI CONCRETE CO LTD (YANC-Non-standard)
   CANG D (CANG-Individual)
GA 201940012Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling carbon dioxide gas and water in a reaction kettle, where carbon dioxide gas is pure carbon dioxide gas or industrial flue gas. Pressure in the reaction kettle is controlled for about 1 5Mpa %. Return plant of a concrete agitating lorry tank is cleaned using cleaning solution for cleaning out excess. Maintaining time of the pressure is about 0.5 to 1h.
   USE - Concrete stirring lorry returning plant residual cleaning method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quickly cleaning the residual tank so as to reduce cleaning time and water consumption. The method enables directly adding cleaning material in a finished concrete mixer lorry so as to improve cleaning efficiency.

PT P
PN WO2019051268-A1; EP3679177-A1; US2021079540-A1; EP3679177-A4
TI System for providing oxygenated organic products comprising two or more carbon and/or oxygen atoms, comprises electrochemical cell, working electrode comprising transition metal or post transition metal binary alloy and oxides.
AU BOCARSLY A
   PARIS A
   FRANCIS S A
   CHU A
AE UNIV PRINCETON (UPRT-C)
   UNIV PRINCETON (UPRT-C)
   UNIV PRINCETON (UPRT-C)
GA 201924222P
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises an electrochemical cell including an electrolyte solution comprising carbon dioxide, a working electrode comprising a transition metal/ post transition metal (TM/PTM) binary alloy and/or oxides for the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to the oxygenated organic products. The oxygenated organic products comprise two or more carbon atoms and/or two or more oxygen atoms.
   USE - System for providing oxygenated organic products (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system shows excellent electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to the oxygenated organic products. The chromium oxide-gallium oxide film on glassy carbon is a catalyst having high oxalate faradic efficiency, good stability, and its ability to perform the electrochemical transformation in water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for forming oxygenated organic products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The TM is a first row transition metal selected from group VIII B, preferably nickel or chromium. The PTM is selected from group II B or III A of the periodic table, preferably aluminum. The electrolyte solution further comprises carbon monoxide. The oxygenated organic products comprise oxalate. The transition metal oxide or post transition metal oxide comprises chromium oxide-gallium oxide. Preferred Properties: The electrolyte solution has an acidic pH. The electrolytic site is anionic.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The alloy comprises at least one of a transition metal and post-transition metal. The TM/PTM binary alloy is of formula: TMxPTMy, and x and y are 1-10.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).)

PT P
PN CN109453720-A
TI Tubular reactor comprises a tubular reactor and carbon dioxide removal assembly, the tubular reactor comprising a reaction pipe provided with reaction space and placed in catalyst device, multi-layer placing plate set on the reaction pipe.
AU ZHOU B
AE YONGKANG DUDI IND & TRADE CO LTD (YONG-Non-standard)
GA 201925442W
AB    NOVELTY - Tubular reactor comprises a tubular reactor and carbon dioxide removal assembly, the tubular reactor comprising a reaction pipe (2) provided with a reaction space; the reaction pipe is placed in the catalyst device. The multi-layer placing plate is set on the reaction pipe, where one end has the air inlet pipe (21) and the other end has air outlet pipe (22). The catalyst device comprises two end gas supplied by the fixed cylinder uniformly arranged.
   USE - Used as tubular reactor.

PN CN109096289-A; CN109096289-B
TI New 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-(1-(phenyl-4-yl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1-ethylpyridinium) porphyrin cobalt(II) bromide complex useful in highly active catalytic carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction.
AU LI J
   ZHAO D
   WANG N
   WANG Y
   SONG D
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 2019024566
AB    NOVELTY - 5,10,15,20-Tetrakis(4-(1-(phenyl-4-yl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1-ethylpyridinium) porphyrin cobalt(II) bromide complex (metal cobalt porphyrin) (I), is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in highly active catalytic carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) exhibits synergistic catalytic effect and excellent catalytic activity and selectivity in the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide, can catalyze turn-over number and turn-over frequency of epichlorohydrin to reach 4320 h-1 and 529 h-1, respectively, and has selectivity of more than 99%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 5,10,15,20-Tetrakis(4-(1-(phenyl-4-yl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1-ethylpyridinium) porphyrin cobalt(II) bromide complex (metal cobalt porphyrin) of formula (I) and its pyridinium salt and a water-soluble cation, is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises heating 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-(1-(phenyl-4-yl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1-ethylpyridinium) porphyrin bromide and cobalt acetate in water under reflux condition to obtain a crude product, and purifying the crude product to obtain (I). Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) dissolving 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-(1-(phenyl-4-yl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1-ethylpyridinium) porphyrin bromide in water, then adding cobalt acetate and heating to reflux until the reaction is completed, (b) cooling the solution to room temperature, adding excess ammonium hexafluorophosphate to the solution to complete precipitation of cobalt porphyrin, filtering, washing solid many times with anhydrous diethyl ether, and drying to obtain a crude product, and (c) dissolving the crude product in acetone, adding an excess of tetrabutylammonium bromide to the solution to precipitate the cobalt porphyrin as a bromine salt, suction filtering, washing solid many times with acetone and naturally drying to obtain a final product.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108998204-A
TI Preparing myrrh volatile oil component involves crushing myrrh, taking powder in extractor supercritical fluid extraction-carbon dioxide, extracting, filtering, and decompressing and concentrating to obtain extract.
AU INVENTOR U
AE QIDONG CHUANGLU NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3558J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing myrrh volatile oil component involves crushing myrrh, taking 10-20 pts. wt. powder in extractor supercritical fluid extraction-carbon dioxide, extracting under 12-14 MPa pressure at 50-54 degrees C temperature with 3-5 L/kg carbon dioxide flow rate for 3-5 hours, placing the extract at 4 degrees C temperature for 23-25 hours, filtering, decompressing and concentrating to obtain the extract, eluting by silica gel columnchromatography with 90:1, 70:1, 55:1, 30:1, 22:1, 1:1 volume ratio dichloromethane-methanol, collecting 22:1 eluting portion, and concentrating to obtain the product.
   USE - Method used for preparing myrrh volatile oil component (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, can quickly obtain a large amount of volatile oil components, has remarkable anti-tumor activity, and can be used in drug preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: Preparing myrrh volatile oil component involves extracvting under 13 MPa pressure at 52 degrees C temperature with 4 L/kg carbon dioxide flow rate for 4 hours, and placing the extract at 4 degrees C temperature for 24 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
