PT P
PN US2018064858-A1; US10603417-B2
TI Preparation of red blood cells for transfusion in            treatment of inherited hemoglobinopathy, by separating            red blood cells from whole blood, depleting oxygen and            carbon dioxide, and storing in anaerobic storage            bag.
AU YOSHIDA T
   VERNUCCI P
AE NEW HEALTH SCI INC (NEWH-Non-standard)
   HEMANEXT INC (HEMA-Non-standard)
GA 202024086D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of red blood cells (RBCs) for                transfusion comprises obtaining whole blood,                separating RBCs from whole blood to form packed                RBCs, depleting oxygen and carbon dioxide from the                packed RBCs prior to storage to prepare depleted                packed RBCs, and storing the depleted packed RBCs                in anaerobic storage bag. The storage bag comprises                an outer bag having barrier film that is                impermeable to oxygen and carbon dioxide, and an                inner bag in contact with the red blood                cells.
   USE - The method is for preparing red blood cells                for transfusion in treatment of inherited                hemoglobinopathy, particularly thalassemia, alpha                -thalassemia, or beta -thalassemia (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The red blood cells reduce complications and                morbidity from chronic transfusion therapy; and                have higher 24-hour recovery and reduced excess                iron when transfused into the subject, compared to                red blood cells not oxygen depleted.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The stored oxygen-depleted                  RBCs are stored for up to 42 days. The method                  further comprises adding additive solution to                  packed RBCs to form suspension of packed RBCs,                  oxygenating the stored depleted RBCs having                  initial oxygen saturation of less than 3 Torr,                  irradiating the depleted packed RBCs with                  gamma-ray or X-ray before transfusion,                  transfusing the RBCs into a subject who requires                  greater than or equal to 30 units of blood per                  year, and reducing iron overload in the                  subject.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anaerobic storage                  bag further comprises sorbent comprising oxygen                  sorbent and/or carbon dioxide sorbent, located                  between inner bag and outer bag. The anaerobic                  storage bag is irradiated by gamma-ray or                  X-ray.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive solution                  comprises additive solution 1 (AS-1), AS-3, AS-5,                  Saline-Adenine-Glucose-Mannitol (SAGM),                  Phosphate-Adenine-Glucose-Guanosine-Saline-Mannitol                  (PAGG-SM),                  Phosphate-Adenine-Glucose-Guanosine-Gluconate-Mannitol                  (PAGG-GM), MAP, SOLX (RTM: alkaline RBC storage                  solution containing bicarbonate, adenine,                  glucose, mannitol and phosphate), ESOL, EAS61,                  OFAS1, and/or additive solution including                  adenine, dextrose, mannitol and sodium dihydrogen                  phosphate (OFAS3).
   ACTIVITY - Antianemic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. GA 201810272A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pressure recovery system provided with a medium-temperature expander and a low-temperature-stage expander, where a system main body comprises a gas cooler, a medium-temperature evaporator, a low temperature evaporator, a reservoir, a throttle valve and an electronic expansion valve. An outlet of the low-temperature-stage expander is connected with the low-temperature-stage compressor inlet through the low temperature evaporator. An outlet of the low temperature grade compressor is connected with an inlet of the middle temperature-stage compressor.
   USE - Residual pressure recovery carbon dioxide based low-temperature freezer refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is energy-saving, and realizes double-level compression, and improves working efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a residual pressure recovery carbon dioxide based low-temperature freezer refrigeration system.

PN CN114588754-A
TI Device for capturing and compressing air carbon            dioxide comprises carbon dioxide inlet pipe, sodium            bicarbonate solution conducting pipe and carbon dioxide            discharging pipe that are sealed through plug.
AU LV L
   HUA B
   WANG S
AE SHENZHEN ANCHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION              TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202287576H
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises purification component                (110) connected with an air collecting component                (130). The purifying component is provided with a                purifying tank (111), where the purification tank                is located in a shell (300). A purification bottle                (111) is filled with saturated sodium bicarbonate                solution. A plug (113) is clamped at a bottle                opening of the purifying bottle. A middle portion                of the plug is clamps with a sodium bilicarbonate                liquid conducting pipe (115). A carbon dioxide                inlet pipe (114) and a carbon dioxide discharging                pipe (116) are respectively connected with two                sides of a middle end of a plug through the plug.                The carbon dioxide insulating pipe, the sodium                chloride solution conducting pipe, and the carbon                dioxide discharge pipe are sealed by the plug                through a plug.
   USE - The device is used for capturing and                compressing air carbon dioxide used as gas                fertilizer, extinguishing fire, manufacturing                refrigerant and for chemical industry as industrial                raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The dust removing effect of the static dust                removing rod and the filter carbon dioxide                permeable film of the carbon dioxide in the air is                captured. The carbon dioxide after capturing by the                saturated sodium bicarbonate solution method,                collecting the purifying bottle, after starting the                servo motor, the negative pressure of carbon                dioxide discharging pipe and the pre-pressing pipe                connected with the film type compressor is changed,                so as to extract the carbon-dioxide gas in the                purifier bottle, to ensure that carbon dioxide                transmission amount introduced into the film-type                compressor is a fixed value, to control the process                of compression, at the same time, saturated                sodium-bicarbonide solution in purifier tank can                clean for carbon dioxide gas. The compression                efficiency is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the using                method of the device for capturing and compressing                air carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an inner structural view of                the purification tank.
   Purifying component (110)
   Purifying tank (111)
   Purifying bottle (112)
   Carbon dioxide inlet pipe (114)
   Carbon dioxide discharge pipe (116)
   Shell (300)

PT P
PN CN112521285-A; CN214193100-U; CN112521285-B
TI Synthesizing di-tert-butyl dicarbonate comprises            using alkali metal salt of tert-butanol, using carbon            dioxide as another reactant and solvent, reacting in            supercritical state of carbon dioxide to generate            mixture of alkali metal salt.
AU LIU Z
   LIU L
   HAO G
AE SHANDONG XISACE NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202131776F
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing di-tert-butyl dicarbonate                comprises (1) using alkali metal salt of                tert-butanol as raw material, using carbon dioxide                as another reactant and solvent, reacting in                supercritical state of carbon dioxide to generate                mixture of alkali metal salt of mono-tert-butyl                carbonate dissolving in supercritical carbon                dioxide, (2) dissolving phosgene compounds in                liquid carbon dioxide, (3) adding obtained solution                to mixture produced by the reaction in (1), and                reacting in supercritical state of carbon dioxide                to generate di-tert-butyl tricarbonate, and (4)                after completing reaction, cooling supercritical                carbon dioxide dissolved in di-tert-butyl                tricarbonate to liquefy carbon dioxide, and                transferring to separation tank, slowly releasing                pressure, heating, and decomposing di-tert-butyl                tricarbonate to produce di-tert-butyl                dicarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                di-tert-butyl dicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids problems of post-treatment                water pollution and product loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing di-tert-butyl dicarbonate                comprises (1) using alkali metal salt of                tert-butanol as raw material, using carbon dioxide                as another reactant and solvent, reacting in                supercritical state of carbon dioxide to generate                mixture of alkali metal salt of mono-tert-butyl                carbonate dissolving in supercritical carbon                dioxide, (2) dissolving phosgene compounds in                liquid carbon dioxide, (3) adding obtained solution                to mixture produced by the reaction in (1), and                reacting in supercritical state of carbon dioxide                to generate di-tert-butyl tricarbonate, and (4)                after completing reaction, cooling supercritical                carbon dioxide dissolved in di-tert-butyl                tricarbonate to liquefy carbon dioxide, and                transferring to separation tank, slowly releasing                pressure, heating, and decomposing di-tert-butyl                tricarbonate to produce di-tert-butyl dicarbonate.                The process uses tert-butanol alkali metal salt and                phosgene compounds as the reaction raw materials,                and the process uses supercritical carbon dioxide                as the reaction raw material and solvent, and                synthesizes di-tert-butyl dicarbonate in the                supercritical state of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal                  tert-butoxide is sodium tert-butoxide or                  potassium tert-butoxide, the phosgene compound is                  triphosgene, diphosgene, phosgene or tert-butyl                  chloroformate. In the step (1), the alkane                  co-solvent is cyclohexane or n-hexane. The ether                  co-solvent is tetrahydrofuran, 1,4-dioxane or                  ethylene glycol dimethyl ether. The amide                  co-solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide or                  N,N-dimethylacetamide. The tertiary amine                  co-solvent is triethylamine or                  N-methylmorpholine. In the step (3), the amide                  catalyst is N,N-dimethylformamide or                  N,N-dimethylacetamide. The tertiary amine                  catalyst is triethylamine or N-methylmorpholine.                  Preferred Method: In the step (1), the reaction                  temperature is 31.1-65 degrees C, the pressure is                  7.39-15.9 MPa, the molar ratio of t-butoxide                  alkali metal salt to carbon dioxide is                  1:6.78-17.54, and the reaction time is 30-120                  minutes. In the step (2), controlling temperature                  within the range of -20 degrees C to -30 degrees                  C, the pressure is 1.9-7 MPa, and the weight                  ratio of phosgene compound to liquid carbon                  dioxide is 1:1-3. In the step (3), the reaction                  temperature is 31.1-65 degrees C, the pressure is                  7.39-15.9 MPa, and the reaction time is 30-120                  minutes, the phosgene compound is tert-butyl                  chloroformate, and the molar ratio of the alkali                  metal salt of tert-butanol to the phosgene                  compound is 1:0.95-1.05, the phosgene compound is                  phosgene, and the molar ratio of the alkali metal                  salt of tert-butanol to the phosgene compound is                  1:0.475-0.525, the phosgene compound is                  diphosgene, and the molar ratio of the alkali                  metal salt of tert-butanol to the phosgene                  compound is 1:0.2375-0.2625, or the phosgene                  compound is triphosgene, and the molar ratio of                  the alkali metal salt of tert-butanol to the                  phosgene compound is 1:0.1583-0.175. In the step                  (4), the decomposition reaction temperature is                  20-60 degrees C, the final pressure is normal                  pressure, and the reaction time is 30-60 minutes.                  In the step (1), adding co-solvent, and the                  co-solvent is an alkane, ether, amide or tertiary                  amine co-solvent, and the molar ratio of the                  co-solvent to the alkali metal salt of                  tert-butanol is 0.01-0.09:1. In the step (3),                  adding catalyst, the catalyst is an amide                  catalyst or a tertiary amine catalyst, and the                  molar ratio of the catalyst to the phosgene is                  0.001-0.01:1. The process adopts a synthesis                  device of di-tert-butyl dicarbonate. The                  synthesis device of di-tert-butyl dicarbonate                  includes a dissolving tank, a reaction tank, and                  a separating tank. The dissolving tank passes                  through the plunger pump is connected with the                  feed port of the reactor. The reaction kettle is                  connected to the feed port of the separation                  kettle through a precision filter, and the top of                  the separation kettle is connected to a vent pipe                  through a pressure reducing valve and vented. The                  bottom of is connected with the discharge valve.                  The feed port of the dissolving kettle is                  connected with the raw material B input pipe. The                  solid feed port of the reactor is connected to                  the feed pipe of raw material A. The air inlet of                  the dissolving kettle, the air inlet of the                  reaction kettle, and the air inlet of the                  separation kettle are respectively connected to                  the gas carbon dioxide input pipe. The liquid                  inlet of the dissolving kettle and the liquid                  inlet of the reaction kettle are respectively                  connected with the liquid carbon dioxide input                  pipe. The liquid inlet of the reaction kettle is                  also connected with the auxiliary material input                  pipe. The bottom of the reaction kettle is also                  connected to the cyclone separator and the bag                  filter in sequence.

GA 2022518060
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical device comprises an anode, a cathode, an electrolyte membrane positioned between the anode and the cathode to separate the anode region and the cathode region, a first inlet for supplying humidified carbon dioxide to the cathode region, an electrochemical cell (10) including a second inlet for supplying water or electrolyte to the anode region, a first outlet for discharging unreacted carbon dioxide, and a second outlet for discharging unreacted water or electrolyte; a humidifier (20) connected to a first inlet for supplying humidified carbon dioxide to the cathode region and supplying humidified carbon dioxide to the cathode region; and an absorption column (61) for discharging a solution obtained by dissolving the unreacted carbon dioxide into an amine solution by connecting a first outlet for discharging the unreacted carbon dioxide, and a recirculation device (60) comprising a stripping column (62) for separating solution into carbon dioxide and an amine solution.
   USE - The device is useful for recycling carbon dioxide using electrochemical method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases efficiency of the gas phase reduction reaction of carbon dioxide; and reduces renewable energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recycling carbon dioxide using electrochemical method, comprising (a) supplying an electrochemical cell with carbon dioxide injected into the first inlet and water or electrolyte that is humidified with a humidifier and injected through the second inlet to perform an electrochemical reaction, (b) preparing a solution by dissolving unreacted carbon dioxide that has not been electrochemically reacted in an amine solution in an absorption column in recirculation device, (c) separating the dissolved solution into carbon dioxide and an amine solution through a stripping column in a recirculation device, and (d) recirculating the carbon dioxide separated in the stripping column (62) and supplying it back to the electrochemical cell as humidified carbon dioxide.

AU PURVIS D C
   ALTENBERG D A
   PECHAL K
   KENDALL E
AE WILSONART LLC (WILS-Non-standard)
GA 2021422240
AB    NOVELTY - Non-flammable contact adhesive comprises a chemically distinct solvent comprising a mixture of flammable and non-flammable components, chemically distinct copolymer rubbers, chemically distinct tackifiers, chemically distinct stabilizer resins and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The adhesive is useful in aerosol applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive is non-flammable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adhesive further comprises nitrogen gas. The adhesive is an aerosol adhesive. The solvent comprises 40-90 wt.% trans-dichloroethylene, 5-20 wt.% hydrofluoroolefin, and 5-20 wt.%, 5-10 wt.% hydrofluorocarbon fluid with zero ozone-depletion potential. The hydrofluoroolefin is trans-1-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene. The hydrofluorocarbon fluid is 1,1,1,2,2,3,4,5,5,5-decafluoropentane. The solvent further comprises tert-butyl acetate and parachlorobenzotrifluoride. The adhesive has a percent solid level of 15-36% with the solvent constituting 64-85 wt.% adhesive.Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide gas is included at a ratio of 1.5 pounds of carbon dioxide per 30 pounds of the cumulative weight of the solvent, copolymer rubbers, tackifiers, and stabilizer resins. The adhesive comprises 70-130 pts. wt. tackifiers and 0.5-8 pts. wt. stabilizers with respect to 100 pts. wt. block copolymer rubber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The copolymer rubbers are a mixture of 80% poly(styrene-isoprene-styrene) resins and 20% poly(styrene-butadiene-styrene) resins. The tackifiers at least two resins chosen from rosin esters, aliphatic resins, cycloaliphatic hydrocarbon resins, water-white thermoplastic resins made from purified aromatic hydrocarbon monomers, polyterpene resins, and terpene phenolic resins. The stabilizing resins are mixtures comprising at least two of tris(2,4,-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite, octadecyl-3-(3,5-di-tert butyl-4-hydrophenyl)-propionate, or pentaerythritol tetrakis(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyhydrocinnamate).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

using                INcell analyzer 2200 (RTM: Cell imaging system)                analysis dyeing of the cell, quantitatively                measuring the number of mitochondria, area and                activity using analysis device, extracting total                RNA cuticle cell using isogen II, performing                microarray analysis, identifying gene of expression                change, establishing the following standard for                obviously up-regulated or down-regulated gene,                up-regulating gene, where Z-score is &#8805; 2                and the ratio #12202, anti-active-&#946;-catenin, #05-665 and                anti-TOMM20, ab186735) overnight at 4&#176;C,                after washing with PBS, incubating cells with                secondary antibodies at 25&#176;C for 2 hours,                washing the cells and staining with Hoechst33342 at                room temperature for 20 minutes, observing with a                viewing device, where all the experiment is                performed for at least 3 times, and providing the                representative data, where result display is                average standard deviation, using bilateral student                t test to determine statistical significance, and                statistical significance is defined as p &lt;                0.05 (*p &lt; 0.05, **p is less TI Comprehensively preserving Munage grapes comprises e.g. selecting picked fresh Munage grapes, pruning, centering air-conditioning preservative device, cooling, pre-cooling, adjusting storage atmosphere when starting pre-cooling.
AU LI P
   FAN X
   ZHANG Y
   YAN S
   CUI Y
   WANG H
AE TIANJIN AGRIC COLLEGE (UNTN-C)
   TIANJIN AGRIC COLLEGE (UNTN-C)
GA 201868368X
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensively preserving Munage grapes comprises e.g. (i) selecting picked fresh Munage grapes without mechanical damage, no plant diseases and insect pests, pruning, centering the air-conditioning preservative device, cooling to 0-4 degrees C, pre-cooling for 24 hours, adjusting the storage atmosphere when starting pre-cooling, (ii) adding beta -cyclodextrin-containing cinnamon oil microcapsules to the Munage grapes storage container in the modified atmosphere preservation device, the microcapsule (in terms of cinnamon oil) and the weight ratio of the Munage grapes is 0.15-0.3 g/kg, and adjusting the storage atmosphere to oxygen volume content 4-5%, carbon dioxide volume content 5-6%, relative humidity 90-95%, insulation -1 degrees C to 1 degrees C, maintaining the atmosphere until the end of storage for more than 150 days, and (iii) in the storage process of the step (ii), on the 8-10th day of storage, and nitrogen oxide content of the nitric oxide is once input to the storage atmosphere is 80-120 mu l/l.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensively preserving Munage grapes.

The beta -cyclodextrin-containing cinnamon oil microcapsule is prepared by (a) dissolving cinnamon oil in 1.8-2.2 volumes of absolute ethanol to obtain a solution of cinnamon oil in absolute ethanol, (b) taking beta -cyclodextrin powder of 7.5-8.5 times of the quality of cinnamon oil in a beaker, adding distilled water, and ultrasonically preparing a saturated solution of saturated beta -cyclodextrin, (c) slowly adding the cinnamon oil anhydrous ethanol solution to the beta -cyclodextrin solution under ultrasonic conditions, maintaining temperature at 38-45 degrees C, and cooling to room temperature, and sonication, and (d) refrigeration at 0-4 degrees C, suction filtration to obtain precipitates, washing filter cake, drying to constant weight to obtain cinnamon oil- beta -cyclodextrin microcapsule having a cinnamon oil content of 6.5-6.7%.

PT P
PN CN113266957-A; CN215909273-U
TI Carbon dioxide phase change liquefying circulation heating system for air conditioner heater, has condenser connected with booster pump to receive carbon dioxide steam discharged by booster pump and transmit latent heat through pipe wall surface of condenser.
AU YANG J
AE SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202199401Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank connected with a pressure air source for alternately introducing high pressure gas to control pressure in the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. An evaporator is connected with the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank to receive liquid carbon dioxide provided by the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. A booster pump is connected with the evaporator. A condenser is connected with the booster pump to receive carbon dioxide steam discharged by the booster pump and transmit latent heat through a pipe wall surface of the condenser.
   USE - Carbon dioxide phase change liquefying cycle heating system for an air conditioner heater(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses liquid carbon dioxide reverse circulation principle to heat. The liquid carbon dioxide absorbs sensible heat of outdoor air in the evaporator tube and converts into gaseous carbon dioxide, enters into the condenser after pressurizing and heating by the pressurizing pump. The high temperature and high pressure gaseous carbon dioxide exchanges heat to the refrigerating medium flowing through the surface of the condenser. The temperature rises after the secondary refrigerant absorbs the latent heat so as to realize heating process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon dioxide phase change liquefying circulation heating method of heating system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic view of a carbon dioxide phase change liquefying cycle heating system for an air conditioner heater. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PN KR2018101010-A; KR2276368-B1
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system for ship, drives turbine using supercritical carbon dioxide as working fluid, and supplies supercritical carbon dioxide discharged from turbine to ship by producing steam as heat source.
AU JANG Y A
   MOON Y S
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 2018730815
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a supercritical carbon dioxide generating unit (100) which is provided for driving a turbine using supercritical carbon dioxide as a working fluid. The supercritical carbon dioxide discharged from the turbine is supplied to a ship by producing steam as a heat source. A compressor (120) is arranged to compress the supercritical carbon dioxide. A heater (130) is arranged to heat the supercritical carbon dioxide compressed in the compressor.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system for ship used in marine or offshore plants.
   ADVANTAGE - By applying a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle, the power can be generated effectively. The steam is produced through the waste heat of the supercritical carbon dioxide discharged from the turbine of the power generation section without consuming additional fuel and the steam is supplied to the inside of the ship, so as to enhance the energy efficiency. The power generation efficiency can be increased by about 20% or more in comparison with the steam turbine when the supercritical carbon dioxide power generation turbine is applied. The specific installation area is reduced, so that the cargo load is increased by applying to the ship having a limited installation area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a supercritical carbon dioxide power generation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system.
   Supercritical carbon dioxide generating unit (100)
   Turbine portion (110)
   Compressor (120)
   Heater (130)
   Heat exchanger (140)
DC Q24 (Ships; waterborne vessels; related equipment (B63)); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))

PT P
PN CN108033820-A; CN207845516-U
TI Microbial fertilizer production system comprises two primary treatment mechanism, mixing mechanism, extruding mechanism and a bent pipe, grinding machines, and fermentation device.
AU JIN J
   ZHOU H
AE JIN J (JINJ-Individual)
GA 201839857U
AB    NOVELTY - Microbial fertilizer production system comprises two primary treatment mechanism, mixing mechanism, extruding mechanism and a bent pipe, grinding machines, and fermentation device. The two primary treatment mechanisms are connected with the mixing mechanism. The grinding mechanism is connected with the mixing mechanism, and the squeezing mechanism is connected with the smashing mechanism through an elbow pipe. The fermentation device is located below the grinding mechanism. The two primary treatment mechanisms, the mixing mechanism, and the extrusion mechanism are all set on a support table.
   USE - Used as microbial fertilizer production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces high quality microbial fertilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Microbial fertilizer production system comprises two primary treatment mechanism, mixing mechanism, extruding mechanism and a bent pipe, grinding machines, and fermentation device. The two primary treatment mechanisms are respectively connected with the mixing mechanism. The grinding mechanism is connected with the mixing mechanism, and the squeezing mechanism is connected with the smashing mechanism through an elbow pipe. The fermentation device is located directly below the grinding mechanism. The two primary treatment mechanisms, the mixing mechanism, and the extrusion mechanism are all set on a support table. The grinding mechanism and fermentation device are all located below the support table. The primary processing mechanism includes a feed hopper, a feed shaft, shaft machine, press trough, chassis, press shaft, press top, disposed inside and below the feed hopper is a feeding shaft. The feeding hopper protruding from both ends of the material shaft is connected with the shaft machine arranged outside the feeding hopper. The outlet end of the feed hopper is connected to the feed trough. Two press shafts are disposed on the press tank. Both ends of the pressure shaft are connected with a chassis disposed outside the pressure groove. The bottom of the pressure tank is provided with a pressing top. The mixing mechanism includes a mixing round tank, rotating arm, rotating shaft and mixing knife. The round mix tank is connected to the end of the feed tank. A rotating shaft is disposed in the center of the mixing circular groove. A rotating knife is arranged on the rotating shaft. The upper end of the rotating shaft is connected with the rotating arm disposed outside the mixing round groove. The squeezing mechanism includes an extrusion groove, a slot wall case, a telescopic rod, and an extruded arc panel. The extruding tank is connected with the mixing round tank. Multiple extruded arc plates are arranged on both sides of the extrusion groove. The extruded arc panel is connected to the telescopic rod. The telescopic rod extends out of the extruding groove and is connected to a slot wall case provided outside the extruding slot. The end of the extrusion groove is connected with the elbow. The grinding mechanism includes a crushed tube, a support frame, a screed shaft, a spiral reamer, a screed front end machine, and a hinged shaft back end machine. The crushed tube is connected to the end of the elbow. The crushed tube is set below the support table through a support frame. A winch shaft is arranged in the crushed tube. The screw shaft is provided with a spiral reamer. The ends of the twisting shaft extends are from the ends of the crushed tube to the front end of the screed shaft and the back end of the shaft. Multiple screening holes are evenly arranged at the bottom of the crushed tube. The fermentation device includes a fermentation tank, a carbon dioxide gas supply mechanism, a steam mechanism, a heating mechanism, and a control mechanism. The fermentation tank is located directly below the cutter tube. Two sides of the fermentation tank are respectively provided with a carbon dioxide gas supply mechanism and a steam mechanism. The other sides of the fermentation tank are respectively provided with a heating mechanism and a control mechanism. The carbon dioxide gas supply mechanism includes multiple carbon dioxide gas tanks and a gas pipe. The carbon dioxide tank is placed outside the fermentation tank. The carbon dioxide gas tank is connected to the fermentation tank through three equally spaced gas pipes. The steam mechanism includes a cold steam box, a steam pipe. The cold steam box is disposed outside the fermentation tank. The cold steam box is connected to the fermentation tank through three rows of steam pipes. The heating mechanism includes a high temperature motor box and a temperature guiding splint. The high temperature motor box is placed outside the fermentation tank. The high temperature motor box is connected to the fermentation tank through three rows of temperature-control splints. The control mechanism includes a control center, sealed telescopic arms, sealing door panel, seal the door slot, fermentation arm, fermentation board, fermentation plate tank and retaining rod. The control center is disposed outside the fermentation tank. A sealable telescopic arm is provided on the side of the control center from top to bottom, three fermentable telescopic arms and one sealed telescopic arm. The sealed telescopic arm is connected to the sealing door. The sealing door is disposed on the sealing door slot. The sealed door panel slots are respectively disposed on both sides of the fermentation tank. The sealed door panel slot extends outside the fermentation tank. The fermentable telescopic arm is connected to a fermentation plate. The fermentation plate is set on the fermentation plate slot. The fermenter tank is disposed on both sides of the fermentation tank and the fermenter tank extends outside the fermentation tank. A side of the fermentation tank is provided with a plurality of openings for sealing the door. The fermentation plate moves back and forth inside and outside the fermentation tank. The stopper is provided at the opening in the fermentation tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the microbial fertilizer production system.

The fermentable telescopic arm corresponds to the number and position of the fermenter. The press shaft and the press top are both two, and the press shaft and the press top are uniformly provided with multiple protrusions and multiple rods are staggered on the dial shaft. The compound knife is a multilayer arc knife structure and three or more extruded arc panels are there. The upper and lower ends of the fermentation tank are connected with an upper wide mouth and lower narrow mouth. There are three carbon dioxide gas tanks and each steam pipe has more than eight steam pipes. Each row of temperature-control splints has more than 6 rows of temperature-control splints.

GA 201889700P
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorber comprises an amine                compound supported on porous particles in which a                hydrophilic fiber and a porous powder are combined                with a hydrophilic binder.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorber is used in carbon                dioxide separation system (claimed) for separating                carbon dioxide from process gas discharged from                boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorber has excellent                abrasion resistance even when used in                continuous-processing system, and quick rate of                carbon dioxide absorption capability, and high                specific gravity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of carbon dioxide absorber,                which involves mixing and granulating the                hydrophilic fiber, porous powder, and hydrophilic                binder aqueous dispersion, drying, producing the                porous particles, preparing an amine aqueous                solution comprising 5-70% amine compound, at 10-100                degrees C, impregnating the amine aqueous solution                with the porous particles, and through-flow drying                the porous particles carrying the amine compound;                and
   (2) carbon dioxide separation system (1),                which has an adsorption tank (11) in which a moving                bed is formed inside with the carbon dioxide                absorber, a desorption tank (12) in which a moving                bed is formed inside with the carbon dioxide                absorber which is passed through the adsorption                tank, a to-be-processed gas supply port (36) for                supplying a to-be-processed gas comprising carbon                dioxide, provided in a lower portion of the                adsorption tank, an off-gas discharge port for                discharging off-gas obtained by adsorption and                removal of the carbon dioxide, provided on an upper                portion of the adsorption tank, a water vapor                supply port for supplying desorption water vapor,                provided in a lower portion of the desorption tank,                and a carbon dioxide discharge port for discharging                carbon dioxide desorbed from the carbon dioxide                absorber, provided on an upper portion of the                desorption tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                moving bed-type carbon dioxide separation system                containing the absorber. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide separation system (1)
   Adsorption tank (11)
   Desorption tank (12)
   Conveyor (15)
   To-be-processed gas supply port (36)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous powder is                  chosen from silica, alumina, zeolite, activated                  carbon, and metal-organic framework. Preferred                  Property: The porous powder has average particle                  diameter of 1-200 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound is                  chosen from amine containing at least one                  hydroxyl group, and polyamine. Preferred                  Properties: The carbon dioxide absorber has                  average particle diameter of 1-5 mm. The porous                  particles have average particle diameter of 1-5                  mm. The log differential pore volume distribution                  of the porous particles has a peak (P1) at 10 nm                  or more and less than 200 nm, and a peak (P2) at                  0.2-10 mu m.

TI System for recovering carbon dioxide from flue gas            in power generation facility, has compressor-condensate            water line which is connected to condensate water drum,            for supplying compressor condensate water as supply            water.
AU MIYAMOTO O
   TSUJIUCHI T
   KAMIJO T
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)

GA 2019754744
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10A) has a carbon dioxide absorber                (18). An absorbent regenerator (20) separates the                carbon dioxide from a rich solution (19) to                regenerate the carbon dioxide absorbent as a lean                solution. A gas discharge line (L21) discharges                carbon dioxide entrained gas (28) from a top                portion (20c) of the absorbent regenerator. A                reflux water drum (30) is mounted in the gas                discharge line, for producing reflux water by                separating the carbon dioxide and water from the                carbon dioxide entrained gas. A condensate water                drum (44) is placed in the separation-gas discharge                line, for producing compressor condensate water by                separating the water from the compressed carbon                dioxide gas. A compressor-condensate water line                (L30) is connected to the condensate water drum,                for supplying the compressor condensate water as                in-system or out-of-system supply water.
   USE - System for recovering carbon dioxide from flue                gas in power generation facility such as thermal                power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The flash drum prevents supplying gas                components in the reclaimer operation and improves                the recovery efficiency of the carbon dioxide                absorption component. The outlet gas temperature of                the emission gas discharged from the absorber is                adjusted to be lower than the inlet gas temperature                of flue gas supplied into the absorber, thus the                water balance is maintained. The reduction in the                amount of water intake from the outside is                achieved. The entrained amount of the carbon                dioxide absorbent entrained in the emission gas is                reduced and the reduction in emissions is achieved.                The discharge of the carbon dioxide absorption                component entrained in the emission gas discharged                to the outside is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of circulating and reusing carbon dioxide absorbent                with carbon dioxide absorber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                system for recovering carbon dioxide.
   System for recovering carbon dioxide                (10A)
   Carbon dioxide absorbent (17)
   Carbon dioxide absorber (18)
   Rich solution (19)
   Absorbent regenerator (20)
   Top portion (20c)
   Carbon dioxide entrained gas (28)
   Reflux water drum (30)
   Condensate water drum (44)
   Gas discharge line (L21)
   Compressor-condensate water line (L30)

PN JP2019089030-A
TI High temperature carbon dioxide gas separator for use as apparatus for processing waste gas emitted from solid oxide fuel cell, has thermally oxidized film that is comprised of oxide containing one metallic element.
AU WAKASUGI T
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 201951349K
AB    NOVELTY - The high temperature carbon dioxide gas separator (10) has a reaction flow path layer (20) that circulates the reactive gas containing carbon dioxide. A medium flow path layer (30) circulates the medium gas for heat-exchanging with the reaction flow path layer provided adjacent to the reaction flow path layer. A base material (22) consists of the metal or non-oxide ceramics. A carbon dioxide absorber layer (24) is formed in the surface in which reactive gas circulates. A thermally oxidized film (26) is inserted between the base material and the carbon dioxide absorber layer. The carbon dioxide absorber layer is formed of complex oxide containing one metallic element contained in base material, and lithium. A carbon dioxide absorber is provided with carbon dioxide absorption/releasing ability. The base material is comprised of material whose heat conductivity is higher than the absorber. The thermally oxidized film is comprised of an oxide containing one metallic element.
   USE - High temperature carbon dioxide gas separator for use as apparatus for processing waste gas emitted from solid oxide fuel cell and factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorber layer is bonded with the base material rigidly through thermally oxidized film so that heat transfer rate between base material and carbon dioxide absorber layer is improved, and the temperature control property of carbon dioxide absorber layer is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The high temperature carbon dioxide gas separator has base material that is formed of silicon carbide, boron carbide, aluminum nitride and copper. The base material is comprised of honeycomb structure or porous body. The carbon dioxide absorber layer is currently formed in the inner surface of the honeycomb structure, or the inner surface of the open pore of the porous body. The downstream drift distance of the flow of the reactive gas is smaller than that of the upstream side of the flow of the reactive gas in the base material. The carbon dioxide absorber is contained with carbonate which can form lithium carbonate and eutectic. The volume of the space at the time of carbon dioxide discharge of the carbon dioxide absorber layer is larger than the difference of the molar volume of the carbon dioxide absorber at the time of carbon dioxide absorption, and the molar volume at the time of carbon dioxide discharge.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the perspective view of the high temperature carbon dioxide gas separator and schematic view of the reaction flow path layer.

A sensor (130) is                installed on a main portion to sense outdoor                temperature and concentration of carbon dioxide in                outdoor air. PN CN114773316-A; CN114773316-B
TI Preparing            2-(2,6-dioxo-piperidin-3-yl)-5-fluoro-isoindole-1,3-dione            comprises e.g. carrying out halogen-magnesium exchange            reaction of 2-bromo-4-fluorobenzoic acid and Grignard            reagent and carrying out dehydration and substitution            with 3-aminopiperidine-2,6-dione.
AU LIU Y
   YANG T
   WEI H
AE SHANGHAI CULLGEN INC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202298432C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing                2-(2,6-dioxo-piperidin-3-yl)-5-fluoro-isoindole-1,3-dione                (I), comprises carrying out halogen-magnesium                exchange reaction of 2-bromo-4-fluorobenzoic acid                (II) and Grignard reagent, cooling the reaction                system to 0-10&#8451;, and introducing carbon                dioxide to quench it to prepare 4-fluorophthalic                acid (III) as first intermediate product, carrying                out dehydration reaction of the first intermediate                product to prepare 5-fluoroisobenzofuran-1,3-dione                (IV) as second intermediate product, and carrying                out substitution reaction of the second                intermediate product with                3-aminopiperidine-2,6-dione (V) to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                2-(2,6-dioxo-piperidin-3-yl)-5-fluoro-isoindole-1,3-dione.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: high product yield and purity; and                is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing                2-(2,6-dioxo-piperidin-3-yl)-5-fluoro-isoindole-1,3-dione                of formula (I), comprises carrying out                halogen-magnesium exchange reaction of                2-bromo-4-fluorobenzoic acid of formula (II) and                Grignard reagent, cooling the reaction system to                0-10&#8451;, and introducing carbon dioxide to                quench it to prepare 4-fluorophthalic acid of                formula (III) as first intermediate product,                carrying out dehydration reaction of the first                intermediate product to prepare                5-fluoroisobenzofuran-1,3-dione of formula (IV) as                second intermediate product, and carrying out                substitution reaction of the second intermediate                product with 3-aminopiperidine-2,6-dione of formula                (V) to obtain final product.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction temperature                  of the magnesium halide exchange reaction is                  20-30&#8451;; and the reaction time of the                  magnesium halide exchange reaction is 0.5-3                  hours. The reaction system after the                  halogen-magnesium exchange reaction is completed                  is cooled to 5-10&#8451; before quenching by                  introducing carbon dioxide. The reaction                  temperature of the substitution reaction is                  95-115&#8451;. The method after the quenching                  with carbon dioxide comprises mixing the quenched                  material with toluene, concentrating, mixing the                  concentrated solution with alkane solvent,                  filtering, and taking the solid to prepare first                  intermediate product, where the alkane solvent is                  n-heptane, n-pentane, n-hexane and/or                  cyclohexane.Preferred Components: The Grignard reagent                  is isopropylmagnesium chloride,                  isopropylmagnesium chloride and/or lithium                  chloride. The molar ratio of (II) to Grignard                  reagent is 1:(2-4). The solvent used in the                  dehydration reaction is acetic anhydride, toluene                  and/or dichloromethane. The solvent used in the                  substitution reaction is acetic acid. The system                  of the substitution reaction also includes sodium                  acetate, and the mol ratio of sodium acetate to                  second intermediate product is (1.2-1.6):1.

PN CN114540615-A
TI Sintering of iron ore powder pellets used in steel            metallurgy, involves ball-milling coarse-grained iron            ore powder, limestone, dolomite, coke powder and return            ore, grinding obtained finely ground material, adding            binder to obtained material, mixing, pelletizing,            drying, sintering, and cooling.
AU DONG T
   MA W
   LI Q
   YANG C
   PAN J
   ZHU D
   GUO Z
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202275006C
AB    NOVELTY - Sintering of iron ore powder pellets involves                ball-milling coarse-grained iron ore powder,                limestone, dolomite, coke powder and return ore to                obtain finely ground material, grinding finely                ground material by high-pressure roller to obtain                pretreated raw material, subjecting pretreated raw                material to optimized ore blending, adding binder,                mixing, pelletizing obtained mixture (A) to obtain                raw pellets with predetermined granularity,                carrying out external coke powder rolling on the                raw pellets to obtain mixed material, uniformly                distributing mixed material into multiple layers of                cloth, drying, igniting, sintering,                heat-preserving, cooling, crushing and                granulating.
   USE - Sintering iron ore powder pellets used in                steel metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - The sintering ore powder pellet has excellent                uniformity, and reduces the solid energy                consumption, water content, and water content of                the material, and prevents the over high moisture                in the process. The pellet improves the hot air                permeability, and improves the dynamic condition of                the mass transfer and heat transfer, and the                oxidation atmosphere, and promotes the rational                preparation of the solid phase consolidation and                liquid phase consolidation of the pellet, thus                improving the strength of the sintering, and                reducing the coke powder dosage, and carbon dioxide                emission, and hence realizing low-carbon dioxide                emission and low-energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                sintering process of iron ore powder pellets.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The coarse-grained iron                  ore powder, limestone, dolomite, coke powder and                  return ore are ground to a particle size of                  -0.074 mm, in an amount of more than 60%. The                  finely ground material is ground by high-pressure                  roller until the specific surface area is                  1500-1900 cm2/g.Preferred Property: The particle size of raw                  pellets is 10-16 mm.Preferred Composition: The mixture (A) has                  binary basicity (calcium oxide/silica mass ratio)                  of 1.75-2.1, magnesium oxide content of 1.6-2.1                  wt.%, and silica content of 5-5.2 wt.%. The                  content of coke powder in the pellets is 5-15                  wt.%, and externally rolled coke powder is 85-95                  wt.%, with respect to the total amount of coke                  powder. The usage amount of total coke powder is                  2.5-3.5 wt.% with respect to total amount of raw                  material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The binder is                  bentonite, organic binder and/or composite                  binder. The usage amount of binder is 0.5-2 wt.%                  with respect to the total amount of raw                  material.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves reacting the gas at a gas diffusion electrode to form a product gas and/or a liquid product. The gas diffusion layer comprises a non-porous layer that is permeable to carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide gas, and a porous layer. The reactant comprises carbon monoxides or carbon dioxides. The electrode separates a cathode compartment from an anode compartment of an electrochemical cell. The product gas or liquid product is collected from the gas compartment or catholyte compartment. The absolute pressure in the cell is 20-200 bar. The non-porous layer is a non-porous, polymeric layer and/or the porous layer is a porous, polymeric layer, wherein preferably the non-porous, polymeric layer and/or the porous, polymeric layer comprise polymers selected from the group consisting of poly l-(trimethylsilyl)-l-propyne (PTMSP), polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), poly(vinyltrimethylsilane) (PVTMS), polymethylpentene (PMP), and poly(p-phenylene oxide) (PPO).

   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the gas diffusion electrode with high electrical conductivity, and avoids flooding of the electrode during electrolysis of the liquid electrolyte, thus ensuring a good performance of the electrodes during the electrolysis process.

   (1) a method for preparing molybdenum carbide                catalyst comprising dissolving chloroplatinic acid                or copper nitrate in deionized water, mixing with                molybdenum trioxide powder, spin-evaporating,                drying, purging the obtained powder in the mixed                gas of methane and hydrogen, carbonizing to obtain                a molybdenum carbide catalyst, and then passivating                in the mixed gas of oxygen and nitrogen and                storing; and
   (2) a method for preparing methanol by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide based on a                molybdenum carbide catalyst comprising using the                molybdenum carbide catalyst for hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide at a reaction temperature of 200-300                degrees C, a reaction pressure of 5 MPa, and a                space velocity of the feed gas of 12000 ml/g/h to                obtain methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process parameters of                  the carbonization are raising the temperature                  from room temperature to 400 degrees C at a rate                  of 5 degrees C/minute, then raising the                  temperature to 700 degrees C at a rate of 0.5                  degrees C/minute, and maintaining for 1 hour.                  Preferred Components: The volume of methane in                  the mixed gas of methane and hydrogen is 20%. The                  flow rate during purging is 150 ml/minutes. The                  volume of oxygen in the mixed gas of oxygen and                  nitrogen is 0.5%. The passivation time is 220                  minutes. The chloroplatinic acid is used as the                  precursor when the catalyst is Pt/ alpha -Mo xC                  y. The copper nitrate is used as the precursor                  when the catalyst is Cu/ beta -Mo xC y. The raw                  material gas is composed of carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen in a volume ratio of 1:3.

PN CN113225918-A
TI Method for forming conductive medium pattern based on laser direct writing curved surface interface, involves drying Polydimethylsiloxane to cool mold, and removing PI substrate slowly to obtain flexible curved circuit.
AU XU K
   CUI S
   HUAI Q
   YANG G
   YANG H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021937671
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming a graphene conductive medium (2) on a PI substrate (1) to form a circuit by using a carbon dioxide laser (3), where processed PI substrate is formed on a bottom portion of a curved surface interface mold, where the curved surface interface mold is a 3-D printing photosensitive resin mold (4). Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) solution (5) is added in the curved surface interface mold. The PDMS solution is fused with a graphene structure. The curved surface interface mold is heated in an oven (6). The PDMS is dried to cool the mold. The PI substrate is slowly removed to obtain a flexible curved circuit. The curved interface mold is put into the oven, where heating temperature of the curved interface mold is measured for about 90 degrees centigrade.
   USE - Method for forming conductive medium pattern based on laser direct writing curved surface interface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quickly establishing the conductive network of any pattern with easy processing and manufacturing, better repeatability, stable performance and large scale production, applying strong adhesive force, reducing need for performing post-packaging process, and breaking traditional conductive medium to perform two-dimensional plane forming process on the bottle neck.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a schematic diagram of a conductive medium pattern.
   PI substrate (1)
   Graphene conductive medium (2)
   Carbon dioxide laser (3)
   3-D printing photosensitive resin mold (4)
   Polydimethylsiloxane solution (5)
   Oven (6)

TI Preparing steel slag auxiliary gelling material by            crushing raw steel slag, selecting iron to obtain steel            slag particles, placing steel slag particles into            stirring pre-processing device, and mixing steel slag            particles with additive.
AU MO L
   LIU P
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021851728
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing steel slag auxiliary gelling                material comprises crushing raw steel slag, and                selecting iron to obtain steel slag particles,                where granularity is 0.01-5 mm; taking steel slag                particles into stirring pre-processing device,                mixing steel slag particles with additive solution                and stirring uniformly, where additive solution is                polyol, ethylene glycol, diethanol                monoisopropanolamine, phosphoric acid,                polycarboxylic acid, sulfonic acid, EDTA, sodium                ethylene diamine tetracetate, sodium carbonate and                sodium bicarbonate, and mass ratio of additive                solution and steel slag particles is 0.02-0.20;                placing pre-treated steel slag particles in mill to                grind, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and grinding                steel slag particles to particle size of not                greater than 0.10 mm; and placing steel slag after                grinding into stirring reaction device, and                introducing carbon dioxide gas in stirring reaction                device for 1-45 minutes.
   USE - The method is used for preparing steel slag                auxiliary cementitious material used in cement or                concrete building material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method quickly eliminates free calcium                oxide and section of free magnesium oxide, so as to                solve the problem of poor stability of steel slag,                and greatly improves the gel activity of the steel                slag, ensuring the steel slag auxiliary gelling                material into cement or concrete to be qualified,                promoting the application of steel slag in cement                and concrete building material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of                preparing steel slag auxiliary gelling material                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

TI Preparing heavy calcium carbonate for giving            single component dealcoholized silicone sealant with            good flame retardant property comprises e.g. dry            grinding the selected dolomite, adding aluminum            hydroxide, and mixing uniformly.
AU TONG Z
   QIN L
   HUANG Y
   LI K
   LI Q
   ZHONG L
   CHEN X
   HUANG H
   YE Y
   BEI J
   XIE Y
AE GUANGXI CALCIUM CARBONATE INDUSTRIALIZAT (UYGU-C)
   GUANGXI HEZHOU KELONG MICRO POWDER CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2021680774
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heavy calcium carbonate comprises                (i) dry grinding the selected dolomite to obtain                the heavy calcium carbonate powder, adding aluminum                hydroxide into the ground calcium carbonate powder                obtained by grinding, mixing uniformly to obtain                the mixed material 1, (ii) adding water into the                mixed material 1 of the step (i) to adjust the                slurry, stirring and raising the temperature to                obtain the mixed solution 2, (iii) adding polyester                unsaturated resin into the mixed solution 2 of step                (ii), slowly introducing carbon dioxide gas after                stirring uniformly, stopping introducing carbon                dioxide gas when the pH of the solution reaches                8-9, obtaining the mixed solution 3, (iv) pouring                the mixed solution 3 in the step (iii) into a                vertical stirring mill, grinding by using zirconia                beads as grinding medium to obtain the mixture 4,                and (v) filtering and dehydrating the mixed                solution 4 in the step (iv), drying, crushing,                grading and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing heavy                calcium carbonate, which is useful for giving                single component dealcoholized silicone sealant                with good flame retardant property.
   ADVANTAGE - The heavy calcium carbonate product has                excellent dispersibility, high activity, strong                hydrophobicity, filling in single component                dealcoholized silicone sealant, and can be                compatible with the polymer in the silicone                adhesive system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                heavy calcium carbonate for imparting good flame                retardancy to one-component dealcoholized silicone                sealant prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  magnesium content of the dolomite is controlled                  at 20-35%. The mesh number of the heavy calcium                  carbonate powder is 300-400. In the step (i),                  10-15% aluminum hydroxide is added to the ground                  calcium carbonate powder obtained by grinding. In                  the step (ii), the mass concentration of the                  slurry is controlled at 18-25%. In the step (ii),                  the temperature is raised to 45-55 degrees C                  while stirring. In the step (iii), 0.3-0.8% of                  polyester unsaturated resin is added to the mixed                  solution 2. The polyester unsaturated resin is                  180-200mg potassium hydroxide/g. In the step                  (iv), the diameter of the zirconia beads is 0.7-1                  mm.

PN CN112618475-A
TI Anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic and antipruritic plant composition for preparing cosmetic, comprises purslane, radix sophorae flavescentis, honeysuckle, Artemisia leaf, Perilla, cypress, sage, pawpaw and Magnolia officinalis.
AU ZHAO X
   LIU Y
   CAI J
AE SHANGHAI JIAXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202140546Q
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic and antipruritic plant composition comprises 10-20 pts. wt. purslane, 5-15 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis, 8-24 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 10-40 pts. wt. Artemisia leaf, 12-24 pts. wt. Perilla, 8-30 pts. wt. cypress, 10-27 pts. wt. sage, 7-14 pts. wt. pawpaw and 20-30 pts. wt. Magnolia officinalis.
   USE - Anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic and antipruritic plant composition used for preparing cosmetic, such as repairing emulsion (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic and antipruritic plant composition has quick anti-itching effect, good anti-inflammatory and anti-allergy effect, and prevents local melanin accumulation after allergies and uneven skin color.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing an anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic and antipruritic plant, which involves:
   (A) crushing radix sophorae flavescentis, cypress and Magnolia officinalis, adding water, ultrasonically processing and extracting it with ethanol to obtain first extract;
   (B) mixing, crushing and extracting purslane, honeysuckle, Artemisia leaf, Perilla, and sage with supercritical carbon dioxide at an extraction pressure of 34-40MPa and at a temperature of 35-40 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30L/hour to obtain second extract;
   (C) crushing pawpaw, adding papaya and fermentation bacteria, mixing it uniformly, adding 3-5 times the weight of deionized water, fermenting it at 25-45 degrees C to obtain fermentation broth, putting fermentation broth at 95-100 degrees C, adding ethanol, extracting and sealing it to prepare pawpaw fermentation broth; and
   (D) mixing first extract, second extract and pawpaw fermentation broth, centrifuging and concentrating it to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: The plant composition comprises 13-18 pts. wt. purslane, 8-12 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis, 12-20 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 20-30 pts. wt. Artemisia leaf, 15-21 pts. wt. Perilla, 13-25 pts. wt. cypress, 14-23 pts. wt. sage, 9-11 pts. wt. pawpaw and 23-28 pts. wt. Magnolia officinalis. Preferred Conditions: The Tween 80 is added to ethanol in amount of 20-30% of amount of ethanol. The fermenting bacteria is yeast, and added amount is 20-30% of amount of papaya, and fermentation time is 24-72 hours. The method involves further involves using 12-20 pts. wt. hydrolyzed eggshell membrane, 6-10 pts. wt. Angelica dahurica, 10-20 pts. wt. nicotinamide and 15-25 pts. wt. glabridin, crushing Angelica dahurica and glabridin, mixing and extracting crushed radix sophorae flavescentis, cypress and Magnolia officinalis to obtain first extracting solution, mixing hydrolyzed egg shell membrane with first extracting solution and second extracting solution. The added amount of plant composition in cosmetic is 1-10%. The cosmetic is repairing emulsion, which is made of 1.5-2 wt.% E-102 emulsifier, 2-3 wt.% cetearyl alcohol, 1.5-3 wt.% stearic acid, 2-7 wt.% mineral oil, 1.5-2.5 wt.% capric triglyceride, 1.5-2.5 wt.% polydimethylsiloxane, 7-9 wt.% phase B glycerin, 0.2-0.3 wt.% xanthan gum, 0.05-0.06 wt.% disodium EDTA, 1-10 wt.% plant composition, 3-7 wt.% asarum volatile oil, 15-25 wt.% beta-cyclodextrin, 0.6-1 wt.% modified corn starch, 1-2 wt.% starch, and rest is deionized water. The repairing emulsion is prepared by dispersing xanthan gum in glycerin, heating it up to 85 degrees C, adding disodium EDTA, and stirring it uniformly to obtain stirred product. The E-102 emulsifier, cetearyl alcohol, stearic acid, mineral oil, capratriglyceride and polydimethylsiloxane are mixed and stirred uniformly to obtain stirred product. The stirred products are mixed and cooled to 50 degrees C, and plant composition is added to adjust pH to 7. The modified corn starch, starch and deionized water are mixed uniformly, gelatinized, asarum volatile oil and beta-cyclodextrin are added, mixed and supercritical carbon dioxide is injected, where flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide is 20L/hour, and is mixed uniformly at 45 degrees C and 25MPa to obtain final emulsion.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Antiallergic; Antipruritic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. General compositions.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PN CN112076741-A
TI Cerium oxide/bismuth oxide composite visible            photocatalyst useful e.g. for photocatalytic            degradation of organic pollutants, and photocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises cerium oxide            nanoparticles and bismuth oxide sub-nanorods.
AU NI G
   WU D
   XING Y
   JIN X
   PENG J
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2020C7955S
AB    NOVELTY - Cerium oxide/bismuth oxide composite visible                photocatalyst comprises cerium oxide nanoparticles                and bismuth oxide sub-nanorods, the cerium oxide                nanoparticles is attached to the bismuth oxide                sub-nanorods, In cerium oxide/bismuth oxide                composite materials, cerium oxide and bismuth oxide                couple to form heterojunction.
   USE - The cerium oxide/bismuth oxide composite                visible photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic                degradation of organic pollutants, photocatalytic                water splitting, photocatalytic nitrogen fixation                and photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic material exhibits better                photocatalytic performance than pure cerium oxide                and pure bismuth oxide. The method adopts                homogeneous precipitation method and the                hydrothermal method to prepare cerium oxide/bismuth                oxide composite visible photocatalyst, has simple                process, good controllability, and strong                repeatability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing cerium oxide/bismuth oxide composite                visible photocatalyst, comprising (i) dissolving                cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate in aqueous                solution, heating to 40-60&#176; C, evenly                stirring, adding aqueous ammonia, continuously                stirring and reacting at 40-60&#176; C for                12-36 hours, collecting the precipitate, washing                and drying, calcining the precipitate at                400-600&#176; C for 0.5-1.5 hours to obtain                spherical cerium oxide nanoparticles, (ii)                dispersing sodium bismuthate dihydrate in water,                adding spherical cerium oxide nanoparticles                obtained in step (i), and evenly stirring to obtain                mixed solution, transferring into reaction kettle                for hydrothermal reaction, the temperature control                condition is 100-180&#176; C for 4-8 hours,                cooling and filtering to obtain solid, washing the                solid, and drying to obtain cerium oxide/bismuth                oxide composite visible photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In cerium                  oxide/bismuth oxide composite materials, the                  molar ratio of cerium oxide to bismuth oxide is                  (0.2-0.6):1, preferably 0.4:1 or 0.6:1. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the aqueous solution is                  60-80% v/v ethylene glycol solution. In the step                  (i), the molar ratio of cerium(III) nitrate                  hexahydrate to ammonia in the aqueous ammonia is                  (0.1-1):3. In the step (i), the calcining                  temperature of the precipitate is 500&#176;                  C, and the calcining time is 1 hour. In the step                  (ii), the molar ratio of sodium bismuthate                  dihydrate and cerium oxide is 1:(0.2-0.6). In the                  step (ii), the temperature control condition of                  the hydrothermal reaction is 160&#176; C for                  6 hours.
