PN CN112023980-A; CN112023980-B
TI New ionic liquid functionalized metal            porphyrin-based porous organic polymer heterogeneous            catalyst useful as ring addition reaction catalyst in            preparing cyclic carbonate.
AU CHEN Y
   JI H
   REN Q
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 2020C6162B
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic liquid functionalized metal                porphyrin-based porous organic polymer                heterogeneous catalyst (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as ring addition reaction                catalyst in preparing cyclic carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): provides multifunctional heterogeneous                catalyst with high specific surface area, high                porosity, high stability, easy separation and                reuse; and achieves carbon dioxide capture under                mild conditions and catalytic conversion into                cyclic carbonate with high efficiency and high                selectivity under the condition of no additives and                solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ionic liquid functionalized metal                porphyrin-based porous organic polymer                heterogeneous catalyst of formula (I) is new.
   M = AlCl, Zn, Co, CoCl, Cu, FeCl, Mg, MnCl, Cr                or CrCl;
   X = halo;
   n = 1-10;
   IL = ammonium ionic moieties of formula                (-N+((R1)3)), pyridinium, 3H-imidazol-1-ium                moieties compound of formula (1a), substituted                3H-(1,2,4)triazol-1-ium moieties compound of                formula (1b), phosphonium salt compound of formula                (-P+((R1)3)); and
   R1 = Me, Et, nBu and Ph.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) preparing cyclic carbonate, comprising                taking (I) as catalyst at 10-200 degrees C without                solvents and additives, epoxide of formula (IV) and                carbon dioxide as reaction substrate, performing                cycloaddition reaction for 0.1-72 hours to obtain                cyclic carbonate of formula (III), filtering,                washing and recovering the catalyst after the                reaction, and reusing it, where the molar ratio                between (IV) and catalyst is 2000:1-200. (IV) is                (1,3)dioxolan-2-one,                4,4-dimethyl-(1,3)dioxolan-2-one, dioxolan-2-one of                formulae (IIIa-IIId). (IV) is oxirane,                2,2-dimethyloxirane, or oxirane compound of                formulae (IVa-IVd).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) adding                  tetraaminometalloporphyrin, hydrochloric acid and                  sodium nitrite into deionized water as solvent at                  -5 to 10 degrees C and reacting for 0.5-48 hours                  to obtain diazonium salt intermediate reaction                  solution, neutralizing the diazonium salt                  intermediate reaction solution to pH 7-8 with                  sodium carbonate aqueous solution, adding aqueous                  solution of phloroglucinol and sodium carbonate                  into the above reaction solution for first                  contact reaction, reacting for 0.5-48 hours, and                  purifying and prepare porous metalloporphyrin                  polymer containing phenolic hydroxy groups, where                  the molar ratio between metalloporphyrin,                  hydrochloric acid and sodium nitrite is                  1:1-25:0.5-50, the molar ratio between tetraamino                  metalloporphyrin and phloroglucinol is 1:0.1-20,                  the molar ratio between phloroglucinol and sodium                  carbonate is 1:0.5-30, and (ii) adding the                  prepared phenolic hydroxy metalporphyrin porous                  polymer and bromide (-(CH2)n-IL+X-), anhydrous                  potassium carbonate into the anhydrous solvent                  for the second contact reaction at 50-150 degrees                  C, reacting for 0.5-48 hours, and purifying to                  obtain final product, the mass ratio between                  bromide and anhydrous potassium carbonate is                  100:20-1000:10-1000. Preferred Components: In                  step (i), the molar ratio between                  metalloporphyrin, hydrochloric acid and sodium                  nitrite is 1: 8-15:2-6; the molar ratio between                  tetraamino metalloporphyrin and phloroglucinol is                  1:0.8-5; and the molar ratio between                  phloroglucinol and sodium carbonate is 1:2-10. In                  step (ii), the mass ratio between bromide and                  anhydrous potassium carbonate is                  100:100-300:150-400. In use method, the initial                  carbon dioxide pressure is 1-12 MPa, and the                  initial carbon dioxide concentration is 5-100%.                  Preferred Method: In step (i), the reaction is                  performed at 0-5 degrees C for 12-24 hours. In                  step (ii), the reaction is performed at 100-140                  degrees C for 18-36 hours. The anhydrous solvent                  described in step (i) is anhydrous acetonitrile,                  anhydrous methanol, anhydrous tetrahydrofuran,                  anhydrous N,N'-dimethylformamide and/or anhydrous                  dimethylsulfoxide.

PN CN111715274-A
TI Preparation of heterogeneous catalyst used for            desorbing carbon dioxide in liquid by adding zirconium            sulfate into water, adding HZSM-5 carrier, heating and            stirring, cooling and filtering to obtain precipitates,            and washing with water.
AU WANG L
   XING L
   WEI K
   LI Q
   QI T
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
GA 2020984679
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of heterogeneous catalyst, which                is sulfate (SO42-)/zirconium dioxide (ZrO2) loaded                on HZSM-5 carrier, comprises adding zirconium                sulfate (Zr(SO4)2.4H2O) into water, adding HZSM-5                carrier, heating and stirring, cooling and                filtering to obtain precipitates; and washing the                precipitates with water, drying and roasting to                obtain SO42-/ZrO2-HZSM-5 heterogeneous                catalyst.
   USE - The method is for preparation of heterogeneous                catalyst used for desorbing carbon dioxide (CO2) in                liquid or improving the absorption performance of                the absorption liquid for CO2 (claimed). The                catalyst is useful for CO2 capture in industrial                boilers and power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The heterogeneous catalyst not only can                promote the absorption liquid to desorb CO2, but                also improve the absorption performance of the                absorption liquid to CO2. It improves the                absorption efficiency of CO2 in the absorption                liquid, and has desorption efficiency of 9-12.24                mmol/minute. Compared with the CO2 capturing                process under the condition of not adding the                catalyst, the desorption rate is improved by                35-45%, the absorption efficiency is improved by                30-40%, the stability and circulation use                performance are good, and the CO2 desorption energy                consumption is greatly reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of the catalyst comprising loading the                catalyst on a substrate to obtain supported                catalyst, used for CO2 desorption tower for                desorbing CO2 in the liquid, or improving the                absorption performance of the absorption liquid for                CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of                  Zr(SO4)2.4H2O to carrier is 1:1-10. The heating                  temperature is 80-90 degrees C, and the stirring                  time is 24-48 hours. The drying temperature is                  110-130 degrees C, and the drying time is 12-24                  hours. The roasting temperature is 500-600                  degrees C, and the roasting time is 4-6 hours.                  The heating speed is 5 degrees C/minute. The                  catalyst is assembled into a catalyst module,                  which is arranged at intervals along the height                  direction of the tower to obtain CO2 absorption                  tower. The lower part of the catalyst module in                  the CO2 desorption tower is provided with a                  stirrer to obtain CO2 desorption tower. The                  application method comprises entering the flue                  gas of CO2 from the bottom of CO2 absorption                  tower, flowing the absorption liquid from the top                  part of the CO2 absorption tower, where the two                  are in reverse contact and reacted under the                  action of the catalyst module, absorbing the CO2                  by the absorption liquid, discharging the clean                  flue gas from the CO2 absorption tower, flowing                  the rich liquid with high load of CO2 from the                  CO2 desorption tower top through lean and rich                  liquid heat exchanger, uniformly mixing the rich                  liquid with the catalyst and reacting in the CO2                  desorption tower by the stirrer, and discharging                  the desorbed CO2 from the CO2 desorption tower                  top.

PN CN111579340-A
TI Pre-processing method of ultra-high pure aluminum/aluminum alloy sample comprises placing aluminum alloy sample in hydrofluoric acid for cleaning, washing, removing residual acid liquid, roasting the sample and removing residual acetone.
AU YAO L
   PAN J
   WANG X
   BIAN Y
   ZHOU J
AE KONFOONG MATERIALS INT CO LTD (KONF-Non-standard)
GA 2020846719
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-processing method of ultra-high pure aluminum/aluminum alloy sample comprises (a) placing the block-shaped ultra-pure aluminum/aluminum alloy sample in the 30-50 wt.%. hydrofluoric acid for cleaning, (b) washing the acid washed sample by water, removing the residual acid liquid, (c) cleaning the washed sample by acetone, removing the residual water and (4) roasting the sample after cleaning the acetone, removing the residual acetone and finishing the pre-treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for pre-processing method of ultra-high pure aluminum/aluminum alloy sample.
   ADVANTAGE - The method selects block sample, proper acid liquid and cleaning method; reduces the detection error caused by the sample oxidation or combustion and improves the accuracy of the detection result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for detecting oxygen content in ultrapure aluminum/aluminum alloy comprising (A) pre-processing the block-shaped ultra-pure aluminum/aluminum alloy sample, (B) taking the pretreated sample, placing it in a graphite crucible, placing it in the LECO ON836 gas analysis and detector, passing high-purity helium gas with a purity of 99.99% or more into the gas analysis and detector, heating, melting the sample in the graphite crucible cavity to produce carbon dioxide gas, detecting, generating by infrared detection pool, measuring the amount of carbon dioxide generated by the graphite crucible under the same heating conditions and (C) calculating the oxygen content in the sample where the oxygen content in the sample = (mass of oxygen in graphite crucible and sample-mass of oxygen in graphite crucible)/(mass of sample).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (c) the acetone cleaning times is 2-4 times, preferably 10-30 seconds. The method comprises (1) placing the block-shaped ultra-pure aluminum/aluminum alloy sample in the 30-50 wt.%. hydrofluoric acid at 18-30 degrees C, ultrasonically cleaning for10-30 seconds at 100-200W ultrasonic power, (2) washing the acid-washed sample 305 times with water for 30-50 seconds for removing the residual acid liquid, (3) cleaning the washed sample by acetone (2-4 times) for 10-30 seconds each time, removing the residual water and (4) roasting the sample cleaned with acetone under a heating lamp, baking at 30-50 degrees C for 60-120 seconds to remove residual acetone and completing the pretreatment.

PT P
PN KR2020063068-A; KR2246992-B1
TI Manufacture of electrode laminate for membrane electrode assembly, involves forming insulating layer on base material, electroplating conductive material on base material, and forming water-repellent layer on base material/catalyst layer.
AU LIM J
   HEO K
   IM J
   KIM H S
   JEONG C H
   WON K T
   PARK J Y
   KIM H H
   KIM M Y
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 202050248R
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of electrode laminate involves (i) forming an insulating layer on one surface of a porous electrode base material to obtain an insulating layer/porous electrode base material, (ii) preparing an insulating layer/porous electrode base material/catalyst layer by forming a catalyst layer comprising a conductive material by electroplating a conductive material on the other surface of the porous electrode base material of the insulating layer/porous electrode base material, (iii) preparing a porous electrode base/catalyst layer by removing the insulating layer from the insulating layer/porous electrode base/catalyst layer, and (iv) forming a water-repellent layer on one surface of the porous electrode base material/catalyst layer and obtaining electrode laminate comprising a water-repellent layer/porous electrode base material/catalyst layer.
   USE - Manufacture of electrode laminate used for membrane electrode assembly for electronic component e.g. hydrolysis apparatus, fuel cell, photoelectric conversion element or carbon dioxide reduction device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable efficiently saving time by directly coating conductive electrode material on the porous electrode base material by electroplating without separately casting the electrode, and efficiently contacting silver nanoparticles catalyst plated on the porous carbon film with carbon dioxide, thereby increasing the reactivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrode laminate, which comprises water- repellent layer, porous electrode base material positioned on the water-repellent layer, and catalyst layer positioned on the porous electrode base material; and
   (2) membrane electrode assembly, which comprises an anode comprising the electrode laminate, a cathode, and an electrolyte membrane between the anode and the cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The porous electrode base material comprises a porous carbon film. The conductive material is at least one chosen from silver, platinum, tin, copper, gold, platinum, palladium, nickel, indium, aluminum, iron, rhodium, ruthenium, osmium, cobalt, molybdenum, zinc, vanadium, tungsten, titanium, manganese and chromium or its alloys.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (i) involves preparing a coating solution by dissolving silica precursor in a solvent, and coating the coating solution on one surface of the porous electrode base material by spray coating and condensing the reaction to form an insulating layer comprising silica to obtain an insulating layer/porous electrode base material. The step (ii) is impregnating insulating layer/porous electrode base material with a plating solution, and applying a current to the plating solution impregnated with the insulating layer/porous electrode substrate to electroplate a conductive substance on the other surface of the porous electrode substrate to form catalyst layer comprising a conductive substance. The step (iii) removing the insulating layer by contacting the insulating layer/porous electrode base material/ catalyst layer with a basic solution. The step (iv) involves dissolving a water-repellent layer precursor in a solvent and preparing a water-repellent layer precursor solution, and preparing an electrode comprising a water-repellent layer/porous electrode base material/catalyst layer by spray coating and drying water repellent layer precursor solution on one surface of the porous electrode base material. Preferred Arrangement: The electrode laminate further comprises an insulating layer between the water-repellent layer and the porous electrode base material. Preferred Property: The catalyst layer has a thickness of 5-100 mu m. Preferred Composition: The silica precursor is chosen from organosilicon-based compound of formula (I) and (II), preferably chosen from tetraethoxysilane, triethoxymethylsilane and 3-glycidyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane. The water-repellent layer precursor comprises an organofluorine-based compound of formula (III), preferably 1H,1H,2H,2H-perfluorooctyltrichlorosilane.R1-R4 = 1-6C alkyl;Y1 = O;Y2-Y4 = bond or O;R5-R7 = 1-3C alkyl;R8 = 1-5C alkylene;X1-X3 = F, Cl, Br or I;m = 1-5;andn = 2-10.

AB    NOVELTY - Smart home device management auxiliary system comprises a smart home management module (100) composed of multiple smart control modules. An indoor air quality monitoring module (200) is connected to the smart home management module. The indoor air quality monitoring module includes an oxygen content monitoring unit, a carbon dioxide content monitoring unit, a carbon monoxide content monitoring unit, and a formaldehyde content monitoring unit. A control module (300) is connected to the indoor air quality monitoring module. The control module is connected with a ventilation device (400) and an air purifier (500).
   USE - Used as smart home device management auxiliary system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: realizes real-time monitoring of the content of formaldehyde, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in the room; purifies the indoor air once the content of oxygen is low and the content of other gases is relatively high; automatically opens the window for ventilation; ensures that there is enough fresh air in the room and people's health; saves the trouble of opening and closing window and air purifiers; improves the current smart home equipment management system; establishes and improves the smart home management system; realizes the automatic closing of the window through the control module; eliminates the trouble of manual switching; and is simple, economical, easy to repair and maintain, and more suitable for popularization and application.

PN EP3628722-A1; WO2020064238-A1
TI Crack-gas-generator (CGG) for cracking hydrocarbon, has first heat exchanger (HE1) designed to directly or indirectly transfer heat energy leaving cracker (CRC) to working fluid (WRF) that is carbon dioxide.
AU AHMAD S
   MOENK T
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 202024724C
AB    NOVELTY - The crack-gas-generator (CGG) has one cracker (CRC), one first crack-gas-compressor (CG1) downstream of CRC for compressing a gas flow (GFH) containing hydrocarbons such as ethane, ethene and propene from a cracking process of CRC. The CGG includes one closed loop Brayton-cycle (BCY) having a working fluid (WRF) cyclically continuously absorbing and releasing energy by energy exchange. The BCY includes a first heat exchanger (HE1), and one expander (EXP) converting energy of the WRF into mechanical energy (TWO) that is partly transmitted to first CG1. The first heat exchanger is designed to directly or indirectly transfer heat energy leaving CRC to WRF that is carbon dioxide.
   USE - Crack-gas-generator (CGG) for cracking hydrocarbon such as ethane, ethene and propene.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat energy is recovered from the crack gas leaving the furnace to reduce the overall energy consumption respectively and to reduce the amount of crack gas generation with or without dilution of the crack gas with steam. The addition of steam to the feed enhances the ethylene yield and reduces the coking tendency in the coils of the furnace. The CGG enhances the energy recovery in the process of improving the overall efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of crack-gas-generation with a CGG.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a CGG for cracking hydrocarbon.
   Closed loop Brayton-cycle (BCY)
   Crack-gas-generator (CGG)
   First crack-gas-compressor (CGI)
   Cracker (CRC)
   Expander (EXP)
   Gas flow (GFH)
   First heat exchanger (HE1)
   Mechanical energy (TWO)
   Working fluid (WRF)

PN US2020071601-A1; WO2020046939-A1; CA3111214-A1; AU2019333270-A1; CN112839994-A; EP3844217-A1; US11214729-B2; EP3844217-A4; RU2021105475-A
TI Swellable composition for controlling fluid flow            comprises crosslinker and polymer matrix comprising            monomer having re-crosslinking moiety, monomer having            acid resistance moiety, and monomer having carbon            dioxide-phillic moiety.
AU LONG Y
   BAI B
   SCHUMAN T P
   SCHUHMANN T
   LONG I
   SHUMEN T P
AE UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
GA 2020189605
AB    NOVELTY - A swellable composition for controlling fluid                flow comprises polymer matrix, 1st crosslinker, and                2nd crosslinker, where the polymer matrix comprises                a 1st monomer having a re-crosslinking moiety, a                2nd monomer having an acid resistance moiety, and a                3rd monomer having a carbon dioxide (CO2)-phillic                moiety.
   USE - A swellable composition for controlling fluid                flow (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The particle gels can be deployed to improve                the conformance of CO2 flooding, CO2 huff-puff, or                Water-Alternative-Gas (WAG). The composition blocks                CO2 leakage and similar CO2 processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) forming the swellable composition which                involves (a) polymerizing 1st monomer, 2nd monomer,                and 3rd monomer in the presence of 1st crosslinker                and 2nd crosslinker to form polymer matrix; (b)                drying the polymer matrix to form a dried polymer                matrix; and (c) grinding the dried polymer matrix                to form the swellable composition; and
   (2) altering or controlling a fluid present in                a subterranean environment which involves (a)                introducing a swellable composition into                subterranean environment so that swellable                composition contacts the fluid, where the swellable                composition comprises a polymer matrix comprising                crosslinkable polymer chains, 1st crosslinker, and                2nd crosslinker, where polymer matrix comprises 1st                monomer having a re-crosslinking moiety, 2nd                monomer having an acid resistance moiety, and 3rd                monomer having a CO2-phillic moiety, where greater                than or equal to 1 portion of 2nd crosslinker is                interspersed among the crosslinkable polymer                chains; and (b) allowing the 2nd crosslinker to                associate with crosslinkable polymer chains upon                exposure to fluid to form a swollen composition;                and (c) partially controlling the flow of fluid                within subterranean environment with swollen                composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition is in                  the form of a particulate gel. The 1st monomer                  comprises a water-soluble carboxylic acid and 2nd                  monomer comprises a water-soluble cationic                  monomer. The 1st monomer comprises                  N-isopropylacrylamide, N,N-diethylacrylamide,                  dimethylaminopropyl acrylamide,                  dimethylaminopropyl methacrylamide, acryloyl                  morpholine, hydroxyethyl acrylate, hydroxypropyl                  acrylate, hydroxyethyl methacrylate,                  hydroxypropyl methacrylate,                  dimethylaminoethylacrylate, dimethylaminoethyl                  methacrylate, maleic anhydride, N-vinyl                  pyrrolidone, vinyl acetate, and/or N-vinyl                  formamide; the 2nd monomer comprises sodium                  vinylsulfonate, potassium vinylsulfonate, sodium                  phenyl vinyl sulfate, potassium phenyl vinyl                  sulfate, sodium vinyl sulfate,                  2-acrylamido-2-methyl-1-propanesulfonic acid                  sodium salt, and/or potassium vinyl sulfate; and                  the 3rd monomer comprises vinyl benzoate, benzyl                  vinyl formate, vinyl acetate, ethyl vinyl ether,                  methyl vinyl ether, vinylidene fluoride, lactic                  acid, lactic acid cyclic dimer, glycolic acid,                  glycolide, hexamethylcyclotrisiloxane, and/or                  1H,1H,2H,2H-perfluorooctyl methacrylate. First                  monomer comprises acrylamide or a derivative,                  where second monomer comprises                  2-acrylamido-2-methyl-1-propanesulfonic acid                  sodium salt, and third monomer comprises vinyl                  acetate. First crosslinker comprises methylene                  bisacrylamide, diallylamine, triallylamine,                  divinyl sulfone, and/or diethyleneglycol diallyl                  ether. Second crosslinker comprises                  chromium(III)-acetate, chromium(III)-propionate,                  zirconium(IV)-acetate, and/or                  zirconium(IV)-lactate. The swellable composition                  further comprises greater than or equal to 1                  additives comprising silica particles, fibers,                  and/or oxygen scavenger. Polymer matrix comprises                  greater than or equal to 40 mol.% 1st monomer,                  0.5-50 mol.% 2nd monomer, and 1-50 mol.% 3rd                  monomer. The swellable composition comprises                  greater than or equal to 80 wt.% polymer matrix.                  Fluid comprises water or brine solvent. Swellable                  composition is in the form particles having an                  initial average particle size prior to                  introducing of step (a) and swollen composition                  is in the form of particles having a 2nd average                  particle size, and the 2nd average particle size                  is greater than or equal to 5 times greater than                  that of initial average particle size. First                  monomer comprises an acrylamide or derivative,                  second monomer comprises                  2-acrylamido-2-methyl-1-propanesulfonic acid                  sodium salt, and third monomer comprises vinyl                  acetate. Preferred Process: Polymerizing occurs                  at 20-200 degrees C. Polymerizing includes                  ultrasonication.
   EXAMPLE - Swellable composition was prepared by                combining 2-crylamido-2-methyl-1-propanesulfonic                acid sodium salt (AMPS-Na) (4.27 g), acrylamide                (22.5 g), zirconium acetate solution (5.86 mL),                vinyl acetate (5.38 mL),                N,N'-methylenebis(acrylamide) (1.75 mg), silica                nanoparticles (20 mg), ammonium persulfate (80 mg),                and distilled water (100 mL) under vigorous                stirring and subjected to the polymerization                conditions. The resulting bulk gel was dried and                ground.

   MAO Y
AE SHANGHAI PULMONARY HOSPITAL (SHFK-C)
GA 202017852P
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a detachable double-auxiliary air bag for relieving hiccup, comprising a bag body, the bag body is a double-layer structure of the bag body, the bag body comprises an inner layer and an outer layer, the inner layer and the outer layer are connected through the inflatable air bag, the bag body is provided with an opening; an opening is located in the centre of the top part of the bag body; the opening is connected with a connecting piece, one end of the connecting contact at the mouth and nose of the person, and the connecting piece with the contact face of the human mouth and nose which is fish head-shaped; the bag body is fixed with connecting piece by a connecting pipe, one end of the connection pipe is embedded at the opening; the other end of the connecting tube is clamped with the connecting piece, the outer wall of the connecting pipe is provided with an annular bump, convex direction of the annular bulge towards outside of the connecting pipe. The utility model uses the bag body covers the mouth and nose of the user exhaled carbon dioxide repeating for inhalation, increasing the concentration of carbon dioxide in the blood, stimulating the carotid body and main artery body chemical sensor, thereby interfering with neural reflex activity of hiccup, so as to adjusting, inhibiting the hiccup.

PN CN110819528-A; CN212199281-U
TI Device for generating methane in biological biogas fermentation tank, in which cathode of direct current (DC) power supply is connected to stainless steel mesh surrounding inner wall of fermentation tank.
AU YU Z
   YAN X
   ZHANG H
   WANG B
   CHANG D
   QIAN Z
AE UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 2020181191
AB    NOVELTY - The device has fermentation tanks (9) that are provided for fermenting organic waste. A feeding device (3), a gas storage device (4) and a discharging device are connected to the top, side walls and bottom of the fermentation tank. A stirrer for stirring organic waste is arranged in the inner side of the fermentation tank. The agitator comprises a motor (2) placed directly above the fermentation tank, a stirring rod and a stirring impeller (7) which are electrically connected to the motor and placed inside the fermentation tank. The stirring rod, the stirring impeller and the fermentation tank form a voltage generating circuit through a DC power supply (1). The anode of the DC power supply is connected to the stirring rod and the stirring impeller. The cathode of the DC power supply is connected to a stainless steel mesh surrounding the inner wall of the fermentation tank such that the carbon dioxide produced by fermentation of organic waste is electrolyzed into methane.
   USE - Device for generating methane in biological biogas fermentation tank.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is used to electrolyze carbon dioxide to methane such that the purity of methane gas in the biogas is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for generating methane in a biogas fermentation tank methane generating device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the Device for generating methane in biological biogas fermentation tank.
   DC power supply (1)
   Motor (2)
   Feeding device (3)
   Gas storage device (4)
   Stirring impeller (7)
   Fermentation tank (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The production of methane involves adding various enzymes, hydrolytic fermentation bacteria, hydrogen-producing acetic acid bacteria and methanogenic bacteria into a fermentation tank containing organic waste for anaerobic fermentation, hydrolyzing organic waste into monosaccharides, polysaccharides, and amino acids by various enzymes and hydrolytic fermentation bacteria, converting monosaccharides, polysaccharides and amino acids into low-molecular volatile acids, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide using hydrogen-producing acetic acid bacteria, converting low-molecular volatile acids, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide into methane and carbon dioxide using methanogenic bacteria, using carbon dioxide produced by anaerobic fermentation to synthesize carbon dioxide to generate methane when direct current power supply is in a closed circuit state.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI Biomass fermentation apparatus for fermentation of organic substance waste material e.g. coppiced timber, has ferment units that are provided with flexible storing bag and bleeding pipe, and gathered by connecting bleeding pipes vertically.
AU FUJII A
   UESAKA H
AE HOKUSAN KK (HKUS-C)
GA 202006608V
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a ferment unit (20) which includes a flexible storing bag (12) which accommodates a biomass fermentation material (2), and a bleeding pipe (14) penetrating the storing bag. Multiple ferment units are gathered by connecting bleeding pipes vertically. The bleeding pipe is currently formed so that a fitting of the lower-end opening portion of the bleeding pipe of the upward ferment unit and the upper-end opening portion of the bleeding pipe of the downward ferment unit is possible.
   USE - Biomass fermentation apparatus for fermentation of organic substance waste materials such as coppiced timber and emitting fermentation heat and carbon dioxide for cultivation of plant, used in greenhouse cultivation facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide levels are raised and the growth of the plant is promoted. The fermentation state is kept favorable over the long period of time, and the maintenance of the apparatus is also made easy. The air-intake amount from the air-intake portion is increased, and stacking of sections is reduced so that fermentation is accelerated. The fresh air is effectively supplied in the ferment unit by the convection by fermentation heat. The installation work and recombination in the house are easily performed. The working efficiency of the biomass fermentation apparatus is high.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic longitudinal cross-sectional view of the biomass fermentation apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Biomass fermentation material (2)
   Biomass fermentation apparatus (10)
   Flexible storing bag (12)
   Bleeding pipe (14)
   Ferment unit (20)

AB    NOVELTY - Genetically altered plant (P1) or its part                comprising at least one genetic alteration that                increases or provides the ability for bicarbonate                to cross a membrane from a plant cell cytoplasm                into a stroma of at least a portion of the                chloroplasts of the plant or comprising at least                one genetic alteration that increases or provides                the ability for bicarbonate to cross a membrane                from a stroma into a lumen of at least a portion of                the chloroplasts of the plant, is new.
   USE - The plant is useful for producing progeny                plant that exhibits increased carbon use                efficiency, where the plant is cowpea, soybean,                cassava, rice, soy, wheat or other C3 crop plants,                or the plant is not maize, sorghum or other C4 crop                plants (all claimed). No biological data                given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                plant (P2) or its part with increased carbon use                efficiency comprising at least one modified nucleic                acid sequence comprising at least one coding                sequence of the green algal bestrophin polypeptide                in the plant, where the bestrophin polypeptide is                expressed in the plant and when the plant is                cultivated under ambient carbon dioxide conditions,                the yield, growth rate, or biomass is greater than                from a corresponding wild-type (WT) plant or its                corresponding WT part that does not overexpress the                bestrophin polypeptide or the yield, growth rate or                biomass is substantially similar to the yield,                growth rate or biomass from the corresponding WT                plant or its WT part thereof that does not                overexpress the bestrophin polypeptide cultivated                under ambient carbon dioxide conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Components: The plants (P1) and (P2)                  further comprise modulated expression of                  endogenous carbonic anhydrases. The gain of the                  bicarbonate membrane crossing ability comprises                  expression of at least one green algal bestrophin                  polypeptide. In the plants (P1) and (P2): the                  green algal bestrophin polypeptide is a first                  polypeptide with at least 70%, preferably at                  least 95% sequence identity to the amino acid                  sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1 (comprising a fully                  defined sequence of 438 amino acids as given in                  the specification), a second polypeptide with at                  least 70%, preferably at least 95% sequence                  identity to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:                  2 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 450                  amino acids as given in the specification),                  and/or a third polypeptide with at least 70%,                  preferably at least 95% sequence identity to the                  amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 3 (comprising a                  fully defined sequence of 466 amino acids as                  given in the specification); the green algal                  bestrophin polypeptide is SEQ ID NOs: 4-111                  (sequences not defined here may be found at                  ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication),                  preferably SEQ ID NO: 10, SEQ ID NO: 11, SEQ ID                  NO: 15, SEQ ID NO: 27, SEQ ID NO: 28, SEQ ID NO:                  29, SEQ ID NO: 42, SEQ ID NO: 62 or SEQ ID NO:                  63; and the modulated expression is increased                  expression, reduced expression and/or expression                  at a different location. In the plant (P1):                  tgreen algal bestrophin polypeptide is a first                  polypeptide with at least 95% identity to SEQ ID                  NO: 1, a second polypeptide with at least 95%                  sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 2 and/or a third                  polypeptide with at least 95% sequence identity                  to SEQ ID NO: 3; and the green algal bestrophin                  polypeptide is localized to a chloroplast                  envelope or a thylakoid membrane of at least one                  chloroplast within a plant cell, where the plant                  cell is a leaf mesophyll cell, and the green                  algal bestrophin polypeptide is expressed in at                  least 70% of leaf mesophyll cells of the plant.                  In the plant (P2): the bestrophin polypeptide is                  localized to a chloroplast envelope or a                  chloroplast thylakoid membrane of at least one                  chloroplast of the plant cell; the modified                  nucleic acid sequence is stably integrated into                  the nuclear genome of the plant; and the modified                  nucleic acid sequence further comprises a second                  nucleic acid sequence encoding a signal peptide                  sequence or targeting sequence operably linked to                  the coding sequence of a green algal bestrophin                  polypeptide, where expression of the signal                  peptide sequence or targeting sequence results in                  localization of the bestrophin polypeptide to a                  chloroplast envelope or a chloroplast thylakoid                  membrane of at least one chloroplast of a plant                  cell.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Gene therapy.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110684881-A; CN110684881-B
TI Performing decarburization by multi-gas injection through an oxygen lance comprises using an argon blowing pipe and an oxygen gun for supplying a carbon dioxide-argon mixed gas are provided on the RH vacuum processing equipment.
AU LI L
   WANG J
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 202007830A
AB    NOVELTY - Performing decarburization by multi-gas injection through an oxygen lance comprises using an argon blowing pipe and an oxygen gun for supplying a CO2-Ar mixed gas are provided on the RH vacuum processing equipment. Insert the RH vacuum processing equipment into the ladle containing molten steel, start the vacuum pump to make the vacuum of the RH vacuum processing equipment less than first pressure, and start blowing the CO2-Ar mixture to circulate the molten steel. Blow the CO2-Ar mixed gas, use an oxygen gun to blow the first gas into the molten steel. When the vacuum pump makes the vacuum of the RH vacuum processing equipment less than the second pressure, the supply of the carbon dioxide-argon mixed gas is stopped and the argon blowing pipe starts to supply the second gas to circulate the molten steel. When the vacuum pump makes the vacuum of the RH vacuum processing equipment less than the third pressure, the first gas and the second gas are continuously supplied for 5-8 minutes.
   USE - Method for performing decarburization by multi-gas injection through an oxygen lance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform decarburization by multi-gas injection through an oxygen lance, which achieve an end carbon content of less than 10 ppm when using an oxygen gun to inject gas, and shorten the smelting time by 3-4 minutes.

AU CLARK E L
   BELL A T
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 202003654A
AB    NOVELTY - The DEMS cell has a casing (12) defining an anolyte chamber, a catholyte chamber and a collection chamber. An ion-conducting membrane separates the anolyte chamber and the catholyte chamber. A counter electrode is mounted in the anolyte chamber. A working electrode is mountd in the catholyte chamber and being a washer-shaped electrode and channel defined in a center of the working electrode. The channel is functioned as an inlet for a catholyte to the catholyte chamber. A first surface of a pervaporation membrane defines a portion of the collection chamber. The collection chamber is provided in fluid communication with the catholyte chamber.

Uses include but are not limited to electrochemical processes such as electrochemical synthesis of organic molecules and the electrochemical reforming of organic solvent/gas e.g. methanol to synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The efficacy of DEMS is heavily reliant on the design of the electrochemical cell, thus, achieving the rapid response time and the high product collection efficiency. The working and counter electrodes are parallel to ensure the uniform potential distribution across the surface of the electrodes and are separated by the ion-conducting membrane to prevent the unwanted parasitic reactions such as the oxidation of CO2R products or the reduction of oxygen.

AB    NOVELTY - Bismuth-based composition catalyst comprises                metal M1 and metal M2 oxide, where M1, M2 each                independently is bismuth, zinc, tin, silver,                indium, lead, or copper, and M2 comprises metal                bismuth.
   USE - The bismuth-based composition catalyst is                useful for the reduction of carbon dioxide to                formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves the acid resistance and                high concentration formic acid inhibition effect                while maintaining high formic acid selectivity,                improves chemical stability; has simple preparation                process, utilizes raw materials and sources, widens                the types of catalysts, and has good properties in                CO2 electroreduction tomic acid process and other                process fields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) electrode for electroreduction of CO2 to                produce formic acid, comprising an ion exchange                membrane and a cathode catalyst coating on one                surface, where the cathode catalyst coating is                provided by bismuth-based composition catalyst;                and
   (2) preparing bismuth-based composition                catalyst, comprising (i) preparing primary product                of bismuth-based composition catalyst, where in the                primary product of M1 and M2 each independently is                the state of elementary substance, oxide or                hydroxide, and (ii) placing the obtained primary                product in a reducing atmosphere, an oxidizing                atmosphere or an inert atmosphere and sintering;                and
   (3) producing a formic acid by CO2                electroreduction, comprising using ruthenium-based                composition catalyst, or using the electrode and                performing CO2 electroreduction to formic acid in                neutral anode two-chamber mode, the electrolytic                cell has an electrolysis voltage of 2.4-4 V and the                electrolysis temperature is 10-80 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal M2 oxide in                  a molar content of 30-100%.The molar ratio of                  metal M1 simple and M2 metal oxide is 1:10-10:1.                  The bismuth-based composition catalyst further                  comprises carbon material carrier. The carbon                  material carrier is graphite, carbon nano tube,                  carbon black or carbon fiber. The cathode                  catalyst coating layer is formed by adhering a                  surface of the ion exchange membrane with a                  hydrophobic binder and/or a conductive adhesive.                  The reducing atmosphere is a hydrogen atmosphere                  or inert atmosphere containing hydrogen, the                  oxidizing atmosphere is atmosphere of at least                  one of inert atmosphere is oxygen, air or carbon                  dioxide. Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  single or multi stage sintering at atmospheric                  pressure, and the stage sintering temperature is                  from low to high, the sintering temperature is                  200-600 degrees C, and the sintering time is                  10-300 minutes.

PN CN109535024-A; CN109535024-B
TI Yellowing-resistant beta-hydroxyalkyl amide curing agent useful for preparing powder coating, comprises reaction product of diethanolamine, dimethyl adipate and supported solid super-base catalyst of sodium-sodium hydroxide-aluminum oxide.
AU LIN G
   YANG Z
   WANG Z
   YE M
   TANG Z
   HE L
   WANG Y
   XIANG C
AE HUANGSHAN HUAHUI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201931039J
AB    NOVELTY - Yellowing-resistant beta -hydroxyalkyl amide curing agent comprises reaction product of diethanolamine, dimethyl adipate and supported solid super-base catalyst of sodium/sodium hydroxide/aluminum oxide obtained by amidation reaction.
   USE - The yellowing-resistant beta -hydroxyalkyl amide curing agent is useful for preparing powder coating (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The yellowing-resistant beta -hydroxyalkyl amide curing agent: has high purity and less alkalinity; and excellent yellowing resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing yellowing-resistant beta -hydroxyalkyl amide curing agent, comprising (i) uniformly mixing the selected molar amount of diethanolamine, dimethyl adipate and the supported solid superbase catalyst sodium/sodium hydroxide/aluminum oxide and heating to 80-130 degrees C for amidation reaction, (ii) maintaining the vacuum in the reactor to less than -0.095 MPa, lowering the amine value of the reactant to 1 mg potassium hydroxide/g, removing the system vacuum and removing the catalyst by high temperature pressure filtration, (iii) filtering, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the product and continuously reacting for 0.5-1 hour, where the quantity of carbon dioxide is 5-10 wt.% diethanolamine, (iv) reducing the reactant amine value to less than 0.5 mg potassium hydroxide/g, stopping the reaction, and adding the selected quantity of the yellowing resistant auxiliary agent and uniformly mixing, and (v) cooling and crushing the reaction product; and
   (2) yellowing-resistant powder coating, comprising yellowing-resistant beta -hydroxyalkyl amide curing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material of the curing agent further comprises yellowing resistance auxiliary agent added in 0.1-0.5 mol.% of the total molar amount of the diethanolamine and dimethyl adipate raw materials. Preferred Components: The dosage mol ratio of diethanolamine and adipic acid dimethyl ester is 30-65: 30-60. The load type solid super basic catalyst sodium/sodium hydroxide/aluminum oxide is added in an amount of 2-5 mol.% based on the total molar amount of the diethanolamine and dimethyl adipate raw materials. The mass ratio of metal sodium and nano- alpha - aluminum oxide is 1:5-10. The yellowing-resistant auxiliary agent is tri (2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphate. Preferred Method: The method for preparing load type solid super basic catalyst sodium/sodium hydroxide/aluminum oxide comprises performing high-temperature dehydration treatment at 350-400 degrees C using nano alpha - aluminum oxide as raw material under high-purity argon atmosphere, reducing the temperature to 200-250 degrees C, adding sodium metal, mixing uniformly and heating to 500-650 degrees C for high temperature calcining process.

AB    NOVELTY - The cyclone has a cyclone body (1) which is provided with an inlet chamber (2), an outlet chamber (3), and stationary helical guide vanes (22) for ensuring rotation of a gas-liquid mixture in the outlet chamber.    USE - Cyclone used in refrigerant accumulator (claimed) for separating gas-liquid mixture. Can be used in a vehicular air conditioning system, and in separating carbon dioxide gas and liquid carbon dioxide in refrigerant accumulator or accumulator with an internal heat exchanger.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a simple and cost-effective cyclone that can be manufactured at reduced cost by injection molding. Ensures that the cyclone is durable and has long life since the cyclone has no inlet pipe that can be easily destroyed. Includes a gas-liquid mixture inlet that does not reduce the efficiency of separating the gas-liquid mixtures introduced in the cyclone. Ensures that the cyclone has an axial design for easy use with a refrigerant accumulator and accumulator with internal heat exchanger.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) the use of the cyclone;

   (3) a refrigerant accumulator; and

Each channel has two identical branch channels distributed in a next stage. The first stage channels are divided into two channels with a predetermined angle and evenly distributed from a material inlet. A second-stage channel is divided into two channels with a predetermined angle and uniform symmetry. A third-stage channel is divided into two channels with a predetermined angle and a uniform symmetry. TI Cold fresh noodle is prepared using flour, Spirulina powder, water, salt, egg white, alkali, tackifier, emulsifier and flavoring agent.
AU XIAO J
   HE L
AE WUHU LVERYOU AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201899565K
AB    NOVELTY - Cold fresh noodle is prepared using 100 pts. wt. flour, 20-35 pts. wt. Spirulina powder, 25-30 pts. wt. water, 1-3 pts. wt. salt, 2-4.5 pts. wt. egg white, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. alkali, 0.15-0.25 pt. wt. tackifier, 0.05-0.1 pt. wt. emulsifier and 0.5-2 pts. wt. flavoring agent. The Spirulina powder is preparing by subjecting Spirulina to ultrafine pulverization, removing fishy odor and performing supercritical carbon dioxide molding.
   USE - As cold fresh noodles.
   ADVANTAGE - The noodle has a smooth taste and in line with the concept of modern living and health, enhanced elasticity and its preparation method improve the homogenization of gluten and retains the taste and nutrients of the noodles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing cold noodle, which involves (a) subjecting Spirulina to ultrafine pulverization for 5-7 minutes, removing fishy odor by ultrasonically mixing the powder with activated carbon for 30-45 minutes under high frequency and performing supercritical carbon dioxide molding to obtain Spirulina powder and (b) dissolving salt, egg white, alkali, tackifier, emulsifier and flavoring agent in water, stirring and mixing at 25-30 degrees C to obtain a dough, mixing the flour with Spirulina powder, mixing mixed powder and dough, kneading the dough into a smooth dough, refrigerating and proofing the dough, molding the dough, cut into strips, freezing the noodles at -10 degrees C to -15 degrees C for 20-30 minutes and storing at -3 degrees C to -0 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The cold fresh noodle is prepared using 100 pts. wt. flour, 28 pts. wt. Spirulina powder, 28 pts. wt. water, 2.5 pts. wt. salt, 3.5 pts. wt. egg white, 0.2 pt. wt. alkali, 0.18 pt. wt. tackifier, 0.08 pt. wt. emulsifier and 1.5 pts. wt. flavoring agent. Preferred Components: The flour has a protein content of 8-12%, wet gluten content of 30-32%, ash content of 0.45-0.6%, descending value of not less than 230 seconds and humidity of 14-15%. The water has a hardness of not greater than 9 and pH of 6-8.5. The tackifier comprises gluten powder and konjac flour in a mass ratio of 1:0.3-0.8. The emulsifier comprises at least two of sorbitan ester, phospholipid, monoglyceride or sucrose ester. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide molding by pre-reacting at 20-25 degrees C and 1-3 MPa and reacting at 35-45 degrees C and 9-15 MPa. The step (b) involves refrigerating and proofing at -4 degrees C to -0 degrees C for 20-30 minutes. The step (a) involves ultrasonically mixing at 150-300 W and 30-40 kHz.

AU WU Y
   LIAO S
   LI P
   WU D
   XU X
AE UNIV NAT CHIN YI TECHNOLOGY (UYYI-C)
GA 201900533H

PN CN108435184-A; CN108435184-B
TI Catalyst useful for preparing methanol comprises metal mixture and copper-zinc-aluminum mixture.
AU LIU Y
   DENG X
   HUANG W
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201868319C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 20-90 wt.% metal mixture and 10-80 wt.% copper-zinc-aluminum mixture in the mass ratio of 1:0.25-9. The metal element is iron, manganese, zinc, or nickel. The molar ratio of the copper-zinc-aluminum mixture is copper/zinc/aluminum in 2-6/2-5/0.5-2.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical; and has high catalytic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of catalyst for preparing methanol comprising applying catalyst to the preparation of methanol by thermochemical conversion of carbon dioxide and water in a fixed bed reactor; and
   (2) preparing catalyst comprising dispersing 19.8 g aluminum isopropoxide in 100 ml distilled water at 80 degrees C to hydrolyze for 1 hours, raising water bath temperature to 95 degrees C, continuously hydrolyzing 2 hours to form sol, and dissolving with an aqueous solution of 58.6 g copper nitrate and 36.1 g zinc nitrate into sol, heating and stirring to form gel, drying gel at 100-450 degrees C and roasting, carrying out reduction treatment in the hydrogen atmosphere at 450 degrees C for 5 hours obtain the copper/zinc/aluminum catalyst, the upper and lower sections of metal iron powder and copper/zinc/aluminum catalyst in the mass ratio of 1:2, placing into the reactor for filling, evaluating under the reaction conditions of airspeed of airspeed is 300 hours, water is 0.01 ml/minutes, 300 degrees C reaction activity evaluation under the condition of 2 MPa, carbon dioxide conversion rate is 3.5 C-mol %, and methanol selectivity of 92.4 C-mol %.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing methanol comprises filling the catalyst in a stepwise manner, the metal element is located on the copper/zinc/aluminum mixture, and reducing the catalyst is in a hydrogen atmosphere at 300-500 degrees C in a tube furnace, subjecting reactor to a thermochemical conversion reaction with a reaction space velocity of 150-1200/hours, a water content of 0.01-0.1 ml/minutes, and reacting at 300-500 degrees C under pressure of 0.5-5 MPa. The copper/zinc/aluminum mixture is prepared by (a) hydrolyzing aluminum isopropoxide at 80 degrees C for 1 hours, increasing the temperature of the water bath to 95 degrees C to continue hydrolysis, and slowly adding mixed solution of copper and zinc until a stable sol is formed, and (b) drying sol at 100-120 degrees C and calcining at 300-500 degrees C, or (a) adding the mixed solution with sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate solution of copper, zinc, aluminum, parallel flow co-precipitating under water bath at 70 degrees C, and (b) drying precipitate at 100-120 degrees C and, roasting at 300-500 degrees C. The method of preparing the catalyst comprises preparing the copper/zinc/aluminum catalyst as the above mentioned method, and placing the upper and lower sections of metal iron powder and copper/zinc/aluminum catalyst in the mass ratio of 1:5 into a reactor for filling, evaluating under the reaction conditions of airspeed of airspeed is 300 hours, water is 0.02 ml/minutes, 500 degrees C reaction activity evaluation under the condition of 5 MPa, carbon dioxide conversion rate is 12.4 C-mol %, and methanol selectivity of 73.5 C-mol %.

TI Mosquito repellent device, has sodium bicarbonate powder that is located between first and second covers and is mixed with exothermic powder, and is decomposed by heat generated from exothermic powder to generate carbon dioxide and moisture.
AU MOON J H
AE HWANG M J (HWAN-Individual)
GA 201849883F
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) comprises a substrate layer (112) which is in the form of a sheet. A capture material layer (114) comprises a mosquito active material (122) comprising a cyclopentanone, a cyclohexanone or both, and an adhesive material, and is applied on one side of substrate layer. A first cover (110) is provided with several micropores passing through substrate layer and capture material layer. A second cover (150) is opposed to the other surface of base layer and whose two ends are coupled with two ends of base layer of first cover. An exothermic powder (132) is positioned between first and second covers, and contains iron powder, and is reacted with external air supplied through several micropores (140) to generate heat. A sodium bicarbonate powder (134) is located between first and second covers and is mixed with exothermic powder, and is decomposed by heat generated from exothermic powder to generate carbon dioxide and moisture.
   USE - Mosquito repellent device.
   ADVANTAGE - The mosquito repellent device improves the mosquito removal performance by forming an environment similar to the human skin, improves the mosquito removal performance by spreading the mosquito induced substance or the mosquito active substance, improves the stability against human body, reduces the risk of explosion or fire, improves the usability of the user, and reduces the manufacturing cost.

   First and second covers (110,150)
   Substrate layer (112)
   Capture material layer (114)
   Mosquito active material (122)
   Exothermic powder (132)
   Sodium bicarbonate powder (134)
   Micropore (140)

   USE - A low volatile organic compound (VOC) foam sandwich light high-strength halogen-free flame retardant composite material for vehicle floor, side top plate and modular building decoration.
   ADVANTAGE - Composite material has high strength, long service life, low VOC, can be recycled, lightweight, and is easy to construct.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing low volatile organic compound (VOC) foam sandwich light high-strength halogen-free flame retardant composite material which involves obtaining halogen-free flame-retardant continuous fiber reinforced polypropylene tape by rolling heat composite halogen-free fire-retardant continuous fiber reinforced polypropylene composite plate layer (1) with thickness of 1.4-1.75 mm; injecting carbon dioxide foaming agent to obtain halogen-free flame retardant polypropylene foam layer (2) where carbon dioxide amount is 0.5-5 mass% and thickness is 7-10 mm; and sandwiching halogen-free flame retardant polypropylene foam layer as core layer by hot press molding with total thickness of 10-12 mm.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the composite material.
   Halogen-free flame-resistant continuous fiber reinforced polypropylene composite plate layer (1)
   Halogen-free flame retardant polypropylene foam layer (2)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Parameters: Composite material has density of 780-860 kg/m3, bending strength of greater than or equal to 60 MPa, bending strength transverse of greater than or equal to 40 MPa, longitudinal modulus elasticity of 6000 MPa, impact toughness of greater than or equal to 45 KJ/m2, flame loss on ignition (LOI) of less than or equal to 30%, total VOC (TVOC) of less than or equal to 50 mu gc/g and odor level of less than or equal to 3. Halogen-free flame retardant polypropylene foam layer is made of 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin B, 17-22 pts. wt. halogen-free flame retardant, and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. auxiliary agent. Polypropylene resin (A) is chosen from greater than or equal to 1 polypropylene copolymer with melt index of 40-150 g/10 minutes, and polypropylene resin B is chosen from homopolymer polypropylene with melt index of less than or equal to 2 g/10 minutes. Continuous fiber is chosen from glass fiber, basalt fiber and carbon fiber.

PN CN108017491-A; CN108017491-B
TI Light hydrocarbons aromatization involves separating light hydrocarbon feedstock into absorption exhaust gas and absorption liquid in absorption tower.
AU JIN X
   HU S
   HE L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 201838525Q
AB    NOVELTY - Light hydrocarbons aromatization involves separating light hydrocarbon feedstock (100) into absorption exhaust gas (101) and absorption liquid (102) in an absorption tower. The absorption liquid is separated into carbon secondary stream (103) and a carbon tertiary and higher stream in a deethanizer tower. The carbon dioxide stream is separated into ethylene and ethane by an ethylene distillation column, where ethylene is decarboxylated to a light hydrocarbon aromatization reactor (V).
   USE - Method for aromatizing light hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method aromatizes the light hydrocarbons in a simple and cost-effective manner with rapid coking activation and high aromatic yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Light hydrocarbons aromatization involves separating light hydrocarbon feedstock (100) into absorption exhaust gas (101) and absorption liquid (102) in an absorption tower. The absorption liquid is separated into carbon secondary stream (103) and a carbon tertiary and higher stream in a deethanizer tower. The carbon dioxide stream is separated into ethylene and ethane by an ethylene distillation column, where ethylene is decarboxylated to a light hydrocarbon aromatization reactor (V). The carbon three and above portion stream is returned to the absorption tower. The depentane tower reactor is processed by dearomatization, where the overhead stream goes to the light hydrocarbon aromatization reactor. The light hydrocarbon aromatization reaction product is cooled and separated into a light hydrocarbon and a liquid phase in a gas-liquid separator. The liquid phase returns to the depentanizer and the light hydrocarbons are recycled back to the absorber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of the method for aromatizing light hydrocarbons. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Light hydrocarbon feedstock (100)
   Absorption exhaust gas (101)
   Absorption liquid (102)
   Carbon secondary stream (103)
   Light hydrocarbon aromatization reactor (V)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The light hydrocarbon feedstock comprises hydrogen, methane, ethylene, ethane, propylene, propane, carbon tetra, carbon penta, benzene, toluene and xylene groups. The light hydrocarbon feedstock is a light hydrocarbon reaction product of an aromatic hydrocarbon produced by methanol. Preferred Conditions: The light hydrocarbon aromatization reaction is accomplished at 500-600 degrees C. The recycled light hydrocarbons are pressurized by a compressor, cooled at 10 degrees C and subjected to a gas-liquid separation operation, a gas phase absorption column, and a liquid phase. The light hydrocarbon raw material is sent to an absorption tower after membrane separation or adsorption separation to remove hydrogen.

PN CN107934916-A; CN107934916-B
TI Preparing stable lead-free all-inorganic double            perovskite A2BB-X6 nanocrystal useful for            photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide into carbon            monoxide, comprises e. g. taking halide metal salt,            oleic acid, oleylamine and octadecene.
AU KUANG D
   ZHOU L
   CHEN B
   XU Y
   SU C
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2018327927
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stable lead-free all-inorganic                double perovskite A2BB-X6 nanocrystal comprises e.                g. taking halide metal salt BX, B-X3 or its                precursor, adding oleic acid, oleylamine and                octadecene as raw materials into three-necked                bottle, placing the three-necked flask in a                high-temperature apparatus, stirring, heating the                precursor solution under nitrogen protection,                injecting the hot oleate solution into the above                system and cooling to room temperature,                centrifuging the cooled reaction product,                discarding the supernatant and dispersing the                resulting precipitate by an organic solvent.
   USE - The nanocrystal is useful for photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide                and methane, optical detectors, lasers, solar                battery and other fields (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, convenient and                environment-friendly, and has good repeatability.                The product has uniform size, good dispersibility,                high stability, and good photocatalysis                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing stable lead-free all-inorganic                double perovskite A2BB-X6 nanocrystal comprises (i)                taking halide metal salt BX, B-X3 or its precursor,                adding oleic acid, oleylamine and octadecene as raw                materials into three-necked bottle, placing the                three-necked flask in a high-temperature apparatus                and stirring under vacuum; (ii) heating the                precursor solution under nitrogen protection,                injecting the hot oleate solution into the above                system and cooling to room temperature; and (iii)                centrifuging the cooled reaction product at low                speed, discarding the supernatant, dispersing the                resulting precipitate by an organic solvent,                centrifuging at high speed and discarding the                precipitate from the lower layer to obtain upper                nanocrystal dispersion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the A2BB-X6                  perovskite, A is caesium, rubidium, sodium, or                  potassium, B is monovalent cations of silver,                  gold, indium, copper, or thallium, B' is                  trivalent cations of bismuth, antimony, indium,                  or thallium, X is chloride, bromide, or iodide.                  In the step (i), the metal halide precursor is                  BX, Bapos, X3, or B, Bapos, nitrate and                  hydrohalic acid solutions, the input molar ratio                  of metal halide precursors BX, B'X3 is                  1:(0.5-1.5) and the input volume ratio of oleic                  acid, oleylamine, octadecene and other reaction                  solvent is 1:0.5-2:1-10. In the step (ii), the                  hot A oleate solution is an octadecene solution                  of A oleate, the amount charged is 1:5-1 of the                  amount of the BX material. In the step (iii), the                  organic solvent used for dispersing the                  nanocrystals may be ethyl acetate, low polar or                  non-polar organic solvents of chloroform,                  toluene, n-octane or n-hexane. The low polarity                  solvent is a mixture of anhydrous ethanol,                  isopropyl alcohol, ethyl acetate, and/or methyl                  acetate. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  heating is carried out at 140-230 degrees C, the                  reaction time after heat injection is controlled                  within about 60 seconds, cooling uses a rapid                  cooling process through an ice bath. In the step                  (iii), the low speed centrifugation is carried                  out at 7000 revolutions/minute, the high speed                  centrifugation is not greater than 10,000                  revolutions/minute, and the centrifugation time                  is 3 minutes. The resulting nanocrystalline                  surface ligands can be removed by low polarity                  solvent wash treatment. The resulting                  nanocrystals are Cs2AgBiBr6 nanocrystal                  dispersions, the absolute ethanol or ethyl                  acetate is added into the nanocrystalline                  dispersion, after shaking the mixed system by                  centrifugation, the lower layer of yellow                  precipitate is obtained, the washing process is                  repeated 2 times or more, the Cs2AgBiBr6                  nanocrystals are obtained after the surface                  ligands washed.

PN CN107927200-A
TI High oleic corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value comprises corn germ, sunflower seed, Camellia seed, olive, and Moringa seed.
AU LIN S
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201832111B
AB    NOVELTY - High oleic corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value comprises 30-45 wt.% corn germ, 30-45 wt.% sunflower seed, 10-20 wt.% Camellia seed, 10-20 wt.% olive, and 5-15 wt.% Moringa seed.
   USE - Used as high oleic corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases yield to more than 30%; reduces acid value less than 0.35 (potassium hydroxide (KOH)/(mg/g)); quickly realizes refining of oil. The oil has unsaturated fatty acid content is more than 92%, and oleic acid content is 55-65%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing high oleic corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value comprising providing raw materials, crushing, mixing uniformly, adding into multistage supercritical carbon dioxide extracting kettle, regulating extraction condition, maintaining extracting temperature at 50-60 degrees C, extracting pressure at 40-55 MPa, extracting for 1.0- 4.0 hours, conveying mixture of carbon dioxide and blend oil under supercritical state into the separation kettle, vacuum extracting, separating, adjusting separating kettle I temperature at 45-55 degrees C pressure at 10-20 MPa to obtain corn sunflower blend crude oil, placing crude oil into molecular distilling sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation vacuum degree at 2-9 Pa, distillation temperature at 100-150 degrees C, rotating speed at 100-300 revolutions/minute, feeding speed at 10-50 ml/minute and condensing temperature at 20-50 degrees C, refining, collecting to obtain high oleic acid corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The high oleic corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value comprises 30-35 wt.% corn germ, 30-35 wt.% sunflower seed, 15-20 wt.% Camellia seed, 10-15 wt.% olive, and 5-8 wt.% Moringa seed. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises providing 30-45 wt.% corn germ, 30-45 wt.% sunflower seed, 10-20 wt.% Camellia seed, 10-20 wt.% olive, and 5-15 wt.% Moringa seed, crushing, mixing uniformly, adding into multistage supercritical carbon dioxide extracting kettle, regulating extraction condition, maintaining extracting temperature at 50-60 degrees C, extracting pressure at 40-55 MPa, extracting for 1.0- 4.0 hours, conveying mixture of carbon dioxide and blend oil under supercritical state into the separation kettle, vacuum extracting, separating, adjusting separating kettle I temperature at 45-55 degrees C pressure at 10-20 MPa to obtain corn sunflower blend crude oil, placing crude oil into molecular distilling sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation vacuum degree at 2-9 Pa, distillation temperature at 100-150 degrees C, rotating speed at 100-300 revolutions/minute, feeding speed at 10-50 ml/minute and condensing temperature at 20-50 degrees C, refining, collecting to obtain high oleic acid corn sunflower blend oil with low acid value. The method further comprises a separating kettle II and separating kettle III, the temperature of separating kettle II is 30-40 degrees C, and pressure is 4-8 MPa. The temperature of separating kettle III is 30-40 degrees C and pressure is 4-8 MPa.
