PN CN107884487-A; CN107884487-B
TI Determining distribution of carbon flux in ethanol anaerobic pre-fermentation of kitchen wastecomprises e.g. adding 13C-glucose to food waste substrate, inoculating activated yeast and performing pre-fermentation.
AU GAO M
   ZOU H
   YU M
   REN Y
   WANG Q
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201828261U
AB    NOVELTY - Determining distribution of carbon flux in ethanol anaerobic pre-fermentation of kitchen waste comprises e.g. (i) adding 13C-glucose to food waste substrate, inoculating activated yeast and performing food kitchen waste ethanol pre-fermentation, (ii) inoculating activated sludge in sealed environment and performing anaerobic fermentation, (iii) collecting gas samples and liquid samples in fermentation system by controlling timing of three-way pipe, and (iv) determining VFAs with labeled 13C, ethanol, methane, and carbon dioxide by GC-MS analysis.
   USE - The method is useful for determining distribution of carbon flux in ethanol anaerobic pre-fermentation of kitchen waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: introduces labeling substrates into food waste; simplifies analysis process; and provides good tool for theoretical research of anaerobic fermentation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining distribution of carbon flux in ethanol anaerobic pre-fermentation of kitchen wastecomprises (i) adding 13C-glucose to food waste substrate, inoculating activated yeast at a ratio of 0.8-2.5% (wt./vol.), and performing food kitchen waste ethanol pre-fermentation for 8-24 hours, (ii) inoculating activated sludge in a sealed environment (VS sludge/VS substrate is equal to 1-2), performing anaerobic fermentation at 33-37 degrees C, (iii) collecting gas samples and liquid samples in fermentation system by controlling the timing of the three-way pipe, (iv) determining volatile fatty acids (VFAs) with labeled 13C, ethanol, methane, and carbon dioxide by GC-MS analysis, (v) determining glucose and lactate with labeled 13C by LC-MS analysis, and (vi) verifying appropriate product yields at different times by external standard methods and quantifying the carbon flow distribution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The added 13C-glucose content is not less than 30% of the reducing sugar concentration in the kitchen waste substrate. The gas sample is collecting with a headspace vial and diluting with 99.999% purity nitrogen during the test. The GC-MS is used to determine the labeled VFAs and ethanol, the chromatographic mass spectrometry conditions were as follows: column is capillary column with specification of 30 m x 0.32 mm x 0.25 mu m, injection volume is 0.56-1 mu l, heating procedure: initial temperature is 45-50 degrees C, holding time is 5-10 minute, heating to 240 degrees C with 10-15 degrees C/minute, holding for 10 minutes, carrier gas is argon (purity of greater than or equal to 99.999%), column flow rate is 1ml/minute, mass spectrometry conditions: EI ion source, electron energy is 70 eV, ion source temperature is 250 degrees C, solvent delay is 2.65 minute, scan mode is scan, scan quality is 29-650 mu . The chromatographic mass spectrometry conditions for the determination of trace methane and carbon dioxide by GC-MS are as follows: column chromatography is styrene-divinylbenzene column with specification of 30 m x 0.32 mmx 40 mu m, gas spectrum conditions: split ratio of 20-25:1, injection volume is 50-100 mu l, carrier gas purity is greater than or equal to 99.999% argon, column flow rate is 1ml/minute, column temperature maintaining at 35-40 degrees C, mass spectrometry conditions: EI ion source, electron energy is 70 eV, ion source temperature is 250 degrees C, solvent delay time is 1.6-1.8 minute (gas), scan mode is scan, scanning mass is 29-650 mu . The content of labeled glucose is determined by LC-MS, where the chromatographic mass spectrometry conditions were as follows: column is amino column with specification of 4.6x 150 mmx 3.5 mu m, liquid spectrum conditions: column temperature is 30-40 degrees C, mobile phase is 50-70% acetonitrile (vol./vol.), flow rate is 0.15-0.25 ml/minute, injection volume is 5-10 mu l, mass spectrometry conditions: drying gas temperature is 325 degrees C, drying gas flow rate is 10 l/minute (liquid nitrogen), electro-spray pressure is 30psi, dwell time is 200 msecond, scanning mode is SIM mode. The content of labeled lactic acid is determined by LC-MS. The chromatographic mass spectrometry conditions were as follows: column is C18 column, with specification of 2.1x 150 mm x 5 mu m, liquid spectrum conditions: column temperature is 25-35 degrees C, mobile phase is 40-60% methanol (vol./vol.), flow rate is 0.1-0.2 ml/minute, injection volume is 5-10 mu l, mass spectrometry conditions: drying gas temperature is 325 degrees C, drying gas flow rate is 10 l/minute (liquid nitrogen), electrospray pressure is 30 psi, dwell time is 200 msecond, scanning mode is SIM mode. The determination method of the reducing sugar in the kitchen waste is DNS method. The saccharification of the kitchen waste is determined before. The saccharification method comprises mixing food waste and water in ratio of 2:1 (vol./vol.), adding 2% (wt./vol.) saccharification enzyme, adjusting pH to 5, performing saccharification for 6 hours in warm water bath (55-60 degrees C). The method for activating the yeast comprises adding a specific amount of dry yeast powder into sterilized 2% (wt./vol.) sucrose solution and thermostatically activating in a water bath at 35 degrees C for 2 hours. The dry yeast powder used is angel active dry yeast.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The composite deoxidizing agent is polyethylene, ethylene vinyl alcohol and polyamide composite material, and has thickness of 0.06-0.1 mm.

TI Preparation of amorphous nanoceramic powder used in manufacture of artificial bone, involves adding calcium hydroxide aqueous solution and phosphoric acid aqueous solution to reactor, adding e.g. hydroxyapatite nanopowder, and reacting.
AU MIN S L
   HO J L
   YOUNG H K
   BEOM K H
   SHU Y Z
AE OSTEONIC CO LTD (OSTE-Non-standard)
GA 2018225482
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of amorphous nanoceramic powder involves adding calcium hydroxide aqueous solution and phosphoric acid aqueous solution to a reactor, adding beta -tricalcium phosphate nanopowder, such that molar ratio of calcium and phosphorus in the resultant mixed solution is 1.5, adjusting pH of the mixed solution to 5 or less, adding hydroxyapatite nanopowder, adjusting the pH of the resultant solution to 12 or more by adding sodium hydroxide, reacting in a carbon dioxide-free atmosphere, irradiating microwaves, and drying the resultant product.
   USE - Preparation of amorphous nanoceramic powder used in manufacture of artificial bone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient preparation of amorphous nanoceramic powder having improved solubility, without requiring high-temperature calcination process and within short period of time. Since the calcination at high temperature is not required, the crystallization of the nanoceramic powder is prevented. The reaction rate of the mixed aqueous solution of calcium hydroxide and phosphoric acid can be improved by irradiating microwave. The artificial bone prepared using the amorphous nanoceramic powder has high strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of amorphous nanoceramic powder involves adding calcium hydroxide aqueous solution and phosphoric acid aqueous solution to a reactor, adding beta -tricalcium phosphate nanopowder, such that molar ratio of calcium and phosphorus in the resultant mixed solution is 1.5, adjusting pH of the mixed solution to 5 or less, adding hydroxyapatite nanopowder such that molar ratio of calcium and phosphorus in the resultant solution is 1.67 or more, adjusting the pH of the resultant solution to 12 or more by adding sodium hydroxide, reacting in a carbon dioxide-free atmosphere, irradiating microwaves, and drying the resultant product.

PN CN107651687-A
TI Preparation of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material used for e.g. carbon dioxide adsorption, involves taking aquatic plant Enteromorpha as carbon source and placing mixed solution of carbon source and solvent into PTFE reactor.
AU CAI J
   TIAN Z
   WU X
   REN M
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2018115213
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material involves taking aquatic plant Enteromorpha as a carbon source, placing mixed solution of carbon source and solvent into a sealed PTFE reactor, increasing the temperature to 100-200 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for 1-30 hours under the condition of pressure generated by high-pressure solvent synthesis to obtain carbide, grinding carbide, melamine and potassium hydroxide, placing in a crucible, increasing the temperature, cooling, washing, and drying.
   USE - Preparation method of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material used for carbon dioxide adsorption, production of capacitor electrode material and electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material with high nitrogen content, aperture and high specific surface area, by simple and industrial process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material involves taking aquatic plant Enteromorpha as a carbon source, placing mixed solution of carbon source and solvent into a sealed PTFE reactor, increasing the temperature to 100-200 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for 1-30 hours under the condition of pressure generated by high-pressure solvent synthesis to obtain carbide, grinding carbide, melamine and potassium hydroxide according to mass ratio of 1:(0.3-2):(0.5-2), placing in a crucible, increasing the temperature to 500-900 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere for 2-3 hours at rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute, cooling, washing, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of sulfuric acid in the dilute sulfuric acid solution is 1-5 %mass. The mass ratio of carbide, melamine and potassium hydroxide is 1:1:2. The nitrogen content of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material is 1.4-15.6 atomic%. Preferred Properties: The pore size of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material is 0.5-3 nm. The specific surface area of pyridine nitrogen element-rich carbon material is 900-2000 m2/g.Preferred Process: The method further comprises pre-carbonizing process which is performed by heating the carbide obtained in the synthesis step at 450 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere at rate of 4 degrees C/minute for 1 hour, and also includes raw material pretreatment process which is performed by washing the collected biomass raw materials, and freeze-drying to obtain carbon source. The washing and drying process is performed by (a) placing the collected Enteromorpha in 2 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, and reacting at 60 degrees C for 2 hours, (b) washing the treated Enteromorpha with deionized water until neutral, (c) rapid freezing the washed Enteromorpha direct immersion liquid under nitrogen environment, and (d) freeze-drying the frozen Enteromorpha in a freeze-drying machine for 3 days to obtain dry carbon source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is water, ethanol, acetone and dilute sulfuric acid solution. Preferred Conditions: In the synthesis step, the temperature is increased to 180 degrees C for 24 hours. The temperature is increased to 800 degrees C for 2 hours at rate of 2 degrees C/minute.

PT P
PN CN107629246-A
TI Recycling of decorative plastic waste involves drying decorative plastic waste, crushing, heating, extracting, introducing nitrogen, adding resultant non-gasified material to reactor, adding crosslinking agent and linseed oil and preheating.
AU DAHAI C
   YUHAN F
AE ANHUI FENGJIA MENTAL RESOURCES CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018101588
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of decorative plastic waste involves drying collected decorative plastic waste, crushing, adding to a gasification furnace, heating to 180-220 degrees C, extracting resultant gasified material, introducing nitrogen, cooling to room temperature, obtaining solid material, adding non-gasified material to a reactor, adding 0.5-3 wt.% crosslinking agent and 2-4 wt.% linseed oil, preheating to 140-160 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide, heating to 240-260 degrees C, cooling to 120-140 degrees C, heat-preserving for 20-40 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature, extruding, and granulating.
   USE - Recycling of decorative plastic waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective, environmentally-friendly and economical recycling of decorative plastic waste, and provides plastic product having excellent mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling of decorative plastic waste involves drying collected decorative plastic waste to moisture content of less than 0.1 wt.%, adding to a crusher, crushing, obtaining waste particles having particle size of 2 cm or less, adding the waste particles to a gasification furnace, heating to 180-220 degrees C in atmosphere with oxygen content of 20-30%, extracting resultant gasified material, introducing nitrogen until output gas is completely nitrogen, cooling to room temperature, obtaining solid material, adding non-gasified material to a reactor, adding 0.5-3 wt.% crosslinking agent and 2-4 wt.% linseed oil, preheating to 140-160 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide, heating to 240-260 degrees C, heat-preserving for 1-2 hours, cooling to 120-140 degrees C, heat-preserving for 20-40 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature, extruding, and granulating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The main raw material of the decorative plastic waste comprises polyethylene, polypropylene, polystyrene, polyvinyl chloride, epoxy resin, polymethyl methacrylate, acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene plastics and polyamide.

TI Preparing emulsion useful e.g. for moisturizing            skin, comprises e.g. fermenting lotus leaves, Garcinia            cambogia and Chrysanthemum, distilling, stirring            distillation residue with pure dew, Aloe gel and            alginic acid, sterilizing and filtering.
AU LIU Q
   WANG L
AE CHANGZHOU LICHUN DIGITAL TECHNOLOGY CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201806317T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing emulsion comprises (i) adding lotus                leaves, amino acid solution and Garcinia cambogia                into a stirrer, stirring and ultrasonically                vibrating to obtain mixture; (ii) mixing the                mixture, bacterial powder and Chrysanthemum,                fermenting and crushing; (iii) mixing the crushed                mixture with ethanol solution, subjecting to                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and                distilling; and (iv) taking distillation residue,                pure dew, lecithin, Aloe gel and alginic acid,                stirring uniformly, sterilizing, filtering and                collecting the filtrate.
   USE - The emulsion is useful for moisturizing skin                (claimed), treating skin inflammation and adverse                skin symptoms, and replenishing skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion reduces water barrier function,                increases absorption of hydrophilic molecules by                the skin, maintains skin activity, and has long                term effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing emulsion comprises (i) adding lotus                leaves, amino acid solution and Garcinia cambogia                into a stirrer, stirring and ultrasonically                vibrating to obtain mixture; (ii) mixing the                mixture, bacterial powder and Chrysanthemum, adding                into a fermentation tank, fermenting, adding into a                crushing tank and crushing; (iii) mixing the                crushed mixture with ethanol solution, subjecting                to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction,                filtering, collecting the filtrate, mixing the                filtrate with activated carbon, allowing to stand,                distilling, recycling ethanol and collecting the                distillation residue; and (iv) taking 60-65 pts.                wt. distillation residue, 15-18 pts. wt. pure dew,                3-5 pts. wt. lecithin, 2-4 pts. wt. Aloe gel and                1-3 pts. wt. alginic acid, stirring uniformly,                sterilizing, filtering and collecting the                filtrate.

TI Method for recovering carbon dioxide from air,            involves directing separated carbon dioxide stream to            gas liquid separator, and separating separated carbon            dioxide stream into recycled water stream and            concentrated carbon dioxide stream.
AU MOLTER T M
   MURDOCH K
   ELLITHORPE C
   MYLES T
   ROY R
AE SKYRE INC (SKYR-Non-standard)
GA 202065146E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing a carbon                dioxide rich stream (2) to a scrubber (10)                including a metal hydroxide, and allowing the                carbon dioxide to react with the metal hydroxide to                form a metal carbonate. A metal carbonate stream                (12) from the scrubber is directed to an                electrochemical concentrator (20), and a potential                is applied to the electrochemical concentrator to                form a metal hydroxide stream (24) and a separated                carbon dioxide stream (22). The metal hydroxide                stream including a recovered metal hydroxide and                hydrogen is directed to an electrochemical                separator (30) and a potential is applied to the                electrochemical separator to separate the hydrogen                forming a hydrogen recycle stream (32) from the                recovered metal hydroxide forming a metal hydroxide                recycle stream (34). The separated carbon dioxide                stream is directed to a gas liquid separator (40)                and the separated carbon dioxide stream is                separated into a recycled water stream (44) and a                concentrated carbon dioxide stream (42).
   USE - Method for recovering carbon dioxide from                air.
   ADVANTAGE - The conductivity is improved and the contact                resistance is reduced by plating the conductive                spacer with a metal such as platinum. The carbonate                salt solution is saturated to minimize the voltage                needed to run the separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                electrochemical carbon dioxide collection                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                electrochemical carbon dioxide collection                system.

PN WO2019162013-A1; TW201937272-A; CN111742267-A; KR2020123426-A; US2021048757-A1; US2021333723-A9; US11287753-B2
TI Carbon dioxide snow cleaning apparatus for cleaning e.g. reticle of lithographic apparatus, has carbon dioxide snow nozzle that is provided in fluid communication with carbon dioxide source.
AU VAN DE KERKHOF M A
   VANDKERHOF M A
   VANDEKERKHOF M A
AE ASML NETHERLANDS BV (ASML-C)
   ASML NETHERLANDS BV (ASML-C)
GA 201974436R
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide snow cleaning apparatus (15) has a carbon dioxide source (16), carbon dioxide snow nozzle (17), charging element (20) and a collection surface (18) that are arranged orderly. The carbon dioxide snow nozzle is in fluid communication with the carbon dioxide source. The collection surfaces are electrically and electrostatically charged. The collection surface is adjacent to the carbon dioxide snow nozzle, and the surface is located between the carbon dioxide snow nozzle and the surface to be cleaned.
   USE - Carbon dioxide snow cleaning apparatus for cleaning surface e.g. reticle of lithographic apparatus (all claimed) used in manufacture of integrated circuits. Can also be used for cleaning optical element, mirror, reflective surface, collector, wall and sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The collection surface prevents the contaminants from transferring to another area of the lithography apparatus or from depositing onto another surface of the reticle, thus reducing the risk of further contamination. The adjacent arrangement reduces the risk of particles escaping from the surface to be cleaned to other areas, and maximize the probability of the collection surface capturing the particle. The risk of inadvertent damage to the reticle is reduced during removal and re-installation. The amount of charge transferred to the particle to be cleaned and the surface to be cleaned can be increased, therefore increasing the repulsive forces between there between by increasing the overall charge of the carbon dioxide snowflakes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of cleaning a surface.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide snow cleaning apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide snow cleaning apparatus (15)
   Carbon dioxide source (16)
   Carbon dioxide snow nozzle (17)
   Collection surface (18)
   Charging element (20)

PT P
PN US2019169046-A1
TI Aquaculture system for degassing comprises aquaculture vessel outputting water with carbon dioxide content at 1st concentration, drum filter assembly,biofilter assembly, trickling assembly, and degassing assemblies connected in series.
AU HOLM T
AE ATLANTIC SAPPHIRE IP LLC (ATLA-Non-standard)
GA 2019493717
AB    NOVELTY - Aquaculture system (100) comprises an aquaculture vessel (102) outputting water with carbon dioxide content at a 1st concentration; a drum filter assembly; a biofilter assembly (108); a trickling assembly (110); and degassing assemblies connected in series. The water is directed serially and sequentially through each of the drum filter assembly, the biofilter assembly, and the trickling assembly to strip the carbon dioxide content out of the water so that the carbon dioxide content is reduced. Each of the degassing assemblies removes portion of the carbon dioxide content, where at least one of the degassing assemblies is in fluid communication with the aquaculture vessel.
   USE - The system is useful for degassing.
   ADVANTAGE - Each of the assemblies will strip or remove a portion of the carbon dioxide from the seawater such that a concentration of carbon dioxide in the seawater is progressively reduced as the seawater migrates through the assemblies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method which involves outputting water from an aquaculture vessel, the water has a carbon dioxide content created by fish in the aquaculture vessel; directing the water through a 1st carbon dioxide stripping assembly to remove a portion of the carbon dioxide content; directing the water from the 1st carbon dioxide stripping assembly through a 2nd carbon dioxide stripping assembly to remove a 2nd portion of the carbon dioxide content; and directing the water from the 2nd carbon dioxide stripping assembly through a 3rd carbon dioxide stripping assembly to remove a 3rd portion of the carbon dioxide content.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of multi-stage degassing assembly.
   Aquaculture system (100)
   Aquaculture vessel (102)
   Biofilter assembly (108)
   Trickling assembly (110)
   Propeller pump (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Process: The water is 1st directed through the drum filter assembly to reduce the carbon dioxide content to a 2nd concentration that is less than the 1st concentration, then through the biofilter assembly to reduce the carbon dioxide content to a 3rd concentration that is less than the 2nd concentration, and then the trickling assembly to reduce the carbon dioxide content to a fourth concentration that is less than the 3rd concentration. The water exiting the trickling assembly is returned to the aquaculture vessel through a propeller pump (114). The water persists in each of the drum filter assembly, the biofilter assembly, and the trickling assembly for a period of time. The water enters a header tank prior to flowing back into the aquaculture vessel, where the water is seawater. The method further involves returning the water exiting the 3rd carbon dioxide stripping assembly to a header tank assembly (104) through a propeller pump. It also involves processing the water in the 1st carbon dioxide stripping assembly for a 1st period of time, processing the water exiting the 1st carbon dioxide stripping assembly in the 2nd carbon dioxide stripping assembly for a 2nd period of time, and processing the water exiting the 2nd carbon dioxide stripping assembly in the 3rd carbon dioxide stripping assembly for a 3rd period of time. The method further involves holding the water exiting the 3rd carbon dioxide stripping assembly in a header tank assembly for a fourth period of time; and directing the water in a header tank into the aquaculture vessel after degassing.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); P14 (Animal management and care (A01K, L, M).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
MC D04-A01F1; D04-A03B; D04-B07; E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; P14-A01A; P14-E01C; Q42-D

PT P
PN GB2567633-A
TI Method for providing carbon dioxide enriched gas to greenhouse, involves controlling feedback flow of gaseous carbon dioxide in order to optimize feedback flow to burner.
AU PEAKMAN K
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 201936813R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing (101) input flows of a hydrocarbon-rich gas, oxygen-rich gas, and carbon dioxide. The input flows are combusted (106) in a burner. The products of the combustion process, the water vapor and carbon dioxide are used (107) in a boiler. The combustion products, water vapor and carbon dioxide are condensed (108) to produce a first output of liquefied water and a second output of gaseous carbon dioxide flow. A feedback flow of gaseous carbon dioxide is extracted (109) from the second output. The remainder of the second output flow is fed to a greenhouse. The feedback flow is supplied (112) as the input carbon dioxide flow. The feedback flow of gaseous carbon dioxide is controlled in order to optimize the feedback flow to the burner.
   USE - Method for providing carbon dioxide enriched gas to greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method with which the carbon dioxide concentration at the point of injection into the greenhouse is increased to an ideal value of around 1600 ppm or higher. The controller controls the flow through the control valves so as to achieve a target composition of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the combined input flow line.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for providing a carbon dioxide enriched gas to a greenhouse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a method for providing carbon dioxide enriched gas to greenhouse.
   Step for providing input flows of hydrocarbon-rich gas (101)
   Step for combusting input flows in burner (106)
   Step for using products of combustion process (107)
   Step for condensing combustion products (108)
   Step for extracting feedback flow of gaseous carbon dioxide from the second output (109)
   Step for supplying feedback flow as input carbon dioxide flow (112)
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC P13-A01C; P13-A07; Q73-A15C

AU XU T
   DU M
   SHEN J
   WANG L
   XIA Y
   LI S
   HOU X
AE CHONGQING CHANGAN AUTOMOBILE CO LTD (CHON-C)
GA 201935039A
AB    NOVELTY - A concentration of carbon dioxide controlling method involves utilizing controller to monitor user information in passenger compartment through passenger compartment indoor camera mounted on automobile, monitoring number of users in perceived passenger compartment, identifying age and gender of each user, through image recognition and feature extraction methods, monitoring user information, applying to different groups of carbon dioxide emission models, predicting changes in carbon dioxide concentration in the passenger compartment, calculating total carbon dioxide emissions in the passenger compartment followed by ensuring that the passenger compartment is not in a state where the carbon dioxide concentration is exceeded.
   USE - Method for controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in passenger compartment of automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling concentration of carbon dioxide with certain precision guarantee at zero cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A concentration of carbon dioxide controlling method involves utilizing controller to monitor user information in passenger compartment through passenger compartment indoor camera mounted on automobile, monitoring number of users in perceived passenger compartment, identifying age and gender of each user, through image recognition and feature extraction methods, monitoring user information, applying to different groups of carbon dioxide emission models, predicting changes in carbon dioxide concentration in the passenger compartment, calculating total carbon dioxide emissions in the passenger compartment, calculating distribution concentration (P) of carbon dioxide in the passenger compartment, combining with comfort of human body at different carbon dioxide concentrations, standard and red interval threshold setting, notifying automobile of relevant controllers that perform ventilation related control (such as window controllers, sunroof controller and air conditioning controller), performing specified protection algorithm and ensuring that the passenger compartment is not in a state where the carbon dioxide concentration is exceeded.

PT P
PN US2019170046-A1; WO2019108410-A1; CN111344384-A; KR2020096213-A; EP3717600-A1; SG11202004438-A1; JP2021504504-W
TI System used to convert carbon dioxide from vehicle exhaust to e.g. liquid fuels comprises carbon dioxide collection system that interfaces with mobile carbon dioxide capture system onboard vehicle, external power source and electrolyzer.
AU HAMAD E Z
   BAAQEL H A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201949646H
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a carbon dioxide collection system, an external power source, an electrolyzer, and a carbon dioxide conversion system. The carbon dioxide collection system interfaces with a mobile carbon dioxide capture system onboard a vehicle to transfer carbon dioxide (CO2) captured from vehicle exhaust to a vessel in the carbon dioxide collection system. The external power source provides the energy required for operation of the carbon dioxide conversion system and the electrolyzer. The electrolyzer separates a water feed into hydrogen and oxygen to generate a hydrogen feed and an oxygen feed and the carbon dioxide conversion system converts the CO2 collected from the exhaust of the vehicles and delivered to the carbon dioxide collection system and the hydrogen feed from the electrolyzer into useful liquid fuels and fuel additives through electrochemical reduction.
   USE - The system is useful for conversion of carbon dioxide from vehicle exhaust to liquid fuels and fuel additives (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: convert the collected CO2 to only one type of fuel blend or more than one blend using multiple CO2 conversion units; and eliminates the need to transport captured CO2 from the fueling stations for conversion and minimizes infrastructure needs for the mobile carbon dioxide capture. The conversion of CO2 at the fuel station will reduce transportation costs and emissions resulting from the fuel burned to transport the fuels to a conversion plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least one product of the carbon dioxide conversion system is mixed with diesel fuel to produce a high-cetane diesel or mixed with gasoline to produce a high-octane gasoline, where dimethyl ether and methanol from the carbon dioxide conversion system is mixed to form a mid-octane liquid fuel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The external power source comprises non-fossil energy. The carbon dioxide conversion system utilizes a catalyst to drive the electrochemical reduction of CO2 to liquid fuels and fuel additives. The system further comprises an oxidation reactor configured to oxidize original fuels, liquid fuels generated by the carbon dioxide conversion system, or a mixture of both into products with a greater octane or greater cetane. The oxidation reactor utilizes the oxygen feed generated in the electrolyzer as an oxidizing agent to oxidize the original fuels, the liquid fuels and fuel additives generated by the carbon dioxide conversion system, or the mixture of both into products with a greater octane or greater cetane. The thermal energy released by the oxidation of fuels in the oxidation reactor is utilized to reduce the energy demands of the external power source. The thermal energy is utilized directly in the carbon dioxide conversion system for the chemical conversion of CO2 in reactions that require heat for initiation to reduce or eliminate the need for alternative supplemental heat. The thermal energy is utilized indirectly to operate a generator to generate electrical power to augment the external power source. The system further comprises a liquid fuel blending system comprising at least one mixing units which combine the liquid fuels and fuel additives produced by the carbon dioxide conversion system in various ratios or combine at least one of the liquid fuels and fuel additives produced by the carbon dioxide conversion system with at least one traditional fossil fuels in various ratios. The external power source comprises at least one of an on-site wind power generator, an on-site photovoltaic array, or an on-site hydroelectric generator.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN IN202011047522-A; IN373515-B
TI Nanofluid used as promoter for carbon dioxide sequestration, comprises copper nitrate (copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, aluminum nitrate (aluminum nitrate nonahydrate), sodium nitrate and sodium hydroxide solution.
AU PANIGRAHI P K
   CHAKRABORTY S
   DAS M K
   ANSARI A A
   RAVESH R
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY KANPUR (IITK-C)
GA 2020B5928J
AB    NOVELTY - Nanofluid, comprises: a copper nitrate (copper(II) nitrate trihydrate); an aluminum nitrate (aluminum nitrate nonahydrate); a sodium nitrate; and 2M sodium hydroxide solution, where the copper nitrate, the aluminum nitrate and the sodium nitrate being in a molar ratio comprising one of 4:1:4 and 4:1:4.7.
   USE - The nanofluid is useful as promoter for carbon dioxide sequestration (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanofluid: associated with high aspect ratio of nanoparticles is enhanced thermal conductivity value; has specific area of nanoparticle are beneficial for hydrate formation; utilizes shape of nanoparticle also plays major role in stability of nanofluid as a cylinder or plate-shaped nanoparticles experience greater Van der Waal attractive force than spherical nanoparticles; improves potential as hydrate promoter; improves Brownian motion, uniform particle dispersion, low particle size, high surface to volume ratio, and high aspect ratio of nanoparticle; helps in improving gas consumption, increasing water to hydrate conversion, and lowering induction time; and is simple and cost-effective .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) synthesis of the nanofluid as promoter for carbon dioxide sequestration, comprising mixing copper nitrate, aluminum nitrate and sodium nitrate with deionized water to form a first aqueous solution, where the copper nitrate, the aluminum nitrate and the sodium nitrate being mixed in a predefined molar ratio, mixing sodium hydroxide pellets with the deionized water to form a second aqueous solution, where the second aqueous solution comprises 2M sodium hydroxide solution, adding the second aqueous solution into the first aqueous solution by dropwise method to form a mixture, where the second aqueous solution being added into the first aqueous solution under continuous stirring until pH of the mixture reaches to pH 10.7, where the second aqueous solution is used as a precipitating agent, aging the mixture under continuous stirring in an atmospheric condition for a duration of 16 hours, filtering the mixture using a filter paper to collect a precipitate, washing the precipitate using the deionized water to remove unreacted sodium hydroxide from the precipitate, redispersing a washed precipitate into the deionized water using additional stirring for a duration of 12 hours, ultrasonicating redispersed precipitate for a duration of 30 minutes to form the nanofluid comprising copper-aluminum layered double hydroxide (LDH); and
   (2) carbon dioxide hydrate formation using the nanofluid, comprising washing a reactor with deionized water to remove unwanted nucleation sites and contaminants, filling the reactor with a mixture of predefined amount of the nanofluid and the deionized water, where the nanofluid comprises copper-aluminum LDH, removing air present inside the reactor via vacuum pump after closing the reactor, stirring the mixture at 200 revolutions per minute for a duration of 30 minutes, charging carbon dioxide into the reactor having the mixture to form the carbon dioxide hydrate, where the charging of the carbon dioxide into the reactor is carried out using one of a single-stage gas charging and a dual-stage gas charging depending on elemental molar composition of the nanofluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The temperature of the reactor being maintained at 2 degrees C. The filling of the reactor with the mixture is carried till half of an optical window to enable visualization of nucleation, formation, and growth of the carbon dioxide hydrate. The single-stage gas charging is carried out for the elemental molar composition of the nanofluid comprising copper: aluminum: sodium = 4:1:4. The dual-stage gas charging is carried out for the elemental molar composition of the nanofluid comprising copper: aluminum: sodium = 4:1:4.7. The dual-stage gas charging comprises two-times charging of the carbon dioxide into the reactor at pressure 30 bar and after dropping the pressure to 20 bar. Preferred Components: The nanofluid comprises LDH of copper-aluminum. The nanofluid functions as carbon dioxide hydrate promoter by enhancing carbon dioxide dissolution in water and improving hydrate formation rate. The copper-aluminum LDH nanofluid is mixed with water by mechanical stirring for duration of 30 minutes and then used for carbon dioxide hydrate formation study. The predefined amount of the nanofluid is varied for different experimental runs to optimize the nanofluid concentration based on effectiveness for carbon dioxide capture while keeping total amount of the mixture constant for all the experiments into the reactor. The single-stage gas charging comprises only one-time charging of the carbon dioxide into the reactor at pressure 30 bar.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   USE - Hair beautification method.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide gas firstly sprayed on hair with a mist solvent strips off impurities adhering on the surface of hair, such as old oil component and silicon, from the surface of hair. Surface of hair can be returned to the state to which impurity has not adhered, and fresh protection component contained in out-of-bath treatment can be directly applied immediately after making surface of hair into the state to which impurity has not adhered. Hair can be effectively protected from the factor which damages hair from exterior environment, such as UV-ray.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hair beautification method is performed by spraying out-of-bath treatment agent (L) from spraying apparatus, by making carbon dioxide gas blow off from gas emission nozzle (5) to discharge mist solvent formed while dissolving carbon dioxide gas in the out-of-bath treatment agent discharged from opening of liquid agent discharge nozzle (6), to dried open cuticle or hair in which rinse-flushing the applied out-of-bath treatment agent is not performed. Spraying apparatus comprises gas emission nozzle which makes carbon dioxide gas blow off from gas outlet port (11), opening in a direction orthogonal to opening direction of gas outlet port, liquid agent discharge nozzle installed in the position close to the front of gas outlet port, gas cylinder (16) connected to spraying part (4) and filled with pressurized carbon dioxide gas, and liquid-filling container (21) which is filled with out-of-bath treatment agent which is connected with spraying part and contains gamma -docosalactone. Gas cylinder is connected through gas flow path (15) independent of liquid-filling container with respect to gas introduction port (13) of gas emission nozzle, and liquid agent flow path connected to liquid-filling container with respect to liquid agent inlet of liquid agent discharge nozzle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an A-A expanded longitudinal cross-sectional diagram of spraying part and an enlarged front view of the spraying part.
   Spraying part (4)
   Gas emission nozzle (5)
   Liquid agent discharge nozzle (6)
   Gas outlet port (11)
   Gas introduction part (13)
   Gas flow path (15)
   Gas cylinder (16)
   Liquid-filling container (21)
   Out-of-bath treatment agent (L)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameter: Mist solvent has particle size of less than or equal to 10 mu m.

TI Purification of carbon dioxide gas in manufacture            of methionine used as animal feed additive, involves            contacting carbon dioxide gas containing            3-methyl-mercaptopropionaldehyde and acrolein with            activated carbon, and removing acrolein.
AU IWASAKI R
   KATAYAMA T
   OMOTO N
   KOIZUMI Y
   KATAGAMI R
AE SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
GA 201885657Q
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of carbon dioxide gas involves                contacting carbon dioxide gas containing                3-methyl-mercaptopropionaldehyde and acrolein with                activated carbon, and removing                3-methyl-mercaptopropionaldehyde and/or                acrolein.
   USE - Purification of carbon dioxide gas in                manufacture of methionine (claimed) used as animal                feed additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective purification of                carbon dioxide gas. The methionine can be                economically manufactured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of methionine, which involves reacting                3-methyl-mercaptopropionaldehyde cyanohydrin with                carbon dioxide and ammonia, obtaining                5-(2-methyl-mercaptoethyl)hydantoin, hydrolyzing in                presence of alkali compound, obtaining reaction                liquid containing alkali salt of methionine,                introducing carbon dioxide into reaction liquid,                obtaining methionine from reaction liquid, and                purifying the carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of purification                of carbon dioxide gas. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Before removal by the                  activated carbon, the carbon dioxide level of the                  recovery carbon dioxide is 70-99.99 vol.%, and                  the pressure of the recovery carbon dioxide is                  more than -0.01 MPa and 0.5 MPa or less in gage                  pressure. Preferred Properties: The activated                  carbon has an average particle diameter of 0.1-5                  mm, specific surface area of 1000-1800 m2/g or                  less, and total pore volume of 0.2-0.6                  mL/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The purification method                  involves reacting methyl mercaptan and acrolein,                  obtaining 3-methyl-mercaptopropionaldehyde,                  reacting with hydrocyanic acid, obtaining                  3-methyl-mercaptopropionaldehyde cyanohydrin,                  reacting with carbon dioxide and ammonia or                  ammonium carbonate, obtaining                  5-(2-methyl-mercaptoethyl)hydantoin, hydrolyzing,                  obtaining the reaction liquid, separating                  methionine by introducing carbon dioxide into the                  reaction liquid, depositing methionine, and                  precipitating.

The cement-based hardened material is recycled aggregate, waste material of building materials consisting of concrete or mortar, or sludge generated by ready-mixed concrete. PN CN112265993-A
TI Activated carbon production device based on blue charcoal includes driving mechanism, which is installed at one end of the main body, and a production mechanism, which is rotatably connected to the inside of the main body.
AU LIU W
   WANG J
   LIU M
   YIN X
AE INNER MONGOLIA WANZHONG WEIYE TECHNOLOGY (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2021122318
AB    NOVELTY - Activated carbon production device based on blue charcoal includes a main body (1), a driving mechanism, which is installed at one end of the main body, a discharge pipe, which is fixed at the center of the bottom end of the main body, and a production mechanism (7), which is rotatably connected to the inside of the main body. The production mechanism includes a roller (71), a connecting rod (72), a joint (73), a second nozzle and an air jet pipe. The inside of the main body is rotatably connected with a multiple of the rollers with arc-shaped side walls, and one end of the drum is fixedly connected to the connecting rod, and the other end of the drum is fixedly connected to the joint having a funnel shape inside.
   USE - Activated carbon production device based on blue charcoal.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon production device based on blue charcoal have the advantages of facilitating cleaning of the ash content on the blue charcoal surface, gradually reducing the particle size of the blue charcoal during the reaction process, and helping the activated coke to keep dry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a blue charcoal activated carbon production process used in the blue charcoal-based activated carbon production device, which involves:
   (A) opening driving mechanism, and the driving mechanism drives the drum to rotate, pulverizing the blue charcoal and pouring into the inside of the feeding mechanism;
   (B) accumulating the blue charcoal material inside the bottom end of the drum;
   (C) turning off the feeding mechanism, and passing high-temperature carbon dioxide into the air intake mechanism;
   (D) pouring the raw materials inside the drum downward at this time, and a large amount of high-temperature carbon dioxide enters the jet pipe, causing high-temperature carbon dioxide to be ejected from the second nozzle;
   (E) using carbon dioxide that contacts the dumped raw materials, and carbon dioxide blows the raw materials with small particle diameters and moves forward with the carbon dioxide, and raw materials with large particles weigh more;
   (F) accumulating raw materials with large particles under the second nozzle, so that the particle size of the raw materials closer to the second nozzle becomes larger and larger;
   (G) opening the driving mechanism, making the drum rotate in the opposite direction, and the drum drives the fixed shaft to rotate;
   (H) causing the fixed shaft to drive the protrusion to approach the mounting plate so that the protrusion abuts against the mounting plate;
   (I) using fixed shaft that drives the closing plate to rotate, causing the closing plate to rotate against the drum, and sealing the drum, and the gap continuously opens the driving mechanism to rotate the drum, so that the raw materials are turned inside the drum;
   (J) using carbon dioxide to drives the ash on the surface of the raw material to be ejected from the inside of the exhaust mechanism, reduces the ash on the surface of the raw material during the activation process, and facilitates the contact of carbon dioxide with the raw material;
   (K) redistributing the particles inside the drum, and then reversing the drum, so that the particle size of the raw material particles inside the drum gradually decreases from bottom to top, which facilitates the contact of carbon dioxide with the raw materials; and
   (L) finishing the production of activated carbon, opening the driving mechanism to make the drum rotate in the reverse direction, and opening the discharge pipe, and allowing activated carbon to fall into the interior of the main body from the drum, and the activated carbon is discharged from the discharge pipe for collection.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of internal structure of the main body.
   Main body (1)
   Production mechanism (7)
   Production mechanism includes a roller (71)
   Connecting rod (72)
   Joint (73)

AE UNIV DO PORTO (UYPO-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4374Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for continuous upgrading of methane and                carbon dioxide gas mixtures, involves (a) feeding a                gas mixture stream comprising carbon dioxide and                methane into a first adsorptive reactor for a first                carbon dioxide adsorption to obtain a first methane                rich gas, where the reactor has an adsorbent and                catalyst filler, when the first adsorptive reactor                is fully saturated or partially saturated, (b)                feeding the gas mixture stream comprising carbon                dioxide and methane into the second adsorptive                reactor for a second carbon dioxide adsorption to                obtain a second methane enriched gas, and feeding a                hydrogen-containing gas stream into the first                adsorptive reactor for reactive regeneration, (c)                feeding the gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide                and methane into the first adsorptive reactor for                carbon dioxide adsorption, feeding a                hydrogen-containing gas stream into the second                adsorptive reactor for reactive regeneration, and                switching the gas streams that are fed into the                adsorptive reactors.
   USE - The method is useful for continuous upgrading                of methane and carbon dioxide gas mixtures.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high carbon dioxide                conversion, enables additional methane production                by converting the separated carbon dioxide into                more methane instead of simply releasing it, and                potentiates the separated carbon dioxide instead of                letting it go to waste and possibly emitted into                the atmosphere, and reduces poor heat dissipation                risks and safety concerns.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for continuous upgrading of methane and                carbon dioxide gas mixtures, involves (a) feeding a                gas mixture stream comprising carbon dioxide and                methane into a first adsorptive reactor for a first                carbon dioxide adsorption to obtain a first methane                rich gas, where the reactor comprises an adsorbent                and catalyst filler, when the first adsorptive                reactor is fully saturated or partially saturated,                (b) feeding the gas mixture stream comprising                carbon dioxide and methane into the second                adsorptive reactor for a second carbon dioxide                adsorption in order to obtain a second methane                enriched gas, and simultaneously feeding a                hydrogen-containing gas stream into the first                adsorptive reactor for reactive regeneration, when                the second adsorptive reactor is saturated or                partly saturated, and (c) feeding the gas mixture                comprising carbon dioxide and methane into the                first adsorptive reactor for carbon dioxide                adsorption, simultaneously feeding a                hydrogen-containing gas stream into the second                adsorptive reactor for reactive regeneration, and                periodically switching the gas streams that are fed                into the adsorptive reactors in order to prevent                the reactors from becoming fully saturated or                partially saturated, where the first and second                adsorptive reactors comprise a carbon dioxide                adsorbent material and a methanation catalyst as a                filler, where the volume ratio of the carbon                dioxide adsorbent material and catalyst is                1:1-30:1. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                cyclic reactor unit for continuous upgrading of                methane and carbon dioxide gas mixtures, comprising                at least one adsorptive reactor for receiving                hydrogen-containing gas stream or methane and                carbon dioxide gas mixtures, at least one moisture                trap for separating water produced from the                adsorptive reactor, where the cyclic reactor unit                switches the gas streams that are fed into the                adsorptive reactors such that the adsorptive                reactors cycle between adsorption stage and                reactive regeneration stage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of                  the carbon dioxide adsorbent material and                  catalyst is 1:1-20:1, preferably 1:1-10:1.                  Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  adsorbent material and methanation catalyst are                  arranged in a layered, fluidized, structured or                  mixed bed configuration. The carbon dioxide                  adsorbent material comprises carbon-based,                  zeolite-based, silica-based, polymer-based,                  clay-based, metal-organic framework-based, alkali                  metal carbonate-based, solid amine-based,                  hydrotalcite-like, single or mixed oxides,                  calcium-based, lithium-based, alkali                  ceramic-based, alkali zirconate-based or alkali                  silicate-based or their mixtures. The methanation                  catalyst comprises nickel, ruthenium, rhodium,                  iron, cobalt, molybdenum, palladium, silver,                  tungsten, osmium, iridium, platinum or gold,                  where the catalyst is dispersed on a conventional                  metal oxide support or a structured metal oxide                  support or a carbon support, or their mixtures.                  The conventional metal oxide support comprises                  aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide, titanium                  dioxide, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide,                  zirconium dioxide, chromium(III) oxide, cerium                  oxide, CexZr1-xO2, lanthanum(III) oxide,                  manganese dioxide or zinc oxide. The carbon                  support comprises activated carbon, carbon                  nanotubes, carbon nanofibers, biochar or carbon                  felts. The structured metal oxide support                  comprises mesostructured silica nanoparticles or                  metal-organic framework materials. The filler                  comprises a dual-function material comprising                  carbon dioxide adsorbent activity and methanation                  catalytic activity. The dual-function material                  comprises a combination of a carbon dioxide                  adsorbent material and a catalyst. The bed                  comprises a carbon dioxide adsorbent material and                  a methanation catalyst, or a dual-function                  material with carbon dioxide adsorbent activity                  and methanation catalytic activity. Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature of the adsorptive                  reactors is 150-500&#176; C, preferably                  250-350&#176; C, and the pressure of the                  adsorptive reactors is 1-300 bar, preferably                  1-100 bar. Preferred Device: The unit can have 1                  or 2 or more than 2 adsorptive reactors, where                  the cyclic reactor unit has a carbon dioxide                  conversion rate of at least 70%, preferably at                  least 90%. The cyclic reactor unit comprises a                  first adsorptive reactor for receiving                  hydrogen-containing gas stream or methane and                  carbon dioxide gas mixtures, a second adsorptive                  reactor for receiving hydrogen-containing gas                  stream or methane and carbon dioxide gas                  mixtures, a first moisture trap for separating                  water produced from the first adsorptive reactor,                  a second moisture trap for separating water                  produced from the second adsorptive reactor, and                  tubing, where the cyclic reactor unit switches                  the gas streams that are fed into the adsorptive                  reactors such that the adsorptive reactors cycle                  between adsorption stage and reactive                  regeneration stage. The adsorptive reactor                  comprises a bed comprising carbon dioxide                  adsorbent material and methanation catalyst, or                  dual-function material with carbon dioxide                  adsorbent activity and methanation catalytic                  activity.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN113899520-A
TI Carbon dioxide component control system of Mars wind tunnel, has flow meter connected with carbon dioxide sensor which is connected with air return end pneumatic pilot valve, and air return end pneumatic pilot valve is connected with sampling air return pipe.
AU ZHANG G
   LIU H
   WANG Z
   QIN W
   WANG Y
   XIONG W
   ZHOU W
AE CHINA AVIATION IND CORP HARBIN AERODYNAM (CHAV-C)
GA 202213665W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide concentration measuring system provided with a carbon dioxide measuring flange (3), a sampling end pneumatic pilot valve (5), an air extracting pump, a flow meter (7), a carbon dioxide sensor (8) and an air return end pneumatic pilot valve (9). The carbon dioxide measuring flange is located on the outer wall of a spark cabin (1). The carbon dioxide measuring flange is provided with a sampling air inlet pipe and a sampling air return pipe (18), and is connected with the spark cabin. The sampling air inlet pipe is connected with the sampling end pneumatic pilot valve. The sampling end pneumatic pilot valve is connected with the air extracting pump. The air extracting pump is connected with the flow meter. The flow meter is connected with the carbon dioxide sensor. The carbon dioxide sensor is connected with the air return end pneumatic pilot valve. The air return end pneumatic pilot valve is connected with the sampling air return pipe.
   USE - Carbon dioxide component control system of Mars wind tunnel.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas pressure in the spark cabin is detected through the absolute pressure sensor, so as to control the working time sequence of the vacuumizing system and the measuring system. Thus, the system can effectively realize the test requirement of carbon dioxide concentration in spark cabin when testing, and will not be affected by the vacuum low pressure, low temperature and sand dust test. The system has simple structure and convenient maintenance. The carbon dioxide gas source system provides stable carbon dioxide power gas source for the spark wind tunnel. The sampling measuring gas pipe and air return pipe avoids the discharge problem of the carbon dioxide in the air inlet pipe and the sampling end pneumatic pilot valve.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide component control method of Mars wind tunnel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide component control system of Mars wind tunnel.

PN US2018071947-A1; WO2018053001-A2; CA3036454-A1; WO2018053001-A3; BR112019004790-A2; MX2019002854-A1; EP3589466-A2; EP3589466-A4
TI Press bonding lignocellulosic material with phenol formaldehyde resin and carbon dioxide, comprises heating the carbon dioxide prior to introducing the carbon dioxide into a press bonding system.
AU SLAYTER J D
   LEEPER D L
   BOWDEN A D
   DOWDEN A D
AE HEXION INC (HEXS-C)
   HEXION RES BELGIUM SA (HEXS-C)
GA 201821160H
AB    NOVELTY - Method-m1 for press bonding lignocellulosic material with phenol formaldehyde resin and carbon dioxide, comprises heating the carbon dioxide prior to introducing the carbon dioxide into a press bonding system.
   USE - The methods are useful for press bonding lignocellulosic material with phenol formaldehyde resin and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: utilize the heated carbon dioxide which improves bond strength compared to an otherwise similar system employing unheated carbon dioxide; provide a faster cure which in turn allows for a shorter cycle time in energy efficient manner; eliminate steam and introduce additional heat to the system using the heated carbon dioxide; provide the pull force with 16-50% increase in bond performance over conventionally produced cellulosic mats; recover and reuse the carbon dioxide used during the press cycle; and are eco-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a press bonding system comprising a heated press and at least one element comprising a blender where binder is added to cellulosic material, a conveyor belt running to a forming bunker, a forming bunker, an out-feed from the forming bunker, a forming line, and a steam preheater, where at least one of the elements is configured to receive heated carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a method-m2 of press bonding lignocellulosic material with phenol formaldehyde resin and carbon dioxide comprising either heating the carbon dioxide prior to introducing it into a press bonding system, where carbon dioxide is employed to introduce heat into the press bonding system and a lignocellulosic material is produced and is oriented strand board, plywood, laminated veneer lumber, oriented strand lumber, particleboard, medium density fiber board or hardboard, or heating the carbon dioxide prior to introducing it into a press bonding system, where the carbon dioxide is heated to 125 degrees C to a temperature, and any steam used in the process does not condense within or onto the lignocellulosic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method-m1, steam is employed to introduce heat into the press bonding system, carbon dioxide is employed to introduce heat into the press bonding system, and carbon dioxide is employed to introduce heat into the press bonding system and no steam is employed there with. The carbon dioxide is recovered for reuse. In the method-m2, hot air and/or steam is coinjected with the heated carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The lignocellulosic material is oriented strand board.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The press bonding system further comprises a carbon dioxide preheater. The press includes a perforated platen and the perforated platen is configured to receive heated carbon dioxide. The system is configured to recover and recycle carbon dioxide. The press bonding system further comprises a subsystem, located downstream from the press, which is configured to recover carbon dioxide or other gasses.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P63 (Working, preserving wood (B27).)

PN CN110684704-A
TI New genetically engineered algae strain of Synechocystis PCC6803 useful for producing cellulase.
AU DAI Y
   NING Y
   SHAO Q
   MENG Q
   ZHAO X
   CHEN G
   LV H
   JIA S
   ZHONG C
   TAN Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
   BIOTECHNOLOGY RES CENT SHANDONG ACAD AGR (SDAC-C)
GA 202008659X
AB    NOVELTY - Genetically engineered algae strain of Synechocystis PCC6803, is new. The exogenous cellulose exonuclease CBHII gene is integrated into Synechocystis PCC6803 genome.
   USE - The genetically engineered algae strain of Synechocystis PCC6803 is useful for producing cellulase (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The strain: provides way to obtain cellulase using inorganic salts and carbon dioxide; and reduces consumption of organic carbon sources in enzyme production process and greenhouse gas emissions during the production of cellulase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for constructing genetically engineered algae strain of Synechocystis PCC6803 producing cellulase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The vector used in the CBHII gene integration process is the expression vector PSNCII comprises fully defined 1518 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 14). In the step (a), the comprises (i) taking 5'-gatgtcgacgctttagcgttccagtg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 1) and 5'-catttggttataattccttatgtat-3' (SEQ ID NO: 2) of the amplification primers, where step (i) the amplification system comprises 1 mu l each of forward and reverse primers, 1 mu l template, 12.5 mu l recombinase, 9.5 mu l double distilled water and a total of 25 mu l in the system and the amplification reaction conditions are pre-denaturating at 94 degrees C for 3 min, denaturating at 98 degrees C for 10 seconds, renaturating at 55 degrees C for 15 seconds, extending at 72 degrees C for 1.5 min after 30 cycles, 72 degrees C for 10 minutes and guaranteed at 4 degrees C and digestion reaction system comprises 3 mu l DNA, 1 mu l endonuclease, 1 mu l 10 x Buffer, 5 mu l double distilled water, where total of 10 mu l. In the step (b), 5'-taaccaaatgcaagcttgctcaagcgt-3' (SEQ ID NO: 7) and fully defined 42 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 8) of the amplification primers , in the step (b), the amplification system comprises 1 mu l each of the forward and reverse primers, 1 mu l template, 12.5 mu l recombinase, 9.5 mu l double distilled water and a total of 25 mu l of the total system and in the step (b), the amplification reaction conditions are pre-denaturating at 94 degrees C for 3 minutes, denaturating at 98 degrees C for 10 s, renaturating at 55 degrees C for 15 seconds, 72 degrees C extension for 1.5 minutes, after 30 cycles at 72 degrees C for 10 min at 4 degrees C and enzyme digestion reaction system comprises 3 mu l DNA, 1 mu l endonuclease, 1 mu l 10x Buffer, 5 mu l double distilled water and total system of 10 mu l. In the step (d), the vector is P5ST1T2-downstream. Preferred Method: The method of constructing expression vector PSNCII comprises (i) taking 5'-gatgtcgacgctttagcgttccagtg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 1) and 5'-catttggttataattccttatgtat-3' (SEQ ID NO: 2) as upstream and downstream primers respectively, wild-type Synechocystis PCC6803 genome as a template, taking 5'-atactgcagccaagcttggctgttttggc-3' (SEQ ID NO: 3) and 5'-ttaggatcccccattattgaagcatttat-3' (SEQ ID NO: 4) as upstream and downstream primers, and Escherichia coli with accession number (U02439.1) as a template, taking fully defined 34 nucleotide sequences (SEQ ID NO: 5) and 5'-agtgagctcttaaccgttgacagcagg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 6) as upstream and downstream primers, and wild Synechocystis PCC6803 as a template for PCR amplification, light-sensing promoter and upstream arm gene sequence promoter-up, the terminator T1T2 gene fragment, and the downstream arm sequence are obtained by technology, where produces fully defined 501, 540 and 1083 sequences as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 9, 10, and 11); (ii) carrying out apaI digestion of the light-sensitive promoter and upstream arm gene sequence promoter-up obtained in step (i), digestion of the T1T2 gene fragment obtained in step (i) with PStI and BamHI and double digestion of SacI and SacII of the downstream arm sequence downstream obtained in step (i); (iii) ligating pBluescript SK plasmid double digested with PStI and BamHI, and the terminator T1T2 gene fragment obtained in step (ii) with T4 ligase to obtain the plasmid pBluescriptSKT1T2, double-digesting downstream gene fragment prepared in step (ii) with SacI and SacII and ligating with the plasmid pBluescriptSKT1T2 cut by the same enzyme to obtain plasmid P5ST1T2-downstream; (iv) treating the puc4K plasmid with the restriction enzyme BamHI to recover the Kana resistant sequence comprising fully defined 1518 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 12); (v) treating plasmid vector P5ST1T2-downstream with a restriction enzyme BamHI, and ligating Kana sequence obtained in step (iv) to the recovered P5ST1T2-downstream vector under the action of a ligase to obtain a plasmid vector P5ST1T2npt; and (vi) treating the plasmid P5ST1T2npt with restriction endonuclease ApaI, and recombining homologously the recovered promoter-up fragment and the exocellulase CBHII gene fragment under the action of recombinase to obtain a recombinant expression vector plasmid PSNCII. Preparation (Claimed): The method of constructing a genetically engineered algae strain of Synechocystis PCC6803 for producing cellulase, comprising (a) constructing plasmid based on the plasmid P5ST1T2-downstream taking fully defined 1518 nucleotide sequences as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 14), (SEQ ID NO: 1) and (SEQ ID NO: 2) as the upstream and downstream primers, wild Synechocystis PCC6803 genome as the template, preparing light-sensing promoter and upstream Promoter-up by PCR amplification technology and adding primers to the ends of Promoter-up + ApaI to obtain fully defined 501 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 9); (b) taking sequences of 5'-taaccaaatgcaagcttgctcaagcgt-3' (SEQ ID NO: 7) and fully defined 42 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 8) and using the corresponding gene sequence of Trichoderma reesei QM9414 in the GenBank database as a template and preparing CBHII gene fragment by PCR amplification technology comprising fully defined 1365 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 13); (c) treating the puc4K plasmid with the restriction enzyme BamHI to recover the Kana resistant sequence comprising 1518 nucleotide sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NO: 12); (d) treating plasmid vector P5ST1T2-downstream with the restriction enzyme BamHI and ligating the Kana sequence obtained in step (c) to the recovered P5ST1T2-downstream vector under the action of T4 ligase to obtain a vector P5ST1T2npt; (e) treating plasmid P5ST1T2npt with a restriction enzyme ApaI, and subjecting fragments obtained in steps (a) and (b) to homologous recombination under the action of a recombinase to obtain a recombinant expression vector plasmid PSNCII; and (f) transforming recombinant expression vector plasmid PSNCII obtained in step (e) naturally into Synechocystis sp. PCC6803, and subjecting to resistance screening and culturing to obtain the genetically engineered algae.
