PT P
PN CN109369602-A
TI Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoropropene carbonate            comprises e.g. preparing trifluoropropylene oxide,            mixing metal conjugated microporous polymer complex            catalyst, with prepared trifluoropropylene oxide,            heating and stirring.
AU LIU J
   DENG W
   YANG F
   YIN S
   SUN T
   MENG Q
   ZHANG L
   WU H
AE SHAXI YANCHANG OIL GROUP FLUORIDE SILICO (SXYP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201920406X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoropropene carbonate                comprises (i) preparing trifluoropropylene oxide                from trifluoropropene as raw material, (ii) taking                metal conjugated microporous polymer complex a                catalyst, mixing with prepared trifluoropropylene                oxide, introducing carbon dioxide at certain                pressure, heating and stirring to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                3,3,3-trifluoropropene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The product: has reduces cost and high yield.                The method is suitable for industrial                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal conjugated                  microporous polymer complex includes cobalt                  complexes of formulae (I) and (II). Preferred                  Method: The step (i) specifically comprises (a)                  treating excess trifluoropropene with mixture of                  bromine and fuming sulfuric acid until reddish                  brown color disappears and heating with acetic                  acid to obtain acetic acid,                  2,2,2-trifluoro-propyl ester, (b) adding                  2-bromo-3,3,3-trifluoro-1-propanol, methanol and                  concentrated sulfuric acid into acetic acid,                  2,2,2-trifluoro-propyl ester and refluxing                  reaction mixture, (c) adding alkali and water                  into reaction bottle equipped with rectification                  device, stirring and heating and adding                  2-Bromo-3,3,3-trifluoro-1-propanol dropwise to                  obtain trifluoropropylene oxide. The step (a)                  comprises heating at 90-100 degrees C for 1-1.5                  hours; The step (b) comprises refluxing at 70-75                  degrees C for 4-4.5 hours; and step (c) comprises                  heating at 80-90 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours. The                  step (b) comprises controlling pressure after                  passing carbon dioxide at 0.1-3 MPa, and heating                  at 25-100 degrees C for 1-48 hours. The step (b)                  comprises catalyst is (I), and adding                  tetrabutylammonium bromide and catalyst into the                  reaction mixture and maintaining pressure after                  introducing carbon dioxide at 3 MPa at 100                  degrees C for 1 hours, where mass ratio between                  tetrabutylammonium bromide and trifluoropropylene                  oxide is 1:5.2-5.9:28-56. The step (b) comprises                  catalyst is (II), and adding tetrabutylammonium                  bromide and catalyst into the reaction mixture                  and maintaining pressure after introducing carbon                  dioxide at 0.1 MPa at 25 degrees C for 48 hours,                  where mass ratio between catalyst and                  trifluoropropylene oxide is 1:28-56. The method                  for synthesizing (II) comprises (d) taking                  3-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde to prepare                  6,17-dibromo-2,21-dioxa-10l(4),13l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltapentacyclo(11.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(15,20))henicosa-3(8),4,6,9,13,15(20),16,18-octaen-1-yl                  acetate, (e) adding Salen-Co(I)-OAc and                  1,3,5-triacetylenylbenzene, mixing cuprous iodide                  and tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0) in a                  dry and clean three-necked flask, sealing the                  reaction device, treating with oxygen-free                  environment and inert gas protection, adding                  toluene and triethylamine, slowly heating at                  35-45 degrees C for 35-45 minutes and heating at                  80-90 degrees C for 70-75 hours to obtain brown                  solid powder as final product. The step (d)                  specifically comprises (f) mixing                  3-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde and bromine,                  stirring reaction mixture for 2-3 hours, and                  purifying to obtain                  5-bromo-3-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde, and                  (g) dissolving                  5-bromo-3-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzaldehyde in                  tetrahydrofuran to obtain solution, adding                  (R,R)-1,2-diaminocyclohexane dropwise, and                  stirring under reflux to obtain                  6,6'-(1E,1'E)-(1R,2R)-cyclohexane-1,2-diylbis(azan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(methan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(4-bromo-2-tert-butylphenol),                  (h) adding                  6,6'-(1E,1'E)-(1R,2R)-cyclohexane-1,2-diylbis(azan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(methan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(4-bromo-2-tert-butylphenol)                  into three-necked flask, sealing, subjecting to                  anaerobic conditions under inert gas protection                  conditions, adding anhydrous toluene, stirring                  continuously, completely dissolving solid matter,                  adding solution of anhydrous cobalt acetate in                  methanol to obtain dark red reaction mixture                  solution and heating at 80-90 degrees C for 5-6                  hours to obtain dark red powdery solid as                  (16R)-6,21-dibromo-4,23-di-tert-butyl-2,25-dioxa-10l(4),17l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltahexacyclo(15.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(11,16).0(19,24))pentacosa-3,5,7,9,17,19,21,23-octaene,                  (i) placing obtained                  16R)-6,21-dibromo-4,23-di-tert-butyl-2,25-dioxa-10l(4),17l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltahexacyclo(15.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(11,16).0(19,24))pentacosa-3,5,7,9,17,19,21,23-octaene                  in three-necked flask, adding toluene and                  dichloromethane in a volume ratio of 1:2.5-3.2,                  stirring, dissolving solid matter completely,                  adding acetic acid until mixed solution rapidly                  changed from red to brown, and stirring at room                  temperature for 5-6 hours to obtain brown powder                  as                  6,17-dibromo-2,21-dioxa-10l(4),13l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltapentacyclo(11.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(15,20))henicosa-3(8),4,6,9,13,15(20),16,18-octaen-1-yl                  acetate. The method for synthesizing (II)                  comprises (j) preparing                  N,N,N-tributyl-6-(2,4,6-triethynylphenoxy)hexan-1-aminium                  bromide, (k) adding 5-bromo salicylaldehyde to                  obtain                  6,17-dibromo-2,21-dioxa-10l(4),13l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltapentacyclo(11.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(15,20))henicosa-3(8),4,6,9,13,15(20),16,18-octaen-1-yl                  acetate, (l) adding cuprous iodide,anhydrous                  toluene and triethylamine as solvent,                  N,N,N-tributyl-6-(2,4,6-triethynylphenoxy)hexan-1-aminium                  bromide, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0)                  catalyst and heating under reflux to obtain final                  product. The volume ratio between anhydrous                  toluene and triethylamine is 3:1 and volume ratio                  between                  N,N,N-tributyl-6-(2,4,6-triethynylphenoxy)hexan-1-aminium                  bromide and                  6,17-dibromo-2,21-dioxa-10l(4),13l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltapentacyclo(11.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(15,20))henicosa-3(8),4,6,9,13,15(20),16,18-octaen-1-yl                  acetate is 2-4:1 and heating at 40-90 degrees C                  for 60-90 hours. The method specifically                  comprises (m) adding 2,4,6-tribromophenol and                  1,6-dibromohexane into dry acetone, mixing,                  adding anhydrous carbonate into reaction mixture                  under the protection of inert gas, stirring at                  55-65 degrees C for 48-60 hours to obtain                  2-((6-bromohexyl)oxy)-1,3,5-tribromobenzene                  compound, (n) adding obtained                  2-((6-bromohexyl)oxy)-1,3,5-tribromobenzene and                  trimethylsilylacetylene into dry triethylamine                  reacting in the presence of                  dichlorobis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II) and                  copper(I) iodide under inert gas protection,                  stirring at 68-72 degrees C for 36-48 hours and                  adding carbonate desilyl alkynyl group to obtain                  2-((6-bromohexyl)oxy)-1,3,5-triethynylbenzene,                  (p) adding chloroform and acetonitrile as a                  solvent in a volume ratio of 1:1 and reacting                  under inert gas protection at 65-75 degrees C for                  72-96 hours to obtain                  N,N,N-tributyl-6-(2,4,6-triethynylphenoxy)hexan-1-aminium                  bromide, where ratio between                  2-((6-bromohexyl)oxy)-1,3,5-triethynylbenzene and                  tri-n-butylamine is 2:3-4. The step (e)                  specifically comprises adding 5-bromosalicylic                  acid into three-necked flask, sealing and                  subjecting to anaerobic environment under inert                  gas protection, adding absolute ethanol with                  syringe, then adding absolute ethanol and                  5-bromosalic acid in a ratio of 1:0.7-1.2,                  stirring using dropping funnel under constant                  pressure to a volume ratio of 2, adding                  ethylenediamine in ethanol solution under inert                  gas protection, refluxing for 15-16 hours and                  purifying to obtain                  2,2'-(1E,1'E)-(1R,2R)-cyclohexane-1,2-diylbis(azan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(methan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(4-bromophenol),                  adding                  2,2'-(1E,1'E)-(1R,2R)-cyclohexane-1,2-diylbis(azan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(methan-1-yl-1-ylidene)bis(4-bromophenol)into                  three-necked flask, sealing, subjecting to                  anaerobic conditions under inert gas protection                  conditions, adding anhydrous toluene, stirring                  continuously, completely dissolving solid matter,                  adding solution of anhydrous cobalt acetate in                  methanol to obtain dark red reaction mixture                  solution and heating at 80-90 degrees C for 5-6                  hours to obtain dark red powdery solid as                  (16R)-6,21-dibromo-4,23-di-tert-butyl-2,25-dioxa-10l(4),17l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltahexacyclo(15.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(11,16).0(19,24))pentacosa-3,5,7,9,17,19,21,23-octaene,                  (i) placing obtained                  16R)-6,21-dibromo-4,23-di-tert-butyl-2,25-dioxa-10l(4),17l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltahexacyclo(15.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(11,16).0(19,24))pentacosa-3,5,7,9,17,19,21,23-octaene                  in three-necked flask, adding toluene and                  dichloromethane in a volume ratio of 1:2.5-3.2,                  stirring, dissolving solid matter completely,                  adding acetic acid until mixed solution rapidly                  changed from red to brown, and stirring at room                  temperature for 5-6 hours to obtain brown powder                  as                  6,17-dibromo-2,21-dioxa-10l(4),13l(4)-diaza-1-cobaltapentacyclo(11.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(15,20))henicosa-3(8),4,6,9,13,15(20),16,18-octaen-1-yl                  acetate. Preferred Components: In step (a),                  concentration of fuming sulfuric acid is 20%;                  mass ratio between fuming sulfuric acid, bromine                  and acetic acid is 5:1:9.5-15; mass ratio between                  2-bromo-3,3,3-trifluoroacetic acid propyl ester,                  methanol and concentrated sulfuric acid is                  50:15:0.8-1.2; in step (c), alkali is calcium                  hydroxide and mass ratio between water,                  2-bromo-3,3,3-trifluoro-1-propanol and alkali is                  4-5:2-3:1. PN CN108850609-A
TI Pig feed comprises crude protein content, copper content, iron content, zinc content and manganese content.
AU HUANG J
   DENG H
   XIAO R
   XIE Y
   JIANG S
   LUO M
AE CHONGQING ACAD ANIMAL SCI (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201895793J
AB    NOVELTY - Pig feed, comprises 11.88-18.38 %mass crude protein content, 21 ppm copper content, 67 ppm iron content, 66 ppm zinc content and 13 ppm manganese content.
   USE - As pig feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The feed has low protein content, reduces the dosage of the protein raw material and concentration of ammonia and carbon dioxide in the harmful gases of the pig house, improves the utilization efficiency and the environment of the pig house and does not affect the production performance of the pig.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The pig feed comprises 0.08-0.086 pt. wt. premix, 60-80.273 pts. wt. corn, 5.2-28.88 pts. wt. soybean meal, 1.1-11.64 pts. wt. vinegar residue, 0.16-0.85 pt. wt. calcium hydrogen phosphate and 0.77-0.93 pt. wt. calcium carbonate, 0-4 pts. wt. fish meal and/or 0-3 pts. wt. cottonseed meal and/or 0-3 pts. wt. oil and/or 0-0.26 pt. wt. lysine and/or 0-0.095 pt. wt. methionine and/or 0-0.101 pt. wt. threonine and/or 0-0.1 pt. wt. choline chloride and/or 0-0.01 pt. wt. phytase and/or 0-0.04 pt. wt. complex enzyme and/or 0-0.312 pt. wt. zeolite powder and/or 0.002 pt. wt. antioxidants and/or 0-0.05 pt. wt. multivitamins and/or 0-0.4 pt. wt. salt. Preferred Components: The premix comprises copper glycinate, iron glycinate, zinc glycinate, manganese glycinate, potassium iodide and selenium rich yeast in a mass ratio of 1:18:15:3:1:5. The antioxidant is ethoxyquinoline.

PN CN114713023-A
TI Purifying nitrous oxide and carbon dioxide in flue            gas biologically, comprises e.g. filling packing ring            and polyethylene packing rope in biological spray            tower, and filling liquid storage tank with nitrite,            ferrous (III)EDTA and nutrients, and spraying spray            liquid and discharging treated gas.
AU XIE P
   ZHAO L
   XIAO Q
   XU X
   CHEN C
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202292379U
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying nitrous oxide and carbon dioxide in                flue gas biologically, comprises (1) mixing and                filling packing ring and polyethylene packing rope                in biological spray tower (2), and continuously                filling a liquid storage tank with nitrite, ferrous                (III)EDTA and nutrients, spraying spray liquid to                packing area (3) of biological spray tower by water                inlet pipe (4) and spray head (8), and running for                at least 40 days, and (2) stop adding nitrite and                ferreous (III)EDTA to the liquid storage tank, and                adding ferreous (II)EDTA to the liquid storage                tank, transporting the flue gas continuously to the                packing area of the biological spray tower through                the aeration pipe and air distribution plate.
   USE - The method is useful for efficiently purifying                nitric oxide and carbon dioxide in flue gas, where                the carbon dioxide is useful in coal-fired power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines the flue gas                decarburization and complex absorption-biological                reduction denitration process, on the basis of the                existing biological spray tower, adding Fe (II)                EDTA into the spray liquid reservoir as the                chemical complexing agent to quickly and                efficiently complex nitric oxide in the liquid                phase, after the biological reduction is nitrogen,                at the same time, using the weak alkali of the                spraying liquid to absorb the carbon dioxide in the                gas, synchronously realizing efficient purification                of nitrogen and carbon dioxide. The method is                suitable for processing small and medium-sized                coal-fired industrial boiler flue gases, and has                low coupling cost and high characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying nitrous oxide and carbon dioxide in                flue gas biologically, comprises (1) mixing and                filling packing ring and polyethylene packing rope                in biological spray tower (2), and continuously                filling a liquid storage tank with nitrite, ferrous                (III)EDTA and nutrients, spraying spray liquid to                packing area (3) of biological spray tower by water                inlet pipe (4) and spray head (8), and running for                at least 40 days, and (2) stop adding nitrite and                ferreous (III)EDTA to the liquid storage tank, and                adding ferreous (II)EDTA to the liquid storage                tank, transporting the flue gas continuously to the                packing area of the biological spray tower through                the aeration pipe and air distribution plate, and                discharging the treated gas from the outlet, and                the nutrient elements in the spray liquid comprises                glucose, ferric EDTA, ammonium chloride,                dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, sodium bicarbonate,                magnesium chloride, sodium nitrite and trace                element concentrate; and the trace element                concentrate comprises zinc sulfate heptahydrate,                calcium chloride anhydrous, manganese chloride                tetrahydrate, ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate,                copper sulfate pentahydrate and cobalt chloride                hexahydrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the structure schematic                diagram of efficient purifying flue gas                device.
   1Spraying liquid storage tank
   2Spraying tower starting stage biological                spraying tower
   3Filling area
   4Water inlet pipe
   6Bottom water outlet pipe
   7Specific implementation manner
   8Spray head
   9Specific implementation manner
   10Aeration pipe

PT P
PN WO2020139538-A1; US2022064805-A1
TI Forming monosaccharide, comprises e.g. providing formaldehyde source containing formaldehyde aqueous electrolyte solution, and feeding formaldehyde source into fluid flow path between and in contact with pair of electrodes.
AU KARIMI T
AE CEMVITA FACTORY INC (CEMV-Non-standard)
   CEMVITA FACTORY INC (CEMV-Non-standard)
GA 2020605783
AB    NOVELTY - Forming (M1) a monosaccharide, comprises: providing a formaldehyde source containing a formaldehyde aqueous electrolyte solution; feeding the formaldehyde source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a monosaccharide generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one monosaccharide generating catalyst; and forming an amount of the monosaccharide from the formaldehyde source by a condensation reaction in the presence of the monosaccharide generating catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for forming a monosaccharide (claimed), which is used as nutrients and for other useful applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces excess carbon dioxide from the environment; and utilizes the system to perform at low gravity environments and/or in the confined and highly controlled environments of space travel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming (M2) a monosaccharide, comprising providing a carbon dioxide source containing a carbon dioxide aqueous electrolyte solution, feeding the carbon dioxide source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a glycerol generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one glycerol generating catalyst, forming a glycerol source by converting the carbon dioxide source into glycerol in the presence of the at least one glycerol generating catalyst, feeding the glycerol source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a glyceraldehyde generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one glyceraldehyde generating catalyst, forming a glyceraldehyde source from the glycerol source in the presence of the monosaccharide generating catalyst, feeding the glyceraldehyde source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a monosaccharide generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one monosaccharide generating catalyst, and forming an amount of the monosaccharide from the glyceraldehyde source by a condensation reaction in the presence of the monosaccharide generating catalyst; and
   (2) an electrochemical system for generating a monosaccharide from carbon dioxide and water, comprising (i) a hydrolysis electrochemical reactor cell comprising two fuel cell chambers separated by an ion exchange membrane, a positive electrode, a negative electrode, an hydrolysis aqueous electrolyte solution, and a power source, (ii) a carbon dioxide source connected to the hydrolysis aqueous electrolyte solution, (iii) a formaldehyde generator vessel containing a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a formaldehyde generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one formaldehyde generating catalyst, and (iv) a monosaccharide generator vessel containing a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a monosaccharide generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one monosaccharide generating catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises: providing a methanol source containing a methanol aqueous electrolyte solution, feeding the methanol source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a formaldehyde generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one formaldehyde generating catalyst, and forming the formaldehyde source by converting the methanol source into formaldehyde and hydrogen gas in the presence of the formaldehyde generating catalyst; providing a carbon dioxide source, providing an electrochemical reactor cell comprising two fuel cell chambers separated by an ion exchange membrane, a positive electrode, a negative electrode, an hydrolysis aqueous electrolyte solution, and a power source, performing hydrolysis of water in the aqueous electrolyte solution in the electrochemical reactor cell to produce hydrogen gas, feeding the carbon dioxide source into the hydrolysis aqueous electrolyte solution and into contact with the negative electrode, and forming the methanol source by reacting the carbon dioxide source with the hydrogen gas in the presence of the negative electrode in a reduction reaction to produce methanol and oxygen gas; providing a carbon dioxide source containing a carbon dioxide aqueous electrolyte solution, feeding the carbon dioxide source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a formaldehyde generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one formaldehyde generating catalyst including a boron doped diamond catalyst, and forming the formaldehyde source by converting the carbon dioxide source into formaldehyde and hydrogen gas in the presence of the at least one formaldehyde generating catalyst; providing a carbon dioxide source containing a carbon dioxide aqueous electrolyte solution, feeding the carbon dioxide source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a formic acid generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one formic acid generating catalyst, thus forming a formic acid source, and forming the formaldehyde source by converting the formic acid source into formaldehyde and water in the presence of at least one formaldehyde generating catalyst; providing a methane source containing a methane aqueous electrolyte solution, feeding the methane source into a fluid flow path between and in contact with at least one pair of electrodes contained in a methanol generating flow electrochemical cell, where the pair of electrodes contains at least one methanol generating catalyst, and forming the methanol source by converting the methane source into methanol and hydrogen gas in the presence of the methanol generating catalyst; performing the condensation reaction at a temperature of 100-300 degrees C, or performing the condensation reaction at a pH of 7.5-10; isolating, condensing, and pumping the methanol source into the fluid flow path of the formaldehyde generating flow electrochemical cell, or isolating, condensing, and pumping the formaldehyde source into the fluid flow path of the monosaccharide generating flow electrochemical cell; and recycling at least one of the water, the methanol, and the formaldehyde. In the method (M1): the condensation reaction converts formaldehyde to the monosaccharide through the production of at least one intermediate species comprising glycolaldehyde, glyceraldehyde, or dihydroxyacetone; and an efficiency of conversion of carbon dioxide to monosaccharide is greater than or equal to 40-80%, preferably 80%-95%, a rate of formation of the monosaccharide is 40-95%, or a rate of conversion of formaldehyde to monosaccharide is 45-99%. The method comprises feeding the carbon dioxide source into the hydrolysis aqueous electrolyte solution at a flow rate of 80-110 ml/minute, or performing hydrolysis at a voltage of 1.5-2.5 V, or performing hydrolysis at a current density of less than or equal to 15 mA, or a concentration of carbon dioxide in the hydrolysis electrolyte solution is 0.5-1.5 g carbon dioxide/kg water. Preferred Components: The monosaccharide comprises 3-6C atoms per molecule. The monosaccharide is glucose, fructose, ribose, erythrose, and glyceraldehydes, or the monosaccharide includes glycerol. The monosaccharide generating catalyst contains sodium silicate, and/or zinc-proline, and the monosaccharide is glucose, or the monosaccharide generating catalyst contains sodium borate and/or hydroxyapatite, and the monosaccharide is ribose, where a total surface area of the pair of electrodes and a total volume of the aqueous electrolyte solution have a surface area to volume ratio of 0.1-1 cm2/cm3. The power source includes solar power, sunlight, and/or electrical power. The positive electrode contains copper, and the negative electrode contains zinc oxide. The formaldehyde generating catalyst includes a cerium oxide catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises: a methanol isolation filter, a methanol condenser, and a pump connected to the hydrolysis electrochemical reactor cell and the formaldehyde generator vessel; a formaldehyde isolating filter and a formaldehyde pump connected to the formaldehyde generator vessel and the monosaccharide generator vessel; a monosaccharide isolation filter connected to the monosaccharide generator vessel; and an oxygen receiver connected to the hydrolysis electrochemical reactor cell.
   EXAMPLE - Formaldehyde was converted to glucose, by the formose reaction. The formose reaction was done in an alkaline environment at high temperature (100 degrees C). The formose reaction was done in the presence of sodium silicate (as a catalyst) to produce glucose. Other catalysts including zinc and proline, also will be used to produce glucose. Hydroxyapatite and sodium borate will be used to produce ribose. Glycerol was produced by a self condensation reaction of formaldehyde molecules at 100 degrees C and pH 8. After the operation of each reaction, samples were collected from the reactions after 2 hours to obtain the monosaccharide.

PT P
PN EP3521278-A1; EP3521278-B1
TI Method for revamping melamine purification section of high pressure melamine plant, involves discontinuing fifth line for adding stream of water to vapors, and discontinuing sixth line for introducing carbonate solution into absorber.
AU DI CARLO G
   SCOTTO A
   GAMBA S
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2019683527
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves installing of a first line (13a) for partially feeding a stream of water, and adding with ammonia, to a first heat exchanger (12) for condensing vapors (10). A second line is installed for partially sending the water leaving the heat exchanger to a quencher (2). A third line (15b) is installed for partially exporting the aqueous solution provided by an absorber (4) from a purification section (200) of the plant. A fourth line (15d) is discontinued for feeding the aqueous solution to the first heat exchanger, for subsequent feeding to a second-exchanger (18) and recycle to the quencher. A fifth line is discontinued for adding stream of water to the vapors extracted from a stripper (3). A sixth line is discontinued for introducing the carbonate solution into the absorber.
   USE - Method for revamping melamine purification section of high pressure melamine plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The undesired sequence of desorption and absorption taking place, respectively, in the stripper and the absorber is avoided because the ammonia content in the absorber is minimized, as well as the carbon dioxide content in the effluent from the stripper. The steam consumption inside the stripper is significantly reduced or eliminated in the case the revamping provides for the stripper elimination. The liquid stream leaving the quencher is not saturated with ammonia and carbon dioxide, which provides for less off-gas extracted from the subsequent stripping and a lower content of ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution collected from the absorber. An even lower content of ammonia and carbon dioxide in the liquid stream leaving the quencher justifies the dismissing of the stripper and the absorber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The quencher is fed with a first melamine-containing stream, which is a raw melamine melt, from a synthesis section and an aqueous solution comprising ammonia and carbon dioxide. The melamine is dissolved and off-gas are extracted. The stripper is fed with a second melamine-containing stream from quencher and with a stripping medium. The purified melamine is obtained and vapors are extracted. The vapors are added with stream of water and are partially condensed by heat exchange with an aqueous solution comprising ammonia and carbon dioxide. An absorber is fed with condensed stream and a carbonate solution and an aqueous solution comprises ammonia and carbon dioxide, which is partially used for condensing the vapors in first heat exchanger and is heated in a second heat-exchanger and recycled to the quencher. The portion of the aqueous solution extracted from the absorber is directly supplied to the quencher. The line for injecting ammonia to stream of water is installed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the melamine plant.
   Quencher (2)
   Stripper (3)
   Absorber (4)
   Vapors (10)
   First heat exchanger (12)
   First line (13a)
   Third line (15b)
   Fourth line (15d)
   Second-exchanger (18)
   Purification section (200)
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PT P
PN US2019099744-A1; WO2019069169-A1; US11311860-B2
TI Catalyst composition used for steam reforming of methane to hydrogen gas and/or syn gas and carbon dioxide (dry) reformation of methane to hydrogen and/or synthesis gas, comprises nickel species of aluminum nickel oxide, and alumina.
AU AL-MARRI M J
   SALAH S M A H
   KHADER M M
   ALI S
   ABDELMONEIM A G
   SALAH SAAD M A H
AE UNIV QATAR (UYQA-Non-standard)
   UNIV QATAR (UYQA-Non-standard)
GA 2019306589
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composition (C1) comprises: a nickel species; and alumina, where the catalyst is in form of nanoparticulate, the nickel species comprises aluminum nickel oxide with nickel and/or nickel(II) oxide, where aluminum nickel oxide is the main form of the nickel species, the nickel species is dispersed on the surface and in the bulk of the alumina, and the nickel species has a higher concentration on the surface of the alumina than in the bulk of the alumina.
   USE - The composition is useful for steam reforming of methane to hydrogen gas and/or syn gas and carbon dioxide (dry) reformation of methane to hydrogen and/or synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: exhibit a relatively low reformation (steam or dry) initiation temperature coupled with a relatively low temperature for quantitative conversion of methane but with high temperature conversion efficiency; reforms methane with only a slight excess of steam over the stoichiometric calculated quantity and an excellent resistance to coking; and exhibits an exceptionally high stability during carbon dioxide reformation of methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) making (M1) composition (C1) by solution combustion synthesis (SCS), comprising dissolving a water-soluble nickel salt, a water-soluble aluminum salt, and a water-soluble reductant in deionized water to form a solution, heating the solution until auto-thermal combustion initiated, allowing auto-thermal combustion to proceed to completion to provide a powder product, and calcining the powder product in air at 800 degrees C for 3 hours at a heating and cooling rate of 1 degrees C/minute and 1 degrees C/minute respectively;
   (2) a catalyst composition (C1) prepared by a solution combustion synthesis (SCS);
   (3) a catalyst composition (C1) prepared by the method (M1);
   (4) steam reforming (M2) of methane to hydrogen gas and/or syn gas, comprising providing the composition (C1) and contacting the composition (C1) with methane and water at a temperature;
   (5) carbon dioxide (dry) reformation (M3) of methane to hydrogen and/or synthesis gas, comprising providing the composition (C1) and contacting the composition (C1) with methane and carbon dioxide at a temperature; and
   (6) a nickel catalyst for carbon dioxide reformation and steam reformation of methane, comprising a nanoparticulate nickel species, and a non-hierarchical alumina support, where the catalyst for steam reforming of methane having initiation of steam reformation of methane at approximately 350 degrees C, complete steam reformation of methane to hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide at 650 degrees C, essentially 100% reformation of methane in the presence of a slight excess of steam over a calculated stoichiometric quantity, stability during methane steam reforming up to at least 650 degrees C consistent catalytic activity for methane steam reforming to at least 650 degrees C, and resistance to coking during methane steam reforming to at least 650 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition (C1) has an x-ray photo-electron spectroscopy plot as shown in figure 9 and figure 10 of the specification. Preferred Components: The catalyst exhibits has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of at least 88 m2/g, or at least 70, 80, 90, 100, 110, 120, 130, 140, 150, 160, 170, 180 or 190 m2/g, or 70-200 m2/g. The nickel specie comprises 5-10 wt.% in the catalyst. The nickel specie comprises 7.75-13.52 (preferably 6-15) wt.% on the surface of the alumina. The aluminum nickel oxide comprises at least 50 wt.%, 60 wt.%, 70 wt.%, 80 wt.% or 90 wt.%, preferably 50-90, 80, 85 90 or 95 wt.% of the nickel species in the bulk of the catalyst. The size of nickel oxide particles is 15 nm, 16 nm, 17 nm, 18 nm, 19 nm, 20 nm or 20 nm, preferably 18 or 15-21 nm. The size of aluminum nickel oxide particles is 10 nm, 11 nm, 12 nm, 13 nm, 14 nm or 15 nm, preferably 11 or 12 nm, or 10-15 nm. The size of alumina particles is 18 nm, 19 nm, 20 nm, 21 nm 22 nm, 23 nm, 24 nm or 25 nm, preferably 21 or 22 nm, or 18-25 nm. The water-soluble nickel salt is nickel nitrate hexahydrate. The water-soluble aluminum salt is aluminum nitrate nonahydrate. The catalyst has initiation of dry reformation of methane at 500 degrees C, maximum activity of 94% dry reformation of methane to hydrogen and carbon monoxide at 800 degrees C, stability up to at least 800 degrees C, consistent catalytic activity to at least 800 degrees C, and resistance to coking during the investigated period of 50 hours to at least 800 degrees C. Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the quantity of nickel nitrate hexahydrate is so that the method produces a catalyst composition having 5-10 wt.% nickel species; the ratio of reductant to the nickel salt is 1:1.1, 1:1.2, 1:1.3, 1:14, 1:1.5, 1:1.6, 1:1.7, 1:1.8, 1:1.9, 1:2, 1:2.1, 1:2.2, 1:2.3, 1:2.4 or 1:2.5, preferably 1:2; the ratio of glycine to nickel nitrate hexahydrate is 1:1.4; and the calcining step is at 800 degrees C for 3 hours at a heating and cooling rate of 1 degrees C/minute and 1 degrees C/minute respectively.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water-soluble reductant is glycine. Preferred Method: In the method (M2): the temperature is at least 350 degrees C; the reaction occurs at 350-650 degrees C; and the water is steam and is maintained in a small excess over a calculated stoichiometric quantity. In the method (M3): the temperature is at least 500 degrees C; the dry reformation reaction occurs at 500-900 degrees C; and methane and carbon dioxide are maintained at a molar ratio of 1:1 in the feed.
   EXAMPLE - 0.194 g Nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate and 6.75 g of aluminum nitrate hexahydrate precursor salts were dissolved in a 50 ml deionized water in a 250 ml capacity beaker and stirred well to get a homogeneous mixture. This was followed by the addition of 1.7 g glycine to obtain fuel to oxidizer ratio of around 1/1.4 was added into the mixture as well. The resulting solution was heated over a hot plate for combustion. The reaction was exothermic in nature and once the combustion initiates, proceeded in an auto-thermal mode without any external heating source. The synthesized nano-powder was then sintered in air by heating at a rate of 1 degrees C/minute till reaches 800 degrees C for 3 hours, then cooled down to room temperature at a cooling rate of 1 degrees C/minute to obtain 1 g nickel/alumina catalyst composition.

TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation system for recycling waste heat, has expansion-linear generator system connected with condenser, carbon dioxide Brayton circulation system located at carbon dioxide pre-cooler.
AU GAO W

   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
GA 201865572D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing input oxygen flow to a mask (140) attached to a subject. Carbon dioxide monitoring system (100) is utilized to monitor carbon dioxide exhaled by the subject into the mask during respiration cycle. A gas sample (146) including exhaled carbon dioxide and oxygen admixture originating from the input oxygen flow is drawn from the mask. Concentration level for the carbon dioxide and for the oxygen admixture in the gas sample is detected. Concentration level of the carbon dioxide as exhaled by the subject is restored during the respiration cycle based on characteristics of the input oxygen flow and the concentration level of the oxygen admixture detected in the gas sample. A signal representing the restored concentration level of the exhaled carbon dioxide is displayed on a computer display (102) for the respiration cycle.
   USE - Method for restoring exhaled carbon dioxide concentration level.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables minimizing total output power to maximize output signal-to noise ratio as the signal in the output remains constant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a system for monitoring exhaled carbon dioxide
   (2) a system for monitoring non-diluted concentration level of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a carbon dioxide monitoring system.

PT P
PN WO2022013197-A1
TI Separating gaseous carbon dioxide from gas mixture, preferably from ambient atmospheric air, flue gas and biogas, by cyclic adsorption/desorption using sorbent material.
AU GEBALD C
   VARGAS A
   ALBANI D
   MICHELS N
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 2022168440
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide involves (a) contacting a gas mixture with a sorbent material to allow at least gaseous carbon dioxide to adsorb on sorbent material by flow-through through an unit under ambient atmospheric pressure and ambient atmospheric temperature conditions in an adsorption step, (b) isolating the sorbent material with adsorbed carbon dioxide in the unit from the flow-through, (c) inducing an increase of the temperature of the sorbent material to a temperature starting the desorption of carbon dioxide, (d) extracting at least the desorbed gaseous carbon dioxide from the unit and separating gaseous carbon dioxide from steam in or downstream of the unit, and (e) bringing the sorbent material (3) to ambient atmospheric temperature conditions. The sorbent material comprises primary and/or secondary amine moieties immobilized on a solid support. The amine moieties, in the ( alpha )-carbon position, are substituted by one hydrogen and one non-hydrogen substituent (R).
   USE - Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide from gas mixture, preferably from ambient atmospheric air, flue gas and biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical capture of carbon dioxide from gas stream with high selectivity and selectivity. The sorbent has excellent adsorption and desorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide involves (a) contacting a gas mixture with a sorbent material to allow at least gaseous carbon dioxide to adsorb on sorbent material by flow-through through an unit under ambient atmospheric pressure conditions and ambient atmospheric temperature conditions in an adsorption step, (b) isolating the sorbent material with adsorbed carbon dioxide in the unit from the flow-through, (c) inducing an increase of the temperature of the sorbent material to a temperature starting the desorption of carbon dioxide, (d) extracting at least the desorbed gaseous carbon dioxide from the unit and separating gaseous carbon dioxide from steam in or downstream of the unit, and (e) bringing the sorbent material (3) to ambient atmospheric temperature conditions. The sorbent material comprises primary and/or secondary amine moieties immobilized on a solid support. The amine moieties, in the ( alpha )-carbon position, are substituted by one hydrogen and one non-hydrogen substituent (R). The gaseous carbon dioxide is separated from gas mixture, preferably from at least one of ambient atmospheric air, flue gas and biogas, containing the gaseous carbon dioxide and further gases different from gaseous carbon dioxide, by cyclic adsorption/desorption using the sorbent material adsorbing the gaseous carbon dioxide in the unit. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) use of sorbent material having solid, preferably polymeric, support material functionalized on the surface with amino functionalities capable of reversibly binding carbon dioxide, for separating gaseous carbon dioxide from the gas mixture;
   (2) unit for separating gaseous carbon dioxide from the gas mixture;
   (3) manufacture of the sorbent material; and
   (4) sorbent material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The sorbent material, preferably in porous form, has specific BET surface area of 0.5-100 m2/g, preferably 1-20 m2/g, and is in the form of a monolith, a layer or several layers, form of hollow or solid fibers, in woven or nonwoven (layer) structures, or the form of hollow or solid particles. The sorbent material is in the form of spherical beads with particle size (D50) of 0.002-4 mm, preferably 0.30-1.25 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The non-hydrogen substituent (R) is chosen from alkyl, alkenyl, or arylalkyl,preferably methyl, which is the same for essentially all primary and/or secondary amine moieties. The sorbent material comprises primary ( alpha )-methylbenzylamine moieties, in which the carbon dioxide capture moieties of the sorbent material comprising primary ( alpha )-methylbenzylamine moieties.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid support of the sorbent material is a porous or non-porous material based on an organic and/or inorganic material, preferably polymer material, preferably chosen from linear or branched, cross-linked or uncross-linked polystyrene, polyethylene, polypropylene, polyamide, polyurethane, and acrylate based polymer including polymethyl methacrylate, preferably poly(styrene) or poly(styrene-co-divinylbenzene) based polymer material, cellulose, or an inorganic material including silica, alumina, and/or activated carbon. The primary and/or secondary amine moieties are portion of a polyethyleneimine structure, preferably obtained using 2,3-dimethylaziridine, which is preferably chemically and/or physically attached to a solid support.

   QINGDAO HAIER CO LTD (HIER-C)
   HAIER SMART HOME CO LTD (HIER-C)

The carbon dioxide sensor is                contacted for detecting the concentration value of                Carbon dioxide at every pre-set time. The carbon                dioxide sensor is caused to detect the                concentration value of the carbon dioxide at every                pre-set interval when determined that there is no                person in a room.
   USE - Method for calibrating a carbon dioxide sensor                for an air conditioning fresh air system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing situation that the                carbon dioxide sensor drifts seriously with time so                as to ensure accuracy of the detection result of                the carbon dioxide sensor in an effective                manner.

PN CN109894098-A
TI Preparing porous composite material useful as carbon dioxide adsorption separation material in power plants, natural gas and coal chemical industry comprises e.g. reacting polyamine with carbon dioxide and supporting polyamine grafting.
AU KONG C
   ZHAO D
   DU H
   LIN Y
   ZHANG Q
   CHEN L
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 201956365S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous composite material comprises (a) reacting a polyamine with carbon dioxide to graft a carbon dioxide molecule onto a polyamine, dispersing a polyamine grafted with a carbon dioxide molecule in an organic solvent to obtain a dispersion I; (b) dispersing liquid I with the porous material, and supporting polyamine grafting the carbon dioxide molecule on the porous material or dispersing the polyamine in the organic solvent to obtain the dispersion liquid II; (c) dispersing liquid II and mixing with the porous material, and introducing carbon dioxide, supporting the polyamine on the porous material, and reacting polyamine with carbon dioxide and grafting the carbon dioxide molecule on the polyamine; and (d) drying product to remove the organic solvent, and then subjecting to purge activation to remove the carbon dioxide molecules grafted on the polyamine.
   USE - The composite material is useful as a carbon dioxide adsorption separation material in power plants, natural gas and coal chemical industry(claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a) carbon dioxide atmosphere is formed by introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system, and the polyamine is easily reacted with carbon dioxide in the atmosphere. In the step (b) the drying temperature is -196-50 degrees C, preferably in room temperature at 40 degrees C. In the step (b) the purge activation is carried out at 50-150 degrees C, preferably, vacuum or inert gas purge is used for activation. Preferred Components: The polyamine material is a linear or branched polyamine, the polyamine material has a molecular weight of 50-200000 and the polyamine material is polyethyleneimine or organic diamine. The porous material is a metal organic framework material, a covalent organic framework material, a porous molecular sieve or a porous silicon dioxide. The porous material has a pore size of 0.4-20 nm, the porous material has a specific surface area of more than 300 m2/g, preferably 300-7000 m2/g. In the polyamine-based porous composite material, the mass of the polyamine accounts for 30-150% of the mass of the porous material. The organic solvent is methanol, ethanol, water, acetone or chloroform.

PT P
PN WO2022197954-A1
TI Making material for capturing carbon dioxide from            earth's atmosphere, by producing acid and base with            acid-base generator, dissolving mineral in acid,            separating silica, forming impurity-depleted solution,            precipitating ferrous hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide            and/or calcium hydroxide, and separating.
AU PHAM A Q
   NIJHAWAN S
   GALLOWAY K
   ALVAREZ A
   WAGNER P
   FATUR S
AE ELECTRASTEEL INC (ELEC-Non-standard)
GA 2022C1776E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for making a material for capturing                carbon dioxide from earth's atmosphere, involves                producing an acid and a base with an                electrochemical acid-base generator, dissolving a                mineral in the acid to produce a mineral rich                solution, separating silica to form a silica                depleted solution, adding a first portion of the                base to the silica depleted solution to remove                impurities by precipitation, forming an                impurity-depleted solution, adding a second portion                of the base to the impurity-depleted solution to                precipitate ferrous hydroxide to form an                iron-depleted solution, pausing base addition and                removing the ferrous hydroxide precipitate from the                solution, adding a third portion of the base to                precipitate magnesium hydroxide and/or calcium                hydroxide to a form a recovered salt solution,                separating the magnesium hydroxide and/or calcium                hydroxide, and directing the recovered salt                solution to the electrochemical acid-base generator                to produce a new acid and a new base.
   USE - The method is used for making material for                capturing carbon dioxide from earth's                atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for making a material for capturing                carbon dioxide from earth's atmosphere, involves                producing an acid and a base with an                electrochemical acid-base generator, dissolving a                mineral in the acid to produce a mineral rich                solution, where the mineral comprises iron, silica,                and magnesium and/or calcium, separating silica                from the mineral rich solution to form a silica                depleted solution, adding a first portion of the                base to the silica depleted solution to remove                impurities by precipitation in a first                precipitation step, forming an impurity-depleted                solution, adding a second portion of the base to                the impurity-depleted solution in a second                precipitation step until solution pH reaches a                range of 7-10 to precipitate ferrous hydroxide to                form an iron-depleted solution, pausing base                addition and removing the ferrous hydroxide                precipitate from the solution, adding a third                portion of the base to the iron-depleted solution                in a third precipitation step to raise the pH to                10-13 to precipitate magnesium hydroxide and/or                calcium hydroxide to a form a recovered salt                solution, separating the magnesium hydroxide and/or                calcium hydroxide from the solution, and directing                the recovered salt solution to the electrochemical                acid-base generator to produce a new acid and a new                base. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method for capturing and sequestering carbon                dioxide from a gas mixture containing carbon                dioxide, which involves producing magnesium                hydroxide and/or calcium hydroxide, and exposing                the magnesium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide to the                gas mixture to form magnesium carbonate or calcium                carbonate;
   a method for capturing and sequestering carbon                dioxide from seawater, which involves producing                magnesium hydroxide and/or calcium hydroxide, and                depositing the magnesium hydroxide and/or calcium                hydroxide in seawater to form bicarbonate;
   a method for removing carbon dioxide from                seawater;
   a method for extracting calcium and carbon                dioxide from seawater, which involves driving a                first volume of aqueous salt solution as acidifying                solution through an anode chamber of an                electrochemical acid-base generator, driving a                second volume of aqueous salt solution as basifying                solution through a cathode chamber of the                electrochemical acid-base generator, applying                electrical power to the electrochemical acid-base                generator to acidify a volume of the acidifying                solution to produce an acid solution and to basify                a volume of the basifying solution to produce a                base solution, mixing a third volume of seawater                containing dissolved inorganic carbon species with                a volume of the base solution sufficient to produce                a mixture with a pH of 12, or 10-13 to convert                bicarbonate in the third volume of seawater to                solid calcium carbonate precipitate, separating the                solid calcium carbonate precipitate from the                mixture to produce a separated liquid mixture and                retaining the solid calcium carbonate precipitate,                adding a volume of the acid solution to a volume of                the separated liquid mixture sufficient to produce                a second mixture with a pH of 8, and returning the                second mixture to a seawater body;
   a method for making cement;
   an electrochemical acid-base generator;
   a method for removing carbon dioxide from a                gas mixture, which involves taking an electrolytic                reactor comprising an anode chamber separated from                a cathode chamber by an ion-selective separator                membrane, reacting an aqueous salt solution to                produce an acid in the anode chamber while                producing a base in the cathode chamber, contacting                a carbon-dioxide-containing gas with the base to                form a metal carbonate solution, introducing the                metal carbonate solution into the anode chamber,                where the metal carbonate solution is converted                into a weak acid solution, withdrawing the weak                acid solution from the anode chamber, subjecting                the weak acid solution to a negative pressure                sufficient to separate carbon dioxide from the weak                acid solution, leaving recovered water, and                directing the recovered water into the cathode                chamber;
   an electrochemical carbon capture system                comprising a stack of electrochemical cells, where                each of the cells comprises an anode chamber                containing a non-vertically oriented planar anode                electrode, an acidifying solution inlet and an                acidifying solution outlet, a cathode chamber                containing a non-vertically oriented planar cathode                electrode, a basifying solution inlet and a                basifying solution outlet, a separator membrane                separating the anode chamber from the cathode                chamber, and a flow-field layer between the anode                electrode and the separator membrane, where the                anode has a hydrophobicity gradient with a                hydrophilic face adjacent to the flow field layer                and a hydrophobic face opposite the hydrophilic                face; and
   a method for making acid and base from a                salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves storing the acid in an acid storage tank                  prior to dissolving the mineral, and storing the                  base in a base storage tank prior to adding a                  first portion of the base to the silica depleted                  solution, directing the recovered salt solution                  to a salt storage tank and directing the                  recovered salt solution to the electrochemical                  acid-base generator to produce a new acid and a                  new base. The second precipitation step involves                  adding base until solution pH reaches a first pH                  range at which a second precipitate product                  comprising a first quantity of ferrous hydroxide                  and less than 1 wt.% magnesium hydroxide is                  precipitated, where base additions are paused                  when solution pH reaches a second pH threshold                  indicating completion of the second                  precipitation. The third precipitation step                  involves adding base until solution pH reaches a                  second pH range higher than the first pH range,                  where a mixture of ferrous hydroxide and                  magnesium hydroxide precipitates in the second pH                  range. The fourth precipitation step involves                  adding base until solution pH reaches a third pH                  range higher than the second pH range to                  precipitate a fourth precipitation product                  comprising magnesium hydroxide and less than 1                  wt.% ferrous hydroxide. The method further                  involves after producing the acid and prior to                  the dissolving step, capturing the acid in an                  acid-capturing ion exchange resin to form a                  captured acid, and subsequently releasing the                  captured acid by regenerating the ion exchange                  resin with a regeneration solution. The second                  precipitation step further involves adding                  metallic iron to the impurity-depleted solution.                  The method involves mixing hydrogen gas with the                  acidifying solution prior to introducing the                  acidifying solution to the anode chamber of the                  electrochemical acid-base generator, or                  introducing hydrogen gas to the anode chamber                  independently of the acidifying solution. The                  method further involves draining at least a                  portion of liquid from the ion exchange tank and                  passing a regeneration solution comprising second                  metal cations through the cation exchange resin,                  where the regeneration solution replaces protons                  in the cation exchange resin with the second                  metal cations such that the regeneration solution                  exiting the exchange tank is acidified, where the                  first metal cations and the second metal cations                  can be the same or different. When the                  regeneration solution comprises seawater, the                  method further involves removing acidified                  seawater, extracting carbon dioxide from the                  acidified seawater, and mixing the basified                  solution with the acidified seawater. When the                  regeneration solution comprises concentrated                  brine from a desalination process, the method                  further involves removing acidified brine,                  extracting carbon dioxide from the acidified                  brine, and mixing the basified solution with the                  acidified brine. The method further involves                  dissolving a mineral in the pure acid solution to                  form a mineral-rich solution, dissolving the                  mineral releases carbon dioxide gas, capturing                  the carbon dioxide as a pure gas stream,                  combining the basified solution with the                  mineral-rich solution to form a precipitated                  product which is a hydroxide or oxide comprising                  elements of the dissolved mineral, and collecting                  the precipitated product. Preferred Conditions:                  During operation, the basifying solution in the                  electrochemical acid-base generator comprises an                  instantaneous ratio of dissolved salt anions to                  produced hydroxyl anions equal to, or within 10%                  of a ratio of their respective ionic mobilities.                  During operation, the acidifying solution in the                  electrochemical acid-base generator contains an                  instantaneous ratio of dissolved salt cations to                  produced protons equal to, or within 10% of a                  ratio of their respective ionic mobilities. The                  regeneration solution is treated to remove                  divalent cations prior to passing the                  regeneration solution through the cation exchange                  resin. Preferred Components: The acid comprises                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, carbonic acid,                  carboxylic acid, citric acid, maleic acid, boric                  acid, and/or organic acid. The fresh water is                  deionized water, micro-filtered water, and/or                  filtered water. The electrochemical acid-base                  generator comprises an anion exchange membrane,                  and a cation exchange membrane (PEM). The                  calcium-containing solid material is calcium                  carbonate extracted from seawater. The                  calcium-containing solid material comprises                  calcite, aragonite, vaterite, limestone, chalk,                  marble, travertine, eggshells, oyster shells,                  limescale, and/or agricultural lime. The                  regeneration solution comprises a purified salt                  solution made by dissolving purified salt in                  deionized water. Preferred Composition: The base                  produced in the electrochemical acid-base                  generator contains a ratio of dissolved salt                  anions to produced hydroxyl anions equal to, or                  within 10% of, a ratio of their respective ionic                  mobilities. During operation, the acid produced                  by the electrochemical acid-base generator                  contains a ratio of dissolved salt cations to                  produced protons equal to, or within 10% of, a                  ratio of their respective ionic mobilities.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN RU2685122-C1
TI METHOD OF WATER EXTRACTION FROM MARTIAN WATER ICE.
AU POPOV A F
AE POPOV A F (POPO-Individual)
GA 201936499Y
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to space research and technology and can be used to extract water from Martian water ice. Water ice is collected from a Martian surface seasonally released from solid carbon dioxide and placed inside a Martian thermo-karst cavity. Then the cavity is clogged up and water ice is heated by increasing pressure of water vapor inside the cavity.
   USE - Astronautics.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention increases efficiency of extracting water from Martian water ice.

PN US2022273906-A1
TI Regenerable carbon dioxide scrubber for use in            underwater divers, astronauts, firefighters, first            responders, and/or other oxygen gas-poor areas,            comprises one housing compartment, at least one housing            compartment including first housing compartment            including inlet, outlet, and interior region.
AU BEACH R
   JIN M H
   JACQUE E D
   MORRIS M L
   STORCK S M
   PAULSON J M
   LUEDEMAN W L
   BIERMANN P J
   BENKOSKI J J
AE UNIV JOHNS HOPKINS (UYJO-C)
GA 2022B0690X
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerable carbon dioxide scrubber (1)                comprises one housing compartment, where at least                one housing compartment including a first housing                compartment including an inlet, an outlet, and an                interior region, and a sorbent material located                within the interior region of the first housing                compartment, where the sorbent material attracts                and/or retains carbon dioxide from an air supply                passing through the sorbent material at an                operating temperature below about 100 &#176;C,                and releases carbon dioxide at a regenerating                temperature above about 150 &#176;C.
   USE - Regenerable carbon dioxide scrubber for use in                underwater divers, astronauts, firefighters, first                responders, and/or other oxygen gas-poor                areas.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a rebreather, which comprises a mouthpiece                operatively connected to an inlet of a regenerated                carbon dioxide scrubber, and an inhalation                counter-lung (ICL) including an ICL-inlet and an                ICL outlet. The ICL-inlet is operatively connected                to an outlet of the carbon dioxide scrubber and the                ICL-outlet is operatively connected to the                mouthpiece; wherein to regenerate carbon dioxide                scrubber comprises at least one housing                compartment. The one housing compartment including                a first housing compartment including an inlet, an                outlet, and an interior region, and a sorbet                material is located within the interior region of                the first housing compartment, where the sorbet                material attracts and/or retains carbon dioxide                from an air supply passing through the sorbet                material at an operating temperature below about                100 &#176;C, and releases carbon dioxide at a                regenerating temperature above about 150&#176;                C.
   2. a method for scrubbing carbon dioxide from                users exhaled air, which involves:
   a. providing a regenerate carbon dioxide                scrubber comprising at least one housing                compartment, at least one housing compartment                including a first housing compartment including an                inlet, an outlet, and an interior region, and a                sorbet material located within the interior region                of the first housing compartment;
   b. receiving the user exhaled air and                directing the user exhaled air into that at least                one housing compartment;
   c. allowing the users to exhale air to pass                through the sorbet material (12) and exit at least                one housing compartment via the outlet of the at                least one housing compartment to provide a stream                of carbon dioxide-scrubbed air, where the users                exhaled air has a first carbon dioxide                concentration, the sorbet material attracts and/or                retains carbon dioxide from an air supply passing                through the sorbet material at an operating                temperature below about 100&#176; C, and                releases carbon dioxide at a regenerating                temperature above about 150 &#176;C, where the                stream of carbon dioxide scrubbed air has a second                carbon dioxide concentration that is less than the                first carbon dioxide concentration; and
   2. a method for performing a task in an oxygen                gas-reduced environment, which involves:
   a. providing a regenerate carbon dioxide                scrubber;
   b. receiving exhaled air by a user and                directing the exhaled air into that at least one                housing compartment;
   c. allowing the exhaled air to pass through                the sorbet material and exit at least one housing                compartment via the outlet of the at least one                housing compartment to provide a stream of carbon                dioxide-scrubbed air, where the stream of carbon                dioxide-scrubbed air has a second carbon dioxide                concentration that is less than the first carbon                dioxide concentration, the task comprises                underwater diving, spacewalks, extinguishing a                fire, or cleaning a chemical spill, and the exhaled                air has a first carbon dioxide concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                regenerable carbon dioxide scrubber.
   1Regenerable carbon dioxide scrubber
   3wearable article

   20outlet manifold

A top portion of the carbon dioxide absorption tower is provided with an air outlet. An inside of the carbon dioxide absorption tower is provided with a first spraying device. A bottom portion of the carbon dioxide absorption tower is provided with a rich liquid outlet. An air outlet is connected with an air inlet of a nitrogen PSA concentrating and purifying system (3). A desorption air outlet is connected with a liquid inlet of a carbon dioxide refining liquefying system (2). The heat exchanger is provided with a high temperature fluid channel and a low temperature fluid channel. A liquid inlet of a first cooler is connected with the liquid outlet of the high temperature fluid channel, where the liquid inlet of the high temperature fluid channel is formed with a barren liquid outlet.

The system has high recovery rate.

PN US10392911-B1
TI Producing reservoir oil from consolidated reservoir involves providing aqueous solution of carbonate salt, providing aqueous solution of acid, injecting, from surface, adding aqueous solution of carbonate salt into wellbore.
AU YEE P W F
AE HUSKY OIL OPERATIONS LTD (HUSK-C)
GA 201973465A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing reservoir oil from consolidated reservoir involves providing aqueous solution of carbonate salt, providing aqueous solution of acid, injecting, from surface, adding aqueous solution of carbonate salt into wellbore positioned inside reservoir, allowing aqueous solution of carbonate salt to enter into reservoir, subsequent to allowing aqueous solution of carbonate salt to enter into reservoir, injecting, from surface, aqueous solution of acid into wellbore positioned inside reservoir, allowing aqueous solution of acid to enter into reservoir and contact aqueous solution of carbonate salt, subsequent to allowing aqueous solution of acid to enter into reservoir, shutting in one wellbore, allowing carbonate salt in aqueous solution of carbonate salt and acid in aqueous solution of acid to react with each other to form carbon dioxide gas, allowing carbon dioxide gas to mix with reservoir oil.
   USE - Method for producing reservoir oil from consolidated reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce reservoir oil from consolidated reservoir that improves performance of sheep production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing reservoir oil from consolidated reservoir involves providing aqueous solution of carbonate salt, providing aqueous solution of acid, injecting, from surface, adding aqueous solution of carbonate salt into wellbore positioned inside reservoir, allowing aqueous solution of carbonate salt to enter into reservoir, subsequent to allowing aqueous solution of carbonate salt to enter into reservoir, injecting, from surface, aqueous solution of acid into wellbore positioned inside reservoir, allowing aqueous solution of acid to enter into reservoir and contact aqueous solution of carbonate salt, subsequent to allowing aqueous solution of acid to enter into reservoir, shutting in one wellbore, allowing carbonate salt in aqueous solution of carbonate salt and acid in aqueous solution of acid to react with each other to form carbon dioxide gas, allowing carbon dioxide gas to mix with reservoir oil, reducing viscosity of reservoir oil, subsequent to allowing carbon dioxide gas to mix with reservoir oil, opening up wellbore, allowing reservoir oil to flow into wellbore and producing to surface reservoir oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate salt is selected from sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and potassium carbonate. The aqueous solution of carbonate salt is saturated aqueous solution of carbonate salt. The acid is selected from group consisting of hydrochloric acid, acetic acid, nitric acid and hydrofluoric acid. Preferred Conditions: The shutting wellbore is for 4-24 hours. The formation of carbon dioxide gas increases reservoir pressure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN RU2685055-C1
TI METHOD FOR MONITORING CARBON-DIOXIDE CORROSION IN TRADE GAS PIPELINES AND DEVICE FOR ITS IMPLEMENTATION.
AU BLOKHIN V A
   MARKIN A N
   MANZHOSOV A K
   DOROSINSKII A YU
AE SONAR SCI PRODN ENTERPRISE LLC (SONA-Non-standard)
GA 201940453H
AB    NOVELTY - Invention is related to the field of failure-free operation of trade pipelines transporting raw gas, and can be used for monitoring of pipelines corrosion developing as to carbon-dioxide type. Method of monitoring carbon dioxide corrosion in trade gas pipelines includes measuring parameters of transported medium by means of corrosion rate sensors, which implement method of electrical resistance and method of linear polarization resistance as corrosion rate sensor, implementing method of linear polarization resistance, corrosion parameter meter is used with assessment of medium mineralization. In close proximity to each other, three corrosion rate sensors are installed, measuring probe of the first sensor, which realizes electric resistance method, is located in central part of gas pipeline, measuring probe of a second similar sensor and a measuring probe of a sensor which realizes a linear polarization resistance method are installed flush with the lower generatrix of the gas line, information outputs of corrosion rate sensors are connected to the telemetry system according to their resident interfaces, in the absence of carbon dioxide-type corrosion, two sensors, which realize the electrical resistance method, will have identical readings, and the sensor, which realizes the linear polarization resistance method, will indicate zero corrosion rate, in the presence of intense condensation of mineralized water and formation of a stream along the lower generatrix, sensor, which realizes the method of linear polarization resistance, will start to give readings of corrosion rate, different from zero values, and certain level of medium mineralization, sensor, which realizes the electric resistance method and is installed flush with the lower generatrix of the gas pipeline, will fix a considerable increase in the corrosion rate compared to the previous values and the readings of the sensor installed in the pipe center, combination of data on mineralization of medium and data from sensors of corrosion rate enables to judge intensity of developing corrosion.
   USE - Transportation.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is rapid monitoring of corrosion of field gas pipelines flowing along carbon-dioxide type.
2 cl, 3 dwg
