PN DE102017008149-A1; EP3449720-A1; EP3449720-B1
TI Apparatus for processing pet food for carnivores, has gas supply line and liquid supply line that are provided with valve arrangement for optional fluidic separation and establishing flow connection with treatment chamber.
AU GOCKEL F
   OLEXOVA D
AE MESSER GROUP GMBH (MESG-C)
   MESSER TATRAGAS SPOL SRO (MESG-C)
   MESSER GROUP GMBH (MESG-C)
   MESSER TATRAGAS SPOL SRO (MESG-C)
GA 201919340N
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) comprises a treatment chamber (2) that is provided with thermally insulated walls and a gas-tight closable access opening (4), into which a gas supply line (11) is connected to a liquid carbon dioxide source (7). A liquid supply line (12) is fluidly connected to the liquid carbon dioxide source. A discharge line (17) discharges gas from the treatment chamber. The gas supply line and the liquid supply line are provided with a valve arrangement (28,22,29) for optional fluidic separation and establishing a flow connection with the treatment chamber.
   USE - Apparatus for processing pet food such as snakes and rodents e.g. mice or rat, and meat or fish product, for carnivores in zoo, zoological garden and animal shelter.
   ADVANTAGE - The treatment chamber is used as a refrigeration unit for quick freezing of fresh meat or fish products. Therefore, separate facilities are not needed for the freezing of freshly delivered meat or fish products and the gassing of live animals. The supply of the gas is very uniform and without the formation of turbulent flows, when killing the feed animals. The carbon dioxide content of the gas is sufficiently high, which causes the animals to lose consciousness quickly even at the beginning of the treatment. The control unit for controlling the gas flow is connected timer, which allows the supply of carbon dioxide in a controlled flow rate during a time determined by the timer, without requiring the intervention of an operator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for processing pet food for carnivores.
   Apparatus for processing pet food for carnivores (1)
   Treatment chamber (2)
   Access opening (4)
   Liquid carbon dioxide source (7)
   Gas supply line (11)
   Liquid supply line (12)
   Discharge line (17)
   Valve arrangement (28,22,29)

PT P
PN DE102020004542-A1; WO2022023387-A1
TI Selectively binding, transporting and storing            carbon dioxide in aqueous media, comprises contacting            gas containing carbon dioxide with acceptor solution            containing acceptor compound, and transporting and/or            storing acceptor solution.
AU DIETZ U
AE DIETZ U (DIET-Individual)
GA 202216320P
AB    NOVELTY - Selectively binding, transporting and storing                carbon dioxide in aqueous media, comprises (a)                providing an aqueous acceptor solution containing                at least one acceptor compound which has a free                guanidino and/or amidino group, (b) contacting a                gas containing carbon dioxide with the acceptor                solution until a carbon dioxide concentration of                the gas of less than 100 ppm is reached, and (c)                transporting and/or storing the acceptor solution                containing bound carbon dioxide/carbon dioxide                derivatives.
   USE - The method is useful for selectively binding,                transporting and storing carbon dioxide in aqueous                media.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the device for selectively binding, transporting                and storing carbon dioxide in aqueous media.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acceptor compound                  is an amino acid and the pH of the acceptor                  solution is 8-13. The receiving and releasing                  chamber contains a receiving and/or releasing                  medium containing at least one compound which                  carries at least one acid group and has an                  isoelectric point of 3-5. At least one reaction                  compounds for reacting and/or binding carbon                  dioxide and/or carbonate/bicarbonate anions are                  present in the accelerator solution and/or the                  uptake and/or release medium. Preferred Method:                  The dissolving and binding of carbon dioxide and                  its derivatives takes place without                  pressurization of the acceptor solution. The                  release of the carbon dioxide bound in the                  acceptor medium as a gas phase. The acceptor                  medium from step (b) is in or is introduced into                  an acceptor chamber of an electrodialysis device                  and the transport of carbon dioxide/carbon                  dioxide derivatives by an electrical gradient                  which runs between the acceptor chamber and an                  uptake and release chamber is produced, the                  acceptor chamber(s) and the uptake and release                  chamber(s) being separated from one another by a                  separating medium. The separation medium is a                  membrane which is permeable to ions and/or gas                  molecules. The carbon dioxide/carbon dioxide                  derivatives transported through the separation                  medium are released in the form of a pure carbon                  dioxide gas in the receiving and releasing                  chamber. The carbon dioxide bound in the acceptor                  solution is converted to a carbon compound by                  means of a reaction compound, after the step (b).                  The carbon dioxide bound in the absorption and/or                  release medium or the carbon dioxide transported                  and released is converted to a carbon compound by                  means of a reaction compound, after the step                  (c).

   USE - Media for air filter for sequestration of                carbon dioxide in residential, commercial and                industrial applications.

PT P
PN WO2020221600-A1; FR3095598-A1; FR3095598-B1
TI Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide            involves contacting carbon dioxide and sacrificial            compound with photocatalyst and activating            photocatalyst by irradiation to reduce carbon dioxide            and oxidize sacrificial compound.
AU FECANT A
   PAGIS C
   ANTOINE F
   CELINE P
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 2020A70775
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide in                a liquid phase and/or in a gas phase involves                contacting a charge-containing carbon dioxide and                at least one sacrificial compound with a                photocatalyst, subjecting the photocatalyst which                is not in electrical contact with field-generating                electrodes, to external electric field, and                irradiating the photocatalyst with at least one                irradiation source to produce at least one                wavelength absorbable by the photocatalyst so as to                reduce carbon dioxide and oxidize the sacrificial                compound in presence of the photocatalyst activated                by the irradiation source to produce an effluent                comprising 1C or more carbon molecules different                from carbon dioxide.
   USE - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is sustainable and more efficient,                and reduces carbon dioxide with improved                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic                  semiconductor is chosen from at least one element                  of group IVA, such as silicon, germanium, silicon                  carbide, and silicon-germanium, at least one                  element of group IIIA and VA, such as gallium                  phosphide, gallium nitride, indium phosphide, and                  indium gallium arsenide, at least one element of                  group IIB and VIA, such as cadmium sulfide, zinc                  oxide, and zinc sulfide, at least one element of                  group IB and VIIA, such as copper iodide and                  silver bromide, at least one element of group IVA                  and VIA, such as lead sulfide, lead oxide, tin                  sulfide, and lead tin telluride, at least one                  element of group VA and VIA, such as bismuth                  telluride and bismuth trioxide, at least one                  element of group IIB and VA, such as cadmium                  phosphide, zinc phosphide, and zinc arsenide, at                  least one element of group IB and VIA, such as                  copper oxide, copper(I) oxide, and silver                  sulfide, at least one element of group VIIIB and                  VIA, such as cobalt oxide, palladium oxide,                  iron(III) oxide, and nickel oxide, at least one                  element of group VIB and VIA, such as molybdenum                  disulfide and tungsten trioxide, at least one                  element of groups VB and VIA, such as vanadium                  pentoxide and niobium pentoxide, at least one                  element of group IVB and VIA, such as titanium                  dioxide and hafnium disulfide, at least one                  element of group IIIA and VIA, such as indium                  trioxide and indium(III) sulfide, at least one                  element of group VIA and lanthanides, such as                  cerium trioxide, praseodymium oxide,                  samarium(III) sulfide, terbium trisulfide, and                  lanthanum trisulfide, and at least one element of                  group VIA and actinides, such as uranium dioxide                  and uranium trioxide. The complex of transition                  metal is in the form of a central metal with                  varying oxidation degree and organic ligand, of                  formula: M(L)n (I).M = metallic element chosen from Ti, Cr, Mn,                  Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Ru, and Ir;L = organic ligand;andn = number of organic ligands.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When the photocatalytic                  reduction is carried out in a gas phase, the                  sacrificial compound is a gaseous compound chosen                  from water, ammonia, dihydrogen, methane, and                  alcohol. When the photocatalytic reduction is                  carried out in a liquid phase, the sacrificial                  compound is a liquid or solid oxidizable compound                  soluble in the liquid phase, chosen from water,                  ammonia, alcohol, liquid or solid aldehyde, and                  amine. A diluting fluid is further used during                  the contacting process and irradiation process.                  Preferred Conditions: The irradiation source is a                  natural or artificial irradiation source, and                  emits at a wavelength of 280-2500 nm. The                  photocatalyst is subjected to an electric field                  of 10 V/m to 100 kV/m. Preferred Components: The                  photocatalyst comprises an inorganic                  semiconductor, an organic semiconductor, or an                  organic-inorganic semiconductor, and/or a                  transition metal complex. The organic                  semiconductor is chosen from tetracene,                  anthracene, polythiophene, polystyrenesulfonate,                  phosphyrene, fullerene, and carbon nitride. The                  organic-inorganic semiconductor is chosen from                  MOF-type crystalline solids.

PT P
PN KR2019069000-A; KR2004690-B1
TI Carbon dioxide fixation method, comprises dissolving inorganic materials by treating inorganic waste with acid, extracting metal hydroxide, adding base to extract basic calcium compound, and mineralizing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas.
AU YOUN M H
   JEONG S K
   PARK K T
   KANG S P
   KIM H J
   LEE W H
   KIM Y E
   YOUN M
   JUNG S
   BAK K T
   KIMHAKJU
   KIM E Y
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2019525382
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide fixation method, comprises (a) dissolving inorganic materials by treating inorganic waste with acid, (b) extracting metal hydroxide from the dissolved inorganic materials, (c) adding base to extract basic calcium compound, and (d) mineralizing carbon dioxide by reaction of extracted calcium compound and carbon dioxide in exhaust gas.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide fixation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method collectively treats inorganic waste and exhaust gas generated in cement industry, provides high purity carbonate by extracting metal ions and fixing carbon dioxide, and reduces process cost for capturing carbon dioxide by regenerating absorption liquid used for absorption and mineralization of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base is metal hydroxide (MOH), where M is group 1 metal. The acid is hydrogen halide (HX), where X is halo ion. The separated calcium compound is calcium hydroxide, and has purity of greater than or equal to 95%. Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises gradually adjusting pH by adding base to the dissolved inorganic materials. In the step (b), metal of metal hydroxide is magnesium, aluminum and/or iron. The step (c) further comprises (c1) recovering metal halide (MX) generated as by-product in the step (c), (c2) electrolyzing the produced metal halide to regenerate metal hydroxide, and (c) introducing the produced metal hydroxide into step (b) or step (c). The step (c) is carried out by precipitating calcium compound by adjusting pH of the inorganic hydroxide extracted metal hydroxide to greater than or equal to 11. The step (d) comprises (d1) preparing acidic aqueous solution containing amine-based compound and acidic calcium compound, (d2) contacting acidic aqueous solution with exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide to produce primary calcium carbonate precipitate, and (d3) recovering calcium carbonate and adding the basic calcium compound to the remaining aqueous solution. The step (d) is carried out by continuously injecting carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN108333311-A; CN108333311-B
TI Method for obtaining fraction of plant inorganic carbon supplementation involves selecting plant which is grown in reference environment, utilizing photosynthetic apparatus followed by calculating relationship between photosynthetic rate.
AU WU Y
   LI H
   RAO S
   FANG L
   SU Y
   WANG S
   LIU C
AE INST GEOCHEMISTRY CHINESE ACAD SCI (GEOC-Non-standard)
GA 201860650S
AB    NOVELTY - A fraction of plant inorganic carbon supplementation obtaining method involves selecting a plant which is grown at the same time in the reference environment and the environment under investigation. Portable photosynthetic apparatus is utilized to determine the carbon dioxide response curve of photosynthesis of plant leaves. The data of the net photosynthetic rate and the extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration Ca are measured, the relationship between the net photosynthetic rate and the extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration is fitted. The relationship between the rate and the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide according to the relationship between the net photosynthetic rate, followed by calculating the relationship between the net photosynthetic rate and the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide on the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide, recovering the share of organic carbon.
   USE - Method for obtaining fraction of plant inorganic carbon supplementation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A share of plant inorganic carbon supplementation obtaining method involves selecting a plant which is grown at the same time in the reference environment and the environment under investigation and which is known to have a utilization fraction of bicarbonate ions in a reference environment. Portable photosynthetic apparatus is utilized to determine the carbon dioxide response curve of photosynthesis of plant leaves grown in the reference environment. The data of the net photosynthetic rate and the extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration are measured by the portable photosynthetic apparatus, the relationship between the net photosynthetic rate and the extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration is fitted by a right angle hyperbolic equation to obtain a net photosynthetic The relationship between the rate and the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide according to the relationship between the net photosynthetic rate and the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide, the net photosynthetic rate Pn100 when the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide is 100 mu mol.mol-1 followed by calculating the relationship between the net photosynthetic rate and the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide on the concentration of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide, and obtain the carboxylation efficiency of plant leaves for extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide and the equation of extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration, substituting the intercellular carbon dioxide concentration in the extracellular atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration to 100 mu mol.mol-1 into the carboxylation efficiency of the intercellular carbon dioxide and the intercellular carbon dioxide concentration equation of the plant leaves, according to the direct utilization fraction of the plant atmospheric carbon dioxide, obtaining the plant inorganic carbon supplementation reuse share, and according to the known bicarbonate ion utilization fraction fb of the plant to be inspected in the reference environment, the plant atmospheric carbon dioxide direct utilization fraction fDA is used to obtain the fraction r of the plant reuse organic carbon, recovering the share of organic carbon, plant direct use of carbon dioxide, obtaining plant bicarbonate ion utilization share.

PT P
PN KR2022058996-A
TI Method for operating carbon dioxide gas turbine,            involves supplying pure oxygen to combustor, cooling            exhaust gas with large amount of carbon dioxide            captured in liquid phase, and dissolving carbon dioxide            in water and supplying to hydrogen production            system.
AU YANG S
   YANG G W
AE YANG G W (YANG-Individual)
GA 202265199A
AB    NOVELTY - Operation of carbon dioxide gas turbine and a                carbon dioxide capture device involves separating                oxygen and nitrogen in the air produced by a                liquefaction separation method and using as a                refrigerant in a gas turbine, supplying pure oxygen                to the combustor, allowing the exhaust gas to flow                into the condenser, cooling with oxygen and                nitrogen refrigerants, collecting carbon dioxide in                the liquid phase, circulating exhaust with a large                amount of carbon dioxide captured in liquid phase                to prevent overheating of this combustor so that                the total amount of working fluid decreases as the                combustor is overheated and 78% nitrogen is removed                by oxy-fuel combustion while cooling the gas,                preheating carbon dioxide and circulating to the                combustor, dissolving remaining small amount of                carbon dioxide in water and supplying to the system                where hydrogen is produced, producing ammonia from                hydrogen produced in this system and nitrogen used                as a refrigerant, so that hydrogen can be stored in                ammonia.
   USE - Operation of carbon dioxide gas turbine and                carbon dioxide capture device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables collecting carbon dioxide                emitted from a gas turbine in a liquid phase with                nitrogen refrigerant, mass-producing hydrogen from                the captured carbon dioxide, producing ammonia from                the produced hydrogen and nitrogen used for carbon                dioxide capture, and generating clean energy                hydrogen so as to greatly improve thermal                efficiency of the gas turbine when ammonia is                stored in the liquid phase and condensed with the                exhaust gas in the condenser to produce                power.

AB    NOVELTY - The super critical carbon dioxide power                generation system comprises a carbon dioxide                injection unit (50) having a turbine (10), a cooler                (20), a compressor (30), and a heater (40), and                injecting carbon dioxide as a working fluid from a                carbon dioxide tank to a suction side of the                compressor. A heater flow path, which is provided                inside the heater, and formed to inject and heat                carbon dioxide from the compressor, and discharge                it to the turbine side. A seal gas flow path is                branched from the heater flow path, and guided to                be used as a seal gas by injecting a portion of                carbon dioxide heated in the heater flow path into                a dry gas seal portion of the compressor. A control unit                for controlling the opening amount of the seal gas                valve according to the temperature of the carbon                dioxide heated in the heater, is provided.

PN KR1931441-B1
TI Carbon-dioxide generation and temperature controller for use in facility for cultivating vegetables, has carbon-dioxide storing unit fixed with first valve, and control unit for controlling operation of second valve through first valve.
AU HAN Y J
AE HAN Y J (HANY-Individual)
GA 2018A2876D
AB    NOVELTY - The controller (10) has a carbon-dioxide storing unit (200) for discharging methanol combustion hot air within an equipment house. A carbon-dioxide storing unit is arranged in an upstream of a first damper valve (310). An outlet of the methanol combustion hot air is fixed with the first damper valve. A downstream of a second damper valve (320) is installed fixed in a third damper valve (330) that is connected to a cooling heat exchanger. The cooling heat exchanger (230) is arranged in an upstream of the first damper valve. The carbon-dioxide storing unit is fixed with a fourth damper valve (340). A control unit controls operation of the first damper valve through the fourth damper valve.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide generation and temperature controller for use in an artificial facility for cultivating vegetables or flowering grasses or tropical plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The controller controls carbon-dioxide concentration and temperature of the equipment house without separating carbon-dioxide generator and a hot blast heater. The controller reduces management cost with environment-friendly. The controller supplies the carbon-dioxide within the equipment house, thus saving redundancy carbon-dioxide in the carbon-dioxide storing unit. The controller efficiently utilizes energy as the operation of the methanol combustion hot air is reduced and greenhouse gas is recycled to photosynthesis of plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon-dioxide generation and temperature controller.
   Carbon-dioxide generation and temperature controller (10)
   Carbon-dioxide storing unit (200)
   Cooling heat exchanger (230)
   Damper valves (310,320,330,340)

TI Textile packing for removing carbon dioxide from            gas, comprises hydrophilic fibers and a support            structure holds the hydrophilic fibers and a top end            and a bottom end of at least a portion of the            hydrophilic fibers are fluidly connected.
AU SALMON S
   YUAN Y
   SHEN J
AE UNIV NORTH CAROLINA STATE (UYNC-C)
GA 2022D3027N
AB    NOVELTY - Textile packing (12) comprises: (a)                hydrophilic fibers; and (b) a support structure,                where the support structure holds the hydrophilic                fibers and a top end and a bottom end of at least a                portion of the hydrophilic fibers are fluidly                connected.
   USE - The textile packing is useful for removing                carbon dioxide from gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The textile packing has high activity yield,                low cost and low environmental impact.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                removing carbon dioxide from a gas, comprising (a1)                feeding a first carbon dioxide-rich gas to a first                reactor, (b1) feeding a carbon dioxide-lean                absorption liquid to the first reactor, (c1)                reacting carbon dioxide in the first carbon                dioxide-rich gas with a component of the carbon                dioxide-lean absorption liquid as the first carbon                dioxide-rich gas and the carbon dioxide-lean                absorption liquid flow through a first reaction                zone to form a carbon dioxide-lean gas and a carbon                dioxide-rich absorption liquid, (d1) removing the                carbon dioxide-lean gas from the first reactor, and                (e1) removing the carbon dioxide-rich absorption                liquid from the first reactor, where the first                reactor comprises the first reaction zone, the                first reaction zone contains a gas-liquid contact                enhancer, and the gas-liquid contact enhancer                comprises at least one of the textile                packings.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                conventional surface wetted solid packing with                liquid travelling along the outside packing                surfaces.
   4Liquid
   6Carbon dioxide gas
   10Semi-solid packing
   12Textile packing
   14Semi-permeable coating
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the process: the                  component of carbon dioxide-lean absorption                  liquid comprises aqueous alkanolamines comprising                  monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA),                  N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA),                  2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol (Tris or                  AHPD), diglycolamine (DGA), 1-amino-2-propanol                  (A2P), 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol (AMP),                  methylmonoethanolamine (MMEA),                  dimethylmonoethanolamine (DMMEA),                  diethylmonoethanolamine (DEMEA), diisopropanol                  amine (DIPA), triisopropanolamine (TIPA), aqueous                  soluble salts (e.g. sodium or potassium salts) of                  N-methylaminopropionic acid or                  N,N-dimethylaminoacetic acid or N-methylalanine,                  N-methylglycine (sarcosine), N,N-diethylglycine,                  N,N-dimethylglycine (DMG), &#946; -alanine                  (3-aminopropanoic acid) or other natural or                  modified amino acids (e.g. N-substituted amino                  acid derivatives), 2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol                  (AEE), triethanolamine (TEA), aqueous soluble                  salts of glycine (e.g. sodium or potassium                  glycinate) and taurine, where the absorption                  liquid and the component are aqueous solutions                  comprising sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  lithium hydroxide, alkali-metal carbonate salts                  (e.g. lithium, sodium, potassium, or ammonium),                  alkali-metal bicarbonate salts, alkali-metal                  phosphate salts, or borate salts, seawater or pH                  adjusted seawater, industrial process water                  comprising divalent cations or pH adjusted                  industrial process water comprising divalent                  cations, saline aquifer water or pH adjusted                  saline aquifer water, aqueous ammonium (NH4OH);                  and/or aqueous electrolyte solutions and                  promoters; the component of the carbon                  dioxide-lean absorption liquid comprises                  potassium carbonate in an amount ranging from 0.5                  wt.% to 30 wt.%, where the component of the                  carbon dioxide-lean absorption liquid comprises                  N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), in content of less                  than 50 wt.%, the component of the carbon                  dioxide-lean absorption liquid comprises                  N,N-dimethylglycine (DMG), in a content of less                  than 30 wt.%; the absorption liquid comprises                  preservatives and/or antimicrobial agents, where                  the absorption liquid comprises Proxel(RTM: Broad                  spectrum biocide), penicillin, and/or nanosilver;                  a source of the first carbon dioxide-rich gas is                  natural gas, biogas, industrial process gas,                  combustion flue gas, contained environments,                  respiration gas, and ambient air; the first                  carbon dioxide-rich gas comprises 1-10000 ppm                  carbon dioxide, or 0.1-10 vol.%, or 1-80 vol.%;                  the percent of carbon dioxide absorbed [defined                  as the (vol.% carbon dioxide in the first carbon                  dioxide-rich gas - vol% carbon dioxide in the                  carbon dioxide-lean gas)/(vol.% carbon dioxide in                  the first carbon dioxide-rich gas)x 100] is at                  least 10%; a diameter of the first reactor ranges                  in size of 1 cm to 10 m; a flow rate of the                  carbon dioxide-lean absorption liquid divided by                  a crosssectional area of the first reactor of                  0.1-5000 L/minute.m2; a flow rate of the first                  carbon dioxide-rich gas divided by a                  cross-sectional area of the first reactor of                  60-2000000 L/minute.m2; the first carbon                  dioxide-rich gas comprises ambient air; and the                  second gas-liquid contact enhancer comprises the                  textile packing, where the textile packing                  comprises (a2) hydrophilic fibers, (b2) an                  optional active enzyme, and (c2) a support                  structure, where the active enzyme is attached to                  the hydrophilic fibers, and the support structure                  holds the hydrophilic fibers and a top end and a                  bottom end of at least a portion of the                  hydrophilic fibers are fluidly connected; the                  carbon dioxide-lean absorption liquid comprises                  an active enzyme, where the active enzyme is an                  oxidoreductase, a transferase, a hydrolase, a                  lyase, an isomerase, and a ligase; and the                  absorption liquid flows down through the first                  reaction zone and the gas flows up                  (counter-current), down (co-current), or                  substantially perpendicular to the flow of the                  absorption liquid through the first reaction                  zone, where the absorption liquid flows down                  through the first reaction zone and the gas flows                  up (counter-current) or down (co-current).                  Preferred Process: The process further comprises:                  (f) feeding the carbon dioxide-rich absorption                  liquid to a pond; (g) releasing carbon dioxide                  from the carbon dioxide-rich absorption liquid as                  the growth medium for a biological system; (h)                  recovering the carbon dioxide-lean absorption                  liquid from the pond; (i) feeding the carbon                  dioxide-rich absorption liquid near a top of a                  second reactor; (j) releasing carbon dioxide to a                  second carbon dioxide-rich gas by a reverse                  reaction of the component of the carbon                  dioxide-rich absorption liquid as the second                  carbon dioxide-rich gas and the carbon                  dioxide-rich absorption liquid flow through a                  second reaction zone to form the second carbon                  dioxide-rich gas and the carbon dioxide-lean                  absorption liquid; (k) removing the second carbon                  dioxide-rich gas from the second reactor; and (l)                  removing the carbon dioxide-lean absorption                  liquid near the bottom of the second reactor,                  where the second reactor comprises the second                  reaction zone, and the second reaction zone                  contains a second gas-liquid contact                  enhancer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the textile                  packing: at least a portion of the hydrophilic                  fibers comprise cellulosic fiber, protein fiber,                  polyamide fiber, acetate fiber, triacetate fiber,                  modified cellulosic fiber, acrylic fiber,                  modacrylic fiber, polyvinyl alcohol fiber                  (vinal), poly(ethylene oxide) (PEO) fiber,                  crosslinked poly(ethylene glycol) diacrylate                  fiber, polyester fiber, hydrophilic modified                  polyester fiber, poly(lactic acid) fiber,                  poly(hydroxyalkanoate) fiber, and/or                  poly(etheretherketone) (PEEK) fiber, cotton,                  jute, flax, hemp, ramie, viscose (rayon),                  lyocell, silk, wool, nylon, aromatic polyamide                  (aramid), cellulose acetate, acrylic, modacrylic,                  polyalanine (PANI), poly(phenylenediamine),                  chitaline, polyvinyl pyrrolidone (PVP),                  crosslinked polyvinylpyrrolidone and/or PEEK; Preferred Components: In the textile                  packing: a filament, a yarn and/or a textile                  comprises the hydrophilic fibers; a diameter of                  the candy roll is 1 cm to 10 m, and where the                  height of the textile packing is 1 cm to 30 m; a                  textile comprises the hydrophilic fibers, where                  the support structure comprises multiple rigid                  rods attached vertically across the textile, and                  the textile packing is in the shape of a candy                  roll formed by winding the rigid-rod-attached                  textile in the horizontal direction; total weight                  of the textile packing, on a dry basis, is less                  than 50 wt.% of glass Raschig ring packing of an                  equivalent volume, where a total weight of the                  textile packing, on a wet basis, is less than 80                  wt.% of the glass Raschig ring packing of an                  equivalent volume; the filament, the yarn, and/or                  the textile comprises immobilized antibiotic or                  metal nanoparticles or protease; at least a                  portion of the hydrophilic fibers are fluidly                  connected in a path from a top of the textile                  packing to a bottom of the textile packing in a                  shape that is substantially linear, a zig-zag in                  the vertical direction, or vertical cork-screw;                  the active enzyme is an oxidoreductase, a                  transferase, a hydrolase, a lyase, an isomerase,                  and/or a ligase, where the active enzyme                  comprises a carbonic anhydrase; the active enzyme                  attachment is entrapment in the hydrophilic                  fibers, entrapment in a polymeric coating on the                  hydrophilic fibers, entrapment in a chitosan                  material coating on the hydrophilic fibers,                  covalent bonding to the hydrophilic fibers,                  covalent bonding to the polymeric coating, and/or                  covalent bonding to the chitosan material                  coating; the active enzyme                  attachment is entrapment in a chitosan material                  coating on the hydrophilic fibers, and/or                  covalent bonding to the chitosan material coating                  and where a mass ratio of the chitosan to the                  active enzyme (on a dry basis) of 0.1-10000; a                  filament, a yarn and/or a textile comprises the                  hydrophilic fibers, and where a weight ratio of                  the filament, the yarn and/or the textile to the                  active enzyme is 1-20000 g/g on a dry basis; retained enzyme                  activity after 10 cycles of washing the textile                  packing in a Tris buffer (pH of 7.2) and drying                  the textile packing is at least 20% of an initial                  enzyme activity; and a retained enzyme activity                  after 10 days incubated continuously in 30% MDEA                  (pH 10.5) at 45&#176; C in a dry bath orbital                  shaker set at 120 rpm is at least 20% of an                  initial enzyme activity.

PN WO2022131381-A1; JP2022094607-A; JP2022094608-A
TI Carbon dioxide transport container placed in cargo transport container, has flexible inner tube that is arranged in tank portion in state in which blow port is connected to one end of tank portion, and is expanded and contracted within tank portion which is disassembled.
AU AKIRA M
   MATSUMIYA A
AE JTSS LTD (JTSS-Non-standard)
   MATSUMIYA A (MATS-Individual)
   MATSUMIYA T (MATS-Individual)
GA 202283844R
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide transport container (2) has a container main portion (4) that is arranged inside a rigid outer support portion (1). The container main portion is comprised with a rigid cylindrical tank portion (6) and a flexible inner tube which is provided for storing carbon dioxide. The flexible inner tube is formed in a balloon shape, and is arranged in the tank portion in a state in which a blow port is connected to one end of the tank portion, and is expanded and contracted within the tank portion. The tank portion is disassembled.
   USE - Carbon dioxide transport container placed in cargo transport container for transporting carbon dioxide from thermal power plant to remote reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide transport container is provided with high utilization efficiency of loading space, when carbon dioxide is not stored. The carbon dioxide is discharged from the carbon dioxide transport vessel in a short time. Since the tank portion is disassembled and the storage space is reduced, the storage space when not used as carbon dioxide transport container is reduced. Hence the utilization efficiency of the storage space is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide transport method; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide discharge method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the carbon dioxide transport container.
   Rigid outer support portion (1)
   Carbon dioxide transport container (2)
   Container main portion (4)
   Rigid cylindrical tank portion (6)
   End side cap (16)

AU ZHANG H
   ZHAN L
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 201864246F
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-based reactive polyurethane hot-melt adhesive comprises 60-80 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based glycol, 5-10 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based tetraol (I), 16-24 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 8-15 pts. wt. tackifying resin, 1-2 pts. wt. antioxidant, and 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. catalyst.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based reactive polyurethane hot-melt adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based reactive polyurethane hot-melt adhesive has excellent adhesiveness, heat resistance, storage stability and environmental aging resistance, and quick surface drying rate. The crosslinked cured hot-melt adhesive can be easily removed from the surface of the bonding material without any residues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide-based reactive polyurethane hot-melt adhesive comprises 60-80 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based glycol, 5-10 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based tetraol of formula (I), 16-24 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 8-15 pts. wt. tackifying resin, 1-2 pts. wt. antioxidant, and 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. catalyst.
   x-z = 1-50;and
   m,n,p = 1-80.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of carbon dioxide-based reactive polyurethane hot-melt adhesive, which involves mixing carbon dioxide-based glycol and carbon dioxide-based tetraol in a reactor, vacuum-dehydrating at 100-110 degrees C for 1-2.5 hours, cooling to 75-80 degrees C, adding diisocyanate, stirring under nitrogen atmosphere, reacting for 2-3 hours, adding a tackifying resin, an antioxidant and a catalyst, vacuuming at 110-130 degrees C until no bubbles appear, and discharging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The diisocyanate is chosen from 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane-4,4'-diisocyanate, naphthalene-1,5-diisocyanate, benzomethylene diisocyanate, 4,4'-diphenyl-dimethylmethane diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane-4,4'-diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, cyclohexane-1,4-diisocyanate, and hexane-1,6-diisocyanate. The antioxidant is tea polyphenol, tocopherol, flavonoids, butylated hydroxyanisole, butylated hydroxytoluene (antioxidant BHT), or tert-butyl hydroquinone, preferably pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate) (antioxidant 1010), 2,6-di-t-butyl-4-methyl phenol (antioxidant 264), tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphate (antioxidant 2112), butylated hydroxytoluene, pentaerythritol bis-(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)diphosphite (antioxidant 626), 2-mercapto-4(5)-methylbenzimidazole (antioxidant AO-60) or phenolic antioxidant (antioxidant AO-50).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide-based tetraol includes 45-75% carbonate unit, has number average molecular weight of 1500-5000 Da, and is obtained by treating carbon dioxide at 50-80 degrees C in a reactor, cooling to room temperature, adding dimethyl carbonate and citric acid to the reactor, stirring under carbon dioxide atmosphere, treating the mixture at 50-80 degrees C, vacuum-filling with carbon dioxide, cooling to room temperature, adding an epoxy compound monomer, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide through a carbon dioxide pressure regulator, and polymerizing in an autoclave in a constant temperature bath. The carbon dioxide-based glycol is of formula (II), includes 45-75% carbonate unit and has number average molecular weight of 1500-5000 Da. The tackifying resin is a rosin resin, a petroleum resin or a terpene resin.a,b = 1-60.

The LNG low pressure pump is arranged between the LNG storage tank and the carbon dioxide liquefying device. The gas outlet of the carbon dioxide liquefying device is connected with the natural gas steam reforming hydrogen production device. The gas outlet of the natural gas steam reforming hydrogen production device is connected with the pressure swing adsorption device. The product gas outlet of the pressure swing adsorption device is connected with the hydrogen collecting device. The exhaust outlet is connected with the carbon dioxide liquefying device liquid the outlet of the carbon dioxide liquefying device is connected with the liquid carbon dioxide collecting device.
   USE - System used for producing hydrogen and liquid carbon dioxide by LNG.
   ADVANTAGE - The system solves the carbon dioxide emission problem of natural gas hydrogen production, realizes the synchronous lifting of the utilization rate of the process system substance and the energy utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for a method for producing hydrogen and liquid carbon dioxide by using the system, which involves:
   (A) passing LNG in the LNG storage tank into the carbon dioxide liquefaction device for heat exchange to obtain gaseous natural gas;
   (B) passing natural gas into the natural gas steam reforming hydrogen production unit, and hydrogen and carbon dioxide are obtained through natural gas steam reforming and carbon monoxide conversion; and
   (C) passing hydrogen and carbon dioxide into the pressure swing adsorption device, purifying and separating to obtain hydrogen, which is collected by the hydrogen collecting device, and passing generated purge gas to the carbon dioxide liquefaction in the device, where the liquid carbon dioxide is obtained by liquefying the cold energy generated by LNG gasification, and collecting by the liquid carbon dioxide collecting device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system.
   LNG storage tank (1)
   Carbon dioxide liquefying device (3)
   Pressure swing adsorption device (4)
   Natural gas pre-heater (5)
   Desulfurization device (6)
   Natural gas heater (7)
   Natural gas conversion hydrogen production device (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The LNG is pressurized to 0.5-5.0 megaPascal and then input to the carbon dioxide liquefaction unit. The temperature of the natural gas is -10-25 degrees C. The natural gas is preheated to 200-250 degrees C, heated to 380-480 degrees C after desulfurization, and then steam reforming of the natural gas is performed to obtain reformed gas. The reformed gas is heated to 250-320oC, the carbon monoxide conversion is performed to obtain the hydrogen and carbon dioxide.

The stirring member is located in the infusion tube. The liquid adding pump is connected with the second liquid inlet of the infusion tube. The liquid outlet of the infusion tube is connected with fracturing pump vehicle.
   USE - Carbon dioxide anhydrous fracturing system for sensitive unconventional tight oil and gas reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the success rate of the fracturing and the supporting effect of the proppant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide anhydrous fracturing method, which involves utilizing the system, providing the proppant into the sand mixing tank to clean and dry the carbon dioxide anhydrous fracturing system, cooling the proppant in the sand mixing tank so that the temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide in the sand mixing tank is consistent with the temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide transport vehicles, reducing the temperature in fracturing pump trucks so that the temperature in fracturing pump trucks is consistent with the temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide transportation trucks, liquid carbon dioxide is output from carbon dioxide transport vehicles, separating gas in the liquid carbon dioxide through a gas-liquid separation tank and pressurizing separated liquid carbon dioxide by a booster pump, entering pressurized liquid carbon dioxide into the sand mixing tank, and transporting pressurized liquid carbon dioxide into the fracturing pump trucks through the sand mixing tank, and passing through fracturing pump trucks, when the displacement of the booster pump reaches the preset value, adding carbon dioxide thickener to the infusion pipe connected to the sand mixing tank through the liquid addition pump, and the stirring member arranged in the infusion pipe at the bottom of the sand mixing tank stirs the proppant coming out of the sand mixing tank, and combining the stirred proppant with carbon dioxide liquid, and injecting liquid carbon dioxide and proppant into the oil well through the fracturing pump trucks to complete the liquid injection and sand injection operations of the oil well.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide anhydrous fracturing system.
   Carbon dioxide transport vehicle (1)
   Gas-liquid separating tank (2)
   Booster pump (3)
   Sand mixing tank (4)
   Liquid adding pump (5)

The waste heat of the low-temperature electrolysis is transferred using the heat pump by at least one working medium flows at least one temperature levels above the electrolysis temperature level. The working fluid stream is formed using at least one working fluids comprising or comprising at least one hydrocarbon, alcohol and/or using a sorption heat pump. The processes associated with the further process steps for further processing at least a portion of the gas product is or will be used.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano level traditional Chinese                medicine external suspension gel involves removing                the impurity from compound traditional Chinese                medicine, cleaning, then using carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction, collecting traditional                Chinese medicine volatile oil; drying the compound                traditional Chinese medicine residue of carbon                dioxide supercritical extraction; grinding the                compound traditional Chinese medicine residue after                drying by a planetary ball mill until the particle                is &lt; 100 nm, obtaining the compound                traditional Chinese medicine powder; adding                according to the 0.5-2 wt.% Carbomer940, 0.1-0.4                wt.% poloxamer, 0.8 wt.% soybean lecithin, 1-6 wt.%                propylene glycol, 0-0.8 wt.% polysorbate 80, 0-0.01                wt.% ethylparaben, little distilled water to swell,                then adding 0.3-2-5 % compound traditional Chinese                medicine powder, 0.3-2.5 % traditional Chinese                medicine volatile oil; adding 20 % triethanolamine                to adjust the pH to 5.5-6.5, finally adding                distilled water to fix the volume to 100 %;                ultrasonically mixing for 10-20 minutes; high                shearing homogenizing, shearing rate is 15000 rpm,                homogenizing time is 15 minutes; and filling the                bottle to seal the finished product and                packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide                  supercritical extraction condition comprises                  extraction temperature, which is 30-70                  &#176;C, the pressure is 14-42 MPa, and the                  extracting time is 1-5 hours. PT P
PN EP4019166-A1
TI Producing metal powder for additive manufacturing process, involves atomizing metal by directing jet of fluid towards stream of molten metal, in which fluid comprises water and carbon dioxide.
AU DUBIEZ S
   KAPLAN B
   DAHLSTROM M
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202284265G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing metal powder involves providing a stream of molten metal (203), and atomizing the metal by directing jet (205) of fluid towards the stream, where the fluid comprises water and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is dissolved in the water, and the fluid further comprises carbon monoxide. The ratio of carbon dioxide to water is greater than or equal to 1 wt.%. The jet is supplied through a convergent-divergent nozzle (206). The fluid is supplied at 100-1000 bar pressure, and at 600-850 l/minute. The metal comprises iron or iron alloy. The powder comprises spherical solid particles having an aspect ratio of 1-1.2.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a metal powder for additive manufacturing process, where the metal powder is useful in additive manufacturing processes including selective laser melting (SLM), direct metal laser sintering (DMLS), selective laser sintering, fused deposition modeling (FDM), or fused filament fabrication (FFF).
   ADVANTAGE - The presence of the spherical or near-spherical particles allows for an enhanced powder flowability and better bulk density which, in turn, will lead to a better quality of the finished product. Smooth round particles possess better packing properties and thus spreading more evenly on the powder bed and form a uniform layer. When the gas is dissolved in water, for instance by way of pre-mixing with water prior to contact with the molten metal, it allows for better interaction of metal particles with the carbon dioxide in the atomizing medium, thus leading to a more efficient metal particle formation. By pressurizing the fluid and maintaining the flow of 600-850 l/minute it is possible to achieve high fluid velocities before colliding the fluid with a molten stream of metal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a rough schematic view of the carbonated water atomizer.
   Atomizer (200)
   Furnace (202)
   Molten metal (203)
   Jet (205)
   Nozzle (206)
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).); S06 (Electrophotography and Photography); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC M22-H01; M22-H03B; P53-C01; P53-C03; P53-V06A; S06-G10; X25-A08C2; X25-A08C3; X25-A08M1; X25-A09

PN CN114622874-A
TI Method for removing blockage and increasing production of liquid carbon dioxide in coal bed gas development well, involves selecting artificial crack in coal reservoir around wellbore, and removing gas extraction wellhead of well.
AU WANG Z

AE GUIZHOU OIL & GAS EXPLORATION DEV ENG (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202283233X
AB    NOVELTY - Removing blockage and increasing production of liquid carbon dioxide in coal bed gas development well comprises selecting an artificial crack (3) in coal reservoir (2) around the well bore, selecting a coal seam gas development wells (1) and a natural (4) as the liquid CO2 injection well, removing the gas extraction wellhead of coal-bed methane development well, using the working machine to generate the extraction pipe column in the well cylinder of the coal bed methane development wells, entering into the coal seam well cylinder into the well washing oil pipe, using positive circular washing way to inject water from the well cleaning oil pipe and a technical sleeve (6) are annularly returned. The ground is connected with the drainage collecting device and the gas and water pipeline, continuing the drainage and gas collection work of the coal bed gas development well.
   USE - Method for removing blockage and increasing production of liquid carbon dioxide in a coal bed gas development well of near-well zone coal reservoir crack blockage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the problem of bad adaptability to the coal-bed methane development well and poor effect of the de-plugging and yield-increasing technique. It uses the volume expansion force generated by the decompression and gasification of the liquid carbon dioxide in the coal storage layer, the artificial crack and the natural crack in the clay storage layer around the well cylinder are opened again. Using the liquid CO2 has strong sand carrying ability, high efficient support for crack, it has good coal bed gas development well plugging and increase yield increasing effect.

PT P
PN CN112812794-A
TI Carbon dioxide returning process for production of semi-coke coke oven gas by performing desulfurization on coke oven gas of semi-coke oven, performing conversion treatment, decarbonizing and mixing combustion gas with oxygen-enriched air.
AU WANG Y
   SHEN J
AE SICHUAN ALLY HI-TECH CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202159387U
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide returning process for production of semi-coke coke oven gas involves (1) performing desulfurization processing on the coke oven gas of the semi-coke oven, (2) performing a conversion treatment on the desulfurized semi-coke oven gas and converting the carbon monoxide in the semi-coke oven gas into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, (3) decarbonizing the converted semi-coke oven gas and reducing the carbon dioxide content in the coke oven gas of the semi-coke oven to obtain a combustion gas and (4) mixing the combustion gas with oxygen-enriched air and returning to the dry distillation furnace.
   USE - As carbon dioxide returning process for production of semi-coke coke oven gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process overcomes the problem of low quality of raw gas produced by the dry distillation of the semi-coke oven, adopts a carbon return technology for coke oven gas production, reduces the nitrogen content of the combustion-supporting air of the coke oven and the nitrogen content in the coke oven gas of the semi-coke oven, improves the coal gas quality of the semi-coke oven and utilization rate of the coke oven gas quality and utilizes the semi-coke production system that ensures the quality of the semi-coke.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a semi-coke production system comprising a semi-coke dry distillation unit for preparing the semi-carbon finished product and a coke oven gas treatment unit for processing raw gas generated in the semi-coke dry distillation unit to obtain coal tar and coke oven gas, where the semi-coke oven gas is used for producing the carbon return unit, the coke oven gas is treated, the quality of the semi-coke oven gas is improved and the coke oven gas is returned to the semi-coke dry distillation unit for reuse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The semi-coke dry distillation unit comprises a coal bin, a crushing device, a belt conveying device, a dry distillation furnace, a coke pushing machine, a coke quenching tank, a scraper, a dryer and a screening device. The coke oven gas treatment unit comprises a collection cover, a gas collection tank, a venturi tower, a cyclone plate type spray tower, an electric tar trap, a gas fan and a tar storage tank. The coke oven gas production return carbon unit comprises a desulfurization tower, a transformation furnace, a decarburization tower, a mixing tank, a PSA oxygen generator and an air blower. The air blower and the gas blower are also respectively connected to the dry distillation furnace.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves adopting a semi-coke dry distillation process, finally preparing a semi-coke finished product, where the coke oven gas treatment process involves processing raw gas generated in the semi-coke dry distillation process and separating to obtain coal tar and coke oven gas; and the carbon dioxide returning process for production of semi-coke coke oven gas involves processing the coke oven gas, improving the quality of the semi-coke oven gas and returning to the semi-coke dry distillation process for reuse. The semi-coke dry distillation process involves preparing raw material coal, feeding the raw coal into a dry distillation to perform dry distillation, feeding the raw gas produced by the dry distillation to the semi-finished product of the semi-finished coke oven gas treatment process, cooling the semi-finished product obtained by dry distillation, processing the semi-finished coke oven gas by the semi-coke oven gas treatment process to obtain the semi-finished coke oven gas as a drying medium and drying the semi-finished semi-coke product. The coke oven gas treatment process involves collecting the raw gas, cooling the raw gas and separating to obtain coal tar, where the raw gas is re-electrically captured to obtain coke oven gas and returning a portion of the coke oven gas to the semi-coke dry distillation process step.

PN CN112320834-A
TI Chip used e.g. for preparing nano zinc oxide with multi-appearances, has substrate, and material layer i.e. inlet channel of reactant and outlet channel of product and gas layer of reactant that is set with channel of carbon dioxide gas.
AU XIE S
   CUI D
   ZHU J
   XU Y
   YANG D
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 202115235P
AB    NOVELTY - A chip comprises substrate, and material layer and gas layer (1) of reactant. The material layer is provided with inlet channel of reactant and outlet channel of product. The gas layer is provided with channel of carbon dioxide gas. The channel portion is separated by ventilating and waterproof film to obtain chip with microchannel. The microchannel chip is used as reaction container to prepare nano zinc oxide with multi-appearances.
   USE - The chip is used as reaction medium for preparing nano zinc oxide with multi-appearances (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chip can be industrialized and simply change reaction condition, is convenient to collect, and has simple device, small test dosage, high sensitivity and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for preparing chip comprising designing and drawing micro-structure and microchannel pattern of each layer in chip by computer aided design software, processing surface of each layer of chip substrate by micro-processing technology, using adhesive film, aligning, bonding, pressurizing and bonding prepared each layer of chip, and forming final interlayer structure chip with ventilating and waterproof film; and
   (2) a method for preparing nano zinc oxide with multi-appearances using chip comprising dispersing primary zinc oxide in water to prepare 1-10 wt.% dispersion, injecting dispersion and 1-5 wt.% salt solution into reactant injection inlet by injection pump, adjusting flow of injection pump so that reactant has enough residence time in chip, introducing carbon dioxide gas into gas injection port, keeping carbon dioxide flow rate at 10-20 m/second, fully reacting carbon dioxide with zinc oxide solution through isolation film, collecting product at product outlet, separating, drying, and calcining at 600 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of upper-layer chip.
   Gas layer (1)

The substrate is polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA), polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), polycarbonate (PC), glass and/or silicon chip. The ventilating and waterproof film is PTFE film, silica gel film and/or polyester film film.

PN CN110123903-A
TI Volatile traditional Chinese medicine suppository useful for treating vaginitis, comprises film former, adhesive, curing agent, volatile oil and ultrapure water.
AU LIU G
AE LIU G (LIUG-Individual)
GA 2019736049
AB    NOVELTY - Volatile traditional Chinese medicine suppository comprises 20-50 pts. wt. film former, 80-120 pts. wt. adhesive, 200-400 pts. wt. curing agent, 300-750 pts. wt. volatile oil and 600-900 pts. wt. ultrapure water.
   USE - The volatile traditional Chinese medicine suppository is useful for treating vaginitis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The volatile traditional Chinese medicine suppository has high active ingredient content, good therapeutic effect on vaginal inflammation caused by microorganisms and releases the drug rapidly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing volatile traditional Chinese medicine suppository, comprising (i) taking Myristica fragrans, Pseudolarix kaempferi, Coptis chinensis, Mosla chinensis, Citrus medica, Rubus parvifolius root, Zanthoxylum bungeanum and Chrysanthemum, mixing uniformly, crushing into a powder of 80-100 meshes, adding pure water according to the solid-liquid mass ratio 1:0.5-1.2, carrying out microwave treatment and soaking for 3-5 hours to obtain pretreatment mixture; (ii) placing the pretreatment mixture in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction kettle, turning on the carbon dioxide high pressure pump to inject high pressure carbon dioxide gas into the extraction tank at a flow rate of 2-8 l/hour, increasing the pressure in the extraction tank to 25-40 MPa, maintaining the pressure inside the kettle unchanged and separating the carbon dioxide gas from the extract in the analytical tank when the high pressure carbon dioxide gas containing the extract enters the temperature to 40-45 degrees C and 4-6 MPa in the analytical kettle to obtain volatile oil; and (iii) adding polyvinylpyrrolidone, gelatin, Bletilla striata polysaccharide, Aloe polysaccharide and ultrapure water into the container according to the mass ratio, placing the container in a 60 degrees C water bath and stirring until dissolved, adding sodium alginate and stirring until dissolved, reducing the temperature of the solution to 53-55 degrees C, adding calcium chloride, stirring uniformly and adding volatile oil, stirring uniformly and pouring into the mold and curing in an oven at 40 degrees C for 4-5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The film former is polyvinylpyrrolidone. The adhesive comprises 30-50 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 10-20 pts. wt. gelatin, 30-35 pts. wt. Bletilla striata polysaccharide and 10-15 pts. wt. Aloe polysaccharide. The curing agent is calcium chloride. The volatile oil comprises 20-30 pts. wt. Myristica fragrans, 30-50 pts. wt. Pseudolarix kaempferi, 10-20 pts. wt. Coptis chinensis, 15-30 pts. wt. Mosla chinensis, 40-60 pts. wt. Citrus medica, 10-20 pts. wt. Rubus parvifolius root, 10-20 pts. wt. Zanthoxylum bungeanum and 10-20 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the microwave processing is carried out for 100-120 seconds with a power of 800-950 W.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

TI System for producing hydrocarbon fuel, comprises            gas phase oxidation reactor configured to receive flue            gas stream comprising nitrogen oxide and carbon            dioxide, chlorine and liquid to produce acids,            electrolytic unit, and Sabatier reactor.
AU MUELLER B
AE ISCA MANAGEMENT LTD (ISCA-Non-standard)
   ISCA MANAGEMENT LTD (ISCA-Non-standard)
   ISCA MANAGEMENT LTD (ISCA-Non-standard)
   ISCA MANAGEMENT LTD (ISCA-Non-standard)
   ISCA MANAGEMENT LTD (ISCA-Non-standard)
   ISCA MAN LTD (ISCA-Non-standard)
GA 201851469C
AB    NOVELTY - A system (100) comprises a gas phase oxidation                (GPO) reactor (140) configured to receive a flue                gas stream (110) comprising nitrogen oxide species                and carbon dioxide gas and further to receive                chlorine gas (130), liquid or solution and to                oxidize the nitrogen oxide species in the flue gas                stream to produce a gas stream comprising nitric                acid and hydrochloric acid, an electrolytic unit                (160') configured to receive the hydrochloric acid                and to electrolyze the hydrochloric acid to produce                hydrogen gas and chlorine gas, and a Sabatier                reactor configured to receive both a gas stream,                downstream from the GPO reactor and portion(s) of                the hydrogen gas from the electrolytic unit and                further to hydrogenate the carbon dioxide gas in                the gas stream into a hydrocarbon fuel comprising                methane (112).
   USE - System e.g. flue gas treatment system used for                producing hydrocarbon fuel e.g. methane to produce                liquefied natural gas (all claimed) in coal-firing                facility to generate heat and power. Can also be                used as fuel for automobile and domestic                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can efficiently remove greenhouse                gases such as sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides,                hydrogen sulfides, hydrochloric acid, water vapor,                carbon dioxide and heavy metals from flue gases and                provide hydrocarbon fuel by Sabatier reaction, and                is suitable for large scale productions. The                hydrocarbon fuel is compressed to reduce its volume                in order to facilitate storage or transport.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) production of hydrocarbon fuel from flue                gas stream; and
   (2) use of Sabatier reaction for converting                carbon dioxide gas into hydrocarbon fuel in                industrial-size flue gas treatment system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                flue gas treatment system used in coal-firing                facility.
   Flue gas treatment system (100)
   Flue gas stream (110)
   Methane (112)
   Chlorine gas (130)
   Gas phase reactor (140)
   Electrolytic unit (160')
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flue gas further                  comprises sulfur oxide species, mercury and/or                  heavy metal trace element(s) chosen from                  antimony, arsenic, cadmium, chromium, nickel,                  selenium and zirconium, and water vapor. The                  Sabatier reactor uses a catalyst chosen from                  nickel catalyst, ruthenium catalyst, alumina                  catalyst and copper catalyst, preferably copper                  catalyst.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN108011150-A
TI Preparation of lithium carbonate involves mixing anode material and cathode material after disassembling used lithium-ion battery, milling, calcining, soaking calcined product in water, introducing carbon dioxide, and reacting.
AU DONG Z
   CHEN R
   XIONG H
   GAO Y
   CHEN H
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV XUCHANG (UYXC-C)
GA 201837337N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lithium carbonate involves disassembling an used ternary lithium-ion battery, mixing an anode powder material and a cathode powder material after the disassembling, ball milling, calcining in a high-temperature vacuum furnace, cooling the resultant calcined lithium carbonate powder product, soaking the calcined product in water, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting to form lithium bicarbonate aqueous solution, filtering in a filter having pore size of 200 mesh or more, leaving still, precipitating small particles of insoluble material in the solution, and spray-drying.
   USE - Preparation of lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly preparation of lithium carbonate with high purity and yield of 19-21%m and excellent environmental compatibility. The recovery rate of lithium element from the used ternary lithium-ion battery is 88-91%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of lithium carbonate involves disassembling an used ternary lithium-ion battery, mixing an anode powder material and a cathode powder material after the disassembling, ball milling at ratio (5-20):1 and 100 rpm for more than 0.1 hour, calcining in a high-temperature vacuum furnace at 500-900 degrees C, 5-20 degrees C/minute and vacuum pressure of -0.1-(-0.05) MPa for 1-6 hours, such that lithium-nickel-cobalt-manganese oxide in the electrode material is reacted with carbon atom to form lithium carbonate, nickel, cobalt and manganese oxide with evolution of carbon dioxide, naturally cooling the resultant calcined lithium carbonate powder product, soaking the calcined product in water in solid-liquid mass ratio of 1:(10-100), stirring uniformly, introducing carbon dioxide at 0.5 L/minute or more, reacting, such that lithium carbonate reacts with water and carbon dioxide to form lithium bicarbonate aqueous solution, filtering in a filter having pore size of 200 mesh or more, leaving still, precipitating small particles of insoluble material in the solution, and spray-drying to obtain pure lithium carbonate solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The anode powder material includes a ternary active material, a binder, and an electroconductive carbon. The cathode powder material includes graphite, a binder, and a thickener.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-A02; L03-E01B3; L03-H05; L03-J01; X16-B01F1; X16-M

PN CN107840963-A
TI Preparing aliphatic carbon dioxide copolyester used in degraded mulching film, involves adding catalyst, propylene oxide and lactide to autoclave, adding appropriate amount of solvent 1 or no solvent, filling autoclave quickly with nitrogen.
AU QIU H
AE TONGXIANG SHOUJING APPL TECHNOLOGY RES (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 201825249P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aliphatic carbon dioxide copolyester involves adding catalyst, propylene oxide and lactide to the autoclave, adding appropriate amount of solvent 1 or no solvent, and filling the autoclave quickly with nitrogen to maintain the pressure in the autoclave at 1.0-5.0 MPa. Polymerization reaction temperature is 60-120 degrees C, the reaction time is 10-48 hours, after the end of the polymerization reaction, a large amount of methanol is added to quench the reaction, and the obtained product is washed with solvent 2 to obtain a poly(lactic acid) (PLA)-polypropylene carbonate (PPC) copolymer.
   USE - Method for preparing aliphatic carbon dioxide copolyester used in degraded mulching film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare aliphatic carbon dioxide copolyester, which has improved flexibility compared with PLA.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing aliphatic carbon dioxide copolyester involves adding catalyst, propylene oxide and lactide to the autoclave, adding appropriate amount of solvent 1 or no solvent, and filling the autoclave quickly with nitrogen to maintain the pressure in the autoclave at 1.0-5.0 MPa. Polymerization reaction temperature is 60-120 degrees C, the reaction time is 10-48 hours, after the end of the polymerization reaction, a large amount of methanol is added to quench the reaction, and the obtained product is washed with solvent 2 to obtain a poly(lactic acid) (PLA)-polypropylene carbonate (PPC) copolymer. Terephthalic acid or terephthalate, succinic acid, butanediol are added to the reactor and nitrogen is introduced followed by heating and stirring, the reaction temperature is 120-200 degrees C, the reaction time is 10-40 hours, to obtain PBST prepolymer. The resulting PLA-PPC copolymer is added to the PBST prepolymer, the catalyst is added, and the vacuum is applied. The temperature is raised to 240-300 degrees C and the reaction is carried out for 2-10 hours to obtain a PBST-PLA-PPC copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of propylene oxide to lactide is 0.5-0.9:2. The molar ratio of catalyst to lactide is 1:100-1000. Preferred Components: The catalyst is a metal porphyrin catalyst or a metal salen catalyst. Solvent 1 is dichloromethane, toluene, tetrahydrofuran, or dioxane. Solvent 2 is methanol or ethanol. The catalyst is antimony trioxide, antimony acetate, or tetrabutyl titanate. Preferred Condition: The PLA-PPC copolymer has a number average molecular weight of 10,000 to 40,000 and a molecular weight distribution of 1.1 to 1.5. The PBST prepolymer has a number average molecular weight of 1,000 to 10,000 and a molecular weight distribution of 1.1 to 1.5. The PBST-PLA-PPC copolymer has a molecular weight of 10,000 to 50,000 and a molecular weight distribution of 1.1 to 1.5.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC A02-A00A; A05-E02A; A05-E04A; A05-E09; A07-A03A; A08-S02; A10-D03; A10-D05; A12-W04A; P13-A04
