TI Food-grade carbon dioxide washing tower comprises e.g. tower body top part set with a first air inlet, and bottom part set with a second air inlet, and spraying mechanism, electric heating mechanism and two electric connecting plates.
AU CAI C
AE TIANJIN WEICHU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201824431J
AB    NOVELTY - Food-grade carbon dioxide washing tower comprises a tower body whose top part is provided with a first air inlet, and bottom part is set with a second air inlet. The top part of the tower body is provided with a spraying mechanism. The tower body is provided with an electric heating mechanism comprising two mutually opposite electric connecting plates set in tower body. The two electric connecting plates are set with many opposite mounting holes. A pair of opposite mounting hole is fixed with an electric heating tube. The bottom of mounting hole is set connected to electric connecting plate.
   USE - Used as food-grade carbon dioxide washing tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The tower can effectively guarantee the clean cleaning inner wall of the tower body, and does not have residual cleaning liquid, thus it is safe to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Food-grade carbon dioxide washing tower comprises a tower body whose top part is provided with a first air inlet, while the bottom part is provided with a second air inlet. The top part of the tower body is provided with a spraying mechanism. The tower body is provided with an electric heating mechanism comprising two mutually opposite electric connecting plates set in the tower body. The two electric connecting plates are provided with many opposite mounting holes. A pair of opposite mounting hole is connected with an electric heating tube. The bottom of the mounting hole is set electrically connected to the electric connecting plate. The wall of the mounting hole is provided with two opposite arc-shaped clamping plates. The back portion of the arc clamping plate is connected with the hole wall of the mounting hole by a spring.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the food-grade carbon dioxide washing tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The spray mechanism comprises spraying pipe set on top part of the tower body. The spraying pipe is equipped with multiple spray heads. The spray pipe is connected with a liquid inlet tube. The two end parts of the electric connecting plates are provided with a wiring post. The tower body is provided with a waste liquid conduit. The one side of the tower body is connected with an air outlet pipe. The electric contact with the electric connecting plate is integrally formed.

PT P
PN WO2021122584-A1; CN114867966-A; EP4048651-A1
TI Power-to-X system for use of off gases, recycles            carbon dioxide which is produced by combustion of            off-gases in oxy-fuel combustion system, through return            line into stream of hydrogen after electrolyzer.
AU HANNEMANN F
   LAMP J
   ZIMMERMANN G
   LANP J
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 2021704304
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has an electrolyser (2) for                generating hydrogen and oxygen. A system (3) for                processing the hydrogen, separates the remaining                oxygen and water from the generated electricity of                hydrogen. A compressor (4) is configured for                compressing the hydrogen or the mixture of hydrogen                and carbon dioxide. A chemical reactor (5) is                configured for producing alcohols or hydrocarbons.                An oxy-fuel combustion system (7) is provided, to                which non-condensable off-gases (8) from the                chemical reactor and oxygen from the electrolyser                are supplied. The carbon dioxide produced by the                combustion of the off-gases in the oxy-fuel                combustion system, is recycled through a return                line (9) into the stream of hydrogen after an                electrolyzer (2). The oxy-fuel combustion system                has a heat exchanger through which the heat is                dissipated and integrated into other portions of                the power-to-x system, into raw methanol                processing, and/or in the auxiliary steam                generation for starting up the chemical                reactor.
   USE - Power-to-X system for use of off-gases. Can                also be used for production of carbon-based                substances such as methanol or hydrocarbons, and                for using or storing electrical energy from                renewable energy such as sun, wind and water                power.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat is integrated into the raw methanol                preparation or into the auxiliary steam generation,                for starting up the chemical reactor. By regulating                the synthesis gas flow, the combustion in the                oxy-fuel combustion system can be supported. The                off-gases produced in a Power-to-X system, are used                for the process instead of having to release them                into the atmosphere, this enables reduced emissions                of the carbon monoxide, entire system with                simultaneous use of the carbon dioxide in the                actual process and the oxygen from the                electrolyser. The energy for the air blower, is                saved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The oxy-fuel incineration plant is supplied                with biomass and/or processed waste and/or that the                oxy-fuel incineration plant comprises a heating                device by which it is heated electrically with                electricity from renewable sources. A withdrawal                flow line is provided through which synthesis gas                from the chemical reactor is fed to the oxy-fuel                combustion system. A lambda probe is arranged in                the return line of the oxy-fuel combustion system,                so that a complete combustion of the off-gases to                carbon dioxide is guaranteed by measuring and                regulating an introduced amount of oxygen. The                compressor for compressing hydrogen and carbon                dioxide, is a piston compressor for operating                pressures above 10 bar, and the system for                processing the hydrogen is a DeOxoDryer. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for the                utilization of off-gases in a power-to-X system,                generating hydrogen and oxygen by an electrolyser.                The remaining water and oxygen are separated in a                system for processing the hydrogen. The hydrogen is                compressed in the compressor. The hydrogen together                with carbon dioxide synthesis gas is generated. The                non-condensable off-gases from the chemical reactor                together with oxygen from the electrolyser, are fed                to the oxy-fuel incinerator. The carbon dioxide is                returned to the stream of hydrogen after the                electrolyser. The impurities such as sulfur, alkali                and halogen compounds and oxygen generated in the                oxy fuel process, are separated in a separation                device for impurities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                power-to-X system.
   Power to X system (1)
   Electrolyzer (2)
   System for processing hydrogen (3)
   Compressor (4)
   Chemical reactor (5)
   Oxy-fuel combustion system (7)
   Off gas (8)
   Return line (9)

PN CN114294808-A; CN114294808-B
TI Control system used e.g. for fresh air, has            central processing module that obtains carbon dioxide            target concentration value according to carbon dioxide            current and target concentration values, and outputs            control signal to fresh air.
AU ZHUANG W
   LIN B
   JI W
   GENG Y
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202253928Q
AB    NOVELTY - A system (100) comprises carbon dioxide                monitoring module (110), human body state                monitoring module (120), central processing module                (130) and fresh air device (140). The carbon                dioxide monitoring module is used for monitoring                current concentration value of carbon dioxide in                indoor gas environment and providing current                concentration value of carbon dioxide to central                processing module. The human body state monitoring                module is used for collecting original information                of indoor human body. The original information is                used for determining human body state information.                The central processing module is used for obtaining                human body state information, obtaining carbon                dioxide target concentration value according to                human body state information and carbon dioxide                current concentration value, and outputting control                signal to the fresh air according to carbon dioxide                current concentration value and carbon dioxide                target concentration value.
   USE - The control system is used for fresh air for                controlling fresh air and in fresh air control                device and non-volatile computer readable storage                medium (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide target concentration value                can be changed according to the state adaptability                of the human body and the current environment, it                can accurately control the fresh air device, change                the human central nervous system excitability, so                as to affect the working efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system (100) comprises carbon dioxide                monitoring module (110), human body state                monitoring module (120), central processing module                (130) and fresh air device (140). The carbon                dioxide monitoring module is used for monitoring                current concentration value of carbon dioxide in                indoor gas environment and providing current                concentration value of carbon dioxide to central                processing module. The human body state monitoring                module is used for collecting original information                of indoor human body. The original information is                used for determining human body state information.                The central processing module is used for obtaining                human body state information, obtaining carbon                dioxide target concentration value according to                human body state information and carbon dioxide                current concentration value, and outputting control                signal to the fresh air according to carbon dioxide                current concentration value and carbon dioxide                target concentration value. The fresh air device is                used for supplementing indoor environment fresh air                according to control signal.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for controlling fresh air.

PN CN111040004-A
TI Extracting long-chain fatty alcohols in crude            alkyl glycosides comprises placing crude alkyl            glycoside in material tank, pumping carbon dioxide,            passing crude alkyl glycosides, recycling and            collecting alkyl glycoside liquid product.
AU ZHAO Y
   SUN M
   GAO X
   HUANG S
   SUN R
AE NANTONG RUIZHI SUPERCRITICAL TECHNOLOGY (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2020346259
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting long-chain fatty alcohols in crude                alkyl glycosides comprises placing crude alkyl                glycoside in a material tank, setting the                countercurrent extraction temperature, pumping the                carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide cylinder into                the extraction tower, continuously passing the                crude alkyl glycosides in the material tank                together with carbon dioxide through the material                pump and the carbon dioxide high pressure pump                allowing the carbon dioxide dissolving in the fatty                alcohol from the top of the extraction tower                entering the separator, collecting the fatty                alcohol in the separator, recycling through the                condenser and recovering the carbon dioxide in the                extraction tower when the extraction time reaches a                predetermined value, collecting high-purity alkyl                glycoside liquid product from the bottom of the                extraction tower through the collection tank and                reusing the carbon dioxide separated from the                separator.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting long-chain                fatty alcohols in crude alkyl glycosides.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the purpose of high                precision purification; has strong extraction                ability, high extraction rate and fast extraction                time; has green separation and purification                technology; ensures that carbon dioxide can achieve                continuous separation without deceleration under                the cooperation of co-current adsorption                components; actively adsorb long-chain fatty                alcohols entrained in carbon dioxide gas while                maintaining temperature and air pressure; improves                the separation effect of carbon dioxide; makes                carbon dioxide maintain a high extraction effect                when recycled; improves the extraction rate of                long-chain fatty alcohol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting long-chain fatty alcohols in crude                alkyl glycosides comprises (a) placing crude alkyl                glycoside in a material tank, starting the                supercritical carbon dioxide continuous                countercurrent extraction separation system,                setting the countercurrent extraction temperature,                (b) setting the countercurrent extraction pressure,                pumping the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide                cylinder into the extraction tower, (c)                continuously passing the crude alkyl glycosides in                the material tank together with carbon dioxide                through the material pump and the carbon dioxide                high pressure pump according to extraction tower                when the temperature and pressure in the extraction                tower reach the set value, (d) allowing the carbon                dioxide dissolving in the fatty alcohol from the                top of the extraction tower entering the separator,                collecting the fatty alcohol in the separator,                recycling through the condenser and (e) recovering                the carbon dioxide in the extraction tower when the                extraction time reaches a predetermined value,                collecting high-purity alkyl glycoside liquid                product from the bottom of the extraction tower                through the collection tank and reusing the carbon                dioxide separated from the separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (c) the                  ratio of the crude feed amount of liquid alkyl                  glycoside to the carbon dioxide is 1: 5-1: 10.                  Preferred Method: In the step (a) the                  countercurrent extraction temperature is 50-90                  degrees C. In the step (b) the counter-current                  extraction pressure is set to 12MPa-40MPa. The                  step (c) comprises stopping the material pump                  after finishing the crude alkyl glycoside                  material in the material tank, continuing to pump                  carbon dioxide and stopping the carbon dioxide                  high-pressure pump after 10 minutes. The method                  comprises a separator including a main tank. The                  upper and lower ends of the main tank are                  respectively connected with an air outlet pipe                  and an air inlet pipe. Multiple uniformly                  distributed half-moon-shaped slide rails are                  fixedly connected in the main tank body and a                  half-moon-shaped slide rails are staggered and                  distributed across each other. The                  half-moon-shaped slide rail comprises an                  integrally formed arc segment and straight                  segment, and the arc segment is located in the                  main tank body. A secondary tank is fixedly                  connected to the outer wall of the main tank and                  the straight section is located in the secondary                  tank. The half-moon-shaped slide rail is                  slidingly connected with a co-current adsorption                  component. The co-current adsorption component                  comprises an adsorption ball. A fixing seat is                  installed on the outer end of the adsorption                  ball. The fixing seat has a ball groove cut at                  one end away from the adsorption ball. A smooth                  ball is movably connected in the ball groove. The                  outer end of the semilunar slide rail is opened                  with an annular raceway matching the smooth ball                  and the smooth ball is movably engaged with the                  annular raceway. Multiple uniformly distributed                  airflow punching holes are cut in the adsorption                  ball. The adsorption ball is filled with an                  adsorption bag and the suction bladder is                  attached to the inner wall of the suction ball. A                  positioning plug is fixedly connected to one end                  of the fixing seat close to the adsorption ball.                  An elastic clamping ball is fixedly connected to                  the upper end of the positioning plug.The                  adsorption ball is drilled with a mounting hole                  matching the positioning plug and the                  interference fit between the mounting hole and                  the elastic ball.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2018257019-A1; WO2018162996-A1; CA3055879-A1; AU2018229963-A1; US10569213-B2
TI Removing acid gas from feed gas stream having acid gas, methane and ethane, comprises alternating input of feed gas stream between two beds of adsorbent particle having zeolite SSZ-98 or zeolite SSZ-105 made from homogeneous mixture.
AU THOMPSON J A
   XIE D
AE CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
GA 2018709199
AB    NOVELTY - Removing an acid gas from a feed gas stream including the acid gas, a methane and an ethane, comprises alternating an input of the feed gas stream between an at least two beds of at least one adsorbent particle made from a homogeneous mixture, where the adsorbent particle comprises a zeolite SSZ-98 or a zeolite SSZ-105, the feed gas stream contacts one of the two beds at a given time by an adsorption step and a tail gas stream is simultaneously vented from another of the two beds by a desorption step, a contacting in the one of the two beds occurs at a feed pressure of 345-6895 kPa for a time to preferentially adsorb the acid gas from the feed gas stream, thus producing a product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% of carbon dioxide, at least 65 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream, and at least 25 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream, the feed gas stream is input at a feed end of each of the two beds.
   USE - The methods are useful for removing an acid gas from a feed gas stream including the acid gas, a methane and an ethane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: exhibit a specific vacuum power consumption of 0-1500 kWh/MMSCF of the feed gas stream (claimed); utilize the zeolite SSZ-98 or a zeolite SSZ-105 that remove the contaminants from a feed gas stream; and are performed in many different types of locations, including at refineries, at well heads, on ships, and even on offshore platforms or other remote locations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing an acid gas from a feed gas stream including the acid gas, a methane and an ethane, comprises alternating an input of the feed gas stream between an at least two beds of at least one adsorbent particle made from a homogeneous mixture, where the adsorbent particle comprises a zeolite SSZ-98 or a zeolite SSZ-105, the feed gas stream contacts one of the two beds at a given time by an adsorption step and a tail gas stream is simultaneously vented from another of the two beds by a desorption step, a contacting in the one of the two beds occurs at a feed pressure of 345-6895 kPa for a time to preferentially adsorb the acid gas from the feed gas stream, thus producing a product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% of carbon dioxide, at least 65 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream, and at least 25 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream, the feed gas stream is input at a feed end of each of the two beds, the product gas stream is removed from a product end of each of the at least two beds, and the tail gas stream is vented from the feed end of the two beds. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) removing an acid gas from a feed gas stream having a methane, an ethane, a carbon dioxide, and 4-1000 wppm of hydrogen sulfide, comprising alternating an input of the feed gas stream between an at least two beds of at least one adsorbent particle made from a homogeneous mixture, where the adsorbent particle comprises a zeolite SSZ-98 or a zeolite SSZ-105, the feed gas stream contacts one of the two beds at a given time in an adsorption step and a tail gas stream is simultaneously vented from another of the two beds in a desorption step, a contacting in the one of the two beds occurs at a feed pressure of 345-6895 kPa for a time to preferentially adsorb the acid gas from the feed gas stream, thus producing a product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% of the carbon dioxide, no greater than 1 wppm of a carbonyl sulfide, no greater than 1 wppm of the hydrogen sulfide, at least 65 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream, and at least 25 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream and the feed gas stream is input at a feed end of each of the at least two beds, the product gas stream is removed from a product end of each of the at least two beds and the tail gas stream is vented from the feed end of the at least two beds;
   (2) removing an acid gas from a feed gas stream, comprising adjusting a selectivity of an adsorbent for retaining an ethane or a propane in a product gas stream by changing a substitution of an ERI phase with a LEV phase in a molecular sieve having an intergrowth of the ERI framework type molecular sieve (ERI phase) and the LEV framework type molecular sieve (LEV phase), and contacting the adsorbent comprising the molecular sieve with the feed gas stream; and
   (3) removing an acid gas from a feed gas stream, comprising contacting a feed gas stream with adsorbent particles comprising a molecular sieve having an intergrowth of an ERI phase and a LEV phase to provide a product gas stream with less than 2 mol.% carbon dioxide, 80-99 mol.% of a methane recovered from the feed gas stream, 40-99 mol.% of an ethane recovered from the feed gas stream, and 60-100 mol.% of a propane recovered from the feed gas stream, where the LEV phase is 31-99% in the molecular sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zeolite SSZ-98 or the SSZ-105 has a cation as a framework ion and the cation comprises sodium (preferred), calcium, potassium, lithium, magnesium and/or barium. The acid gas comprises carbon dioxide, a hydrogen sulfide and/or a carbonyl sulfide. The molecular sieve has a cation as a framework ion and the cation comprises a sodium, a calcium, a potassium, a lithium, a magnesium and/or a barium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The two beds of the adsorbent particle are four beds of the adsorbent particle and the product gas stream contains at least 80 mol.% of the methane recovered from the feed gas stream and at least 40 mol.% of the ethane recovered from the feed gas stream. The feed gas stream comprises the carbon dioxide and 0-1000 wppm of hydrogen sulfide. The zeolite SSZ-98 or the zeolite SSZ-105 has a silicon:aluminum mole ratio of 5-100. The feed gas stream has a flow rate of 1-100 MMSCFD during the adsorption and the adsorption occurs at an adsorption-temperature of 20-80 degrees C. The product gas stream comprises at least 95 mol.% of total-methane and at least 3 mol.% of total-ethane. The product gas stream contains no greater than 50 wppm, preferably no greater than 40 wppm of hydrogen sulfide. The water in the feed gas stream is adsorbed by the two beds of the adsorbent particle and a carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of the adsorbent particle is lowered by less than 30 mol.%. The method utilizes two beds of the adsorbent particles and further comprises (a) following the adsorption step in one of the two beds and a simultaneous desorption step in the other of the two beds, equalizing a pressure of the one of the two beds and the other of the two beds through the product end of each of the one of the two beds and the other of the two beds at an end of the adsorption and the simultaneous desorption step, and (b) re-pressurizing the other of the two beds having just completed the simultaneous desorption step by sending a slipstream of the product gas stream through the product end of the other of the two beds having just completed the simultaneous desorption step. The two beds of the adsorbent particle are four beds and further comprises following a first adsorption step in a first bed of the four beds, a first equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to pressure equalize with a second bed of the four beds having a lower-pressure than the first bed through a first line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the second bed, following the first equalization step, lowering the feed pressure in the first bed and passing a gas from the first bed to a third bed of the four beds through a second line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the third bed in a providing purge step such that the third bed of the four beds is purged, following the providing purge step, a second equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to pressure equalize with the third bed of the four beds having the lower-pressure than the first bed through a third line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the third bed, (d) following the second equalization step, depressurizing a first adsorbent bed to the feed pressure of 6.89-138 kPa through the feed end of the first adsorbent bed in a blowdown step comprising either allowing the gas in the first adsorbent bed to vent to a purge tank, or using a vacuum pump to lower the feed pressure of the first adsorbent bed, following the blowdown step, the first bed is purged in a purging step, where the gas is provided to the first bed through the product end of the first bed from a fourth bed of the four beds while the first bed is at the feed pressure of 6.89-138 kPa and the gas is purged through the feed end of the first bed, following the purging step, a third equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to pressure equalize with the fourth bed having a higher-pressure than the first bed through a fourth line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the fourth bed, following the third equalization step, a fourth equalization step occurs, where the first bed is allowed to equalize with the second bed having the higher-pressure than the first bed through a fifth line connecting the product end of the first bed and the product end of the second bed, following the fourth equalization step, passing a slipstream of a product-gas or a stream of a storage gas from a storage tank through the product end of the first bed to repressurize the first bed to an adsorption-step-pressure in a repressurization step, and following the repressurization step, operating the first bed in an independent adsorption step for a period of time for the third bed and the fourth bed to be pressure equalized and the second bed to be depressurized prior to beginning a second adsorption step, where the second bed, the third bed, and the fourth bed are sequenced to cycle through the first adsorption step, the first equalization step, the providing purge step, the second equalization step, the blowdown step, the purging step, the third equalization step, the fourth equalization step and the independent adsorption step in the same order as the first bed. The first adsorption step, the first equalization step, the providing purge step, the second equalization step, the blowdown step, the purging step, the third equalization step, the fourth equalization step and the independent adsorption step occur in a total cycle time of 400-3600 seconds. The method is performed on an offshore platform. Greater than 0-50 wt.% of the tail gas stream is recycled to the feed gas stream, thus producing the product gas stream containing no greater than 2 mol.% of the carbon dioxide, at least 90 mol.% of the methane in the feed gas stream, and at least 85 mol.% of total hydrocarbons in the feed gas stream. A first equilibrium adsorption of the carbon dioxide in the feed gas stream is greater than 5 mol/kg, a second equilibrium adsorption of the methane is less than 4 mol/kg, a third equilibrium adsorption of the ethane is less than 2.5 mol/kg, and a fourth equilibrium adsorption of a propane is less than 1 mol/kg. Preferred Process: The changing comprises increasing the substitution to have 35-99% of the LEV phase in the molecular sieve, so that the increasing the substitution provides a higher kinetic selectivity for retaining the ethane or the propane in the product gas stream. The changing of the substitution reduces an equilibrium adsorption of a carbon dioxide in the feed gas stream by zero to 1 mol/kg in a gas adsorption test. The molecular sieve has a first calculated diffusion coefficient for a ethane uptake of 3E-04 to 1E-06 per second. The molecular sieve has a second calculated diffusion coefficient for a propane uptake of 3E-06 to 1E-07 per second. Preferred Components: The feed gas stream additionally comprises a water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2020079908-A; KR2161594-B1
TI Method of estimating carbon dioxide emissions using carbon dioxide emission estimator, involves calculating carbon dioxide emission of recognized construction equipment, and providing estimated carbon dioxide emissions.
AU HANSEUNGWOO
   EUN K J
   LEEJINWOO
   CHAE Y B
   LEE J
   CHO M D
   KWON J
   CHOI Y
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 202062389K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving (S510) an image captured from a camera installed at a construction site. The photographed image is analyzed using an object recognition algorithm. The one of a type, brand, and model name of construction equipment in use at the construction site is recognized (S520). The carbon dioxide emission of the recognized construction equipment is calculated (S540) by using a database in which representative data of carbon dioxide emission by type, brand, model name, reads per million (RPM) value of construction equipment is stored. The estimated carbon dioxide emissions is provided (S550).
   USE - Method of estimating carbon dioxide emissions using carbon dioxide emission estimator of construction equipment of construction equipment in construction site.
   ADVANTAGE - The process of low-carbon emission is considered by calculating and providing the total amount of carbon dioxide emissions per day of construction equipment used at the construction site, thus enabling eco-friendly process management. The carbon dioxide emission amount of all equipment input to the construction site is easily grasped, so that has the possibility of preparing in advance for the carbon emission right to be granted to the construction company. The accuracy of the construction equipment recognition is increased in the construction site using the object recognition technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device of estimating carbon dioxide emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method of estimating carbon dioxide emissions. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for receiving image (S510)
   Step for recognizing one of a type, brand (S520)
   Step for determining the type of work currently being performed (S530)
   Step for calculating carbon dioxide emission of the recognized construction equipment (S540)
   Step for providing estimated carbon dioxide emissions (S550)

PT P
PN CN110950339-A; CN211998836-U
TI Preparation of high-purity carbon dioxide involves preparing carbon dioxide crude gas using e.g. carbonate, condensing moisture and carbon dioxide in crude gas into liquid and rectifying liquid to collect rectified and pure carbon dioxide.
AU YAO G
   XI S
   MA Y
   SHANG H
   GUO J
   QIAO B
   SUN Q
   LUO W
AE CSIC HANDAN PAIRUI SPECIAL GAS CO LTD (CSIC-Non-standard)
GA 202028297S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-purity carbon dioxide involves reacting carbonate or bicarbonate with acid liquid to obtain carbon dioxide crude gas, condensing the moisture in the carbon dioxide crude gas into a liquid at 0 to -50 degrees C to remove the moisture, condensing carbon dioxide gas in carbon dioxide crude gas into liquid at -60 to -75 degrees C to remove unliquefied air and other gas impurities and rectifying the liquefied carbon dioxide to collect rectified and purified carbon dioxide. The rectification is carried out by using a rectification tower, where the top temperature of the tower is -20 to -10 degrees C, the temperature of the tower kettle is -10 to -0 degrees C, the temperature difference between the top of the tower and the tower kettle is 5 degrees C or more, the pressure in the tower is 1-5 MPa and reflux ratio is 3-15.
   USE - Preparation of high-purity carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the pollution problems caused by other processes which require the use of adsorption or pressure swing adsorption process, avoids the composition of carbonyl sulfide treatment, reduces the pressure of the distillation equipment and improves the distillation efficiency. The method utilizes device which is safe, stable, energy-saving and has simple structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for preparing high-purity carbon dioxide, which comprises carbon dioxide reaction kettle, cooler (A), cooler (B), cold trap (M), rectification tower, cold trap (N) and storage tank. The carbon dioxide reaction kettle is equipped with carbonate/bicarbonate feed port, acid liquid feed port and gas outlet. The cold trap (M) and cold trap (N) are equipped with air inlet, emptying port and liquid outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate comprises lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. The bicarbonate comprises lithium hydrogen carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or potassium bicarbonate. Preferred Composition: The crude carbon dioxide has moisture content of below 20 ppm after condensing at 0 to -50 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The temperature in the cooler (A) is 0 to -20 degrees C and cooler (B) is -30 to -50 degrees C. The temperature in cold trap (N) is -60 to -75 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The package inside the rectification tower comprises stainless steel Bauer ring, rectangular saddle ring, Raschig ring or theta ring.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid liquid comprises trifluoromethanesulfonic acid, bistrifluoromethanesulfonic acid, bisfluorosulfonimide or sulfuric acid.

AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide state carbon                monitoring and control system based on infrared                spectrum characteristic analysis comprises a test                section for the passage of carbon dioxide. An                infrared light source for detecting the light beam                emitted by the carbon dioxide passing through the                test section. An infrared spectrometer for                receiving and analyzing the detection beam passing                through carbon dioxide. A pressure control module                for controlling the pressure of the carbon dioxide                at a set value. A temperature control module is                used for adjusting the temperature of the carbon                dioxide. The test section is provided with a first                test section and a second test section.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide state monitoring                and control system based on infrared spectrum                characteristic analysis is useful for Brayton cycle                system.

The temperature control module adjusts the                supercritical carbon dioxide temperature by                adjusting the power of the pre-cooler so as to                realize the adjustment of the temperature of carbon                dioxide. The system utilizes the infrared                spectrometer to analyze the obtained carbon dioxide                in the transmissivity of the special wave band                close to or the transmittance under the critical                state, so that the gas under different pressure of                physical data is different, and the pressure                control module controls the pressure of the gas at                the set value, thus stabilizing the carbon dioxide                pressure at the inlet of the device under the                actual operating conditions in the Brayton cycle                system at the set value.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  carbon dioxide-temperature dual response type                  chitosan composite microcapsule comprises taking                  1-30 pts. wt. plant essential oils, 0.5-5 pts.                  wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 0.1-5 pts. wt. carbon                  dioxide-oxygen dual gas-responsive oligomers,                  0.1-5 pts. wt. temperature-responsive oligomers,                  10-150 pts. wt. deionized water, 0.5-10 pts. wt. The equipment method of the carbon                  dioxide-oxygen dual gas response oligomer                  comprises taking 1-10 pts. wt.                  2-(dimethylamino)ethyl methacrylate, 1-10 pts.                  wt. 3-(pentafluorophenyl)pentafluoro-1-propene,                  1-5 pts. wt.                  &#947;-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane,                  and 0.1-1 pts. wt. azobisisobutyronitrile, adding                  2-(dimethylamino)ethyl methacrylate and                  3-(pentafluorophenyl)pentafluoro-1-propene into                  the polymerization tube, vacuuming and filling                  with nitrogen for 3 times, adding                  &#947;-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane                  and azobisisobutyronitrile into the                  polymerization tube, sealing the polymerization                  tube, and conducting the polymerization reaction                  at 30-80&#8451; for 3-8 hours, terminating,                  sedimentating, drying and purifying to obtain                  carbon dioxide-oxygen dual gas-responsive                  oligomer containing silane coupling agent. The                  preparation method of the temperature-responsive                  oligomer comprises 1-10 pts. wt.                  N-isopropylacrylamide, 1-5 pts. wt.                  &#947;-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane,                  0.1-1 pts. wt. azobisisobutyronitrile, and 10-50                  pts. wt. methanol, dissolving                  N-isopropylacrylamide,                  &#947;-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane                  and azobisisobutyronitrile in methanol, reacting                  at 30-80&#8451; for 3-8 hours, centrifuging,                  washing with water, and drying to obtain a                  temperature-sensitive responsive oligomer                  containing a silane coupling agent.Preferred Components: The plant essential                  oil comprises wormwood, lemon, mint, thyme,                  grapefruit, clove, cinnamon, and/or tea tree                  essential oil. The composite microcapsule                  finishing solution is composed of chitosan                  microcapsules and adhesives in a mass ratio of                  1-5:1. The adhesive comprises sericin, gum                  Arabic, polyurethane, and/or polyimide.

PN CN113680346-A
TI Core-shell structure useful e.g. for reducing carbon dioxide photo-catalyst obtained from 3D honeycomb-shaped nitrogen-doped graphitic carbon frame carbon material and a copper-containing compound through a polymer heat treatment reaction.
AU HE L
   ZHAO Y
AE NANJING MEDICAL ELECTRONICS APPLIED SCI (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021E13531
AB    NOVELTY - Core-shell structure, which is obtained from 3D honeycomb-shaped nitrogen-doped graphitic carbon frame carbon material and a copper-containing compound through a polymer heat treatment reaction.
   USE - The core-shell structure is useful for reducing carbon dioxide photo-catalyst and preparing photo-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction film (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, low cost and is convenient for popularization; utilizes the composite material for photo catalytic carbon dioxide reduction under visible light irradiation and enables maximum yields of 38.89 mumol/g methane and 27.78 mumol/g carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing core-shell structure comprising (a) dissolving the carbon material containing copper compound and 3D honeycomb nitrogen-doped graphite carbon frame in deionized water, performing magnetic stirring, maintaining the mixture at 80-100degrees Celsius, stirring, drying, forming light blue powder to obtain copper and copper(I) oxide nano-particles embedded in a 3D honeycomb-shaped nitrogen-doped graphitic carbon frame composite material and (b) calcining the dried sample in a tubular furnace under the protection of inert gas in the step (a), heating to 500-700degrees Celsius for 0.5-2 hours at a rate of 1-10degrees Celsius /minute to obtain copper and copper(I) oxide embedded 3D honeycomb nitrogen-doped graphitic carbon frame composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon material of the 3D honeycomb nitrogen-doped graphite frame comprises melamine, graphene, graphitic carbon, urea and polyvinylpyrrolidone. The copper-containing compound comprises copper acetate and/or copper nitrate. The copper-containing compound is copper nitrate. The carbon material of the 3D honeycomb nitrogen-doped graphitic carbon frame is polyvinylpyrrolidone and the ratio of the two is 3:2. The inert gas comprises nitrogen and/or argon.Preferred Method: The heating process is carried out to 500degrees Celsius at a rate 5degrees Celsius/minute for 1 hour. The preparation of photo-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction film comprises placing the 50 mg core-shell structure reduction carbon dioxide photo catalyst into a glass petri dish, adding 5ml deionized water, where the ratio of core-shell structure reduction carbon dioxide photo catalyst to deionized water is 10:1, ultrasonically dispersing the catalyst for 1-5 minute, placing the petri dish in an oven, drying at 50-70degrees Celsius, evenly distributing deionized water on the surface of the dried catalyst to obtain a photo catalytic carbon dioxide reduction film. The ultrasonic dispersion time is 3 minutes and the drying temperature is 60degrees Celsius.

AU LONG X

AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021318139
AB    NOVELTY - The tower has a tower body whose inner side is formed with an evaporation cavity. An absorbing sub-system comprises an absorbing bed fixing plate that is fixed on upper and lower ends of the evaporation cavity. An absorbing bed is fixed in the evaporation cavity along vertical direction. Upper and lower ends of the absorbing bed are connected with the absorbing bed fixing plate. The spraying sub-system comprises a spraying main pipe, a spraying branch pipe and a spraying pipe. The spraying main pipe is formed as an annular structure. The spraying pipe is fixed in the evaporation cavity and the absorbing bed fixing plate. An end of the spraying pipe is connected with the spraying main pipe or the spraying branch pipe. The spraying pipe is opened with uniformly distributed spraying holes towards direction of the absorbing bed. The spraying pipe and the evaporating cavity are connected with a pressure control sub-system.
   USE - Liquid carbon dioxide spraying-type pressing swing temperature controlling and absorbing tower.

PN CN111871462-A
TI Iron-cobalt organic catalyst used to convert            carbon dioxide to synthesize benzazepine heterocyclic            compounds, comprises microsphere with specific            diameter, and surface of microsphere has layered            crystals.
AU ZHENG H
   SHEN Y
   YANG S
   SUN C
   XIE R
   LI S
AE UNIV HANGZHOU NORMAL (UYNL-C)
GA 2020B22763
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-cobalt organic catalyst comprises a                microsphere with a diameter of 200-400 nm, where                the surface of the microsphere has layered                crystals.
   USE - The iron-cobalt organic catalyst is used to                convert carbon dioxide to synthesize benzazepine                heterocyclic compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and has high                repeatability. The method has mild reaction                conditions, which effectively fix greenhouse gases                and generate pharmaceutical intermediates with                added value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the iron-cobalt                organic catalyst, comprising adding an aromatic                acid to a mixed solvent of dimethylformamide and                absolute ethanol and stirring to dissolve, adding                ferrous chloride and stirring for 5-15 minutes,                adding cobalt nitrate hexahydrate and stirring for                5-15 minutes, where the amount of ferrous chloride                and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate is 1-10 times than                aromatic acid, reacting at 150-200 degrees C for                10-20 hours, washing the product, and vacuum drying                to obtain the product; and
   (2) the use of the iron-cobalt organic                catalyst to convert carbon dioxide to synthesize                benzazepine heterocyclic compounds, comprising                adding 1-10 mg iron-cobalt organic catalyst to 5-20                ml 0.05-1 mmol/l tetrahydrofuran solution of                o-aminobenzonitrile compound under atmospheric                pressure conditions, continuously blowing carbon                dioxide gas, carrying out photocatalytic reaction                under the sunlight simulator to synthesize the                benzazepine heterocyclic compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of                  dimethylformamide and absolute ethanol in the                  mixed solvent is 1:(0.1-10). Preferred Method:                  The washing step adopts anhydrous ethanol and                  dimethylformamide alternately, where the amount                  of each washing liquid is 5-10 ml washing liquid                  per mole of product, and washing until the                  product quality remains unchanged. Preferred                  Conditions: The vacuum drying is drying at 80-100                  degrees C for 5-10 hours. The reaction                  temperature of the synthesis of benzazepine                  heterocyclic compounds is 20-80 degrees C.

The evaporator, compressor, air cooler gas side and an expansion valve are connected in sequence through pipes to form a carbon dioxide circulation system. The water side of the air cooler, a peak heater (12), and the heat user are connected in sequence with the circulating water pump to form a heating cycle system. The evaporator is installed in the condenser, and the low-temperature and low-pressure carbon dioxide exchanges heat with the exhaust steam of the steam turbine through the evaporator.
   USE - System for recovering waste heat from steam turbine exhaust heat.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes the effect of higher comprehensive benefit, wider application range, more energy saving and environmental protection than the existing heating unit through the combination of transcritical carbon dioxide cycle system, water vapor Rankine cycle system and heating system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for recovering waste heat from steam turbine exhaust heat.
   Steam turbines (1)
   Generator (2)
   Evaporator (3)
   Condenser (4)
   Compresso (5)
   Air cooler gas (6)
   Expansion valve (7)
   Thermostats (8)
   Regulating valves (9)
   Heat users (10)
   Peak heater (12)

PN WO2020049081-A1; EP3846936-A1; US2021322957-A1; US11491468-B2
TI Supported catalyst used to synthesize methanol            comprises indium oxide in form of indium oxide and            noble metal having palladium, deposited on support            having calcined supported catalyst comprising palladium            and zirconium dioxide.
AU STEWART J
   CURULLA-FERRE D
   PEREZ-RAMIREZ J
   MONDELLI C
   FREI M
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 2020206954
AB    NOVELTY - Supported catalyst comprises indium oxide in                the form of indium oxide and at least one noble                metal having palladium, where both indium oxide and                at least one noble metal are deposited on a support                having the supported catalyst is a calcined                supported catalyst comprising 0.01-10 wt.% of                palladium and at least 50 wt.% zirconium                dioxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesizing                methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves conversion of carbon                dioxide to methanol, together with high space-time                yield and high selectivity to methanol; and                exhibits improved stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst by impregnation or                by deposition precipitation; and
   (2) methanol synthesis comprising providing a                feed stream comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides                comprising carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon                dioxide and carbon monoxide, where carbon dioxide                represents 1-50 mol% of the total molar content of                the feed stream, carbon monoxide is contained of                0-85 mol% of the total molar content, and hydrogen                is comprised of 5-99 mol% of the total molar                content of the feed stream, providing the catalyst                and/or prepared by the method, putting in contact                the feed stream with the catalyst at a reaction                temperature of at least 373 K (99.85 degrees C) and                under a pressure of at least 0.5 Mpa and recovering                the methanol from the effluents by a separation                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  0.5-2 wt.%. of palladium. The support comprises                  at least 80 wt.%, preferably at least 90 wt.%                  zirconium dioxide. The catalyst is a calcined                  supported catalyst and in that the indium oxide                  content in the form of indium oxide is of 1-20                  wt.%, preferably 5-15 wt.%. The catalyst is a                  calcined supported catalyst and has preferably a                  surface area of 5-400 m2/g, preferably 30-200                  m2/g as determined according to nitrogen sorption                  analysis according to ASTM D3663-03. The average                  particle size of the noble metal phase is less                  than 5 nm as determined by STEM-EDX, and/or the                  average crystal size of indium oxide is less than                  20 nm, as determined by X-ray powder diffraction.                  The supported catalyst is a calcined supported                  catalyst, and method comprises calcination of the                  catalyst performed at a temperature of at least                  473 K (199.85 degrees C), with preference of at                  least 573 K (299.85 degrees C). The pressure is                  at least 0.5 MPa, preferably at least 5 Mpa,                  and/or the reaction temperature is at least 463 K                  (189.85 degrees C), preferably at least 553 K                  (279.85 degrees C). The feed stream is put in                  contact with the catalyst at a weight hourly                  space velocity of 1000-60000 cm3 at STP g                  catalyst per hour, 24000-48000 cm3 at STP g                  catalyst per hour. The molar ratio of hydrogen to                  carbon dioxide in the feed stream is at least                  1:1, preferably at least 4:1, where before                  reaction the catalyst is activated in situ by                  raising the temperature to at least 553 K (279.85                  degrees C) in a flow of a gas feed stream for                  activation comprising inert gases, hydrogen,                  carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide, preferably                  the gas feed stream for activation is or                  comprises nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - To obtain 9 wt.% indium oxide and 1 wt.%                palladium, 0.63 g indium(III) nitrate hydrate and                0.05 g palladium(II) nitrate dehydrate were                dissolved in 70 cm3 deionized water in a round                bottom flask. 1.8 g zirconia was sieved to have a                particle size of 125 mu m and was added to the                metal salts solution. To this mixture, an 10 wt.%                aqueous sodium carbonate was added dropwise until a                pH of 9.2 was reached at which the slurry was aged                for 60 minutes. The solid was then separated by                high pressure filtration and washed with deionized                water. Then dried in a vacuum oven and calcined                either at 773 K for 3 hours in static air.

GA 201952631G
AB    NOVELTY - Buffer solution is an aqueous solution that is                used in gas exchange with the extra corporeal                blooded blood of the patient and first buffer and                contains second buffer. The first buffer is                consists of one buffer substance which has a pK                value of 7.9 plus minus 0.2 at 37 degrees C. The                second buffer is consists of one buffer substance                which has a pK-value of 6.9 plus minus 0.2 at 37                degrees C, and the solution at a partial pressure                of carbon dioxide (pCO2) is 0.2 plus minus 0.2                millimeter of mercury, after titration has a pH in                the range of 8.25-8.35.
   USE - Buffer solution for reducing carbon dioxide                content in blood in treatment of patient suffering                from pulmonary insufficiency or complete lung                function failure.
   ADVANTAGE - The buffer solution is used to eliminate                carbon di-oxide from patient blood.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for extracorporeal reduction of the carbon dioxide                content in the blood, which comprises first                delimited area for receiving extracorporeal blood,                and a second delimited area for receiving a buffer                solution, the first and the second region are set                in a contact zone abutting each other are separated                only by a membrane through which a gas exchange is                take place between the blood and the buffer                solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: The buffer solution                  of carbon dioxide (pCO2) is 40 millimetre of                  mercury has an effective carbon dioxide affinity                  of greater than or equal to 10 milliliter per                  liter per millimetre of mercury, preferably                  greater than or equal to 15 milliliter per liter                  per millimetre of mercury, effective carbon                  dioxide affinity of greater than or equal to 20                  milliliter per liter per millimetre of mercury,                  preferably greater than or equal to 25 milliliter                  per liter per millimetre of mercury, more                  preferably greater than or equal to 30 milliliter                  per liter per millimetre of mercury. The buffer                  solution of carbon dioxide (pCO2) is 40                  millimetre of mercury has an effective carbon                  dioxide transport capacity of greater than or                  equal to 500 millimeter per liter at pCO2 of 10                  millimetre of mercury an effective carbon dioxide                  transport capacity of greater than or equal to                  250 millimeter per liter. The buffer solution                  contains fol-ing ingredients such as 36.0                  millilimole per liter puffer ATRIS, 34.0                  millilimole per liter puffer BNa2HP04 and 12,0                  millilimole per liter titer HCl. Preferred                  Conditions: The flow rate at which the blood                  flows through the first defined area is 15 wt.%                  cardiac output of the patient by using the                  apparatus to control the carbon dioxide content                  in the blood which is extracorporeally                  reduced.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2019067706-A1; US10978718-B2
TI Preparing metal organic framework for carbon            dioxide reduction reaction catalyst which is used to            make inks, comprises reacting organic ligand with            transition metal ion and pyrolyzing the metal organic            framework.
AU LIU D
   REBOLLAR D
AE UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
   UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
   UNIV NORTHERN ILLINOIS (UYNI-Non-standard)
GA 201920741S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a metal organic framework comprises                reacting an organic ligand with a transition metal                ion and pyrolyzing the metal organic framework at a                temperature of 700-800 degrees C as well as 1050                degrees C for a period of 120 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing metal                organic framework for carbon dioxide reduction                reaction catalyst (all claimed) which is used to                make inks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides catalyst that: has a                selectivity of at least 95%, preferably at least                70% for single product formation and a dual                selectivity of at least 95% for single product                formation; are composed of highly porous transition                metal organic frameworks exhibiting large pores                with regular distribution of transition metals                within the structure; exhibit excellent efficiency                and durability for converting carbon dioxide                towards selective production of formic acid as well                as methanol under low overpotentials; exhibit                excellent stability as well as cycleability; and                has active and stable in aqueous media, easy                application to surfaces as well as thin films or on                substrates and using low cost, earth abundant                transition metal materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                pyrolyzed metal organic framework for carbon                dioxide reduction reaction catalyst comprising a                pyrolyzed metal organic framework consisting of                nitrogen-free carbonaceous supports and zero                valence transition metals, where the metal organic                framework consists pores having a pore size of                10-15 Angstrom .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pyrolizing temperature                  is 800-810 degrees C for 60-120 minutes. The                  pyrolizing period is 60-120 minutes. The                  pyrolizing consists of converting at least a                  portion of the transition metal ions to zero                  valence state transition metal. The micropores                  are formed within the metal organic framework and                  during pyrolyzing, a transition metal                  microcrystalite is formed within at least a                  portion of the micropores. The method further                  comprises replacing a portion of the transition                  metal reacted with the organic ligand with a                  second transition metal, where a multi-metallic                  metal organic framework is formed. Preferred                  Components: The organic ligand comprises                  1,4-dicarboxylic acid, 1,3,5 tricarboxylic acid,                  methylimidazole or ethylimidazole. The transition                  metal comprises copper, zinc, copper/zinc,                  nickel, iron or cobalt (preferred). The catalyst                  is a powder having an average grain size of 25                  nm. The metal organic framework includes a                  dopant.
   EXAMPLE - 2.25 g Trimesic acid was added to 1.2 g copper                acetate hydrate in 120 ml water. The solution was                stirred at 1200 rpm for 1 hour at room temperature                and copper(II) benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate was                subsequently collected. The product was washed 3                times through repeated sonication in ethanol and                further centrifugation at 3000 rpm for 45 minutes.                The sky blue product was dried at 85 degrees C                overnight under vacuum. The resulting metal organic                framework had a surface area 950 m2/g. Copper(II)                benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate sample was the                pyrolyzed at multiple temperatures of 400-800                degrees C under argon inert atmosphere using a ramp                rate of 10 degrees C per minute and dwell time at                the desired temperature of an hour. The resulting                powder was collected.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

The carbonated water in the container is injected by pressure of carbon dioxide gas from the cleaning head through the tube.
   USE - Carbonated water shower apparatus for cleaning a to-be-cleaned area i.e. head hair, for use during treatment.

PN CN109012743-A
TI Composite catalyst for direct hydrogenation of            carbon dioxide to gasoline, comprises nano metal oxide            comprising indium oxide, zinc oxide and zirconium            oxide, and ZSM-5 molecular sieve.
AU GAO P
   CUI X
   YANG H
   DANG S
   WANG H
   WEI W
   SUN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 2018A5384Y
AB    NOVELTY - A composite catalyst comprises 20-80 %mass                nano metal oxide comprising 10-40 %mass indium                oxide, zinc oxide and zirconium oxide, and 20-80                %mass ZSM-5 molecular sieve.
   USE - Composite catalyst for direct hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide to gasoline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is prepared by simple, easy and                economical process with excellent reaction                stability on large scale, and has excellent                catalytic performance and high carbon dioxide                conversion rate, and provides target product                gasoline hydrocarbons which have high selectivity                and pentacarbon content of 80.2% or less, and                provides by-product methane and carbon monoxide                which have low selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   preparation of the composite catalyst, which                involves (S1) mixing mixed solution of metal salt                containing zinc and metal salt containing zirconium                with a precipitant solution, reacting to obtain a                precipitate, (S2) aging, washing, drying, and                calcining the precipitate to obtain a zinc                oxide-zirconium oxide metal oxide support, (S3)                dissolving a metal salt containing indium in water                to obtain an immersion liquid, immersing the metal                oxide support in immersion liquid, drying and                calcining to obtain indium oxide/zinc                oxide-zirconium oxide nano metal oxide, and (S4)                mixing inn2O3 indium oxide/zinc oxide-zirconium                oxide nano metal oxide with ZSM-5 molecular sieve;                and
   application of the composite catalyst in                direct hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to                gasoline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  zinc oxide to zirconium oxide is 1:8-4:1. The                  ZSM-5 molecular sieve is selected from ZSM-5                  molecular sieves having silica and alumina in a                  molar ratio of 25-200. The molar concentration of                  the total metal in the mixed solution of metal                  salt containing zinc and metal salt containing                  zirconium is 0.1-1.0 mol/L. The molar ratio of                  the precipitant to the total metal ions is                  4-20:1. Preferred Process: The mixed solution of                  metal salt containing zinc and metal salt                  containing zirconium is obtained by dissolving                  metal salt containing zinc and metal salt                  containing zirconium in water and/or alcohol. The                  precipitant solution is obtained by dissolving                  the precipitant in water or alcohol. In step                  (S4), the mixing is mechanical mixing, grinding                  mixing or ball milling mixing. The mechanical                  mixing involves tableting and sieving indium                  oxide/zinc oxide-zirconium oxide nano metal oxide                  with ZSM-5 molecular sieve to obtain 20-80 mesh                  nanoparticles, and packaging in a glass container                  for uniform mixing by vibration. The grinding                  mixing involves grinding the indium oxide/zinc                  oxide-zirconium oxide nano metal oxide with ZSM-5                  molecular sieve in an agate mortar, and pressing                  and sieving to obtain nanoparticles of 20-80                  mesh. The ball milling mixing involves ball                  milling indium oxide/zinc oxide-zirconium oxide                  nano metal oxide with ZSM-5 molecular sieve, and                  tableting and sieving to obtain nano-particles of                  20-80 mesh. The catalyst is activated at                  250-500&#176; C in an inert gas atmosphere                  for 1-12 hours, before using. The application of                  the composite catalyst involves performing direct                  hydrogenation of carbon dioxide at                  300-400&#176; C, 1.0-5.0 MPa, and mass space                  velocity of 1000-12000 mL.gcat-1hour-1. The molar                  ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 1-4.                  Preferred Components: The metal salt comprises                  hydrochloride, nitrate and/or acetate. The                  precipitant comprises aqueous ammonia, ammonium                  carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or sodium                  hydroxide. Preferred Conditions: In step (S1),                  the reaction temperature is 10-40&#176; C. In                  step (S2), the aging is carried out at                  40-100&#176; C for 0.1-10 hours, drying                  temperature is carried out at 60-100&#176; C                  for 6-24 hours, and calcining is carried out at                  250-500&#176; C for 2-12 hours. In step (S3),                  the drying is carried out at 60-100&#176; C                  for 6-24 hours, and calcining is carried out at                  250-500&#176; C for 2-12 hours.

PN WO2022013550-A1
TI Capture of carbon dioxide from gas stream discharged from e.g. combustion process, involves contacting gas stream with composition comprising phase comprising aqueous solution of salt of carboxylic acid, and phase which is effective solvent for carbon dioxide and chemically inert to carbon dioxide.
AU BARNES D
   SCHOOLDERMAN C
   HOLDSWORTH D
   RAYNER C
AE C-CAPTURE LTD (CCAP-Non-standard)
GA 2022167690
AB    NOVELTY - Capture of carbon dioxide from gas stream involves (i) contacting gas stream containing carbon dioxide with a capture composition comprising a liquid phase (a) comprising capture reagent(s) and an aqueous solution of salt(s) of carboxylic acid(s), and a liquid phase (b) which is an effective solvent for carbon dioxide and chemically inert to carbon dioxide, in a gas-liquid contacting apparatus to generate a loaded capture composition, and (ii) releasing carbon dioxide from the loaded capture composition.
   USE - Capture of carbon dioxide from gas stream discharged from combustion process (claimed) including energy generation and industrial process, or non-combustion industrial process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances rate of carbon dioxide capture, and reduces interfacial mass transfer resistance by reducing interfacial resistance due to the usage of two partially-miscible liquid phases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The gas-liquid contacting apparatus is chosen from packed column (with random or structured packing, in co-flow, counter-flow, or cross-flow configurations), spray tower, plate or tray column, stirred tank reactor (in continuous or batch configuration), tubular flow reactor (under laminar or turbulent flow condition), bubble column reactor, falling film reactor, and membrane contactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cation of the salt of carboxylic acid is alkali metal and/or alkaline earth metal. The carboxylic acid comprises only carbon, hydrogen and oxygen. The aqueous solution of salt of carboxylic acid is aqueous solution of alkali metal salt of 2-5C aliphatic carboxylic acid. The solution has a concentration, such that the molar ratio of salt with respect to water is 1:2.5 to 1:15. The carboxylic acid is preferably acetic acid, propanoic acid, butyric acid or its branched derivative, pentanoic acid or its branched derivative, hexanoic acid or its branched derivative, heptanoic acid or its branched derivative, and/or octanoic acid or its branched derivative. The phase (a) further comprises carbonate salt(s), preferably alkali metal carbonate and/or alkaline earth metal carbonate, and an enzyme, preferably natural carbonic anhydrase or engineered carbonic anhydrase. The capture reagent is present in the phase (a) at a concentration of 2-15 M. The phase (b) is an organic solvent. The phase (b) comprises silicone/siloxane and ether. The organic solvent is chosen from 1,2-dimethoxypropane, 1,2-diethoxypropane, dipropylene glycol dimethyl ether, dipropylene glycol diethyl ether, dipropylene glycol methyl ethyl ether, tripropylene glycol dimethyl ether, tripropylene glycol diethyl ether, tripropylene glycol methyl ethyl ether, 1,2-dimethoxyethane, 1,2-diethoxyethane, ethylene glycol methyl ethyl ether, diethylene glycol dimethyl ether, diethylene glycol diethyl ether, diethylene glycol methyl ethyl ether, triethylene glycol dimethyl ether, triethylene glycol diethyl ether, triethylene glycol methyl ethyl ether, diisopropyl ether, dibutyl ether, ethyl butyl ether, methyl tert-butyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, octamethyltrisiloxane, decamethyltetrasiloxane, hexamethylcyclotrisiloxane, octamethylcyclotetrasiloxane, butyl acetate, pentyl acetate, pentyl propionate, hexyl propionate, hexyl butyrate, heptyl butyrate, gamma -butyrolactone, gamma -octanoic lactone, 2-pentanone, 3-heptanone, 4-octanone, hexanal, heptanal, octanal and decanal. The volume ratio of the phase (a) with respect to phase (b) is 1:3 to 9:1. The phase (b) comprises a solvent which is ether-based compound of formula: R1-O-(CH2CHR2-O)n-R3 (I) or its mixture.R1,R3 = unsubstituted 1-4C alkyl;R2 = H or Me;andn = 1-4.Preferred Property: The physical solubility of carbon dioxide in the phase (b) is greater than the physical solubility of carbon dioxide in the phase (a). Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide is released by heating the loaded capture composition, and/or subjecting the loaded capture composition to a stream of stripping gas e.g. air, and/or reducing the pressure above the loaded capture composition to provide a stripped capture composition.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Separation and recovery of carbon dioxide for e.g. for global warming countermeasure, involves flowing tertiary amine solution and gas containing carbon dioxide, stopping flow of gas, and heating solid absorbent and tertiary amine solution.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202112865L
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and recover of carbon dioxide involves (1) flowing a tertiary amine aqueous solution and a gas containing carbon dioxide through a solid absorbent material on which amine is supported on a porous carrier to absorb carbon dioxide, (2) stopping the flow of a gas containing carbon dioxide, supplying a tertiary amine aqueous solution, and replacing a gas other than carbon dioxide with a tertiary amine aqueous solution, (3) heating a solid absorbent and a tertiary amine aqueous solution to desorb carbon dioxide, and (4) cooling the solid absorbent and the tertiary amine aqueous solution.
   USE - Separation and recovery of carbon dioxide for global warming countermeasure, coal-fired power generation, and fuel conversion from coal to biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separation and recovery of carbon dioxide with high purity, low energy consumption, improved absorption efficiency, and excellent durability.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine comprises at least one chosen from ethanolamines such as monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, and their alkylated product, ethyleneamine such as ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine, piperazine, and aminoethylpiperazine, and their alkylated products, ethylene oxide adducts, and propylene oxide adducts. The tertiary amine comprises at least one chosen from ethanolamine such as triethanolamine, N,N-dimethylethanolamine or N-methyldiethanolamine, ethyleneamine derivative such as tetrakis(hydroxyethyl)ethylenediamine, tetrakis(hydroxypropyl)ethylenediamine, methylated form of hydroxyethylethylenediamine, methylated form of hydroxyethylpiperazine, morpholine derivatives such as methylmorpholine and hydroxyethylmorpholine, and imidazole derivatives such as dimethylimidazole.
