PN WO2018097747-A1
TI Method of recycling carbon dioxide gas using trees, involves sending carbon dioxide gas from source of its release to place where vegetation is concentrated and carbon dioxide is consumed by vegetation with release of oxygen into atmosphere.
AU KASATKIN E I
AE KASATKIN E I (KASA-Individual)
GA 201842158S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sending carbon dioxide gas from the source of its release to places where vegetation is concentrated and the carbon dioxide is consumed by vegetation, with the release of oxygen into the atmosphere. The fire-prone areas of a surface are isolated from atmospheric oxygen by a layer of carbon dioxide gas that spreads along the earth.
   USE - Method of recycling carbon dioxide gas by means of using trees, plants, and life forms by virtue of photosynthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The risk of fires is decreased. The plants, while receiving additional quantities of carbon dioxide gas, begin vigorously growing and bearing fruit, which increases their yield and increases the total amount of herbage on the planet, which accelerates the process of converting carbon dioxide into oxygen. The rate at which carbon dioxide is converted to oxygen constantly increases, thus eliminating the problem of global warming.

TI Generating selected material from natural brine involves heating natural brine in vessel to first predetermined temperature, and adding carbon dioxide into vessel where carbon dioxide mixes with natural brine forming mixture.
AU NAKANO J N
   NAKANO A
   BENNETT J P
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
GA 202118157K
AB    NOVELTY - Generating selected material from natural brine involves heating natural brine in vessel to first predetermined temperature, and brine comprises portion of selected material, and adding carbon dioxide into vessel where carbon dioxide mixes with natural brine forming mixture such that carbon dioxide/P is first predetermined value. The method involves holding mixture for first predetermined time after carbon dioxide addition such that solid is precipitated from mixture, separating precipitated solid from mixture, leaving second brine substantially comprising selected material, heating second brine to second predetermined temperature, adding carbon dioxide into vessel where carbon dioxide mixes with second brine forming second mixture such that carbon dioxide/P is second predetermined value, and holding second mixture for second predetermined time after carbon dioxide addition such that selected material is precipitated from second mixture, and removing selected material precipitate.
   USE - Method for generating selected material from natural brine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves holding second mixture for a second predetermined time by adjusting pressure, temperature, or surface energy of the natural brine. The first predetermined value is greater than 18 g/atm. The second predetermined value is greater than 200 g/atm. The first predetermined temperature is equal to or greater than 260?oC. The carbon dioxide is a liquid. The carbon dioxide is a gas. The first predetermined time is longer than 20 minutes. The precipitated solid is further separated by moving mixture into a second vessel, and passing the mixture through a separation device. The second predetermined temperature is equal to first predetermined temperature. The second predetermined time is at least 20 minutes. The selected material precipitate is removed by moving the mixture into a second vessel. Preferred Components: The selected material is lithium. The selected material is a rare earth element. The natural brine is sea water, saline water, fresh water, synthetic solutions, or industrial liquid wastes.

AE DAQING JINJU PETROLEUM TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 201905393U
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide composite model preparation method involves injecting pre-liquid before injecting carbon dioxide into puff, performing self-priming, pumping, injecting carbon dioxide, such that effective thickness of layer needs to be injected with 1-2.5 tons liquid carbon dioxide and utilizing displacement fluid, where the pre-liquid is soluble chloride salt solution, concentration of chloride salt solution is 5-30 mass%, the pre-liquid is continuously injected from annular space of oil jacket, injection amount is 100-120 m3, wellhead injection pressure does not exceed 10 MPa, the displacement fluid is mixture of hot water and soluble chloride solution, content of chloride salt in the displacement fluid is 2-7% and 10 m3 displacement fluid is required every 1000 m.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide composite model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing carbon dioxide composite model by utilizing ion exchange effect of carbon dioxide and chloride salts, effectively reducing pH around near well and neutralizing acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chloride salt is potassium chloride or ammonium chloride.

PN WO2021182162-A1; JP2021141003-A; CN115210906-A
TI Method for manufacturing secondary battery e.g.            all-solid-state battery, involves merging and mixing            slurry with carbon dioxide gas or supercritical fluid            of liquefied carbon dioxide gas or carbon dioxide gas,            and coating mixed mixture or layer coating several            layers on target object.
AU MASAFUMI M
   MATSUNAGA M
AE MTEK-SMART CORP (MTEK-Non-standard)
   MTEK-SMART CORP (MTEK-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6628E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressurizing and moving a                slurry (51) to a next process. The pressurized                carbon dioxide gas or a liquefied carbon dioxide                (2) gas or a supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide                gas is moved to a next process. The slurry is                merged and mixed with the carbon dioxide gas or the                supercritical fluid of liquefied carbon dioxide gas                or carbon dioxide gas. The mixed mixture is coated                or several layers are layered coated on the target                object with a coating device (5). The electrode                binder is polyvinylidene fluoride. 70 % or more of                the volatile matter excluding the carbon dioxide                gas or the carbon dioxide gas supercritical fluid                is normal methyl pyrrolidone.
   USE - Method for manufacturing secondary battery                e.g. all-solid-state battery (claimed) such as                lithium ion battery and lithium ion polymer battery                mounted in large-sized electric vehicles.

   MIAO S
   HE M
   WANG G

AE HARBIN BOILER FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 202276077B
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium-based heat carrier circulating flue                gas carbon dioxide capturing system comprises a                calcium-based compound heat carrier two-phase                mixing heater connected with a cyclone separating                heater and a generating device (B1) for receiving                high temperature flue air containing carbon dioxide                emitted from the generating device. A temperature                regulator is distributed to a hot carrier of the                flue oil gas and the calcium compound. The flue                acid and calcium carbonate heat carrier are                connected with the mixing calcining reactor. The                mixing calcine reactor is connected to a carbon                dioxide cooler for calcining the carbonate and                calcium sulfate by using the calcium-type heat                carrier to heat the generated carbon dioxide into                the carbonic cooler. The carbonic cooler is                connected with storage tank for cooling the carbon                dioxide entering, and cooling carbon dioxide to the                storage tank is stored.
   USE - Calcium-based heat carrier circulating flue                gas carbon dioxide capturing system for capturing                carbon dioxide in smoke. Uses include but are not                limited to thermal power plant, cement,                papermaking, industrial boiler and furnace                production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium-based compound has high efficiency                of carbon dioxide in the flue gas, low cost, high                concentration of trapping and recycling of the heat                carrier under the condition of multiple recycling                and utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                calcium-based heat carrier circulating flue gas                carbon dioxide capture method, which involves                mixing limestone replenishment system provides                calcium-based compounds to the mixed calcination                reactor, first calcium-based compound distributor                is based on the demand for the two-phase mixing                heater and the temperature regulator of the flue                gas and the calcium-based compound heat carrier,                and when the two-phase mixing heater of flue gas                and calcium-based compound heat carrier receives                high-temperature flue gas containing carbon                dioxide, mixing the high-temperature flue gas                containing carbon dioxide and the calcium-based                compound to obtain a high-temperature mixture,                entering the cyclone separation heater, heating and                separating the high temperature mixture to obtain                flue gas and calcium-based compound heat carrier                and send it to a temperature regulator, using a                calcium-based compound to absorb carbon dioxide in                the cooled flue gas to obtain a product, heating                and calcining the calcium carbonate and calcium                sulfate with the calcium-based compound heat                carrier to generate carbon dioxide;, cooling the                carbon dioxide, and storing the cooled carbon                dioxide in a carbon dioxide storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the calcium-based heat                carrier cycle flue gas carbon dioxide capture                system flow chart.

   H6Cyclone carbonizer
   H7Carbon dioxide cooler
   S1Cooler first calcium-based compound                distributor

GA 201956365M
AB    NOVELTY - Conjugated polymer and boron nitride composite                material comprises conjugated polymer i.e. biphenyl                compounds (I) and boron nitride.
   USE - The composite material is useful for adsorbing                carbon dioxide and monitoring slow release of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supramolecular polymer system regulates                aggregation state or conformation of conjugated                polymer and boron nitride by carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conjugated polymer and boron nitride composite                material comprises conjugated polymer i.e. biphenyl                compounds of formula (I) and boron nitride.
   n = polymerization degree (4-25);
   (x+y+z) = 1;
   x = 0.80-0.97;
   y = 0.01-0.2;
   z = 0-0.1;
   m = 1-12;
   q = 2-12;
   X = C, N or Si;
   Y1, Z = C or N; and
   R1 = 1-(dimethylamino)ethylideneamino,                dimethylamino, 1H-imidazole-4-yl, or formic acid                group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) method of absorbing carbon dioxide and                monitoring slow release of carbon dioxide,                comprising detecting series of carbon dioxide                concentrations and fluorescence change relationship                between carbon dioxide and (I) and boron nitride                composite material, (i) dissolving (I) in water to                obtain aqueous solution of (I), (ii) dispersing                boron nitride in water to obtain boron nitride                dispersion solution, (iii) preparing series of                known concentration of carbon dioxide aqueous                solution, (iv) mixing series of known concentration                of carbon dioxide aqueous solution with step (i)                product and step (ii) product in the same volume                ratio to obtain series of mixed solution containing                (I) and boron nitride complex having different                carbon dioxide concentration, (v) performing                fluorescence measurement on the step (iv) product                under action of excitation light, then recording                fluorescence emission spectrum of (I) in the series                of mixed solution respectively, and calculating the                fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET)                ratio, where the FRET ratio is ratio of                fluorescence intensity of two specific receptors to                donor peak of fluorescence resonance energy                transfer of (I) in the mixed solution containing                different carbon dioxide concentration, (vi)                changing the curve of FRET ratio with carbon                dioxide concentration based on the FRET ratio of                (I) in the series of mixed solutions obtained in                step (v) and the concentration of carbon dioxide in                the corresponding mixed solution, and (vii) the                difference between the obtained curve and the curve                without boron nitride is the ratio of the                difference between the difference and the ratio of                the FRET containing no boron nitride at the                concentration of the carbon dioxide is the                percentage of the carbon dioxide adsorbed by the                composite material; and
   (2) preparing supramolecular polymer system,                comprising mixing carbon dioxide aqueous solution                with aqueous solution of (I) and boron nitride                dispersion solution, incubating, initially                adsorbing carbon dioxide, then slowly releasing                carbon dioxide, and assembly the released carbon                dioxide promotes of (I) and boron nitride by                hydrophobic interaction and electrostatic                interaction to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of (I)                  to boron nitride is 1:1-1:10. In step (i) or (1),                  the concentration of (I) in aqueous solution of                  (I) is 0.1-20 mg/ml; and in step (ii) or (2), the                  concentration of boron nitride in boron nitride                  dispersion solution is 0.1-20 mg/ml. In step (iv)                  or (4), the volume between aqueous solution of                  (I), boron nitride dispersion solution to carbon                  dioxide aqueous solution is 10:5:485 ( mu l).                  Preferred Method: The fluorescence change                  relationship between (I) and boron nitride                  composite material under action of fixed                  concentration of carbon dioxide, comprises (1)                  dissolving (I) in water to obtain aqueous                  solution of (I), (2) dispersing boron nitride in                  water to obtain boron nitride dispersion                  solution, (3) preparing known concentration of                  carbon dioxide aqueous solution, (4) mixing known                  concentration of carbon dioxide aqueous solution                  with step (i) product and step (2) product in a                  fixed volume ratio to obtain mixed solution                  containing (I) and boron nitride composite                  material containing fixed carbon dioxide                  concentration, (5) performing fluorescence                  measurement on the step (4) product under action                  of excitation light, then recording fluorescence                  emission spectrum of (I) in the mixed solution at                  different incubation time, and calculating the                  fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET)                  ratio, (6) changing the curve of FRET ratio with                  incubation time based on the FRET ratio of (I) in                  the mixed solutions obtained in step (5) and the                  corresponding incubation time, and (7) the                  difference between the FRET value corresponding                  to any incubation time of the above curve and the                  FRET value of the incubation time of 0 minutes,                  the ratio of the difference to the FRET value of                  the incubation time of 0 minutes is the                  percentage of carbon dioxide released by the                  composite material. The step (iii) comprises                  preparing saturated aqueous solution of carbon                  dioxide, and mixing with ultrapure water in                  different ratio to obtain series of uniformly                  spaced 0 to saturated carbon dioxide aqueous                  solution, i.e. series of known concentration of                  carbon dioxide aqueous solution, where the                  concentration interval is 0.10-20 mM. In step (v)                  or (5), the wavelength of the excitation light is                  375-450 nm; the FRET ratio is ratio of                  fluorescence intensity (I2) of the conjugated                  polymer in the mixed solution at wavelength of                  531 nm to fluorescence intensity (I1) of (I) in                  the mixed solution at wavelength of 418 nm, i.e.                  I2/I1.

PN CN113436998-A; CN113436998-B
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide silicon block cleaning device useful in silicon block processing system, has compression device for compressing gasified carbon dioxide from tank body to form liquid carbon dioxide, and carbon dioxide storage tank for storing liquid carbon oxide.
AU WU P
   SUN J
   TIAN X
   WU F
AE JIANGSU XINHUA SEMICONDUCTOR MATERIAL (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4770D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a groove body provided with a closed space for cleaning a silicon block. A pressure adjusting device adjusts pressure in the closed space to be higher than supercritical pressure of carbon dioxide before cleaning the silicon block, and maintains pressure unchanged in the cleaning process. A temperature control device adjusts temperature in the sealed space to-be higher than the supercritical temperature of the carbon dioxide. A compression device compresses gasified carbon dioxide from the tank body to form liquid carbon dioxide, and stores the carbon carbon dioxide required by the tank. The carbon dioxide storage tank stores the liquid carbon oxide, and provides the carbon-carbon dioxide needed by the storage tank and recycles the gaseous carbon dioxide needed from the compression device.
   USE - The device is used for silicon block processing system (claimed) of an electronic-grade polysilicon in integrated circuit industry
   ADVANTAGE - The residue in the silicon block micro-pore can be effectively washed out, so that the impurity on the surface of the silicon blocks is qualified. The repeated utilization of carbon dioxide is realized, so as to effectively reduce the production cost. The etching device in the second stage processing unit improves the etching effect of the surface impurity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) silicon block processing system; and
   (2) silicon block processing method.

PN CN111764875-A
TI Method for determining carbon dioxide injection amount for auxiliary throughput and oil increasing, involves determining crude oil compression coefficient and carbon dioxide injection amount in negative correlation relationship.
AU YUAN G
   LUO Q
   GU Y
   SONG Z
   XU X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP HENAN OILFIE (SNPC-C)
GA 2020A20055
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining pore volume, oil saturation, crude oil density, gas oil ratio, gas-water ratio and crude oil compression coefficient for substituting into a carbon dioxide injection amount model to obtain carbon dioxide injection amount. The carbon dioxide injection quantity model is constructed according to relationship of the carbon dioxide injection amount, the pore volume, the oil saturation, the crude oil density, the gas oil ratio, the gas-water ratio and the crude oil compression coefficient. The pore volume, the crude oil density, the gas-oil ratio and the gas-water ratio are determined in positive correlation with the carbon dioxide injection amount. The crude oil compression coefficient and the carbon dioxide injection amount are determined in negative correlation relationship.
   USE - Method for determining carbon dioxide injection amount for auxiliary throughput and oil increasing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing considering a physical property parameter of an oil layer and solubility of carbon dioxide in oil and water and improving obtained carbon dioxide injection amount and high oil extraction efficiency and high crude oil yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an oil extraction method; and
   (2) a device for determining carbon dioxide injection amount for auxiliary throughput and oil increasing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the Method for determining carbon dioxide injection amount for auxiliary throughput and oil increasing. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Producing argon and carbon dioxide involves performing oxy-combustion of fuel (9) with oxygen-depleted oxygen but still containing argon from cryogenic air separation apparatus and compressing carbon dioxide rich fumes to produce gas.
AU TERRIEN P
   ASSE A
   CHAMBRON N
   JOLY L
   DAVIDIAN B
   LOCKWOOD F
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201930677D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing argon and carbon dioxide comprises: performing oxy-combustion of a fuel (9) with an oxygen-depleted oxygen but still containing argon from a cryogenic air separation apparatus (3); compressing carbon dioxide rich fumes produced by the oxy-combustion to produce a gas to be treated; catalytically oxidizing the excess oxygen present in the gas to be treated, thus obtaining an oxygen-free gas (19); performing water purification of the gas to be treated; separating carbon dioxide in the gas to be treated at a sub-ambient temperature, by partial condensation and/or distillation to produce a carbon dioxide enriched stream (27) and an incondensable stream (29), depleted in carbon dioxide; separating the remaining carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide depleted stream to form product carbon dioxide and a mixture of nitrogen and argon; and cryogenically separating the nitrogen and argon mixture in the air separation apparatus to produce argon as a product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing argon and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the size and chooses exactly the flow of argon produced regardless of the size of the oxy-combustion unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of producing argon and carbon dioxide.
   Cryogenic air separation apparatus (3)
   Fuel with oxygen-depleted oxygen (9)
   Oxygen-free gas (19)
   Carbon dioxide enriched stream (27)
   Incondensable stream (29)
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   (3) a computer device comprising a processor                and a memory to implement a method for controlling                carbon dioxide concentration of a fresh air system                installed in an underground railway; and
   (4) a storage medium for storing computer                program to execute a method for controlling carbon                dioxide concentration of a fresh air system                installed in an underground railway.

TI New carbon-dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate            used for e.g. food packaging, is carbon-dioxide-based            polyester-polycarbonate containing double bonds before            crosslinking, or is carbon-dioxide-based            polyester-polycarbonate having network structure after            crosslinking.
AU HUANG Z
   HUANG S
   HAN D
   WU C
   WANG S
   XIAO M
   LIANG J
   MENG Y
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2022962766
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate                which is a carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate (I) containing double bonds                before crosslinking, or a carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate (II) having a network                structure after crosslinking, is new.
   USE - The carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate is used for food packaging                and medical material.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate containing double bond does                not crosslink in the aggregate-processing process,                so as to avoid too large viscosity in the kettle or                difficult to process. The double bond in the                presence of high temperature and thermal initiator                can be opened in situ and connected with different                chain polymer forming a net-shaped structure, thus                greatly reducing the melt index and improving the                mechanical strength of the copolymer. It also can                adjust the dosage of the tetrahydrophthalic                anhydride to control the melt-index of the final                product, different satisfy requirements.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon-dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate                which is a carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate of formula (I) containing                double bonds before crosslinking, or a                carbon-dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate of                formula (II) having a network structure after                crosslinking, is new.
   a,b,c = 1 or more; d = 0 o more; B' =                structure (I); and A = structure of formula                (a1).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate, which involves adding                monomer phthalic anhydride, propylene oxide, a                Lewis base catalyst, an organic borane,                tetrahydrophthalic anhydride as an additive to an                autoclave, filling carbon dioxide, heating to carry                out ring-opening copolymerization reaction, and                precipitating, drying and granulating to obtain a                purified carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate containing double bonds,                banburying the purified carbon-dioxide-based                polyester-polycarbonate and a thermal initiator at                110&#176;C for 1-10 minutes, crosslinking the                mixed resin by hot pressing on a flat vulcanizer at                160-180&#176;C for 1-10 minutes to obtain an                in-situ crosslinked ultra-low-melting-index                carbon-dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate                film.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  tetrahydrophthalic anhydride is 0.01-5 %mass. The                  usage amount of phthalic anhydride is 10-40                  %mass. Structure (I) or (II) contains a                  polyether. The polymer comprises less than 5                  %mass polyether. The molar ratio of Lewis base                  catalyst and organoborane is 1:0.5-5. The                  charging ratio of Lewis base catalyst and                  phthalic anhydride is 1:50-5000. The feeding                  ratio of tetrahydrophthalic anhydride and                  phthalic anhydride is 1:1-25. The molar feeding                  ratio of propylene oxide and phthalic anhydride                  is 1:2-20.Proffered Conditions: The carbon dioxide                  pressure in the ring-opening copolymerization                  reaction is 0.5-3 MPa, the reaction temperature                  is 40-100&#176;C, and the reaction time is                  4-20h.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thermal initiator                  is one or more of dicumyl peroxide, di-tert-butyl                  peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, and                  azobisisobutyronitrile. The usage amount of                  thermal initiator is less than 1 %mass of the                  carbon-dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate                  containing double bonds. The Lewis base catalyst                  is bis(triphenylphosphoranyl)ammonium chloride or                  tetra-n-butylammonium chloride. The organic                  borane is triethylborane, tributylboron or                  triphenylboron.

A carbon dioxide overflow after crushing concrete material is tested to determine whether carbon dioxide is chemically reactive curing or physically sealed in the material. PN CN108543530-A; CN108543530-B
TI Oxygen vacancy-rich zinc oxide nanosheet useful            for electroreduction of carbon dioxide.
AU KONG X
   LIU Y
   GENG Z
   LIU Z
   ZENG J
AE UNIV SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINA ADVANCED TEC (UYSC-Non-standard)
GA 201875915W
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen vacancy-rich zinc oxide nanosheet, is                claimed. The average size is 800 to -1 m.
   USE - The nanosheet is useful for electroreduction                of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanosheet has high catalytic activity,                high selectivity and good the stability. The method                is economical and easy for mass synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing an oxygen vacancy-rich zinc oxide                nanosheet, comprising etching the zinc oxide                nanosheet under a hydrogen plasma atmosphere to                obtain the oxygen vacancy-rich zinc oxide                nanosheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The average thickness                  is 10-15 nano. The hydrogen plasma is a power                  source of 150-250 watts; preferably, the hydrogen                  plasma is a power of 200 watts. In the process of                  etching, the hydrogen pressure is maintained at                  5-15 Torr; preferably 10 Torr, and the etching                  time is 100-150 seconds; preferably 120 seconds.                  Preferred Method: The preparation method of the                  zinc oxide nanosheet is as follows: dissolving                  the zinc nitrate hexahydrate and                  hexamethylenetetramine into the water to obtain                  the mixed solution, adding an organic solution of                  a surfactant to the mixed solution, reacting for                  80-100 minutes, stirring at 55-65&#176; C,                  finishing reaction, cooling, centrifuging,                  washing and drying, calcining to obtain the zinc                  oxide nanosheet in the air. In the mixed solution                  the concentration of zinc nitrate hexahydrate and                  hexamethylenetetramine is 20-30 mmol/l. The                  organic solution of the surfactant is a                  chloroform solution of sodium dodecyl sulfate.                  The calcination temperature is 180-220&#176;                  C, and the calcination time is 50-70 seconds. The                  zinc oxide nanosheet is prepared by following                  process: dissolving the zinc nitrate hexahydrate                  and hexamethylenetetramine in 17 ml water to                  obtain mixed liquid, where the mixed solution,                  the concentration of zinc nitrate hexahydrate and                  hexamethylenetetramine are 25 mmol/l, adding 10                  &#956; l chloroform solution and 1 &#956;                  g sodium dodecyl sulfate into the mixed solution,                  stirring uniformly and placing in oven reacting                  at 60&#176; C for 90 minutes, finishing                  reaction, cooling, centrifuging, washing and                  drying, calcining to obtain the zinc oxide                  nanosheet in air, where the calcining temperature                  is 200&#176; C and the calcining time is 60                  seconds.

GA 2021C12499
AB    NOVELTY - The PM filter regeneration system has a heating unit, which heats the gas made to flow in into a particulate matter (PM) filter. A detection unit detects the carbon dioxide levels in the gas which flows out of the PM filter, and oxygen concentration. A controller controls the heating state of PM filter. The controller determines the presence or absence of fault combustion based on the variation of carbon dioxide and oxygen levels and the oxygen concentration. The heating of the filter is stopped, when it is determined that the fault combustion has arisen.
   USE - PM filter regeneration system for exhaust gas purification system of internal combustion engine, such as diesel engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The generation of fault combustion of particulate matter in the particulate filter can be rapidly determined by judging the presence or absence of the fault combustion in the filter based on the variation of the carbon dioxide levels in the gas flowing out of the filter. The heating of the particulates filter is stopped or suppressed rapidly, when determining that fault combustion has arisen, so that the damage and deterioration by the heat of the particles filter is prevented appropriately.

PN CN113247898-A
TI Performing decarbonization and co-production of            carbon monoxide from coal gangue based on carbon            dioxide oxidation involves grind coal gangue to 100-200            mesh, and placing the ground gangue in high-temperature            reactor.
AU ZHANG T
   DOU Z
   LV G
   LIU Y
   ZHANG Z
   HE J
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
   NEU NONFERROUS SOLID WASTE TECHNOLOGY (NEUN-Non-standard)
GA 202197616H
AB    NOVELTY - Performing decarbonization and co-production                of carbon monoxide from coal gangue based on carbon                dioxide oxidation involves grind coal gangue to                100-200 mesh; placing the ground gangue in a                high-temperature reactor filled with carbon dioxide                (CO2) gas; adjusting and controlling the partial                pressure and flow rate of CO2 gas in the reactor to                ensure that the partial pressure of CO2 is less                than 1 atmosphere (atm), and rapid decarburization                in the range of 950-1150 degrees C for 5-120                minutes, or using induced air to keep the pressure                in the reactor more than 1 atm, so that the coal                gangue can be quickly decarbonized in the range of                800-1000 degrees C by 5-90 minutes. The residual                CO2 in the decarburization gas is separated to                obtain pure carbon monoxide (CO) fuel, and at the                same time, high-activity decarburization tailings                is used for comprehensive utilization of high-value                resources.

PN CN112973704-A
TI Preparing copper/zinc oxide catalyst with light enhancement effect in the photothermal catalysis of carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide and/or methane, involves dissolving copper salt and zinc salt in water to obtain solution A.
AU ZHENG Z
   HE X
   HUANG B
   WANG Z
   LIU Y
   WANG P
   ZHANG X
   CHENG H
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202171869T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper/zinc oxide catalyst with light enhancement effect involves dissolving copper salt and zinc salt in water to obtain solution A. The carbonate solution is added dropwise to solution A under heating and stirring conditions to obtain precursor solution. The precursor solution is aged and separated at room temperature to obtain the precursor. The precursor is first heat-treated in an air atmosphere, and then the second heat-treatment is performed in an inert atmosphere containing hydrogen to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing copper/zinc oxide catalyst with light enhancement effect used in the photothermal catalysis of carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide and/or methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has obvious light enhancement effect and exhibits excellent stability, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a photothermal catalyst, which comprises a catalytically active component and a carrier, where the catalytically active component is the copper/zinc oxide catalyst with light-enhancing effect; and
   (2) a method for preparing carbon monoxide and/or methane by photothermal catalysis of carbon dioxide, which involves placing copper/zinc oxide catalyst with light enhancement effect or the photothermal catalyst in a mixed in the atmosphere, carrying out thermal catalytic reaction under the conditions of light and heating, where the mixed atmosphere includes carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and the heating condition is 250-350 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of copper salt, zinc salt and carbonate is 1:4:5-4:1:5, the temperature at which the carbonate solution is added dropwise to the solution A for reaction is 40-90 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-3.5 hours. The reaction temperature is 55-75 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1.5-2.5 hours. The aging time is 6-18 hours. The method of separation after aging is suction filtration, washing, and drying, and the drying condition is drying at 90-110 degrees C for 8-12 hours. The conditions of the first heat treatment includes temperature of 300-400 degrees C for 3-5 hours, the volume percentage of hydrogen in the inert atmosphere containing hydrogen is 10-15%. The conditions of the second heat treatment includes temperature of 250-350 degrees C for 1.5-4.5 hours. The thermal catalytic reaction time is 2.9-3.1 hours or the molar ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen is 1:3.6-4.4. The light intensity is 250-350 mW/cm2.

   Ball type valves (2)
   Syringe pump (3)

In step (II), spectra from a mixture of gases, preferably CO2 and CH4 is obtained, in molar concentrations of 0-100% under pressure of 5-300 bar, preferably 200 bar, at 0-300 degrees C, preferably 20-60 degrees C. In step (III), chemometric model is used to quantify adsorbed CO2 present in natural gas at high pressure. Preferred Components: In step (IV), the adsorbent is modified silica with amount of 0.1-5 g, preferably 1 g. PN CN110257042-A; CN110257042-B
TI Preparation of fracturing fluid for e.g. breaking gel, involves mixing methacrylate with aqueous dimethylamine solution, obtaining tertiary amine product, obtaining quaternary ammonium salt and adding clay stabilizer and thickener.
AU ZHENG C
   HUANG Z
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 201992239Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of fracturing fluid involves mixing short carbon chain methacrylate with aqueous dimethylamine solution in a ratio of 1:1-3 at room temperature for 24 hours, removing excess dimethylamine solution by rotary evaporation to obtain a tertiary amine product (1) as stimulus-responsive counterion and having a carbon dioxide/methane response property, passing carbon dioxide through 0.5-2 wt.% tertiary amine product (1), obtaining quaternary ammonium salt as counter ion salt, adding 1-5 wt.% anionic surfactant thickener, 0.3-2 wt.% clay stabilizer and balance water.
   USE - Preparation of fracturing fluid used for breaking gel, tight gas, coalbed methane and shale gas pressure cracking in reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaning fracturing fluid automatically breaks the gel in the reservoir and highly reducing the viscosity, which helps the return flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of fracturing fluid involves mixing short carbon chain methacrylate with aqueous dimethylamine solution in a ratio of 1:1-3 at room temperature for 24 hours, removing excess dimethylamine solution by rotary evaporation to obtain a tertiary amine product of formula (1) as stimulus-responsive counterion and having a carbon dioxide/methane response property, passing carbon dioxide through 0.5-2 wt.% compound (1), obtaining quaternary ammonium salt as counter ion salt, adding 1-5 wt.% anionic surfactant thickener, 0.3-2 wt.% clay stabilizer and balance water.
   R = 4-8C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (1) is protonated to quaternary ammonium salt under the action of carbon dioxide and can be used as a counter ion, and deprotecting the amine product by introducing methane continuously. The anionic surfactant comprises at least one of sodium oleate, sodium erucate, sodium lauryl sulfate and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. The quaternary ammonium salt counterion formed by the protonation of the compound (1) is unstable under the action of reservoir temperature and natural gas, deprotonating slowly and becoming a tertiary amine, and the clean fracturing fluid can automatically break the gel.

PN CN109592092-A; CN109592092-B
TI Method for ensuring polypropylene infusion bottle filled with sodium bicarbonate injection bottle type, involves adding outer bag, filling carbon dioxide between outer bag and polypropylene infusion bottle, and sealing outer bag.
AU LIU T
   SUN P
AE CISEN PHARM CO LTD (CISE-Non-standard)
GA 201935026P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves treating the preparation system with a disodium edentate solution. The water for pre-filling is prepared with carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is stirred and dissolved and used to adjust the pH value. The filling choose polypropylene bottle weight 12-16g, filling temperature is 45-55 degrees C, exhaust parameter 2.5 inside, outer 3.0, continuous detection PH value during filling and is sterilized at 115 degrees for 30 minutes, product temperature is 60-80 degrees C. The pressure of the sterilizing cabinet is controlled to reach a constant pressure state to ensure constant pressure sterilization. The constant pressure is 220-240 kPa. When the temperature is lowered to 50-60 degrees C, the compressed air pressure is automatically controlled, and the temperature of the outlet is 40-50 degree C. The outer bag is added, the CO2 is filled between the outer bag and the polypropylene infusion bottle, and the outer bag is sealed.
   USE - Method for ensuring polypropylene infusion bottle filled with sodium bicarbonate injection bottle type.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for ensuring the bottle type of the sodium bicarbonate injection in the polypropylene infusion bottle solves the convexity caused by the decomposition and dissolution of carbon dioxide in the bottle type of the sodium bicarbonate injection bottle in the polypropylene infusion bottle and the phenomenon of the bottom and the bottle.

PT P
PN CN213642922-U
TI Portable carbon dioxide fire extinguisher automatic releasing device, has releasing units fixed on machine frame, and cylinder connected with air pump through air pipe, where cylinder extends out of rod to pressing handle to release carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN Z
   HU C
   GU H
   TIAN L
   CHEN S
AE PLA NO 63691 TROOPS (PLAS-Non-standard)
GA 2021810225
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a portable carbon dioxide fire extinguisher automatic releasing device, belonging to the technical field of carbon dioxide fire extinguisher starting device. comprising a machine frame and several releasing units; several said releasing units are fixed on the machine frame; the releasing unit respectively a cylinder; the cylinder is provided with a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher; the pressure handle of the carbon dioxide fire extinguisher is set in the bracket; the cylinder extending rod is opposite to the pressing handle; the cylinder is connected with the air pump through the air pipe, driven by the air pump; the cylinder extends out of the rod close to the pressing handle to release carbon dioxide or away from the pressing handle to stop releasing carbon dioxide. The invention drives the carbon dioxide fire extinguisher by the air pump to automatically release carbon dioxide and stop releasing in time, which saves carbon dioxide. not only has simple structure, convenient operation, low cost, but also can test when simulating training.
DC P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).)
MC P35-C01A1; P35-C01C1; P35-C03; P35-C99
IP A62C-013/68; A62C-013/76; A62C-013/78; A62C-099/00

AD CN213642922-U    CN21347616    10 Jul 2020
PI CN21347616    10 Jul 2020
UT DIIDW:2021810225
ER

PT P
PN DE102020101054-A1; WO2021144359-A1; CN114945545-A
TI Producing at least one 2-8C olefin, comprises e.g.            feeding carbon dioxide and hydrogen into hydrogenation            step, and adding carbon dioxide and hydrogen into            hydrogenation step, reacting with one another in on a            bifunctional catalyst.
AU MEISWINKEL A
   ZANDER H
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 202182364N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one 2-8C olefin, comprises                feeding carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a                hydrogenation step , adding at least part of carbon                dioxide and at least part of hydrogen into                hydrogenation step, reacting with one another in on                a bifunctional catalyst via an oxygenate as an                intermediate product to at least one olefins,                providing hydrogen that is fed to hydrogenation                step at least in part by a reforming step in which                methane and water are converted into hydrogen,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, feeding carbon                dioxide into hydrogenation step partly using                reforming step, providing at least a portion of                hydrogen and a part of carbon dioxide by reforming                to provide a gas mixture which contains hydrogen,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, reacting at                least a part of gas mixture without separating                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide,                partly providing carbon dioxide which is fed to                hydrogenation step using reforming step.
   USE - The method is useful for producing at least                one 2-8C olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces other comparatively                short-chain olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing at least one 2-8C olefin, comprises                feeding carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a                hydrogenation step, adding at least part of the                carbon dioxide and at least part of the hydrogen                into the hydrogenation step, reacting with one                another in on a bifunctional catalyst via an                oxygenate as an intermediate product to the at                least one olefins, providing the hydrogen that is                fed to the hydrogenation step at least in part by a                reforming step in which methane and water are                converted into hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide, feeding carbon dioxide into the                hydrogenation step partly using the reforming step,                providing at least a portion of the hydrogen and a                part of the carbon dioxide by the reforming to                provide a gas mixture which contains hydrogen,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, reacting at                least a part of the gas mixture without separating                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide,                partly providing carbon dioxide which is fed to the                hydrogenation step using the reforming step,                providing at least a part of the hydrogen and a                part of the carbon dioxide by the reforming step                using the reforming step to provide a gas mixture                containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide and to supply at least part of the gas                mixture to the hydrogenation step without                separating hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant (100) for production of at least one 2-8C                olefin, comprising with a hydrogenation reactor                which is set up to subject carbon dioxide and                hydrogen to a hydrogenation step in which part of                the carbon dioxide and part of the hydrogen, which                are subjected to the hydrogenation step are reacted                with one another over a bifunctional catalyst via                an oxygenate as an intermediate product to form the                at least one olefins, a reforming reactor provided                to provide the hydrogen that is fed to the                hydrogenation step which is set up to carry out a                reforming step in which methane and water are                converted to hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide, and the plant is set up to convert the                carbon dioxide that is fed to the hydrogenation                step, partly using the reforming step and partly                independently of the reforming step providing the                step, where providing at least a portion of the                hydrogen, a portion of the carbon monoxide and a                portion of the carbon dioxide by means of the                reforming step (20) comprises, using the reforming                step (20), a gas mixture containing hydrogen,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and at least                part of the gas mixture is fed to the hydrogenation                step (10) without separating hydrogen, carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the plant.
   Plant (100)
   Feed stream (101)
   Product or process stream (102)
   Feed stream (103)
   Feed stream (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methane is                  provided at least in part using natural gas. The                  carbon monoxide is formed in the reforming step                  and fed to the hydrogenation step, where the                  molar ratio between carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide in a synthesis gas formed in the                  reforming step is not less than 1:1. The molar                  ratio between the carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide fed to the hydrogenation step is not less                  than 1:1 due to an addition of the carbon dioxide                  provided independently of the reforming step. A                  gas mixture is formed in the reforming step, at                  least a portion of which is fed to the                  hydrogenation step in an unchanged material                  composition, in particular except for the water                  content. The molar ratio between the carbon                  dioxide and the hydrogen fed to the hydrogenation                  step is 1:6-1:1. A molar ratio between the                  methane and the water which are fed to the                  reforming step is 1:8-4:1. The carbon dioxide                  which is provided using the reforming step is fed                  into the hydrogenation step in a first gas                  stream, and in which the carbon dioxide which is                  produced independently of the reforming step is                  supplied to the hydrogenation step in a second                  gas stream, a quantitative ratio between the                  first and the second gas stream is selected such                  that the components provided with the first gas                  stream and the second gas stream meet a                  stoichiometric requirement in the hydrogenation                  step. The one olefin is propylene or multiple                  olefins comprising propylene. At least part of                  the carbon dioxide that is fed into the                  hydrogenation step and/or at least part of the                  hydrogen that is fed to the hydrogenation step in                  the hydrogenation step is not implemented. At                  least one olefins is removed from the                  hydrogenation step as part of a product mixture                  which, in addition to the at least one olefins,                  comprises at least carbon dioxide, hydrogen                  and/or at least one paraffin as further                  components, with at least a portion the further                  components are separated from the product mixture                  and at least some of them are returned to the                  hydrogenation step and/or the reforming step. The                  pressure of 10-100 bar is used in the reforming                  step and a pressure of 1-100 bar and a                  temperature of 100-520 degrees C are used in the                  hydrogenation step. The reforming step is carried                  out at a higher pressure level than the                  hydrogenation step.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E10-J02C3; E11-D02; E11-E02; E11-F03; H04-E04; H04-E05; H04-E08; H04-F02E; N07-B02; N07-C02; N07-D03; N07-D04

PN WO2021188897-A1; AU2021237707-A1
TI Preparation of ethylene from carbon dioxide            involves providing gaseous stream including carbon            dioxide, providing water, converting carbon dioxide and            water to organic intermediate and oxygen gas,            separating oxygen gas and converting.
AU POWERS D H
   KARIMI T
   ZELLER R L
   PACE J
   GARMON M A
   NGUYEN T H
AE OXY LOW CARBON VENTURES LLC (OXYL-Non-standard)
   OXY LOW CARBON VENTURES LLC (OXYL-Non-standard)
GA 2021B58880
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ethylene from carbon dioxide                involves providing a gaseous stream including more                than 1 vol.% carbon dioxide, providing water,                converting the carbon dioxide and the water to an                organic intermediate and oxygen gas in the presence                of light, separating the oxygen gas from the                organic intermediate and converting the organic                intermediate to ethylene and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Preparation of ethylene from carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Ethylene can be biosynthesized efficiently and                economically.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                (20) for producing ethylene, which comprises a                bioreactor (21) including photosynthetic                microorganisms that convert carbon dioxide to                organic intermediate, and having a carbon dioxide                inlet and an outlet for the organic intermediate,                and a bioreactor (41) in fluid communication with                the bioreactor (21) and including microorganisms                that convert the organic intermediate produced in                the bioreactor (21) to ethylene. The bioreactor                (41) has an outlet for gaseous materials including                ethylene, and outlet for fluid materials including                unreacted organic intermediate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system.
   System (20)
   Bioreactors (21,41)
   Reactor (23)
   Conduit (51)
   Carbon dioxide separator (61)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conversion of organic                  intermediate to ethylene and carbon dioxide takes                  place in the presence of excess water, and                  consumes only a portion of the organic                  intermediate, and further involves routing the                  excess water and organic intermediate to process                  of converting the carbon dioxide and water to                  organic intermediate. The conversion of carbon                  dioxide and water to organic intermediate and                  oxygen gas takes place in a bioreactor (21)                  containing a microorganism capable of producing                  organic intermediate and oxygen gas from the                  carbon dioxide in the presence of light energy.                  The gaseous stream includes more than 3 vol.%                  carbon dioxide. The flue gas stream from                  combustion process is compressed to form the                  stream containing carbon dioxide. The organic                  intermediate-rich stream includes less than 30                  ppm oxygen. Oxygen gas is vented from bioreactor                  (21). The conversion of organic intermediate to                  ethylene and carbon dioxide takes place within a                  bioreactor (41) containing a microorganism                  capable of producing enzyme for converting the                  organic intermediate to ethylene and carbon                  dioxide in absence of light energy. Ethylene and                  the carbon dioxide form a product stream. Oxygen                  gas within the product stream is converted to                  carbon dioxide. Water and carbon dioxide are                  removed from product stream. Carbon dioxide is                  separated from ethylene to produce an                  ethylene-rich stream and a carbon dioxide stream,                  which is introduced to primary process of                  converting carbon dioxide to organic intermediate                  and water. The oxygen gas is separated from the                  organic intermediate by venting the oxygen gas                  from an aqueous medium in which the organic                  intermediate is contained.

PT P
PN CN108079617-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method for waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte, involves adding waste lithium-ion battery residue containing electrolyte, adding carbon dioxide and solubility enhancing agent, extracting.
AU LIU Y
   LIU D
   XIA S
   LIU C
   NIE B
   CUI H
AE CAMEL GROUP WUHAN GUANGGU RES & DEV CENT (CAME-Non-standard)
GA 201843533J
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method for waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte, involves (S1) closing the discharge port at the bottom of extraction kettle, adding waste lithium-ion battery residue containing electrolyte, closing charging opening at the top of extraction kettle, (S2) closing the top of carbon dioxide outlet, opening the bottom of carbon dioxide inlet, opening the extraction kettle heating device, using pressure pump to press the carbon dioxide and solubility enhancing agent into extraction kettle, maintaining the pressure of carbon dioxide in the extraction kettle above the critical pressure, extracting of waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte, (S3) opening the top of carbon dioxide outlet, making the carbon dioxide loaded with electrolyte to flow into the separation vessel, decompressing or heating, returning the carbon dioxide gas to the storage tank by pressure pump and collecting the electrolyte enriched in the bottom of separation vessel.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method for waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte with improved production efficiency, avoids the human body poisoning and environmental pollution caused by extraction of solvent residues, effectively prevents the degradation and dissipation of heat-sensitive substances and achieves electrolyte recovery rate of more than 80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The solubility enhancing agent is selected from chain carbonate, chain alkane or chain alcohol. The solubility enhancing agent is used in an amount of 5-30% by weight of the amount of electrolyte contained in waste lithium ion battery. The step (S1) comprises closing charging opening at the top of extraction kettle to complete the charging process. The step (S2) comprises opening the extraction kettle heating device, so that the temperature of the extraction kettle is set above the critical temperature of carbon dioxide, and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte. The step (S3) involves opening the top of carbon dioxide outlet after extraction of 10-60 minutes, decompressing or heating, so that the carbon dioxide and electrolyte gas-liquid are separated. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for waste lithium-ion battery electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solubility enhancing agent is selected from dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate, ethane, propane, butane, methanol and/or ethanol.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. AU CHEN J
   WU X
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV LIAONING TECHNICAL (ULNT-C)
GA 2022630969
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing foam particle wrapped with carbon                dioxide gas comprises (a) placing the phenolic foam                particles in an sealed container for degassing                treatment to obtain degassed foam particles, (b)                introducing a carbon dioxide gas into the sealed                container in a vacuum state, naturally filling the                degassed foam particles with carbon dioxide gas to                obtain foam particles filled with carbon dioxide                gas, (c) releasing the foam particles filled with                carbon dioxide gas into a melt adhesive tank                containing liquid hot melt adhesive, rolling the                foam particles filled with carbon dioxide gas on                the surface of the liquid hot-melt adhesive, fully                wrapping the hot-melt adhesive layer on the surface                to obtain foam particles with the hot-melt adhesive                layer wrapped on the surface and (d) using foam                particle mold sieve to take out the foam particles                coated with hot melt adhesive surface, cooling,                separating and processing to obtain the foam                particles coated with carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing foam                particle wrapped with carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam particle wrapped with carbon dioxide                gas has light weight and good fluidity; blocks the                cracks in the goaf; increases the ventilation                resistance of the goaf and inhibit air                leakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing foam particle wrapped with carbon                dioxide gas comprises (a) placing the phenolic foam                particles in a sealed container for degassing                treatment until the sealed container reaches a                vacuum state to obtain degassed foam particles, (b)                introducing a carbon dioxide gas into the sealed                container in a vacuum state, naturally filling the                degassed foam particles obtained in (a) with carbon                dioxide gas for 1 hour to obtain foam particles                filled with carbon dioxide gas, (c) releasing the                foam particles filled with carbon dioxide gas                obtained in (b) into a melt adhesive tank                containing liquid hot melt adhesive, rolling the                foam particles filled with carbon dioxide gas on                the surface of the liquid hot-melt adhesive for 30                minutes, fully wrapping the hot-melt adhesive layer                on the surface to obtain foam particles with the                hot-melt adhesive layer wrapped on the surface and                (d) using foam particle mold sieve to take out the                foam particles coated with hot melt adhesive                surface obtained in the (c), cooling, separating                and processing to obtain the foam particles coated                with carbon dioxide gas.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of foam particle wrapped with carbon dioxide gas                comprising releasing the foam particles wrapped                with carbon dioxide gas to the target position of a                goaf through a release device, blocking the                fracture in the goaf, preventing air leakage,                rupturing the outer coating layer of the foam                particles wrapped with carbon dioxide gas when the                ambient temperature reaches the initial stage of                coal spontaneous combustion, releasing carbon                dioxide gas, inerting the goaf and preventing                spontaneous combustion of coal in the goaf, where                the temperature in the initial stage of spontaneous                combustion of the coal is 50-70degrees                Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a) the                  phenolic foam particles has a porosity of 50% and                  a particle size specification of 3-5 mm. The                  purity of the carbon dioxide gas is greater than                  or equal to98%.Preferred Method: In the step (a) the                  pressure in the vacuum state is 10-5Pa to 10-1                  Pa. In the step (c) the melting point of the hot                  melt adhesive is 65degrees Celsius The step (c)                  process is carried out in a closed environment                  filled with carbon dioxide gas. The release                  device comprises a funnel and a pipeline. The                  pipeline is provided with a funnel and the end                  port of the pipeline extends into a goaf through                  an isolation net.

60revolutions/minute, (3) performing fractionation                reflux solvent to reactant, reacting to product                moisture and fully fractionate, (4) heating the                reaction water,                adjusting the temperature at 90-98degrees Celsius,                stirring, and controlling the stirring speed to be                100-150 revolutions/minute for 1.5-3 hours to                obtain reactant C, and (H3) cooling the reactant C,                introducing into a centrifuge for centrifugal                dehydration, feeding the dehydrated material into dioxide concentration within the isolated lung compartment over time, or method of assessing the functionality of a lung compartment in a patient, involves introducing a diagnostic catheter into the lung compartment, where the diagnostic catheter comprises a distal end, an occluding member at the distal end, and a proximal end configured to be attached to a console, inflating the occluding member to isolate the lung compartment, measuring oxygen concentration within the isolated lung compartment over time, and determining whether collateral ventilation is present in the isolated lung compartment based on the measured oxygen concentration within the isolated lung compartment over time, or method of assessing the functionality of a lung compartment in a patient, involves sealing a distal end of a catheter in an airway feeding the lung compartment by using an occluding member that is adapted to be expanded in an airway which feeds the lung compartment such that access to the lung compartment is provided only through a passage of the catheter when the occluding member is expanded, allowing air to enter the lung compartment through the passage in the catheter while the patient is inhaling, blocking air from being expelled from the lung compartment through the catheter passage while the patient is exhaling by using a one-way flow element adapted to be disposed within or in-line with the passage of the catheter so that flow in a proximal-to-distal direction is allowed and flow in a distal-to-proximal direction is inhibited or prevented, measuring flow into the lung compartment, and determining whether collateral ventilation is present in the lung compartment based on the measured measuring-flow into the lung compartment, or method of assessing the functionality of a lung compartment in a patient, involves sealing a distal end of a catheter in an airway feeding the lung compartment by using an occluding member that is adapted to be expanded in an airway which feeds the lung compartment such that access to the lung compartment is provided only through a passage of the catheter when the occluding member is expanded, allowing air to enter the lung compartment through the passage in the catheter while the patient is inhaling, blocking air from being expelled from the lung compartment through the catheter passage while the patient is exhaling by using a one-way flow element adapted to be disposed within or in-line with the passage of the catheter so that flow in a proximal-to-distal direction is allowed and flow in a distal-to-proximal direction is inhibited or prevented, measuring pressure within the lung compartment and flow into the lung compartment, and determining whether collateral ventilation is present in the lung compartment based on the measured pressure and flow.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step of determining whether collateral ventilation is present in the isolated lung compartment involves determining that no collateral ventilation is present if the carbon dioxide concentration within the isolated lung compartment plateaus over time, and determining that collateral ventilation is present if the carbon dioxide concentration within the isolated lung compartment fluctuates with breathing. The method involves determining a degree of collateral ventilation based on the slopes of different regions of the carbon dioxide concentration curves.

PN CN111134009-A
TI Forestry breeding method based on tissue culture comprises e.g. preparing culture medium, laying cotyledon leaf discs face down on culture medium, selecting regenerated buds and healthy growth, and detecting moisture content.
AU TONG B
   LIU D
   DING P
   LI W
   XIE X
AE SHANDONG FOREST GERMPLASM RESOURCE CENT (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202044071V
AB    NOVELTY - Forestry breeding method based on tissue culture comprises e.g. selecting leaves, rinsing with tap water, soaking, rinsing, and absorbing by sterile filter paper to dry residual liquid, removing leaf tip and petiole, laying cotyledon leaf discs face down on culture medium, real-timely monitoring system breeding situation through high-definition camera, culturing medium in dark, removing callus and transferring to medium, detecting light intensity, detecting moisture content, detecting concentration, unscrewing cap, performing timing operations on the time of light supplement, water supply, washing, heating, ventilation and cooling, disinfection and sterilization, and replenishing nutrient solution through timing program, real-timely transmitting detected light intensity, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide concentration, substrate PH and monitoring video to central controller via wireless connection, displaying detected light intensity, temperature, and humidity.
   USE - The method is useful for forestry breeding method based on tissue culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has higher callus induction rate, and induced callus is light green or green and has dense structure, and many green shoots and strong meristematic ability, with high rate of adventitious shoot differentiation; establishes efficient in vitro regeneration system, which improves survival rate and robustness of breeding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Forestry breeding method based on tissue culture comprises (i) selecting leaves of 10-15-year old healthy plant growing normally in the field, rinsing with tap water for 20-40 minutes, soaking in 75% alcohol for 20-50 seconds, rinsing with sterile water 3-5 times through rinsing device, and absorbing by sterile filter paper to dry residual liquid, removing the leaf tip and petiole, and cutting remaining part into leaf disc with size of 0.5x 1 cm, (ii) laying cotyledon leaf discs face down on culture medium, 20-30 cotyledon leaf discs per dish, and real-timely monitoring system breeding situation through high-definition camera, culturing medium in dark for 2-3 days, then transferring to light for cultivation 15-18 hours per day under light intensity of 2800- 3200 lx and culture temperature of room temperature to induce buds, replenishing water and nutrients to culture medium and culture substrates of culture bottle through water supply device and nutrient solution supplement device, (iii) selecting regenerated buds with normal terminal buds and healthy growth, removing callus and transferring to medium, at the room temperature, culturing for 16 hours every day under the light intensity of 1000- 2000, detecting light intensity and the temperature of culture medium and culture bottle by light sensor and temperature sensor, and controlling light supplement device, heating device and ventilation cooling device through central controller to adjust light intensity and temperature, (iv) detecting the moisture content of the culture medium and culture bottle through a soil moisture collector and a humidity sensor, detecting concentration of carbon dioxide of the culture medium and culture bottle and the pH of the culture substances by the carbon dioxide sensor and the soil PH sensor respectively, and using solar photovoltaic panels to power entire forestry breeding system, (v) unscrewing cap of culture bottle with robust seedlings rooting for about 3 weeks and with root length of about 4 cm, placing for 4 days, then half opening for 4 days, and replenishing water continuously during the period, when the root length reaches about 8 cm, hardening the seedlings for 4 days, washing away the culture medium at the roots of the seedlings with pure water through the rinsing device, and disinfecting and sterilizing the robust seedlings by a disinfection and sterilization device, moving into the autoclaved substances and covering with film to keep quality, 15 days later, gradually exposing to air, removing plastic film to grow and develop naturally, and (vi) performing timing operations on the time of light supplement, water supply, washing, heating, ventilation and cooling, disinfection and sterilization, and replenishing nutrient solution through timing program, real-timely transmitting detected light intensity, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide concentration, substrate PH and monitoring video to central controller via wireless connection, displaying the detected light intensity, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide concentration, substrate PH and monitoring video on high-definition display. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tissue culture-based forestry breeding system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of is flowchart of forestry breeding based on tissue culture. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the preparation of culture medium is adding 0.10 mg/l 6-BA, 4.0 mg/l NAA, 10.0 mg/l silver nitrate, and 3% glucose to MS basic medium In the step (v), the substrate after autoclaving is composed of vermiculite and turf in a mass ratio of 1:2.
