PN CN113789531-A; CN113789531-B
TI Preparation of sodium bicarbonate by using            ion-replacement electric membrane reactor involves            installing bubble bottle filled with pure water or            polyethylene imine solution on bubble machine, and            driving carbon dioxide in gas tank into bubble bottle            through bubble machine.
AU WANG Y
   WANG H
   XU T
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2022012055
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sodium bicarbonate by                using ion-replacement electric membrane reactor                involves installing bubble bottle filled with pure                water or polyethylene imine solution on bubble                machine; driving carbon dioxide in gas tank into                bubble bottle through bubble machine to obtain pH                value of 3.8-4.0 or 6.5-8.5 of carbonic acid                solution; using bubble bottle filled with carbonic                acid solution as carbonic solution storage tank to                connect second material liquid chamber; putting                sodium sulfate solution storage tank into first                material liquid chamber of ion exchange electric                film reactor device; introducing carbonic acid                solution into second material liquid chamber;                introducing pure water filled in alkali chamber                liquid storage tank into alkali chamber; and                introducing polar chamber liquid filled in polar                chamber liquid storage tank to anode chamber and                cathode chamber.
   USE - The method is used for preparing sodium                bicarbonate by using ion replacement                electro-membrane reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes waste gas carbon dioxide                and waste salt sodium sulfate generated in                vanadium-producing industry that effectively                reduces emission of carbon dioxide, relieves                greenhouse effect, changes waste into valuable                products, converts into sodium bicarbonate product                with high additional value, and realizes low carbon                circulation and waste utilization. The method uses                ion replacement electro-membrane reactor, has high                current efficiency, and low energy consumption. It                can convert two different material liquid into two                products, reduces production cost, and realizes                environment-friendly chemical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sodium bicarbonate by                using ion-replacement electric membrane reactor                composed of reactor membrane stack and anode plate                and cathode plate on two sides of reactor membrane                stack. The reactor membrane stack is composed of                anion exchange membrane and cation exchange                membrane which are alternately arranged and added                with flow passage separating net sealing gasket;                anode chamber formed between anode plate and                adjacent anion exchange membrane; cathode chamber                formed between cathode plate and adjacent cation                exchange membrane; and repeating units of first                material liquid chamber, second material liquid                chamber and alkali chamber in electroosmosis                membrane stack. The anode chamber and cathode                chamber are respectively connected with pole                chamber liquid storage tank. The first material                liquid chamber is connected with sodium sulphate                solution storage tank. The second material liquid                chamber is connected with calcium carbonate                solution storage tank. The alkali chamber is                connected with alkali chamber liquid storage tank.                The peristaltic pump, anode chamber, cathode                chamber, first material liquid chamber, second                material liquid chamber, and alkali chamber in                material liquid in ion exchange membrane reactor                and connected with storage tank to form circulating                flow. The method for preparing sodium bicarbonate                by using ion replacement electro-membrane reactor                involves installing bubble bottle filled with pure                water or polyethylene imine solution on bubble                machine; and driving carbon dioxide in gas tank                into bubble bottle through bubble machine to obtain                pH value of 3.8-4.0 or 6.5-8.5 of carbonic acid                solution; using bubble bottle filled with carbonic                acid solution as carbonic solution storage tank to                connect second material liquid chamber; putting                sodium sulfate solution storage tank into first                material liquid chamber of ion exchange electric                film reactor device; introducing carbonic acid                solution into second material liquid chamber;                introducing pure water filled in alkali chamber                liquid storage tank into alkali chamber;                introducing polar chamber liquid filled in polar                chamber liquid storage tank to anode chamber and                cathode chamber; connecting anode plate and cathode                plate of ion exchange membrane reactor connected                with external power supply; applying constant                current and controlling chamber material liquid                flow rate 2-5 cm/s and current density at 2-6                mA/cm2 for producing sodium bicarbonate; and                sending bicarbonate ion ionized by carbonate                solution into alkali chamber through anion exchange                membrane under action of electric field, in which                sodium ion in sodium sulfate solution enters alkali                chamber through cation exchange membrane and                bicarbonate ion is combined with sodium ion to form                sodium bicarbonate solution in alkali                chamber.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                method of claim.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of                current efficiency and energy consumption of sodium                bicarbonate in method for preparing sodium                bicarbonate (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polyethyleneimine                  solution has mass concentration of 2-8%. The                  polyethyleneimine has molecular weight of                  600-10000.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is                  added to maintain pH of carbonate solution at                  3.8-4.0 or 6.5-8.5. The production is ended when                  conductivity is reduced to less than 10                  ms/cm.Preferred Component: The sodium sulfate                  solution filled in sodium sulfate solution                  storage tank has concentration of 0.1-0.3 mol/l.                  The polar room liquid is sodium sulfate solution                  with concentration of 0.1-0.3 mol/l. The carbon                  dioxide and sodium sulfate used are derived from                  waste gas carbon dioxide and waste salt sodium                  sulfate produced in vanadium making                  industry.

PN CN112300080-A
TI Producing carbendazim involves using lime nitrogen, carbon dioxide, cyanamide, methyl chloroformate, sodium hydroxide, methyl cyanurethane sodium salt, o-phenylenediamine, hydrochloric acid and water, and preparing carbendazim solution.
AU ZHU G
   HU Z
   NING F
   XU Y
   LIANG H
   PANG Y
   ZHANG H
AE ANHUI GUANGXIN AGROCHEMCIAL CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021138874
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbendazim involves using 10-15 pts. wt. lime nitrogen, 10-15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 20-25 pts. wt. cyanamide, 15-20 pts. wt. methyl chloroformate, 15-20 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, 20-24 pts. wt. methyl cyanurethane sodium salt, 10-12 pts. wt. o-phenylenediamine, 12-15 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid and 10-15 pts. wt. water. The carbendazim solution is prepared by subjecting lime nitrogen, water, and carbon dioxide to hydrolysis and decalcification process to produce a cyanamide aqueous solution, where reaction temperature is 50-60 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The amination process is performed by reacting cyanamide with methyl chloroformate and sodium hydroxide at a temperature of 70-80 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours to produce methyl cyanurethane sodium salt. The methyl cyanurethane sodium salt is subjected to condensation process and reacted with o-phenylenediamine and hydrochloric acid to form a carbendazim solution, where reaction temperature is 50-80 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours.
   USE - Method for producing carbendazim.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce carbendazim, which improves production efficiency, reduces energy consumption and material loss, improves environment, and improves work safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing carbendazim involves using 10-15 pts. wt. lime nitrogen, 10-15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 20-25 pts. wt. cyanamide, 15-20 pts. wt. methyl chloroformate, 15-20 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, 20-24 pts. wt. methyl cyanurethane sodium salt, 10-12 pts. wt. o-phenylenediamine, 12-15 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid and 10-15 pts. wt. water. The carbendazim solution is prepared by subjecting lime nitrogen, water, and carbon dioxide to hydrolysis and decalcification process to produce a cyanamide aqueous solution, where reaction temperature is 50-60 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The amination process is performed by reacting cyanamide with methyl chloroformate and sodium hydroxide at a temperature of 70-80 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours to produce methyl cyanurethane sodium salt. The methyl cyanurethane sodium salt is subjected to condensation process and reacted with o-phenylenediamine and hydrochloric acid to form a carbendazim solution, where reaction temperature is 50-80 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours. The carbendazim solution is transported to a centrifuge for solid-liquid separation, and separated solid is dried to obtain a carbendazim solid. The carbendazim solid is crushed, dust is removed by dust removal equipment, and extruded to obtain carbendazim. The dust removal equipment is operated by starting exhaust fan, sucking dust particles produced after the carbendazim solid is crushed into the dust removal chamber, and adhering it to the filter screen, and when filter screen is saturated on upper surface then squeeze block is pressed downward to drive the sliding rod to slide in the third chute. The limit block is driven to push down, and first sliding block separates it from the third sliding groove, and the turning handle is screwed to drive the rotation rod to rotate. The rotation rod drives the first sliding block in ring which is rotated inside the round table, and when first slider rotates to the position of the third chute below then it enters the third chute under first telescopic spring. The screwing of rotating handle is stopped so that rotating rod rotates 180 degrees C, and rotating rod drives the filter screen to rotate 180 degrees . The telescopic cylinder is started, and drive its output end to drive the bearing seat to slide upward in the fourth sliding groove. The eccentric disk is driven on rotating shaft to move upward until the top of eccentric disks is in contact with the lower surface of the filter screen, and then drive belt is tightened. The rotating motor is started, and its output end is driven to drive the first pulley to rotate. The first pulley drives second pulley to rotate through the transmission belt. The second pulley drives the rotating shaft to rotate. The two eccentric disks are driven to alternately contact the filter screen, and beat the filter screen. The dust particles adhering to the filter screen are separated from the filter screen and enters the dust removal liquid in the storage pool to be absorbed, and the dust removal process is completed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass fraction of the hydrochloric acid is 30 wt.%. The dust removal equipment comprises a dust removal room, and two symmetrically distributed exhaust fans are installed on the inner top of the dust removal room, and a filter screen is provided below the exhaust fan. The two ends of filter screen are fixed with symmetrically distributed fixing rods, where one of fixed rods is rotatably connected with the inner side wall of the dust removal chamber, and other fixed rod is fixed with a rotating rod at one end away from the filter screen. An annular round platform is sleeved on outer side of the rotating rod, and one side wall of circular round table is fixed to the outer side wall of the dust removal chamber. The top of rotating rod is provided with a first sliding groove. The first chute is located inside the annular round table. A first sliding block is arranged inside the first sliding groove. A symmetrically distributed second sliding block is fixed at the bottom ends of both sides of the first sliding block. The two inner sides of the first slide groove are provided with symmetrically distributed second slide grooves. The second sliding block is slidably connected with the second sliding groove. A first telescopic spring is fixed at the bottom end of the first slider. The end of the first telescopic spring away from first sliding block is fixed to inner bottom end of the first sliding groove. The inner top and bottom ends of circular round table are provided with symmetrically distributed third sliding grooves. A limit block is provided inside the third chute. The limiting block is located on side of first sliding block away from first sliding groove and is in contact with the first sliding block. A sliding rod is fixed on side of the limiting block away from first sliding block, where a rotating shaft is provided below the filter screen. An oppositely arranged eccentric disk is fixed on the rotating shaft. The inner two side walls of dust removal chamber are provided with symmetrically distributed fourth sliding grooves. The fourth sliding groove is provided with a bearing seat. The bearing seat is in sliding connection with the fourth sliding groove. A telescopic cylinder is provided under the bearing seat. A rotating motor is fixed on the outer side wall of the dust removal chamber. The output end of rotating motor is fixed with a first pulley. A second pulley is fixed on rotating shaft. A drive belt is installed between first pulley and second pulley. The end of the sliding rod far away from limiting block passes through circular circular platform and is slidably connected to the circular circular platform. The two ends of limiting block far away from first sliding block are fixed with symmetrically distributed second telescopic springs. The end of second telescopic spring away from limiting block is fixed to the inner top end of the third sliding groove. An extruding block is fixed at one end of the sliding rod away from the limiting block. The rotating rod passes through the dust removal chamber and is rotatably connected with the dust removal chamber, and end of the rotating rod away from fixed rod is fixed with a rotating handle. The third sliding groove is adapted to the first sliding groove, and top of first sliding groove is located inside the third sliding groove. The filter screen is provided with multiple uniformly distributed filter holes, and inner bottom of the dust removal chamber is provided with a reservoir. The output end of telescopic cylinder is fixed to the bottom of the bearing seat, where two ends of the rotating shaft are respectively connected to the two bearing seats in rotation, and output end of the rotating motor penetrates through the dust removal room and is rotatably connected with the dust removal room.

PT P
PN WO2018114747-A1
TI Production of ethylene and vinyl acetate involves contacting in reaction zone gas stream comprising ethane and ethylene with oxygen-containing gas stream in presence of dehydrogenation catalyst and separating stream in separation zone.
AU LANGE J P A M
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 201851003H
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated production of ethylene and vinyl acetate (VAM) involves contacting in reaction zone (3) a gas stream comprising ethane and optionally ethylene with an oxygen-containing gas stream (14) in the presence of a dehydrogenation catalyst under oxidative dehydrogenation conditions to produce a product stream (1), separating the stream (1) in a separation zone (5) into a stream (s2), optionally concentrating the stream (s2), dewatering the stream (4) in a dewatering zone (9) to produce a dewatered stream (10) comprising ethane and ethylene, contacting in a reaction zone (r2) the stream (4) or stream (2) with a stream comprising concentrated acetic acid and with an oxygen-containing gas stream in the presence of vinyl acetate, catalyst, to produce a product stream (s5) and recovering vinyl acetate and ethylene the product stream (s5). The portion(s) of the stream (s2) comprising acetic acid is used to recover vinyl acetate from the product stream (s5).
   USE - Integrated production of ethylene and vinyl acetate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and economically provides ethylene and vinyl acetate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The product stream (1) comprises ethane, ethylene, acetic acid and water. The stream (s2) comprises acetic acid and optionally water and a stream (4) comprising ethane, ethylene and optionally water. The product stream (s5) comprises vinyl acetate, water, ethane, ethylene, and optionally other compounds. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reaction system for oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane and co-production of vinyl acetate, which comprises the reaction zone (3) comprising an inlet for receiving an ethane-containing feed stream, an inlet for receiving an oxygen-containing feed stream, and an outlet for discharging an effluent from the reaction zone (3), the separation zone (5) comprising an inlet for receiving an effluent from the reaction zone (3), optionally an inlet for receiving a washing agent stream (6), an outlet for discharging an acetic acid-depleted effluent stream from the separation zone (5), and an outlet for discharging an acetic acid-enriched stream from the separation zone (5), optionally, an acetic acid recovery unit comprising an inlet for receiving an acetic acid-enriched stream from the separation zone (5), and an outlet for discharging a concentrated acetic acid stream, and an outlet for discharging water or recovered selective solvent, optionally, a dewatering zone (9) comprising an inlet for receiving an acetic acid-depleted effluent stream from the separation zone (5), and an outlet for discharging a dewatered effluent stream, a reaction zone (r2) comprising an inlet for receiving the acetic acid-depleted effluent stream from the separation zone (5) or the dewatered effluent stream from the dewatering zone as an ethylene-containing feed stream, an inlet for receiving an oxygen-containing feed stream, an inlet for receiving a concentrated acetic acid- containing feed stream, and an outlet for discharging an effluent from the reaction zone (r2), a separation zone (z2) comprising an inlet for receiving an effluent from the second reaction zone, an inlet for receiving an acetic acid-enriched washing agent stream, an outlet for discharging a vinyl acetate-enriched liquid stream from the separation zone (z2), and an outlet for discharging a vinyl acetate-depleted gaseous stream from the separation zone (z2), purification zone (19) comprising an inlet for receiving a vinyl acetate-enriched liquid stream from separation zone (z2), an outlet for discharging a concentrated acetic acid-containing stream from the purification zone, and an outlet for discharging a concentrated vinyl acetate-containing stream from the purification zone, and one or more outlets for discharging further compounds, and optionally a carbon dioxide removal zone comprising an inlet for receiving a vinyl acetate-depleted gaseous stream from a washing zone (w2), an outlet for discharging a carbon dioxide-depleted gaseous stream from carbon dioxide removal zone, and, optionally, an ethane/ethylene separation unit comprising an inlet for receiving a carbon dioxide-depleted effluent stream from the carbon dioxide removal zone, an outlet for discharging an ethylene-containing stream from said ethane/ethylene separation unit, and an outlet for discharging an ethane-containing stream from said ethane/ethylene separation unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the process.
   Ethane, ethylene, acetic acid and water-comprising stream (1)
   Oxygen-containing gas stream (2)
   Reaction zone (3)
   Stream comprising ethane and ethylene (4)
   Separation zone (5)
   Washing agent stream (6)
   Stream comprising vinyl acetate (7)
   Dewatering zone (9)
   Dewatered stream (10)
   Oxygen-containing gas stream (14)
   Purification zone (19)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The separation zone comprises an extractive distillation unit or an absorption unit configured to extract or absorb or extract acetic acid from the effluent of the reaction zone (3) with a selective solvent. The system further comprises unit for recirculating the concentrated acetic acid-containing stream from the purification zone to the reaction zone (r2) or to separation zone (z2), and/or unit for recirculating recovered selective solvent from the acetic acid recovery unit to the separation zone (5), and/or unit for recirculating recovered selective solvent from the purification zone to the separation zone (5), and/or unit for recirculating an ethylene-enriched stream from the ethane/ethylene separation unit to the second reaction zone, and/or unit for recirculating an ethane-enriched stream from the ethane/ethylene separation unit to the first reaction zone, and/or unit for recirculating the carbon dioxide-depleted gaseous stream from the carbon dioxide removal zone to the reaction zone (3) , unit for by-passing the reaction zone (r2) and separation zone (z2), and unit for directly feeding the acetic acid-enriched stream from the separation zone (5) to the purification zone. Preferred Components: The separation zone (5) is a condensation zone in which the stream (1) is condensed, and a washing zone (w1) in which product stream (1) is separated by contacting the stream (1) with a washing stream comprising water or solvent. The stream (s2) is at least partially fed to the acetic acid recovery unit to produce a stream (7) comprising concentrated acetic acid. The product stream (s5) is contacted in a washing zone (w2) with a stream (s2) and/or stream (7) to produce a stream (s7) comprising vinyl acetate, acetic acid and water and/or selective solvent and a gaseous stream (s8) comprising ethane, ethylene, carbon dioxide and optionally other gaseous compounds. The stream (s7) is fed to the purification zone to produce a stream (s9) comprising concentrated acetic acid and stream (s10) comprising vinyl acetate, and one or more streams comprising water and/or selective solvent. The portion(s) of the stream (7) and portion(s) of the stream(s) comprising concentrated acetic acid, are used as the feed stream (15) comprising concentrated acetic acid supplied to the reaction zone (z2). The stream (s8) is fed to the carbon dioxide removal zone to remove carbon dioxide and optionally light condensable compounds to produce a stream (s11) enriched in ethane and ethylene. The stream (s11) enriched in ethane and ethylene is further separated in the ethane/ethylene separation unit to produce a stream (s12) enriched in ethane and a stream (s13) enriched in ethylene. The portion(s) of the stream (s12) enriched in ethane is recirculated to the reaction zone (3) and/or the stream (s13) enriched in ethylene is recirculated to the reaction zone (r2). The acetic acid recovery unit further produces a stream comprising concentrated selective solvent, and all or at least a portion of concentrated selective solvent stream is recirculated to the separation zone (5).
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112961136-A
TI Extracting polyphenols comprises subjecting            tobacco leaves to carbon-dioxide supercritical            extraction to obtain tobacco volatile oil and raffinate            and mixing the raffinate with the extraction solvent            for ultrasonic extraction.
AU HUANG Z
   GU L
   ZHANG H
   LI J
   ZHANG L
   LI Z
   TANG X
   PENG J
   YANG H
   QU X
   ZHOU W
   CUI Y
AE YUXI YUNNAN TOBACCO CO (CNTA-C)
   UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202168606A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting polyphenols comprises (i)                subjecting tobacco leaves to carbon-dioxide                supercritical extraction to obtain tobacco volatile                oil and raffinate; and (ii) mixing the raffinate                with the extraction solvent for ultrasonic                extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting                polyphenols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes polyphenol substance is                easier to be dissolved, and improves extraction                rate of polyphenol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the                  carbon-dioxide supercritical extraction is 40-65                  degrees C, the pressure is 10-35 MPa, the flow                  rate of the carbon-dioxide supercritical fluid is                  5-30 g/minute, and the time is 30-180 minutes.                  The material-to-liquid ratio of the ultrasonic                  extraction is 1:10-40. The temperature of the                  ultrasonic extraction is 20-70 degrees C, the                  ultrasonic power is 100-250 W, and the time is                  20-60 minutes. The method further comprises:                  crushing and sieving the tobacco leaves before                  performing the carbon-dioxide supercritical                  extraction; where the particle size of the                  tobacco leaves after sieving is 40-60 mesh and                  rotating extraction liquid obtained by the                  ultrasonic extraction to obtain polyphenols. The                  vacuum degree of the rotary steaming is 4-8 kPa,                  the temperature is 35-45 degrees C, and the time                  is 90-120 minutes. Preferred Components: The                  extraction solvent comprises methanol, ethanol,                  water, aqueous methanol or aqueous ethanol. The                  tobacco leaves comprise freshly-cured tobacco                  leaves, fresh tobacco leaves and/or discarded                  tobacco leaves.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)
MC E11-Q01A; E31-N05C

TI Preparing raspberry fruit beverage by subjecting part to anaerobic fermentation, mixing with aerobic fermentation solution of ripened raspberry fruit, and adding fruit extract of supercritical carbon dioxide to it.
AU KAPINOS R
AE KAPINOS FITOFERM RENATA (KAPI-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7303N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a raspberry fruit beverage involves subjecting part of ripened raspberry fruit to aerobic fermentation, subjecting another part to anaerobic lactic acid fermentation, subjecting another part to extraction with supercritical carbon dioxide, mixing the solutions obtained in both fermentation processes, and adding the extract to it.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing raspberry fruit beverage.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical.

TI Gas generating agent useful for cleaning gas including hydrogen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water and in civil and military applications, comprises potassium perchlorate and azodicarbonamide.

AB    NOVELTY - A gas generating agent comprises 40-60 wt.% potassium perchlorate and 40-60 wt.% azodicarbonamide.
   USE - The gas generating agent is useful for cleaning gas including hydrogen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water and in civil and military applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas generating agent uses easy-to-obtain raw material, is safe and cost-effective, and has low combustion temperature and stable sensitivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gas generating agent comprises 43-47 wt.% potassium perchlorate and 53-57 wt.% azodicarbonamide. Preferred Components: The potassium perchlorate is oxidant. The azodicarbonamide is reducing agent. Preferred Properties: The potassium perchlorate has particle size of 150 mu m. The azodicarbonamide has particle size of 20 mu m.

PN CN114984726-A; CN217490329-U
TI System for capturing carbon dioxide based on ionic            liquid in coal-fired power plant, has flue gas inlet            connected with compression system and absorption tower,            where tower is connected with inlet of flash evaporator            through heat exchanger and top portion of evaporator is            provided with gas outlet.
AU MENG Y
   NIU G
   JIA L
   LIU Y
AE XIAN XI BOILER ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (CHHN-C)
GA 2022B8931P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a flue gas inlet (1) connected                with a compression system (2) and an absorption                tower (3) i.e. filler absorption tower. A top                portion of the absorption tower is provided with an                absorption tower flue gas outlet (3c). An absorbing                tower liquid outlet (3d) is fixed at a bottom                portion of the absorbing tower. The absorbtion                tower is connected with an inlet of a flash                evaporator (5) through the first heat exchanger                (4). A top portion of the flash evaporator is                provided with a flash evaporator gas outlet (5a). A                flash evaporator liquid outlet (5b) is fixed at a                bottom portion of the flash evaporator that is                fixed with an absorption tower barren liquid inlet                (3b). The absorption tower barren liquid inlet is                fixed on a side surface of a top portion of the                absorption tower through a barren liquid pump (6)                and second heat exchanger (7).
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide based on                ionic liquid in a coal-fired power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has low heat characteristics, and                can utilize ionic liquid as de carburization medium                and realize the absorption of carbon dioxide using                carbon dioxide in the physical dissolution process                of ionic liquid and avoids absorption process                limited by reaction balance during chemical                absorption process so as to improve de                carburization efficiency, thus reducing energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for capturing carbon dioxide based on ionic                liquid.
   The method involves filling barren solution,                where the barren solution is a compound composed of                anion and cation, and cation is imidazole ion,                benzimidazole ion, pyridine ion, pyrrole ion,                quaternary ammonium ion, quaternary phosphorus ion                and sulfonium ion and derivative, anion is halogen                ion, nitrate, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid root,                Hexafluorophosphate,Tetrafluoroborate, methoxy                diphenidine, dicyandiamide ion, methane sulfonate,                p-methyl benzene sulfonate, bis                trifluoromethanesulfonyl imide anion, formate,                acetate, lactate and its derivative and halogen,                iron, tin, nickel or copper.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for capturing carbon dioxide based on ionic                liquid.
   1Flue gas inlet
   2Compression system
   3Absorption tower
   3bAbsorption tower barren liquid inlet
   3cAbsorption tower flue gas outlet
   3dAbsorbing tower liquid outlet
   4First heat exchanger
   5Flash evaporator
   5aFlash evaporator gas outlet
   5bFlash evaporator liquid outlet
   6Barren liquid pump
   7Second heat exchanger

AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a ground with a brine tank and a water pump. The brine in the salt cavity is pumped into the brine pool. A brine liquid level position is detected by a liquid level detector. The water pump and a first valve are turned off when the liquid level of the brined brine is reduced to the lower edge of the interlayer. The oil pump is started to inject crude oil into a salt cavity. The volume of the crude oil injected with the salt chamber liquid flow meter is measured when the injection amount of crude oil reaches the set value. The third valve and the high-pressure air pump are turned on to inject carbon dioxide into the salt tank when the pressure value measured by the pressure gauge reaches a set value, and the gas is stopped.
   USE - Method for storing carbon dioxide by using layered salt karst cavity for underground storage of oil, natural gas and compressed air, can also be used for storing supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method injects the crude oil or film forming agent into the interlayer, blocking the inter layer seepage channel, protecting the inter-layer from being eroded by supercritical CO2, improving the safety of the layered salt rock dissolving cavity storing supercritical carbon dioxide. The air pressure of the fluctuation can enhance the impact of crude oil/film forming agent as to more easily inject the crude-oil-or-film-forming-agent into interlayer and the injection depth is larger. The ultrasonic probe of the ultrasonic detector is set on the lower end of the water pipe extending into the salt cavity, and the transmission line of the probe passes through the inner part of water pipe and the threading connector to be connected with the main machine of ultrasonic.

   ADVANTAGE - The ultra-low interfacial tension                self-assembled carbon dioxide foam oil-displacing                agent has good salt resistance and acid resistance,                high viscosity of foaming liquid, so that the                oil-water interfacial tension can reach the order                of 10-3millinewton/m or below, and the generated                foam is fine and rich, and has good stability, has                good injection performance, improves the oil-water                mobility ratio, significantly expand the sweep                efficiency, reduces the oil-water interfacial                tension, and significantly improves the oil washing                efficiency.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ultra-low interfacial tension self-assembled                carbon dioxide foam oil-displacing agent comprises                0.2-0.3 wt.%                (bis[N-methyl-N-(3-dodecyloxy-2-hydroxy)propanyl-N-(2-hydroxy-3-sodium                sulfonate)propanyl]ammonium chloride)ethane of                formula (I), which is a hydroxysulfobetaine-type                Gemini surfactant, 0.2-0.3 wt.%, betaine-type                surfactant, 0.1-0.35 wt.% nonionic surfactants,                0.02-0.07 wt.% counter ionic compound, 0.04-0.14                wt.% chelating agent, 5-7 wt.% inorganic salts, and                rest is water. n=12.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation method of the ultra-low interfacial                tension self-assembled carbon dioxide foam                oil-displacing agent, which involves heating water                to 70&#8451;, adding inorganic salt, adding                (bis[N-methyl-N-(3-dodecyloxy-2-hydroxy)propanyl-N-(2-hydroxy-3-                sodium sulfonate) propanyl] ammonium chloride)                ethane, stirring and dissolving, adding                cocamidopropyl betaine, stirring and dissolving,                adding nonionic surfactant dodecyl dimethyl amine                oxide and chelating agent                ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid successively,                stirring and dissolving, and adding sodium                salicylate to the mixed solution, stirring and                dissolving.

PN CN114033517-A
TI Carbon dioxide compression energy storage based geothermal power generation and cold and heat supply system, has energy storage system that is connected to heat recovery system for recovering heat, and heat recovery system is connected to cold and heat supply system.
AU ZHANG B
   XIE N
   ZHONG S
   YIN L
   SU W
   LIN X
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
   CHINA THREE GORGES CORP (CTGC-C)
GA 202228883U
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide compression energy storage based geothermal power generation and cold and heat supply system has an energy storage systems that are provided for compressed energy storage. The compression process of the energy storage system adopts staged compression and intermediate cooling. The energy storage system is connected to an energy release system for releasing energy. The energy release process of the energy release system adopts graded expansion and intermediate reheating. The energy storage system is connected to a heat recovery system for recovering heat. The heat recovery system is connected to the cold and heat supply system.
   USE - Carbon dioxide compression energy storage based geothermal power generation and cold and heat supply system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the operating efficiency of the system, and staged compression intermediate cooling and staged expansion intermediate reheating are used in the energy storage process and energy release process, respectively. The system adjusts the number of stages of the staged compression and staged expansion process according to the pressure in the high-pressure storage tank. The heat energy recovery system is used to collect the compression heat generated in the compression process and the waste heat of the exhaust gas at the turbine outlet. The system is used to drive the lithium bromide refrigeration unit to provide cooling capacity, and is connected to the urban heating pipe network for supply. The system applies carbon dioxide to the energy storage system, and realizes the combined supply of cooling, heating and power, and has the advantages of high system efficiency, compact structure, economical and environmental protection and so on.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an operation method of the geothermal power generation and cold and heat supply system based on carbon dioxide compression energy storage.

PT P
PN CN111592014-A
TI Preparing potassium sulfate, calcium carbonate and ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum comprises reacting phosphogypsum with aqueous ammonia and performing metathesis reaction with potassium chloride.
AU ZHOU S
AE ZHOU S (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 202085781W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing potassium sulfate, calcium carbonate and ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum comprises (i) reacting phosphogypsum with aqueous ammonia with a mass concentration of 20-25%, dissolving calcium sulfate in ammonia and introducing carbon dioxide gas and reacting, (ii) adding the filtered ammonium sulfate solution to industrial potassium chloride and recycled potassium chloride for metathesis reaction, (iv) the mixed solution of potassium chloride and ammonium chloride is added to the calcium carbonate for mixing, and steam is introduced for high temperature decomposition, insulating and filtering and cooling the filtered solution to 50-65 degrees C for high-temperature crystallization and (iv) the ammonia water and carbon dioxide undergo carbonation reaction with the calcium chloride solution to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate, filtering and drying to obtain a light calcium carbonate product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing potassium sulfate, calcium carbonate and ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing potassium sulfate, calcium carbonate and ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum comprises (i) reacting phosphogypsum with aqueous ammonia with a mass concentration of 20-25%, dissolving calcium sulfate in ammonia and introducing carbon dioxide gas and reacting to obtain calcium carbonate precipitation and ammonium sulfate solution with a mass concentration of 30-35%, during this process, impurities contained in the phosphorite paste enter the calcium carbonate, and reacting at 25-35 degrees C, (ii) adding the filtered ammonium sulfate solution to industrial potassium chloride and recycled potassium chloride for metathesis reaction, with the help of the salting-out effect of high-concentration ammonium sulfate, potassium sulfate crystallizes out of the solution, and the potassium sulfate product is obtained after centrifugal separation and reacted solution is a mixed solution of potassium chloride and ammonium chloride, (iv) the mixed solution of potassium chloride and ammonium chloride is added to the calcium carbonate for mixing, and steam is introduced for high temperature decomposition, and the decomposition temperature is 150-170 degrees C, the gas phase obtains a mixture of ammonia, carbon dioxide and water vapor, which is concentrated in the distillation section at the top of the reactor to obtain ammonia water and carbon dioxide gas with a mass concentration of 15-20%, the ammonia distilled solution is a mixed liquid of potassium chloride and calcium chloride, and the temperature reaches 120-140 degrees C, insulating and filtering and cooling the filtered solution to 50-65 degrees C for high-temperature crystallization and (iv) the ammonia water and carbon dioxide undergo carbonation reaction with the calcium chloride solution to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate, filtering and drying to obtain a light calcium carbonate product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system device for preparing potassium sulfate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of ammonium sulfate takes phosphogypsum, ammonia water and synthetic ammonia by-product carbon dioxide as raw materials to react at 25-35 degrees C, preferably, the mass percentage of calcium sulfate dehydrate in the phosphogypsum in step (1) is 20%, 21%, 22%, 23%, 24%, 25%. Preferably, the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in step (1) is 80-95%, more preferably, 80%, 85%, 90% and 95%. The molar ratio of NH3 in aqueous ammonia: sulfate in phosphogypsum in step (1) is 1.04-1.1:1, preferably, 1.04:1, 1.06:1, 1.08:1, 1.1:1. In the step (i), the mixing and stirring time of phosphogypsum and ammonia water is 15-20 minutes, preferably 15 minutes, 18 minutes and 20 minutes. In the step (i) and step (iv), the carbonation reaction time is 30-50 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, 40 minutes and 50 minutes. The potassium chloride raw material used in the potassium sulfate preparation tank satisfies the mass percentage of potassium oxide is not greater than 55%, and also comprises the crystalline product potassium chloride after the ammonia distillation reaction. The ammonia distillation reaction uses steam as the heating source to achieve the decomposition of ammonium chloride, the subsequent mixed gas of ammonia and water vapor is enriched by the rectification section set up in the upper part of the ammonia distillation reactor to obtain qualified ammonia water, carbon dioxide separates out and is compressed and decarbonated, preferably, the ammonia distillation reaction temperature is 150-170 degrees C, more preferably 150 degrees C, 155 degrees C, 160 degrees C, 165 degrees C and 170 degrees C and the ammonia water mass concentration obtained after ammonia distillation is 15-20%, more preferably 15%, 16%, 17%, 18%, 19% and 20%. The solution after ammonia distillation is a mixed liquid of potassium chloride and calcium chloride, when the temperature reaches 120-140 degrees C, heat preservation and filtration are carried out through filtration, the silicon, iron and other impurities are separated, and the solution of potassium chloride and calcium chloride is cooled and then crystallized at high temperature, potassium chloride crystallizes from calcium chloride, preferably, the mixed solution of potassium chloride and calcium chloride is cooled to 50-65 degrees C, more preferably 50 degrees C, 55 degrees C, 60 degrees C and 65 degrees C. The carbonation process is to distill ammonia to obtain ammonia water, and to react carbon dioxide with calcium chloride solution to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate. Preferably, the carbonation reaction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, more preferably, 30 degrees C, 35 degrees C, 40 degrees C. The carbonation reaction time is 30-50 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, 40 minutes and 50 minutes.

   UNIV DI PISA (UYPI-Non-standard)
   UNIV CALABRIA (UYCA-Non-standard)
   CONSIGLIO NAZ DELLE RICERCHE (CNDR-C)
   UNIV DI PISA (UYPI-Non-standard)
   UNIV CALABRIA (UYCA-Non-standard)
GA 2019788427
AB    NOVELTY - A dense poly(ionic liquid)-based membrane is obtained by co polymerization via UV-curing of a mixture comprising 10-50 wt.%, preferably 20-30 wt.% IL monomer trialkyl((4-vinylphenyl)alkyl)phosphonium with ion counterion (I), 0.5-20 wt.%, preferably 2-10 wt.% cross linkers, 0.001-5 wt.%, 0.1-1 wt.% photo-initiator, and remainder of the water.
   USE - Dense poly(ionic liquid)-based membrane for gas separation from flue gas, where gases to be separated are carbon dioxide, hydrogen, methane, nitrogen and oxygen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has carbon dioxide absorption rate, and increased carbon dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/nitrogen and carbon dioxide/hydrogen selectivity, thus ensuring gas permeability and selectivity, and can be prepared in simple, easy and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A dense poly(ionic liquid)-based membrane is obtained by co polymerization via UV-curing of a mixture comprising 10-50 wt.%, preferably 20-30 wt.% IL monomer trialkyl((4-vinylphenyl)alkyl)pbosphonium with ion (X-) as counterion having structure of formula (I), 0.5-20 wt.%, preferably 2-10 wt.% cross linkers, 0.001-5 wt.%, 0.1-1 wt.% photo-initiator, and remainder of the water.
   R = 3-20C alkyl chain, preferably butyl, hexyl or octyl;
   L = linker consisting of a simple alkanediyl 1-20C chain, or an ethereal chain containing 1-20C or 1-10 oxygen atoms, preferably methanediyl group; and
   X = anion selected from BF4-, PF6-, SbF6- , TsO-, CF3-SO3-, NC-N--CN, (MeO)2PO2-, EtSO4- or F3CO2SN-SO2CF3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing dense poly(ionic liquid)-based membrane, which involves providing a mixture, applying the mixture to a support by using film casting knife or casting mixture between two plates so that the thickness of the membrane ranges to 0.02-200 mu m, and curing curable monomer mixture by UV radiation to induce polymerization
   (2) a method for separating two components, A and B, of a gas mixture, which involves passing gas mixture across a separation membrane having a feed side and a permeate side, where separation membrane has selective layer, providing a driving force for transmembrane permeation, withdrawing from the permeate side a permeate stream enriched in component A compared to the gas mixture and withdrawing from the feed side a residue stream depleted in component A compared to the gas mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The ethylenically unsaturated co-monomer is 2-hydroxyethyl acrylate (HEMA), and is present in the composition in the amount of 50-60 wt.%.

PN CN108745287-A; CN108745287-B
TI Preparation of three-dimensional graphene oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves performing homogeneous dispersion of commercial graphene oxide in solution of triethyl orthoacetate, processing, and adding toluene sulfonic acid.
AU LIU Y
   YU X
AE UNIV FUJIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYFI-C)
GA 201888711T
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of three-dimensional graphene oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent involves (1) performing homogeneous dispersion of commercial graphene oxide in a solution of triethyl orthoacetate, and ultrasonically processing, (2) adding toluene sulfonic acid to the solution to-be-used, placing in a hydrothermal reactor, reacting, cooling, and centrifugally separating the precipitate in the solution, (3) dispersing precipitate in deionized water, ultrasonically processing, adding 1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide, uniformly stirring, adding hydroxybenzotriazole, oscillating and stirring until dissolved, ultrasonically processing, and centrifugally separating precipitate, (4) dispersing the precipitate in methanol solution, adding long-chain amine compound, ultrasonically treating, and centrifugally separating precipitate, and (5) dispersing the precipitate in deionized water, ultrasonically treating, vacuum freeze-drying, and drying the obtained precipitate.
   USE - Preparation method of three-dimensional graphene oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of three-dimensional graphene oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent with excellent performance, reduced preparation difficulty, and improved adsorption performance, which reduces adsorption-desorption diffusion resistance, accelerates the adsorption and desorption rate, and increases the adsorption selectivity and adsorption amount of the adsorbent to carbon dioxide, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of three-dimensional graphene oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent involves (1) weighing a certain amount of commercial graphene oxide, performing homogeneous dispersion in a solution of triethyl orthoacetate with concentration of 3-5 mg/1 ml, and ultrasonically processing for 10-30 minutes, (2) adding toluene sulfonic acid having mass of 0.02 times of graphene oxide to the solution to-be-used, placing in a hydrothermal reactor, reacting for 36-48 hours under the condition of 403 K, cooling to room temperature, and centrifugally separating the precipitate in the solution, (3) dispersing the precipitate obtained in step (2) in deionized water with concentration of 1-4 mg/1 ml, ultrasonically processing for 10-30 minutes, adding 1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide with mass of 2-3 times that of graphene oxide, uniformly stirring, adding hydroxybenzotriazole with the mass of 2-3 times of graphene oxide, oscillating and vigorously stirring until completely dissolved, ultrasonically processing for 6-24 hours, and centrifugally separating the precipitate, (4) dispersing the precipitate in methanol solution with concentration of 3-5 mg/1 ml, adding long-chain amine compound having 10 times the mass of graphene oxide, ultrasonically treating for 12 hours, and centrifugally separating the precipitate, and (5) dispersing the precipitate obtained in step (4) in deionized water with concentration of 1-5 mg/ml, ultrasonically treating for 20 minutes, vacuum freeze-drying for 24 hours, and drying the obtained precipitate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The long-chain amine compound in the step (4) is tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine or polyethyleneimine.

PN CN110899929-A; CN110899929-B
TI Dioxygen welding of sheet head comprises e. g. making groove for opening of child and mother mouth, making V-shaped butt groove between openings, welding groove and keeping surface free of debris, slag, oil and rust.
AU YU K
AE TANGSHAN DATONG METAL PROD CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 202024218Y
AB    NOVELTY - Dioxygen welding of sheet head comprises e. g. (i) making a groove for the opening of the child and mother mouth, making V-shaped butt groove between the openings, welding the groove and the 20-30 mm surface on both sides of the groove of the son and mother mouth with sandpaper to polish the metallic luster, keeping the surface free of debris, slag, oil, rust and other debris, and rinsing the groove 2-4 times with sodium hydroxide solution; (ii) heating the to-be-welded area of the sub-mother mouth using the heating equipment, using a thermometer to monitor the temperature of the surface of the child and mother mouth, and waiting until the surface temperature of the film head reaches 150-180 degrees C; and (iii) spraying carbon dioxide gas using jet equipment, and making the area where the opening of the opening and the opening of the opening need to be welded in a carbon dioxide environment.
   USE - The method is useful for dioxygen welding of sheet head.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures large current density, concentrated arc energy, melting efficiency of the welding wire is high, large penetration depth of the base material, fast welding speed, less residue after welding, improves the welding rate, preheats the head of the mother and child before welding, avoids the phenomenon of cracking of the head of the child and mother mouth when welding, welds with high temperature resistant welding wire, improves the strength of the welding surface and improves the ability to resist cold cracks, and is simple and easy to promote.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dioxygen welding of sheet head comprises (i) making a groove for the opening of the child and mother mouth, making V-shaped butt groove between the openings, welding the groove and the 20-30 mm surface on both sides of the groove of the son and mother mouth with sandpaper to polish the metallic luster, keeping the surface free of debris, slag, oil, rust and other debris, and rinsing the groove 2-4 times with sodium hydroxide solution; (ii) heating the to-be-welded area of the sub-mother mouth using the heating equipment, using a thermometer to monitor the temperature of the surface of the child and mother mouth, and waiting until the surface temperature of the film head reaches 150-180 degrees C; (iii) spraying carbon dioxide gas using jet equipment, and making the area where the opening of the opening and the opening of the opening need to be welded in a carbon dioxide environment; (iv) choosing the welding wire so that the welding wire is in contact with the groove of the opening of the opening and the opening of the opening of the opening, melting the welding wire using welding torch, adopting spot welding form, choosing the starting point of base welding, where the distance between each solder joint is 3-6 mm, the bottom welding can be completed after the distance between the last welding point and the initial welding point is 3-6 mm, and removing the welding slag on the surface of the welding spot immediately after finishing the welding; (v) completing the priming welding process in step (iv), melting the welding wire with a welding gun using high temperature welding wire, filling the V-groove, filling and welding after the welding wire fills the V-groove, and making the welding surface 5-8 mm higher than the groove; (vi) using the same high temperature welding wire after the filling and welding operation in step (v) is completed, melting the wire with a welding gun, making the welding wire melt completely cover the welding surface and extending to 10-20 mm on both sides of the V-groove; (vii) pressing the polishing wheel to the welding place, rolling the abrasive and tracing the surface of the welded pipe, obtaining bright processed surface with surface roughness of Ra0.5-0.08 microns; and (vii) inspecting after repair welding and ultrasonic flaw detection, testing, and competing the opening if there is no crack in the welding place.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (v) and step (vi), the high-temperature-resistant welding wire includes 20-30 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 15-30 pts. wt. magnesium carbonate, 6-12 pts. wt. titanium pigment, 3-6 pts. wt. o-hydroxy benzoic acid, 8-12 pts. wt. talc powder, 9-16 pts. wt. mica powder and 15-19 pts. wt. silicon dioxide. In the step (iv), the parameter current of the bottom welding is 100-110 A, and the voltage is 30-35 V. In the step (iii), the concentration of carbon dioxide is 99.5-99.8%. In the step (iv), the diameter of the welding wire is 1-1.4 mm and the diameter of the welding wire in steps (v) and (vi) is 1.6-1.8 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the parameter current of the cover welding is 150-180 A, the voltage is 15-25 V, and the welding speed is 100-130 mm/minute. In the step (vi), the parameter current of the filler welding is 120-130 A, the voltage is 25-30 V and the welding speed is 150-180 mm/minute.

TI Extracting tea saponin using biological technology comprises e.g. crushing tea seed cake meal by crushing module, sterilizing, adding purified water to obtain aqueous solution, mixing tea seed meal aqueous solution with extracting solution.
AU TANG P
   ZHU L
   WANG W
   ZHANG Q
   LI B
   YANG Y
   TANG S
   HU X
AE YONGZHOU VOCATIONAL TECH COLLEGE (YONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018A35754
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting tea saponin using biological technology comprises e.g. crushing tea seed cake meal by crushing module, sterilizing, adding purified water to obtain aqueous solution, where sterilization adopts use of carbon dioxide sterilization model in combination with Box-Behnken module of the response surface method, where three factors and three levels, and the processing time, temperature and pressure are defined as independent variables i.e. X1, X2 and X3, Xi = (Xi-X0)/ Delta X, where X0 is the true value of the independent variable, and Delta X is the change step of the independent variable, the magnitude of the high pressure carbon dioxide (HPCD) killing lactic acid bacteria as the response value is represented as Y= lgN0/Nt, where N0 is the initial number of bacteria in the meat sample, and Nt is the number of residual bacteria in the meat sample after HPCD treatment, and the regression equation for establishing the model is represented as given in the specification.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tea saponin using biological technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting tea saponin using biological technology comprises (i) crushing tea seed cake meal by crushing module, sterilizing, adding purified water to obtain aqueous solution, where sterilization adopts use of carbon dioxide sterilization model in combination with Box-Behnken module of the response surface method, where three factors and three levels, and the processing time, temperature and pressure are defined as independent variables i.e. X1, X2 and X3, Xi = (Xi-X0)/ Delta X, where X0 is the true value of the independent variable, and Delta X is the change step of the independent variable, the magnitude of the high pressure carbon dioxide (HPCD) killing lactic acid bacteria as the response value is represented as Y= lgN0/Nt, where N0 is the initial number of bacteria in the meat sample, and Nt is the number of residual bacteria in the meat sample after HPCD treatment, and the regression equation for establishing the model is represented as given in the specification, (ii) mixing aqueous solution of the tea seed meal with extracting solution by mixing module, carrying out crude extraction treatment to obtain a tea saponin extract, where the extraction temperature is 53 degrees C, the concentration of ethanol is 45%, time is 95 minutes, and liquid-solid ratio is 10.5, (iii) mixing tea saponin extract with sterol complexing agent by complexation processing module, performing complexation treatment to obtain tea saponin complex, where the complex on the particle surfaces of each model point P and its neighboring regions of 8- constituting a micro surface, according to the surface theory of differential geometry, the surface parameter equation is z=z(x,y), where x and y are the coordinates of the point in the image substrate, the Gaussian curvature K and the mean curvature H is represented by equation as given in the specification, (iv) mixing tea saponin complex with decomplexing by decomplexing separation module, performing decomplexation and separation to obtain tea saponin, and (v) purifying tea saponin. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for extracting tea saponin comprising smashing module, sterilizing module, mixing module, complex processing module, decomposing separation module, and purifying module. The smashing module is connected to the sterilizing module for pulverizing the tea seed mash. The sterilization module is connected with the smashing module and mixing module for sterilizing and disinfecting pulverized tea seed mash, and adding pure water to prepare an aqueous solution. The mixing module is connected with the sterilization module and the complex processing module for mixing the aqueous solution of the tea seed meal with the extract, and performing rough extraction treatment to obtain a tea saponin extract. The complexation processing module is connected to the mixing module and the decomposing separation module for mixing the tea saponin extract with a sterol complexing agent and performing complexation treatment to obtain a tea saponin complex. The decomposing separation module is connected to the complexation processing module and the purification module for mixing tea saponin complex with the decomplexing solvent to perform decomplexation and separation to obtain tea saponin. The purification module is coupled to the decomposing separation module for purification of the tea saponin.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the extracting tea saponin using biological technology (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The extraction liquid is methanol, ethanol, water, n-propanol, isopropanol and/or butanol. The ratio of seed meal to extraction liquid is 1 g:1-12 ml. Preferred Method: The crude extraction treatment comprises mixing tea seed meal and extracting liquid heating, filtering to obtain tea saponin liquid extract, where the heating temperature is 50-110 degrees C and time is 1-3 hours. The purification comprises (1) dissolving crude tea saponin in 80% alcohol solution in the ratio of 11-15 g: 100 ml, centrifuging, and collecting supernatant, (2) placing supernatant in Soxhlet extraction flask, providing cholesterol, wrapping with filter paper, placing in Soxhlet extraction flask, where the mass ratio of crude tea saponin to cholesterol is 11-15:7-12, heating, refluxing, extracting for 6-7 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering extract, washing to obtain tea saponin-cholesterol complex, and (3) drying tea saponin-cholesterol complex, wrapping with filter paper, loading into Soxhlet extraction tube, adding extracting agent in Soxhlet flask, heating, refluxing for 5-6 hours, and cooling to obtain 95-98% pure tea saponin.

TI Producing and constructing soil-improving soil            conditioner, comprises preparing quicklime for            recycling oyster shells, producing soil conditioner and            constructing produced soil improving agent by mixing            quicklime, fly ash power and gypsum.
AU JUNG Y Y
   CHOO Y M
AE JUNG Y Y (JUNG-Individual)
   CHOO Y M (CHOO-Individual)
GA 2022C1873Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing and constructing soil-improving soil                conditioner using oyster shells, comprises (1)                preparing quicklime for recycling oyster shells,                (2) producing soil conditioner using prepared                quicklime, and (3) constructing produced soil                improving agent. The production of quicklime for                recycling oyster shells comprises (a) removing                foreign matter, (b) crushing oyster shells in which                foreign substances are removed and separating, (c)                powdering crushed and separated oyster shells, and                (d) removing carbon dioxide by burning oyster shell                powder at high temperature to calcinate. The step                of producing a soil conditioner comprises (I)                mixing quicklime, fly ash power, gypsum obtained                from chemical company and fine powder of blast                furnace slag, (II) constructing produced soil                improving agent, (III) producing soil conditioner                by mixing quicklime, (IV) removing foreign                substances, (V) washing oyster shells with water,                (VI) pulverizing oyster shells, and (VII) removing                carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing material                i.e. ground grouting and ground cavity                filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, improves soil,                strength of construction materials and water                permeability, helps in crop growth, preserve                natural resources, reduces toxicity of nitrogen                oxides and heavy metals, prevents marine                environmental pollution and reduces environmental                pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing and constructing soil-improving soil                conditioner using oyster shells comprises (1)                preparing quicklime for recycling oyster shells,                (2) producing soil conditioner using prepared                quicklime and (3) constructing produced soil                improving agent on site. The step of producing                quicklime for recycling oyster shells comprises (a)                removing foreign matter, (b) crushing oyster shells                from which foreign substances are removed and                separating them by size, (c) powdering crushed and                separated oyster shells and (d) removing carbon                dioxide by burning oyster shell powder at high                temperature to calcinate. The step of producing                soil conditioner by using prepared quicklime                comprises (I) mixing quicklime, fly ash of power                containing silicon dioxide and aluminum oxide,                gypsum of calcium sulfate and fine powder of blast                furnace slag of steel mill to produce soil                conditioner, and (II) constructing the produced                soil improver on site, adding a mineral component                to the produced soil improver and constructing on                the site..
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for producing soil improving material                using oyster shells (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step of preparing                  quicklime or calcium hydroxide by burning the                  oyster shell powder at a high temperature to                  remove carbon dioxide. The method is carried out                  using soil improving material and green cement                  composite comprising 5-35 wt.% quicklime for                  oyster shells, 15-35 wt.% quicklime for green                  cement, 20-40 wt.% unground blast furnace slag,                  2-6 wt.% gypsum, and 15-35 wt.% fly ash.
   EXAMPLE - The example provide details of manufacturing                and producing quicklime that do not support the                claims.

PN CN115058309-A
TI Producing greengage honey whisky prepared wine            involves putting sorted green plums into container            filled with carbon dioxide, impregnating, adding            glutathione and potassium metabisulfite, centrifuging            and clarifying, adding activated deacidification yeast,            fermenting, extracting green plum extract.
AU CHENG B
   LI L
   GAO M
   ZHANG Y
   TANG J
   ZHANG X
AE YANTAI GAOSHENG WINERY CO LTD (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 2022C24401
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing greengage honey-made wine involves                (i) sorting green plum; (ii) putting the sorted                green plums into hot water for heating; (iii)                draining water of heat-activated green plums,                putting green plums into container filled with                carbon dioxide, adding potassium metabisulfite, and                starting pressure impregnation; (iv) separating                kernels and pulps of the green plums, crushing and                pulping the pulps of the green plums, and adding                glutathione and potassium metabisulfite; (v) adding                a complex enzyme into the slurry obtained,                squeezing to obtain juice, and centrifuging and                clarifying; (vi) adding honey into the juice                obtained, adding activated deacidification yeast,                and performing alcohol fermentation to obtain                fermented wine of green plum; (vii) putting the                kernel of the green plum into extraction tank,                adding whisky, pressurizing and filtering to obtain                a green plum kernel extract; (viii) mixing the                fermented wine, whiskey, honey, and greenage core                extract to obtain preparing liquid.
   USE - Method for producing greengage honey whisky                prepared wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The greengage honey whiskey prepared wine                produced by the method has rich fragrance                components and organic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing greengage honey-made wine involves                (i) sorting green plum; (ii) putting the sorted                green plums into hot water for heat shock                treatment; (iii) draining water of heat-activated                green plums, putting the green plums into a closed                container filled with carbon dioxide, adding                potassium metabisulfite, and starting pressure                impregnation; (iv) after the impregnation is                finished, separating kernels and pulps of the green                plums, crushing and pulping the pulps of the green                plums, and adding glutathione and potassium                metabisulfite; (v) adding a complex enzyme into the                slurry obtained, where complex enzyme comprises but                is not limited to pectinase, cellulase and                amygdalase, squeezing to obtain juice after                enzymolysis is finished, and centrifuging and                clarifying; (vi) adding honey into the juice                obtained, adjusting the sugar degree of the juice,                adding activated deacidification yeast, and                performing alcohol fermentation to obtain fermented                wine of green plum; (vii) putting the kernel of the                green plum into an ultrahigh-pressure extraction                tank, adding whisky, pressurizing and filtering to                obtain a green plum kernel extract; (viii) mixing                the fermented wine, whiskey, honey, and greenage                core extract to obtain preparing liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The fresh plump greengage                  is selected from seven to eight ripe greengages                  in greenish to greenish yellow color.Preferred Condition: The hot water used in                  the step (S2) is 40-50 &#8451;, and the heat                  shock treatment time is 3-10 min. The adding                  amount of the potassium metabisulfite is 30-50                  mg/kg, the dipping temperature is 30-35                  &#8451;, and the dipping time is 8-15 days.                  The addition amount of the glutathione is 100-200                  mg/L, and the total amount of the supplemented                  potassium metabisulfite is 60-80 mg/L. The                  inoculation amount of the deacidification yeast                  is 150-250 mg/L, the fermentation temperature is                  15-18 &#8451;, and when the total sugar of                  the fermentation liquor is reduced to 4g/L, the                  temperature is reduced to 10-15 &#8451; to                  terminate the fermentation.

TI Preparing polypropylene composite automotive interior trim by roasting zeolite, magnesium oxide and calcium oxide, mixing e.g. polypropylene, ethylene acrylate, epoxy resin, ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber and zinc borate, extruding, heating with inorganic nanoparticles, and mixing.
AU XU J
AE XU J (XUJJ-Individual)
GA 202178972M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing toughness-enhanced polypropylene composite automotive interior trim involves (a) roasting zeolite, magnesium oxide and calcium oxide at 400-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (b) mixing polypropylene, ethylene acrylate, epoxy resin, ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, maleic anhydride grafted ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, zinc borate, nano silica, nano zinc oxide and nano barium titanate with roasted materials and mixing at 60-90 degrees C for 30-60 minutes to obtain a slurry, (c) setting the extruder screw speed at 300-350 rpm, extruding the slurry at 200-250 degrees C to obtain the inorganic nanoparticle reinforced polypropylene composite material, (d) heating the polypropylene composite material reinforced with inorganic nanoparticles at 80-100 degrees C to obtain a plasticized molten material, and (e) mixing the plasticized molten material with the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, molding the mixed material in an automobile interior mold to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing toughness-enhanced polypropylene composite automotive interior trim.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite automotive interior trim has enhanced toughness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing toughness-enhanced polypropylene composite automotive interior trim involves (a) roasting zeolite, magnesium oxide and calcium oxide at 400-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (b) mixing polypropylene, ethylene acrylate, epoxy resin, ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, maleic anhydride grafted ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, zinc borate, nano silica, nano zinc oxide and nano barium titanate with roasted zeolite, magnesium oxide and calcium oxide, mixing at 60-90 degrees C for 30-60 minutes to obtain a slurry, (c) setting the extruder screw speed at 300-350 rpm, extruding the slurry at 200-250 degrees C to obtain the inorganic nanoparticle reinforced polypropylene composite material, (d) heating the polypropylene composite material reinforced with inorganic nanoparticles at 80-100 degrees C to obtain a plasticized molten material, and (e) mixing the plasticized molten material with the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, molding the mixed material in an automobile interior mold to obtain the product, where the mass of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is 0.1-0.5 times the mass of the plasticized molten material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano-silica, nano-zinc oxide and nano-barium titanate are all surface treated with a silane coupling agent at 60-90 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The silane coupling agent is vinyl silane, amino silane or methacryloxy silane. Preferred Composition: The step (b) involves mixing 100 pts. wt. polypropylene, 10-30 pts. wt., preferably 20 pts. wt. ethylene acrylate, 5-20 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 2-10 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, 2-10 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 3 pts. wt. zinc borate, 1-3 pts. wt., preferably 3 pts. wt. nano silica, 1-3 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. nano zinc oxide, 1-3 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. nano barium titanate, 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. zeolite, 2-10 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. magnesium oxide and 2-10 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. calcium oxide. Preferred Properties: The nano-silica, nano-zinc oxide and nano-barium titanate have a particle diameter of 60-120 nm.
