TI Performing hydrogen gas turbine combined cycle            polygeneration involves using hydrogen, using            high-purity oxygen produced by air separation as            oxidant, using carbon, burning in split gas turbine to            generate power.
AU SONG G
   CUI X
   ZHANG S
   WANG H
   GUO S
AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 2020B30444
AB    NOVELTY - Performing hydrogen gas turbine combined cycle                polygeneration involves using hydrogen or CxHyOz                gas-liquid nitrogen-free fuel. The high-purity                oxygen produced by air separation is used as                oxidant, and carbon is used instead of nitrogen as                circulating flue gas, then burned in split gas                turbine to generate power and produce                high-temperature flue gas whose main components are                carbon dioxide, oxygen, water to recover the heat                in the high-temperature flue gas through waste heat                boiler (HRSG), and rankine cycle is used for power                generation or cogeneration. The energy utilization                efficiency of the system are improved. The flue gas                discharged is cooled from the waste heat                boiler.
   USE - Method for performing hydrogen gas turbine                combined cycle polygeneration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform hydrogen gas                turbine combined cycle polygeneration that achieves                continuous operation of the hydrogen gas turbine                and avoid thermal NOx problems caused by                conventional air combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing hydrogen gas turbine combined cycle                polygeneration involves using hydrogen or CxHyOz                gas-liquid nitrogen-free fuel. The high-purity                oxygen produced by air separation is used as                oxidant, and carbon is used instead of nitrogen as                circulating flue gas, then burned in split gas                turbine to generate power and produce                high-temperature flue gas whose main components are                carbon dioxide, oxygen, water to recover the heat                in the high-temperature flue gas through waste heat                boiler (HRSG), and rankine cycle is used for power                generation or cogeneration. The energy utilization                efficiency of the system are improved. The flue gas                discharged is cooled from the waste heat boiler.                The cold flue gas is obtained with carbon dioxide                and oxygen as main components. The cold flue gas                returns to the split gas turbine to form closed                cycle. The high-purity and high-pressure nitrogen                produced is used by air separation and used as                nitrogen raw material or used to generate                electricity through expansion generator. The                principle of expansion and refrigeration is used to                carry out combined cooling and power generation.                The electricity-heat/cold-nitrogen polygeneration                system is used as whole. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a device, which comrises hydrogen fuel                conditioning unit, split gas turbine, air                separation and storage unit, waste heat boiler,                condensation heat exchanger, flue gas circulation                and regulation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The purity of oxygen                  separated from air is above 95%, and amount of                  oxygen produced under normal operation is                  theoretical combustion oxygen consumption. The                  oxygen excess coefficient is 2.0-3.6, oxygen                  concentration in the flue gas after combustion is                  9.9-22.7 mol.%, carbon dioxide concentration is                  77.3-90.1 mol.%. The main component is carbon                  dioxide, and concentration is in the range of                  62.3-87.4 mol.%.

The operation accuracy of reversing valve is higher, the direction change of reversing valve is more precise, and the operation stability of reversing valve is ensured. TI Converting lower alkane to alkene by converting portion of lower alkane to alkene in oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, converting carbon monoxide and water to carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and converting oxygenate and hydrogen to alcohol.
AU GOODARZNIA S
   SIMANZHENKOV V
   OLAYIWOLA B
   STYLES Y
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 202061953S
AB    NOVELTY - Lower alkane is converted to alkene by providing input stream comprising oxygen and lower alkane to oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) reactor; converting at least a portion of lower alkane to alkene in ODH reactor and producing ODH outlet stream comprising alkene, oxygenate, water, and carbon monoxide; providing at least a portion of ODH outlet stream to a water gas shift/hydrogenation (WGS/H) reactor, which comprises WGS/H catalyst; converting within WGS/H reactor at least a portion of carbon monoxide and at least a portion of water to carbon dioxide and hydrogen; and converting within WGS/H reactor at least a portion of oxygenate and at least a portion of hydrogen to an alcohol and producing an alcohol outlet stream comprising at least a substantial portion of the alcohol.
   USE - Conversion of lower alkane to alkene.
   ADVANTAGE - Operating the WGS/H reactor in fluid communication with the ODH reactor enables a reduction in energy required to produce the alcohol relative to operating the processes separately. The method can provide a route to convert an alkane into an alkene, reduction of an undesired product, and an opportunity to run at a high alkane conversion (e.g., greater than 95%), where an increase in oxygenate selectivity may result and generate an additional marketable product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) apparatus for oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of a lower alkane to an alkene, comprising ODH reactor with ODH inlet and ODH outlet, where ODH inlet transports ODH inlet stream comprising lower alkane and oxygen into ODH reactor, and ODH outlet transports ODH outlet stream comprising alkene, oxygenate, water, and carbon monoxide, WGS/H reactor with WGS/H inlet and WGS/H outlet, where WGS/H inlet is in fluid communication with ODH outlet to receive ODH outlet stream, WGS/H reactor includes WGS/H catalyst and suitable to generate, in situ, hydrogen and carbon dioxide from carbon monoxide and water of the ODH outlet stream, and WGS/H outlet transports alcohol outlet stream comprising an alcohol; and
   (2) system for ODH of lower alkane to alkene, comprising ODH reactor configured to receive input stream comprising oxygen and lower alkane, ODH reactor configured to produce ODH outlet stream comprising alkene, oxygenate, water, and carbon monoxide, and WGS/H reactor configured to receive oxygenate outlet stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises dehydration reactor comprising dehydration inlet and dehydration outlet, where dehydration inlet is in fluid communication with WGS/H outlet to receive the alcohol outlet stream, and dehydration outlet transports 2nd alkene stream comprising 2nd alkene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The WGS/H catalyst is non-acidic comprising copper, iron, platinum, tin, zinc, magnesium, aluminum, and/or chromium. ODH outlet stream further comprises at least one of an unreacted alkane, carbon dioxide, and/or oxygen. ODH outlet stream comprises greater than or equal to 5 wt.% water, preferably greater than or equal to 30 wt.% water. ODH outlet stream comprises greater than or equal to 2 wt.% carbon monoxide. ODH outlet stream comprises greater than or equal to 5 wt.%. ODH outlet stream is substantially free of hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further comprises maintaining the WGS/H reactor at a temperature of 100-500 degrees C. The method further comprises maintaining the WGS/H reactor at a pressure of 100-500 kPag. ODH outlet stream is provided to the WGS/H reactor at a temperature of 50-400 degrees C. The method further comprises providing alcohol outlet stream to a dehydration reactor and converting at least a portion of the alcohol in the alcohol outlet stream to 2nd alkene. The method further comprises producing olefin derivatives from alkene and/or 2nd alkene. Preferred Compositions: Oxygenate comprises acetic acid, acrylic acid, maleic acid, and/or maleic anhydride. Alkane comprises ethane, and alkene comprises ethylene. Alcohol comprises ethanol. The 2nd alkene comprises ethylene. Preferred Conditions: Temperature of dehydration reactor is 100-300 degrees C, and pressure is 100-500 kPag.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Olefin derivatives comprise polyethylene, polypropylene, ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, polyethylene oxide, polypropylene oxide, thermoplastic elastomers, and/or thermoplastic olefins. Polyethylene comprises homopolymers of ethylene, copolymers of ethylene and alpha -olefins, high density polyethylene (HDPE), medium density polyethylene (MDPE), low density polyethylene (LDPE), linear low density polyethylene (LLDPE), and/or very low density polyethylene (VLDPE).

PT P
PN US2020101251-A1; US11413416-B2
TI Portable endopharyngeal airway device for            ventilating patient, has universal adapter that is            connected to ventilation pump tube for connecting to            ventilation pump, such that air flow to patient is            controlled during procedure.
AU THOMAS D B
AE THOMAS D B (THOM-Individual)
GA 2020252480
AB    NOVELTY - The device (8) has a single-lumen                endopharyngeal nasal ventilation tube (60)                including an opening (6) at a tapered distal end                and an oblong opening in a sidewall spaced apart a                selected distance from the tapered distal end. An                endopharyngeal nasal tube adaptor (15) is provided                with a distal male end for removable cooperative                engagement with a proximate end of the single-lumen                endopharyngeal nasal tube and a portion of a tube                (16) extending from an end-tidal carbon dioxide                (CO2) monitoring port (9). The endopharyngeal nasal                tube adaptor is provided with an opposing male end                in removable cooperative engagement with a sleeve                of a positive pressure Jackson-Reese ambu-bag (32).                The universal adapter is provided with a tee stem                that connects to a ventilation pump tube (30) for                connecting to a positive pressure ventilation pump.                The positive airway pressure device main portion is                configured to allow for controlled air flow to the                patient during a procedure.
   USE - Portable endopharyngeal airway device for                ventilating patient, thorough nasopharyngeal airway                in surgical settings for patients such as                obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) and morbid obesity,                at risk for airway obstruction in surgical                procedures requiring conscious sedation/monitored                anesthesia care (MAC), used by medical                practitioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The end-tidal carbon dioxide (ETCO2) port is                provided for continuous monitoring of carbon                dioxide providing feedback, such that the control                of the amount of air to the patient is enabled. The                opening in the sidewall in cooperation with the                distal eye opening helps distribute positive                pressure air into the airway of patient and thus,                the tube is prevented from being dislodged from the                nostril of the patient due to excessive pressure                buildup in the airway of the patient. The delivery                of the positive pressure air into the airway of the                patient causes the periglottic area within the                airway to expand or distend and causes the larynx                to open, thus the pulmonary exchange of air is                facilitated. The modified JACKSON-REESE ambu bag is                squeezed in synchronization with the inspiratory                phase by the medical practitioner, so that                sufficient time for exhalation by the patient is                allowed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                assembled endopharyngeal airway device.
   Endopharyngeal airway device (8)
   End-tidal Co2 monitoring port (9)
   Endopharyngeal nasal tube adaptor (15)
   Tube (16)
   Ventilation pump tube (30)
   Ambu bag (32)
   Single-lumen endopharyngeal nasal ventilation                tube (60)

PN CN110451477-A
TI Preparation of phthalonitrile-based porous carbon material used for adsorbing carbon dioxide gas, involves mixing phthalonitrile, curing agent and activator, activating obtained mixture, removing excess activator, washing, and drying.
AU JIAN X
   WENG Z
   ZHANG K
   WANG J
   ZHANG S
   LIU C
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019980318
AB    NOVELTY - One-step preparation of phthalonitrile-based porous carbon material involves (i) mixing phthalonitrile, a curing agent and an activator at a mass ratio of 1:0.5-10:0.1-10, transferring the obtained mixture to a corundum ark and activating it in an inert gas atmosphere at high temperature for 1-6 hours, and (ii) removing excess activator by washing with dilute acid, washing with deionized water to neutrality and drying in a vacuum oven for 24 hours. The phthalonitrile is chosen from phthalonitrile-based compounds (I)-(IV), imide-containing phthalonitrile chosen from compounds (V)-(VII) and nitrogen-containing silane-based phthalonitrile compound (VIII). The activator is at least one chosen from potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, calcium acetate, phosphoric acid, and phosphate.
   USE - One-step preparation of phthalonitrile-based porous carbon material is used for adsorbing carbon dioxide gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables one-step preparation of phthalonitrile-based porous carbon material with improved carbon dioxide adsorption amount of up to 7.27 mmol/g.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - One-step preparation of phthalonitrile-based porous carbon material involves (i) mixing phthalonitrile, a curing agent and an activator at a mass ratio of 1:0.5-10:0.1-10, transferring the obtained mixture to a corundum ark and activating it in an inert gas atmosphere at high temperature for 1-6 hours, and (ii) removing excess activator by washing with dilute acid, washing with deionized water to neutrality and drying in a vacuum oven for 24 hours. The phthalonitrile is chosen from phthalonitrile-based compounds of formulae (I)-(IV), imide-containing phthalonitrile chosen from compounds of formulae (V)-(VII) and nitrogen-containing silane-based phthalonitrile compound of formula (VIII). The curing agent is at least one chosen from zinc chloride, aluminum trichloride, ferric chloride, stannous chloride, cobalt chloride, cobalt bromide, boron chloride, antimony chloride, titanium chloride, urea, melamine, trifluorobenzenesulfonic acid, aniline, p-phenylenediamine, p-toluenesulfonic acid, 4,4 diaminodiphenylsulfone, 4,4-diaminodiphenyl ether, 4,4-diaminodiphenylmethane, 2,2'-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)propane, 1,3-bis(3-aminophenoxy)benzene, 4,4'-bis(3-aminophenoxy)diphenyl sulfone and 2,6-bis(4-aminophenoxy)benzonitrile. The activator is at least one chosen from potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, calcium acetate, phosphoric acid, and phosphate.
   R = group chosen from groups of formulae (i)-(xii).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inert gas is nitrogen or argon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The high temperature is 500-1000 degrees C. Preferred Process: The step of removing excess activator involves uniformly dispersing the material obtained from step (i) in a dilute acid solution, ultrasonically processing for 30 minutes to 2 hours or stirring for 24-48 hours, and filtering or centrifuging. The step of washing with deionized water involves performing suction filtration or centrifugal washing to neutrality.

TI Liquid fermentation medium for cultivating            Pleurotus eryngii, comprises sugar cane juice,            distiller's grains powder, soybean powder, maize flour,            peptone, potassium phosphate, magnesium chloride,            calcium carbonate and sterile water.
AU CHANG F
   LIN X
   HONG J
   WANG D
   YAO Z
AE CHENGDU ZHONGYAN RONGZHEN MUSHROOMS CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019318181
AB    NOVELTY - A liquid fermentation medium comprises 3-6                pts. wt. sugar cane juice, 1-3 pts. wt. distiller's                grains powder, 1-3 pts. wt. soybean powder, 1-3                pts. wt. maize flour, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. peptone,                0.5-1.5 pts. wt. potassium phosphate, 0.5-1.5 pts.                wt. magnesium chloride, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. calcium                carbonate, 80-85 pts. wt. sterile water, where the                pH of the liquid fermentation medium is                6.5-7.5.
   USE - Liquid fermentation medium for cultivating                Pleurotus eryngii (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid fermentation medium enhanced                efficiency and increases density of Pleurotus                eryngii.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a method for cultivating a liquid strain                of Pleurotus eryngii, which involves inoculating a                Pleurotus eryngii into a liquid fermentation medium                and culturing at 20-30 degrees C for 6-8 days to                obtain the finished product, where the rotation                speed of the shaker during the cultivation process                is 180-220 r/m
   (2) a method for cultivating Pleurotus                eryngii, which involves constructing a Pleurotus                eryngii planting rack in a greenhouse is equipped                with a temperature control system and a carbon                dioxide generator, dividing the planting frame into                3-4 layers by partitions and each layer has a                height of 0.5-0.7 m, inserting the liquid strain                into a culture bag containing the medium and the                volume ratio of the liquid strain to the medium is                1:8 to 10, stacking the bags on the planting racks                layer by layer at 25-27 degrees C for 2-4 days, the                concentration of carbon dioxide is 0.03-0.05%,                until the mycelium of Pleurotus eryngii is full of                cultivation bags, overgrowing the mycelium of                Pleurotus eryngii with the cultivation bag at 20-22                degrees C for 1-2 days, opening the bag of the                cultivation at 25 degrees C for 2-3 days until the                original length is 1-2 cm and growing the original                base at 12-18 degrees C, the carbon dioxide                concentration is 0.3-0.5% and the light intensity                is 600-800 Lx for 15-18 days until the Pleurotus                eryngii matures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Pleurotus eryngii                  strain is Pleurotus eryngii CICC 14011, Pleurotus                  eryngii ACCC 51678 and Pleurotus eryngii ACCC                  51330.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The medium in the                  cultivating bag comprises 20-30 pts. wt. bagasse,                  10-15 pts. wt. distiller's grains, 1-3 pts. wt.                  strontium, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. magnesium                  sulfate.

PT P
PN CN109485053-A
TI Preparing white carbon black for producing soda ash and sodium silicate comprises carrying out the reaction with carbon dioxide gas, filtering, pulping, drying, crushing, packing, storing, treating sodium carbonate solution and evaporating.
AU GAO H
AE JIANGXI SHUANGLONG SILICON MATERIAL TECH (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201927174W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing white carbon black for producing soda ash and sodium silicate comprises (i) carrying out the reaction with carbon dioxide gas using sodium silicate solution to obtain suspension comprising a silica precipitate and a sodium carbonate solution; (ii) filtering the suspension through a plate frame to obtain white carbon black filter cake and a sodium carbonate solution, washing the silica filter cake with water, pulping, drying, crushing and packing and storing water washing liquid to be processed; (iii) treating sodium carbonate solution and water washing solution by reverse osmosis membrane to obtain concentrated sodium carbonate solution having a concentration of 10-20% and producing a lot of pure water simultaneously; and (iv) evaporating the concentrated sodium carbonate solution through a multi-effect evaporator, directly returning the distilled water with heat to the production of white carbon black and carrying out comprehensive utilization of the cycle.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing white carbon black for producing soda ash and sodium silicate comprises.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally-friendly; has zero discharge production process and realizes comprehensive resource recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing white carbon black for producing soda ash and sodium silicate comprises (i) carrying out the reaction with carbon dioxide gas using sodium silicate solution to obtain suspension comprising a silica precipitate and a sodium carbonate solution; (ii) filtering the suspension through a plate frame to obtain white carbon black filter cake and a sodium carbonate solution having a concentration of 2-6%, washing the silica filter cake with water, pulping, drying, crushing and packing to obtain the white carbon black product and storing water washing liquid to be processed; (iii) treating sodium carbonate solution and water washing solution by reverse osmosis membrane to obtain concentrated sodium carbonate solution having a concentration of 10-20% and producing a lot of pure water simultaneously, where the pure water is used for the production of white carbon black; and (iv) evaporating the concentrated sodium carbonate solution through a multi-effect evaporator to obtain sodium carbonate and distilled water, directly returning the distilled water with heat to the production of white carbon black and carrying out comprehensive utilization of the cycle, where the soda ash produced is used to produce sodium silicate and sodium silicate is used to produce white carbon black.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing silica precipitate and the suspension of sodium carbonate solution comprises (a) dissolving sodium silicate having a modulus of 3.2-3.5 and diluting with water to obtain sodium silicate solution having a density of 1.08-1.24 g/cm3 and filtering the solution through a plate to obtain clear sodium silicate solution; (b) adding a certain amount of the clear liquid into the reaction kettle, starting stirring, heating to 40-95 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide gas to react after the temperature reaches the process setting and is stable, controlling the reaction time at 90-240 minutes and stopping carbon dioxide gas until the pH reaches 8-8.5; and (c) maintaining the above reaction solution warm and aging for 15-60 minutes under stirring. In the step (ii), the filter cake is washed with water to a conductivity of less than 1000 mu s/cm and a high speed beater is used for pulping and the drying and crushing is carried out by pulping, drying and crushing, controlling the product moisture to 5-6.5% and the crushing particle size to 8-15 mu m. In the step (iv), mixing the soda ash produced and the quartz sand having a mesh number of 40-80 meshes and stirring according to a weight ratio of 1:2, mixing uniformly in the horseshoe flame furnace, reacting at 1380-1450 degrees C to obtain sodium silicate with a modulus of 3.2-3.5 and preparing sodium silicate solution using sodium silicate.

AB    NOVELTY - Welding Q500qE high-strength bridge steel angle joint comprises (i) taking welding base material having 40-58 mm thickness bottom plate and 36-44 mm thickness rib plate, where bottom plate and rib plate are Q500qE high-strength bridge steel, longitudinal and transverse tensile strength is 659-730 MPa, longitudinal and transverse yield strength is 528-573 MPa, elongation is 20.5%-266%, low temperature impact is at (-40 degrees C) 232-300 J and shear section rate is at (-40 degrees C) 90-100%, and (ii) fusing two sides of ribs partially through double-sided symmetrical groove, assembling ribs vertically and positioning on bottom plate, shielding ER60Q solid welding wire and carbon dioxide gas, synchronously and symmetrically welding two paths, fixing rib plate vertically on bottom plate, taking H60Q submerged arc welding wire, SJ105q submerged arc flux and submerged arc automatic welding process, filling ship's position and covering on both sides in sequence and ship's angle is 45 degrees .
   USE - The high-strength bridge steel angle joint is useful for welding high-strength heavy plate bridge steel plate units.
   ADVANTAGE - The steel angle joint: has stable weld quality, small welding deformation and high production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding Q500qE high-strength bridge steel angle joint comprises (i) taking welding base material having 40-58 mm thickness bottom plate and 36-44 mm thickness rib plate, where bottom plate and rib plate are Q500qE high-strength bridge steel, longitudinal and transverse tensile strength is 659-730 MPa, longitudinal and transverse yield strength is 528-573 MPa, elongation is 20.5%-266%, low temperature impact is at (-40 degrees C) 232-300 J and shear section rate is at (-40 degrees C) 90-100%, and (ii) fusing two sides of ribs partially through double-sided symmetrical groove, where groove angle is 48 degrees , assembling ribs vertically and positioning on bottom plate, shielding ER60Q solid welding wire and carbon dioxide gas, synchronously and symmetrically welding two paths, fixing rib plate vertically on bottom plate, taking H60Q submerged arc welding wire, SJ105q submerged arc flux and submerged arc automatic welding process, filling ship's position and covering on both sides in sequence and ship's angle is 45 degrees .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The welding process of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding has welding current of 230-250 A, welding voltage of 27-29 V, welding speed of 310-330 mm/minutes and welding line energy of 11-15 KJ/cm; and welding process of submerged arc automatic welding has welding current of 600-630 A, welding voltage of 28-30 volts, welding speed is 380-410 mm/minutes, welding line energy is 24-30 kJ/cm and interlayer temperature is 175 degrees C or lower. The chemical composition of ER60Q solid wire includes 0.050 wt.% carbon, 1.40 wt.% manganese, 0.40 wt.% silicon, 0.006 wt.% sulfur, 0.012 wt.% phosphorus and 0.885 wt.% nickel, 1.2 mm wire diameter, purity of carbon dioxide shielding gas is greater than or equal to 99.5% and H60Q submerged arc welding wire has 4 mm diameter. The carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire ER60Q welding test has tensile strength of 664 MPa, yield strength of 604 MPa, elongation of 20.5%, low temperature impact is at -40 degrees C average 145 J; submerged arc welding wire H60Q and submerged arc flux SJ105q has tensile strength of 634 MPa and yield strength of 545 MPa where elongation is 25% and low temperature impact is at (-40 degrees C) averages 108 J.

TI System used to pyrolysis coal, comprises reaction            chamber for pyrolyzing combination of coal in            supercritical carbon dioxide atmosphere, and            recuperating and condensing circuit that removes some            dissolved pyrolysis products from atmosphere.
AU GOODRICH B L
   WALTER J C
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
GA 2018782524
AB    NOVELTY - System (s1) (400) for pyrolyzing coal (404)                comprises at least one reaction chamber capable of                pyrolyzing a combination of coal in a supercritical                carbon dioxide atmosphere, and a recuperating and                condensing circuit that removes at least some                dissolved pyrolysis products from the supercritical                carbon dioxide atmosphere and then recovers carbon                dioxide for reuse in the reaction chamber.
   USE - The system is useful for pyrolyzing                coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The system exhibits improved flexibility, and                selectively fractionate the pyrolysis                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) pyrolyzing coal comprising flowing an                inlet stream of carbon dioxide in a supercritical                state into a reaction chamber containing coal,                removing, after a contact time, supercritical                carbon dioxide containing dissolved pyrolysis                products from the reaction chamber as an outlet                stream, directing a first portion of the outlet                stream through a first recuperator/collector stage                in which the outlet stream is cooled by a return                stream of carbon dioxide en route to the reaction                chamber, where the first recuperator                (406a)/collector stage includes a recuperator                followed by a condensate collector to obtain a                first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a                first stage pyrolysis product condensate, directing                at least a portion of the first stage carbon                dioxide effluent stream through a second                recuperator/collector stage to obtain a second                stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a second                stage pyrolysis product condensate, and                reconditioning at least some carbon dioxide from                the first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream or                the second stage carbon dioxide effluent steam by                passing the at least some carbon dioxide as the                return stream through the first                recuperator/collector stage to obtain the inlet                carbon dioxide stream; and
   (2) a system (s2) for manufacturing fibers                from coal comprising at least one reaction chamber                capable of pyrolyzing a combination of coal in a                carbon dioxide atmosphere, a separation system                configured to receive the carbon dioxide atmosphere                from the reaction chamber after pyrolysis of the                coal and condense pitch from the carbon dioxide                into a pitch container, and an extruder connected                to the separation system configured to receive and                extrude the pitch condensed by the separation                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a process flow diagram for a                batch of flexible pyrolysis system that can be                tuned to change the pyrolysis products obtained                from a given feedstock.
   System (400)
   Coal (404)
   First recuperator (406a)
   Bypass valve (412)
   Controller (430)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction chamber                  is capable of heating the combination to at least                  350 degrees C at a pressure up to 7-30 MPa. The                  recuperating and condensing circuit includes at                  least one recuperator/collector stage, where each                  recuperator/collector stage has a heat exchanger                  and condensate collector connected to receive                  carbon dioxide atmosphere from the reaction                  chamber as a carbon dioxide process stream,                  transfer heat from the carbon dioxide process                  stream to a carbon dioxide return stream to                  obtain a cooled carbon dioxide process stream,                  and condense and collect pyrolysis products from                  the cooled carbon dioxide process stream. The                  system (s1) further comprises: an additive                  injection system that injects at least one                  additives into the carbon dioxide return stream;                  and a bypass system including many process stream                  bypass valves that distributes the flow of the                  carbon dioxide process stream to the                  recuperator/collector stages, and a controller                  (430) that controls the process stream bypass                  valves, thus controlling the flow of the carbon                  dioxide process stream to the                  recuperator/collector stages. The bypass system                  further includes a plurality of return stream                  bypass valves that distributes the flow of the                  CO2 return stream to the one or more                  recuperator/collector stages. The controller                  controls the return stream bypass valves, thus                  controlling the flow of the carbon dioxide return                  stream to the recuperator/collector stages. The                  controller determines a condensate fraction                  collected by at least one of the                  recuperator/collector stages by controlling, via                  the bypass system, the flow of the carbon dioxide                  process stream and the carbon dioxide return                  stream through the recuperator/collector stages.                  The controller determines the condensate fraction                  collected by the recuperator/collector stages                  based on: a predetermined goal; or information                  received from at least one temperature sensor                  monitoring temperature at least at point in the                  recuperating and condensing circuit. The system                  (s1) further comprises a heat source that                  provides thermal energy to the carbon dioxide                  atmosphere prior to delivery of the carbon                  dioxide atmosphere to the reaction chamber. The                  heat source is a nuclear reactor. The separation                  system in the system (s2) includes at least pitch                  collection chamber configured to condense pitch                  from the carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: directing a second portion of the                  outlet stream through the second                  recuperator/collector stage; and maintaining the                  reaction chamber at a pyrolysis temperature and                  pressure sufficient to maintain the carbon                  dioxide in the reaction chamber in a                  supercritical state, thus pyrolyzing the coal to                  obtain a char and supercritical carbon dioxide                  containing dissolved pyrolysis products. The                  inlet carbon dioxide streams temperature is                  300-600 degrees C and pressure is 7-30 MPa. The                  method further comprises flowing at least a                  portion of the carbon dioxide and dissolved                  pyrolysis product stream through a third                  recuperator/collector stage to obtain a third                  stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a third                  stage pyrolysis product condensate, flowing at                  least a portion of the carbon dioxide and                  dissolved pyrolysis product stream through a                  fourth recuperator/collector stage to obtain a                  fourth stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a                  fourth stage pyrolysis product condensate, and                  flowing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide                  and dissolved pyrolysis product stream through a                  fifth recuperator/collector stage to obtain a                  fifth stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a                  fifth stage pyrolysis product condensate. The                  first, second, third, fourth and fifth                  recuperator/collector stages all operate at                  different temperatures and the method further                  comprises controlling the different temperatures                  of the recuperator/collector stages by setting a                  position of at least one bypass valve                  (412).

PN KR2019087690-A; KR2019670-B1
TI Carbon dioxide recovery system comprises absorption reactor of high-speed fluidized bed type for recovering carbon dioxide by contacting solid absorbent with exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide supplied from outside.
AU PARKYOUNG-CHEOL
   LEECHANGKEUN
   JIN G
   SHUN D
   PARK J H
   MOON J

   KIM H W
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201965434Q
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide recovery system (100) comprises an absorption reactor (10) of a high-speed fluidized bed type for recovering carbon dioxide by contacting a solid absorbent with exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide supplied from the outside through an exhaust gas supply pipe. A recovery cyclone is provided for discharging the solid absorbent and the remaining gas into the solid discharge unit by discharging the solid absorbent, where the residual gas is discharged through the discharge pipe above the former absorption reactor. The solid absorbent contacts the exhaust gas supplied through the former inlet connected to the solid discharge portion and connected with the exhaust gas bypass pipe branched from the exhaust gas supply pipe to recover carbon dioxide. A regeneration reactor is provided for fluidizing the solid absorbent supplied through the solid discharge pipe into regeneration gas to regenerate the solid absorbent.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery system enables to improve solid conversion rate, and determine size of regeneration reactor to treat collected carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic configuration diagram of the carbon dioxide recovery system.
   Exhaust gas supply pipe (2)
   Exhaust gas bypass pipe (3)
   Absorption reactor (10)
   Discharge pipe (11)
   Carbon dioxide recovery system (100)

TI Apparatus for separating solid carbon dioxide from liquids in cold suspension, has sealed system cooling device in thermal contact with liquid, solid carbon dioxide, compacted solid carbon dioxide, or filter within housing.
AU BAXTER L
   BAXTER A
   FRANKMAN D
   CHAMBERLAIN S
   STITT K
AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   BAXTER A (BAXT-Individual)
   FRANKMAN D (FRAN-Individual)
   CHAMBERLAIN S (CHAM-Individual)
   STITT K (STIT-Individual)
GA 2018591004
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a housing (208) provided with an interior and an exterior. A filter (202) is located within the interior of the housing. A compactor is contained within the interior of the housing that compacts solid carbon dioxide against the filter to form the compacted solid carbon dioxide. A sealed system cooling device (230) is in thermal contact with liquid, the solid carbon dioxide, the compacted solid carbon dioxide, or the filter within the housing that keeps the solid carbon dioxide at temperatures below a melting point of the solid carbon dioxide while the solid carbon dioxide is compacted against the filter. The sealed system cooling device is partially controlled by temperature of the liquid. The filter is constructed from mesh, stainless steel, metal, ceramic, carbon, fibrous materials, plastic, diamond, or interstitially formed material.
   USE - Apparatus for separating solid carbon dioxide from liquids in cold suspension.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus utilizes a gas vent to regulate pressure within the housing in an efficient manner. The apparatus connects the gas vent to post-combustion gas input for recycling gas material.

   Housing (208)

PN CN211233432-U
TI Carbon dioxide refrigerating system for refrigerating e.g. fruit, has compressor, condenser, throttle valve and refrigerant passage connected to end to form refrigerant circulation, and carbon dioxide channel connected to liquid return port of reservoir.
AU GU Z
   RAN C
   XUAN F
AE ZHEJIANG INNO THERMAL TECHNOLOGIES CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202078957P
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide refrigerating system suitable for refrigerating fruit and vegetable, relating to the technical field of refrigerating device. The utility model comprises a carbon dioxide reservoir, a heat exchanger, a first throttle valve, a second throttle valve, a carbon dioxide pump, a condensing evaporator set, a compressor and a condenser; the condensation evaporator set comprises a carbon dioxide channel and a refrigerant channel which exchange heat with each other; the carbon dioxide liquid storage device is provided with a liquid outlet and a liquid return opening; the liquid outlet of the carbon dioxide reservoir is orderly connected with the first throttle valve, a carbon dioxide pump, a heat exchanger, a carbon dioxide channel is connected to the liquid return port of the carbon dioxide reservoir, forming carbon dioxide circulation; the compressor, the condenser, the second throttle valve and the refrigerant passage are connected end to end to form a refrigerant circulation. The utility model is suitable for fruit and vegetable refrigerating process, solves the problem of fruit and vegetable dry consumption and freezing, improves the economic benefit of the fruit and vegetable product; it can inhibit the frosting phenomenon in the fruit and vegetable cold storage; it has high efficiency and good refrigerating effect.

TI Removing scale from wellbore by directing            supercritical carbon dioxide into a wellbore, directing            supercritical carbon dioxide at inner surface of            wellbore, and removing scale from the wall of wellbore            with supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU BULEKBAY A
   ATTIAH S
   AL-MUTAIRI T
   ALSOUSY A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201978151S
AB    NOVELTY - Scale (130) is removed from wellbore (106) by                directing supercritical carbon dioxide (114) into a                wellbore to a specified depth; directing the                supercritical carbon dioxide at inner surface of                wellbore at the specified depth; and removing scale                from the wall of wellbore with supercritical carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Removal of scale from wellbore.
   ADVANTAGE - Descaling operations are performed in an                underbalanced condition to prevent formation                damage. The method saves cost and time and                non-soluble scale is removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) reducing skin-effects performed by                supercritical carbon dioxide (114) in a wellbore                (106) which involves traveling through a workstring                (108) positioned in a wellbore to a specified depth                within the wellbore; impacting a wall of wellbore                at specified depth; and reducing skin-effects on                the wellbore in response to impacting the                supercritical carbon dioxide; and
   (2) method which involves pumping                supercritical carbon dioxide into a wellbore to a                target depth; spraying the supercritical carbon                dioxide onto a wall of the wellbore at the target                depth within the wellbore; removing scale (130)                from the wall of the wellbore with the carbon                dioxide; and sweeping the wellbore with a gel after                removing the scale.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side cross-sectional view                of the wellbore cleaning system.
   Wellbore (106)
   Workstring (108)
   Jetting tool (110)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide (114)
   Scale (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Directing the                  supercritical carbon dioxide onto the wall of                  wellbore comprises directing through a nozzle.                  The nozzle is isentropic nozzle. The method                  further includes changing a phase of                  supercritical carbon dioxide to solid, liquid, or                  gas phase of carbon dioxide. The method further                  comprises extracting the removed scale from the                  wellbore, sweeping the wellbore with a gel after                  extracting the removed scale, and flowing                  reservoir fluid through the wellbore in an                  underbalanced condition. The method includes                  lifting the removed scale to a topside facility.                  The method further comprises the supercritical                  carbon dioxide changing phase to solid phase,                  liquid phase, and gas phase of carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Components: The scale is an                  acid-soluble scale.

 culture medium containing 5-20% fetal calf serum,                placing at 37&#177;1&#8451;, 5&#177;1%                carbon dioxide, 95% relative humidity culture box                to culture, TI Preparing flower-shaped cadmium sulfide/silver            sulfide quantum dot composite photocatalyst comprises            preparing flower-shaped cadmium sulfide nanosheet and            silver sulfide quantum dot by oil bath process, and            assembling flower-shaped cadmium sulfide/silver sulfide            quantum dot composite photocatalyst.
AU TU X
   YUE X
   WU Y
   LI F
   FU W
   XIANG Q
AE UNIV CHINA YANGTZE DELTA REGION INST (UEST-C)
GA 2022A9503K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flower-shaped cadmium sulfide/silver                sulfide quantum dot composite photocatalyst                comprises preparing flower-shaped cadmium sulfide                nanosheet and silver sulfide quantum dot by oil                bath process, and assembling a flower-shaped                cadmium sulfide/silver sulfide quantum dot                composite photocatalyst by deposition                adsorption.
   USE - The composite photocatalyst is used in                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite photocatalyst: CO generation                rate is as high as 16.6 &#956;mol.g-1.h-1,                which has potential use prospects in the field of                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction; and                capable of efficiently and selectively reducing                carbon dioxide by photocatalysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the flower-shaped cadmium sulfide/silver                sulfide quantum dot composite photocatalyst                comprising (1) dissolving 5 mmol sodium citrate                evenly in 50 ml deionized water, adding 10 ml                cadmium chloride was (0.3 mol/l) to mixed solution,                stirring for 20 minutes, dropwise adding 5 ml                aqueous ammonia and 10 ml thiourea (0.9 mol/l) to                solution in turn, transferring homogeneous orange                suspension to oil bath and keeping at 60&#176;C                for 3 hours, cooling reaction, centrifuging and                collecting the generated orange precipitate,                washing many times with deionized water and                ethanol, and drying in oven at 60&#176;C for                more than 12 hours; (2) preparing silver sulfide                quantum dot by: adding 10 ml ethylene glycol (EG)                to three-necked flask, introducing nitrogen for 30                minutes to completely remove the air in the system,                during the entire reaction process, continuously                injecting nitrogen to above liquid, heating to                110&#176;C, adding 0.05 mol silver nitrate and                0.1 ml 3-mercaptopropionic acid in turn, after                reactants are evenly mixed, heating to                145&#176;C and keeping for 15 minutes, during                this period, reaction solution gradually changed                from clear and transparent to wine red, finally to                dark brown, cooling the solution to room                temperature, adding deionized water and                centrifuging, and washing obtained product with                deionized water and ethanol; and (3) adding 0.2 g                cadmium sulfide nanosheets and 0.01 g silver                sulfide quantum dots to 20 ml ethanol, stirring for                2 hours, transferring mixed solution to                60&#176;C oven for 24 hours, until the ethanol                is completely volatilized, obtained product is                flower-shaped cadmium sulfide/silver sulfide                quantum dot composite photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1),                  concentration of aqueous ammonia is 25-28                  wt.%.

PN CN113896188-A
TI Preparing boron-doped graphene heat-conducting            film for solar cell of non-metal cathode material,            involves mixing 1,8-di-aza-bicyclo (5.4.0)            undec-7-alkene with alkyl alcohol, introducing carbon            dioxide gas into solution.
AU SUN Y
   LI Y
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202214413B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing boron-doped graphene heat-conducting                film involves mixing 1,8-di-aza-bicyclo (5.4.0)                undec-7-alkene with alkyl alcohol according to mol                ratio of 0.5-10:1, introducing carbon dioxide gas                into the mixed solution at 25degrees Celsius, after                60 minutes, forming the white gel-shaped solid is                dimethylhydantoin/ethylene glycol (DBUH) reversible                ion liquid, taking the DBUH reversible ion liquid                stripping agent, water as solvent, adding graphite                powder, stirring uniformly to obtain graphite                pre-peeling dispersion liquid, transferring the                graphite pre-peeling dispersion liquid into a high                pressure water-heating reaction kettle for peeling                and dispersing to obtain the graphene mixed                dispersion system, the graphene mixed system by                centrifuging, filtering to obtain the pure graphene                product, dispersing the graphene product in the                surfactant water solution with concentration of                3-30 wt.%.
   USE - Method for preparing boron-doped graphene                heat-conducting film used for solar cell of                non-metal cathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, low cost, and can                realize industrial production of boron-doped                graphene heat conducting film. The method can                effectively peel the graphite sheet layer, prevent                the agglomeration of the graphene, and has small                damage to the intrinsic structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing boron-doped graphene heat-conducting                film involves mixing 1,8-di-aza-bicyclo (5.4.0)                undec-7-alkene with alkyl alcohol according to mol                ratio of 0.5-10:1, introducing carbon dioxide gas                into the mixed solution at 25degrees Celsius, after                60 minutes, forming the white gel-shaped solid is                DBUH reversible ion liquid, taking the DBUH                reversible ion liquid stripping agent, water as                solvent, adding graphite powder, stirring uniformly                to obtain graphite pre-peeling dispersion liquid,                transferring the graphite pre-peeling dispersion                liquid into a high pressure water-heating reaction                kettle for peeling and dispersing to obtain the                graphene mixed dispersion system, the graphene                mixed system by centrifuging, filtering to obtain                the pure graphene product, dispersing the graphene                product in the surfactant water solution with                concentration of 3-30 wt.%, adding boron salt with                mass of 0.5-10% graphene product, fully stirring to                obtain graphene dispersion liquid with                concentration of 0.5-10 mg/mL, the graphene                dispersion liquid by vacuum filtering film forming,                drying and calcining to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyl alcohol is                  methanol, ethanol, hexanol, isopropanol,                  diethylene glycol, glycerol, 1, 4-butanediol,                  1-propanol, tert-butanol, 1, 3-propylene glycol,                  cyclohexanol, glucose. The graphite powder is                  crystalline flake graphite powder, expanded                  graphite powder, oxidized graphite powder and                  natural graphite powder. The surfactant in                  surfactant water solution is sodium dodecyl                  benzene sulfonate, polyethylene pyrrolidone,                  sodium dodecyl sulfate, polypropylene benzene                  sulfonate, sodium naphthalene sulfonate, anion                  polyacrylamide, carboxylate and sulfate salt. The                  boron salt is aryl borate of formula (I). The                  aryl borate is benzene borate, naphthalene                  borate, pyrene borate, 2-boric acid anthracene                  salt, 3-methyl phenyl borate, 4-methyl phenyl                  borate, 2-(bromomethyl) phenyl borate,                  4-hydroxy-phenyl borate, 2-methoxy-1-naphthyl                  borate, 4-butoxy-phenyl borate, 4-amino acid                  phenyl borate, 2-carboxyl benzene borate,                  4-fluoro-benzene borate, 4-biphenyl borate,                  4-tert-butyl benzene borate. Preferred                  Conditions: The carbon dioxide gas flow rate is                  0.1-50 mL/minutes. The graphite powder                  concentration in the graphite pre-peeling                  dispersion liquid is 0.5-10 mg/mL. The condition                  of peeling and dispersing incldues atmosphere is                  carbon dioxide, the pressure is 1-10 megaPascal,                  the hydrothermal temperature is 80-160degrees                  Celsius and the time is 3-12 hours. The                  centrifugal condition is that the rotating speed                  is 100-3000 r/minutes and the time is 1-10                  minutes.R^1=aryl;R^2, R^3, R^4=H, alkyl, aryl, halogen or                  acyl; andY+=metal Na or K.

A third output end (6-5) of the heat exchanger is communicated with the input end of the low-pressure circulation accumulator and is used for outputting the refrigerant after re-separation. The first input end (6-1) of the heat exchanger is internally communicated with the first output end, and the second input end (6-2) of the heat exchanger is internally communicated with the second output end and the third output end.

The heat exchanger is connected with the high-temperature refrigerant after primary separation, so that the heat generated by the refrigerating system is effectively utilized, and the energy utilization rate is improved. The refrigerant after the primary separation after heat exchange enters the low-pressure circulation liquid storage device again, and is separated again after reaching the heat exchanger through the oil collecting structure, so that all the refrigerant after the primary separation are separated for multiple times, and an efficient circulating oil return system is formed.

TI Supercritical preparation method of cubilose peptide by adding cubilose peptide and purified water into supercritical reaction kettle, adding phosphatidylserine, linseed oil, docosahexaenoic acid algae oil and lecithin, introducing carbon dioxide gas, pre-expanding and spraying microemulsion.
AU HU Y
AE GUANGDONG PRCIMED BIOENG CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021882036
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical preparation method of cubilose peptide involves (a) adding cubilose peptide and purified water into a supercritical reaction kettle, as water phase, (b) adding phosphatidylserine, linseed oil, docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) algae oil and cosurfactant lecithin into the supercritical reaction kettle, (c) starting the gas bottle to the high pressure kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas, replacing the air in the kettle and lifting pressure to reach the set pressure in the kettle, (d) starting the reaction kettle outlet valve, adjusting the fluid flow rate, and starting the magnetic stirrer under the reaction kettle for stirring at high speed to form supercritical microemulsion, (e) when the system pressure and flow rate is stable, pre-expanding the supercritical microemulsion under the set pressure and temperature condition and then rapidly spraying to the supercritical collecting kettle through the nozzle at a certain flow rate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cubilose peptide for improving sleep.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves efficacy of the bird's nest peptide nutritional product, does not cause damage to the nutritional components of bird's nest nutritional products, removes taste of fats and peculiar smells and increases curative effect and performance of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical preparation method of cubilose peptide involves (a) adding cubilose peptide and purified water into a supercritical reaction kettle, as water phase, (b) adding phosphatidylserine, linseed oil, docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) algae oil and cosurfactant lecithin into the supercritical reaction kettle, (c) when the temperature of the reaction kettle reaches the set temperature, starting the gas bottle to the high pressure kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas, replacing the air in the kettle and lifting pressure to reach the set pressure in the kettle, (d) starting the reaction kettle outlet valve, adjusting the fluid flow rate, and starting the magnetic stirrer under the reaction kettle for stirring at high speed to form supercritical microemulsion, where the process is in the supercritical state and the surface tension after mixing two phases is 0, (e) when the system pressure and flow rate is stable, pre-expanding the supercritical microemulsion under the set pressure and temperature condition and then rapidly spraying to the supercritical collecting kettle through the nozzle at a certain flow rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves magnetically stirring at 1000-3000 revolutions per minute (rpm) or 1000-2000 rpm for 20-50 minutes or 25-35 minutes. The step (e) involves pre-expanding the supercritical microemulsion at 15-45 MPa or 25-30 MPa and 315-395 K or 339-345 K and spraying the supercritical microemulsion into the aqueous medium at a flow rate of 0.5-3 l/minute or 1.5-2.5 l/minute. In method, the finished liposome has average particle size of 100-900 nm.
   ACTIVITY - Hypnotic.

AU ALVES-FILHO O
AE WAISTER AS (WAIS-Non-standard)
GA 202147954G
AB    NOVELTY - Heat pump comprises a closed loop conduit containing carbon dioxide as refrigerant, where the heat pump comprises a compressor in flow connection with the closed loop conduit having an inlet conduit and an outlet conduit, a gas cooler located in the closed loop conduit, located immediately downstream of the compressor in upstream flow connection with compressor outlet conduit and a downstream outlet conduit, an evaporator arranged to heat the refrigerant downstream of the compressor having an inlet conduit and an outlet conduit, and an expansion valve located in closed loop conduit upstream of the evaporator.
   USE - The heat pump is useful for supplying heat to an external process or drying solid materials and liquid-solid mixtures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heat pump comprises a closed loop conduit containing carbon dioxide as refrigerant, where the heat pump comprises a compressor in flow connection with the closed loop conduit having an inlet conduit and an outlet conduit, a gas cooler located in the closed loop conduit, located immediately downstream of the compressor in upstream flow connection with compressor outlet conduit and a downstream outlet conduit, an evaporator arranged to heat the refrigerant downstream of the compressor having an inlet conduit and an outlet conduit, and an expansion valve located in closed loop conduit upstream of the evaporator, where the expansion valve has an inlet conduit and an outlet conduit, where the heat pump further comprises an external heat exchanger in the closed loop conduit, located immediately downstream of the gas cooler in upstream flow connection with gas-cooler outlet conduit, a medium pressure separator arranged downstream of the expansion valve, arranged to separate two-phase refrigerant into liquid refrigerant in conduit and gaseous refrigerant in conduit, a medium pressure expansion valve downstream of the medium pressure separator in flow connection with its liquid refrigerant conduit arranged to expand the liquid refrigerant to a lower pressure into conduit serving as an inlet to the evaporator, and a pressure control valve in flow connection with conduit containing gaseous refrigerant from separator and an outlet conduit, where pressure control valve being arranged to balance pressure in outlet conduit before being combined in junction with outlet conduit from evaporator to form compressor inlet conduit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The liquid refrigerant conduit from separator is connected with a first internal heat exchanger arranged to sub-cool liquid refrigerant before entry into the expansion valve and evaporator by heat exchange with heated refrigerant in conduit from evaporator in conduit. The first internal heat exchanger is arranged to cool recycle gas from a dryer for solids and liquid-solid mixtures and condense water in the same. The second internal heat exchanger is arranged to heat a drying air supply to an end dryer for partially dried solids and liquid-solid mixtures ejected from a dryer. The junction is replaced by a second separator exhibiting a first inlet from the conduit, a second inlet from the conduit, a first outlet at stage containing gaseous carbon dioxide, and a second outlet containing a mixture of oil and liquid carbon dioxide, and a safety control valve, where the first outlet is combined with the second outlet and fed to the internal heat exchanger.

PN US2020270647-A1; TW202031900-A; TW713999-B1
TI New transgenic strain comprising autotrophic host cell and exogenous genes within host cell, useful for producing succinate.
AU LAN E I
   LAI M
   TUNG R
   TSAI J
   KU J
AE UNIV NAT CHAO TUNG (UNCT-C)
   UNIV NAT CHAO TUNG (UNCT-C)
GA 202082692Q
AB    NOVELTY - Transgenic strain comprising: an autotrophic host cell; and many exogenous genes within the host cell, is new.
   USE - The transgenic strain is useful for producing succinate.
   ADVANTAGE - The transgenic strain provides the succinate: with low cost and simple structure with respect to common petrochemical raw materials, thus reducing the production cost of succinate and improving the dependence on petrochemical energy; and which improves the environmental impact and harm (for example, greenhouse effect) caused by excessive carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Transgenic strain comprising: an autotrophic host cell; and many exogenous genes within the host cell, comprising a first exogenous gene, which is a gene encoding a-ketoglutarate decarboxylase (Kgd) or a gene having greater than or equal to 80% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 1638 nucleotides as given in the specification) and having Kgd activity, a second exogenous gene, which is a gene encoding succinate semialdehyde dehydrogenase (GabD) or a gene having greater than or equal to 80% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 2 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 1449 nucleotides as given in the specification) and having GabD activity, a third exogenous gene, which is a gene encoding citrate synthase (GltA) or a gene having 80% or higher sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 3 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 1314 nucleotides as given in the specification) and having GltA activity, and a fourth exogenous gene, which is a gene encoding phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase (Ppc) or a gene having 80% or higher sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 4 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 2760 nucleotides as given in the specification) and having Ppc activity, where expression of at least one of native genes encoding glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase (GlgC), succinate dehydrogenase subunit A (SdhA), and succinate dehydrogenase subunit B (SdhB) is suppressed in the host cell, is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (M1) succinate comprising providing the transgenic strain, and culturing the transgenic strain under a first preset condition, where the first preset condition comprises providing light and providing carbon dioxide; and
   (2) producing (M2) succinate, comprising (a) providing the transgenic strain, and (b) culturing the transgenic strain under a first preset condition, and/or a second preset condition, where the first preset condition comprises providing light and providing carbon dioxide, and the second preset condition comprises providing xylose.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The expressions of the native genes encoding GlgC and SdhB are both suppressed in the host cell. The transgenic strain further comprises a recombinant plasmid in the host cell, where the recombinant plasmid comprises the first exogenous gene, the second exogenous gene, the third exogenous gene, and the fourth exogenous gene. The first exogenous gene, the second exogenous gene, the third exogenous gene, and the fourth exogenous gene are interposed in the genome of the host cell. The host cell is Synechococcus elongatus PCC7942, Synechococcus elongatus UTEX2973, Synechocystis sp. PCC6803, or Synechococcus sp. PCC7002. The host cell is a cyanobacterium comprising the native genes encoding GlgC, SdhA, and SdhB, and capable of producing phosphoenolpyruvate with carbon dioxide. The transgenic strain is capable of consuming carbon dioxide to produce succinate under the condition of providing carbon dioxide and light, and excreting the produced succinate out of the host cell. The knockdown of at least one of the genes encoding GlgC, SdhA, and SdhB in the transgenic strain is performed by clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeats interference for gene suppression. The exogenous genes further comprise a gene encoding a succinate transport protein. The exogenous genes further comprises genes encoding D-xylose-proton symporter (xylE), D-xylose dehydrogenase (xylB), D-xylono-1,5-lactone lactonase (xylC), one of xylonate dehydratase (yjhG) or xylanase D (xylD), 2-keto-3-deoxyxylonate dehydratase (xylX), or alpha -ketoglutaric semialdehyde dehydrogenase (xylA). The transgenic strain is capable of consuming xylose to produce succinate in the presence of xylose, and excreting the produced succinate out of the host cell. Preferred Components: In the method (M1): the first preset condition comprises providing light at a light intensity of 100-800 mu E, preferably 200 mu E; and the first preset condition further comprises introducing mixed air having a gas composition comprising 0-5% carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 20-80 cc/minute to a culture medium in which the transgenic strain is cultured at a culture temperature of 30 degrees C. In the method (M2), the step (b) further comprises performing the first preset condition and the second preset condition alternately.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Improving lentivirus transfection into human            pluripotent stem cell by culturing stem cell, cloning,            centrifuging, culturing cells in Gelatinous protein            mixture-coated culture plate, adding lentiviral            particles, and supplementing polybrene.
AU LIU Z
   YUAN Z
   ZHU X
   WANG S
AE ACAD MILITARY MEDICAL SCI PLA (JSYX-C)
GA 202024124W
AB    NOVELTY - Improving lentivirus transfection into human                pluripotent stem cells, involves (a) culturing stem                cells under feeder-free culture conditions,                culturing, and cloning with ReLeSR(RTM: Enzyme-free                reagent) cell dispersion for stem cell dispersion,                (b) removing the dispersion, adding fresh                serum-free growth medium mTeSR(RTM: Serum-free,                stabilized cell culture medium), using cell                scraping, and transferring the cells to a                centrifuge tube, (c) centrifuging, removing the                supernatant, adding fresh growth medium, and                pipetting to resuspend, (d) inoculating the cells                in Matrigel(RTM: Gelatinous protein mixture)-coated                culture plate, supplementing culture medium, adding                Y-27632, and incubating in carbon dioxide                incubator, (e) replacing the culture medium, and                adding Y-27632, (f) adding lentiviral particles,                and supplementing polybrene to assist transfection,                and (g) incubating the incubator, and replacing                with fresh growth medium to obtain transfected                cells.
   USE - The method is useful for improving lentivirus                transfection into human pluripotent stem                cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the transfection                efficiency of foreign genes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving lentivirus transfection                into human pluripotent stem cells, involves (a)                culturing stem cells under feeder-free culture                conditions, culturing to 80% confluence at passage,                and cloning with ReLeSR(RTM: Enzyme-free reagent)                cell dispersion for stem cell dispersion, (b)                removing the dispersion, adding fresh serum-free                growth medium mTeSR(RTM: Serum-free, stabilized                cell culture medium), using cell scraping to obtain                the cells, and transferring the cells to a                centrifuge tube, (c) centrifuging at 1000 rpm for 5                minutes, removing the supernatant, adding 1 ml                fresh growth medium, and gently pipetting to                resuspend to prevent dispersion into single cells,                (d) inoculating the cells in 0.1 mg/ml                Matrigel(RTM: Gelatinous protein mixture)-coated                culture plate at a cell passage ratio of 1:6,                supplementing 2 ml per well culture medium, adding                10-50 &#956; M Y-27632, and incubating in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator at 37&#176; C, (e)                replacing the culture medium after 24 hours, and                adding 10-50 &#956; M Y-27632, (f) adding 10-20                multiplicity of infection lentiviral particles                after 6 hours, and supplementing 5 mg/ml polybrene                to assist transfection, and (g) incubating in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator at 37&#176; C for                12-24 hours, removing clear fluid, and replacing                with fresh growth medium for normal culture to                obtain transfected cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves always                  adding Y-27632 during the lentivirus incubation                  process to maintain its colony in a relatively                  dispersed state. Preferred Conditions: In step                  (6), lentiviral particles are added at a 20                  multiplicity of infection dose. The incubation                  time for the incubator culture is 24 hours.                  Preferred Components: The stem cells are human                  embryonic stem cells or induced pluripotent stem                  cells.

TI Five-fruit wine manufacturing device comprises crusher, first fermentation tank, separator, second fermentation tank, clarifying tank, and filter tank.
AU MAN Z
AE XUZHOU WUGUOYE FRUIT WINE CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202009948V
AB    NOVELTY - A five-fruit wine manufacturing device comprises crusher, first fermentation tank, separator, second fermentation tank, clarifying tank, and filter tank. The crusher comprises a feeding port and a discharging port. The discharging port is connected to the first fermenting tank. The first fermentation tank and the second fermentation tank are provided with temperature sensor, timer and heating sheet. The separator is connected with the outel of first fermentation tank and inlet of second fermentation tank. The outlet of second fermentation is connected with the inlet of of clarifying tank. The outlet of the clarifying tank is connected to the inlet of filtration tank.
   USE - Five-fruit wine manufacturing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that traditional fruit wine has rough taste and low pulp utilization rate. The wine is beneficial to human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (A) fruit wine comprising 20-50 kg fruit, 50-100 g yeast, 40-50 mg nutritious yeast powder, 40-60 mg sulfur dioxide, 20-30 mg protein, 40-60 mg tannin, 100-150 mg edible gelatin, 5-12 kg liquor, 100-150 g granulated sugar, and 6-8 g tartaric acid; and
   (B) preparation of fruit wine comprising selecting fruit with good maturity and no rot or mildew, removing fruit core, crushing to obtain juice, pulp, seed, and seed pulp; adding granulated sugar to obtained fruit pulp in fermentation process, controlling fermentation liquid total sugar content at 14-16%, adjusting acid by adding 6-8 g tartaric acid/L of fruit pulp, mixing fruit pulps of different acidity, and adding 40-60 mg sulfur dioxide/L; adding adjusted fruit pulp into first fermentation tank, inoculating yeast, adding yeast nutrition powder while fully stirring, gradually heating to 23 degrees C, 27 degrees C and 30 degrees C, pressing wine cap down 2-4 times/day for 10-15 minutes each time until fermentation is finished; immediately ending fermentation when residual sugar in fruit pulp drops to 1%, separating fruit pulp out of the barrel through separator. to avoid undesirable substances; putting fruit pulp into second fermentation tank, sealing with wood, plugging with rubber stopper in the middle, installing fermenting plug to discharge carbon dioxide for 2-3 weeks, removing fermenting plug when carbon dioxide release stops, and ending post-fermentation when wine is not sour and has slight carbon dioxide and alcohol taste; filtering and clarifying, performing cold-heat processing, and completing fruit wine production in about 1 month time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The alcohol content of fruit wine is 10-16 degrees .

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) based energy generation system for expanding supercritical CO2 includes double walled supercritical CO2 turboexpander having inner and outer chambers, with outer chamber having thermal energy attenuator.
AU WILKES J C
AE SOUTHWEST RES INST (SWRI-C)
GA 2019217677
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical CO2 based energy generation system includes heat source that provides supercritical CO2 at first temperature and first pressure. A double walled supercritical CO2 turboexpander has inner chamber (112) housing expansion turbine (225) and outer chamber (114) at least partially surrounding the inner chamber, with the outer chamber having thermal energy attenuator. The inner chamber wall (210) has first thickness fluidly isolating the inner chamber and the outer chamber. The outer chamber wall (220) has second thickness fluidly isolating the outer chamber from ambient environment about the turboexpander. The thermal energy attenuator maintains the outer chamber wall at or below the third temperature and third pressure, with the third temperature less than the first temperature and the third pressure less than the first pressure.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide based energy generation system for expanding supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Maintains thermal attenuator of the outer chamber at elevated pressure so as to reduce the differential pressure across the inner chamber wall in manner requiring less high-temperature alloy material in the construction of the double walled turboexpander. Reduces operating temperature of the outer chamber wall so as to allow use of inexpensive lower-temperature alloy to provide structural strength to the double walled turboexpander. Allow use of thinner wall to form the inner chamber to reduce the quantity of high-temperature alloy material used in fabrication of the equipment. Reduces temperature to permit the use of relatively lower cost materials to form the external or outer walls of the equipment so as to reduce reduce or even eliminate the need for high-temperature alloy material in forming and/or fabricating the external or outer surfaces of the equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for expanding supercritical CO2 to produce shaft work using double-wall turboexpander.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is the partial cross-sectional view of the turboexpander illustrating the inner chamber and flow-through outer chamber, with inner chamber wall separating the inner chamber from the flow-through outer chamber, and the outer chamber wall forming at least one portion of the external housing of the turboexpander.
   Inner chamber (112)
   Outer chamber (114)
   Inner chamber wall (210)
   Outer chamber wall (220)
   Expansion turbine (225)
