PN CN108918716-A
TI Detection of neotame in sweet beverage involves removing carbon dioxide and alcohol in beverage, adding ultrapure water and calculating neotame concentration in obtained sample by liquid chromatography based on volume and weight of sample.
AU WANG T
   WANG J
AE JIANGSU TINGWEI IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018982530
AB    NOVELTY - Detection of neotame content in beverage involves accurately weighing 50 ml beverage, adding the weighed beverage into a beaker, record the total weight of the beverage and the beaker, placing the beaker in a water bath at 100 degrees C to remove carbon dioxide and alcohol, cooling to room temperature, adding ultrapure water to obtain sample treatment solution, preparing different concentrations of neotame standard solution, detecting the neotame standard solution using liquid chromatography, plotting peak curve of neotame concentration with concentration of neotame as the abscissa, and the peak area as the ordinate, analyzing the sample treatment solution under the same liquid chromatography conditions, calculating the peak area of the neotame, establishing the standard curve, and calculating the concentration (X) of neotame in the sample solution based on the volume of the sample solution and the weight of the sample.
   USE - Detection of neotame content in beverage e.g. sweet beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and efficient detection of neotame content in beverage with high accuracy and improved chromatographic condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detection of neotame content in beverage involves accurately weighing 50 ml beverage, adding the weighed beverage into a beaker, record the total weight of the beverage and the beaker, placing the beaker in a water bath at 100 degrees C to remove carbon dioxide and alcohol, cooling to room temperature, adding ultrapure water to obtain sample treatment solution, preparing different concentrations of neotame standard solution, detecting the neotame standard solution using liquid chromatography, plotting peak curve of neotame concentration with concentration of neotame as the abscissa, and the peak area as the ordinate, analyzing the sample treatment solution under the same liquid chromatography conditions, calculating the peak area of the neotame, establishing the standard curve, and calculating the concentration (X) of neotame in the sample solution based on the volume of the sample solution and the weight of the sample. The concentration (X) of neotame is calculated using relation: X=((A(sample)x m(standard))/ (A(standard)x m(sample)))/100x F', where A(sample) is the peak area of the sample treatment solution, m(standard) is the peak area of the standard solution, A(standard) is the amount of material in the sample processing solution, m(sample) is the amount of substance in the standard solution, and F' is purity of the neotame standard solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The liquid chromatography is performed using a mobile phase containing 30% acetonitrile and 70% ammonium acetate buffer solution.

TI Preparing refined sulfuric acid reagent involves crushing low-grade waste sulfur, high-grade waste sulfur and residue and sieving, mixing low-grade waste sulfur and sieved residue, and calcining high-grade waste sulfur and raw material.
AU WANG Z
   XU X
   MA B
AE LULONG SHUANGYI PHOSPHORIZATION CO LTD (LULO-Non-standard)
GA 201861187B
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing refined sulfuric acid reagent involves (a) selecting petroleum refinery gas, natural gas and coke oven gas to produce unpurified low-grade waste sulfur with low sulfur content, (b) crushing low-grade waste sulfur, high-grade waste sulfur and residue and sieving, (c) mixing low-grade waste sulfur and sieved residue to form mixed raw material, (d) calcining high-grade waste sulfur and mixed raw material in a boiling furnace, (e) introducing gas into cyclone, and passing through the cyclone and discharging solid particles, (f) introducing processed gas into sulfur dioxide analytical tower, and purifying carbon dioxide, converting and exhausting, purifying and drying sulfur dioxide, introducing desorption column into evaporator, rectifying and purifying the sulfur dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing refined sulfuric acid reagent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of refined sulfuric acid reagent with high purity and low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing refined sulfuric acid reagent involves (a) selecting petroleum refinery gas, natural gas and coke oven gas to produce unpurified low-grade waste sulfur with low sulfur content, where the main component of sulfur comprises 56-62 wt.% water, 28-32 wt% organic matter, 3-6 wt.% oxide impurities, 4-7 wt.% selected petroleum refinery gas and residue, the residue is comprises quartz sand having silica content of less than or equal to 98.5% and pyrite cinder having silica content of less than or equal to 70%, (b) crushing low-grade waste sulfur, high-grade waste sulfur and residue with particle size of greater than or equal to 6 mm and sieving, (c) mixing 48-52 wt.% low-grade waste sulfur and 48-52 wt.% sieved residue to form mixed raw material, (d) calcining high-grade waste sulfur and mixed raw material in a boiling furnace, (e) introducing gas into cyclone, where the main component of the gas comprises sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and solid particles, and passing through the cyclone and discharging solid particles, (f) introducing processed gas into sulfur dioxide analytical tower, and purifying carbon dioxide, converting and exhausting, purifying and drying sulfur dioxide, introducing desorption column into evaporator, rectifying and purifying the sulfur dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The calcining step is carried out at temperature of 800-900 degrees C. Preferred Components: The evaporator is made of stainless steel.

TI Measuring content of styrene-butadiene-styrene modifier in styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt involves collecting infrared spectrum data of styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt standard samples as training set.
AU WANG K
   DUAN J
   ZHENG H
   WANG P
AE UNIV CHANGAN (UCHA-C)
   SHANXI ROAD & BRIDGE CONSTR GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201858967D
AB    NOVELTY - The method for measuring content of styrene-butadiene-styrene modifier in styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt involves (a) collecting infrared spectrum data of styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt standard samples as a training set, and measuring styrene-butadiene-styrene modifier content in standard sample different, (b) collecting infrared spectrum data of styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt to-be-measured sample as test set, (c) preprocessing infrared spectrum data of styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt standard sample in the training set, carrying out carbon dioxide absorption peak deduction, baseline noise deduction, baseline correction and variance treatment, abnormal data removal and variance treatment to obtain preprocessed spectral data of styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt standard sample, and (d) preprocessing infrared spectrum data of the test set.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring content of styrene-butadiene-styrene modifier in styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and quick measurement of content of styrene-butadiene-styrene modifier in styrene-butadiene-styrene modified asphalt with high accuracy, repeatability, stability and efficiency.

PN CN107815368-A
TI Preparing sparkling wine, by conveying grape pulp into press, clarifying, adding activated dry yeast, simultaneously adding sulfur, clarifying, fining, filtering, freezing and subjecting to pressure filtration using cardboard filter.
AU SONG M
   NIU Y
   MA C
AE WINE PLANT GANSU MOGAO IND DEV CO LTD (WINE-Non-standard)
GA 201826849G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sparkling wine, by conveying grape pulp after crushing into press with automatic nitrogen filling function, clarifying at low temperature, recovering supernatant, feeding into temperature-controlled fermentation tank, filling with nitrogen, adding activated dry yeast, simultaneously adding 45 mg/l sulfur, clarifying, adjusting free sulfur dioxide content and potassium sorbate content, fining the mixture, filtering, transferring into sealed storage tanks for low temperature storage, freezing, and subjecting to pressure filtration using cardboard filter.
   USE - The method is used for preparing sparkling wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sparkling wine, involves conveying grape pulp after crushing into press with automatic nitrogen filling function for pressing, cooling to 8-10 degrees C by tubular heat exchanger, controlling juice yield to 55-60%, transferring to clarification tank, clarifying at low temperature, recovering supernatant, feeding into temperature-controlled fermentation tank, filling with nitrogen, adding activated dry yeast, controlling temperature to 12-13 degrees C, when residual sugar and alcohol content in temperature-controlled fermentation process is detected to be close to process requirement value, simultaneously adding 45 mg/l sulfur, rapidly cooling to 0 degrees C, stopping fermentation, clarifying at 0 degrees C for 7-10 days, stopping fermentation to retain carbon dioxide produced by alcoholic fermentation, transferring clarified liquor to sealed storage tank, controlling temperature to 0 degrees C, maintaining dissolved carbon dioxide gas, storing for more than half a year, adjusting free sulfur dioxide content to 45 mg/l and potassium sorbate content to 200 mg/l, mixing uniformly, fining the mixture, filtering with a pressure filter, controlling temperature to 0 degrees C, transferring into sealed storage tanks for low temperature storage, naturally warming to 3-5 degrees C, controlling pressure to 0.5-0.6 MPa at 20 degrees C, freezing in a freezing tank, controlling temperature to -4.5 degrees C to -5.5 degrees C, subjecting to pressure filtration using cardboard filter, maintaining at 0-4 degrees C, saturating carbon dioxide with sparkling wine mixer, slowly warming to 4-6 degrees C, relieving pressure by circulation pump microcirculation until pressure reaches 0.3-0.34 MPa at 20 degrees C, and controlling temperature to 5 plus minus 0.5 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The clarifying is performed at 8-10 degrees C for 24-48 hours. The fining is performed using bentonite gel. The cardboard filter has size of 0.45 mu m.

PN CN107790106-A
TI Polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material useful for removal of methylene blue in aqueous solutions, is prepared by dispersing polysilazane precursor into toluene, adding tetrabutyl titanate and divinylbenzene and reacting.
AU LIU H
   DENG Q
   LI H
   LI J
   LI Y
   LIU Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201821751P
AB    NOVELTY - A polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material is prepared by (a) dispersing polysilazane precursor into toluene, adding tetrabutyl titanate and divinylbenzene, stirring to obtain a mixed dilute solution, transferring the mixed dilute solution to a hydrothermal reaction kettle, placing the reaction kettle containing the mixed dilute solution in a drying oven, reacting, cooling to obtain a wet gel, placing the wet gel into a beaker containing toluene and leaving, (b) immersing the wet gel in liquid carbon dioxide and drying to obtain the composite aerogel material.
   USE - Polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material is useful for the removal of dyes such as methylene blue in aqueous solutions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material has high porosity, large specific surface area and low density, can be prepared in a simple manner, under mild reaction condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material is prepared by (a) dispersing 4-10 pts. wt. polysilazane precursor into 4-10 pts. wt. toluene, sequentially adding 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. tetrabutyl titanate and 4-8 pts. wt. divinylbenzene, rapidly stirring to obtain a mixed dilute solution, transferring the mixed dilute solution to a hydrothermal reaction kettle, sealing the reaction kettle, placing the reaction kettle containing the mixed dilute solution in a drying oven, reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 1-6 hours, cooling to room temperature of 20-25 degrees C to obtain a wet gel, placing the wet gel into a beaker containing toluene and leaving for 24-36 hours, (b) transferring the wet gel to a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, immersing in liquid carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours and drying at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C and a pressure of 5-10 MPa for 5-9 hours to obtain the composite aerogel material. The polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material has a density of 0.05-0.20 g/cm3, a porosity of 85-98%, a specific surface area of 400-500 m2g-1, a thermal conductivity of 0.015-0.028 W/m.K, an uniform pore size distribution and 2-100 nm mesoporous material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for preparing the polysilazane-titanium dioxide composite aerogel material, which involves carrying out the above-mentioned steps (a) and (b).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of: polysilazane precursor is 5-8 pts. wt.; toluene is 5-8 pts. wt.; tetrabutyl titanate is 1-2 pts. wt.; and divinylbenzene is 5-6 pts. wt. Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the reacting step is carried out at 180-200 degrees C for 2-5 hours and leaving step is carried out for 25-30 hours. In the step (b), the immersing step is carried out for 1-1.5 hours and the drying step is carried out at a temperature of 45-48 degrees C and a pressure of 8-10 MPa for 6-8 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The polysilazane has a molar mass of 1000-1200 g/mol.

PN CN109651041-A; CN109651041-B
TI Preparing low carbon olefin comprises contacting            and separating heavy oil with supercritical carbon            dioxide, separating light fluid phase into light oil            and carbon dioxide and subjecting to steam            pyrolysis.
AU LIU T
   WANG G
   DU Z
   LI W
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG L
   XUE L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP BEIJING CHEM (SNPC-C)
GA 2019393861
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low carbon olefin comprises (i)                contacting and separating heavy oil with                supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain a light                fluid phase containing light oil and carbon                dioxide, and a heavy fluid phase containing heavy                oil, (ii) separating the light fluid phase                containing light oil and carbon dioxide into a                light oil and carbon dioxide, and (iii) subjecting                the light oil to steam pyrolysis to obtain low                carbon olefin.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low carbon                olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can make full use of heavy oil, can                relieve problem of shortage of petrochemical raw                material, cracking device can run for a long time,                avoids the emission of carbon dioxide into the                atmosphere to cause greenhouse effect by using the                carbon dioxide, and the carbon dioxide can be                recycled to achieve the synergistic effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the                  pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide is                  7-50MPa, preferably 7-20MPa, and the temperature                  of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 30-70                  degrees C. The method further comprises                  pressurizing the heavy oil to 7-50 MPa,                  preferably 7020 MPa, and heating the heavy oil to                  30-70 degrees C. The heavy oil is oil obtained by                  primary processing, secondary processing, crude                  oil and/or coal-based oil, preferably the primary                  processing is atmospheric distillation and/or                  vacuum distillation, and preferably the secondary                  processing is catalytic cracking and/or hydro                  cracking. In the step (i), the mixing weight                  ratio of the heavy oil to supercritical carbon                  dioxide is 1:(0.5-20), preferably 1:(2-8). In the                  step (ii), the method of separating the                  components is a temperature rising method and/or                  a pressure reducing method. The supercritical                  carbon dioxide further comprises modifier,                  preferably, the modifier is a lower carbon                  alcohol, preferably, the weight ratio of the                  supercritical carbon dioxide to the modifier is                  1:(0.001-0.15). In the step (iii), the steam                  pyrolysis conditions is as follows: the weight                  ratio of water vapor to light oil is (0.1-0.9):1,                  preferably (0.4-0.8):1, the temperature of the                  cracking furnace cross section is 500-750 degrees                  C, preferably 540-700 degrees C, the outlet                  temperature of the furnace section of the                  cracking furnace is 780-950 degrees C, preferably                  800-900 degrees C, the residence time is 1-500                  ms, preferably 10-400 ms and the pressure is                  0.15-0.22 MPa, preferably 0.15-0.2 MPa.

PN KR2021148749-A; KR2392508-B1
TI Producing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises manufacturing biochar using biomass, and performing potassium hydroxide activation and carbon-dioxide activation on biochar.
AU OK Y
   LEE G B
   HWAN K J
   WAN C
   GALABITANA A D S
   KEPABANIDULANZA D
   JOE Y L
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2021E1847Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises manufacturing biochar using biomass; and performing at least one of potassium hydroxide activation and carbon-dioxide activation on the biochar, where the surface area is 1000 m2/g or more and the total pore volume is 0.5 cm3/g or more.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent produced has high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity and regeneration efficiency and low manufacturing cost; is easily suitable for large-scale facility; treats waste in livestock farms, thus it is eco-friendly; and having pores of the biochar can be uniformly expanded, and the pores provided are strong and structurally stable, thus enabling stable reuse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide adsorbent, has a functional group including S or N, and has carbon dioxide selectivity to N2(SCO2/N2) of 14-46 according to formula as given in the specification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The biomass contains a mixture of 100 wt.% tree by-products, is prepared by sequentially performing potassium hydroxide activation and carbon dioxide activation on the biochar; or 60-80 wt.%, preferably 70 wt.% forest by-products and 20-40 wt.%, preferably 30 wt.% chicken manure and is prepared by only potassium hydroxide activation on the biochar, and the biomass is pulverized and sieved to a size of 50 mu m or less. The biomass is produced as a bio-tea by providing a feedstock into the stationary bed downdraft vaporizer at a rate of 8-15 kg/hour, and the gas flow rate in the stationary bed downdraft vaporizer is 3-5 l/second, and the residence time of the biomass in the stationary phase downdraft vaporizer is 2-5 hours. The step of potassium hydroxide activation comprises immersing the biochar in a 1 M potassium hydroxide solution of 100-150 wt. based on the weight of the biochar for 1-3 hours; and preparing biochar activated with potassium hydroxide by heating the impregnated biochar in a nitrogen atmosphere at 800-900 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours; and washing potassium hydroxide-activated biochar with deionized water to neutralize the pH, and drying in an oven at 100-150 degrees C, and the carbon-dioxide activation is performed in a reactor at a rate of 400-700 ml/minute, heating for 0.5-2 hours at 500-700 degrees C while injecting oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - Carbon dioxide adsorbent was produced with mesquite wood chips pulverized and then sieved using a sieve to obtain biomass composed of 100 wt.% mesquite wood chips with a mesh size of 50 mu m. Biochar was prepared while using the prepared biomass as a feedstock and providing it to a fixed-bed downdraft gasifier at a rate of 10 kg/hour. At this time, the gas flow rate of the vaporizer was 4 l/second, and the residence time of the feedstock in the vaporizer was 3 hours.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2019212359-A2; NO201800621-A; WO2019212359-A3; NO344627-B1; CA3098811-A1; EP3787780-A2; CN112423867-A; US2021229031-A1; JP2021522992-W; EP3787780-B1
TI Gas separation membrane for e.g. separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture, comprises gas permeable or porous support layer, and carbon dioxide selective polymer layer comprising carbonic anhydrase enzymes fixed within polymer layer.
AU SANDRU M
   SANDRU E M
   STENSTAD P M
   MARIANA SANDRU E
AE SINTEF TTO AS (SNTF-C)
   SINTEF TTO AS (SNTF-C)
   SINVENT AS (SNTF-C)
   SINTEF TTO AS (SNTF-C)
   SINTEF TTO AS (SNTF-C)
GA 2019937762
AB    NOVELTY - A gas separation membrane comprises a gas permeable or porous support layer, and at least one carbon dioxide selective polymer layer comprising carbonic anhydrase enzymes fixed within the at least one carbon dioxide selective polymer layer.
   USE - Gas separation membrane is used for separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture e.g. flue gas from power plants, natural gas, biogas, air and/or fermentation processes and anesthetic gases, and in membrane contactor for separating carbon dioxide from blood, or water in aquaculture and pisciculture (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas separation membrane has improved carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity, and carbon dioxide flux. The membrane is capable of reducing undesirable side reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture using the gas separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The carbonic anhydrase enzymes are thermostable enzymes, preferably resistant to temperature of at least 50 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer layer further comprises amine groups. The polymer layer is based on a hydrophilic and/or water vapor permeable polymer. The hydrophilic polymer is chosen from polyvinyl alcohol, polyacrylamide, polyvinyl amide, alginate, chitosan and polyvinyl amine. The gas separation membrane further comprises a protective gutter layer made of high gas and water vapor permeability material. Preferred Properties: The polymer layer has a thickness of 0.1-10 mu m, preferably 0.1-5 mu m.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Medium-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent used in adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide in industrial flue gas or water-gas shift, is obtained by using two-dimensional layered nanomaterial hydrotalcite as precursor.
AU LIN Y
   ZHANG X
   LI K
   YANG Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
   UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2018145985
AB    NOVELTY - A medium-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent is obtained by modifying two-dimensional layered nanomaterial hydrotalcite as precursor by intercalation of organic anions and cellulose-based additives, and activating by calcining.
   USE - Medium-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent used in adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide in industrial flue gas or water-gas shift (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medium-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent has improved specific surface area and pore volume, and is prepared efficiently and economically by simple method with reduced consumption of energy and without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of medium-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent; and
   (2) use of medium-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent in adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide in industrial flue gas or water-gas shift.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The cellulose additive is methylcellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose or hydroxypropyl cellulose.

TI Installation for cooling gas stream with e.g.            carbon dioxide in water washing tower, has drawing unit            drawing water downstream of pump and being connected to            tower to send pressurized water at temperature to            indirect heat exchanger.
AU LECLERC M
   TRAN M
   CALORO G L
   HARBAL A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022D45744
AB    NOVELTY - The installation has a washing tower (3), a                pipe for sending gas flow to first temperature at                bottom of the tower, a pipe (13) for sending water                to a second temperature lower than the first                temperature at a first level at top of the washing                tower. A pump (7) is connected to a tank of a                column to withdraw water (5) from the tank and to                the pump for pressurizing the water withdrawn from                the tank. A drawing unit draws water downstream of                the pump, and is being connected to the tower to                send pressurized water at a third temperature to an                indirect heat exchanger (25) located in the tower                at a second level above the first level. The third                temperature is higher than the second temperature                and lower than the first temperature.
   USE - Installation for cooling a gas stream with                carbon dioxide, water and other component in a                water washing tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation can reduce heating cost and                reduce electrical consumption of the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for cooling a gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a                washing tower.

PN CN114345319-A
TI Preparing modified nano-ceria catalyst that is used for preparing dimethyl carbonate, involves dropping adding the cerium nitrate solution to sodium hydroxide solution to obtain cerium hydroxide suspension under stirring, and adding obtained cerium hydroxide suspension.
AU WANG L
   HUANG Y
   XU W
   HAN Y
   CHENG Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG QUZHOU RES INST (UYZH-C)
GA 2022581569
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified nano-ceria catalyst involves dropping cerium nitrate solution into a sodium hydroxide solution, stirring to obtain a cerium hydroxide suspension, adding the obtained cerium hydroxide suspension into a reaction kettle for curing, performing alcohol washing and water washing on the cerium hydroxide, dispersing the obtained cerium hydroxide in deionized water, performing ultrasonic oscillation, freezing overnight, freezing, drying, and calcining the dried cerium hydroxide powder to obtain the nano-ceria catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing modified nano-ceria catalyst that is used for preparing dimethyl carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can obtain a high-activity catalyst, and when the catalyst is used for the reaction of synthesizing dimethyl carbonate from carbon dioxide and methanol, a dimethyl carbonate product can be obtained with high yield, and the selectivity of the dimethyl carbonate is close to 100%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a modified nano-ceria catalyst, which is prepared by the preparation method, and the modified nano-ceria catalyst is in the form of short rod-shaped nano-ceria; and
   2. a method for preparing dimethyl carbonate, which involves: subjecting carbon dioxide and methanol to a synthesis reaction under the effect of the modified nano-ceria catalyst to obtain the dimethyl carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for preparing modified nano-ceria catalyst specifically involves dropping cerium nitrate solution into a sodium hydroxide solution, stirring at speed of 400-800 revolutions per minute for 20-40 minutes to obtain a cerium hydroxide suspension, adding the obtained cerium hydroxide suspension into a reaction kettle for curing for 10-14 hours, performing alcohol washing and water washing on the cerium hydroxide for 3-5 times, dispersing the obtained cerium hydroxide in deionized water, performing ultrasonic oscillation for 20-40 minutes, freezing overnight, freezing, drying at -60-80degrees Celsius for 10-14 hours, and calcining the dried cerium hydroxide powder at 600-800degrees Celsius for 4-6 hours, and the heating rate is 2-5degrees Celsius/minutes to obtain the nano-ceria catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the modified nano-ceria catalyst and the amount of methanol is 1:50-100, the carbon dioxide pressure is 3-5 megapascal, and the mass ratio of the methanol and the amount of 2-cyanopyridine is 1:1-1.5. The synthesis reaction is performe at 120-160degrees Celsius for 1-5 hours, and the stirring speed is 400-800 revolutions per minute. The dehydrating agent 2-cyanopyridine forms aqueous-absorbing product 2-pyridine amide after the synthesis reaction, and the aqueous-absorbing product 2-pyridine amide generates 2-cyanopyridine through a dehydration process and returns it to the reaction kettle as a liquid-phase raw material.

AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202256313N
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing nickel-gold (NiAu) bimetallic alloy nano catalyst involves dissolving nickel precursor salt and gold precursor salt in deionized water at same time, and adjusting pH value to 10-12; adding carrier, fully mixing and stirring for 1-5 hours; collecting solid component; transferring mixture into blast drying oven at temperature of 30-100degrees Celsius for drying for 1-24 hours; roasting obtained dry solid at 100-300degrees Celsius for 4-8 hours; and roasting for 1-10 hours at 300-800degrees Celsius in reducing atmosphere to obtain NiAu bimetallic alloy nano catalyst.
   USE - Method for synthesizing NiAu bimetallic alloy nanocatalyst used for catalyzing reduction of carbon-dioxide (CO2) to carbon monoxide (CO) through reverse water gas shift reaction, followed by taking CO2 and hydrogen (H2) as reaction raw material, and reacting at 300-600degrees Celsius (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for synthesizing NiAu bimetallic alloy nanocatalyst enables to exhibit excellent catalytic activity and has air speed of 180000 ml gcat-1hour-1, operates for 40 hours without obvious inactivation under condition of 400degrees Celsius.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a NiAu bimetallic alloy nanocatalyst, which is prepared by above method.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel precursor salt comprises nickel acetylacetonate, nickel chloride, nickel acetate, nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate and corresponding hydrates. The carrier comprises silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, cerium dioxide, molybdenum oxide, graphene and graphene oxide. The reducing atmosphere comprises hydrogen-containing atmosphere, which is hydrogen or atmosphere consists of more than 5 vol.% hydrogen and rest gases, where rest gases are nitrogen, argon and helium.Preferred Conditions: The molar concentration of precursor salt of nickel in deionized water is 50-200 millimoles per liter, preferably 80-120 millimoles per liter, and atomic ratio of nickel (Ni) to gold (Au) in aqueous solution is 40:1-1:40, preferably 4:1-1:4. The total loading of NiAu is 1-20%, preferably 1-5%. The roasting temperature is 300-800degrees Celsius, preferably 500-600degrees Celsius, and the roasting time is 1-10 hours, preferably 2-6 hours. The NiAu bimetallic alloy particles of nanocatalyst are spherical particles with morphology ranging from 2 to 5 nanometers.Preferred Method: The method for synthesizing NiAu bimetal alloy nano catalyst involves providing pH adjustment mode comprises adding one or more of 15-28% ammonia water, more than 0.5 mol/L sodium hydroxide, more than 0.5 mol/L potassium hydroxide and other alkaline substances or corresponding aqueous solutions; collecting solid components by combination of vacuum filtration, evaporation to dryness or centrifugation, where temperature of 30-100degrees Celsius in fourth step includes any temperature point in 30-100degrees Celsius, preferably 60-80degrees Celsius and time is 1-24 hours, preferably more than 10 hours; in fifth step, 100-300degrees Celsius includes any temperature point in 100-300degrees Celsius, preferably 120-200degrees Celsius.

TI Air conditioner control system has control unit that compares sensing values of measurement elements by sensing unit with preset reference value, and adjusts damper opening rate and plasma output amount of plasma module based on result.
AU KIM J H
   SUK Y J
   JUNG D
   KIM Y K
AE CHEONG NAM AIR CONDITIONING (CHEO-Non-standard)
GA 202240142L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a plasma sensing unit sensing fine dust, carbon dioxide, formaldehyde and airborne bacteria. A set of measurement elements determines air quality of an air conditioning space. A control unit compares sensing values of the measurement elements by the sensing unit with a preset air quality reference value, and adjusts a damper opening rate and plasma output amount of a plasma module based on the comparison result. The control unit reduces amount of external air introduced by lowering the opening rate of the damper to reduce the amount of plasma output.
   USE - Air conditioner control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The control unit adjusts the damper opening rate and the plasma output amount of the plasma module based on the comparison result, so that concentration of fine dust and carbon dioxide in the air of the air conditioning space can be improved and operating state of an air conditioner can be adjusted according to the air quality of the space, thus increasing energy consumption efficiency and air conditioning efficiency by controlling amount of outside air introduced.

PN CN114014949-A; CN114014949-B
TI Preparation of water-soluble yeast glucan includes            mixing yeast glucan into absolute ethanol to obtain            slurry, adding calcium hydrogen phosphate, wet ball            milling to obtain pretreated slurry, introducing carbon            dioxide gas, filtering and spray drying to obtain            prefabricated powder.
AU WANG S
   HE L
   NING Y
   SHAO F
   DING J
   QI C
AE QI C (QICC-Individual)
GA 202228216B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of water-soluble yeast glucan                comprises mixing yeast glucan into absolute ethanol                to obtain slurry, adding calcium hydrogen phosphate                into slurry for sealed planetary wet ball milling                to obtain pretreated slurry, mixing pretreated                slurry into water to obtain diluent, introducing                carbon dioxide gas, treating with microwave for 2-5                hours, filtering to obtain filtrate, mixing                filtrate, spray drying to obtain prefabricated                powder, adding acetic acid to premixed powder,                mixing and drying filtered residue two times. The                mass ratio of yeast glucan to absolute ethanol is                5:1-3. The addition amount of calcium hydrogen                phosphate is 3-9 times the mass of yeast glucan.                The mechanical energy of grinding ball acting on                yeast glucan is transformed into internal energy of                glucan solid powder to improve crystal structure.                The yeast lucan comprises 55-65% soluble glucan.                The soluble lucan is converted to soluble                phosphorylated glucan.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of                water-soluble yeast glucan.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the defects of existing                water-soluble yeast glucan and uses wet planetary                ball milling combined with microwave micro                dissolution reaction to phosphorylate the hydroxyl                on the surface of yeast glucan to achieve the                effect of water-soluble modification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Preferred Components:                  The mass ratio of yeast glucan to absolute                  ethanol is 5:1-3. The addition amount of calcium                  hydrogen phosphate is 3-9 times the mass of yeast                  glucan. The mass ratio of pretreatment slurry to                  water is 1:15-25. The filtrate is adsorbed by                  activated carbon. The activated carbon is                  negative ion activated carbon. The mass ratio of                  acetic acid to prefabricated powder is                  3-5:2.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  yeast glucan to absolute ethanol is 5:1-3. The                  addition amount of calcium hydrogen phosphate is                  3-9 times the mass of yeast glucan. The mass                  ratio of pretreatment slurry to water is 1:15-25.                  The filtrate is adsorbed by activated carbon. The                  activated carbon is negative ion activated                  carbon. The mass ratio of acetic acid to                  prefabricated powder is 3-5:2.

TI Continuously producing expanded foam particles involves impregnating granulates with feeding liquidized carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen as blowing agent and separating the expanded foam particles from the gaseous blowing agent.
AU SCHERZER D
   NOGUEIRA DUARTE M S
   THOMPSON E S
   AHLERS J
   WASSNER E
   STAMMER A
   NOGUEIRA D M S
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2021E7364P
AB    NOVELTY - A process for continuously producing expanded foam particles involves (a) charging thermoplastic polymer granulates from a storage tank (R1) into a pressurized impregnation zone (R2), (b) continuously feeding liquidized carbon dioxide, nitrogen or mixture as blowing agent into the impregnation zone, the impregnation zone is maintained under supercritical conditions at a temperature of 31-250 degrees C and a pressure of 1-30 MPa, (c) impregnating the thermoplastic polymer granulates during a residence time of 5-600 minutes in the impregnation zone with the blowing agent, (d) discharging the impregnated thermoplastic polymer granulates from the impregnation zone into a low-pressure expansion zone (R3) with expansion of the thermoplastic polymer granulates to form expanded foam particles, and (e) separating the expanded foam particles from the gaseous blowing agent in a separation zone (R4).
   USE - A process for continuously producing expanded foam particles.
   ADVANTAGE - The process allows to control the foaming and quality of the foamed particles by adjusting the temperature, pressure and time of the impregnation. The polyolefin foam beads can be produced with increased efficiency and productivity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the equipment for carrying out the process.
   Pressurized chamber (F1)
   Storage tank (R1)
   Pressurized impregnation zone (R2)
   Low-pressure expansion zone (R3)
   Separation zone (R4)

PN CN113624918-A
TI Method for evaluating indoor air quality, involves calculating total score of indoor air quality according to weight and piecewise function, and evaluating air quality by comparing numerical value of total score with unified standard.
AU YU F
AE NANJING DONGXU INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D1497G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves scoring five indoor air parameters of temperature, humidity, particulate matter (PM 2.5), total volatile organic compound (T-VOC) and carbon dioxide according to characteristics of different parameters to obtain a temperature parameter score, a humidity parameter score, a carbon dioxide parameter score, PM 2.5 parameter score and T-VOC parameter score. The temperature parameter score is calculated by adopting different standards in winter and summer, and the humidity parameter score is calculated and divided into dehumidifying seasons according to difference between winter and summer. A weight system and a unified standard of five indoor air parameters are formulated according to different seasons and human comfort levels, a total score of indoor air quality is calculated according to weight and piecewise function, and the air quality is evaluated by comparing a numerical value of total score with the unified standard.
   USE - Method for evaluating indoor air quality.
   ADVANTAGE - The total score of indoor air quality is calculated according to weight and piecewise function, and the total score value is compared with the uniform standard to evaluate air quality, so that the air quality total score is calculated by adopting a mode of weighted average of five parameters, and the indoor air quality is measured through the total score.

AU LEI L
   YANG B
   FENG S
   HOU Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG QUZHOU RES INST (UYZH-C)
GA 2021B4155M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monoatomic nickel-nitrogen coordination co-doped porous carbon/nano zirconia composite electrode comprises (i) hydrothermally reacting zirconium salt, 2,2-bipyridine-5,5-dicarboxylic acid and formic acid in an organic solvent and centrifuging to dry to obtain MOFs, (ii) mixing MOFs material and nickel salt in a solvent, centrifuging, washing, and drying to obtain solid, and (iii) calcining solid, removing metallic nickel particles by etching to obtain monoatomic nickel-nitrogen coordination co-doped porous carbon/nano zirconia composite electrode. The organic solvent includes N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-diethylformamide and dimethylsulfoxide. The solvent includes acetonitrile ethanol and/or methanol.

   HAN J

   WANG Z

GA 202181725S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting waste gas into a                low temperature humidifier. The waste gas is cooled                and condensed by using the low temperature                humidifier to control temperature of volatile                organic matter in the waste gas in preset                temperature range. The waste gas is input after                cooling and condensing into a zeolite rotating                wheel concentrating device. The waste gas is                concentrated by using the zeolite rotating wheel                concentrating device. The concentrated waste gas is                input into a combustion tower. Inorganic substance                such as carbon dioxide and water are directly                discharged after combustion.
   USE - Method for processing waste gas volatile                organic matter by using a zeolite rotating wheel                concentrating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature of the waste gas is controlled                in the temperature range suitable for the zeolite                rotating wheel concentrating device, so as to                protect the expensive zeolite adsorbent, thus                reducing the waste gas processing cost.

PN CN112917796-A; CN112917796-B
TI Starch-free polyvinyl alcohol rubber cotton foaming method, involves extruding pre-foamed structure into mold by extruder work, under action of air pressure, and obtaining finished product after foaming, curing and demolding injection mold.
AU HE W
   CHEN S
   LIU Y
AE SUQIAN JIAHE PLASTIC METAL PROD CO LTD (SUQI-Non-standard)
GA 202165495G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing the polyvinyl alcohol raw material into the reaction device, and blowing protective gas into the reaction device to remove the air, and then sealing the reaction device and heating to 150-160 degrees C to melt the raw material. The protective gas is replenished into the reaction device at a rate of 300-400mL/min, so that the pressure value in the reaction device reaches predetermined value. The pre-foamed structure is pressed into the extruder, and the pre-foamed structure is extruded into the mold by the work of the extruder, under the action of air pressure. A finished product is obtained, after foaming, curing and demolding the injection mold.
   USE - Starch-free polyvinyl alcohol rubber cotton foaming method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding portion of the material dissolved carbon dioxide gas quantity is obviously higher than other portions, and ensuring subsequent uniformity of a finished product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an intelligent production equipment used in the foaming process of starch-free polyvinyl alcohol rubber cotton.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the overall structure.

   Carbon dioxide storage tank (2)
   Extrusion molding machine (6)
   Feeding pipe (7)

PN JP2021075419-A
TI Manufacturing Cs- and/or Rb-CHA and/or PHI-type zeolite for adsorption separation material by heat-processing solid mixture containing FAU-type zeolite and solid alkali source, and ion-exchanging cation in obtained zeolite to Cs and/or Rb.
AU TANAKA S
   MIYAGAWA S
AE UNIV KANSAI (UYKK-C)
GA 202153601M
AB    NOVELTY - Cesium (Cs)- and/or rubidium (Rb)-CHA type and/or PHI-type zeolite is manufactured by (Process 1) heat-processing solid-state mixture containing FAU-type zeolite and solid alkali source, without requiring addition of organic structure-defining agent by steam atmosphere to obtain Na-CHA-type and/or Na-PHI-type zeolite by crystal conversion method; and (Process 2) ion-exchanging the cation in the CHA-type and/or PHI type zeolite obtained at Process 1 to Cs ion and/or Rb ion, and obtaining Cs- and/or Rb-CHA type, and/or PHI type zeolite.
   USE - Manufacture of cesium (Cs)- and/or rubidium (Rb)-CHA type and/or PHI-type zeolite for adsorption separation material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require the use of organic structure defining agent, and can provide zeolite in a simple manner with smaller environmental load.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) Cs- and/or Rb-CHA type, and/or PHI type zeolite in which carbon-dioxide adsorption-and-desorption isothermal line contains a sigmoid curve; and
   (2) adsorption separation material comprising Cs- or Rb-type CHA zeolite, or Cs-type or Rb-type PHI zeolite, which is characterized by exhibiting a sigmoid-type carbon dioxide adsorption or desorption isotherm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: FAU type zeolite is Na-FAU type zeolite. Alkali source is an alkali metal hydroxide. Mass ratio of FAU type zeolite with respect to alkali source is 1-25. Mass ratio of FAU type zeolite with respect to water in the enclosed space is 1-50. Preferred Conditions: Temperature of heat processing is 115-200 degrees C. Heat processing is performed within an enclosed space. Preferred Process: In process 2, the cation is ion-exchanged to Cs ion.

PN CN112730533-A; CN112730533-B
TI Preparing nickel modified niobium oxide gas sensor            in a light-assisted gas sensor, involves hydrothermally            reacting washed fluorine doped tin oxide interdigital            electrode sheet in a mixed solution containing nickel            nitrate hexahydrate.
AU DAI W
   WANG X
   CHEN X
   ZHANG Z
   FU X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202147286K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel modified niobium oxide gas                sensor involves hydrothermally reacting washed                fluorine doped tin oxide (FTO) interdigital                electrode sheet in a mixed solution containing                nickel nitrate hexahydrate and ammonium niobate                oxalate hydrate. The FTO interdigital electrode                with powdered nickel oxide/niobium oxide is grown                on the surface. The electrode sheet is taken out,                rinsed with deionized water, dried, calcined at                high temperature and reduced to prepare                product.
   USE - Method for preparing nickel modified niobium                oxide gas sensor used in a light-assisted gas                sensor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel oxide/niobium pentoxide                gas-sensitive element displays better response                performance to carbon dioxide and hydrogen under                the room temperature and ultraviolet light                condition, and has good stability and                repeatability, which simplifies the preparation                process of the gas-sensitive element. Moreover, it                has good application prospect in preparation of                semiconductor light-assisted gas sensor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using nickel modified niobium oxide gas sensor                used in a light-assisted gas sensor, which involves                producing gas-sensitive response by gas sensor to                carbon dioxide or hydrogen under room temperature                and ultraviolet light conditions, using as a                light-assisted gas sensor in a light-assisted gas                sensor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The FTO interdigital                  electrode sheet is a 1.5 x 1cm FTO conductive                  glass that is etched, so that the middle of the                  conductive surface is cut off with a gap of 0.1                  millimeter wide, and the two ends of the formed                  electrode sheet have conductive properties. The                  molar ratio of nickel to niobium in the mixed                  solution is 1:10. The temperature of the                  hydrothermal reaction is 175 degrees C, and the                  time is 14-16 hours. The drying temperature is                  50-70 degrees C. The temperature of the                  high-temperature roasting is 300-500 degrees C,                  and the time is 2 hours. The reduction is                  performed in an atmosphere of 3% hydrogen + 97%                  nitrogen at 250 degrees C for 1 hour.

AB    NOVELTY - Detecting thermal reaction strength of smokeless lump coal comprises (i) adding the coke and anthracite coal into the bottom with holes of corundum crucible, (ii) placing the corundum crucible in the high temperature reactor, connecting the gas path pipeline, starting heating after checking, heating speed is 10 degrees C/minute, heating at 400 degrees C, introducing nitrogen protection, heating at 1100 degrees C, keeping the constant temperature, blowing the mixed gas of carbon dioxide gas or carbon dioxide gas and carbon monoxide gas, continuously 2 hours, after gas is changed into nitrogen, and stopping heating, (iii) taking out the sample cooled to room temperature, separating and weighing the coke and anthracite block, after weighing, placing the sample into drum, rotating for 30 minutes, sieving by a round hole sieve of 10 mm, the coke and anthracite blocks on the round hole sieve respectively take the reaction intensity of the coke and anthracite blocks after reacting.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The prepared material                  is bismuth-tin alloy material comprising 0.1-99.9                  mol.% and bismuth 0.1-99.9 mol.% tin, bismuth                  doped tin comprising 0-0.1 mol.% bismuth or tin                  doped bismuth comprising 0-0.1 mol.% tin solid                  thin film or nanostructure for metal                  electrocatalyst or electrode material. Preferred                  Method: The method involves (a) loading the metal                  bismuth and metal tin in an evaporation boat                  comprising molybdenum boat, placing under the                  conductive substrate or gas diffusion electrode                  or ion exchange membrane substrate, and                  vaporizing the metal by heating, and (b)                  depositing on the conductive substrate or a solid                  film or forming micro-nano structure on the gas                  diffusion electrode or ion exchange membrane                  substrate to obtain bismuth-tin alloy material                  comprising 0.1-99.9 mol.% bismuth and 0.1-99.9                  mol.% tin, bismuth doped tin comprising 0-0.1                  mol.% bismuth or tin doped bismuth comprising                  0-0.1 mol.% tin electrocatalyst material, where                  the heating and evaporation condition of metal                  bismuth is 0.1-100 Angstrom /second under a                  certain degree of vacuum, and the deposition rate                  of metal tin heating and evaporation condition is                  0.1-100 Angstrom /second under a certain degree                  of vacuum, and during the deposition process, a                  crystal oscillator is used to monitor the                  thickness of the catalyst layer, where the                  thickness measuring instrument comprises a film                  thickness meter and an ellipsometer used to                  measure the thickness of the catalyst layer. The                  prepared bismuth-tin bimetallic material is used                  for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                  to produce at least one single carbon product,                  where the electrocatalytic process is carried out                  in alkaline electrolyte. Preferred Conditions:                  The deposition rate of metallic bismuth and                  metallic tin determines the ratio of bismuth to                  tin in the bismuth-tin bimetal material, and the                  composition ratio of bismuth-tin bimetal can be                  adjusted by adjusting the deposition rate.

PN CN111454326-A
TI Preparing balsam pear-peptide useful as cosmetic raw material and health food additive for preventing wrinkle, comprises e.g. removing balsam pear-seed, crushing, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction and freeze-drying.
AU PAN W
AE ZHEJIANG XITAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202075203J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing balsam pear-peptide comprises (1) removing the balsam pear-seeds and crushing to 20-60 mesh to obtain balsam pear-seed powder, (2) subjecting the balsam pear-seed powder to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain defatted balsam pear seed powder, (3) adding defatted balsam pear-seed powder to water 5-15 times the weight of defatted balsam pear-seed powder, and adjusting the pH to 9-10 with 0.05-0.15 mol/l sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring, extracting for 2-6 hours at 30-60 degrees C, centrifuging, separating the precipitate from supernatant to obtain the extract residue and extract respectively, and (4) ultra filtering extract with an ultrafiltration membrane with a retention molecule of 8000-15000 Da, and then freeze-drying to obtain balsam pear-peptide.
   USE - The balsam pear-peptide is useful for preventing wrinkle and cosmetic (claimed)(e.g. cosmetic raw material and a health food additive).
   ADVANTAGE - The balsam pear-peptide improves the economic benefits of anti-wrinkle repair balsam pear-peptide; and has anti-glycation effect. The method has simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for balsam pear-peptide for preventing wrinkle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) removing the balsam pear seeds and crushing them to 20-60 mesh to obtain balsam pear-seed powder, (2) subjecting the balsam pear powder to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain defatted balsam pear seed powder, (3) adding defatted balsam pear seed powder to water 5-15 times the weight of defatted balsam pear seed powder, and adjusting the pH to 9-10 with 0.05-0.15 mol/l sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring and extracting for 2-6 hours at 30-60 degrees C, centrifuging, and separating the precipitate from the supernatant to obtain the extract residue and the extract respectively, (4) adding the extract residue to water 5-15 times the weight of the extract residue, and adding 10-20% bromelain extract residue, adding an enzyme activator with 0.1-0.4% of the weight of the extracted residue, stirring at 40-60 degrees C for 9-16 hours, inactivating the enzyme, centrifuging, and discarding the precipitating to obtain the enzyme hydrolysate, and (5) mixing the extract and the enzymolysis solution uniformly to obtain the mixed solution, which is obtained by ultrafiltration with an ultrafiltration membrane with a retention molecule of 8000-15000 Da and freeze-drying. The conditions for the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction are that the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10-20 kg/hour, the extraction pressure is 30-40MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, and the extraction time is 1-5 hours. The rotation speed of the stirring is 100-500 revolutions/minute. The rotation speed of the centrifugation is 5000-8000r/min, and the time is 15-25 minutes. The freeze drying includes vacuum freeze drying, controlling the height of the material to be 3-8 mm, setting the pre-freezing temperature to -20 to -30 degrees C, when the sample temperature drops to the set temperature, maintaining for 1.5-2.5 hours, setting the sublimation temperature to 5-15 degrees C, the resolution temperature to 30-40 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 10-30 Pa, and the drying time is 15-25 hours. The temperature of the enzyme inactivation is 90-100 degrees C and the time is 7-12 minutes. Preferred Components: The enzyme activator is sodium L-aspartate and/or sodium L-ascorbate.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a power distribution cabinet with radiating function, comprising a power distribution cabinet body, air inlet mechanism and an exhaust mechanism; the air inlet mechanism comprises a Laval nozzle, and fan air supply port assembled in the Laval nozzle; exhausting mechanism comprises a sliding connection on the power distribution cabinet body top part of the exhaust fan; it further comprises a connecting pipe front end of the fan, the connecting pipe is connected with a carbon dioxide storage tank through an electromagnetic valve; it further comprises an alarm and a temperature control device are arranged in the power distribution cabinet main body; the air inlet mechanism and the exhaust mechanism and temperature control device of low-temperature conductive end is electrically connected with the alarm device and electromagnetic valve is electrically connected with the temperature control device of high temperature conductive end. In the embodiment of the invention, using the Laval nozzle inlet to obtain high flow gas into the power distribution cabinet, and the mixed gas is discharged from the exhaust, high ventilation efficiency, and flame temperature in the cabinet has, at the same time of warning by carbon dioxide fire-extinguishing and temperature-reducing.

AE UNIV ANTWERPEN (UANT-C)
   LUREDERRA CENT TECNOLOGICO (LURE-Non-standard)
   UNIV PADOVA (UNPA-C)
   LUREDERRA TECHNOLOGY CENT (LURE-Non-standard)
   UNIV PADOVA (UNPA-C)
GA 202036019U
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is produced by dissolving requisite                quantities of copper nitrate and nickel nitrate in                de-ionized water to provide a sub-0.3 molar aqueous                solution of copper nitrate and nickel nitrate;                providing ammoniacal solution by adding                concentrated aqueous solution of ammonia in a                quantity of 6-10 times the quantity required to                realize both a 1:6 molar ratio for Cu(II) to                ammonia and 1:6 molar ratio for Ni(II) to ammonia;                loading &#947; -alumina with 1-30 wt.% copper                and nickel in weight ratio of nickel to copper of                1:5-2:1 by suspending the requisite quantity of                &#947; -alumina in ammoniacal solution to                achieve the required loading of copper and nickel;                stirring the resulting &#947; -alumina                suspension for &#8805; 4 hours at room                temperature; and evaporating volatile components                under ambient conditions leaving dry loaded                &#947; -alumina, which is calcined at                &#8805; 260&#176; C for &#8805; 30                minutes with constant heating up rate.
   USE - Production of catalyst or catalyst mixture for                converting exhaust gasses from internal combustion                engine into carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Nickel(II) hydroxide is prevented during the                production process. Copper aluminate formation upon                calcination is prevented.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: Heating up rate is                  1&#176; C/minute. Stirring time is                  &#8805; 48 hours. Calcination is performed at                  &#8805; 550&#176; C. Dry loaded                  &#947; -alumina is calcined for at least 6                  hours. Preferred Components: Concentrations of                  copper and nickel nitrate in aqueous solution are                  &#8805; 0.005 molar, preferably &#8805;                  0.02 molar. Loading of copper is 2-25 wt.% or                  3-22 wt.% with respect to &#947; -alumina if                  completely deposited. Weight ratio of nickel to                  copper is 1:4-1.5:1, preferably 1:3-1.3:1.                  Preferred Process: Copper nitrate trihydrate                  (0.57 g) and nickel nitrate hexahydrate (0.22 g)                  are added to a vial containing 75 mL deionized                  water; concentrated aqueous solution of ammonia                  is added to solution with a volume equal to 6                  times that volume of ammonia required to obtain                  both a molar ratio of 1/6 for copper(II)/ammonia                  (NH3) and molar ratio of 1/6 for nickel(II)/NH3;                  suspending 1.5 g &#947; -alumina into the                  solution to achieve a copper (Cu)/nickel (Ni)                  loading on &#947; -alumina of 10 wt.% Cu and                  3 wt.% Ni if completely deposited and stirring                  suspension for 48 hours at room temperature; and                  evaporating volatile components under ambient                  conditions leaving dry loaded &#947;                  -alumina, and finally calcining at 550&#176;                  C for 6 hours. Copper nitrate trihydrate (0.57 g,                  preferably 1.14 g) and nickel nitrate hexahydrate                  (0.72 g, preferably 0.48 g) are added to vial                  containing 75 mL de-ionized water; adding                  concentrated aqueous solution to solution with a                  volume equal to 6 times that volume of ammonia                  required to obtain both a molar ratio of 1/6 for                  copper(II)/NH3and a molar ratio of 1/6                  nickel(II)/NH3; suspending 1.5 g &#947;                  -alumina into solution to achieve a Cu/Ni loading                  on &#947; -alumina of 10 wt.% Cu and 10 wt.%                  Ni if completely deposited and stirring                  suspension for 48 hours at room temperature;                  evaporating volatile components under ambient                  conditions leaving dry loaded &#947;                  -alumina, which is finally calcined at                  550&#176; C for 6 hours.

TI Imaging system for capturing and measuring point source plumes of atmospheric gasses such as, methane, carbon dioxide, comprises telescopic component having multiple reflective and refractive elements equipped within body.
AU THORPE A K
   GREEN R O
   MOUROULIS P
   THOMPSON D R
   AUBREY A D
   FRANKENBERG C
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
GA 202031792P
AB    NOVELTY - The imaging system (200) comprises a telescopic component (202) having multiple reflective and refractive elements equipped within a body of the telescopic component, such that the reflective and refractive elements receive light images from a desired location, where the light images are in the form of light rays transmitted through the telescopic component and where at least one of the refractive elements is a lens with a freeform surface. A spectrometer (204) is optically coupled to the telescopic component and has one or more spectral reflective elements that receive the light from the telescopic element.
   USE - Imaging system for capturing and measuring point source plumes of atmospheric gases such as, methane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and nitrous oxide emitted from industrial sources, such as oil and gas industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The imaging system maximizes the functionalities of high spectral and spatial resolution in order to accurately detect and discern point source gas plumes of various atmospheric gases. The overall functionality of increased spatial and spectral resolution for improved gas detection is maintained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for detecting atmospheric gases.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of a spectrum mapping resolution.
   Imaging system (200)
   Telescopic component (202)
   Spectrometer (204)
   Slit (206)
   Detector array (210)

PT P
PN CN110028671-A; CN110028671-B
TI Benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole polymer useful for gas adsorption, preferably carbon dioxide adsorption, prepared by protecting terephthalaldehyde by thioacetal, carrying out benzoin condensation reaction, and oxidizing.
AU CHEN H
   CHEN N
   LIU Y
   YANG M
   LI H
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201965127U
AB    NOVELTY - Benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole polymer, prepared by protecting terephthalaldehyde by thioacetal, carrying out benzoin condensation reaction, and oxidizing to obtain 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl, imidazole ring formation, butyl-substituted imidazolium, and de-protecting to obtain 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole, reacting 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole with 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetra-salt to obtain benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole polymer, is claimed.
   USE - The benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole polymer is useful for gas adsorption, preferably carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer is easy to prepare, has low-cost, good absorbing effect, and enhances gas adsorption capacity by expanding the specific surface area of the polymer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole polymer, comprising (i) subjecting silicon dioxide to activation treatment, soaking the activated silicon dioxide with sodium bisulfate to obtain silica catalyst loaded sodium bisulfate, (ii) protecting terephthalaldehyde by thioacetal, carry out benzoin condensation reaction, oxidizing to obtain 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl, imidazole ring formation, butyl-substituted imidazolium, and de-protecting to obtain 2,4,5-tri (4 -formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole, and (iii) dissolving 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole and 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetra salt in a solvent to carry out reaction to obtain benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, protecting terephthalaldehyde by thioacetal to react with terephthalaldehyde and mercaptan to obtain 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde, and/or the benzoin condensation reaction is a reaction of 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde with cyanate or vitamin B1 in alcohol solution to obtain 1,2-bis(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-2-hydroxyethanone. The oxidation is reaction of 1,2-bis(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-2-hydroxyethanone with oxidizing agent to obtain 4,4-di(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl, and/or cyclically reacting the imidazole with 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl and 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde to obtain 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1H-imidazole. The butyl-substituted imidazole hydrogen is reacting 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1H-imidazole with butyl-containing compound to obtain 2, 4, 5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole, and/or carrying out deprotection by reacting 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole with metal complexing agent to obtain 2, 4, 5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole, and/or the 1,2,4,5-benzenetetramine tetra hydrochloride is 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetra hydrochloride. In the method, the step (i) specifically comprises placing silicon dioxide in muffle furnace at 150-300 degrees C (preferably 160-280 degrees C, more preferably 180-240 degrees C) for 1-4 hours (preferably 1.2-3 hours, more preferably 1.5-2.5 hours), immersing the activated silicon dioxide in sodium bisulfate for 12-48 hours (preferably 18-36 hours, more preferably 20-32 hours), suction filtering, drying (preferably vacuum drying) to obtain silica catalyst loaded sodium bisulfate, and/or the volume concentration of sodium bisulfate is 5-30%, preferably 6-25%, more preferably 8-20%, and the mass ratio of activated silicon dioxide to sodium bisulfate is 1:2-20, preferably 1:4-18, more preferably 1:6-16. In the step (ii), the preparation of 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole specifically comprises (a) dissolving terephthalic acid in organic solvent (preferably ethyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran and/or methylene chloride), adding silica catalyst loaded sodium bisulfate obtained in the step (i), adding mercaptan (preferably methyl mercaptan, ethanethiol and/or ethanedithiol), stirring the reaction (preferably stirred at room temperature for reacting), separating (preferably by column chromatography, more preferably separating by column chromatography with a petroleum ether/ethyl acetate volume ratio of 10:1) to obtain product 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde, (b) dissolving 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde in solvent (preferably ethanol solution, more preferably 70-99% ethanol solution, most preferably 80-98% ethanol solution), adding cyanate or vitamin B1 (preferably sodium cyanide), heating (preferably heating to 50-75 degrees C, more preferably heating to 55-70 degrees C), carrying out the reaction, washing (preferably using deionization) water washing), separating (preferably by suction filtration), repeating washing of the solid (preferably washing twice with deionized water), re-crystallizing (preferably re-crystallizing with absolute ethyl alcohol) to obtain 1,2-bis(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-2-hydroxyethanone, (c) dissolving 1,2-bis(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-2-hydroxyethanone in solvent (preferably mixture of glacial acetic acid and/or water, more preferably mixed solution of glacial acetic acid and water in a volume ratio of 5-20:1), dissolving by heating (preferably heating to 80-160 degrees C, more preferably 100-140 degrees C), adding oxidizing agent (preferably ferric chloride), carrying out the reaction, washing (preferably washing with deionized water), separating (preferably by suction filtration), and separating by column chromatography to obtain 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl, (d) dissolving 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl and 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde in solvent (preferably ammonium acetate in a mixed solution of acetic acid), carrying out reaction (preferably, heating to 80-160 degrees C in an inert gas to carry out reaction, more preferably heating to 100-140 degrees C), washing (preferably washing with cold water) to obtain 2, 4, 5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1H-imidazole, (e) dissolving 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1H-imidazole and butyl-containing compound (preferably n-bromobutane) in solvent (preferably in a mixed solution of tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB), sodium hydroxide and butanone, heating the mixture under reflux to carry out a reaction to obtain 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole, (f) reacting 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole, metal complexing agent (preferably oxidized mercury and/or mercury nitrate), dissolving catalyst (preferably boron trifluoride and/or nitric acid) in a solvent (preferably mixed solution of tetrahydrofuran and water, more preferably, in a mixed solution of tetrahydrofuran/water in a volume ratio of 5-20:1, heating (preferably heating to 50-100 degrees C, further preferably 60-80 degrees C), carrying out the reaction, and separating (preferably by column chromatography) to obtain 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl))-1-butyl-imidazole. In the method, the step (iii) specifically comprises in an nitrogen atmosphere of -50 to -20 degrees C (preferably -40 to -25 degrees C), dissolving 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole in a solvent (preferably anhydrous dimethylformamide solution), adding 11,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetra salt (preferably dimethylformamide solution of 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetrahydrochloride), carrying out the reaction until no more solids is produced, continuously stirring for 6-48 hours (preferably stirring at room temperature for 8-24 hours), carrying out the reaction in an oxygen atmosphere at 80-180 degrees C for 1-12 days (preferably 2-6 days under oxygen atmosphere at 100-160 degrees C) to obtain benzimidazole-linked triphenylimidazole-containing polymer, where the mass concentration of 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole dissolved in the solvent is 1-10 g/l, preferably 2-8 g/l, the mass concentration of 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetrahydrochloride in the dimethylformamide solution of 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetrahydrochloride is 0.1-8 g/l, preferably 0.5-5 g/l, and the molar ratio of 2,4,5-tris(4-formylphenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole to 1,2,4,5-phenyltetramine tetrahydrochloride is 1:1-3, preferably 1:1.2-2. In the step (a), the concentration of terephthalaldehyde dissolved in organic solvent is 40-200 g/l, preferably 60-100 g/l, the mass ratio of silicon dioxide loaded sodium bisulfate to terephthalaldehyde is 1:2-20, preferably 1:3-10, the mass ratio of mercaptan to terephthalaldehyde is 1:1-10, preferably 1:2-8, in the step (b), the concentration of 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde dissolved in solvent is 50-500 g/l, preferably 80-400 g/l, the mass ratio of cyanate to 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde is 1:2-15, preferably 1:3-10, in the step (c), the mass concentration of 1,2-bis(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-2-hydroxyethanone dissolved in the solvent is 40-200 g/l, preferably 60-100g/l, the mass ratio of the oxidizing agent to 1,2-bis(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-2-hydroxyethanone is 1:0.5-5, preferably 1:0.8-2, in the step(d), the molar ratio of 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl to 4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)benzaldehyde is 1:0.6-2, preferably 1:0.8-1.5, the molar ratio of 4,4-bis(1,3-dithiopentyl)bibenzoyl to ammonium acetate is 1:5-20, preferably 1:8-15, in the step (e), the molar ratio of 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1H-imidazole to butyl-containing compound is 1:0.6-2, preferably 1:0.8-1.5, the mass concentration of 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1H-imidazole dissolved in solvent is 20-200 g/l, preferably 40-100 g/l, the volume ratio of sodium hydroxide and butanone is 1:1-3, preferably 1:1.2-2.5; the mass concentration of TBAB in the mixed solution is 1-20 g/l, preferably 30-15 g/l, and in the step (f), the mass ratio of ,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole to metal complexing agent is 1:0.8-4, preferably 1:1-3, the mass ratio of 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole to catalyst is 1:4-20, preferably 1:5-10, and the concentration of 2,4,5-tris(4-(1,3-dithiopentyl)phenyl)-1-butyl-imidazole dissolved in solvent is 10-100 g/l, preferably 12-80 g/l.
