PT P
PN EP3501298-A1; WO2019121797-A1
TI Producing puffed fibrillated cellulose-based material, involves combining fibrillated cellulose-containing material and extrusion aid, and puffing mixture by extruder to obtain puffed fibrillated cellulose-based extrudate.
AU BUDDE T
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 201952673B
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing puffed fibrillated cellulose-based material, involves (a) providing at least one fibrillated cellulose-containing material, (b) providing at least one extrusion aid, (c) combining the fibrillated cellulose-containing material provided in step (a) and the extrusion aid provided in step (b) to obtain a mixture, and (d) puffing the mixture obtained in step (c) by an extruder to obtain a puffed fibrillated cellulose-based extrudate, where the extrusion aid provided in step (b) is a surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of ground natural calcium carbonate (GNCC) or precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC) treated with carbon dioxide and one or more hydronium ion donors, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an external source.
   USE - The process is useful for producing puffed fibrillated cellulose-based material, which is used in food product for human consumption, preferably breakfast cereals and/or snacks, or food product for animal consumption, preferably pet food, and more preferably fish food, bird food, dog food and/or cat food, or a packaging material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The food product has an improved crispness, and mouthfeel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a puffed fibrillated cellulose-based material, which is obtainable according to the above-mentioned process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fibrillated cellulose-containing material provided in step (a) is chosen from micro fibrillated cellulose, nanofibrillated cellulose, nano-crystalline cellulose, fractionated cellulosic materials and their mixtures, preferably micro fibrillated cellulose, nanofibrillated cellulose and their mixtures, more preferably at least one fibrillated cellulose-containing material is nanofibrillated cellulose. The extrusion aid has (i) a volume median grain diameter d50 (vol) of 1-75 mu m, preferably 2.5-7 mu m, and/or (ii) a volume grain diameter d98 (vol) of 2-150 mu m, preferably 10-30 mu m. The extrusion aid has a specific surface area of 15-200 m2/g, preferably 48-140 m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method according to ISO 9277:1995. The mixture obtained in step (c) comprises 0.01-10 wt.%, preferably 0.2-1.8 wt.% extrusion aid. The mixture obtained in step (c) further comprises the following additives: (i) water in amount of 0.01-15 wt.%, preferably 0.2-5 wt.%, and/or (ii) whole grains in amount of 0.1-30 wt.%, preferably 1-15 wt.%, and/or (iii) sucrose in amount of 0.01-20 wt.%, preferably 0.2-5 wt.%, and/or (iv) sodium chloride in amount of 0.001-5 wt.%, preferably 0.1-1 wt.%. Preferred Biomolecule: The hydronium ion donors are chosen from (i) strong acids having pKa of 0 or less at 20 degrees C and/or (ii) medium-strong acids having pKa value of 0-2.5 at 20 degrees C, and/or (iii) weak acids having pKa of 2.5 or more and less than or equal to 7 at 20 degrees C, associated with the ionization of its first available hydrogen, where a corresponding anion is formed on loss of this first available hydrogen capable of forming a water-soluble calcium salt, where at least one water-soluble salt, which in the case of a hydrogen-containing salt has pKa of 7 or more at 20 degrees C, associated with the ionization of the first available hydrogen, and the salt anion of which is capable of forming water-insoluble calcium salts, is additionally provided. The natural calcium carbonate is chosen from marble, chalk, dolomite, limestone and their mixtures, and/or (ii) the precipitated calcium carbonate comprises aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic mineralogical crystal forms or their mixtures. Preferred Process: The surface-reacted calcium carbonate is obtained by (a) providing a suspension of GNCC or PCC, (b) adding at least one acid having pKa value of 0 or less at 20 degrees C, or having pKa value of 0-2.5 at 20 degrees C to the suspension provided in step (a), and (c) treating the suspension provided in step (a) with carbon dioxide before, during or after step (b). The surface-reacted calcium carbonate is obtained by (a) providing GNCC or PCC, (b) providing at least one water-soluble acid, (c) providing gaseous carbon dioxide, and (d) contacting the GNCC or PCC provided in step (a), at least one acid provided in step (b) and gaseous carbon dioxide provided in step (c), where (i) at least one acid provided in step (b) has pKa of 2.5 or more and less than or equal to 7 at 20 degrees C, associated with the ionization of its first available hydrogen, and a corresponding anion is formed on loss of this first available hydrogen capable of forming a water-soluble calcium salt, and (ii) following contacting at least one water-soluble acid provided in step (b) and the GNCC or PCC provided in step (a), at least one water-soluble salt, which in the case of a hydrogen-containing salt has a pKa of 7 or more at 20 degrees C, associated with the ionization of the first available hydrogen, and the salt anion of which is capable of forming water-insoluble calcium salts, is additionally provided. The mixture obtained in step (c) is heated to 100-150 degrees C, preferably 115-130 degrees C, and/or the extruder operates at a minimum pressure of 0.5 MPa, preferably 6 MPa, and/or the extruder operates at a maximum pressure of 10 MPa, preferably 5 MPa.

PN CN109628209-A
TI Preparing tea seed product by pulverizing tea seed meal, extracting powder with methanol, separating tea residue, drying tea residue, extracting, filtering extracting liquid, evaporating, concentrating, storing and adding isothiazolinones.
AU JIANG W
AE JIANG W (JIAN-Individual)
GA 2019385763
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing tea seed product, involves (i) pulverizing tea seed meal, extracting the powder with methanol using a extractor, and separating the tea residue using a disc centrifuge to obtain an extraction mixture, (ii) conveying the wet meal from the extractor through a wet meal scraper conveyor line, removing the solvent, passing to a condenser, recovering methanol by conveying to a methanol recovery turnover library, where the methanol recovery turnover library is connected with a methanol reservoir, and drying the slag by passing through a slag conveyor line into a dryer, (iii) filtering the extracting liquid twice after reaches to 5 Baume degrees, evaporating using a first long tube evaporator, passing to a second long tube evaporator when it is 8 Baume, evaporating until it reaches to 12 Baume degree, concentrating by sending to multiple sets of single-effect concentrators to 25-26 Baume degree, (iv) sending to a storage tank and adding 0.3% isothiazolinones.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tea seed product. The obtained waste material is used as bio-organic fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents flatulence caused by carbon dioxide production and fermentation of carbon dioxide by proteins and polysaccharides under the action of external bacteria, and is environmentally-friendly, and realizes resource of conservation and recycling process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves extracting using 90% methanol, heating and refluxing the mixture in a hot water bath at 50 degrees C for 3-4 hours. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the working pressure of the first long tube evaporator is controlled at 0.4-0.5 mPa, and the temperature of the extraction mixture is controlled at 90-100 degrees C. The working pressure of long tube evaporator is controlled at 0.3-0.4 MPa and the temperature of the extraction mixture is controlled at 85-95 degrees C. The working pressure of single-effect concentrators is 0.1-0.2 MPa, and the temperature of the extraction mixture is 80-90 degrees C. Preferred Device: The first long tube evaporator, second long tube evaporator, set of single effect concentrators, desolventizer and dryer are powered by a steam boiler, and the steam boiler generates steam for condensation through the condenser, the cooling tower and the circulating water tank used for circulating condensed water.

PT P
PN US2018355276-A1; US10465144-B2
TI Producing oil involves treating distillers corn oil having high free fatty acid content with mixture of monohydric alcohol and alkali and separating oil from low-free fatty acid oily phase having impurities e.g. waxes by membrane filtration.
AU DASARI M P A
   WARREN H T
   KNOX K J
   GALLOP C C
AE CORN OIL ONE LLC (CORN-Non-standard)
GA 2018992862
AB    NOVELTY - Producing oil comprises: treating distillers corn oil having a high free fatty acid content with a mixture comprising at least 15 wt.% of a monohydric alcohol i.e. a solvent and optionally an alkali to form a low-free fatty acid oily phase comprising oil and at least one impurity, and an alcohol phase; and separating the oil and the impurities using membrane filtration, where the impurities are free fatty acid soaps, waxes (both preferred) and/or unsaponifiables.
   USE - The method is useful for producing oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and highly effective in recovering free fatty acids while minimizing loss of valuable neutral oil (that is used for animal feed, industrial purposes including lubricants, biodiesel, polymers and paints, and potentially food) and emulsion formation; utilizes products and byproducts (e.g. corn oil, aqueous ethanol and carbon dioxide) of an ethanol production facility, and thus eliminates or reduces the release of carbon dioxide into the air, and is an environmentally friendly; and utilizes aqueous ethanol which provides cost savings as there is no need to process the aqueous ethanol prior to using same as a solvent, and effectively hydrates the phospholipids and separates them from the fats and oils.
TF EXAMPLE - Batch reactor was used to extract free fatty acids from distillers corn oil (DCO) containing 13.2% free fatty acids. Reaction was performed by adding 207.8 g of DCO (feedstock) to a 500 ml flask. The temperature of the corn oil was raised from ambient temperature to 65 degrees C. A solvent phase was then prepared by initially creating a solution of aqueous ethanol containing 40 wt.% ethanol, followed by addition of 3.9 g sodium hydroxide to 127.6 g of aqueous ethanol. In a separate flask, the solvent phase and alkali were mixed and heated from ambient temperature to 65 degrees C. The alkaline solvent was added to the feedstock and the mixture was then agitated for 1 minute, and then the mixture was allowed to separate, in a 65 degrees C environment, into two distinct phases. The top phase was collected and dried to yield 179.8 g of oil with free fatty acid content of 0.2%. 114.6 g Bottom solvent phase was collected into a separate beaker to which concentrated sulfuric acid was added until the pH of the mixture was 2. The mixture was then agitated for 1 minute, and then allowed to separate, in a 65 degrees C environment, into two distinct phases. The top phase was separated and dried to yield 27.3 g of fatty acids.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A; D10-B01

PN CN108117281-A
TI Production of magnesium oxide, involves crushing magnesite ore raw material, filtering, controlling particle size, calcining, microwave heating, heat-exchanging, cooling, adding to deionized water, reacting, and pouring into carbon dioxide.
AU LU C
   YIN Z
   SHI L
   YU Z
AE UNIV LIAONING SHIHUA (ULNS-C)
GA 201845502K
AB    NOVELTY - The production method of magnesium oxide involves (S1) crushing magnesite ore raw material by a jaw crusher, filtering, and controlling the particle size of magnesite ore raw material to less than 20 mm, (S2) placing the processed raw material in a calcining furnace, and microwave heating during calcining process using microwave energy at 700-800 degrees C for 1-2 hours, (S3) heat-exchanging the calcined raw material through a heat exchanger, cooling the raw material to room temperature, adding the raw material to deionized water, and reacting at 75-80 degrees C for 25-35, (S4) introducing the solution generated after digestion reaction into carbon dioxide, where the solid-liquid weight ratio of the solution is 3:10, (S5) filtering the carbonized solution, passing air at 100-120 degrees C, decomposing, and precipitating, (S6) drying and calcining the precipitate at 800-900 degrees C and gas pressure of 4-7 MPa, and (S7) removing impurities from calcined raw materials using activated carbon.
   USE - Production method of magnesium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of magnesium oxide with high purity and excellent energy-saving effect, improves the utilization efficiency and calcining efficiency of raw materials, and prevents the waste of resource, by economical and environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The microwave energy in the step (S2) has power of 150-250 kW, wavelength of 10-50 mm, and frequency of 500-800 MHz. The carbonization reaction temperature in the step (S4) is 28-32 degrees C. The drying temperature in the step (S6) is 120-130 degrees C. The temperature of removing impurities in the step (S7) is 18-22 degrees C and the timer is 80-100 minutes. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the raw material in the step (S3) and water is 1:12. The concentration of carbon dioxide is 30-40%.

PT P
PN CN107935540-A
TI Preparing pre-oxygen fiber composite silica            aerogel blanket comprises e.g. preparing silica aerogel            glue, forming aerogel filament aerogel, aging            pre-oxygen filament aerogels, surface modifying, drying            aerogel fiber aerogels, and testing.
AU TIAN G
   SONG D
   DENG L
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 201833774E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pre-oxygen fiber composite silica                aerogel blanket comprises (i) preparing silica                aerogel glue, (ii) forming aerogel filament                aerogel, (iii) aging pre-oxygen filament aerogels,                aging and surface modifying, (iv) drying aerogel                fiber aerogels, or drying and testing, where e.g.                method uses sol-gel method to prepare silica                aerogel glue, spray method causes the pre-oxidized                silk fiber blanket to saturate and absorb the                silica aerogel glue, gel under specific conditions,                aging in specific solvents, surface modification in                specific solutions, supercritical fluid carbon                dioxide drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pre-oxygen                fiber composite silica aerogel blanket.
   ADVANTAGE - The insulation blanket has excellent heat                insulation performance, mechanical performance,                heat-prevention and waterproof performance, simple                operation and controllable process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pre-oxygen fiber composite silica                aerogel blanket comprises (i) preparing silica                aerogel glue, (ii) forming aerogel filament                aerogel, (iii) aging pre-oxygen filament aerogels,                aging and surface modifying, (iv) drying aerogel                fiber aerogels, or drying and testing, where the                method uses sol-gel method to prepare silica                aerogel glue, spray method causes the pre-oxidized                silk fiber blanket to saturate and absorb the                silica aerogel glue, gel under specific conditions,                aging in specific solvents, surface modification in                specific solutions, supercritical fluid carbon                dioxide drying to obtain enhanced aerogel                insulation material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide fluid                  control temperature is 45-55 degrees C, the                  pressure is 10-18 MPa; and flow of the carbon                  dioxide fluid through the extraction kettle is                  1000-4000 Kg/h and the extraction time is 5-8                  hours. The aging is carried out by placing                  alcohol gel of the pre-oxygen silk fiber blanket                  in anhydrous ethanol solution soaked at 35-45                  degrees C for 10-24 hours; and surface                  modification is carried out by soaking alcohol                  gel of aged pre-oxygen fiber blanket aerogel in                  absolute ethanol solution with alkylating agent                  and then soaking it in a solution of 50-60                  degrees C for 8 hours. The pre-oxygen fiber                  carpet is made of pre-oxygen fiber material with                  diameter of 2-5 mu m, density of not less than                  200 Kg/m3, and uniform texture blanket. The                  forming of pre-oxygen filament aerogel is carried                  out by spraying silica aerogel glue solution on                  the pre-oxygen silk fiber blanket so that the                  pre-oxygen silk fiber blanket is saturated and                  allowing glue liquid to stand for 15-25 minutes                  to form pre-oxidized fiber alcoholic gel fiber                  blanket aerogels. The drying is performed after                  aging or after surface modification, when no                  surface modification is performed after aging,                  the drying is performed after aging, drying at                  this time by placing the aged pre-oxygen fiber                  blanket aerogel material in extraction kettle,                  injecting carbon dioxide fluid into the                  extraction kettle, adjusting the temperature and                  pressure in the extraction vessel to contact with                  the material in the supercritical state of the                  carbon dioxide fluid, dissolving solvent in the                  material in supercritical fluid and collecting                  solvent from the discharge port of the separation                  tank to obtain hydrophilic type pre-oxygen fiber                  composite silica aerogel blanket; and when the                  surface is modified after aging, performing                  drying after surface modification, placing                  surface-modified pre-oxygen fiber blanket aerogel                  material in extraction kettle, injecting carbon                  dioxide fluid into extraction kettle, adjusting                  temperature and pressure in the extraction vessel                  to contact with material in the supercritical                  state of the carbon dioxide fluid, dissolving                  solvent in the material in supercritical fluid,                  collecting solvent from the outlet of the                  separation kettle to obtain hydrophobic                  pre-oxygen fiber composite silica aerogel                  blanket. Preferred Components: The silica aerogel                  glue is composed of ethyl orthosilicate, 95%                  anhydrous ethanol, water and catalyst in a volume                  ratio of 10:40-80:1-5:0.1-1.5. The silica aerogel                  glue raw materials include silicon source                  precursor, hydrolysis catalyst, additive and                  solvent; silicon source precursor includes                  tetraethylorthosilicate, methyl orthosilicate                  and/or trimethylchlorosilane; hydrolysis catalyst                  includes pure water, hydrochloric acid, nitric                  acid, sulfuric acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid,                  aqueous ammonia, ammonium carbonate and/or                  ammonium bicarbonate; and solvent includes                  methanol, ethanol, butanol, and linear alcohol                  having mass fraction of greater than or equal to                  95%.

PN CN107873456-A
TI Cultivating soybean seedling by foliar spraying mixed aqueous solution comprising magnesium salt and iron salt on soybean seedlings, carrying out shading treatment and illuminating soybean seedlings.
AU HU B
AE BENGBU NONGXING MODERN AGRIC SPECIALIZED (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201828157M
AB    NOVELTY - Method of cultivating soybean seedling involves foliar spraying mixed aqueous solution comprising magnesium salt and iron salt on the soybean seedlings, carrying out shading treatment and illuminating the soybean seedlings. The shading treatment involves covering the soybean seedlings using a light-shielding film.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating soybean seedling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes growth and increases growth rate of soybean seedlings, shortens the harvest cycle of soybeans and improves market value and economic efficiency of the soybeans.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of cultivating soybean seedling involves foliar spraying mixed aqueous solution comprising magnesium salt and iron salt on the soybean seedlings once in every 3-5 days at 7 o'clock in the morning when the soybean seedlings grow to a height of 15-20 cm, carrying out shading treatment every morning for 50-80 minutes at 26-28 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 0.0351-0.0359% in the soybean seedling growth environment, then carrying out shading treatment every afternoon for 40-60 minutes at 28-30 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 0.0452-0.0463% in soybean seedling growth environment and illuminating the soybean seedlings with light intensity of 30-35% normal light intensity. The shading treatment involves covering the soybean seedlings using a light-shielding film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The mixed aqueous solution comprises 1.235 mol/l magnesium salt and 0.283 mol/l iron salt. The iron salt is iron chloride, iron sulfate, iron nitrate or their combinations. The magnesium salt is magnesium chloride, magnesium nitrate, magnesium sulfate or their combinations. The light-shielding film is a polyimide film, where the polyimide film comprises 3.5 %mass aluminum powder. The aluminum powder has a particle size of 800 mesh. Preferred Method: The method involves carrying out shading treatment every morning at 9:00 hours and every afternoon at 15:00 hours, where the normal light intensity is 40000 lx.

PN CN107695080-A
TI Method for processing waste circuit board or carbon fiber composite material, involves heating water using water molecular heat energy generator to produce steam and heating steam continuously to produce gaseous water molecules.
AU WEI Y
   LIU Y
   CHEN W
   ZHENG Y
AE GUANGZHOU BAOSHI WIRELESS POWER SUPPLY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201815522M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating water up to 100 degrees using water molecule heat energy generator to produce steam. Steam is heated continuously up to more than 400 degrees to generate gaseous water molecules. Gas explosion is established to generate and output continuously high temperature, high enthalpy gaseous water molecules. The molecules decompose the waste circuit board or composite material in a sealed space to generate carbon black, water, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. Metal, carbon black and or carbon fibers are collected. The gases, water and molecules are purified for exhaust.
   USE - Method for processing waste circuit board or carbon fiber composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not rely on electrolysis and chemicals, avoiding secondary pollution to the environment. The generated water and carbon dioxide exhausted are purified so as to be harmless to humans. The method takes up less time, reducing recovery costs. High value metals such as copper and carbon black or fibers can be recovered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for processing waste circuit board or carbon fiber composite material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart of a method for processing waste circuit board or carbon fiber composite material. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Producing kvass involves mixing rye malt, barley malt, rye flour, sugar, concentrate of leavened wort and drinking water under heating to obtain mixture, which is added with drinking water, lactic acid and sourdough, and then fermented.
AU KVASENKOV O I
AE KVASENKOV O I (KVAS-Individual)
GA 201808967T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing kvass involves mixing rye malt, barley malt, rye flour, sugar, concentrate of leavened wort and drinking water under heating to obtain mixture. The obtained mixture is added with drinking water, lactic acid and sourdough, and then fermented to obtain fermented material. The fermented material is added with miscella and fennel extracted with liquid carbon dioxide, and then bottled to the separated liquid phase. The obtained material is dried in the microwave to a residual moisture of 20%, and added with cut oat root at 80-90 degrees C for 1 hour.
   USE - Method for producing kvass (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing kvass involves mixing rye malt, barley malt, rye flour, sugar, concentrate of leavened wort and drinking water under heating to obtain mixture. The obtained mixture is added with drinking water, lactic acid and sourdough, and then fermented to obtain fermented material. The fermented material is added with miscella and fennel extracted with liquid carbon dioxide, and then bottled to the separated liquid phase. The obtained material is dried in the microwave to a residual moisture of 20%, and added with cut oat root at 80-90 degrees C for 1 hour. The separated miscella is impregnated with a simultaneous increase in pressure.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable examples given.

PN CN114133330-A
TI Preparing methyl ethyl carbonate by using pipeline continuous reactor, involves adding carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol into pipeline reactor, and carrying out continuous reaction in the presence of catalyst.
AU ZHANG Y
   PENG L
   HE R
AE CHONGQING WEIERYI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202238907L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methyl ethyl carbonate by using a pipeline continuous reactor, involves adding carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol into a pipeline reactor, and carrying out continuous reaction in the presence of a catalyst to obtain a reaction mixture, where catalyst is aluminum oxide, zirconium phosphate, cerium oxide and nickel, the mass ratio of the methanol and ethanol is 0.9-1.2:1.1-1.5, and the mass ratio of the carbon dioxide and total amount of the methanol and the ethanol is 3-5:2-3. The reaction mixture is added into a rectifying tower for rectification, and separated out a product of methyl ethyl carbonate and a byproduct of diethyl carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing methyl ethyl carbonate by using pipeline continuous reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The comprehensive selectivity of ethyl methyl carbonate in method by using pipeline continuous reactor, is as high as 80-90 %, and the obtained ethyl methyl carbonate reaches electron-level purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The catalyst comprises aluminum oxide, zirconium phosphate, cerium oxide, and nickel in ratio of 0.4-0.6: 0.2-0.3: 0.15-0.2: 0.1-0.2. Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the pipeline is 3-5 MPa, and the reaction temperature is 90-150degrees Celsius.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The unreacted carbon dioxide comes out of the pipeline reactor after being purified and returned to the pipeline reactor to continue to participate in the reaction. The rectifying process includes inputting the reaction mixture into a first rectifying tower for rectification, inputting the tower bottom extract of the first rectifying tower into a second rectifying tower for rectification, where tower top distillate is methyl ethyl carbonate, and the tower bottom extract stream is diethyl carbonate. The extracted material is inputted at the top of the first rectifying tower into a third rectifying tower for rectification. The extracted material at the top of the tower comprises dimethyl carbonate, methanol, ethanol and water, and the extracted material at the bottom of the tower comprises methyl ethyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate and water. The components are extracted from the top of the third rectifying tower return to the pipeline continuous reactor to continuously participate in the reaction. Preferred Conditions: The first rectifying tower has 40-60 plates, the tower top temperature is 72-76degrees Celsius, the tower kettle temperature is 108-112degrees Celsius, and the reflux ratio is R3-6. The number of tower plates of the second rectifying tower is 50-70, the tower top temperature is 106.9degrees Celsius, the tower kettle temperature is 124-130degrees Celsius, and the reflux ratio is R4-6. The number of tower plates of the third rectifying tower is 30-50, the tower top temperature is 68-72degrees Celsius, the tower kettle temperature is 96-100 degrees Celsius, and the reflux ratio is R2-5. The first rectifying tower has 50 plates, the tower top temperature is 74plus minus1degrees Celsius, the tower bottom temperature is 109plus minus1degrees Celsius, and the reflux ratio R is 4. The number of the tower plates of the second rectifying tower is 60, the tower top temperature is 106plus minus1 degrees Celsius, the tower bottom temperature is 127plus minus1degrees Celsius, and the reflux ratio R is 5. The third rectifying tower has 40 plates, the tower top temperature is 70plus minus1degrees Celsius, the tower kettle temperature is 98plus minus1degrees Celsius, and the reflux ratio R is 3.

   CHENG G
   ZHAO H
   GUO Z
   FU K
   YANG S
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021A7731S
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic ceramic filler loaded with composite                metal oxides comprises a ceramic filler and a                catalytically active component attached to the                ceramic filler, and the catalytically active                component is a composite oxide solid base                AmOn-BxOy.
   USE - The catalytic ceramic filler is used in                absorption towers for chemical absorption of carbon                dioxide by organic amines (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic ceramic filler improves the                absorption speed and capacity of carbon dioxide,                and solves various problems caused by the                arrangement of catalysts and fillers in the                absorption tower.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the catalytic ceramic filler loaded with                composite metal oxide, comprising (a) pre-treating                the ceramic filler, (b) adding the AmOn precursor                and BxOy precursor into de-ionized water, and                stirring uniformly to obtain the dipping solution,                and (c) immersing the ceramic filler in the dipping                solution, performing ultrasonic dipping, drying and                constant-temperature calcining, and naturally                cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ceramic filler is                  Pilbauer ring, Raschig ring, ceramic ball or                  corrugated plate. The AmOn is magnesium oxide,                  calcium oxide or barium oxide. The BxOy is                  aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, gallium                  trioxide, cerium dioxide or lanthanum oxide. The                  mass ratio of the AmOn precursor to the BxOy                  precursor in the dipping solution is 0.2-5.                  Preferred Method: The specific operation of step                  (a) includes removing the surface impurity of the                  ceramic filler, using 50% oxalic acid solution to                  wash the ceramic filler, condensing and refluxing                  and boiling for 1 hour, cooling, using de-ionized                  water to wash until the pH of the washing liquid                  is 7, and drying at 110 degrees C for 12 hours.                  The specific operation of step (c) comprises                  immersing the ceramic filler in the dipping                  solution, performing ultrasonic dipping, drying                  for 2-4 times, where the temperature of the                  ultrasonic dipping is 35 degrees C, the                  ultrasonic dipping time is 1-3 hours, the                  temperature in the drying process is 110 degrees                  C, the drying time is 12 hours, heating to                  500-700 degrees C at the heating speed of 5                  degrees C/minute under the inert gas atmosphere,                  calcining at constant temperature for 2-4 hours,                  at last, naturally cooling to room temperature                  under the inert gas atmosphere.

A transistor                power amplifier circuit is connected with the radio                frequency power supply, an alarm protection control                circuit and a motor control circuit. The upper                computer transmits signal data to client according                to abnormal data information tracing circuit                fault.
   USE - Used as Carbon dioxide (CO2) laser smart                development and control system based on radio                frequency power supply.

PN WO2021110691-A1; CN114729114-A; KR2022111266-A; EP4069762-A1
TI Producing polyether carbonate polyol, comprises            e.g. reacting alkylene oxide with carbon dioxide in            presence of hydroxy-functional starter compound and            double metal cyanide catalyst, and placing            hydroxy-functional starter substance.
AU BRAUN S
   HOFMANN J
   TRAVING M
   WOHAK M
   TRAVEN M
   HOFFMANN J
AE COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 202162960F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyether carbonate polyol,                comprises reacting alkylene oxide with carbon                dioxide in presence of hydroxy-functional starter                compound and double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst,                placing partial amount of hydroxy-functional                starter substance and/or suspending agent which                does not contain any hydroxy-functional groups in a                reactor together with DMC catalyst, adding a                portion of alkylene oxide into the mixture at                90-150 degrees C, interrupting the addition of the                alkylene oxide, alkylene oxide, carbon dioxide and                a mixture containing non-activated DMC catalyst and                hydroxy-functional starter compound, the                hydroxy-functional starter compound being a polyol                with number average molar mass according to DIN                55672-1 of 550-2000 g/mol, continuously to the from                resulting mixture is dosed.
   USE - The method is useful for producing polyether                carbonate polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method leads to shorter residence times,                and reduces reactor volume and lower investment                costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - Preferred Components: The polyol in the                  mixture has number-average molar mass according                  to DIN 55672-1 of 600-1500 g/mol. The polyol in                  the mixture includes polyether polyol, polyester                  polyol, polyester ether polyol, polycarbonate                  polyol, polyether carbonate polyol and                  polyacrylate polyol. The hydroxy-functional                  starter compound in the mixture containing                  non-activated double metal cyanide catalyst and                  hydroxy-functional starter compound is polyether                  polyol with molecular weight according to DIN                  55672-1 of 550-2000 g/mol with a functionality of                  2-3 and polyether carbonate polyol with a                  molecular weight of 550-2000 g/mol with a                  functionality of 2-3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction product                  is continuously withdrawn from the reactor. The                  mean residence time of the reaction mixture is                  0.5-5.0 hours or 1.5-5. The mixture containing                  non-activated DMC catalyst and hydroxy-functional                  starter compound contains less than or equal to                  500 ppm activated DMC catalyst, based on the                  hydroxy-functional starter compound. The                  proportion of non-activated DMC catalyst in the                  mixture containing non-activated DMC catalyst and                  hydroxy-functional starter compound is 50-97                  wt.%, based on the sum of the mass of the                  non-activated and activated DMC catalyst in the                  mixture. A component K is added so that the                  reaction product resulting from step contains a                  component K in a proportion of 5-2000 ppm. The                  component K includes phosphoric acid, phosphonic                  acid, phosphorous acid, phosphinic acid,                  phosphonous acid, phosphinous acid, phosphine                  oxides and salts, esters, halides and amides of                  phosphoric acid, phosphonic acid, phosphorous                  acid acid, phosphinic acid, phosphonous acid,                  phosphinous acid, phosphorus sulfide, phosphorus                  tribromide, phosphorus trichloride and phosphorus                  triiodide. The double metal cyanide catalyst in                  is present in a proportion of 10-10000 ppm based                  on the entire reaction mixture. The alkylene                  oxide used is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide or                  a mixture of propylene oxide and ethylene oxide.                  The double metal cyanide catalyst includes zinc                  hexacyanocobaltate (III), zinc hexacyanoiridate                  (III), zinc hexacyanoferrate (III) and cobalt                  (II) hexacyanocobaltate (III).

PN CN112295604-A; CN112295604-B
TI Preparing metal organic framework nanosheet comprises mixing and stirring N,N-dimethylformamide, absolute ethanol, water, organic ligands, soluble copper salts and triethylamine in protective atmosphere, and reacting.
AU SUN Y
   ZU X
   XIE Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202116354M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal organic framework nanosheet comprises mixing and stirring N,N-dimethylformamide, absolute ethanol, water, organic ligands, soluble copper salts and triethylamine in protective atmosphere, and performing the reaction under ultrasonic conditions to obtain final product.
   USE - The metal-organic framework nanosheet is useful as photocatalyst in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; does not require a complicated environment; can be prepared at normal temperature and normal pressure; ensures the prepared metal organic framework nanosheet is atomic level thickness; and generates many uncoordinated surface atoms with more reactant adsorption activity sites, which is conducive to the photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, has high efficiency and high stability as carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for metal organic framework nanosheet prepared by above method. The metal organic framework nanosheet has thickness of 0.5-1.5 unit cell layers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio between N,N-dimethylformamide, absolute ethanol, water and triethylamine is 30-40:1-3:0.6-1:0.6-1, preferably 35:2:0.8:0.8. The organic ligand is terephthalic acid or 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid. The soluble copper salt is copper nitrate, copper chloride or copper acetate. The concentration of the soluble copper salt in the solution after mixing is 4-8 mol/l. Preferred Method: The mixing time is 3-5 hours. The reaction time is 6-10 hours; the power of the ultrasonic condition is 100-200 W. The method comprises centrifuging and washing to obtain metal organic framework nanosheets after the reaction; and the speed of the centrifugation is 10000-20000 revolutions/minute. The method comprises mixing N,N-dimethylformamide, anhydrous ethanol and water in a protective atmosphere, adding organic ligands for dissolution, adding soluble copper salts, stirring until clear, adding triethylamine, mixing and stirring, and reacting under ultrasonic conditions to obtain metal organic framework nanosheets. The mixing and stirring process are performed first stirring at 200-400 revolutions/minute for 0.2-1 hours, and stirring at 50-150 revolutions/minute for 2-4.5 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Compound (I) is prepared                  by adding palladium metal precursor and                  diphosphine ligand into organic solvent under                  protection of nitrogen, reacting for 30 minutes                  at room temperature, adding compounds (II) and                  (III) under the protection of nitrogen, stirring,                  reacting for 5-30 hours at 40-100 degrees C, and                  separating product. The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, toluene,                  1,4-dioxane, and/or dichloromethane.

AE YAN W (YANW-Individual)
GA 2020878054
AB    NOVELTY - An organometallic complex (I) or (II) having a stable structure, is new.
   USE - New organometallic complex is used as catalyst in carbonylation and esterification of alkyne (claimed) in production of high value-added alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid ester.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An organometallic complex of formula (I) or (II) having a stable structure, is new.
   M = Ru, Co, Ir, Ni, Cu, Mn, Zn, or Fe;
   L = group of formula (i) or (ii);and
   R3-R6 = H, 1-6C alkyl, methoxy, or trifluoromethyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbonylation and esterification of alkyne with carbon dioxide, which involves performing esterification reaction of olefin of formula (III) with carbon dioxide and alcohol of formula: ROH (IV) in the presence of the organometallic complex as a catalyst to obtain carboxyl group-containing compound of formula (V) or (VI).

PN CN111187194-A
TI Extracting astaxanthin by drying Haematococcus pluvialis, crushing, soaking in water, extracting soaked material in extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, separating to obtain astaxanthin, and purifying.
AU WANG J
AE NANJING MENGYUAN KANGDE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202048682D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting astaxanthin, involves (a) drying Haematococcus pluvialis, crushing in a crusher, soaking in water at a material-liquid ratio of 1:(5-50) for 24 hours, (b) extracting the soaked material to the ultra-high pressure extractor by sealing at room temperature and a pressure of 30-50 MPa and relieving the pressure instantly, (c) extracting the processed material in an extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extractor at 25-35 degrees C and a pressure of 20-30 MPa for 2 hours, introducing carbon dioxide extraction agent at a flow rate of 15-25 kg/hour and separating to obtain astaxanthin from the separation kettle, and (d) purifying the obtained astaxanthin with ether and removing the residual water to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting astaxanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, provides product with sufficient color and fragrance retention property, generates low pesticide residue, and has no pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves extracting at a pressure of 30-50 MPa. The step (c) involves extracting at 25-35 degrees C and a pressure of 20-30 MPa. The step (d) involves purifying with ether and water for 3 times in a volume ratio of (3:1), and concentrating under reduced pressure to recover the ether.

GA 202047737B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Kaempferia galanga extract, involves (i) crushing elecampane into 50-80 mesh, adding to a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, setting temperature at 50-60 degrees C and pressure at 15-30 MPa, introducing liquid carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 2-3 ml/minute/g raw material, while introducing methanol as an entrainer at a flow rate of 0.1-0.4 ml/minute/g, extracting for 60-100 minutes and analyzing to obtain an extract, and (ii) dissolving the extract with an ethanol solution, adding active carbon, refluxing and decolorizing, concentrating the decolorized solution, extracting with ethyl acetate, concentrating the extraction liquid, crystallizing, dissolving the crystals with ethanol, adopting high-speed counter-current chromatography separation, collecting fraction according to the chromatography, concentrating under reduced pressure and drying to obtain the product.
   USE - Method for preparing K.galanga extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient, adopts simple preparation process, has less pollution, and is easy to realize industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high-speed counter-current chromatography step involves mixing acetonitrile, methanol and disodium hydrogen phosphate solution in a ratio of (3-5):(1-4):(3-7) at a pH of 6.5 as a solvent system, and taking the upper phase as the stationary phase, and the lower phase as the mobile phase.

AE SEO J H (SEOJ-Individual)
GA 202013988E
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of metal thin film involves preparing a polymer substrate comprising 100 pts. wt. polymers, and 1-60 pts. wt. materials chosen from glass fibers and carbon fibers, heating the polymer substrate at a temperature of 80-150 degrees C to improve adhesion with respect to a conductive metal organic deposition, applying the conductive metal organic deposition ink composition to the polymer substrate, heat-treating to obtain a metal thin film, laser sintering using carbon dioxide laser having a wavelength of 9000-11000 nm to reduce metal ions contained in the composition to metal, and removing the composition remaining in a region not irradiated with carbon dioxide laser by washing to obtain a metal thin film pattern.
   USE - Manufacture of metal thin film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of metal thin film having high reliability, by increasing adhesiveness between the polymer substrate and conductive metal organic deposition ink composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Process: The ink composition is formed by mixing a metal formate-amine ink comprising an amine group as a ligand, and a metal formate formed by reacting metal precursor(s) chosen from copper precursor and nickel precursor with formic acid, and a silver-amine ink comprising an amine group as a ligand, and silver precursor(s) chosen from silver oxide and silver nitrate. Preferred Composition: The ink composition comprises 1-50 wt.% metal nanoparticles chosen from copper nanoparticles and silver nanoparticles, 1-50 wt.% silver nanowires, and substance(s) comprising graphene and/or carbon nanotubes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer is polyimide, polyamide, polycarbonate, polyethylene terephthalate, polyethylene naphthalate, polypropylene, nylon, polytetrafluoroethylene, acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene copolymer, polybutylene terephthalate, polyethylene terephthalate, polyvinyl chloride, polyphthalamide, polyphenylene sulfide, polyester, liquid crystal polymer, and/or cellulose. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide laser is carbon dioxide laser having wavelength of 9000-11000 nm and a pulse duration of less than 1 msecond.

PN CN110616134-A
TI Alcohol-free IPA beer has alcohol content, bitter taste, raw wort concentration, carbon dioxide content, total alcohol, total hop aroma.
AU YIN H
   DONG J
   XING L
   CHANG Z
   HU X
   QIAN Z
   HUANG S
   LIU X
   ZHANG W
   YUE J
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 2020035459
AB    NOVELTY - 0.0 wt.% vol alcohol-free IPA beer has an alcohol content of less than 0.05 wt.% vol, a bitter taste of 30EBC-60EBC, a raw wort concentration of 5.5-7.5 degrees P, a carbon dioxide content of 0.50-0.60 wt.%, total alcohol of greater than or equal to 15mg per litre, ester of greater than or equal to 5 milligram per litre, total hop aroma of greater than or equal to 50 microgram per litre.
   USE - 0.0 wt.% vol alcohol-free IPA beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The 0.0 wt.% vol alcohol-free IPA beer is capable ofsolving the problems of current non-alcohol beerlacking alcohol ester flavor of traditional beer withlight taste and obvious acidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing IPA beer, which involves sequentially preheating, degassing, dealcoholizing, cooling, carbon dioxide filling, post-modifying, stabilizing, and filling steps to obtain a 0.0 wt.% vol alcohol-free IPA beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The preparation method involves degassing the IPA beer preheated to 40-43 degrees C by vacuum, the vacuum degree is 80-90 millibar, until the carbon dioxide in the IPA beer is completely discharged. The degassed IPA beer is vacuum distilled at 40-43 degrees C, and the vacuum degree is 80-90 millibar, until the ethanol content falls below 0.05 wt.% vol. The post-modifying includes adding natural beer flavor, natural hop oil and natural sweet taste ingredients to the dealcoholized IPA beer. Preferred Compositions: The added amount of the natural beer flavor is 0.05-0.2 wt.%, preferably 0.1 wt.%. The added amount of natural hop oil is 0.005-0.015 wt.%, preferably 0.01 wt.%. The natural hop oil is selected from alcohol-free natural products containing linalool, terpineol, citronellol, and the like, and the natural sweetness component is selected from fructose syrup, maltose syrup, sucrose syrup, glucose syrup and caster sugar. The preparation method further involves mixing aluminum malt and coke fragrant malt and pulverizing, then mixing with a material-to-water ratio of 1:3-1:4, and performing saccharification after 50-55 degrees C protein rest. The mashed mash is filtered at 73 plus minus 1 degrees C to obtain a clear wort and boiled, and hops or hop products were added during the boiling process. The cyclic precipitation after boiling, hops or hop products are added during the cyclotron precipitation process and then cooled to 16-20 degrees C, and at the same time oxygenated and connected to the above yeast for fermentation. During the fermentation process, hops or hop products are added or not to obtain a beer fermentation broth.    USE - The method is useful for processing energy                conversion and storage based on electric gas                generation technology.

PN CN109292757-A
TI Preparation of graphene involves placing mixture of magnesium metal and magnesium oxide in container, heating, introducing carbon dioxide gas to container, and cooling to obtain black powder, adding with acid and reacting.
AU CHEN J
   GAO H
   ZHANG W
   YU R
   YANG Y
AE ZHANGJIAGANG BOWEI NEW ENERGY INST CO (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201914707K
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of graphene involves placing mixture of magnesium metal and magnesium oxide in container, heating the container at 400-900 degrees C and rate of 1-15 degrees C/minute under protection of argon atmosphere, introducing carbon dioxide gas to container after the temperature reaches the highest value, heat preserving for 10-180 minutes, stop introducing carbon dioxide gas under protection of argon atmosphere, cooling the container to room temperature to obtain black powder, adding appropriate amount of acid to black powder, reacting, washing with deionized water to neutral, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of graphene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of graphene with specific surface area, high electrical conductivity and high energy saving effect using wide source of raw materials by simple, economical, environmentally-friendly and industrial process, and achieves low processing equipment requirement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium oxide is porous magnesium oxide. The porous magnesium oxide is magnesium carbonate trihydrate. The acid added to the black powder is 1-3 mol/l hydrochloric acid. Preferred Process: The graphene is prepared by mixing magnesium carbonate trihydrate with 1 mol/l sodium carbonate solution and 1 mol/l magnesium chloride solution in mass ratio of 1:1, washing with ethanol for 3-5 times, and drying in a vacuum environment at 40-50 degrees C. The mixture of magnesium and magnesium oxide is prepared by grinding magnesium metal and magnesium oxide in mass ratio of 1:8 in a mortar for 10-40 minutes. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of argon gas is 250 ml/minute. The heating temperature is 500 degrees C and rate is 10 degrees C/minute and heat preserving time is 1 hour. The flow rate of argon gas is 60-120 ml/minute. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 100-140 ml/minute and flow rate of argon gas is reduced to 40-60 ml/minute after the temperature reaches the highest value. The drying temperature is 25-40 degrees C.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

TI Increasing absorbance of pepper red pigment by mixing capsanthin with phosphoric acid solution, adding n-hexane, extracting, evaporating, vacuum-concentrating, distilling, adding anhydrous ethanol and supercritical carbon dioxide extracting.
AU SHI X
AE HANDAN MEIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAND-Non-standard)
GA 201848102D
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing absorbance of pepper red pigment comprises placing capsanthin into transparent glass extraction tank, mixing with 1 pt. wt. 5% phosphoric acid solution, water bath heating at 40-45 degrees C, and stirring uniformly; stabilizing, adding 2-3 pts. wt. n-hexane, stirring, extracting, standing at room temperature, pumping upper n-hexane phase liquid into evaporation tank, vacuum-concentrating mixture, distilling without solvent, and obtaining initial solution of pepper red pigment; and introducing into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank, adding anhydrous ethanol as entrainer, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain red pepper. The pigment and yellowish pepper red pigment are vacuum concentrated to obtain high absorbance pepper red. The yellowish red pepper pigment is vacuum concentrated to obtain common red pepper pigment.
   USE - Method for increasing absorbance of pepper red pigment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves quality of paprika oleoresin light absorption ratio.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 25-28 MPa and 40-45 degrees C for 1-2 hours.

PT P
PN WO2018075123-A1; US2019315624-A1; US10995000-B2
TI Making many carbon nanotubes which are used in aircraft and spacecraft materials comprises applying current across catalytically passive anode and catalytic cathode, at specified current density, thus forming carbon nanotubes on the cathode.
AU DOUGLAS A
   PINT C
AE UNIV VANDERBILT (UVAN-C)
   UNIV VANDERBILT (UVAN-C)
GA 201832153K
AB    NOVELTY - Making many carbon nanotubes comprises applying a current across a catalytically passive anode and a catalytic cathode, at a current density of 25-500 mA/cm2, thus forming many carbon nanotubes on the catalytic cathode. The catalytic cathode comprises a catalyst which comprises iron, cobalt, molybdenum and/or copper, and the catalytically passive anode and the catalytic cathode are in electrochemical contact with a molten carbonate electrolyte and a source of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for making many carbon nanotubes (claimed) which are useful in aircraft and spacecraft materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides carbon nanotubes at lower cost as compared to conventional method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a device comprising the catalytically passive anode, the catalytic cathode, the molten carbonate electrolyte and the source of carbon dioxide; and
   (2) use of the device to capture carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The current is applied for 1 minute to 10 hours, preferably 1 minute to 1 hour. The current density is greater than or equal to 25 mA/cm2, preferably 25-100 mA/cm2. The method further comprises: forming the catalytically passive anode by depositing the passivating layer on the conductive substrate; heating a metal carbonate to produce the molten carbonate electrolyte; collecting the carbon nanotubes from the catalytic cathode; washing the collected many carbon nanotubes; and drying the collected many carbon nanotubes. The passivating layer is deposited on the conductive substrate by atomic layer deposition. The metal carbonate is heated at 400-850 degrees C, preferably 725-775 degrees C. The source of carbon dioxide is provided by injecting the carbon dioxide source into the molten carbonate electrolyte. The step of collecting the carbon nanotubes from the catalytic cathode comprises: sonicating the catalytic cathode to separate the carbon nanotubes from the catalytic cathode by dispersing the carbon nanotubes into a solvent and centrifuging or filtering the solvent with the carbon nanotubes to thus collect the carbon nanotubes; and mechanically scraping the catalytic cathode to separate the carbon nanotubes from the catalytic cathode. The collected many carbon nanotubes are washed with an acid and/or water. The step of drying the collected many carbon nanotubes comprises heating the collected many carbon nanotubes at 60-200 degrees C for 30 minutes to 24 hours. Preferred Components: The conductive substrate comprises a metal, a metal oxide and/or a carbon material. The passivating layer comprises alumina (preferred), titanium dioxide, magnesium oxide, titanium nitride and/or vanadium nitride. The passivating layer has a thickness of 2-100 nm, preferably 45-55 nm. The catalytically passive anode comprises: a metal, a metal oxide and/or a carbon material, with the proviso that the catalytically passive anode does not comprise nickel; or a conductive substrate coated with a passivating layer, where the conductive substrate comprises nickel. The catalytic cathode comprises: a metal, a metal oxide and/or a carbon material; or preferably galvanized steel, 1010 steel and/or stainless steel. The catalytic cathode comprises: a conductive substrate and a catalytic layer deposited on the conductive substrate, where the catalytic layer comprises the catalyst; or a conductive substrate and many catalytic particles deposited on the conductive substrate, where the catalytic particles comprise the catalyst. The catalytic layer has a thickness of 0.1-25 nm, preferably 0.5-5 nm. The catalytic cathode further comprises a passivating layer, where the passivating layer is disposed between the conductive substrate and the catalytic layer or catalytic particles. The catalytic particles comprise iron, preferably iron oxide. The catalytic particles have an average particle size of 0.5-50 nm. The catalyst comprises iron. The catalytic cathode comprises iron in an amount of greater than or equal to 75%, preferably greater than or equal to 99%. The molten carbonate electrolyte comprises lithium carbonate (preferred), sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, rubidium carbonate, cesium carbonate, francium carbonate, beryllium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, strontium carbonate, barium carbonate and/or radium carbonate. The source of carbon dioxide comprises carbon dioxide, air, exhaust from an industrial process, and/or exhaust from an internal combustion engine. The carbon nanotubes are: crystalline; and single walled carbon nanotubes, and/or multi-walled carbon nanotubes. The average length of the carbon nanotubes is 100 nm to 1 cm. The average outer diameter of the carbon nanotubes is less than or equal to 100 nm, preferably 25-30 nm. The carbon dioxide is captures from the atmosphere, exhaust from an industrial process, and/or exhaust from an internal combustion engine. The captures carbon dioxide is electrolytically converted into many carbon nanotubes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further comprises a power source that is in electrical contact with the catalytically passive anode and the catalytic cathode.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); P73 (Layered products (B32).)
MC E05-U03; E11-A01; E11-D02; E11-F03; E11-G05; E11-N; E11-Q02B; E11-Q02C; E31-N05A; E31-N05C; E33; E34-A; E34-B01; E34-B02; E34-D03; E34-E03; E35-K02; E35-U02; E35-Y; N06-C02; N07-G; P73-V99

PN CN207268784-U
TI Hospital weather monitoring device, has controller connected with temperature sensor, humidity sensor connected with carbon dioxide concentration sensor, and organic volatile matter concentration sensor fixed with air concentration sensor.
AU XUE X
AE SHENZHEN DAS INTELLITECH CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018331467
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a weather monitoring device for hospital, comprising a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, the organic volatile matter concentration sensor. of suspended particulates in the air concentration sensor and for collecting data in real time and sending the data collected in real time to hospital IBMS system controller, the controller is connected with the temperature sensor, humidity sensor, carbon dioxide concentration sensor, the organic volatile matter concentration sensor and suspended particulates in the air concentration sensor communication connection. outdoor activities of this utility model through the hospital outdoor space designated area to add a weather monitoring device, the outdoor environment and air quality for comprehensive monitoring and real-time feedback to remind medical care personnel and patient according to actual condition of body and the external environment, and effectively help patients to avoid the unnecessary hurt to health.

PT P
PN CN113877553-A
TI Regenerating blast furnace gas desulfurization activated carbon comprises e.g. desulfurizing and absorbing blast furnace gas by activated carbon in adsorber, initiating regeneration of activated carbon, separating elemental sulfur, recycling carbon dioxide, and washing activated carbon.
AU LI W
AE MCC CISDI TECHNOLOGY RES CENT CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 202212651X
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating blast furnace gas desulfurization activated carbon comprises (i) directly sending blast furnace gas hydrolyzed by carbonyl sulfide into adsorber to make it enter desulfurization adsorption state, and desulfurizing and absorbing blast furnace gas by activated carbon in adsorber, (ii) initiating regeneration of activated carbon by stopping sending blast furnace gas, opening carbon dioxide storage tank, flowing supercritical carbon dioxide into adsorber to be regenerated, diffusing into activated carbon pores, and dissolving small particles of sulfur, (iii) reducing supercritical carbon dioxide dissolved with elemental sulfur and flowing into separator for separating elemental sulfur, (iv) compressing low-pressure carbon dioxide fluid flowing out of separator by compressor and entering into carbon dioxide storage tank for carbon dioxide recycling, and (v) washing activated carbon by supercritical carbon dioxide, and (vi) circulating steps (i) to (v).
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating blast furnace gas desulfurization activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmental friendly; has low the regeneration temperature and negligible carbon loss rate, and close adsorption performance after regeneration to the fresh adsorbent; utilizes the supercritical carbon dioxide as activated carbon regeneration solvent, which is chemically inactive, colorless, odorless, non-toxic, and safe; collects and utilizes the separated sulfur element; and does not change the original structure of the activated carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating blast furnace gas desulfurization activated carbon comprises (i) directly sending blast furnace gas hydrolyzed by carbonyl sulfide into adsorber to make it enter desulfurization adsorption state, and desulfurizing and absorbing blast furnace gas by activated carbon in adsorber, (ii) initiating regeneration of activated carbon by stopping sending blast furnace gas, when activated carbon in adsorber needs to be regenerated, opening carbon dioxide storage tank, flowing supercritical carbon dioxide into adsorber to be regenerated, diffusing into activated carbon pores, and dissolving small particles of sulfur in it, where supercritical carbon dioxide flow rate is 100-300 m3/hour, temperature is 25-45degrees Celsius, pressure is 25-35 Mpa, and regeneration time of activated carbon is 8-18 hours, (iii) reducing supercritical carbon dioxide dissolved with elemental sulfur in pressure through pressure reducing valve and flowing into separator for separating elemental sulfur, (iv) compressing low-pressure carbon dioxide fluid flowing out of separator by compressor and entering into carbon dioxide storage tank for carbon dioxide recycling, and (v) washing activated carbon by supercritical carbon dioxide, completing activated carbon regeneration of adsorber, closing carbon dioxide storage tank, and stopping supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and (vi) circulating steps (i) to (v).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide dissolved with elemental sulfur is reduced in pressure to 10-15 MPa through a pressure reducing valve, heating to 50-65degreesC through a first heat exchanger, and flowing into the separator. In the step (iv), the carbon dioxide compressed by the compressor, raising the pressure to 25-35 Mpa, cooling the second heat exchanger to 35-45degreesC, and flowing the supercritical carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide storage tank for recycling.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E31-N05B; E31-N03C; E31-N04C; E11-Q01A; E11-Q02B; J01-G
IP B01J-020/20; B01J-020/34; C10K-001/00; C10K-001/32

PT P
PN KR2022141050-A
TI Producing bio-sulfur, useful for treating            degenerative brain diseases e.g. Alzheimer's disease,            comprises e.g. immersing sulfur powder in sodium            hydroxide aqueous solution, and immersing sulfur powder            into acetic acid solution.
AU HYUN S
AE HYUN S (HYUN-Individual)
GA 2022D19395
AB    NOVELTY - Producing bio-sulfur, comprises (a) immersing                20 kg sulfur powder in 10% sodium hydroxide aqueous                solution for 2 hours; (b) immersing the sulfur                powder in a 5% acetic acid solution for 30 minutes                by discarding the sodium hydroxide (NaOH) aqueous                solution and dehydrating; (c) discarding acetic                acid aqueous solution is and dehydrating, washing                and dehydrating in purified water; (d) putting the                sulfur powder and dehydrating in a supercritical                reactor; (e) calculating the supercritical                synthesis by putting 0.5 l nitrogen in 10 l carbon                dioxide in the supercritical synthesis reactor and                pressurizing it at a temperature of 70&#176; C,                under pressure of 300 bar for 30 minutes; (f)                collecting the sulfur powder by spraying the sulfur                powder treated with supercritical synthetic carbon                dioxide to a rapid cooling device at -20&#176;                C; and (g) drying the collected sulfur powder at a                drying shelf temperature of -10-40&#176; C of                the freeze-drying device.
   USE - The method is useful for to produce bio-sulfur                for treating degenerative brain diseases including                Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease and Lou                Gehrig's disease (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides bio-sulfur which improves                cell viability by reducing oxidative stress in HT22                cells and reducing nitrite, and induces                inflammatory mediator without side effects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sulfur powder is                  treated with an aqueous sodium hydroxide solution                  and an aqueous acetic acid solution.
   ACTIVITY - Cerebroprotective; Neuroprotective; Nootropic;                Antiparkinsonian. Test details are described but no                results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - 50 l sodium hydroxide aqueous solution was put                in a 100 l drum of high-density polyethylene,                immersed in 20 kg sulfur powder, the solution was                stirred for 2 hours; the sodium hydroxide (NaOH)                aqueous solution was discarded, the solution was                dehydrated, 40 l acetic acid aqueous solution was                added, and the mixture was stirred for 30 minutes;                the aqueous solution of acetic acid was dehydrated,                and washed with purified water; dehydration                treatment of the sulfur powder in the supercritical                reactor was performed; the sulfur powder treated                with the supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide was                sprayed to a rapid cooling device at -20&#176;                C; and the sulfur powder was dried at a shelf                temperature of -10-40&#176; C in a                freeze-drying apparatus to obtain bio-sulfur.
DC B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC B05-A01B; B05-C06; B05-C08; B10-C04E; B14-J01A3; B14-J01A4; B14-N16; E31-F02
IP A61K-033/04; A61P-025/16; A61P-025/28; C01B-017/02

PT P
PN CN115142093-A
TI Pre-baked anode antioxidant used in preparation of            pre-baked anode for aluminum electrolysis, comprises            structure promoting phase, which includes expanded            graphite and expandable graphite, and            structure-regulating phase, which includes aluminum            trichloride and ferric trichloride.
AU LI S
   LIAO Y
   WEI L
   HUANG B
   DENG Z
   ZHANG Z
   LI Y
   HE C
   WEI D
   DU S
   HUANG H
   LIANG Z
   CHEN S
AE GUANGXI QIANGQIANG CARBON CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 2022C95039
AB    NOVELTY - Prebaked anode antioxidant, comprises a                structure-promoting phase and a                structure-regulating phase. The structure promoting                phase comprises expanded graphite and expandable                graphite, and the structure-regulating phase                comprises aluminum trichloride and ferric                trichloride.
   USE - Pre-baked anode antioxidant in preparation of                the pre-baked anode for aluminum electrolysis                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prebaked anode antioxidant effectively                reduces cracks of the prebaked anode carbon block,                improves interface strength of calcined petroleum                coke and asphalt coke in the prebaked anode carbon                block and improve the graphitization degree of the                asphalt coke,and further reduces reactivity of air                and carbon dioxide of the anode carbon block in the                production of electrolytic aluminum, effectively                reduces consumption of the carbon anode and reduces                discharge amount of carbon dioxide.

PN CN114700030-A
TI Preparing anti-fuel absorption regeneration agent,            comprises adding silicon-containing solution into            aluminum-containing solution, allowing to stand,            introducing carbon dioxide gas, washing, drying,            placing dry powder into rotating plate machine,            spraying and roasting.
AU TAN Y
   GAO M
   ZHONG X
   LI S
   WANG T
   GUO K
AE QINGDAO HUICHENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202291174H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-fuel adsorption regeneration                agent, comprises (i) slowly adding                silicon-containing solution into                aluminum-containing solution, controlling the                slurry temperature at &#8815; 25&#8451;,                continuously stirring for 30 minutes, and allowing                to stand for 4-12 hours, (ii) adding phosphoric                acid into aluminum sulfate solution, and stirring                uniformly, (iii) adding the solution obtained in                the step (ii) into the solution obtained in the                step (i) to obtain mixed solution, controlling the                pH value of the mixed solution to 9.5-9.8, allowing                to stand for 0.5-1 hours, and controlling the pH                value of the mixed solution to 3.5-4.5 to obtain                slurry, (iv) introducing carbon dioxide gas into                the slurry until the pH value is 6.5-7.5,                filtering, washing and drying, and (v) placing the                dry powder into the rotating plate machine,                rotating and spraying mist-like binder to obtain                spherical catalyst, and roasting.
   USE - The method is used for preparing anti-fuel                adsorption regeneration agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces pellets with different                grain diameters of different occasions. The                regeneration agent has a strong adsorption capacity                for polar compounds in inferior oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  silicon-containing solution is silica sol or                  sodium silicate, the aluminum containing solution                  is aluminum sol or sodium aluminate, and the                  ratio of silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, sodium                  oxide and water is (5-12):1:(0-8):(100-200). In                  the step (ii), the concentration of the aluminum                  sulfate solution is 90&#177;2 g/l of aluminum                  oxide, the concentration of the phosphoric acid                  is 10-20 wt.% of phosphoric acid, and the mass                  ratio of phosphoric acid and aluminum oxide is                  (0.03-0.5):1. In the step (iv), ammonium chloride                  and/or ammonium sulfate are used for washing. In                  the step (v), the sprayed mist-like binder is                  water or the mixed solution of water and                  hydrochloric acid

TI Method for flowing fluid from and/or to subsurface            formation, involves disposing main production tubular            in main borehole penetrating subsurface formation,            where production tube is coupled to shunt            assembly.
AU TUTTLE E
   WAGES D
AE BAKER HUGHES OILFIELD OPERATIONS INC (BAKO-C)
   WAGES D (WAGE-Individual)
   TUTTLE E (TUTT-Individual)
   BAKER HUGHES OILFIELD OPERATIONS INC (BAKO-C)
GA 202281586B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves disposing a main                production tubular in a main borehole penetrating a                subsurface formation, where the production tube is                coupled to a shunt assembly. The assembly defines                an opening oriented and biased in a desired                direction for a lateral borehole that penetrates                the formation. The borehole is drilled through the                opening in the assembly, and a completion device is                installed in the borehole through the opening. A                fluid e.g. hydrocarbons, is flown from the                formation to the tube and/or from the tube to the                formation through the completion device.
   USE - Method for flowing fluid from and/or to                subsurface formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables drilling lateral boreholes                from a main borehole and installing completions                while not interfering with fluid flows in the main                bore hole in hydrocarbon recovery and carbon                dioxide sequestration industries. The method allows                the fluid to be injected to stimulate the formation                to improve hydrocarbon production or to sequester                carbon dioxide in the formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus for flowing a fluid from and/or to a                subsurface formation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                an embodiment of a main production tubular.
   Borehole (2)
   Subsurface formation (4)
   Shunt assembly (6)
   Completion device (11)
