PN CN114621875-A
TI Co-producing high amylose starch by introducing            air rich in carbon dioxide into nutrient salt water            comprising ammonia nitrogen, adding carbonate or            bicarbonate, accumulating high amylose starch, and            harvesting algal cells.
AU ZHANG Y
   XIE T
   PAN Y
   ZHANG H
   SHEN Y
   YAO C
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202282929S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for co-producing high amylose starch                using microalgae to remove ammonia involves                introducing air rich in carbon dioxide into                nutrient salt water comprising ammonia nitrogen,                adding carbonate or bicarbonate to the mixture,                maintaining suitable pH and photosynthetic                activity, thus the microalgae photosynthetic carbon                absorbs ammonia nitrogen, accumulating high amylose                starch, after absorbing the ammonia nitrogen                completely and generating high amylose, and                harvesting the algal cells.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing high                amylose starch.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves ammonia nitrogen removal                rate of the system up to 100% and maximum ammonia                nitrogen absorption rate up to 57 mg/l/day when the                ammonia nitrogen concentration is less than 100                mg/l, amylose-to-branch ratio (Am/Ap) of the                accumulated starch up to 1-8.6, total starch                content up to 20.45-37.5% with respect to dry cell                weight and total starch concentration up to                0.64-0.96 g/l. The method improves total starch                content by a maximum of 70.4-212.5%, amylose                content by 38.8-157.5%, and total starch                concentration by 60-140% compared with the                high-amylose starch produced by the existing                microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for high                amylose starch, which is obtained by method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition of                  the ammonia nitrogen-comprising nutrient salt                  water comprises 10-100 mg/l ammonium. The                  composition of the ammonia nitrogen-containing                  nutrient salt water further comprises 4-178 mg/l                  phosphate, 0.33-0.98 mg/l ferric ion, 2.18-6.54                  mg/l disodium EDTA, 1.95-5.84 &#956;g/l                  copper divalent ion, 2.45-7.34 &#956;g/l                  molybdate, 4.42-13.28 &#956;g/l zinc divalent                  ion, 1.24-3.72 &#956;g/l cobalt divalent ion,                  30.46-91.38 &#956;g/l manganese divalent ion,                  7.37-12.33 g/l sodium ion, 13.29-24.31 g/l                  chloride ion, 63.18-189.54 mg/l bicarbonate,                  0.53-1.59 g/l magnesium divalent ion, 0.19-0.57                  g/l calcium divalent ion, 94.19-310.31 mg/l                  potassium ion, 28.91-92.10 mg/l bromide ion, and                  11.42-32.35 mg/l borate. The amount of the carbon                  dioxide is 1-5% of the air volume content. The                  added amount of the carbonate or bicarbonate is                  10-90 mM. Preferred Organism: The microalgae are                  Tetraselmis. Preferred Components: The carbonate                  comprises magnesium carbonate and/or calcium                  carbonate and substances rich in magnesium                  carbonate and/or calcium carbonate. The                  bicarbonate comprises sodium bicarbonate and                  substances rich in sodium bicarbonate. Preferred                  Method: The timing of adding carbonate or                  bicarbonate is 0-4 days after inoculation. The                  harvest time is 5-8 days after carbonate or                  bicarbonate addition.

AB    NOVELTY - Reservoir reconstruction method involves (s1) making main seam gap using high-viscosity glue, (s2) creating and expanding the gap with aqueous fracturing fluid, (s3) injecting carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid carrying proppant, (s4) performing jacking method, and (s5) repeating the steps (s1)-(s4) for fracturing construction of other sections until all sections are finished.

AB    NOVELTY - Batch preparation of oily perforated graphene conductive agent involves (1) mixing expanded graphite and potassium hydroxide powder in a ratio of 1:3, and sending the blended expanded graphite into an activation furnace for initial treatment of activation reaction, (2) introducing carbon dioxide gas into air at an air intake rate of 3-5 L/minute for activation reaction and reprocessing, and obtaining a powder after a constant temperature of 1000degrees Celsius for 4-5 hours, (3) adding N-methylpyrrolidone and the obtained powder into the reactor, introducing nitrogen gas into air at an air intake rate of 1-2 L/hour, and stirring and dispersing simultaneously at a stirring speed of 90-110 rpm and a dispersing rotation speed of 1100-1200 rpm to obtain a dispersed material, (4) redispersing the dispersed material by shearing and peeling to obtain a redispersed material, (5) fully peeling off the redispersed material, and (6) repeating the steps (4) and (5) 4-6 times.

PN CN113772672-A
TI Treating carbon emission of waste gas by fire-driven oil extraction used in petroleum exploitation waste gas, involves removing residual acid from reformed waste gas, dehydrating acid-removing waste gas for separating carbon monoxide.
AU LEI G
   ZHANG X
AE CHENGDU QICHUAN NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9725L
AB    NOVELTY - Treating carbon emission of waste gas by fire-driven oil extraction involves dewatering waste gas of fire drive by dehydration unit; reacting water-removed waste gas with plasma recovery unit to generate water gas; using hydrogen in water gas to react with nitrogen to generate ammonia gas; using ammonia in exhaust gas to generate ammonium salt through action of cooling and dust removal unit, and removing solvent to obtain ammonium salts solid; removing residual acid from reformed waste gas through deacidification unit and dehydrating acid-removing exhaust gas by pressure swing adsorption unit, adsorbing and separating nitrogen and carbon monoxide.
   USE - Method for treating fire-driven oil extraction waste gas carbon emission reduction used in petroleum exploitation waste gas processing field, particularly for reducing carbon emission of carbon dioxide and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps in reducing water into plasma reforming dissociating into oxygen, avoids generating source of carbon dioxide, and waste gas is recycled and provided at same time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of structure of fire-driven oil extraction waste gas carbon emission reduction treatment method.
   Sewage tank (2)
   Plasma power supply (5)
   De-ionized water tank (8)
   Acid liquid tank (11)
   Alkali liquid tank (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of gas-liquid separator is 10-50degrees Celsius, preferably 35degrees Celsius, pressure is 80-600 Kilopascal (KPa), preferably 100-250 KPa. The average temperature of ion torch is 2000-4000degrees Celsius, preferably 2500-3000degrees Celsius, where working medium gas adopted by plasma torch is nitrogen. The distance between plasma torch and section of waste gas inlet is 200 to 500 millimeter (mm), preferably 300 mm. The concentration range of acid liquid is 1-20 mol/L, preferably 5-10 mol/L. The pressure range of pressure swing adsorption process is 400-800 KPa, preferably 500-600 KPa.Preferred Components: The acid solution is sulfuric acid solution, phosphoric acid solution, preferably sulfuric acid solution. The alkali solution is potassium hydroxide solution, sodium hydroxide solution, calcium hydroxide solution, preferably saturated calcium hydroxide solution. The working medium gas adopted by plasma torch is nitrogen. The waste gas of fire driver comprises carbon dioxide, nitrogen, water, methane, oxygen, carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide, ethane and propane.Preferred Method: The method for treating carbon emission of waste gas by fire-driven oil extraction involves mixing waste gas and plasma in carbon dioxide reforming tower, reacting carbon dioxide and methane in waste gas to generate carbon monoxide and hydrogen under catalytic action of active particles of plasma hydrogen which is further reacted with active ion of plasma to generate ammonia; using waste gas which enters in carbon dioxide reforming tower in way of tangential rotational flow, where plasma torch is arranged at position higher than section of waste gas inlet in reverse tangential direction to make plasma enter carbon dioxide reforming tower; using waste gas after reforming enters into filling tower to counter-flow with acid liquid at upper end of filling tower to transfer heat to filling tower, where alkaline gas in waste gas is absorbed to form ammonium salt solution which is saturated and then sent to centrifuge under action of centrifuge, throwing out ammonium salt solid for bagging, then liquid conveyed to acid solution tank; providing waste gas and alkali liquid which are carried out mass transfer heat transfer reaction, where waste gas is opposite to flow direction of alkali liquid then alkali liquid absorbing residual acid flows into alkali liquid tank from gravity and is conveyed to alkali washing tower by alkali liquid pump to circularly spray wash and absorb residual acid, providing alkali liquid tank with pH meter when pH value is reduced to neutral, then discharging portion of liquid, supplementing new alkali liquid to tank; using waste gas after deacidifying is pressurized by compressor, then sent to dewatering tank, where waste gas enters pressure swing adsorption tank, after removing water in dewatering tank, then pressure swing adsorption uses tower operation where one tower is in feeding adsorption state, tower is in desorption state, and whole process is adsorption, pressure equalization, desorption, pressure equalization; using desorbed nitrogen as working medium gas of plasma torch and other portion of gas is evacuated and discharged desorbed carbon monoxide which is sent to carbon monoxide storage tank for resource utilization.

TI Preparing polymerized high internal phase            emulsions multistage porous material containing            fluorine with ultra high specific surface area useful            in absorbing carbon dioxide, comprises dispersing            surfactant and initiator in oil phase, carrying out            polymerization reaction and vacuum drying.
AU XIA X
   WANG Y
   LIN J
   GENG B
AE UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 2021E9023T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polymerized high internal phase                emulsions (polyHIPE) multistage porous material                containing fluorine with ultra high specific                surface area comprises (i) dispersing surfactant                and initiator in the oil phase including                fluorine-containing monomer, styrene and                cross-linking agent, and adding the aqueous                electrolyte solution to the oil phase as the                aqueous phase by mechanical stirring to obtain                water-in-oil type fluorine-containing high internal                phase emulsion, and then carrying out                polymerization reaction, and drying to obtain a                monolithic fluorine-containing porous polymer                precursor, (ii) placing the monolithic                fluoropolymer precursor obtained in the step (i) in                an organic solvent for swelling, adding Lewis acid                catalyst and crosslinking agent, and reacting under                the protection of inert gas, and (iii) cooling to                room temperature, rinsing material obtained from                the reaction with solvent and extracting with                solvent, and then vacuum drying.
   USE - The material is useful in absorbing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines emulsion template method                with ultra-cross-linking knitting method to obtain                porous material with ultra-high specific surface                area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                fluorine-containing hierarchical porous material                prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  fluorine-containing monomer accounts for 10-80                  wt.% of the oil phase mass. The crosslinking                  agent accounts for 10-80 wt.% of the oil phase                  mass. The styrene accounts for 10-80 wt.% of the                  oil phase mass. The surfactant accounts for 2-25                  wt.% of the oil phase mass. The initiator                  accounts for 0.1-2 wt.% of the mass of the oil                  phase. In the step (i), the fluorine-containing                  monomer includes fluorine-containing acrylate                  compound. The fluorine-containing monomer                  includes trifluoroethyl methacrylate,                  hexafluorobutyl methacrylate, dodecafluoroheptyl                  methacrylate, trifluoroethyl acrylate,                  hexafluorobutyl acrylate and/or                  dodecafluoroheptyl acrylate. In the step (i), the                  crosslinking agent includes divinylbenzene. The                  surfactant includes Hypermer-B246 (Polymeric                  surfactant with outstanding dispersion and                  emulsion stability properties), Hypermer-A70                  (High molecule weight, polymeric surfactant),                  Hypermer-2524 (High-performance polymeric                  surfactant), and Hypermer-2296 (High-performance                  polymeric surfactant). The initiator includes                  azobisisobutyronitrile, benzoyl peroxide,                  ammonium persulfate and/or sodium persulfate. In                  the step (ii), the organic solvent includes                  dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon                  tetrachloride, 1,2-dichloroethane or                  tetrahydrofuran, preferably 1,2-dichloroethane.                  In the step (ii), the Lewis acid catalyst                  includes anhydrous ferric chloride, anhydrous                  aluminum chloride or anhydrous tin chloride. The                  external linking agent includes dimethoxymethane,                  and monolithic fluoropolymer precursors. The mass                  ratio of the Lewis acid catalyst and the                  crosslinking agent is 1:(2-6):(1.5-4). The inert                  gas includes nitrogen or argon. In the step                  (iii), the solvent includes methanol, ethanol,                  dichloromethane, acetone or                  tetrahydrofuran.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  aqueous phase accounts for 74-90 wt.% of the mass                  of the water-in-oil fluorine-containing high                  internal phase emulsion. The electrolyte solution                  includes sodium chloride aqueous solution,                  calcium chloride aqueous solution or magnesium                  chloride aqueous solution, and the solubility of                  the electrolyte solution is 0.1-0.3 mol/l. The                  polymerization reaction is carried out at                  55-80degrees Celsius for 24 hours. In the step                  (ii), the swelling time is 12-24 hours. In the                  step (ii), the reaction is as follows reaction at                  45degrees Celsius for 4-5 hours, and then at                  80degrees Celsius for 20-28 hours. In the step                  (iii), the extraction is Soxhlet extraction,                  extraction temperature is 40-140degrees Celsius,                  and extraction time is 24-48 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tumor antigen CA125 comprises (a) cell culturing by resuscitating ovarian cancer cell line (OVCAR-3) cells to Roswell Park Memorial Institute Medium (RPMI) 1640 medium, adding 20% fetal bovine serum and 5% carbon dioxide to culture, passing for1:3 and continuing to grow, (b) expressing antigen by changing medium after cells have grown to sufficient amount, using RPMI-1640 medium and 1% fetal bovine serum to grow and performing antigen expression, (c) purifying antigen by collecting supernatant of cell culture and removing remaining cells after centrifugation to obtain clear supernatant solution, using concentration tube with a molecular weight cut-off of 50 kDa to concentrate supernatant solution by centrifugation at 3000 revolutions/minute, using molecular sieve purification method of protein rapid purification instrument and using Superdex 200 (High-resolution gel filtration column) 16/60 molecular sieve purification column to purify and collecting components.
   USE - The tumor antigen CA125 is useful as a calibrator and/or standard (claimed), is useful in test agents.
   ADVANTAGE - The antigen has high biological activity and specificity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Use of tumor antigen CA125 in detecting reagents, where the tumor antigen CA125 is prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the cell expression is resuscitated 106 numbers of OVCAR-3 cells to culture in a cell culture flask T25 and the medium is changed 1-2 times a week. The method of resuscitation in step (a) includes shaking frozen cells removed from the liquid nitrogen in a 37degrees Celsius water bath, transferring cells to a centrifuge tube after thawing, adding culture medium, removing supernatant after centrifugation, resuspending cells in culture medium, transferring to a cell culture flask and shaking them crisscross to make the cells evenly dispersed. In step (a), pass 1:3, when the cells grow to more than 80%.Preferred Components: In the step (c), buffer system used for purification is phosphate-buffered saline, pH 7.4, 10% glycerol and 50 mM glycine. In the step (c), controlling flow rate at 1 ml/minute, when using Superdex 200 (High-resolution gel filtration column) 16/60 molecular sieve purification column, collecting components after 36 minutes of purification.

PT P
PN WO2021185879-A1; CN115298157-A
TI Catalytic process for preparing ethylenically            unsaturated carboxylic acid salt involves contacting            alkene and carbon dioxide with carboxylation catalyst,            organic solvent, and alkoxide and subjecting portion to            mechanical separation.
AU HUGUET-SUBIELA N
   WEISSKER W
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2021B5923Q
AB    NOVELTY - A catalytic process for preparing an alpha ,                beta -ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid                salt involves (a) contacting an alkene and carbon                dioxide with a carboxylation catalyst, an organic                solvent, and an alkoxide having a secondary or                tertiary carbon atom directly bound to oxide group,                to obtain a crude reaction product comprising the                alpha , beta -ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic                acid salt and alcohol by-product which is the                conjugate acid of the alkoxide, (b) allowing the                alpha , beta -ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic                acid salt to precipitate out from the crude                reaction product; and (c) subjecting greater than                or equal to 1 portion of crude reaction product to                a mechanical separation step while maintaining the                alcohol by-product in liquid form to obtain a solid                phase comprising alpha , beta -ethylenically                unsaturated carboxylic acid salt and a liquid phase                comprising the carboxylation catalyst, organic                solvent and alcohol by-product.
   USE - A catalytic process for preparing an alpha ,                beta -ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid                salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The alpha , beta -ethylenically unsaturated                carboxylic acid salt, due to its ionic nature, has                a finite solubility in most organic solvents and                precipitates out from the crude reaction product.                The alpha , beta -ethylenically unsaturated                carboxylic acid salt can be easily separated by a                mechanical separation operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkoxide is a                  sodium alkoxide, preferably a sodium alkoxide                  having a tertiary carbon atom directly bound to                  oxide group. The carboxylation catalyst is a                  nickel or palladium complex which comprises a                  bidentate P,X ligand in which X is selected from                  phosphorus (P), nitrogen (N), oxygen (O), and                  carbene, and the P and X atoms are separated by a                  bivalent linker that comprises 2-4 bridging                  atoms.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mechanical separation                  step (c) comprises a filtration step. The                  catalytic process additionally comprises (d)                  distilling the alcohol by-product and optionally                  the organic solvent off from the liquid phase to                  obtain an alcohol by-product distillate fraction                  and optionally a solvent distillate fraction, and                  a residue fraction. The catalytic process                  additionally comprises recycling greater than or                  equal to 1 portion of the residue fraction to                  step (a). The catalytic process additionally                  comprises (e) contacting greater than or equal to                  1 portion of alcohol by-product distillate                  fraction obtained in step (d) with an alkaline                  material to regenerate an alkoxide. The catalytic                  process additionally comprises recycling greater                  than or equal to 1 portion of the regenerated                  alkoxide to step (a). The catalytic process                  additionally comprises (f) contacting the solid                  phase obtained in step (c) with a wash liquid to                  obtain a spent wash liquid and a purified solid                  phase in which the carboxylic acid salt is                  enriched. Step (c) is performed at 0-150 degrees                  C. Preferred Components: The wash liquid is                  selected from alkanes and alcohols. The wash                  liquid is alcohol by-product. The wash liquid is                  the organic solvent. The organic solvent is                  selected from N,N-disubstituted formamide,                  N,N-disubstituted acetamide, N-substituted                  2-pyrrolidone and 1,3-disubstituted                  2-imidazolidinone. The organic solvent is                  N,N-disubstituted formamide, preferably                  N,N-dialkylated formamide. The alkene is ethene                  and the a,b-ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic                  acid salt is sodium acrylate.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN112853377-A
TI Preparing dual-function metal-free nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst used in e.g. oxygen reduction, by dispersing raw materials, stirring, performing hydrothermal reaction, centrifuging, drying, carbonizing, and naturally cooling.
AU WEN Z
   LI J
   JIA C
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 2021619957
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing dual-function metal-free nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst, involves (i) preparing precursor of metal framework compound zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8) by uniformly ultrasonically dispersing raw materials, magnetically stirring for a period of time, performing a hydrothermal reaction, centrifuging and drying to obtain the ZIF-8 precursor, and (ii) carbonizing the ZIF-8 precursor at a high temperature of 900-1100 degrees C in a nitrogen atmosphere for 3-5 hours, and naturally cooling to obtain a highly active nitrogen-carbon catalyst.
   USE - The method is used for preparing dual-function metal-free nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst in highly active oxygen reduction catalytic reaction and electrocatalytic reaction for reducing highly active carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, efficient, cheap and reliable to operate without high energy consumption and high noise process steps such as ball milling, and convenient for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use of the dual-functional metal-free nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst, involves using the dual-functional metal-free nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst to a highly active oxygen reduction catalytic reaction and electrocatalytic reaction for reducing highly active carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves dissolving 4.76 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 5.3 g 2-methylimidazole in 67 ml methanol and 100 ml methanol at 20-27 degrees C, ultrasonically processing the two separately for 15 minutes, mixing and magnetically stirring for 1 hour, reacting in a reaction kettle at 120 degrees C for 4 hours, centrifuging at 9000 rpm for 5 minutes, washing with ethanol for 3 times, and drying in a vacuum-drying oven at 70 degrees C 12 hours to obtain the ZIF-8 precursor. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the optimal high-temperature carbonization temperature is maintained at 1000 degrees C. The dual-functional metal-free nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst has excellent oxygen reduction activity under alkaline conditions, a half-wave potential close to 0.87 V, to achieve the performance of platinum/carbon catalyst, and with 1 M potassium bicarbonate as the electrolyte solution, the catalyst has extremely high selectivity for reduction to carbon monoxide at a voltage of -0.27 to -0.97 V, and highest Faraday efficiency of 95%.

PT P
PN CN112385799-A
TI Removing fishy odor and partially hydrolyzing fish maw by rinsing fish maw with sodium chloride solution, performing carbon dioxide treatment, filling with filling containing yeast powder, sterilizing and coating with e.g. glycerol.
AU CAI J
AE SHENZHEN CHAIFOO IND CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202121136Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing fishy odor and partially hydrolyzing fish maw involves (a) rinsing intact fish maw with 2-4 %mass sodium chloride solution while passing ozone and stirring for 40-80 minutes, drying, then performing high-density carbon dioxide treatment at less than 20 degrees C and 40-50 MPa for 20-30 minutes, (b) cooling the edible culture source to 20-35 degrees C, adding yeast powder or/and Aspergillus oryzae powder, sealing and fermenting at 50-60 degrees C for 12-20 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding protease and mixing to obtain a filling, where the protease is chosen from at least one of acid protease and fish collagenase, (c) filling the filling into the fish maw and allowing to stand at 20-40 degrees C for 4-10 hours, (d) sterilizing and inactivating the enzyme by processing the fish maw at 90-100 degrees C for 20-30 minutes and (e) coating the surface of the fish maw with a mixture of modified corn starch, glycerol and cooking wine.
   USE - The method is useful for removing fishy odor and partially hydrolyzing fish maw.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves quality of the fish maw, hydrolyzes fish maw into easily absorbable small molecules, retains appearance of the fish maw and increases taste of food.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves passing ozone at rate of 10-20 g/hour. In step (a), the dried fish maw has water content of less than 20%. In step (b), the mass ratio of edible culture source, yeast powder or/and A.oryzae powder and protease is 500-1000:1:1 and the edible culture source is rice. The step (c) involves allowing to stand at 25 plus minus 5 degrees C for 6 hours. In step (e), the mass ratio of modified corn starch, glycerol and cooking wine is 1:2-4:10-20 and the cooking wine is scallion ginger garlic cooking wine. The method involves adding fish maw into a suspension containing 5-30% activated carbon, soaking for 60-120 minutes for softening, rinsing with clear water to remove the activated carbon and then performing step (a).
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.)

PT P
PN WO2020178684-A1; CN113557220-A; EP3935034-A1; US2022162144-A1
TI Producing methanol involves reacting carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with hydrogen in a reactor to produce crude methanol, adding alkali to crude methanol, and separating obtained crude methanol/alkali mixture into a multiple streams.
AU BODAS V D
   LEAL G
   ANSARI M B
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202089828W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol involves reacting carbon dioxide (CO2) and/or carbon monoxide (CO) with hydrogen (H2) in a reactor to produce crude methanol, adding an alkali to the crude methanol to produce a crude methanol/alkali mixture, and separating the crude methanol/alkali mixture into a multiple streams. The separating step is performed by using separation unit comprising one or more distillation columns. The multiple streams comprises an overhead stream comprising primarily methanol, a bottoms stream comprising primarily water and alkali, collectively, and a vapor side draw stream comprising primarily fusel oil, where the vapor side draw stream comprises less than 100 Parts per billion alkali. The vapor side draw stream is recycled to a reformer adapted to produce synthesis gas and a methyl tertiary butyl ether (MTBE) synthesis reactor.
   USE - Method for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves adding alkali solution to crude methanol to produce crude methanol/alkali mixture that results in reducing the corrosive effects of organic acids that may be in crude methanol. The fusel oil by product from the methanol production process is recycled to produce synthesis gas and/or MTBE. The recycled fusel oil has a limited amount of alkali so that there is no need to remove alkali from the recycled fusel oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The separating involves distilling, in a first distillation column, the crude methanol/alkali mixture into an overhead intermediate stream comprising dimethyl ether and a bottoms intermediate stream comprising primarily methanol, water, alkali, and ethanol. The bottoms intermediate stream is distilled in a second distillation column into a multiple streams comprising the overhead stream, the bottoms stream, and the vapor side draw stream. Preferred Conditions: The separating comprises extracting the vapor side draw stream from the second distillation column by operation of a jet ejector, a steam ejector, and/or a heat exchanger adapted to cool and condense the vapor side draw stream. The vapor side draw stream is extracted from a tray that is one of the sixth to the eighth tray from the bottom tray of the second distillation column. The vapor side draw stream is extracted from a tray of the second distillation column that collects material that has the highest concentration of fusel oil. Operating conditions of the first distillation column and second distillation column includes a bottom column temperature of 100-150 degrees C and a top column temperature of 60-75 degrees C and a pressure of 1.2-8 bar absolute. 99.9% Water, alkali, and sodium compounds collectively in the crude methanol are recovered in the bottoms stream. The Reid vapor pressure of the MTBE from the synthesis reactor is 7-8 pound per square inch, preferably 7 pound per square inch. The vapor side draw stream is not subjected to a separation process before recycling to the reformer or the MTBE synthesis reactor. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide, the carbon monoxide, and the hydrogen are obtained from synthesis gas. Preferred Composition: 99 Wt.% dimethyl ether from the crude methanol is recovered in the overhead intermediate stream. The overhead stream comprises a minimum of 99.85 wt.% methanol and a maximum of 0.1 wt.% water. The bottoms stream comprises a total organic carbon content of 500 ppm. The vapor side draw stream comprises 100 Parts per billion alkali, a minimum of 97 wt.%, preferably 89 wt.% fusel oil, and a maximum of 1 wt.%, preferably 10 wt.% water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN US2020255290-A1
TI Chemical looping reforming involves heating oxygen            carrier in presence of catalyst and plasma radicals to            react oxygen carrier with fuel to provide reduced            oxygen carrier and contacting reduced oxygen carrier            with water or carbon dioxide.
AU UDDI M
   RANGANATHAN R V
   FONDRIEST S M
AE UNIV ALABAMA (UABR-C)
GA 202077654P
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical looping reforming (M1), comprises:                heating an oxygen carrier in the presence of a                catalyst and plasma radicals to react the oxygen                carrier with a fuel to provide a reduced oxygen                carrier; and contacting the reduced oxygen carrier                with water or carbon dioxide to produce hydrogen or                carbon monoxide, respectively, and regenerate the                oxygen carrier.
   USE - The method is useful for chemical looping                reforming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes plasma catalytic that                enhance reactions at lower temperatures                efficiently, while maintaining the integrity of                materials over long hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a catalyst comprising a rare earth metal                oxide sintered with a perovskite, where the                perovskite is transition metal containing compound                of formula (A1xB11-xM1O3) (I); and
   (2) preparing a perovskite catalyst,                comprising dissolving salts of nickel, lanthanum,                and cerium to form a homogenous solution, drying                the solution to form a salt mixture, calcining the                salt mixture to form a perovskite having nitric                oxide compound of formula (LaxCe1-xNiO3) (II),                blending the perovskite with a rare earth metal                oxide oxygen carrier to form a blend, and sintering                the blend to form the perovskite catalyst.
   A1 = lanthanide;
   B1 = polyvalent cation;
   M1 = transition metal; and
   x = 0.02-0.98.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The chemical looping                  reform method is carried out at a temperature of                  150-1000 degrees C, preferably 150-500 degrees C.                  The metal oxide oxygen carrier and the perovskite                  catalyst are sintered prior to reacting the                  oxygen carrier and the fuel. The after sintering,                  the metal oxide oxygen carrier and the perovskite                  catalyst has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET)                  surface area of greater than or equal to 10 m2/g.                  The water is provided in the presence of a gas                  comprising argon and/or nitrogen. The blend is                  sintered of 600-1000 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The catalyst comprises a perovskite                  catalyst, a nano-yolk shell catalyst, a                  nano-shaped catalyst supported nano-metal                  particle, a nano-carbide catalyst, a doped                  graphene based catalyst, and/or zeolite. The                  catalyst is a perovskite catalyst having (I),                  preferably (II). The oxygen carrier is a metal                  oxide oxygen carrier comprising a metal                  comprising cerium, nickel, copper, iron, cobalt,                  manganese, aluminum, titanium, zirconium,                  silicon, lanthanum and/or magnesium. The metal                  oxide oxygen carrier comprises cerium oxide. The                  metal oxide oxygen carrier and the perovskite                  catalyst are present in a weight ratio of                  1:5-5:1. The fuel comprises a hydrocarbon                  comprising an alkane, an alkene, a cycloalkane, a                  cycloalkene, an aryl group, and/or a syngas. The                  rare earth metal oxide comprises cerium oxide.                  The perovskite and the rare earth metal oxide are                  in a weight ratio of 1:5-5:1. The catalyst has a                  BET surface area of greater than or equal to 10                  m2/g. The catalyst has an average particle size                  of 50-100 nm, as determined by scanning electron                  microscopy (SEM) images.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating a molten                carbonate fuel cell comprising an anode and a                cathode for a first time period at a first                operating condition comprising a first transference                of 0.95 or less and a first average current density                of 60 mA/cm2 to generate an anode exhaust                comprising carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, and                a cathode exhaust comprises 2.0 vol% or less carbon                dioxide, 1.0 vol% or more oxygen, and 1.0 vol% or                more hydrogen oxide. The molten carbonate fuel cell                is operated for a second time period at a second                operating condition comprising a second                transference of greater than 0.95 to generate an                anode exhaust and a cathode exhaust.
   USE - Method for producing electricity.

The reduction in fuel cell lifetime is                minimized or avoided by performing periodic                regeneration of the fuel cell. The lifetime of a                molten carbonate fuel cell is reduced or                minimized.

PT P
PN CN210559433-U
TI System for preparing calcium carbonate by using phosphogypsum, has second reaction tank whose discharge port is connected with second solid-liquid separating device whose solid outlet is connected with washing groove connected with filter.
AU LAN Z
   LI B
AE SINOCHEM FULING CHONGQING CHEM IND CO (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 202045920X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a system for preparing calcium carbonate via phosphogypsum, comprising the first reaction tank and the second reaction tank, the feeding port of the first reaction tank is connected with a phosphorus gypsum storage bin, the discharge port of the first reaction tank is connected with a first solid-liquid separating device; the feeding port of the solids outlet of the device connected with the second reaction tank, the feeding port of the feeding port of the first reaction tank and the second reaction tank is further connected with an ammonia tank and a carbon dioxide tank, the discharging port of the second reaction tank is connected with a second solid-liquid separating device; the solid outlet of the second solid-liquid separating device is connected with the washing groove and the filter, the utility model phosphorus gypsum with ammonia water and carbon dioxide after two times of fully reaction, and by further washing, filtering to obtain high hardness, calcium carbonate product is good, and it can be widely used in the building material, and the by-product ammonium sulphate can be directly used for producing sulphur-based compound fertilizer, but also can be used for acidification treatment before phosphorite flotation.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-D03A
IP C01F-011/18

TI Turbomachine for compressing supercritical carbon dioxide used in closed, indirect-heated Brayton cycle, has inlet whose radius is of specific value to radius of impeller, and inducerless radial impeller that includes blade.
AU GOODHAND M M
   BUNCE I M
   GOODHAND M N
AE ROLLS-ROYCE PLC (RORO-C)
   ROLLS-ROYCE PLC (RORO-C)
GA 202017217X
AB    NOVELTY - The turbomachine has an inlet (201), an inducerless radial impeller (202) that includes blades and a fully vaneless diffuser (203). The radius of the inlet (ro) is from 25 to 50 percent of the radius of the impeller (r2). The inlet is radially flared to induce a radial component in flow prior to an entry to the impeller. The blades comprises a set of main blades (211) and a set of splitter blades (212).
   USE - Turbomachine for compressing supercritical carbon dioxide used in closed, indirect-heated Brayton cycle (claimed) such as air-breathing gas turbines.
   ADVANTAGE - The turbomachine provides the widest possible operating range due to increased stability margin. The absolute Mach number is reduced at the impeller exit, thus, reducing losses in the diffuser and improving efficiency. The inlet is large relative to the size of the impeller to facilitate sufficient margin in inlet velocity for a given mass flow to the velocity at which the flow becomes subcritical, thus, reducing the risk of condensation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating turbomachine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a plan view of the compressor.

   Inlet (201)
   Impeller (202)

   R1, R2 = H or CH3;
   R3 = H or alkyl, aryl or CO2-alkyl (all optionally substituted);
   n = greater than 0; and
   m = greater than or equal to 0.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:

   (2) polymer is of formula (II);
   (3) an electrode material comprising (II) and an electrochemically active material;
   (4) a positive electrode comprising the electrode material on a current collector;
   (5) a negative electrode comprising the electrode material on a current collector;
   (6) an electrochemical cell comprising the negative electrode, the positive electrode and the electrolyte; and
   (7) trapping carbon dioxide generated in a battery or electrochemical cell, comprising contacting the carbon dioxide with (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is an inorganic catalyst e.g. lithium bromide or an organic catalyst comprising quaternary ammonium salts, phosphonium salts, imidazolium salts and any other known organic base catalysts for carbon dioxide cycloaddition. The quaternary ammonium salt is a tetraalkylammonium halide (e.g. benzyltriethylammonium chloride). The shell is a pouch, where the shell is of cylindrical or prismatic shape or the shell is button shaped. The active material comprises metal oxide particles, lithiated metal oxide particles, metal phosphate particles and lithiated metal phosphate particles. The metal is a transition metal comprising iron, manganese, vanadium, nickel and/or cobalt. The active material is a manganese-containing oxide or phosphate. The active material further comprises at least one doping element (e.g. magnesium).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solvent is a solid solvent, e.g. a polymer solvent. The solid solvent is poly(N-vinylpyrrolidone). The electrode material further comprises a polymer binder and/or an electronically conductive material. The polymer binder is present and comprising fluorine containing polymers (e.g. polyvinylidene fluoride (PVdF) and PTFE), water soluble binders (e.g. styrene-butadiene rubber, acrylonitrile butadiene rubber (NBR), hydrogenated NBR, CHR, and polyacrylic), and ion-conductive polymer binders e.g. copolymers composed of at least one lithium-ion solvating segment (e.g. polyether segments) and at least one crosslinkable segment (e.g. poly(ethylene oxide)-based polymers comprising methyl methacrylate units). The polymer binder is a fluorine containing polymer (e.g. PVdF). The polymer is a binder additive. The conductive material is present and comprises acetylene black (preferred), carbon black, graphite, graphene, carbon fibers, carbon nanofibers and/or carbon nanotubes. The conductive material is a combination of acetylene black and carbon fibers (e.g. vapor grown carbon fibers (VGCF)). The composition forms a coating on a surface of a battery component. The battery coating is on a surface of a current collector, on a thin film inert insertion, or an internal surface of a battery or electrochemical cell shell (e.g. solid casing or flexible pouch). The battery or electrochemical cell component comprises current collector, thin film inert insertion and internal surface of the battery or electrochemical cell shell (e.g. solid casing or flexible pouch). Preferred Method: The method further comprises applying the composition.

PT P
PN KR2019129507-A; KR2090194-B1
TI Noble metal-free hybrid Z-type photocatalyst for converting carbon dioxide to methane, comprises multiple zinc oxide nanorods loaded with multiple copper zinc tin sulfide nanoparticles forming hetero-junction with zinc oxide nanorods.
AU IN S I
AE DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 2019A34862
AB    NOVELTY - Noble metal-free hybrid Z-type photocatalyst comprises multiple zinc oxide (ZnO) nanorods loaded with multiple copper zinc tin sulfide CZTS (Cu2ZnSnS4) nanoparticles. The CZTS nanoparticles and ZnO nanorods form hetero-junctions.
   USE - Noble metal-free hybrid Z-type photocatalyst for converting carbon dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The non-noble metal-hybrid Z-type photocatalyst has a broad light absorption spectrum without using a noble metal component, and the generated photocharges are efficiently separated. The conversion rate is about 30 times higher than the conventional photocatalyst zinc oxide nanorods and about 20 times higher than CZTS nanoparticles. The non-noble metal-hybrid Z-type photocatalyst manufacturing method can produce the photocatalyst in high yield and economical efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing metal-free hybrid Z-type photocatalyst, which involves preparing multiple ZnO nanorod assemblies, mixing the ZnO nanorod aggregate and the CZTS precursor, followed by stirring to prepare a stirring solution, performing hydrothermal synthesis at 170-200 degrees C for 16-24 hours, then leaving the solution at 20-35 degrees C for cooling to obtain powder of a cooled composite, washing and drying the obtained powder to obtain metal-free hybrid Z-type photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The noble metal free hybrid Z type photocatalyst comprises 5-45 pts. wt. CZTS nanoparticles based on 100 pts. wt. ZnO nanorods. The CZTS nanoparticles include 29.5-31.5 atomic % copper, 13.20-14.10 atomic % zinc, 9.40-10.50 atomic % tin, and 45-47.5 atomic % sulfur. The CZTS precursor is 0.57-0.87 pts. wt. copper (II) nitrate trihydrate, 0.06-0.36 pts. wt. zinc chloride, 0.13-0.43 pts. wt. tin chloride and 0.78-1.08 pts. wt. sodium sulfide. The stirring solution comprises 20-80 pts. wt. CZTS precursor based on 100 pts. wt. ZnO nanorod assembly. Preferred Conditions: The non-noble metal-hybrid Z type photocatalyst has a Raman spectral peak at 328-332 cm-1, 434-437 cm-1, and 570-575 cm-1 as measured by Raman spectroscopy. The concentration of sodium hydroxide aqueous solution is 6.5-8 M, and the concentration of zinc chloride aqueous solution is 2.5-3.5 M. The dropwise addition is performed by adding 40-70 pts. wt. zinc chloride aqueous solution with respect to 100 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. The photoluminescence (PP) spectrum has a peak in a wavelength range satisfying equations: 385 nm not less than wavelength ( lambda ) not less than 393 nm 708 nm not less than wavelength ( lambda ) not less than 724 nm. Under artificial sunlight of AM 1.5 G condition, the experiment of converting carbon dioxide to methane by gas phase reaction of carbon dioxide and water vapor for 1 hour, The methane evolution rate is 50 ppm per gram per hour. Preferred Process: The ZnO nanorod assembly is prepared by adding dropwise aqueous solution of zinc chloride to an aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, heating at 60-80 degrees C while continuously stirring for 5-10 hours and then filtering to obtain a reaction product, washing and drying the reaction product, followed by performing a sintering process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CA2998025-A1
TI Hydrocarbon fractions e.g. biogas being recovered from hydrothermal liquefaction reaction by placing feedstock slurry from waste solids into heat transfer reactor, processing materials, and separating into liquid and solid fractions.
AU KRISTIAN J
   EIDEM B
   VOLDENG P
AE NULIFE GREENTECH INC (NULI-Non-standard)
GA 2019821195
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbon fractions are recovered from hydrothermal liquefaction reaction which is performed by placing feedstock slurry from waste solids into a pressurized heat transfer reactor where it is maintained at temperature and pressure for a predetermined period, processing the materials at a moisture level of 50% or more, a pressure of 2000-4000 psi and a temperature of 250-450 degrees C contained in a heat transfer reactor, and separating the processed emulsion into liquid and solid fractions, where the fraction is chosen from (i) biogas, biofuels, biosolids and bio crude, (ii) silica, phosphates, potash and low concentration nitrogen based fertilizer, (iii) carbonaceous material, and/or (iv) industrial or even food grade carbon dioxide being off gassed from the reaction (Incomplete specification, abstract based on available information published by the patent office).
   USE - As hydrocarbon fraction chosen from (i) biogas, biofuels, biosolids, bio crude, or hydrocarbon (ii) silica, phosphates, potash and low concentration nitrogen based fertilizer, (iii) carbonaceous material, (iv) industrial or even food grade carbon dioxide being off gassed from the reaction, and/or (v) chemical compounds e.g. phenol, 4-methylphenol, 2-propenol, decane, heptadecane and ethanone.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrocarbon fraction is recovered at high yield in a cost-effective and environmentally-friendly manner without using clean water.

PN CN109704279-A; CN109704279-B
TI Method for converting hydrocarbons to produce synthesis gas, involves preheating raw material gas, mixing with circulating gas, preheating conversion inlet gas, introducing oxygen-containing gas into conversion reactor to convert inlet gas.
AU JIAN S
   ZHOU J
   LUO C
   DUAN Z
AE SICHUAN TIANYI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCC-C)
   SOUTHWEST CHEM IND RES & DESIGIN INST (CNCC-C)
GA 201942323Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method for converting hydrocarbons to produce synthesis gas, involves (1) preheating raw material gas, mixing with circulating gas returned by steam ejector to obtain conversion inlet gas, preheating conversion inlet gas and entering into conversion reactor, (2) introducing oxygen-containing gas into conversion reactor to convert inlet gas to perform hydrocarbon conversion reaction, (3) recovering the conversion outlet gas from conversion reactor by heat, diverting back portion of converted outlet gas to step (1) as a recycle gas by steam ejector, and using another portion of converted outlet gas by heat, and carrying out separation by steam and water.
   USE - Method for converting hydrocarbons to produce synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process does not use a hydrocarbon steam reforming reactor, and saves the heating fuel required for the steam reforming reaction. The hydrocarbon steam reforming reactors are eliminated, thus saving investment. The carbon dioxide cycle returned by the subsequent process is used as a portion of raw material gas, which reduces carbon dioxide emissions and precisely controls the hydrogen to carbon ratio in the synthesis gas. The reaction temperature control is achieved by reducing the steam consumption by partially converting the outlet gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system of carrying out process for conversion of hydrocarbons to produce synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material gas in step (1) is a mixture of hydrocarbon gas or hydrocarbon gas and carbon dioxide returned in subsequent process. The hydrocarbon gas is a hydrocarbon which is gaseous at normal temperature, or a liquid hydrocarbon which is gaseous after heating, or a mixed hydrocarbon gas which is gaseous after heating. The oxygen-containing gas in step (2) includes oxygen enrichment, pure oxygen, or air. Preferred Composition: The conversion water to carbon ratio of the conversion reactor is required to be 1.5 to 5.5. Preferred Process: The method for converting hydrocarbons to produce synthesis gas is carried out by (1) mixing hydrocarbon gas or hydrocarbon gas with carbon dioxide gas returned in the subsequent process, preheating to 300-550 degrees C, mixing with circulating gas returned by steam ejector to obtain converted inlet gas, preheating conversion inlet gas to 480-680 degrees C into conversion reactor, (2) introducing an oxygen-containing gas into conversion reactor to convert inlet gas to perform a hydrocarbon conversion reaction, controlling the flow rate of oxygen-containing gas that is passed, maintaining the temperature at outlet of conversion reactor at 850-1350 degrees C, controlling the steam ejector steam flow, maintaining steam injector power and controlling the water to carbon ratio of inlet of conversion reactor, maintaining the water to carbon ratio of 1.5-5.5, (3) the reforming outlet gas from the conversion reactor enters the steam generator to exchange heat to generate steam, lowering the temperature to about 700 degrees C, exchanging heat with conversion inlet gas, returning a portion of converted outlet gas to the conversion reactor as a recycle gas by steam ejector after temperature drops to 600 degrees C and other portion enters the hydrocarbon preheater for heat exchange, cooling converted outlet gas by heat recovery system and water cooler after heat exchange, and carrying out separation by steam and water.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  preparing the obtained blend sample in a mold of                  a molding machine, and then rapidly cooling and                  cooling the mold containing the sample of the                  blend on the cold press by the same pressure, and                  opening the mold, quenching the sheet; finally                  cutting the sheet with the sample machine to                  obtain the impact spline for the impact                  performance test. In the step (1), the time for                  introducing carbon dioxide gas is 2 minutes after                  the end of the feeding. The method further                  comprises organic modified montmorillonite                  (OMMT), the organic modifying agent is                  hydrogenated tallow dimethyl ammonium and                  siloxane, the PBAT and organic modified                  montmorillonite (OMMT) to conventional melt                  blending to obtain PBAT-OMMT material, then                  adding PBAT-OMMT, PLA and ADR into the chamber,                  and then infusing carbon dioxide gas, performing                  supercritical carbon dioxide-assisted mixing and                  melt blending; and the ratio of the PBAT to the                  OMMT mass fraction is 30:0.3-30:1.0,                  respectively. TI Chemical blowing agent used for foaming            thermoplastic polymer precursor, comprises            functionalized particulate bicarbonate comprising            additive(s) containing rosin acid and/or its            derivatives, preferably abietic acid, and rosin acid            ester.
AU KABBABE MALAVE J A
   CAVALIER K
   PASCAL J
   KABBABE M J A
   PASCAL J P
   MALAVE J A K
AE SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
GA 201909634P
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical blowing agent comprises a                functionalized particulate bicarbonate comprising                additive(s) comprising rosin acid and/or its                derivative and salts, preferably abietic acid,                dihydroabietic acid, neoabietic acid and/or rosin                acid ester.
   USE - Chemical blowing agent used for foaming                thermoplastic polymer precursor (claimed). Uses                include but are not limited to foamed wall papers,                artificial leather, floor coverings, wall                coverings, carpet backings, thermal insulation                materials, insulation sealants, footwear,                automotive components, cable insulation, and                packaging.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical blowing agent has excellent                flexibility, low decomposition rate, and provides                uniform structure of foamed composition with                desired yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) foamable polymer composition, which                comprises polymer, foaming stabilizer, and chemical                blowing agent;
   (2) manufacture of the polymer, which involves                heating the foamable polymer composition comprising                the functionalized particulate bicarbonate for                liberating carbon dioxide, and melting the polymer                at temperature above glass transition temperature                of the polymer for gelation time of 130 seconds or                less; and
   (3) manufacture of polyvinyl chloride foamed                polymer, which involves heating the polyvinyl                chloride foamable composition comprising the                functionalized particulate bicarbonate and                polyvinyl chloride resin at temperature suitable                for liberating carbon dioxide, and melting the                polyvinyl chloride at 190-210 degrees C, preferably                200-210 degrees C for gelation time of 90-120                seconds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer is                  polyvinyl chloride, polyurethane, or polyamide.                  The additive(s) comprises one or more components                  selected from polymers, amino acids and/or its                  derivatives and salts, inorganic salts, oils,                  fats, rosin acids and/or its derivatives and                  salts, fatty acids and/or its derivatives and                  salts, carboxylic or polycarboxylic acids, soaps,                  and/or waxes, preferably polymer(s) which is                  selected from polyoxyalkylene and/or its                  derivatives, polyethylene glycol,                  poly(meth)acrylate and/or its derivatives,                  polyvinyl alcohol, and polysaccharide. The                  chemical blowing agent further comprises compound                  (C) selected from carboxylic acid, or                  polycarboxylic acid and/or its derivatives. The                  foamable polymer composition is a foamable                  polyvinyl chloride plastisol composition.                  Preferred Composition: The functionalized                  particulate bicarbonate contains at least 50-100                  wt.%, preferably 65-100 wt.% bicarbonate                  component, and 0.01-50 wt.%, preferably 0.02-35                  wt.% additive.

PN CN109247190-A
TI New white Schizophyllum commune LZJ-5 strain            deposited in China National Culture Collection, and            deposit number of CCTCC NO: M 2018576.
AU MA B
   LI R
AE YUNNAN MUSHROOM WORLD BIO-TECHNOLOGY CO (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 201910523F
AB    NOVELTY - A white Schizophyllum commune LZJ-5 strain,                deposited in a China National Culture Collection,                CCTCC, the deposit date of August 29, 2018, and the                deposit number of CCTCC NO: M 2018576, is                new.
   USE - White Schizophyllum commune LZJ-5                strain.
   ADVANTAGE - The strain ensures stable yield and quality of                Schizophyllum commune.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                cultivation of Schizophyllum commune, which                involves preparing cultivation medium including                48-52% wood chips, 14-16% cotton seed shell, 22-24%                wheat bran, 6-8% maize flour, 1-3% rapeseed cake,                0.5-1.5% sugar, 0.5-1.5% light calcium carbonate,                0.5-1.5% gypsum, inoculating and mycelial culturing                by adding LZJ-5 liquid strain to the cultivation                medium, culturing in the dark at a temperature of                24 plus minus 1 degrees C and an air humidity of                68-72% for 7-9 days, inducing primordium for 2                days, at temperature of 22 plus minus 1 degrees C,                air humidity of 88-90%, carbon dioxide                concentration of 450-550 ppm, light for 28-32                minutes/hours, light intensity of 500 lx, growing                fruiting bodies for 10 days, at temperature of 22                plus minus 1 degrees C, air humidity of 89-91%,                carbon dioxide concentration of 550-650 ppm, light                for 10 minutes/hour, light intensity of 350 lx, in                the spores seven to eight minutes, and ejecting                spores when harvested.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is                  given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

TI Vehicle lithium battery pack, has mounting cylinder movably fixed with connecting rod, where end of connecting rod is connected with buffer spring and end of buffer spring is connected to inner wall of shell.
AU YE W
AE MENGKE ELECTRIC TRANSPORTATION TECHNOLOG (MENG-Non-standard)
GA 201902009F
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a vehicle lithium battery pack, comprising a shell, top of the shell is provided with a rectangular opening structure, the opening of the top of the shell is provided with a cover plate, a shell and a cover plate is provided with a fixing device; the upper surface of the cover plate two side are provided with wire outlet cover plate on the upper surface is provided with a carbon dioxide tank, carbon dioxide tank is connecting with an air outlet pipe, and the air outlet one end pipe runs through the cover plate in the shell, air outlet pipe is installed on one side of the electromagnetic valve, the shell of inner wall is set with a protective cover; one side of the shell inner wall is equipped with a control device; the shell bottom inner wall is equipped with a lithium battery pack, the two side inner wall is uniformly provided with damping rubber ring along the vertical direction, the vibration damping rubber ring is provided with a mounting cylinder. This utility model structure simple and novel design, which greatly improves the safety performance of the lithium battery in the process of using, low device production cost, obvious effect, and is worth to be popularized.

PN JP6442014-B1; JP2019051452-A
TI Processing apparatus comprises reaction tank for the liquid to be treated and a filtration device for the liquid to be treated discharged from the reaction tank, and the reaction tank is provided with a partitioning member.
AU NISHIMURA A
   WATANABE K
   SAWAME T
AE RYUKI ENG KK (RYUK-Non-standard)
GA 2018A16427
AB    NOVELTY - Processing apparatus comprises reaction tank (21) for the liquid to be treated and a filtration device (10) for the liquid to be treated discharged from the reaction tank. The reaction tank is provided with a partitioning member (22) for partitioning the interior of the reaction tank into a first region and a second region. The supply port (24) is provided for supplying the liquid to be processed to the first region, a blowing unit for blowing carbon dioxide into the first region, and a discharge port (25) for discharging the liquid to be treated from the second region. The liquid to be treated supplied from the supply port comes into contact with the carbon dioxide and flows along the partitioning material in the first region and then flows into the second region and is discharged from the discharge port.
   USE - Processing apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The partition member is tubular and is provided on the central bottom surface of the reaction tank and the supply port and the blowing unit are provided at the bottom of the first region apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a processing apparatus.
   Filtration device (10)
   Reaction tank (21)
   Partitioning member (22)
   Supply port (24)
   Discharge port (25)

PT P
PN CN108997689-A
TI Fully biodegradable gas phase anti-rust material used for metallic material and packaging of other commodity, artwork and chemicals, comprise bio-based resin, starch, gas phase anti-rust agent, auxiliary agent and plasticizing agent,.
AU ZHANG W
   ZHU C
   LI F
AE ZHANG W (ZHAN-Individual)
   ZHU C (ZHUC-Individual)
GA 2018A3569X
AB    NOVELTY - Fully biodegradable gas phase anti-rust material comprises 30-65 pts. wt. bio-based resin, 10-80 pts. wt. starch, 0.5-10 pts. wt. gas phase anti-rust agent, 0.5-10 pts. wt. auxiliary agent and 5-25 pts. wt. plasticizing agent. The bio-resin is polyvinyl alcohol, polylactic acid,polyhydroxybutyrate, polybutylene adipateterephthalate, polybutylene succinate, carbon dioxide polymer and polycaprolactone ester.
   USE - Fully biodegradable gas phase anti-rust material used for metallic material and packaging of other commodity, artwork and chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The fully biodegradable gas phase anti-rust material is eco-friendly and cost-effective, and solves the problem of white pollution caused by the non-degradability of gas phase anti-rust materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing fully biodegradable gas phase anti-rust material, which involves:
   (A) mixing bio-resin selected from polyvinyl alcohol, polylacticacid, polyhydroxybutyrate, polybutylene adipate terephthalate, polybutylenesuccinate, carbon dioxide polymer and polycaprolactone resin;
   (B) mixing and churning;
   (C) melting, extruding andpelletizing;
   (D) film blowing;
   (E) injection molding, film casting, where mixing stirring time is controlled at 10-60mim and temperature is 40-120 degrees C; and
   (F) melting, extruding and pelletizing at 40-220 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The gas phase antirusting agent is dioctyl formate, cyclohexane, o-nitro-ester sodium salt,benzotriazole, monoethanolamine, sodiumbenzoate, benzoic acid monoethanol amine, phyticacid, triethanolamine, fatty acid amine, cyclohexylamine, phosphate acid triethanolamine salt, borate,methenamine, sodium sebacate, laurate alcoholester acyl, hexamethylene tetramine and sodium tetraborate. The auxiliary agent comprises 0.5-10 pts. wt. bacteriostatic agent, 0.1-3 pts. wt. lubricant, 0.5-5 pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.1-10 pts. wt. nucleating agent and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. interface activating agent. The bacteriostat is selected from NM silverion zeolite, sodium alginate, trimethyl, triethyl andtri-n-butyl quaternary ammonium salt. The lubricantis selected from calcium stearate, zinc stearate, oleic acid amide, erucamide, hard acid amide and dimethicone. The antioxidant is selected from antioxidant pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4- hydroxyphenyl)propionate),antioxidant octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate, antioxidant 168, antioxidant 300, antioxidant 330, antioxidant 2,4,6-trinitrophenol, and antioxidant butylated hydroxytoluene. The nucleating agent selected from calciumphosphate, silicon dioxide, talcum powder and montmorillonite. the interface agent is selected from silane couplingagent, titanate coupling agent, boric acid ester coupling agent, sugar fatty acid ester, polyvinyl ether and lauryl ether. The plasticizer is selected from glycerol, propyleneglycol, glyceryl triacetate, polyalcohol, glycol,polyethylene glycol, formamide, acetamide, triethylcitrate, tributyl citrate, mono-stearic acid glyceride,N-hydroxyethyl formamide, hydroxyalkyl amide,two supporting dimethyl amide, fatty amide diol or glycerol triacetate. The gas phase antirust material made of gas phase anti-rust-prevention and damp-proof packing film. The gas phase rust-proof moisture-proof packaging film has a 1-5 layer structure, where a gas phase rustproof material having a different composition in a structure of different layers is used. The bio-based resin in the layer gas phase rustproof material is a hydrophobic base material.

PT P
PN CN108949666-A
TI Isolating firefly luminescent sac cells, by placing luminescent sac tissue in pancreatin, cutting tissue, digesting tissue in carbon dioxide incubator, and terminating digestion in culture medium containing fetal bovine serum.
AU LI N
   HUANG X
   ZHAO C
AE WUHAN MYHALIC BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A27556
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating firefly luminescent sac cells, involves placing the luminescent sac tissue in 80-120 mu l pancreatin, cutting the tissue, digesting the tissue in carbon dioxide incubator for 3-7 minutes, and terminating the digestion in Sf-900TMIII SFM culture medium containing 8-12% fetal bovine serum (FBS).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for cryopreservation of firefly luminescent sac cells, involves collecting two bottles of firefly luminescent sac cells in logarithmic growth phase by trypsinization, discarding the supernatant after centrifugation at 1000 g for 5 minutes, resuspending the cells in 1 ml Sf-900TMIII SFM medium containing 20% FBS and 10% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), placing the cells in cryotube, and placing the cryotubes in programmed cooling box at -80 degrees C for overnight under liquid nitrogen of 196 degrees C; and
   (2) method for recovering cryopreserved firefly luminescent sac cells, involves obtaining frozen tube from liquid nitrogen, rapidly thawing the cells in water bath of 37 degrees C, centrifuging the cells at 1000 g for 5 minutes, culturing the cells at 27 degrees C, attaching the cells to the wall, discarding the supernatant, replacing the culture solution, and culturing the cells.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The contacting step is                  carried out at 1-50 bar, 250-3000 degrees C,                  and/or gas hourly space velocity of 100-100000                  hour-1. Preferred Properties: The support                  material has honeycomb structure having hole                  number of 25-1600 cells per square inch, cross                  section of 150x 150 mm, hole diameter of 2-9 mm,                  wall thickness of 0.5-1.4 mm, catalyst pitch of                  10-2.5 mm, open porosity of 60-75%, and specific                  surface area of 10-1300 m2/g. The metal sulfide or metal                  oxysulfide comprises an alkaline earth metal,                  transition metal or post transition metal.                  Preferred Process: The process further involves                  coating the three dimensional structured support                  material with slurry comprising particulate                  support material and binder, heat-treating the                  coated material, and contacting the coated                  material with slurry comprising catalytically                  active metal to form catalytically active layer                  (204) on the coated material, and heat-treating                  the catalytically active metal coated                  material.

A dry hot rock body crack generating process                is performed.

AB    NOVELTY - Saline-alkali land with anti-corrosion based enamel protective layer comprises an enamel anti-corrosion cushion layer, a reinforced concrete base body and an enamel anti-corrosion layer, the enamel anti-corrosion cushion layer utilizes carbon dioxide plain concrete with high strength and high toughness shell is formed by paving further set with reinforced concrete base body, the reinforced concrete base body through C40 self-compacting concrete and reinforcing steel bar which is set with enamel anticorrosive layer, the enamel anticorrosive layer is made of high strength and high toughness of boron carbide-silicon carbide functional composite ceramic grain mortar is poured.
   USE - Used as saline-alkali land with anti-corrosion based enamel protective layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The enamel protective layer has good durability and corrosion resistance property, and prolongs service life of base.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for saline-alkali land with anti-corrosion based enamel protective layer comprising (i) carrying out excavation of foundation pit, preparing enamel anticorrosive cushion layer; (ii) performing first template die on enamelized anticorrosive cushion, binding reinforcing bars in base plate and column ribs, and pouring concrete foundation body; (iii) carrying out second branch outside concrete foundation body, pouring enamelized anti-corrosion layer; and (iv) drying enamelized anti-corrosive layer, and backfilling tamping pit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The enamel anticorrosive cushion layer in each stere concrete includes 403 kg common silicate cement, 480 kg high strength and high toughness shell gravel, 836 kg sand, 1140 kg stone, and 208 kg water. The high strength and high toughness shell is broken, and the shell enamel layer is 80% intact. The shell thickness is greater than 1.5 mm, and the compressive strength is higher than 60 MPa with particle size of 10-20 mm. The reinforced concrete foundation body is having concrete with 450 kg P.O42.5 ordinary Portland cement, 810 kg sand, 1120 kg stone, 180 kg water, 6.3 kg water reducing agent, and 0.063 kg thickening agent. The coarse sand particle size in sand is 2.5 mm. The fine sand particle size is 0.7 mm with fineness modulus of 2.3-2.9. The stone has a particle size of 10-20 mm. The enamelized anticorrosive layer includes high-strength and high-toughness boron carbide-silicon carbide functional composite ceramic particle mortar group with anti-corrosion mortar having 400 kg P.O52.5 ordinary Portland cement, 25 kg silicon powder, 250 kg sand, 750 kg ceramsite, 35 kg polypropylene powder, 1.5 kg polypropylene fiber, 15 kg boron carbide powder, 175 kg water, 4 kg water reducing agent. The silicon powder includes silicone dioxide powder with particle size of 0.1-0.3 mu m, the ceramsite is processed from a high strength and high toughness boron carbide-silicon carbide boron carbide-silicon carbide functional composite ceramic with diameter of 0.25-2 mm using continuous grading, where the shape of ceramsite is icosahedron with rough surface, the polypropylene fiber having a fineness of 10-18 dtex. The fiber length is 3-60 mm. The polypropylene powder is having a particle diameter of 50-80 nm. The boron carbide powder is having a particle diameter of 50-60 nm. The enamelized anticorrosive cushion layer is having a thickness of 100-150 mm. The thickness of enamelized anticorrosive layer is 10-25 mm. The enamel anticorrosive cushion with multiple bottom concrete blocks are arranged between reinforced concrete foundation bodies.
