TI Self-contained electrical power generation system for producing liquid fuel comprises hydrocarbon source, fuel cell unit configured to output anode exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide, and reformer configured to output syngas.
AU JAHNKE F C
   LAMBRECH M
AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 201819411G
AB    NOVELTY - A self-contained electrical power generation system for producing liquid fuel (400) comprises hydrocarbon source; fuel cell unit (300) configured to output anode exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide (CO2); and reformer (200) configured to receive 1st hydrocarbon feed portion from hydrocarbon source and anode exhaust gas from fuel cell unit. Reformer is configured to output a syngas having a first hydrogen/carbon monoxide (H2/CO) ratio of less than or equal to 2:1.
   USE - A self-contained electrical power generation system for producing liquid fuel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell unit can be sized to produce power equivalent to what the balance of plant, making the self-contained electrical power generation system ideal for stranded natural gas locations since the gas will be converted to methanol, dimethyl ether, or other high value liquid chemicals for easy transportation, and no outside electrical power will be required at the site. The fuel cell unit provides low cost power to the reformer, even if a grid is unavailable.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the gas flow within the fuel cell unit and between the fuel cell unit and the steam methane reformer.
   Reformer (200)
   Fuel cell unit (300)
   Anode (312)
   Anode gas oxidizer combustor (314)
   Liquid fuel (400)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: Hydrocarbon source is natural gas from production gas well, pipeline, and/or biogas comprising a methane source. Liquid fuel is methanol, where hydrocarbon source is a natural gas source, and reformer is a steam methane reformer configured to receive a natural gas feed from the natural gas source. Preferred Parameter: Reformer is configured to operate at a temperature outside of the range of 30-250 degrees C. Preferred Devices: Anode of fuel cell unit is configured to receive a 2nd hydrocarbon feed portion from hydrocarbon source. Fuel cell unit is further configured to receive 1st portion of CO2 output by the reformer. The system further comprises anode gas oxidizer combustor configured to receive 2nd portion of CO2 output by the reformer, where cathode of fuel cell unit is configured to receive CO2 output by the anode gas oxidizer combustor. Fuel cell unit comprises direct molten carbonate fuel cell (MCFC) configured to operate at 600-700 degrees C. Fuel cell unit comprises a solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) configured to operate at 800-1000 degrees C. MCFC is configured to output anode exhaust gas comprising 20-40% (H2+CO) and 60-80% CO2, syngas is configured to receive (H2+CO) output from anode exhaust gas, and cathode of MCFC is configured to receive CO2 from CO2 separated from syngas, CO2 output by anode gas oxidizer combustor, or CO2-containing flue gas. SOFC is configured to output anode exhaust gas comprising 40-60% (H2+CO) and 40-60% CO2, where syngas is configured to receive (H2+CO) output from anode exhaust gas, and reformer is further configured to receive recycled CO2 separated from the syngas. Preferred Processes: Syngas is converted to liquid fuel comprising methanol and/or Fischer-Tropsch liquid. Anode exhaust gas is added to 1st hydrocarbon feed portion such that the reformer is configured to receive the 1st hydrocarbon feed portion and the anode exhaust gas as a single stream.

PT P
PN CN107557335-A
TI Preparing chimeric antigen receptor-natural killer (NK) cells used for targeting small cell lung cancer, by isolating NK cells from peripheral blood, culturing, transfecting, culturing and preparing small cell lung cancer cell preparation.
AU ZHANG Z
AE ANHUI BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201804783W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-natural killer (NK) cells, involves collecting peripheral blood of patients, isolating NK cells, adding in NK culture medium, adjusting cell concentration to 3-5x 105, maintaining the cells in carbon dioxide incubator, inoculating into fresh culture medium, adjusting the cell concentration to 1-2x 105, culturing, supplementing the culture medium once in every three days, performing Lentivirus-based transfection of the NK cells, performing amplification culture and preparing small cell lung cancer cell preparation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing CAR-NK cells which are used for targeting small cell lung cancer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-natural killer (NK) cells, involves (a) collecting peripheral blood of patients and isolating NK cells by Ficoll separation method, (b) adding the NK cells in NK culture medium, adjusting cell concentration to 3-5x 105, and maintaining the cells in 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) incubator at 37 degrees C for 48-72 hours, (c) inoculating the collected cells into fresh culture medium, adjusting the cell concentration to 1-2x 105, continuously culturing for 12-15 days, and supplementing the culture medium once in every three days, (d) performing Lentivirus-based transfection of the NK cells and performing amplification culture, and (e) preparing small cell lung cancer cell preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves collecting 100 ml peripheral blood of patients, placing in a blood collection tube containing anticoagulant, adding 100 ml physiological saline to dilute the blood, adding diluted blood to Ficoll lymphocyte separation fluid in a volume ratio of 1:2, centrifuging at 1500-2000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 20 minutes, and separating cells by adding 5 mmol/l pectin methylesterase (PME) and maintaining at room temperature for 40 minutes. The step (e) involves mixing human serum albumin and trypsin in a ratio of 2:1, adding the CAR-NK cells into the mixture, and diluting with physiological saline to obtain small cell lung cancer cell preparation. Preferred Components: The NK culture medium is added with cytokine serum-free medium AIMV, 1000 U/ml interleukin (IL)-2, 10 ng/ml IL-12 and 10% human AB serum.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN WO2021246313-A1; JP2021187721-A; JP2021187720-A
TI Utilizing carbon dioxide in cement production exhaust gas for generating methane, by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production or separating and recovering carbon dioxide from exhaust gas to generate methane, and using methane as substitute fuel for fossil fuel.
AU TAKAYAMA Y
   KAWASAKI H
   KOMA T
   KOMATSU T
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 2021E02887
AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in cement production exhaust gas, involves adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility or separating and recovering carbon dioxide from exhaust gas to generate methane, and using methane as substitute fuel for fossil fuel.
   USE - Method for utilizing carbon dioxide in cement production exhaust gas for generating methane from exhaust gas of cement manufacturing facilities and the generated methane is used as alternative fuel for fossil fuels, such as coal, petroleum, and liquefied natural gas used in thermal power plants, oil refining facilities, natural gas refining facilities, waste incineration facilities such as garbage, fuel cells, and various industrial facilities, and city gas alternatives, and used as alternative fuel in ordinary households, and for reducing carbon dioxide originating from energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method suppresses the release of carbon dioxide from combustion exhaust gas to atmosphere, reduces use of fossil fuels, reduces the carbon dioxide originating from energy, reduces the greenhouse gases, and enhances the greenhouse gas reduction effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for utilization of carbon dioxide in waste gas from cement production, comprising cement production facility with methanation device for generating methane by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in waste gas from cement production facility or carbon dioxide separated and recovered from waste gas, and a methane supply device for supplying methane as alternative fuel for fossil fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves forming carbon dioxide by separating and recovering exhaust gas from cement production facility by contacting exhaust gas with carbon dioxide absorbent. The method involves directly adding hydrogen to exhaust gas for producing methane. Preferred Device: The device for separating and recovering carbon dioxide is provided for separating and recovering carbon dioxide by contacting exhaust gas from cement production facility with carbon dioxide absorbent. The methanation apparatus involves directly adding hydrogen to exhaust gas for producing methane.

PT P
PN CN112337448-A
TI Solid amine hollow fiber for removing low-concentration carbon dioxide, comprises large number of active amine groups, gas containing carbon dioxide passes through wall, where amine group in fiber chemically react with carbon dioxide.
AU TAN X
   ZHANG J
   ZHENG X
   WANG C
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   SUISHAN NINGBO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUIS-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI SUISHAN IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202118121G
AB    NOVELTY - Solid amine hollow fiber comprises large number of active amine groups at room temperature, gas containing carbon dioxide passes through hollow fiber porous tube wall, where the active amine group in the hollow fiber quickly chemically react with carbon dioxide, trapping the carbon dioxide in the hollow fiber, and the hollow fiber after adsorbing the carbon dioxide can be heated to resolve the carbon dioxide to achieve a solid amine hollow regeneration of fiber.
   USE - Solid amine hollow fiber used for removing low-concentration carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid amine hollow fiber has large specific surface area, high adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide and fast adsorption speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a solid amine hollow fiber, which involves preparing non-solvent-containing metastable organic polymer spinning solution, preparing porous hollow fibers by the steam-immersion induced phase inversion method, and applying the solvent replacement method to dry the hollow fiber membranes and preparing organic amine solution, large number of active amine groups are grafted into the porous tube wall of the hollow fiber by a grafting reaction to obtain product; and
   (2) a method for using solid amine hollow fiber, which involves passing the gas containing carbon dioxide into one end of the hollow fiber membrane and making it flow out from the other end of the hollow fiber membrane, adsorbing carbon dioxide by amine groups to achieve the purpose of removing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous hollow fiber polymer used is polyetherimide, polyacrylonitrile, polyimide-imide, polyimide methyl methacrylate and poly-2-methenylbutyrolactone. The pore former used is polyvinylpyrrolidone, poloxamer, polyethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, ethylene glycol monomethyl ether. The organic amine grafted with active amine group used is ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine and polyethylene amines. The solvent used is one of N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone and dimethylsulfoxide. Preferred Conditions: The mass percentage of the high molecular polymer in the prepared spinning solution is 10-25%, and the mass percentage of the pore former is 0-20%. The mass percentage concentration of organic amine in the prepared graft amine solution is 10-50%.

GA 2019197134
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a fan coupled to an air inlet portion of a filter housing (101). An elongated duct includes a set of holes on a lower surface of the elongated duct, and is coupled to an outlet portion of the filter housing. A filter is arranged between the air inlet portion and the outlet portion of the filter housing. A carbon dioxide input is coupled to the outlet portion of the filter housing. The fan directs ambient air into the filter housing, where filtered air mixed with carbon dioxide flows through the holes in the elongated duct to plants (211) growing under the elongated duct. A light ballast (145) is coupled to a lower surface of the filter housing.
   USE - Apparatus for dispersing carbon dioxide gas to stacked shelves in a vertical gardening system.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus utilizes the fan with the filter to clean the air and to circulate the air with less space to be used and lower energy costs. The apparatus utilizes a carbon dioxide controller to maintain a predetermined carbon dioxide level by decreasing the carbon dioxide flow when the detected carbon dioxide level is high and to increase the carbon dioxide flow when the detected carbon dioxide level is low. The apparatus eliminates warm pockets of air by providing concentrated air movement, carbon dioxide dispersion, and filtration.

TI High pressure carbon dioxide miscible mixed-phase driving method, involves injecting degassing crude oil into oil layer of slug as mixing phase such that carbon dioxide is injected to realize carbon dioxide mixed-phase driver.
AU LIU Y
   ZHAO G
   YANG Q
   ZHANG J
   WU Y
   SUN W
   BAI W

   WANG Y
   XIAO L
   YANG W
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   DAQING OILFIELD LTD CO (CNPC-C)
GA 201948931U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting formation temperature and planning phase pressure mixed with a carbon dioxide degassing crude oil. A degassing crude oil and a block-stratum crude oil are formed with a low colloidal asphaltene content. The degassing crude oil is injected into an oil layer of a slug as mixing phase such that carbon dioxide is injected to realize carbon dioxide mixed-phase driver.
   USE - High pressure carbon dioxide miscible mixed-phase driving method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing lowest phase pressure of carbon dioxide to tank oil slug, which is convenient to recycle and avoids difficult of solid phase deposition.

A self-moving system comprises an infrared distance sensor, a mobile trolley (1), an automatic lifter (10) and a hydraulic support frame. An automatic spraying system comprises a pressure gauge, a foam nozzle (2), a bubble conveying flow control pipeline, a speed adjusting valve, where the flame sensor is selected as a purple infrared dual-flame sensor, and contains narrow band filter function working at central frequency wavelength of 4.4 m. The flame sensor performs integral operation by a microprocessor with a high performance and advanced mathematical algorithm model.
   USE - Moving frame working surface anti-spark foam spraying and processing equipment for use in coal bed water injection and anti-extinguishing technical field.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment synthesizes proper foam by processing a foam production system, and generates spark or smoke when the hydraulic bracket moves in friction, and transmits a signal to the foam production system and the foam automatic spraying system through the flame sensor or the smoke sensor in the flame detection system, so as to real-timely track moving direction and distance to extinguish the spark or smoke by the infrared distance sensor on the self-moving system during moving process of the hydraulic bracket, thus preventing fire accident of the underground working surface. The equipment selects the flame sensor as a purple infrared dual-flame sensor, where the flame sensor contains narrow band filter function working at the central frequency wavelength realize integral operation by the microprocessor with high performance and advanced mathematical algorithm model, so that radiation spectrum that conforms to the flame characteristics identified as a flame signal when the false flame signals formed by other interference factors is excluded.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a moving frame working surface anti-spark foam spraying and processing method.

PN CN114240181-A
TI Carbon emission assessment method based on complementary process of fishing and light, involves calculating carbon absorption amount according to carbon dioxide absorbed by photosynthesis of aquatic plants and algae in pond and offset carbon emission in photovoltaic power generation.
AU ZHANG T
   XIAO Q
   XU H
AE SHENZHEN RUNKE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022492447
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing (S102) a carbon emission evaluation model in the process of fishery and light complementation, the carbon emission evaluation model includes a carbon emission calculation model and a carbon absorption calculation model. The carbon emission calculation model calculates (S104) the carbon emission according to pond disinfection, water ecological cultivation, production and use of feed and medicine, pond pumping and oxygenation, daily maintenance of photovoltaic panels and aquaculture water treatment. The carbon absorption calculation model calculates (S106) the carbon absorption amount according to the carbon dioxide absorbed by the photosynthesis of aquatic plants and algae in the pond and the offset carbon emission in photovoltaic power generation. The carbon emission assessment model obtains (S108) the net carbon emission according to the difference between the carbon emission and the carbon absorption.
   USE - Carbon emission assessment method based on complementary process of fishing and light that is utilized for cultivating fresh water fish and shrimp in an aquaculture field.
   ADVANTAGE - The evaluation of the energy consumption and carbon emission of each breeding link is realized. The weak links of energy saving and emission reduction are found, and provide data support for the optimization of carbon reduction in the aquaculture industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. a carbon emission assessment system based on the complementary process of fishing and light;
   2. a computer device; and
   3. a computer readable storage medium storing programs for the carbon emission assessment method based on complementary process of fishing and light.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the carbon emission assessment method based on complementary process of fishing and light. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Step for establishing a carbon emission evaluation model in the process of fishery and light complementation, the carbon emission evaluation model includes a carbon emission calculation model and a carbon absorption calculation model (S102)
   Step for calculating the carbon emission according to pond disinfection, water ecological cultivation, production and use of feed and medicine, pond pumping and oxygenation, daily maintenance of photovoltaic panels and aquaculture water treatment (S104)
   Step for calculating the carbon absorption amount according to the carbon dioxide absorbed by the photosynthesis of aquatic plants and algae in the pond and the offset carbon emission in photovoltaic power generation (S106)
   Step for obtaining the net carbon emission according to the difference between the carbon emission and the carbon absorption (S108)
   Step for establishing a carbon emission coefficient data table, according to the parameters involved in the calculation process of the carbon emission calculation model and the carbon absorption calculation model and the corresponding carbon emission coefficient (S110)

PN CN114133996-A
TI Environment-friendly intelligent brewing system comprises a cloud control module and a raw material mixing module connected with the cloud control module, an anaerobic expansion module, and constant temperature saccharification module.
AU ZHONG J
AE JIANGSU YILU INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202244351F
AB    NOVELTY - Environment-friendly intelligent brewing system comprises a cloud control module and a raw material mixing module connected with the cloud control module at the same time, an anaerobic expansion module, a constant temperature saccharification module, a constant temperature fermentation module, a waste gas collecting and processing module, a waste processing module and a wine outlet module. The cloud control module is used for controlling the whole process of the brewing system from feeding to the wine outlet, realizing remote control. The raw material mixing module is connected with the feeding port of the anaerobic swelling module. The constant temperature saccharification module is connected with the discharging end of the anaerobic swelling module. The constant temperature fermentation module is connected with the discharging end of the constant temperature saccharification module.
   USE - Environment-friendly intelligent brewing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The environment-friendly intelligent brewing system combines the modern process control technology with the traditional brewing process, applying cloud control technology, realizing automation and unmanned brewing process; by collecting the carbon dioxide gas can reduce the discharge of the carbon dioxide gas to zero, and the other environment-friendly processing of the gas after discharging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an intelligent brewing technique, which involves mixing hulls of sorghum, rice, sticky rice, wheat, corn, and jujube according to mass ratio of 30: 22: 18: 16: 8: 6, through the video collecting system dynamic for monitoring the mixing process, ensuring that the sorghum, rice in the mixed material without flat, ntroducing the mixed raw material into the anaerobic expansion module, heating to 55-60degrees Celsius, keeping the temperature for 2 to 3 hours, introducing the expanded raw material into the constant temperature saccharification module, keeping the temperature at 65degrees Celsius, introducing steam, saccharifying time is 24 to 36 hours, ntroducing the saccharified raw material into a constant temperature fermentation module for pasting, after pasting, directly cooling to 37degrees Celsius, after stirring for 4 hours, standing for 7 days to 40 days, collecting the fermentation gas generated to the waste gas collecting and processing module, and separating the carbon dioxide gas in the waste gas to be stored separately, discharging the residual gas after removing the smell, finally starting the wine outlet module according to the requirement, finishing the production of finished wine, dry-wet separating the vinasse generated, compressing the separated dry vinasse into cake shape, ensuring the water content of the cake-shaped wine lees is less than 20 %, refrigerating the separated lees residual liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an environment-friendly intelligent brewing system arrangement diagram. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN114016074-A; CN114016074-B
TI Preparing high load amount transition metal            single-atom carbon-based catalyst for preparing carbon            monoxide, involves adding concentrated ammonia water            into zinc ion water solution, and carrying out            hydrothermal reaction.
AU LEI L
   LIU H
   YANG B
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202228499Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-load amount of transition metal                single-atom carbon-based catalyst involves                preparing special crystal face exposed zinc oxide                template by hydrothermal synthesis method, adding                concentrated ammonia water to the water solution of                the transition metal salt, where the molar ratio of                the zinc oxide and the metal salt is 99.5:0.5-97:3,                starting dropping timing, dipping for 0.5 to 2                hours, filtering, washing and drying to obtain a                transition metal-single-atom load of zinc oxide.                The zinc oxide exposed by the special crystal                surface is a non-polarity crystal surface. The                transition metal is loaded with zinc oxide,                2-methylimidazole, argon gas as a carrier gas, and                reacting at a temperature of 600-1000oC for about                0.25-2 hours under a temperature condition to                obtain the final product.
   USE - For preparation of high-load transition metal                single-atom carbon-based catalyst used for                preparing carbon monoxide used in coal, fossil                fuels such as oil and natural gas and related                industrial products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high active site exposure                ratio and high atom utilization rate. The catalyst                can realize directional catalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide under low voltage, exhibits                excellent selectivity and stability, and controls                the exposed special crystal face of the zinc oxide                template by adjusting the hydrothermal condition                not only can effectively control the dispersion of                the iron atom in the dipping process, but also can                promote the reaction rate of zinc oxide and                2-methyl imidazole at high temperature, which is                good for the single atom state dispersion on the                carbon base.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for utlizing carbon-based catalyst loaded by                transition metal single atom for use in                electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide as                carbon monoxide, involving:
   a. utilizing Silver/Silver chloride electrode                as reference electrode, Pt column is used as                counter electrode carbon-based catalyst loaded by                transition metal single atom according to claim 6                is used as raw material to prepare working                electrode and electrolyte solution is 0.1-0.5 M                KHCO3 solution and dissolving saturated state for                carbon dioxide and performing constant voltage                piezoelectric catalytic reduction in the range of                0.84-1.44 V.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a non-polar crystal surface                exposed zinc oxide in Example 1 of the present                invention, zinc oxide of the load and the                carbon-based catalyst of the appearance pattern of                the - based catalyst and the element distribution                of the carbon-based catalyst of the oxide and the -                based catalyst load of the metal load.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mol ratio of the                  zinc oxide to the transition metal ion is                  99:1.The mol ratio of the zinc oxide loaded by                  the transition metal single atom to the 2-methyl                  imidazole is 1:2:4.Preferred Components: The transition metal                  is one or combination of iron, cobalt and                  nickel.

PT P
PN CN113667541-A; CN113667541-B
TI Preparing essential oil microcapsule, comprises: preparing primary essential oil, modifying essential oil, preparing oil phase, preparing aqueous phase, preparing primary essential oil microcapsule, and post-processing.
AU DAI J
   LI H
   WANG L
   GUO H
   SHENG W
   ZHENG S
AE WEIFANG JIAYIJIA BIO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WEIF-Non-standard)
GA 2021D7631X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing essential oil microcapsule, comprises: preparing primary essential oil, modifying essential oil, preparing oil phase, preparing aqueous phase, preparing primary essential oil microcapsule, and post-processing. In the preparation of primary essential oil, mixing the fresh orange peel and fresh Origanum, vacuum drying, drying, crushing to 80-90 meshes; obtaining the crushed orange peel powder and Origanum powder; then placing the crushed orange peel powder and Origanum powder into the extraction kettle; adding anhydrous ethanol; then performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; controlling the extraction pressure, temperature and time, obtaining liquid extract after extraction; then vacuum distilling and concentrating the liquid extract, obtaining the concentrated solution; adding carvacrol and nano boron nitride into the concentrated solution; controlling the concentrated solution; performing mechanical oscillation, obtaining primary essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing essential oil microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the micro-capsule pore, prevent essential oil volatilization in the essential oil microcapsule at the same time, improve the release of the effective component in use, and reduce the essential oil irritation to the skin in the essential oil micro-capsule.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing essential oil microcapsule, comprises: preparing primary essential oil, modifying essential oil, preparing oil phase, preparing aqueous phase, preparing primary essential oil microcapsule, and post-processing. In the preparation of primary essential oil, mixing the fresh orange peel and fresh Origanum according to mass ratio of 1:2-3 uniformly, vacuum drying, controlling the vacuum degree at 0.07-0.08 MPa, drying at 50-60degrees Celsius for 1-1.5 hours, crushing to 80-90 meshes; obtaining the crushed orange peel powder and Origanum powder; then placing the crushed orange peel powder and Origanum powder into the extraction kettle; adding anhydrous ethanol of 3-4 times mass; then performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; controlling the extraction pressure at 25-30 MPa, extraction temperature at 40-45degrees Celsius; the extracting time is 2-2.5 hours, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 120-130 kg/hour, obtaining liquid extract after extraction; then vacuum distilling and concentrating the liquid extract, controlling the temperature of the vacuum distilling at 35-40degrees Celsius, vacuum distilling at pressure of 0.07-0.075 MPa for 0.5-1 hour to obtain the concentrated solution; then adding carvacrol and nano boron nitride into the concentrated solution; controlling the concentrated solution; the mass ratio of carvacrol and nano-boron nitride is 50-60: 6-8: 1-2: 1-2, then performing mechanical oscillation, controlling the rotating speed of the mechanical oscillation is 200-210 revolutions/minute (rpm), the mechanical oscillation time is 45-50 minutes; after mechanical oscillation, obtaining primary essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The essential oil is modified; the primary essential oil and the nano zeolite powder are mixed uniformly and then added to the high shearing reaction kettle; the shearing speed of the high shearing reaction kettle is set to 2000-2500 rpm, shearing for 30-40 minutes, adding diallyl dimethyl ammonium chloride, polyvinylamine and tea polyphenol; controlling the shearing speed to 2500-3000 rpm, continuously shearing for 40-50 minutes to obtain the modified essential oil. In the essential oil modifying step, primary essential oil, nano zeolite powder, diallyl dimethyl ammonium chloride, polyvinyl amine and tea polyphenol are in mass ratio of 30-40:30-40: 20-23: 1-2: 1-2: 3-5. The oil phase is prepared by mixing the modified essential oil with xanthan gum and then adding n-hexadecane, then stirring at a speed of 100-120 rpm, and carrying out radiation modification by using a medium-energy electron beam, controlling the energy of the middle energy electron beam as 3-4 MeV, the radiation dose is 200-250 kGy, stopping stirring and radiating after 30-40 minutes to obtain the oil phase. In step of preparing oil phase, the mass ratio of the modified essential oil, xanthan gum and n-hexadecane is 10-12: 1-2: 50-60. In preparing aqueous phase, the sodium dodecyl sulfate and amphoteric ion polyacrylamide are mixed uniformly and then dissolved in the deionized water to obtain the aqueous phase. The mass ratio of sodium dodecyl sulfate, amphoteric ion polyacrylamide and de-ionized water in the step of preparing aqueous phase is 5-8: 2-4: 25-30: 25-30. In preparing primary essential oil microcapsule; the oil phase and the aqueous phase are mixed and then added to the high shearing reaction kettle; the temperature of the high shearing reaction kettle is set to be 30-35degrees Celsius; cutting speed is set to 2000-2500 rpm, cutting for 30-40 minutes, adding polyethylene glycol 6000 and diethylenetriamine in the high shearing reaction kettle, then the temperature of the high shearing reaction kettle is set to 35-40degrees Celsius, the cutting speed is set to 2500- 3000 rpm; cutting for 25-30 minutes, stopping high cutting; setting the temperature of the high shearing reaction kettle to 50-55degrees Celsius, allowing to stand for 45-60 minutes to obtain the reactant, filtering the reactant, then using de-ionized water to wash for 2-3 times to obtain primary essential oil microcapsule. In the step of preparing primary essential oil microcapsule, the mass ratio of oil phase, aqueous phase, polyethylene glycol 6000, diethylene triamine is 20-22: 60-70: 2-5: 1-3. In the post-treatment, the primary essential oil microcapsule is placed in the nitrogen atmosphere, the pressure of the nitrogen is controlled at 0.15 -0.18 MPa, the temperature is increased to 40-45 degrees Celsius; then spraying the primary essential oil microcapsule surface after processing liquid; controlling the mass ratio of the primary essential oil microcapsule and the postprocessing liquid is 1:1.4-1.6, spraying speed is 30-35 kg/h, spraying the micro-current processing, controlling the current at 4-6 uA, after finishing spraying, stopping the micro-current; obtaining the primary essential oil microcapsule after spraying; putting the primary essential oil microcapsule after spraying in the oven to dry; controlling the drying temperature to be 40-45degrees Celsius; the drying time is 2-2.5 hours, and drying to obtain the essential oil microcapsule.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN113651342-A
TI Producing lithium product by processing lepidolite            using nitric acid atmospheric pressure method comprises            mixing lepidolite with water, calcining, adding nitric            acid, calcium oxide or magnesium oxide, introducing            carbon dioxide for carbonization and filtering to            obtain lithium carbonate.
AU LIU Y
   SONG S
   GAO B
   JIN C
   DAN Y
   HE Y
   ZHAO L
AE SICHUAN SHUNYING POWER BATTERY MATERIALS (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D63476
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) mixing lepidolite with                water, calcining, (ii) crushing calcined material,                uniformly mixing crushed material with water,                adding nitric acid to perform atmospheric pressure                leaching, filtering obtained solid-liquid mixture,                washing, obtaining leaching liquid and leaching                residue, (iii) adding calcium oxide or magnesium                oxide into the leaching residue obtained in step                (ii), adjusting the pH value, filtering to obtain                filtrate I and filter residue I, (iv) adding                calcium oxide or magnesium oxide into the filtrate                I, filtering to obtain the filtrate II and filter                residue II, (v) evaporating and crystallizing to                obtain lithium hydroxide monohydrate product and                crystallization mother liquor, (vi) concentrating,                calcining, washing to obtain mixed solution                containing lithium hydroxide and potassium                hydroxide and calcium oxide or magnesium oxide                particles, introducing carbon dioxide for                carbonization reaction, washing, filtering, and                drying to obtain lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for processing lepidolite                to produce lithium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses atmospheric pressure acid to                extract lithium; ensures low temperature and low                pressure; has no danger coefficient; can recycle                the acid-alkali medium; improves large slag yield                in the traditional production process; is                economical; increases the product additional value;                ensures the maximization of resource utilization;                is simple; has low device dependency; and is easy                to realize industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method comprises (i) uniformly mixing                lepidolite with water, calcining for 2-3 hours at                900-1300degrees Celsius, (ii) crushing the calcined                material, uniformly mixing the crushed material                with water, then adding nitric acid to perform                atmospheric pressure leaching, filtering the                obtained solid-liquid mixture after finishing                reaction, washing, obtaining leaching liquid and                leaching residue, (iii) adding calcium oxide or                magnesium oxide into the leaching residue obtained                in step (ii), adjusting the pH value to 3.5-6.0,                fully stirring at 40-80degrees Celsius, finishing                reaction, allowing to stand, filtering to obtain                filtrate I and filter residue I, (iv) adding                calcium oxide or magnesium oxide into the filtrate                I, adjusting the pH value to 10-14, fully stirring                at 40-80degrees Celsius, allowing to stand,                filtering to obtain the filtrate II and filter                residue II, (v) evaporating and crystallizing the                filtrate II obtained in step (iv) to obtain                LiOH.H2O product and crystallization mother liquor,                (vi) concentrating the crystallized mother liquid                obtained in step (v), calcining, washing the                calcined product by water to obtain mixed solution                containing lithium hydroxide and potassium                hydroxide and calcium oxide or magnesium oxide                particles, introducing carbon dioxide into the                mixed solution for carbonization reaction, then                washing, filtering; drying the obtained filter                residue to obtain Li2CO3 product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the method for processing lepidolite to produce                lithium carbonate (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid-solid ratio                  of the mixed lepidolite and water in the step (i)                  is 1:0.1-0.5. The liquid-solid ratio of the                  mixture of the crushed material and water in the                  step (ii) is 2.5-6: 1. The nitric acid in step                  (ii) is concentrated nitric acid with                  concentration of higher than 60 wt.%. The amount                  of nitric acid added is based on 100-150% of the                  theoretical amount required for the reaction of                  nitric acid with the elements iron, manganese,                  aluminum, and lithium in the lepidolite.Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises                  crushing calcined material until the average                  grain diameter of less than 48 microns. The step                  (ii) comprises leaching at 50-100degrees Celsius                  for 1-5 hours. The step (iii) comprises stirring                  for 0.5-4 hours. The step (iv) comprises stirring                  for 0.5-4 hours. The step (v) comprises                  crystallizing for 30-60 hours. The step (vi)                  comprises calcining at 500-700degrees Celsius,                  where the calcined product comprises potassium                  oxide, calcium oxide or magnesium oxide and                  lithium oxide solid mixture and nitrogen dioxide,                  and filtering to obtain the filtrate comprising                  potassium hydroxide solution. The step (vi)                  further comprises preparing the nitrogen dioxide                  in the calcined product for preparing nitric                  acid, returning the prepared nitric acid to the                  step (ii) to be used circularly, returning the                  calcium oxide or magnesium oxide obtained in the                  calcining product to the step (iii) and step (iv)                  to be used circularly, filtering to obtain                  potassium hydroxide filtrate, which is used for                  preparing potash fertilizer product.

PN CN112875838-A
TI Supercritical oxidation wastewater treatment            method by adding wastewater and oxidant into batching            tank, mixing, pressurizing tank, and adding mixture to            supercritical water reactor, preheating reactor, and            pumping pressurized wastewater.
AU ZHAN S
AE HANGZHOU ANJIXING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION              TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2021648465
AB    NOVELTY - A supercritical oxidation wastewater treatment                method includes adding wastewater and oxidant into                batching tank, mixing, pressurizing batching tank,                and adding pressurized mixed oxidant and wastewater                into supercritical water reactor; preheating                supercritical water reactor, high-pressure pumping                pressurized wastewater, heating the reactor,                installing oxygenation device, feeding oxygen into                the reactor, and adding to supercritical water                oxidation reactor together with the passing                wastewater; and adding preheated wastewater into                the reactor, filling the reactor with preheated                oxygen, pressurizing, heating, oxidizing to                desalinate the wastewater, flowing into                supercritical water oxidation reactor, controlling                system pressure, performing supercritical water                oxidation reaction to degrade pollutants in                wastewater, adding clean high-temperature and                high-pressure liquid to heater, and heat exchanging                with wastewater and oxidant respectively.
   USE - The method is for supercritical oxidation                wastewater treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the separation and                recovery of solids and liquid in the reaction                process by controlling the pressure and                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A supercritical oxidation wastewater treatment                method comprises:
   (A) adding wastewater and oxidant into                batching tank, mixing, pressurizing batching tank                which has two liquid outlets with different                heights, and adding pressurized mixed oxidant and                wastewater into supercritical water reactor;
   (B) preheating supercritical water reactor,                high-pressure pumping pressurized wastewater,                heating the reactor, installing oxygenation device,                feeding oxygen into the reactor according to the                amount of oxidant required to preheat it to 300                degrees C, and adding to supercritical water                oxidation reactor together with the passing                wastewater;
   (C) adding preheated wastewater into the                reactor from the outlet of heater, filling the                reactor with preheated oxygen, pressurizing,                heating, oxidizing to desalinate the wastewater,                flowing into supercritical water oxidation reactor                from both ends, controlling system pressure by back                pressure valve, performing supercritical water                oxidation reaction to degrade pollutants in                wastewater into non-toxic harmful substances such                as carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water, adding clean                high-temperature and high-pressure liquid to                heater, and heat exchanging with wastewater and                oxidant respectively;
   (D) subjecting to gas-liquid separation to                separate carbon dioxide and nitrogen from the                product, adding product to cooler, cooling, adding                to high-pressure gas-liquid separator, separating                excess oxidant and returning back to supercritical                water oxidation reactor to continue the reaction,                and adding liquid product to liquid recovery                device;
   (E) flowing into salt separator, heating,                lowering temperature and pressure of solid waste                residue at the bottom of the salt separator to                remove slag, and adding solid waste residue into                the waste residue collection tank; and
   (F) flowing out clean water, discharging                carbon dioxide, nitrogen and other gasses from top                of gas-liquid separator, discharging treated clean                water from the bottom of gas-liquid separator and                collecting in the recovery water tank, and                testing.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN112266427-A; CN112266427-B
TI Preparing hydrophobic agarose aerogel microsphere,            comprises e.g. preparing wet agarose gel microsphere,            preparing agarose aerogel microsphere, and performing            hydrophobization modification of agarose aerogel            microsphere.
AU SAI H
   YANG X
   FU R
   WANG Y
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIN-C)
GA 2021131332
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobic agarose aerogel                microsphere, comprises e.g. (1) preparing wet                agarose gel microsphere: (1a) mixing agarose and                deionized water to prepare an agarose solution with                a concentration of 0.5-3%; (1b) pouring 5-10 cm                deep silicone oil into the beaker, and placing it                at -2 degrees C to -6 degrees C for 10-20 minutes;                using a syringe to suck the agarose solution stored                at a constant temperature in step (1a) into the                refrigerated silicone oil; dropping the solution in                the syringe into the silicone oil; removing the gel                microsphere from the silicone oil and washing the                silicone oil on the surface of the microsphere with                excess deionized water; then performing sufficient                solvent replacement with absolute ethanol; (2)                drying the wet agarose gel microsphere with                supercritical carbon dioxide; and (3) placing the                agarose aerogel microsphere and modifier obtained                in step (2) into a drier, and placing the drier                into an oven at 50-80 degrees C for                hydrophobization modification for 2 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrophobic                agarose aerogel microsphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a physical gel process, has no                chemical reaction, and is environmentally friendly                and simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrophobic agarose aerogel                microsphere, comprises (1) preparing wet agarose                gel microsphere: (1a) mixing agarose and deionized                water to prepare an agarose solution with a                concentration of 0.5-3%; (1b) pouring 5-10 cm deep                silicone oil into the beaker, and placing it at -2                degrees C to -6 degrees C for 10-20 minutes; using                a syringe to suck the agarose solution stored at a                constant temperature in step (1a) into the                refrigerated silicone oil; during the dripping                process, the syringe is perpendicular to the                silicone oil surface and the vertical distance is                8-15 cm, and dropping the solution in the syringe                into the silicone oil at 0.2-4 seconds intervals;                afterwards, removing the gel microsphere from the                silicone oil and washing the silicone oil on the                surface of the microsphere with excess deionized                water; then performing sufficient solvent                replacement with absolute ethanol to obtain agarose                wet gel microsphere; (2) preparing agarose aerogel                microsphere: drying the wet agarose gel microsphere                prepared in step (1) with supercritical carbon                dioxide for 1-3 hours to obtain agarose aerogel                microsphere; where the temperature of supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid is 40-60 degrees C, and the                pressure is 10-20 MPa; and (3) performing                hydrophobization modification of agarose aerogel                microsphere: placing the agarose aerogel                microsphere and modifier obtained in step (2) into                a drier, and placing the drier into an oven at                50-80 degrees C for hydrophobization modification                for 2 hours to obtain hydrophobic agarose aerogel                microsphere; where the modifier is at least one of                methyltrimethoxysilane, trimethylchlorosilane,                dimethyldimethoxysilane, and                methyltrichlorosilane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The specific operation                  method of step (1a) is as follows: mixing the                  agarose, de-ionized water in the beaker, then                  placing the beaker in the microwave oven to heat                  until the solution is boiling, then taking out                  for 5-10 seconds of cooling, then heating until                  the solution is boiling, then taking out and                  cooling for 5-10 seconds; repeating the                  operations for many times until the agarose is                  completely dissolved; then quickly pouring the                  agarose solution into the round bottomed flask,                  stirring at a rotating speed of 10-30                  revolutions/minute (rpm) in the oil bath at 50-90                  degrees C. Preferred Components: The diameter of                  the syringe needle is 0.6 mm. The macroscopic                  form of the agarose aerogel is microsphere with a                  diameter of 0.5-2.5 mm, the density is                  0.015-0.065 g.cm-3, the specific surface area is                  180-360 m2/g, the porosity is 88.02-98.91%, and                  the contact angle is 120-145 degrees .
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).)

PN CN111920948-A; CN111920948-B
TI Pharmaceutical composition useful for treating liver cancer, and inhibiting proliferation of liver cancer cell, comprises monoclonal antibody specific to programmed death-ligand 1, and extracted immune cell.
AU LIU H
   ZHU X
   LI W
AE BEIJING GUANGWEI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   ANKERUI SHANXI BIOLOGICAL CELLS CO LTD (ANKE-Non-standard)
GA 2020B54970
AB    NOVELTY - Pharmaceutical composition comprises a monoclonal antibody and extracted immune cell. The monoclonal antibody is specific programmed death-ligand 1 (PD-L1) monoclonal antibody 4-G3. The 4-G3 antibody has a heavy chain variable region having a 113 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) fully defined in the specification, and a light chain variable region having a 112 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 2) fully defined in the specification.
   USE - The pharmaceutical composition is useful for treating liver cancer (claimed), and inhibiting proliferation of liver cancer cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pharmaceutical composition comprises a monoclonal antibody and extracted immune cell. The immune cell is prepared by (a) coating stimulating factor with a culture flask, where stimulating factor comprises 5000 IU/ml interleukin (IL)-2, 5000 IU/ml interferon (IFN)- gamma , 2000 IU/ml IL-15, 2000 IU/ml granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF), 750 IU/ml IL-4, and 1000 IU/ml tumor necrosis factor (TNF)- alpha in saline, (b) separating mononuclear cells from the blood by using density gradient centrifugation, (c) adding Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 medium, 2 mg/l linoleic acid, 20 mg/l amino acid, 50 mg/l vitamin, 5000 IU/ml IL-2 and 5000 IU/ml IFN- gamma to culture flask coated with stimulating factor, inoculating 5x 106 cells/ml mononuclear cells into stimulating factor-coated culture flasks at 37 degrees C, and culturing under condition of 5% carbon dioxide, (d) culturing for 3 days, transferring mononuclear cells into an empty culture flask, adding 2 mg/l linoleic acid, 20 mg/l amino acid, 50 mg/l vitamin, 5000 IU/ml IL-2, 5000 IU/ml IFN- gamma and RPMI1640 medium to empty culture flask, and continuously culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, and (e) culturing for 7 days, transferring mononuclear cells into culture bag, add 2 mg/l linoleic acid, 20 mg/l amino acid, 50 mg/l vitamin, 5000 IU/ml IL-2 and 5000 IU/ml IFN- gamma and RPMI 1640 medium to the culture bag, continuously culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, and collecting mononuclear cells after 15 days of culture to-be immune cells. The monoclonal antibody is specific programmed death-ligand 1 (PD-L1) monoclonal antibody 4-G3. The 4-G3 antibody has a heavy chain variable region having a 113 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) fully defined in the specification, and a light chain variable region having a 112 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 2) fully defined in the specification. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of monoclonal antibody and immune cell in preparation of a pharmaceutical composition for treating liver cancer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The pharmaceutical composition comprises an isotonic agent chosen from sugar, mannitol, sorbitol or sodium chloride.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition in form of solution, suspension, emulsion or liposome is administered by intravenous, subcutaneous, intraperitoneal, intramuscular, oral, nasal, pulmonary, ocular, vaginal or rectal route (all claimed).
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN111715194-A
TI Preparing modified Ginkgo biloba biochar comprises            e.g. pyrolyzing and carbonizing Ginkgo biloba, soaking            in potassium hydroxide, activating with carbon dioxide            gas, esterifying with tartaric acid, and reacting with            beta-cyclodextrin.
AU GAO M
   GAO G
AE ZHEJIANG HAOYU ECOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020989351
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified Ginkgo biloba biochar,                comprises e.g. (i) washing Ginkgo biloba, drying,                crushing, and then pyrolyzing and carbonizing to                obtain unactivated biochar, (ii) soaking the                unactivated biochar in potassium hydroxide                solution, then filtering, drying, activating,                cooling to room temperature, and transferring the                obtained solid to a tube furnace, then passing                carbon dioxide gas, heating to 700 degrees C,                activating, and then cooling to obtain activated                biochar, (iii) adding the activated biochar into 1                mol/l tartaric acid solution, stirring, heating and                esterifying at 100 degrees C under microwave                condition for 3 hours, then cooling, neutralizing,                filtering and washing, then adding the obtained                product, beta -cyclodextrin, distilled water and                potassium acetate into a reaction kettle, stirring                and reacting in a microwave at 110 degrees C for 2                hours, then cooling, washing the resulting product                with ultrapure water for multiple times, and drying                to obtain final product.
   USE - The modified Ginkgo biloba biochar is useful                as adsorbent for adsorbing organic pollutant                bisphenol A in polluted water sources                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is reliable and easy to implement;                has low cost, and wide range of raw material                sources; and is suitable for large-scale                production. The modified Ginkgo biloba biochar:                efficiently removes bisphenol A in water; is                suitable in water with wide range of pH; and has                resistance to ion interference.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing modified Ginkgo biloba biochar,                comprises (i) washing Ginkgo biloba, drying,                crushing, and then pyrolyzing and carbonizing in an                inert atmosphere to obtain unactivated biochar,                (ii) soaking the unactivated biochar in potassium                hydroxide solution for 12 hours, then filtering,                drying, activating the product at 500 degrees C in                an inert atmosphere for 1.5 hours, cooling to room                temperature, and transferring the obtained solid to                a tube in a furnace, then passing carbon dioxide                gas, heating to 700 degrees C, activating for 1                hour, and then cooling to room temperature to                obtain activated biochar, (iii) adding the                activated biochar into 1 mol/l tartaric acid                solution, stirring evenly, heating and esterifying                at 100 degrees C under microwave condition for 3                hours, then cooling, neutralizing with potassium                hydroxide, filtering and washing repeatedly, then                adding the obtained product, beta -cyclodextrin,                distilled water and potassium acetate into a                reaction kettle, stirring and reacting in a                microwave at 110 degrees C for 2 hours, then                cooling to room temperature, washing the resulting                product with ultrapure water for multiple times,                and drying to obtain beta -cyclodextrin-modified                biochar i.e. modified Ginkgo biloba biochar, where                the mass ratio between biochar, tartaric acid, beta                -cyclodextrin, distilled water and potassium                acetate is 1:15:20:28:0.3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): the pyrolysis                  reaction temperature is 250 degrees C, and the                  reaction time is 1 hour; the carbonization                  reaction temperature is 460-650 degrees C, and                  the reaction time is 3-6 hours; and the soaking                  ratio between the unactivated biochar and                  potassium hydroxide solution is 1:2.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN111676482-A; CN111676482-B
TI Preparing electrode for electrochemical reduction            of carbon dioxide involves using a catalyst, where the            catalyst is an ordered core-she catalyst, and the            catalyst is copper-vacant copper/copper oxide, where            copper-vacant copper is shell.
AU ZHONG H
   DIAO G
   ZHANG J
   PAN L
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 202093644E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electrode involves using a catalyst,                where the catalyst is an ordered core-she catalyst,                and the catalyst is copper-vacant copper/copper                oxide, where the copper-vacant copper is the shell,                copper oxide is a core body, and the catalyst                comprises 50-90 wt.% copper, 5-30 wt.% vacancy                copper and 5-20 wt.% copper oxide, washing the                copper mesh, foamed nickel, and copper foil base                layer with water, ethanol or acetone, and then                adjusting in phosphoric acid with a mass                concentration of 50-95% and a constant potential                voltage of 3-5V for 5-200 seconds, then washing and                drying for later use, immersing the base layer in a                mixture of a certain amount of sodium hydroxide and                an oxidant, chemically oxidizing for 30 seconds-120                minutes, cleaning.
   USE - Method for preparing electrode used for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare electrode                improves the selectivity and activity of the                hydrocarbon, avoids the catalyst loss in the carbon                dioxide reduction process and improves the                stability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing electrode involves using a catalyst,                where the catalyst is an ordered core-she catalyst,                and the catalyst is copper-vacant copper/copper                oxide, where the copper-vacant copper is the shell,                copper oxide is a core body, and the catalyst                comprises 50-90 wt.% copper, 5-30 wt.% vacancy                copper and 5-20 wt.% copper oxide, washing the                copper mesh, foamed nickel, and copper foil base                layer with water, ethanol or acetone, and then                adjusting in phosphoric acid with a mass                concentration of 50-95% and a constant potential                voltage of 3-5V for 5-200 seconds, then washing and                drying for later use, immersing the base layer in a                mixture of a certain amount of sodium hydroxide and                an oxidant, chemically oxidizing for 30 seconds-120                minutes, cleaning, drying at 40-150 degrees C to                obtain a copper oxide nanowire/copper base layer,                mixing copper salt, sulfuric acid and stabilizer,                magnetically stirring for 30-120 minutes to form a                precursor copper salt solution under the protection                of an inert atmosphere and at room temperature,                electrodeposition catalyst on the surface of CuOx                nanowires/copper substrate layer at variable                current density, placing prepared electrode in a                carbondioxide saturated electrolyte for reduction                at a constant current density for 20-10000 seconds,                washing and drying with inert gas to prepare a gas                diffusion electrode. The prepared gas diffusion                electrode is soaked in a concentrated hydrochloric                acid solution with a concentration of 36-38 wt.%                for 10-30 minutes, washed and dried to prepare                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant is                  ammonium persulfate, potassium persulfate, sodium                  persulfate, hydrogen peroxide, potassium                  dichromate. The stabilizers are                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, trisodium citrate,                  ethylenediamine disodium acetate, the                  concentration ratio of the stabilizer to copper                  in the copper salt is 20:1-1:30. The carnon                  dioxide saturated electrolyte is potassium                  hydrogen carbonate, sodium hydrogen carbonate,                  sodium hydroidxe, potassium hydroxide, sodium                  chloride, potassium chloride, potassium bromide,                  or soidium bromide. Preferred Conditions: The                  concentration of the oxidant is 0.01-0.2M, the                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide is 0.5-4M                  and the concentration of sodium hydroxide and the                  oxidant ratio is 5-80:1. The precursor copper                  salt solution is copper nitrate trihydrate,                  copper chloirde dihydrate, copper sulfate                  pentahydrate, where the concentration of copper                  ions is 1-1.4 M. The pH of the copper salt                  solution is adjusted to 0.5-2.0 by sulfuric acid.                  The deposition current density is -100-200                  mAcm-2. The electrodeposition time is 5-120                  seconds, the preferred condition is -100-150                  mAcm-2, and the deposition time is 20-60 seconds.                  The concentration of the carbondioxide saturated                  electrolyte is 0.02-2 M, the current density of                  the constant current is -5-150 mAcm-2. The                  current density is -40-80 mAcm-2, the reduction                  time is 10-5000 seconds, and the preferred time                  is 120-2400 seconds.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PN CN109954043-A
TI Extracting purified saponin from cherry tomatoes comprises beating tomatoes, enzymatic hydrolysis by pectinase, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, centrifuging, eluting in macro-porous absorption resin, concentrating and drying.
AU LI C
   XIN M
   SUN J
   SUN Y
   LI L
   HE X
   TANG Y
   SHENG J
   ZHENG F
   LI J
   LIU G
   LI Z
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG NATIONALITY AUTONOMOUS RE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201959928E
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting purified saponin from cherry tomatoes comprises cleaning the fresh cherry tomatoes, beating in pulping machine, adjusting the pH to obtain slurry, adding pectinase, mixing uniformly, performing enzymatic hydrolysis under vacuum to obtain first enzymatic slurry, adding pectinase, mixing uniformly, enzymatic hydrolysis under vacuum to obtain a second enzymatic slurry, adding pectinase, mixing uniformly, performing enzymatic hydrolysis under vacuum to obtain the third enzymatic slurry, placing into a supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, extracting to obtain extract slurry, adding water, centrifuging and filtering to obtain a supernatant, adding the supernatant to the D101 macro-porous absorption resin column, eluting the impurities with deionized water and ethanol, collecting the eluent, adding saturated n-butyl alcohol to the eluent for extracting n-butanol which is colorless and collecting n-butanol phase, concentrating the n-butanol phase by vacuum extracting and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting purified saponin from cherry tomatoes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the diversity of the saponin extraction method with good extraction effect, high yield and saponin purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting purified saponin from cherry tomatoes comprises (a) cleaning the fresh cherry tomatoes, placing into pulping machine and beating, adjusting the pH to 4-4.5 to obtain the cherry tomato slurry, (b) adding 0.03-0.05 wt.% pectinase to the cherry tomato slurry, mixing uniformly, performing enzymatic hydrolysis for 10-15 minutes at a vacuum of 0.06-0.08 MPa and a temperature of 48-50 degrees C for obtaining the first enzymatic slurry, adding 0.02-0.03 wt.% pectinase to the first enzymatic slurry and mixing uniformly, performing enzymatic hydrolysis under the vacuum of 0.2-0.5 MPa at 43-46 degrees C at 15-20 minutes to obtain the second enzymatic slurry, adding 0.01-0.02 wt.% pectinase to the second enzymatic slurry and mixing uniformly, performing enzymatic hydrolysis under vacuum at 0.7-0.9 MPa at 40-43 degrees C for 15-20 minutes to obtain the third enzymatic slurry, (c) placing the third enzymatic slurry into a supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, extracting at 40 degrees C under 10-12 MPa pressure for 20-35 minutes to obtain an extract slurry, (d) adding water into the extract slurry and mixing uniformly, centrifuging by placing in a centrifuge and filtering to obtain a supernatant, (e) adding the supernatant to the D101 macro-porous absorption resin column, eluting the impurities first with 6-8 times of de-ionized water and then with a volume fraction of 80-90 % ethanol, collecting the eluent, adding saturated n-butyl alcohol to the eluent for extracting n-butanol which is colorless and collecting the n-butanol phase and (f) concentrating the n-butanol phase by vacuum extracting and drying to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method the step (c) comprises adjusting the pH of third enzymatic slurry to 3.5 before placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extractor. The step (d) comprises adding water in an amount of one half the volume of extraction slurry and the rotation speed of centrifuge is 2500-3000 revolutions/minute. The adsorption amount of the macroporous adsorption resin column is 130-140 ml and the amount of ethanol is 3-4 times the column volume.

PN CN109673680-A
TI Preparing controlled release microsphere by mixing magnetic nanoparticles with alkaline water, adding pyrethrin-thiazinone, allowing to stand, stirring under magnetic field, reacting, heating and drying controlled release microspheres.
AU LI Y
   DAI J
AE SHAANXI MICROBIOLOGY INST (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201940830T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing controlled release microsphere involves mixing the magnetic nanoparticles with alkaline water, adding ethanol, adding pyrethrin-thiazinone to the magnetic nanoparticle solution (A), dissolving the mixture and polymer in the oil phase to obtain an oil phase group, dissolving the magnetic nanoparticle solution and emulsifier in an aqueous phase, and stirring to obtain an aqueous phase group, allowing to stand, stirring the oil phase group under a paramagnetic field and aqueous phase group under reverse magnetic field, adding supercritical carbon dioxide solvent to the mixing vessel, adding antifoaming agent, reacting, heating and drying controlled release microspheres.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing controlled release microsphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The controlled release microsphere is environmentally-friendly and has uniform particle size, prolonged retention period and high heat storage and low temperature stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing controlled release microsphere involves (a) mixing the magnetic nanoparticles with alkaline water in a volume ratio of 1:(10-25), ultrasonically oscillating and cleaning for 10-15 minutes, filtering, adding ethanol to prepare magnetic nanoparticles having concentration of 14-18%, dividing the nanoparticle solution into magnetic nanoparticle solution (A) and magnetic nanoparticle solution (B), (b) adding 20-25 vol.% pyrethrin-thiazinone to the magnetic nanoparticle solution (A), ultrasonically dispersing and dissolving the mixture and environmentally-friendly polymer in the oil phase in a volume ratio of 1:(2-4) and ultrasonically mixing to obtain an oil phase group, (c) dissolving the magnetic nanoparticle solution (B) and emulsifier in an aqueous phase at a volume ratio of (4-6):0.01, and stirring at 6000-8000 revolutions per minute (rpm) to obtain an aqueous phase group, (d) allowing the oil phase group and the aqueous phase group to stand in a low temperature environment of 2-4 degrees C for 4-6 hours respectively, (e) stirring the oil phase group under a paramagnetic field environment in clockwise direction for 2-4 minutes and stirring the aqueous phase group under a reverse magnetic field environment in counterclockwise direction for 2-4 minutes, so that the oil phase group and the aqueous phase group oppositely magnetic, (f) adding supercritical carbon dioxide solvent to the mixing vessel and adding the magnetized oil phase group and the aqueous phase group to the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent at the same rate while stirring, (g) adding 4-8 drops antifoaming agent, reacting at 15-20 degrees C and 20-25 MPa for 9-11 hours, (h) heating at 40-45 degrees C for 2 hours, decompressing to 4-6 MPa and drying controlled release microspheres and (i) adding dispersant, wetting agent and adsorbent to the microspheres and uniformly sieving to obtain the wettable powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In step (a), the alkaline water is an aqueous sodium carbonate solution having pH of 11-12. In step (b), the environmentally friendly polymer is chosen from one or more combinations of polyhydroxyalkanoate, polybutylene succinate, aliphatic polycarbonate, polylactic acid or aliphatic/aromatic copolyester. In step (b), the oil phase is chosen from one or more combinations of chloroform, acetone, dichloromethane, toluene and xylene. In step (c), the emulsifier is chosen from one or more combinations of calcium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, gelatin, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, polyvinyl alcohol or Tween (RTM: Polysorbate). In step (e), the magnetic field strength of the paramagnetic field environment and the reverse magnetic field environment is 3500-4000 Gauss. In step (f), the mixing vessel comprises base, water bath jacket, conical container bottle, Y-shaped tube, pusher (P1) and pusher (P2). The lower port of the conical container bottle is connected to the upper center of the base, and the upper port of the conical container bottle is provided with a sealing cover. The main port of the Y-shaped tube extends through the sealing cover into the conical container bottle, and the main port of the Y-shaped tube is provided with a valve. The pusher (P1) is provided with an oil phase group. The pusher (P2) is provided with a aqueous phase group. The pusher (P1) and pusher (P2) are respectively connected to the two branch ports of the Y-shaped tube one by one. The water bath jacket is wrapped around the conical container bottle (3), and the bottom is fixedly connected with the base. The base includes rotating impeller, rotating electrical machine, heating rod, heater and control panel. The rotating electrical machine and the heater are located inside the base, and are electrically connected to a control panel located on an outer surface of the base. The rotary impeller is coupled to the output shaft of the rotary electric machine and extends to the inside of the conical container. The heating rod is electrically connected to the heater and is arranged on the upper surface of the base and extends into the interior of the water bath jacket. In step (f), the oil phase group and the aqueous phase group are adding into a supercritical carbon dioxide solvent at an equivalent rate of 5-6 ml/minute. In step (g), the antifoaming agent is chosen from one or more combinations of polydimethylsiloxane, polyether modified silicon and lauryl phenylacetate. In step (i), the dispersant is sodium lauryl sulfate or saponin, the wetting agent is benzene powder, saponin powder, SOPA or sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate and the adsorbent is diatomaceous earth, attapulgite, kaolin, activated clay or bentonite. The step (i) involves adding 1-3 wt.% dispersant, 3-5 wt.% wetting agent and 4-6 wt.% adsorbent.
DC C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C02 (Heterocyclic.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures.
