PN CN109392693-A
TI Culturing selenium-enriched Nostoc sphaeroides, involves inoculating Nostoc sphaeroides strain in culture solution comprising boric acid and zinc sulfate, introducing carbon dioxide, culturing under natural light and adding sodium selenite.
AU PENG Z
   YU A
   ZHU Y
AE ENSHI XIGU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ENSH-Non-standard)
GA 201922861S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for artificially culturing selenium-enriched Nostoc sphaeroides, involves (i) preparing culture solution comprising sodium bicarbonate, disodium phosphate, citric acid, ammonium ferric citrate, calcium chloride, magnesium sulfate, boric acid, manganese chloride, zinc sulfate, sodium molybdate, copper sulfate and cobalt nitrate, (ii) placing the culture solution in an ultrasonic plate, pressurizing and ultrasonically processing, (iii) inoculating N.sphaeroides strain in the culture solution, controlling temperature, introducing pure carbon dioxide and culturing to obtain N.sphaeroides activated strain liquid, (iv) inoculating the strain liquid, culturing, controlling illumination intensity in the culture process and culturing under natural daylight, (v) adding sodium selenite under natural light, prolonging darkening and culturing, and (vi) filtering and taking out N.sphaeroides, ultrasonically cleaning a ultrapure water and filtering.
   USE - The method is useful for artificially culturing selenium-enriched N.sphaeroides.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cultivates N.sphaeroides with a diameter of 0.8-1.4 cm, a selenium content of 350-2000 ppm and a protein content of more than 52%, and is rich in nutrition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for artificially culturing selenium-enriched Nostoc sphaeroides, involves (i) preparing culture solution comprising 25-45 mg/l sodium bicarbonate, 70-90 mg/l disodium phosphate, 4-7 mg/l citric acid, 3-8 mg/l ammonium ferric citrate, 180-240 mg/l calcium chloride, 60-90 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 1.5-2 mg/l boric acid, 1.3-3 mg/l manganese chloride, 0.2-0.5 mg/l zinc sulfate, 0.3-0.4 mg/l sodium molybdate, 0.05-0.1 mg/l copper sulfate and 0.02-0.06 mg/l cobalt nitrate, (ii) placing the culture solution in an ultrasonic plate at 120-140 Hz, pressurizing 9.5-10.5 MPa after ultrasound and ultrasonically processing for 20-40 minutes, (iii) inoculating N.sphaeroides strain in the culture solution according to the specific gravity of 1-5%, controlling temperature at 16-25 degrees C, introducing pure carbon dioxide and culturing for 1-4 hours to obtain N.sphaeroides activated strain liquid, (iv) inoculating the strain liquid in a ratio of 1:250-1:80 (weight/weight), culturing at 20-25 degrees C, controlling illumination intensity in the culture process and culturing for 2-5 days under natural daylight, (v) adding 40-120 mmol/l sodium selenite under natural light, prolonging darkening time is not less than 12-14 hours per day and culturing for 5-9 days, and (vi) filtering and taking out N.sphaeroides, ultrasonically cleaning a ultrapure water for 3-5 times, each time for 14-16 minutes and filtering.

PN CN108912622-A
TI Preparing high-toughness impact resistant polyethylene terephthalate protection film comprises e.g. placing calcium hydroxide powder, sucrose and distilled water in beaker, mixing, stirring, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into beaker.
AU QIU Y
   JIANG M
   ZHU C
AE FOSHAN GAOMING ZHUAHE NEW MATERIAL TECHN (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201898399L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-toughness impact resistant polyethylene terephthalate (PET) protection film comprises (i) placing calcium hydroxide powder, sucrose and distilled water in beaker, mixing, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide gas into beaker, filtering, drying to obtain reaction product, (ii) placing reaction product, distilled water in three-necked flask, heating, stirring, mixing, adding distearyl oxy isopropyl aluminate, stirring, adding octadecyl amine, stirring , filtering to obtain filter cake, drying in oven, grinding by 200 meshes sieve to obtain modified reaction product, (iii) mixing polymethyl methacrylate and methyl methacrylate/butadiene/styrene terpolymer, drying to obtain mixed product, placing in double-screw extruder, extruding, granulating with cold water to obtain mixed solid, and (iv) mixing mixed solid and polycarbonate in a melt granulation device.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-toughness impact resistant polyethylene terephthalate (PET) protection film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enhances hardness, impact resistances and mechanical strength of PET protective film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-toughness impact resistant polyethylene terephthalate (PET) protection film comprises (i) placing 1.5-1.8 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide powder, 0.6-1.0 pts. wt. sucrose and 110-120 pts. wt. distilled water in beaker under vacuum conditions, mixing, stirring with a stirrer, introducing carbon dioxide gas into beaker, filtering to obtain filter residue, drying in an oven to obtain reaction product, (ii) placing 8-10 pts. wt. reaction product, 20-25 pts. wt. distilled water in three-necked flask, heating, stirring, mixing with stirrer, adding 0.5-0.8 pts. wt. distearyl oxy isopropyl aluminate, stirring, adding 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. octadecyl amine, continuously stirring for 3-4 hours, suction filtering to obtain filter cake, drying in oven, grinding by 200 meshes sieve to obtain modified reaction product, (iii) mixing polymethyl methacrylate and methyl methacrylate/butadiene/styrene terpolymer according to mass ratio of 3:1, placing into oven, drying to obtain mixed product, placing the mixed product into double-screw extruder, extruding, cooling, granulating with cold water to obtain mixed solid, (iv) mixing mixed solid and polycarbonate according to mass ratio of 5:1 in a melt granulation device, melt blending, extruding, cooling and granulating to obtain melt blended product, (v) placing 70-73 pts. wt. terephthalic acid, 38-42 pts. wt. glycol, 3-5 pts. wt. ethylene glycol oxime, 10-12 pts. wt. melt blended product and 6-8 pts. wt. modified reaction product into beaker, emulsifying with high-speed cutting emulsifying machine, pulping, allowing to stand at room temperature for 15-20 minutes to obtain mixed slurry, and (vi) placing mixed slurry in polymerization kettle, reacting under nitrogen atmosphere, vacuumizing polymerization kettle, raising the temperature for constant temperature reaction, extrusion forming, and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i) stirring is carried out at 600-650 rotations/minute (rpm), stirring and mixing for 30-40 minutes, the inletting speed of carbon dioxide gas is 500-600 ml/minute, the temperature in the oven is 110-120 degrees C, and drying for 3-4 hours. In the step (ii) heating the three-necked flask at 85-90 degrees C, stirring at speed of 600-650 rpm for 30-40 minutes, the temperature of the oven is 110-120 degrees C. In the step (iii), drying is carried out at in the oven at 80-85 degrees C for 10-11 hours, the rotating speed of double-screw extruder is 200-220 rpm at 255-265 degrees C. In the step (iv) the temperature in the melt granulation device is 270-280 degrees C, the stirring is carried out at 120-150 rpm, and melt blending for 2-3 hours. In the step (v) the rotation speed high-speed cutting emulsifying machine is 1000-1100 rpm, and emulsifying for 30-40 minutes. In the step (vi) the air pressure in the polymerization kettle is 0.28-0.32 MPa, the stirring is carried out at 230-240 degrees C under 60-80 rpm, reacting for 3-4 hours, the vacuum degree in the polymerization kettle is 40-80 Pa, the temperature is 280-290 degrees C, and the constant temperature reacting for 2-3 hours.

PN CN108522141-A
TI Method for planting Hericium erinaceus, involves            taking fresh straw after harvesting rice, crushing            fresh straw into particles, drying straw particles,            spraying salt water, adding ammonia water solution and            urea water solutionand stirring.
AU LU H
AE XISHUI FUNONG MUSHROOM PLANTING FARMERS              SPECIALIZED COOP (XISH-Non-standard)
GA 201875388G
AB    NOVELTY - A Hericium erinaceus planting method involves                taking fresh straw after harvesting rice, crushing                fresh straw into particles, drying the straw                particles after crushing, spraying salt water and                drying by solar light to keep the straw particle                content to 30-35%. The straw particles are added                with ammonia water solution and urea water                solution, mixed and stirred uniformly, placed in                the ammonia tank for ammonification by keeping the                external temperature and stirred. The straw                particles after ammoniation are added with                cottonseed cake, maize powder, bagasse, maize core,                bean cake, rice bran, sucrose, urea, potassium                dihydrogen phosphate and citric acid, controlled                the water content of Hericium erinaceus culture                medium to 55-60%, controlled the pH value, mixed                the raw material and stirred uniformly and                packaged, performed illumination with 60-100Lux                during mycelium growing period, illuminated with                200-300Lux, illuminated with 300-400Lux during                fruiting body growing period.
   USE - Method for planting Hericium erinaceus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables planting the Hericium                erinaceus with effectively saving the wood                resource, improving the additional value of                planting rice and income of the farmer, in                eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Hericium erinaceus planting method involves                taking fresh straw after harvesting rice, crushing                fresh straw into the length of 2-2.8 mm into                particles, drying the straw particles after                crushing, spraying 3-5% salt water and drying by                solar light to keep the straw particle content to                30-35%. The straw particles are added with 20-23%                ammonia water solution and 20-25% urea water                solution, mixed and stirred uniformly, placed in                the ammonia tank for ammonification by keeping the                external temperature at 20-30 degrees C for 7-9                days, stirred for 2-3 minutes. The straw particles                after ammoniation are added with 60-75% cottonseed                cake, 4-6% maize powder, 5-7% bagasse, 5-8% maize                core, 5-9% bean cake, 6-8% rice bran, 6-9% sucrose,                1.2% urea, 0.5-0.3% potassium dihydrogen phosphate                and 0.2% citric acid, controlled the water content                of Hericium erinaceus culture medium to 55-60%,                controlled the pH value to 4.5-6, mixed the raw                material and stirred uniformly and packaged,                performed illumination with 60-100Lux, air relative                humidity of 61-62% at 22-24 degrees C during                mycelium growing period, controlled the                concentration of carbon dioxide to 0.04%,                illuminated with 200-300Lux, the air relative                humidity of 62-63% at 15-18 degrees C during                fruiting body differentiation stage, controlled the                concentration of carbon dioxide to 0.05%,                illuminated with 300-400Lux, relative humidity of                air of 63-64% at 16-19 degrees C during fruiting                body growing period and controlled the                concentration of carbon dioxide to 0.06%.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN WO2018118650-A2; WO2018118650-A3; CA3047764-A1; AU2017382765-A1; IN201947027498-A; SG11201905603-A1; EP3559247-A2; CN110462050-A; JP2020501597-W; ID201907645-A; MX2019007508-A1; PH12019501441-A1; HK40016479-A0; RU2019122805-A; US2021130851-A1; SG10202106797-A1; ZA201904161-A; RU2765490-C2
TI Fermenting gaseous substrate to form liquid            product e.g. ethanol and butanol, by introducing            gaseous substrate into bioreactor comprising            fermentation broth comprising fermenting species, and            introducing ionophore into bioreactor.
AU TERRILL J B
   TERRILL Y B
AE SYNATA BIO INC (SYNA-Non-standard)
   SYNATA BIO INC (SYNA-Non-standard)
   SYNATA BIO INC (SYNA-Non-standard)
   SYNATA BIO INC (SYNA-Non-standard)
GA 201855735E
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for fermenting gaseous substrate                to form a liquid product, involves (a) (i)                introducing the gaseous substrate into a bioreactor                comprising a fermentation broth, where the                fermentation broth comprises a fermenting species,                and optionally the gaseous substrate comprises at                least one of constituents chosen from carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, (ii)                introducing at least one type of ionophore into the                bioreactor, where the ionophore has selectivity for                minimally inhibiting the fermenting species from                growing, and (iii) allowing the substrate to                ferment to produce the liquid product, where the                gaseous substrate is the primary carbon source for                fermentation product.
   USE - The method (M1) and system are useful for                fermenting gaseous substrate to form the liquid                product. The liquid product is chosen from ethanol,                n-propanol, iso-propanol, n-butanol, iso-butanol,                n-pentanol, n-hexanol, acetic acid, propanoic acid,                butanoic acid, pentatonic acid, and hexanoic acid,                preferably ethanol or butanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method (M1) exhibits high selectivity for                inhibiting competing species from growing and/or                producing undesired product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method (M2) for anaerobically fermenting a                gaseous substrate to form a liquid product,                involves (a) introducing the gaseous substrate into                a bioreactor comprising fermentation broth, where                the fermentation broth contains at least two types                of microorganisms, one type comprises at least one                fermenting species, and the other type comprises at                least one competing species, and optionally the                gaseous substrate comprises at least one of the                constituents carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and                hydrogen, (b) introducing at least one type of                ionophore into the bioreactor, where the ionophore                has selectivity for preferentially inhibiting the                at least one competing species from growing and/or                producing an undesired product, and (c) allowing                the substrate to ferment by exposure to at least                one fermenting species to produce the liquid                product, where the gaseous substrate is the primary                carbon source for the fermentation product;                and
   (2) fermentation system comprises (a)                fermentation vessel, (b) gaseous substrate supply                conduit for introducing the gaseous substrate into                the fermentation vessel, where the gaseous                substrate comprising at least one of carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, (c) an                aqueous fermentation broth filled in the                fermentation vessel, where the fermentation broth                comprises fermenting species, or at least two types                of microorganisms, one type comprising at least one                fermenting species, and the other type comprising                at least one competing species, (d) an ionophore                supply conduit in communication with the                fermentation vessel for introducing at least one                type of ionophore into the vessel, where the                ionophore selected to minimally inhibit the                fermenting species from growing, and (e) liquid                recovery conduit for collecting the liquid product                after the gaseous substrate is allowed to ferment                by exposure to at least one fermenting                species.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method (M2) further                  involves introducing media into the bioreactor.                  The ionophore is introduced in a liquid feed. The                  method further involves introducing anti-foaming                  agent into the bioreactor. The liquid product is                  produced by a microorganism within the                  fermentation broth to form a fermentation                  mixture, where a portion of the fermentation                  mixture is withdrawn from the bioreactor, and the                  liquid product is separated from the fermentation                  broth. The separation is by distillation. The                  fermentation mixture is treated by centrifugation                  to recover the immobilized and/or insoluble                  ionophores, or by solids removal to recover                  ionophores. The introduction of the ionophore and                  gaseous substrate is continuous, segmented or                  semi-continuous. The ionophore is immobilized in                  a porous particle using electron force between                  the ionophore and porous particle. The method                  further involves introducing a base into the                  bioreactor. At least a portion of the gaseous                  substrate is prepared from gasification of solid                  substrate. At least a portion of the gaseous                  substrate is prepared from reforming a gaseous                  precursor, where steam and oxygen are used in the                  reforming. At least a portion of the gaseous                  substrate is prepared from coal. Preferred                  System: The ionophore supply conduit further                  introduces media into the vessel. The system                  further comprises a media supply conduit for                  introducing media into the vessel. The system                  further comprises a base supply conduit for                  introducing a base and/or a broth into the                  vessel. The system further comprises a distiller,                  where the distiller is in communication with the                  vessel to receive the portion of the fermentation                  mixture withdrawn from the bioreactor, where the                  distiller is adapted to separate the liquid                  product from the fermentation mixture. The system                  further comprises treatment unit for treating the                  fermentation mixture. The system further                  comprises valve unit in relation to the ionophore                  supply conduit for regulating continuous,                  semi-continuous or staggered introduction of the                  ionophore. The system further comprises valve                  unit in relation to the gas supply conduit for                  regulating continuous, semi-continuous or                  staggered introduction of the gaseous substrate.                  Preferred Components: The pH of fermentation                  broth is 1-9, preferably 2-9, 3-7 or 2-6. The                  base comprises hydroxide ions. The base comprises                  one or more of lithium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, magnesium                  hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, lithium                  bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and potassium                  bicarbonate. The media comprises one or more of                  constituents chosen from vitamin, mineral, and                  metal. The vitamin comprises a nutrient selected                  to enhance growth of the at least one fermenting                  species. The metal comprises one or more of                  lithium, manganese, cobalt, nickel, copper,                  gallium, arsenic, selenium, aluminum, silicon,                  phosphorous, molybdenum, zirconium, silver,                  palladium, zinc, tungsten, and cadmium. The                  mineral comprises one or more of nitrogen,                  calcium, chloride, iron, iodine, potassium,                  molybdenum, magnesium, sulfur, phosphorous, and                  sodium. The liquid product is alcohol acid or                  their combinations. The gaseous substrate is                  syngas. The gasified solid substrate is biomass,                  coal, woodchips or municipal solids. The gaseous                  precursor is methane. The gaseous substrate is                  scrubbed before introduction into the bioreactor,                  where the gaseous precursor is scrubbed. The                  gaseous substrate is partially oxidized. The                  ionophore is an antibiotic. The ionophore is                  chosen from 12-crown-4, 15-crown-6, 18-crown-6,                  dibenzo-18-crown-6, and diaza-18- crown-6,                  A23187, 4-bromo-A23187, alamethicin, beauvencin,                  bapta(AM), calcimycin, cezomycin, carbonyl                  cyanide m-chlorophenyl hydrazone, CA 1001,                  enniatin, gramicidin A, hellebrin, ionomycin,                  lasalocid, monensin, nigericin, nonactin,                  phenamil methanesulfonate, salinomycin,                  tetonasin, ETH-2120, ETH 227, ETH 157,                  2,3:11,12-Didecalino-16-crown-5, DD-16-C-5, ETH                  4120, dodecylmethylmalonic acid                  bis((12-crown-4)methyl ester),                  bis((12-crown-4)methyl) 2,2-didodecylmalonate,                  4-tert-Butylcalix(4)arene-tetraacetic acid                  tetraethyl ester, BME 44, and valinomycin. The                  ionophore is present in the liquid feed in a                  concentration of 100 mM or less. The fermentation                  broth further contains water. The ionophore is                  soluble in the fermentation broth. The ionophore                  is immobilized and/or insoluble in the                  fermentation broth. Preferred Microorganisms: The                  competing species is a contaminant and the one                  fermenting species is a CI fixing microorganism.                  The fermenting species is an acetogen,                  homoacetogen or their combinations. The                  fermenting species and competing species are                  chosen from Acetitomaculum ruminis,                  Acetoanaerobium noterae, Acetogenium kivui,                  A.noterae, Acetohalobium arabaticum,                  Acetobacterium woodii, A.carbinolicum, A.malicum,                  A.wieringae, A.psammolithicum, A.fimetarium,                  A.paludosum, A.tundrae, A.bakii, Acetonema                  longum, Alkalibaculum bacchi, Blautia coccoides,                  B.hydrogenotrophica, B.producta, B.schenckii,                  Butyribacterium methylotrophicum,                  Caldanaerobacter subterraneous, C.subterraneous                  subsp. pacificus, Carboxydothermus                  hydrogenoformans, Clostridium aceticum,                  C.acetobutylieum, C.acetobutylicum,                  C.autoethanogenum, C.carboxidivorans, C.coskatii,                  C.difficile, C.drakei, C.formicaceticum,                  C.glycolicum, C.ljungdahlii, C.magnum,                  C.mayombei, C.methoxybenzovorans, C.pasteurianum,                  C.ragsdalei, C.scatologenes, C.thermoaceticum,                  C.ultunense, Desulfotomaculum kuznetsovii,                  Eschericichia coli, Eubacterium aggregans,                  E.limosum, Fuchsiella alkaliacetigena, Geobacter                  sulfurreducens, Holophaga foetida, Methanosarcina                  acetivorans, M.barkeri, Moorella mulderi,                  M.thermoacetica, M.thermoautotrophica, Oxobacter                  pfennigii, Peptostreptococcus productus,                  Ruminococcus productus, Sporomusa paucivorans,                  S.sphaeroides, S.malonica, S.termitida, S.ovata,                  S.silvacetica, S.acidovorans, S.rhize,                  Thermoanaerobacter kivui, Thermoacetogenium                  phaeum, and Treponema primitia. The competing                  species uses sodium gradients for ATP production,                  where the ionophore inhibits the sodium gradients                  from producing ATP. The fermenting species uses                  hydrogen gradients for ATP production.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107746065-A
TI Preparing high performance SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane comprises e.g. preparing seed crystal reaction liquid, heating, crystallizing, drying to obtain SAPO-34 molecular sieve seed crystal, sonicating , calcining and drying.
AU LIU S
   XU L
   LI H
   SUN G
   DING Y
   XIONG Y
   WU X
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 201818931B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high performance SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane comprises e.g. adding de-ionized water, aluminum source, tetraethyl ammonium hydroxide solution, silicon source and phosphoric acid, mixing, stirring and aging to obtain seed crystal reaction liquid, heating, crystallizing, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain SAPO-34 molecular sieve seed crystal, sonicating porous carrier in deionized water, ethanol and acetone and drying, calcining, placing porous support in a toluene solution of a silane coupling agent and allowing to react under a nitrogen atmosphere and vacuum-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high performance SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes silane coupling agent modified porous carrier which increases adhesive force between carrier surface and zeolite crystal, improves synthesis repeatability of SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane, shortens synthesis time of the film with membrane for hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation selectivity and can be applied in gas separation of carbon dioxide/methane and carbon dioxide/nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high performance SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane comprises (i) adding de-ionized water, aluminum source, tetraethyl ammonium hydroxide (TEAOH) solution, silicon source and phosphoric acid, mixing, stirring and aging to obtain seed crystal reaction liquid, heating at 170-210 degrees C, crystallizing for 24-36 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain SAPO-34 molecular sieve seed crystal; (ii) sonicating porous carrier in deionized water, ethanol and acetone for 1-2 hours and drying at 40-80 degrees C for 6-12 hours, calcining at 350-600 degrees C for 8-12 hours; (iii) placing porous support obtained in step (ii) in a toluene solution of a silane coupling agent and allowing to react under a nitrogen atmosphere at 80-120 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours and vacuum-drying for 24 hours; (iv) sealing porous carrier obtained in step (iii) at both ends and placing in ethanol solution of SAPO-34 molecular sieve seed crystal obtained in step (i) and drying to obtain carrier uniformly loaded with SAPO-34 seed; (v) mixing phosphoric acid, deionized water, aluminum source, silicon source, tetraethylammonium hydroxide and di-n-propylamine and aging at 50 degrees C for 48-72 hours to form zeolite membrane synthesis mother liquor, placing SAPO-34 seed-supported carrier obtained in step (iv) in a stainless steel reactor filled with synthetic mother liquor and hydrothermally crystallizing at 170-210 degrees C for 6-10 hours to form porous carrier outer wall SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane, washing and drying; and (vi) roasting SAPO-34 molecular sieve membrane tube obtained in step (v) at 450-600 degrees C for 4-8 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) mixing deionized water and tetraethylammonium hydroxide solution and stirring for 3-5 minutes, adding aluminum source at room temperature and stirring for 1-6 hours, adding silicon source dropwise after hydrolysis, stirring for 1-6 hours, adding phosphoric acid slowly dropwise and aging, stirring rapidly for 12-48 hours to obtain a seed reaction solution. In the step (vi) calcining at a reduction rate of not exceeding 2 K/minutes. Preferred Composition: In the step (i) ratio of aluminum oxide, phosphorus pentoxide, silicon dioxide, TEAOH and water is 1:(1-2):(0.3-0.6):(1~3):(55-150). In the steps (1-5) aluminum source comprises aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum hydroxide, aluminum n-butyl alcohol or aluminum chloride. In the steps (i) and (v) silicon source comprises silica sol, tetraethyl orthosilicate, methyl orthosilicate and/or sodium silicate. In the step (iii) silane coupling agent is 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane or gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane. The molar fraction of toluene solution of silane coupling agent in step (iii) is 1-4 mmol/l. The ethanol solution of SAPO-34 molecular sieve seed in step (iv) is 0.2-0.8%. In the step (v) synthetic mother solution comprises aluminum oxide, phosphorus pentoxide, silicon dioxide, TEAOH, diphenylamine and water is 1:(1-3):(0.2-0.6):(1-3):(1-3):(55-450).
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN KR2022037862-A; KR2423673-B1
TI Cathode material for lithium-sulfur batteries            using structure-controlled maxine comprises heating            exfoliated maxine in carbon dioxide gas atmosphere so            exfoliated maxine is subjected to carbon dioxide            treatment.
AU LEE D G
   AN C
   LEE J
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202244891D
AB    NOVELTY - Cathode material comprises heating exfoliated                maxine (d-MXene) in a carbon dioxide (CO2) gas                atmosphere so the exfoliated maxine (d-MXene) is                subjected to carbon dioxide (CO2) treatment, where                composite powder is formed by heating a mixed                powder in which maxin oxide powder and sulfur                powder are mixed, which is a powder produced by                oxidation.
   USE - The cathode material is useful for                lithium-sulfur batteries using structure-controlled                maxine.
   ADVANTAGE - The cathode material prevents shuttle                phenomenon by lithium polysulfide (LPS) due to the                strong chemical bond between the structure and                lithium polysulfide, prevents loss of active                material and degradation of cycling performance in                lithium-sulfur (Li-S) batteries, oxidized MXene has                resistance to natural oxidation, increased                durability of positive electrode and efficiency                during charging and discharging degradation can be                minimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) separator for lithium-sulfur batteries                using structure-controlled maxine, comprising oxide                maxine powder, a porous film formed of glass fibers                and in the form of a film, and surface of which is                coated with the maxin oxide powder;
   (2) lithium-sulfur battery, comprising a                cathode comprising cathode material for a                lithium-sulfur battery using the                structure-controlled Maxine, negative electrode                formed including lithium and lithium-sulfur battery                separator using the structure-controlled                Maxine;
   (3) manufacturing cathode material for                lithium-sulfur battery using a structure-controlled                maxine, comprising (i) reacting a MAX phase                material with a strong acid to obtain maxine, (ii)                providing vibration to a mixture of the maxine and                deionized water to obtain the exfoliated maxine,                (iii) heating the exfoliated maxin in a carbon                dioxide (CO2) gas atmosphere to obtain the maxin                oxide powder, (iv) heating the mixed powder in                which the maxin oxide powder and the sulfur powder                are mixed, cooling and pulverizing to obtain the                composite powder and (v) mixing the composite                powder, carbon material and binder; and
   (4) manufacturing separator for a                lithium-sulfur battery using a structure-controlled                maxine, comprising obtaining maxine by reacting a                MAX phase material with a strong acid, (a) forming                the porous film in the shape of a film using glass                fibers, (b) providing vibration to a mixture of the                maxine and deionized water to obtain the exfoliated                maxine, (c) heating the exfoliated maxin in a                carbon dioxide (CO2) gas atmosphere to obtain the                maxin oxide powder, and (d) coating the oxide maxin                powder on the surface of the porous film and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cathode material                  further comprises carbon material comprising                  carbon black, acetylene black, denka black and/or                  carbon fiber. The carbon material comprises 60-95                  pts. wt. composite powder. Preferred Components:                  The composite powder is prepared by a melt                  diffusion method using the oxide maxin powder and                  sulfur powder. The heating temperature for the                  exfoliated maxine is 700-1000 degrees C. The                  maxine (MXene) used to form exfoliated maxine is                  expressed by (Ti3C2(Tx)), where T is a surface                  terminator (T = O, OH, F). Preferred Method: The                  heating temperature for the peeled maxine is                  300-700 degrees C. In the step (iii), heating                  temperature for exfoliated maxine is 700-1000                  degrees C. In the step (v), 5-20 pts. wt. binder                  was added based on 100 pts. wt. carbon material.                  In the step (c), heating temperature for the                  oxide maxin powder is 300-700 degrees C. In the                  step (d), using a vacuum filtration method to                  coat the oxide maxin powder on the surface of the                  porous film.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of preparation of                S/Oxi-d-MXene900 that do not support the                claims.

PT P
PN US2021188633-A1; WO2021133661-A1; US11247897-B2
TI Production of base oils e.g. lube base oils, involves providing feed comprising carbon dioxide and natural gas, converting methane and carbon dioxide by dry reforming, discharging syngas, subjecting water to electrolysis, and converting syngas.
AU JAMAL A
   ALSOLAMI B H
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO AMERICAS (SAOI-C)
GA 202170218N
AB    NOVELTY - Production of base oil involves providing a feed (104) comprising carbon dioxide and natural gas comprising methane to a dry reformer vessel, converting the methane and the carbon dioxide via reformer catalyst in the dry reformer vessel into syngas (106) comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen, discharging the syngas from the dry reformer vessel to a Fischer-Tropsch (FT) reactor vessel, subjecting water to electrolysis to generate supplemental hydrogen, adding the supplemental hydrogen to the syngas in route to the FT reactor vessel to increase a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas to at least 1.2, and converting the syngas via FT catalyst in the FT reactor vessel into intermediate products comprising FT waxes. The converting step involves dry reforming and does not involve autothermal reforming (ATR).
   USE - Production of base oils e.g. lube base oils (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) Base-oil production system (100), which comprises a dry reformer vessel comprising a reformer catalyst to convert a feed comprising carbon dioxide and natural gas into syngas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen via dry reforming and an outlet to discharge the syngas via a conduit to a Fischer-Tropsch (FT) reactor vessel operationally coupled to the dry reformer vessel, a water electrolysis cell to generate supplemental hydrogen for addition via a control valve to the conduit conveying the syngas, in which the FT reactor vessel comprises an FT catalyst to convert the syngas into FT intermediate products comprising FT waxes, and
   (2) System for producing Fischer-Tropsch (FT) waxes, which comprises the dry reformer vessel comprising a reformer catalyst to dry reform a feed comprising natural gas and at least 30 vol.% carbon dioxide into syngas and an inlet to receive the feed and an outlet to discharge the syngas via a conduit to a FT reactor vessel, a water electrolysis unit comprising an electrolytic cell to generate additional hydrogen to provide via a control valve to the conduit conveying the syngas, in which the FT reactor vessel comprising an FT catalyst to convert the syngas into FT reactor products comprising at least 50 wt.% FT waxes comprising at least nineteen carbons.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the base-oil production system.
   Base-oil production system (100)
   Dry reformer (102)
   Feed (104)
   Syngas (106)
   Fischer-Tropsch reactor (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves discharging an effluent comprising the intermediate products from the FT reactor vessel, and processing the effluent to generate a base oil. The method involves adding the supplemental hydrogen to increases the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas to 1.5-2.5, preferably 1.2-2.5. The production of base oil involves reforming methane in presence of carbon dioxide and catalyst in a dry reformer (102) into syngas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen, excluding steam reforming and autothermal reforming (ATR), discharging the syngas from the dry reformer to a Fischer-Tropsch (FT) reactor (108), providing supplemental hydrogen from a water electrolysis cell to a conduit conveying the syngas to the FT reactor to increase a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas upstream of the FT reactor, converting the syngas via FT catalyst in the FT reactor into FT reactor products comprising FT waxes, discharging an effluent comprising the FT reactor products from the FT reactor, and processing the effluent to produce a base oil. The reforming does not involve mixed-steam carbon dioxide reforming (MSCR).Preferred Components: The reformer catalyst comprises magnesium oxide nanoparticles promoted with nickel and molybdenum. The FT catalyst comprises a transition metal comprising cobalt, iron and/or ruthenium. The catalyst in the dry reformer comprises a solid oxide support with an active metal site available for reaction.Preferred Properties: The operating pressure of the dry reformer vessel is more than an operating pressure of the FT reactor vessel to give a pressure differential adequate as a motive force for flow of the syngas from the dry reformer vessel to the FT reactor vessel. The operating pressure of the dry reformer is at least 25 bar, and the operating temperature of the dry reformer is 650-1000degrees Celsius.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base oil comprises an American petroleum institute (API) group III base oil.Preferred Composition: The intermediate products comprise at least 50 wt.% FT waxes. The feed comprises 30-70 vol.% natural gas or methane, 30-70 vol.%, preferably at least 30 vol.% carbon dioxide, less than 1 vol.% oxygen, in which additional oxygen is not provided to the dry reformer vessel, less than 1 mol% water, and in which steam is not provided to the dry reformer vessel, and less than 1 mol% natural gas. The syngas discharged from the dry reformer vessel comprises hydrogen to carbon monoxide in molar ratio of 0.95-1.05. The base oil comprises at less than 0.03 wt.% sulfur.Preferred Property: The base oil has viscosity index of at least 120.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The dry reformer vessel comprises a fixed-bed reactor vessel comprising the reformer catalyst in a fixed bed. The fixed-bed reactor comprises a multi-tubular fixed-bed reactor. The dry reformer vessel is not configured to receive additional oxygen in operation. The dry reformer vessel is not configured for steam reforming. The dry reformer vessel is not configured for mixed-steam carbon dioxide reforming (MSCR) and autothermal reforming (ATR). The system comprises a separation system comprising a distillation column and a conversion system comprising a reactor vessel to process the effluent from the FT reactor vessel to produce the base oil, at least one feed conduit to convey the feed to the dry reformer vessel, and a control system to alter a set point of the control valve to adjust an amount of the additional hydrogen provided to the conduit conveying the syngas. The dry reformer vessel is not configured to receive steam in operation.

The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN US2020317594-A1; US11111199-B2
TI Preparing formaldehyde from carbon dioxide comprises obtaining bis(silyl)acetal and generating formaldehyde by e.g. adding fluoride compound to solution of bis(silyl)acetal in solvent and reacting bis(silyl)acetal with water in solvent.
AU PARKIN G
   STRATER Z
   RAUCH M
AE UNIV COLUMBIA NEW YORK (UYCO-C)
GA 2020981735
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M1) formaldehyde from carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) obtaining a bis(silyl)acetal; and (b) generating formaldehyde by any one of (i) adding a fluoride compound to a solution of the bis(silyl)acetal in a solvent, (ii) reacting the bis(silyl)acetal with water in a solvent, or (iii) reacting the bis(silyl)acetal with an acid in a solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing formaldehyde from carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) completing (M2) a transformation that involves formaldehyde, comprising substituting formaldehyde with a bis(silyl)acetal;
   (2) incorporating (M3) carbon derived from carbon dioxide into a complex organic molecule derived from formaldehyde, comprising (a1) obtaining a bis(silyl)acetal by the reaction between carbon dioxide and a silane in the presence of a compound prepared from a multidentate ligand including 11,11,13,13,32,32-hexamethyl-9,15,30-tripropyl-2- lambda (4),9,15,22- lambda (4),23- lambda (4),30-hexaaza-11,13,32-trisila-1-magnesanonacyclo(10.10.10.0(1,12).0(2,10).0(3,8).0(14,22).0(16,21).0(23,31).0(24,29))dotriaconta-2(10),3,5,7,14(22),16,18,20,23(31),24(29),25,27-dodecaene or 1,11,11,13,13,32,32-heptamethyl-9,15,30-tripropyl-2- lambda (4),9,15,22- lambda (4),23- lambda (4),30-hexaaza-11,13,32-trisila-1-magnesanonacyclo(10.10.10.0(1,12).0(2,10).0(3,8).0(14,22).0(16,21).0(23,31).0(24,29))dotriaconta-2(10),3,5,7,14(22),16,18,20,23(31),24(29),25,27-dodecaene and tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane, where the silane is of formula ((R)3SiH) (I), and (b1) substituting formaldehyde with the bis(silyl)acetal in a reaction that generates the complex organic molecule;
   (3) generating (M4) an isotopologue of a complex organic molecule derived from formaldehyde, comprising generating isotope-labeled bis(silyl)acetal by using isotope-labeled carbon dioxide and/or isotope-labeled silane in the reaction mixture, and generating the isotopologue of the complex organic molecule by using the obtained isotope-labeled bis(silyl)acetal;
   (4) a probe obtained by the method (M4); and
   (5) carrying out (M5) a chemical reaction with a formaldehyde surrogate comprising replacing formaldehyde in the chemical reaction with a bis(silyl)acetal.
   R = H, alkyl or aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method (M2), the transformation comprises forming a terminal olefin in a Wittig reaction, synthesizing hexamine from ammonia, Pictet Spengler reaction for preparing heterocycles, or synthesizing benzazoles. In the method (M3): the reaction comprises forming a terminal olefin in a Wittig reaction, synthesizing hexamine from ammonia, Pictet Spengler reaction for preparing heterocycles, or synthesizing benzazoles; the bis(silyl)acetal generated in step (a1) is isotope-labeled by using isotope-labeled carbon dioxide and/or isotope-labeled silane in the reaction mixture, where the carbon dioxide is isotope-labeled using 11carbon, 13carbon, or 14carbon, and the silane is isotope-labeled using 2hydrogen or 3hydrogen; and the bis(silyl)acetal generated in step (a1) is isotope-labeled by using 13carbon-labeled carbon dioxide and/or 2hydrogen-labeled silane in the reaction mixture. In the method (M5), the chemical reaction involves: forming bonds including carbon-carbon, carbon-nitrogen, carbon-oxygen or carbon-sulfur and/or forming heterocycles; and those depicted in figures-3 to figures-5 of the specification. Preferred Components: The bis(silyl)acetal is 1,1,1,5,5,5-hexaphenyl-2,4-dioxa-1,5-disilapentane. The formaldehyde generated is anhydrous. The fluoride compound is selected from cesium fluoride (preferred), potassium fluoride, sodium fluoride, lithium fluoride, tetra(n-butyl)ammonium fluoride or trimethyltin fluoride. The solvent in each of step (b)(i), step (b)(ii) and step (b)(iii) comprises acetonitrile, acetone, dimethylformamide, dimethylsulfoxide, gamma -butyrolactone, ethers, aromatic solvents or aliphatic solvents. The solvent in step (b)(i) is acetonitrile, the solvent in step (b)(ii) is dimethylsulfoxide, and the solvent in step (b)(iii) is acetonitrile. The acid comprises sulfuric acid (preferred), nitric acid, hydrohalic acids, carboxylic acid or phosphoric acid. The isotopologue generated is a probe for positron emission tomography (PET), where the probe for PET comprises e.g. (11carbon) acetate, (11carbon) carfentanil, (11carbon) pittsburgh compound B, (11carbon) raclopride, (11carbon) verapamil, (11carbon) N-methylspiperone, (11carbon) martinostat, (11carbon) methionine and (11carbon) choline. The probe is a diagnostic probe, where the probe is PET probe.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111111464-A; CN111111464-B
TI Ultra-high carbon dioxide gas selective separation composite membrane structure comprises e.g. electrospinning polyacrylonitrile fiber film layer, high gas selectivity cyclodextrin metal organic framework material layer and polymer layer.
AU ZHANG S
   FAN S
   LI B
AE NANJING HEFENG INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU JITRI ADVANCED POLYMER MATERIALS (JIAN-Non-standard)
   NANJING HEFENG INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020421726
AB    NOVELTY - Ultra-high carbon dioxide gas selective separation composite membrane structure comprises an electrospinning polyacrylonitrile (PAN) fiber film layer, a high gas selectivity cyclodextrin metal organic framework material (MOF) layer and a polymer layer which are arranged from bottom to top to form a three-layer structure. The three-layer structure is tightly connected together through intermolecular hydrogen bond, where layer of cyclodextrin MOF material with high gas selectivity in the middle is uniformly dispersed and tightly combined with the upper and lower layers. The entire film is in an optically transparent state with a light transmittance greater than 60%.
   USE - Used as ultra-high carbon dioxide gas selective separation composite membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has transmission efficiency ratio (selectivity carbon-dioxide/nitrogen) for carbon dioxide and nitrogen of 100-120, good mechanical properties, sufficient capacity to separate, capture and purify carbon-dioxide, thus it can reduce the greenhouse effect, and reducing air pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the composite membrane structure, comprising preparing PAN electrospinning fiber film by taking PAN dissolved in N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF) solution (concentration 5-15 wt.%), and allowing it to defoam to obtain a spinning solution, spraying onto the tin foil through a spinneret of electrospinning machine, and peeling off tin foil from the cylinder to obtain a fiber membrane with a thickness of 0.5-5 mu m; (ii) preparing PAN-MOF layer film by mixing suitable amount of MOF crystals with chloroform (concentration 8-20 wt.%), performing magnetic stirring at room temperature under ultrasound to obtain uniformly dispersed MOF dispersion, adjusting the speed of the coating machine from 0.2-0.5 thousand revolutions/minute (rpm), applying MOF dispersion to PAN layer to form a uniformly distributed MOF layer with MOF layer thickness of 3-10 mu m; (iii) preparing PAN-MOF-polymer composite membrane by dissolving a suitable amount of polymer in tetrahydrofuran or chloroform (concentration 10-25 wt.%), dissolving by magnetic stirring at room temperature, removing bubbles by ultrasound, placing the PAN-MOF film prepared in the previous step on the homogenizer, adjusting to suitable speed again (0.5-1 thousand rpm), coating bubble-removing polymer solution to form a PAN-MOF-Polymer film with polymer film thickness of 0.2-4 mu m; and (iv) placing the prepared membrane at room temperature for one day, removing most of the solvent and vacuum drying in an oven.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The cyclodextrin MOF material layer with high gas selectivity is a metal organic framework material formed by cyclodextrin or cyclodextrin derivatives through self-assembly with metal ions, including alpha -dextrin/sodium MOF, alpha -cyclodextrin/potassium MOF, beta -cyclodextrin/sodium MOF, beta -cyclodextrin/potassium MOF, gamma -cyclodextrin/sodium MOF or gamma -cyclodextrin/potassium MOF. The crystal size of cyclodextrin MOF is between 0.15-10 mu m. The cyclodextrin-based MOF is coated on the PAN layer by a spin coating method. The polymer layer is a polar polymer capable of forming a strong hydrogen bond with the cyclodextrin MOF, including polyurethane, polyvinyl alcohol or polyacrylic acid.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN110963520-A
TI Producing cubic calcium carbonate with lime foam by calcining lime foam in lime kiln, adding quick lime and water, slag removing, entering refined lime milk, and adding crystal form control agent, and dehydrating, crushing and packaging.
AU WEN J
AE HUNAN JINJIAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2020289620
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing cubic calcium carbonate with lime foam, involves (a) calcining lime foam in lime kiln is used as raw material, (b) adding the quick lime and water in step (a) to the drum digester to carry out the digestion reaction to obtain crude cooked lime, (c) 3-5 levels of slag removing the crude slaked lime in step (b) and aging for 4-6 hours to obtain refined lime milk, (d) entering the refined lime milk in step (c) the carbonization tower, and adding 0.1-0.5% crystal form control agent of amino acid, protein, sucrose, controlling the carbon dioxide concentration between 25-100%, with continuous carbon dioxide gas flow for 3-6 hours, where the emulsion in the carbonization tower is neutral, continuously passing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction for 2-4 hours to obtain cubic calcium carbonate emulsion, and (e) dehydrating the calcium carbonate emulsion in step (d), drying, crushing and packaging to obtain a cubic calcium carbonate product.
   USE - The method is used for producing cubic calcium carbonate with lime foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves existing calcium carbonate production uses calcium carbide slag and limestone as raw materials, when compared to complex process, uneven particle size and poor dispersion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing cubic calcium carbonate with lime foam involves (a) calcining lime foam in lime kiln is used as raw material, grinding and sieving to a particle size of not less than 25 mm to obtain quick lime, (b) adding the quick lime and water in step (a) to the drum digester to carry out the digestion reaction to obtain crude cooked lime, (c) 3-5 levels of slag removing the crude slaked lime in step (b) and aging for 4-6 hours to obtain refined lime milk, (d) entering the refined lime milk in step (c) the carbonization tower, the concentration is controlled at 10-15 degrees C, and controlling the temperature at 70-90 degrees C, and adding 0.1-0.5% crystal form control agent of amino acid, protein, sucrose, controlling the carbon dioxide concentration between 25-100%, with continuous carbon dioxide gas flow for 3-6 hours, where the emulsion in the carbonization tower is neutral, continuously passing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction for 2-4 hours to obtain cubic calcium carbonate emulsion, and (e) dehydrating the calcium carbonate emulsion in step (d), drying, crushing and packaging to obtain a cubic calcium carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of quicklime to water during the digestion reaction is 1:4-8. Preferred Method: The dehydration step comprises pressing water content of the filter cake after and dehydrating by the filter press by 30-35%. In drying process, secondary drying is used, the moisture content of the outlet of the primary blade dryer is less than or equal to 25%, and the moisture content of the outlet of the secondary drum dryer is 0.5%. Preferred Property: The pH value of the finished cubic calcium carbonate is 8-10 and the whiteness is greater than or equal to 92.

AB    NOVELTY - Testing for reaction law of carbon dioxide and                rock under different gas-water ratio condition                comprises e.g. (i) obtaining the core and cutting                the core into multiple stratum samples, measuring                the first permeability of the formation rock                sample, using micro CT for scanning the formation                rock sample, and inverting the pore network                structure of the first core, taking the formation                water near the core, dividing the formation water                into multiple parts to obtain multiple parts of                carbon dioxide, matching formation water and carbon                dioxide into combination of formation water and                carbon dioxide with different ratios, (ii) placing                the formation rock sample and the formation water                of the first ratio into the reaction vessel and                reacting with the carbon dioxide of the first                ratio, and(iii) measuring the second permeability                of the formation rock sample, using micro-CT to                scan the formation rock sample, and reacting with                formation water and carbon dioxide in the first                ratio.
   USE - The method is useful for testing for reaction                law of carbon dioxide and rock under different                gas-water ratio condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Testing for reaction law of carbon dioxide and                rock under different gas-water ratio condition                comprises (i) obtaining the core and cut the core                into multiple stratum samples, measuring the first                permeability of the formation rock sample, using                micro CT for scanning the formation rock sample,                and inverting the pore network structure of the                first core, taking the formation water near the                core, dividing the formation water into multiple                parts to obtain multiple parts of carbon dioxide,                matching formation water and carbon dioxide into                combination of formation water and carbon dioxide                with different ratios, (ii) placing the formation                rock sample and the formation water of the first                ratio into the reaction vessel and reacting with                the carbon dioxide of the first ratio, (iii)                measuring the second permeability of the formation                rock sample, using micro-CT to scan the formation                rock sample, reacting with formation water and                carbon dioxide in the first ratio, and reversing                the second pore network structure, (iv) replacing                the formation water and carbon dioxide in the first                ratio with the formation water and carbon dioxide                in the second ratio, and repeating steps (ii) to                (iii) to obtain the third pore network structure                and the third permeability of the formation rock                sample reacted with the formation water and carbon                dioxide of the second ratio, and (v) repeating step                (iv) to obtain pore network structure data and                permeability data after the reaction of formation                water and carbon dioxide with the formation rock                sample under different combinations of ratios, and                the pore network structure and permeability change                laws of formation rock samples with different                ratios of carbon dioxide to formation water are                classified.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) further                  comprises measuring the first element                  distribution content of the formation water,                  measuring the distribution of the second element                  in the formation rock sample, and finding out the                  critical ratio of carbon dioxide and water that                  can dissolve calcium scale, strontium scale                  and/or barium scale in formation rock samples,                  and(ii) where the carbon dioxide content in the                  first ratio is selected before and after the                  critical ratio. The obtained stratum rock sample                  is cube with a side length of 6 mm. The method                  comprises when measuring the gap network                  structure of the stratum rock sample, cutting the                  stratum rock sample into multiple layers when the                  stratum rock sample is scanned by micro-CT, and                  performing micro CT scan of each layer of the                  rock sample, and inverting the pore network                  structure of the formation rock sample, when                  measuring the permeability of the formation rock                  sample, using the PDP-200 pulse core permeability                  tester for measuring the permeability of the                  formation rock sample, when performing element                  analysis on the formation aqueous solution, using                  ion chromatograph for measuring the element                  distribution content of the formation water. In                  the step (i), where obtained stratum rock samples                  are cores of various geology, and performing the                  tests of steps (i)-(v) on each core to obtain                  pore network structure and permeability change                  law of different cores. In the step (ii), the                  method comprises placing the formation rock                  sample and the formation water of the first                  proportion into the reaction vessel, and reacting                  with the carbon dioxide of the first proportion,                  placing the formation rock sample and the                  formation water of the first ratio into the                  reactor, vacuuming and sealing the inside of the                  reactor, and injecting the first proportion of                  carbon dioxide into the vacuum reactor for                  reacting. The method further comprises heating                  the reactor temperature to 45 degrees C to make                  the carbon dioxide gas in the reactor and                  reacting with the formation water, using booster                  pump for pressurizing the reactor to make the                  reactor pressure lower than the set target                  pressure, and setting the reactor, simulating                  formation temperature and pressure conditions,                  and reacting for 1-5 hours.

TI Extraction of grapeseed oil involves drying grapeseed and grinding, mixing glass microsphere in grapeseed powder, cooling carbon dioxide and pressurizing, extracting carbon dioxide expansion ethanol fluid, and evaporating.
AU DENG G
   LI H
   HUANG L
   ANDREA D
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NORMAL (UYZN-C)
GA 202075957C
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of grapeseed oil from carbon dioxide expanded ethanol circulation involves (i) drying the grapeseed and grinding to obtain grapeseed powder, (ii) mixing after the glass microsphere in the grapeseed powder, (iii) cooling the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank, mixing the ethanol from the reagent container and heating the pre-heating coil to the setting temperature of the extracting kettle, and (iv) extracting the carbon dioxide expansion ethanol fluid in the material, de-pressurizing the obtained extracting liquid and carbon dioxide expansion ethanol fluid through the expansion valve, discharging the separated carbon dioxide gas from the top of the separating kettle, returning to the carbon dioxide storage tank, collecting the extracting liquid at the bottom of the separating kettle, rotating and evaporating to obtain grapeseed oil and ethanol, returning the ethanol to the reagent container and recycling the ethanol, and collecting.
   USE - Extraction of grapeseed oil from carbon dioxide expanded ethanol circulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of grapeseed oil having excellent oil extraction efficiency and reduced use of organic solvents, under mild operating conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of grapeseed oil from carbon dioxide expanded ethanol circulation involves (i) drying the grapeseed and grinding to obtain grapeseed powder, (ii) mixing after the glass microsphere in the grapeseed powder into the extraction kettle, (iii) cooling the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank at 3-5 degrees C and pressurizing by the plunger pump, mixing the ethanol from the reagent container by the high-efficiency liquid phase pump and pumping the carbon dioxide by the plunger pump through the mixer, heating the pre-heating coil to the setting temperature of the extracting kettle and entering the extracting kettle as the carbon dioxide expanding ethanol fluid, and (iv) extracting the carbon dioxide expansion ethanol fluid in the material in the extraction kettle for 2-2.5 hours, de-pressurizing the obtained extracting liquid and carbon dioxide expansion ethanol fluid through the expansion valve of the extraction kettle bottom outlet for pressure release, entering into the separation kettle for gas-liquid separation, discharging the separated carbon dioxide gas from the top of the separating kettle, returning to the carbon dioxide storage tank after recycling treatment, collecting the extracting liquid at the bottom of the separating kettle, rotating and evaporating to obtain grapeseed oil and ethanol, returning the ethanol to the reagent container and recycling the ethanol, and collecting. The carbon dioxide has molar fraction of 0.1-0.5 mol/mol. The extraction kettle in step (iii) is performed at 40-60 degrees C, and has pressure of 3.8-10.0 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (iv) involves discharging the carbon dioxide gas from the top of the separation kettle is recovered by the flash tank, pressurizing the compressor and cooled to liquid, and returning to the carbon dioxide storage tank for recycling, cooling the ethanol and returning the reagent container for recycling use, discharging the carbon dioxide gas discharged from the top of the separation kettle passes through the gas rotameter and recovering by the flash tank. In step (ii), involves mixing the grapeseed powder and glass beads with a diameter of 2-5 mm, in a mass ratio of 1:(0.95-1.05) and adding into the extraction kettle. In step (i), involves using drying the grapeseeds at 55 degrees C for 48 hours, and grinding the grapeseeds into a grapeseed powder with a diameter of 0.45 mm or less using a grinder. Preferred Condition: In step (iv), the separation kettle is performed at 40 plus minus 5 degrees C and pressure of 1-2 MPa to perform gas-liquid separation. In step (iii), the extraction kettle is preferably performed at 40-43 degrees C and pressure of 7.1-7.5 MPa, and the carbon dioxide having mole fraction of preferably 0.3 mol/mol.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves roasting kaolin and sodium hydroxide to                  obtain a roasted product and dissolving the                  calcined product with water to leach the mixed                  solution of the sodium aluminosilicate and the                  sodium silicate. The method involves adding                  porous material to the organic solution of                  polyethyleneimine for dispersion, mixing and                  immersion, evaporating for 3.5-4.5 hours, and                  drying at 45-55 degrees C for 4.5-5.5 hours. The                  step (a) involves: placing the mixed solution of                  the sodium aluminosilicate and the sodium                  silicate at 25-85 degrees C, introducing the                  carbon dioxide to react under the stirring                  condition of 250-350 rpm, stopping the reaction                  when the pH of the reaction system is 9-9.8,                  filtering the reaction product, washing and                  drying to obtain a porous material precursor;                  placing the porous material precursor at 400-550                  degrees C, calcining in an oxygen atmosphere for                  3.5-4.5 hours, and cooling and grinding to less                  than or equal to 200 mesh to obtain the porous                  material. The step of roasting involves placing                  the kaolin and the sodium hydroxide at 500-800                  degrees C in an air atmosphere to react for 1.5-3                  hours, where the mass ratio of the kaolin to the                  sodium hydroxide is 1:0.8-1:1.6, and the mass                  ratio of aluminum oxide to silicon dioxide in the                  kaolin is 1:1-1:6. The step of loading involves                  mixing the polyethyleneimine and the organic                  solvent to a mass ratio of 1:20-1:200 to obtain                  an organic solution of polyethyleneimine, loading                  the organic solution of polyethyleneimine on the                  porous material to obtain the carbon dioxide                  adsorbent, and the solvent comprise one or a                  mixed solvent of methanol, ethanol or acetone.                  The flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 800-4000                  ml/minute per liter of the mixed solution of the                  sodium aluminosilicate and the sodium silicate.                  Preferred Components: The concentration of carbon                  dioxide is 12-20 vol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid amine active                  material is polyethyleneimine.

PN CN111229195-A
TI Preparing electro-reduction carbon dioxide            catalytic material comprises mixing melamine and amino            acid into a precursor of catalytic material, and            carbonizing precursor of the catalytic material in an            inert atmosphere.
AU HUANG Y
   LIU S
   FAN S
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202051998K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electro-reduction carbon dioxide                catalytic material comprises (i) mixing melamine                and amino acid into a precursor of catalytic                material, and (ii) carbonizing precursor of the                catalytic material at 800-1200 degrees C in an                inert atmosphere at high temperature, obtaining                doped graphene material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                electro-reduction carbon dioxide catalytic                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) electroreduction carbon dioxide catalytic                material prepared by above mentioned method;
   (2) use of electro-reduction carbon dioxide                catalytic material in the catalytic reaction of                electro-reduction of carbon dioxide; and
   (3) gas diffusion electrode loaded with carbon                dioxide electroreduction catalyst material used in                the electroreduction carbon dioxide catalytic                reaction, where in the gas diffusion electrode is                loaded with carbon dioxide electroreduction                catalytic material, and the weight of the supported                carbon dioxide electroreduction catalytic material                is 0.1-10 mg/cm2, and the gas diffusion electrode                is carbon paper, carbon felt, carbon cloth or                carbon fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  melamine: amino acid in the precursor of the                  catalytic material is 1:1-8:1, and the amino acid                  is cysteine, glycine, alanine, phenylalanine, or                  tryptophan. Preferred Method: The carbonization                  time of the electro-reduction carbon dixoide                  catalytic material is 10 minutes to 10 hours, the                  inert atmosphere high-temperature carbonization                  reactor is a tube furnace, and the atmosphere is                  argon gas, helium gas and/or nitrogen gas. The                  high-temperature carbonization process comprises                  heating catalyst material precursor from room                  temperature to 150-450 degrees C at a rate of 1-3                  degrees C/minute in an inert atmosphere,                  maintaining for 0.5-4 hours, heating to 450-650                  degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, and                  maintaining for 0.5-4 hours, heating to 800-1200                  degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute,                  maintaining for 0.5-5 hours, naturally cooling to                  room temperature, and taking out                  electro-reduction carbon dioxide catalytic                  material. The method comprises placing melamine                  and amino acid in a ball mill tank for ball                  milling to obtain a homogeneously mixed catalyst                  material precursor, and adding zirconia boat for                  high-temperature carbonization to obtain doped                  graphene material, which is the electro-reduction                  carbon dioxide catalytic material. The gas                  diffusion electrode of the carbon dixoide                  electroreduction catalytic material is obtained                  by dispersing the doped squalene into the mixed                  solution of isopropyl alcohol and water, adding                  1-10 wt.% perfluorosulfonic acid resin Nafion                  solution, stirring, obtaining mixed solution,                  coating obtained mixed solution on the gas                  diffusion electrode, drying, where the volume                  ratio of isopropyl alcohol, and water in the                  mixed solution is 1:5-5:1, the ratio of the                  carbon material to the mixed solution of                  isopropyl alcohol and water is 1-20 mg: 1 ml, and                  the volume ratio of the 1-10 wt.%                  perfluorosulfonic acid resin Nafion solution and                  mixed solution of isopropyl alcohol and water is                  1:100-1:10. The drying is vacuum drying at 60-120                  degrees C.

AU GROUT L

GA 2021C9751N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for milling high-fat comestible products into powder involves applying solid carbon dioxide to freeze-dried high-fat comestible product and cooling the freeze-dried comestible product below the melting temperature of the contained fat and feeding the mixture, or combination of, solid carbon dioxide and cooled freeze-dried comestible product mixture into a milling apparatus.
   USE - The method is useful for milling high-fat comestible products into powder (claimed), preferably milling animal organs and glands.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cost-effective milling of high-fat or high-oil freeze-dried product/food into powder with particle size less than 1000 micron, which is suited to small batch size production for use in nutraceuticals and functional food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) milling apparatus; and
   (2) high-fat comestible powder produced by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The freeze-dried high-fat comestible product is produced by freeze drying a high-fat comestible meat product that is been minced, chopped or cubed and layered between layers of carbon dioxide (CO2) snow in a contained device. The product is initially cooled to less than its brittle point and is maintained up to 20 degrees C below the melting point of the fat during the milling operation. The solid CO2 is mixed, interspersed or layered through the freeze-dried comestible product prior to being fed into the milling apparatus. The method further includes evaporating solid CO2 prior to packaging. Preferred Components: The solid CO2 includes dry ice pellets and CO2 snow. Preferred Product: The powder is free flowing and the particles are less than 1000 mu m.

GA 2021C3582X
AB    NOVELTY - A hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material is prepared by coating hexagonal boron nitride with thin layer of cuprous oxide. The hexagonal boron nitride is a regular octahedron. The diameter of the hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material is 500 nm to 1 mum.
   USE - Hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce ethylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material solves the selectivity and stability problems faced by copper-based catalysts. The hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material has excellent ethylene product selectivity and stability when compared with existing product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material; and
   (2) use of the hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide material for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce ethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of boric acid to urea is 1:24. The working electrode is hydrophobic carbon paper loaded with the hexagonal boron nitride doped cuprous oxide. The reference electrode is silver/silver chloride electrode. The additive electrode is a platinum electrode. In this electrolytic cell, the working electrode and the reference electrode are placed in the cathode chamber, and the additive electrode is placed in the anode chamber. The cathode chamber and the anode chamber are separated by a proton exchange membrane.
