PT P
PN CN112624172-A
TI Preparing barium carbonate comprises dissolving barium hydroxide in high-purity water, adding aqueous ammonia, passing carbon dioxide, performing precipitation filtration, and drying barium carbonate precipitate in desiccator.
AU LIN F
AE YICHANG HUAHAO NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (YICH-Non-standard)
GA 202140263C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing barium carbonate, comprises (1) adding barium hydroxide into high-purity water, dissolving in a dissolving pump, and obtaining a barium hydroxide solution, (2) adding aqueous ammonia to the barium hydroxide solution, and stirring for 10-60 minutes at a constant temperature at 30-50 degrees C, (3) passing carbon dioxide into the barium hydroxide solution with aqueous ammonia through the micro-reactor under agitation, and stopping the passing of carbon dioxide when the pH=6-9 to obtain a barium carbonate solution, (iv) heating and reacting the barium carbonate solution, and performing precipitation filtration to obtain barium carbonate precipitate, and (5) drying the barium carbonate precipitate in a desiccator to obtain high-purity electronic grade barium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing barium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has no side product, high purity and it will not cause pollution to the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), heating the high-purity water to 30-50 degrees C before being mixed with barium hydroxide, where the barium hydroxide accounts for 75-100% of the weight of the high-purity water, heating to 30-80 degrees C and stirring to dissolve, and configuring into barium hydroxide solution. In the step (3), the carbon dioxide is derived from the reaction of barium carbonate ore and hydrochloric acid to prepare carbon dioxide. The method comprises mixing barium carbonate ore with hydrochloric acid, hydrogen peroxide and water in a weight percentage of (0.5-0.7): (0.25-0.45): (0.05-0.15) to prepare carbon dioxide gas, and the addition amount of hydrochloric acid is 29-35 wt.%, the addition amount of hydrogen peroxide greater than or equal to 30 wt.%. In the step (3), the prepared carbon dioxide gas is used in the operation after being washed with water once, saturated sodium bicarbonate solution, washed with water twice, and dried by a desiccant. In the step (3), the speed of introducing carbon dioxide is 0.5-3 kg/min, the reaction temperature of the micro-reaction device is 43-55 degrees C, and the residence time of the micro-reaction device is 35-65 minutes. In the step (4), heating and reacting the barium carbonate solution, where the specific operation is heating the barium carbonate solution to boiling within 25-35 minutes, keep boiling for 10-30 minutes, and cooling to 44-51 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (2), the amount of aqueous ammonia is 1-10 wt.% of the barium hydroxide solution. In the step (3), the molar ratio of barium hydroxide to carbon dioxide is 1: (1.1-1.3). In the step (3), the micro-reactor device is a reactor with micro-aperture channel.

PN CN112495411-A; CN112495411-B
TI Preparation method of carbon nitride nanosheet            loaded indium vanadate quantum dot photocatalyst useful            for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to            carbon monoxide, comprises e.g. hydro-thermally            reacting the reaction.
AU CHEN Z
   GONG S
   TENG X
   NIU Y
   XU M
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2021286521
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of carbon nitride nanosheet                loaded indium vanadate quantum dot photocatalyst                comprises (i) respectively dissolving indium                trichloride tetrahydrate and sodium orthovanadate                in de-ionized water, obtaining indium chloride                aqueous solution and sodium orthovanadate aqueous                solution, dropping indium chloride solution into                the sodium orthovanadate aqueous solution,                adjusting the pH to clear, obtaining InVO4                precursor solution; and (ii) taking graphitic                carbon nitride (g-C3N4) ultrasonic dispersing into                InVO4 precursor solution, hydro-thermally reacting,                centrifuging the obtained product, washing and                drying to obtain the target product.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst uses sunlight with lambda                less than 420 nm to irradiate the photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon nitride nanosheet loaded indium vanadate                quantum dot photocatalyst prepared by the                preparation method of photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  molar ratio of indium trichloride tetrahydrate                  and sodium orthovanadate is 1:1 and the pH is                  adjusted to 1-3. In the step (ii), the                  concentration of the InVO4 precursor solution is                  0.3 mmol/ml and the ratio of the graphitic carbon                  nitride and InVO4 precursor solution is 15-100                  mg: 30 ml. Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  the hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 180                  degrees C for 18 hours and the preparation of                  g-C3N4 is prepared taking melamine, heating to                  500-550 degrees C and calcinating for 2-6 hours                  by high temperature pyrolysis in an air                  atmosphere.

AE JBKLAB CO LTD (JBKL-Non-standard)
GA 202123540Q
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid composition containing anthocyanin-based compound, comprises water or an organic solvent for solubilizing the anthocyanin-based compound and carbon dioxide dissolved in a saturated concentration of more than 80%.
   USE - The liquid composition containing anthocyanin-based compound is useful in development of cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition improves storage stability of anthocyanin compound by more than 30%, anthocyanin compound can be stably stored for a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing a liquid composition, comprising dissolving the anthocyanin-based compound powder in water or an organic solvent to prepare an anthocyanin-based compound solution, preparing a mixed solution by adding water or an organic solvent to the anthocyanin-based compound solution and injecting carbon dioxide into the mixed solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a result of measuring the amount of anthocyanin-based compound remaining in each solution when 21 days have elapsed after the start of the test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The anthocyanin-based compounds are black rice, black bean, black currant, chokeberry, black chokeberry, cranberry, Aronia, mulberry, cherry, raspberry, blueberry, blackberry, eggplant, acai, grapefruit and grapes isolated from plants.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anthocyanin-based compounds is at least one of peonidin, cyanidin 3-arabinoside, cyanidin-3-(xylosylglucose)-5-galactose, cyanidien-3-xyloside, cyanidin3-glucoside, cyanidin 3-galactoside, cyanidin-3-(coumaroyl-xylosyl glucose)-5-galactose, delphinidin 3-glucoside, delphinidin 3-rutinoside, peonidin 3-arabinoside, peonidin 3-galactoside, petunidin 3-glucoside, cyanidin, delphinidin, malvidin, pelargonidin, peonidin, cyanidin 3,5-diglucoside, cyanidin 3-rutinoside, pelargonidin 3-glucoside, peonidin 3-glucoside, malvidin 3-glucoside and malvidin 3,5-diglucoside. The organic solvent is at least one of alcohol having 1-6C atoms, acetone, ether, benzene, chloroform, ethyl acetate, methylene chloride, chloride, hexane, cyclohexane, and ether. The anthocyanin-based compound is dissolved in water or organic solvent in an amount of 0.001-10% (w/v). The carbon dioxide dissolved at a saturation concentration of more than 80% is present in an amount of 0.1-5% (w/v) based on the total volume of the liquid composition. Preferred Method: The anthocyanin-based compound powder is obtained by crushing black rice, black bean, black currant, chokeberry, black chokeberry, cranberry, Aronia, mulberry, cherry, raspberry, blueberry, blackberry, eggplant, acai, grapefruit and grapes isolated from plants by adsorbing fruit juice, eluting and powdering the adsorbed material.

PN CN112221532-A
TI Preparing sulfur indium zinc/graphite phase carbon nitride hierarchical hollow heterojunction in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, involves using melamine as raw material, and forming rod-like structure of supramolecular assembly.
AU YU Y
   WANG X
   LI Q
   CHEN F
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202109239W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sulfur indium zinc/graphite phase carbon nitride hierarchical hollow heterojunction involves using melamine as raw material. The rod-like structure of supramolecular assembly is formed by hydrolysis-hydrothermal, and the rod-like structure is thermally polymerized to form graphite phase carbon nitride microtubes through calcination. The zinc chloride, indium chloride, and thioacetamide are used as the zinc source, indium source, and sulfur source, respectively, and indium zinc sulfide is grown in situ on the graphite phase carbon nitride microtube using low-temperature hydrothermal to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing sulfur indium zinc/graphite phase carbon nitride hierarchical hollow heterojunction used in the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good environmental benefit, promotes the separation of photo-generated electron-hole pair, exhibits excellent performance on the reduction of the photocatalytic carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves weighing an appropriate amount of nitrogen source, heating and stirring in deionized water until clear to prepare solution A, pouring the solution A obtained into the reaction kettle, hydrothermally reacting. The solution is cooled in the furnace, centrifuged, washed, and dried to obtain a white powder B, calcined the white powder B under nitrogen to obtain light yellow powder C, added light yellow powder C after the reaction to a round-bottom flask, ultrasonically dispersed in acid deionized water, and then magnetically stirred to form solution D, weighed zinc chloride, indium chloride, and thioacetamide into D obtained, and stirred at room temperature for 30 minutes to obtain solution E, placed the solution E in a low-temperature oil bath on an oil bath and stirred magnetically, centrifuged, washed and dried to obtain product. Preferred Components: The nitrogen source is melamine, and the amount is 1-4g. Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature is controlled to be 90 degrees C, and the deionized water is 60-240ml. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out 180 degrees C hydrothermal reaction for 24 hours. The calcination parameter includes heating rate of 2 degrees C/minutes, calcination temperature is 520 degrees C, holding time is 4 hours. The acid deionized water pH is 2.5, acid deionized water volume is 30ml. The stirring speed is 300 rpm, and the stirring time is 30 minutes. The order of addition is that zinc chloride and indium chloride are added first, and thioacetamide is added last, and the addition interval is 30 minutes, where the ratio of zinc chloride, indium chloride and thioacetamide is 1:1:2. The oil bath parameter is carried out at 80 degrees C/12 hours and the stirring speed is 300 rpm.

TI Treating emulsified oil wastewater with            carbondioxide atmosphere-responsive material involves            adding carbon dioxide atmosphere-responsive material to            emulsified oil wastewater and stirring to the            emulsified oil wastewater.
AU ZHANG T
   LIU Y
AE RES CENT ECO ENVIRONMENTAL SCI ACAD SIN (CAEM-C)
GA 202056495R
AB    NOVELTY - Treating emulsified oil wastewater with                carbondioxide atmosphere-responsive material                involves adding carbon dioxide                atmosphere-responsive material to emulsified oil                wastewater and stirring to the emulsified oil                wastewater The oil is fully adsorbed. The carbon                dioxide atmosphere-responsive material consists of                a base material and carbondioxide                atmosphere-responsive polymer supported on the base                material. The base material is sponge, carbon fiber                cloth, polyester resin, stainless steel mesh,                activated carbon, zeolite, the carbondioxide                atmosphere responsive polymer is selected from                homopolymers of any one monomer in the                amidine-based system, tertiary amine-based system,                and nitrogen-containing azole hybrid systems, or                copolymerization of two or more monomers.
   USE - Method for treating emulsified oil wastewater                with carbondioxide atmosphere-responsive                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat emulsified oil                wastewater with carbondioxide atmosphere-responsive                material recovery rate has good material stability,                high oil adsorption and desorption efficiency,                important environmental benefit and economic value,                and reaches more than 95.0%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The monomer of the                  amidino system is N-amidino lauryl acrylamide,                  and the tertiary amino group The monomer of the                  system is N,N-diethylammonium ethyl methacrylate,                  and the monomer of the nitrogen-containing azole                  hybrid system is methyl                  3-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)acrylate. The organic solvent                  is ethanol or acetone. The crosslinking agent is                  hexamethylene diamine, polyethylene glycol, ethyl                  orthosilicate, or acrylic acid butyl esters. The                  oil is crude oil, n-hexane, petroleum ether,                  dimethyl silicone oil or soybean oil, and the                  surfactant is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate,                  polyethylene monolaurate or Tween 80. Preferred                  Conditions: The carbond dioxide                  atmosphere-responsive material is prepared by                  dissolving carbondioxide atmosphere-responsive                  polymer in an organic solvent, where the mass                  concentration of the carbon dixoide                  atmosphere-responsive polymer in the resulting                  solution is 1-30 wt.% crosslinking agent,                  stirring to get polymer solution, immersing the                  base material in the polymer solution, taking out                  the base material and placing in a blast drying                  oven at 80-120 degrees C for drying and                  crosslinking for 8-24 hours to obtain the                  carbondioxide atmosphere response material. The                  emulsified oil wastewater is prepared by mixing                  oil, water and surfactant after ultrasonic                  emulsification using an ultrasonic breaker, Where                  the mass ratio of water to oil is 20000:1-40:1,                  and the mass ratio of surfactant to oil is                  1:40-1:10. The emulsified oil adsorbed in the                  carbond dioxide atmosphere-responsive material is                  recovered and the carbondioxide                  atmosphere-responsive material is restored to its                  adsorption characteristics. The carbondioxide gas                  is introduced into the CO2 atmosphere-responsive                  material to which the emulsified oil is adsorbed                  to spontaneously desorb the adsorbed oil and                  realize oil recovery. The nitrogen is introduced                  into the above-mentioned carbondioxide                  atmosphere-responsive material, so that the                  carbondioxide in the material is discharged, and                  the carbondioxide atmosphere responds to the                  material's recovery of adsorption                  characteristics.

PN CN109942876-A
TI Preparing foamed polypropylene particle comprises            mixing polypropylene particles and auxiliary agent,            extruding, granulating, foaming, injecting high            pressure carbon dioxide, dehydrating foamed particles,            crushing, shaping and air drying.
AU CHEN J
AE JINAN TAIDE PACKAGING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 201958712M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing foamed polypropylene particles                comprises (1) uniformly mixing polypropylene                particles and first auxiliary agent, extruding,                granulating to obtain polypropylene particles for                foaming, (2) adding polypropylene particles, second                auxiliary agent and water in high pressure kettle,                stirring, Injecting high pressure carbon dioxide                using steam, heating to soften polypropylene                particles, maintaining temperature and pressure to                ensure that high pressure carbon dioxide fully                soaks polypropylene particles, allowing                polypropylene particles to foam, discharging to                obtain polypropylene foamed particles, (4)                dehydrating to wash surface residues, (5) crushing,                drying, (6) allowing to stand in antistatic net bag                to shape the polypropylene foamed particles and (7)                air drying.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing foamed                polypropylene particles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures continuous production                operation and high efficiency; is simple and                suitable for industrialized promotion and                application. The foamed polypropylene particle has                adjustable expansion ratio; even and detailed                cells, excellent mechanical properties; and                excellent compressive strength, tensile strength                and cushioning properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polypropylene                  particles has a melting index of 3.3-4 g/10                  minutes. The first auxiliary agent comprises 2-10                  pts. wt. talc. The second auxiliary agent                  comprises 0-2 pts. wt. kaolin as dispersing                  agent, 0-2 pts. wt. phosphorus-containing mineral                  as surfactant, 0-2 pts. wt. talc as modifier and                  0-2 pts. wt. modifier ACR as auxiliary agent. The                  foaming comprises 100 pts. wt. polypropylene                  particles 100-300 pts. wt. water. The temperature                  of steam in step (2) is 130 degrees C. The                  process of allowing to stand is for 8-24 hours.                  The air drying is at room temperature at an air                  pressure of 0.7-1 MPa. The obtained polypropylene                  foamed particles have a particle diameter of 1-5                  mm, density of 0.02-0.18 g/cm3 and the expansion                  ratio of 5-50 times. Preferred Method: The                  preparation of the foamed polypropylene in the                  step (3) comprises maintaining temperature at                  130-150 degrees C and pressure at 0.6-1.0 MPa for                  1-4 minutes.

PT P
PN CN109299560-A; CN109299560-B
TI Method for determining optimal exhaust pressure characteristic variable of transcritical carbon dioxide system, involves constructing binary linear function as transfer function to generate intermediate model.
AU CAO F
   CUI C
   YIN X
   WANG L
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201914542Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting samples obtained during the test of the condition of the transcritical CO2 heat pump water heater system. The performance prediction model is constructed with input for ambient temperature, evaporator outlet temperature, suction temperature, exhaust temperature, pump inlet temperature, pump outlet temperature, air-cooled outlet temperature, evaporator coil temperature and suction pressure. The data sample set is divided. The reference function is determined as the mapping relationship between the independent variable and the dependent variable. The binary linear function is constructed as a transfer function to generate an intermediate model.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves weighing hemoglobin and                template agent in a pressure kettle. Carbon dioxide                is generated in the pressure kettle after mixing                and stirring supercritical carbon dioxide at a                certain time. TI Preparing 3-ethoxy-4-carboxyphenylacetic acid            comprises e.g. taking dihalogenated product compound as            raw material, heating with metallic magnesium, reacting            double Grignard reagent with excess of carbon dioxide,            and hydrolyzing.
AU CHEN C
   YANG T
   PAN C
AE UNIV JIANGSU TECHNOLOGY (JIAA-C)
   UNIV JIANGSU TECHNOLOGY (JIAA-C)
GA 201888183E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3-ethoxy-4-carboxyphenylacetic acid                (I) comprises (i) taking dihalogenated product                compound (IV) as raw material, heating (IV) with                metallic magnesium in solvent to obtain double                Grignard reagent compound (V), and (ii) reacting                double Grignard reagent with excess of carbon                dioxide, and hydrolyzing under acidic conditions to                obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                3-ethoxy-4-carboxyphenylacetic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple post-treatment and no                toxic and side effects; and utilizes easily                available raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 3-ethoxy-4-carboxyphenylacetic acid                of formula (I) comprises (i) taking dihalogenated                product compound of formula (IV) as raw material,                heating (IV) with metallic magnesium in solvent to                obtain double Grignard reagent compound of formula                (V), and (ii) reacting double Grignard reagent with                excess of carbon dioxide, and hydrolyzing under                acidic conditions to obtain final product.
   X1 and X2 = Br or Cl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), molar                  ratio between dihalogen and magnesium is 1:3-10.                  In step (i), solvent is freshly distilled                  anhydrous ether and/or anhydrous tetrahydrofuran.                  In step (ii), molar ratio between double Grignard                  reagent and carbon dioxide is 1:2.5-12. In step                  (ii), acidic condition has pH of 1-2, and the                  acid used to adjust the acidic condition is                  dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute sulfuric acid.                  Preferred Method: In step (ii), carbon dioxide is                  added by either passing dry carbon dioxide gas or                  adding solid dry ice.

PN CN108695140-A
TI Substrate cleaning liquid comprises supercritical carbon dioxide solvent, hydrofluoro alcohol, ethyl acetate, trichloroethylene and deionized water.
AU LIU J
   YANG X
AE BEIJING SHENJING PHOTOELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201884904V
AB    NOVELTY - Substrate cleaning liquid comprises 20-30 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide solvent, 5-8 pts. wt. hydrofluoro alcohol, 5-8 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, 5-8 pts. wt. trichloroethylene, 30-40 pts. wt. deionized water, 10-12 pts. wt. ethylene glycol monoethyl ether, 10-15 pts. wt. N-methylpyrrolidone and 10-15 pts. wt. sodium hydrogencarbonate.
   USE - Used as substrate cleaning liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for: preparing substrate cleaning solution comprising dissolving 20-30 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide solvent, 5-8 pts. wt. hydrofluoro alcohol, 5-8 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, 5-8 pts. wt. trichloroethylene, 30-40 pts. wt. deionized water, 10-12 pts. wt. ethylene glycol monoethyl ether, 10-15 pts. wt. N-methylpyrrolidone and 10-15 pts. wt. sodium hydrogencarbonate in 30-40 parts of deionized water to obtain a second mixed liquid and mixing first mixed liquid and the second mixed liquid; and #cleaning for an integrated circuit substrate comprising cleaning a substrate by placing the substrate in the substrate cleaning solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method immersing substrate in the substrate cleaning solution for 20-30 minutes, and carrying out ultrasonic cleaning at a frequency of 50-70 KHz for 10-15 minutes. The substrate cleaning solution comprises immersing the substrate in an environment with a pressure of 70-80 atm at 35-45 degrees C in the substrate cleaning solution. The first ultrasonic cleaning is completed, continuously placing substrate in the substrate cleaning liquid, and the substrate cleaning liquid temperature is lowered by 1-2 degrees C/minutes for 5-10 minutes, and the pressure is reduced by 5-10 minutes according to the depressurization speed is 3-5 atm/min. The second ultrasonic cleaning is performed for 5-10 minutes at a frequency of 80-120 KHz during the cooling and depressurization of the substrate cleaning solution. The second ultrasonic cleaning is intermittent cleaning, and the ratio of the working time of the second ultrasonic to the resting time is 2:1. The substrate is taken out from the substrate cleaning liquid and rinsed in pure water. The first ultrasonic cleaning and the second ultrasonic cleaning are performed on the substrate under a rotating condition, and the substrate is rotate at speed of 10-20 rotations/minutes.
DC D25 (Detergents - other than soap and used for cleaning (C11D)); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. GA 2018575446

PN KR2021094337-A; KR2331736-B1
TI Carbon dioxide absorbing laminate used in filter composite for plasma reactor, comprises substrate, carbon dioxide absorption layer on substrate, metal layer including chalcogenide on absorption layer, and organic compound layer to surround substrate, carbon dioxide absorption layer, and metal layer.
AU BYEON J H
AE UNIV YEUNGNAM RES COOP FOUND (UYYE-C)
GA 202191204E
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorption laminate comprises a substrate, a carbon dioxide absorption layer formed on one region of the substrate, a metal layer formed on the carbon dioxide absorption layer and including a chalcogenide, and an organic compound layer formed to surround the substrate, the carbon dioxide absorption layer, and the metal layer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbing laminate used in bead-type filter composite for plasma reactor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbing laminate has excellent carbon dioxide absorption and reduction performance, and improves durability of the plasma reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a sandwich type filter composite, which comprises the carbon dioxide absorbing laminate, and a filter formed on one surface of the carbon dioxide absorbing laminate;
   (2) a plasma reactor, which comprises a chamber including an inlet through which outside air is introduced, an outlet through which the bet is discharged, and a receiving portion formed between the inlet and the outlet, and is located in the receiving portion, bead-type filter complex, and a wire electrode passing through the chamber and positioned from the inlet to the outlet; and
   (3) manufacture of the carbon dioxide absorbing laminate, which involves providing a substrate, forming a coating layer sequentially including a carbon dioxide absorption layer, a metal layer, and an organic compound layer on the substrate, chalcogenizing the metal layer by irradiating light on the substrate on which the coating layer is formed, removing the solvent on the substrate on which the coating layer is formed, and washing and drying the substrate on which the coating layer is formed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorption layer is chosen from soda lime, zeolite, activated carbon, and metal-organic framework (MOF). The metal layer comprises a pure metal and an oxide. The pure metal is chosen from rubidium, rhodium, platinum, palladium, gold, silver, copper, nickel, iridium, tin, gadolinium, samarium, lanthanum, zirconium and magnesium. The oxide is chosen from cerium oxide, zirconium oxide, yttrium oxide, titanium dioxide, nickel oxide, iron oxide, cobalt oxide, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, lanthanum oxide and gallium oxide. The metal layer includes a metal oxide. The filter composite is a bead-type filter composite having a spherical crumpled shape. The surface of the wire electrode is a plasma reactor having a nanopin shape. The plasma reactor comprises air ionizer positioned at the inlet and configured to supply ions into the receptacle. The carbon dioxide absorption layer is formed on one region of the substrate. The metal layer is formed on the carbon dioxide absorption layer. The organic compound layer is formed to surround the substrate, the carbon dioxide absorption layer and the metal layer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic compounds are chosen from bovine serum albumin (BSA), human serum albumin (HSA), alkanolamines, monoethanolamine, polyethyleneimine, diethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine, piperazine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine, diethylenetriamine, ethylenediamine, triethylenetetramine, triethanolamine, polyallylamine and 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol.

PN CN109852470-A
TI Synergistically extracting seabuckthorn seed oil by using ultrasonic-microwave method comprises adding extracting solvent to seabuckthorn seed powder, and extracting seabuckthorn seed oil by ultrasonic-microwave synergistic extraction.
AU NIU G
   ZHU D
   LIU X
   WEI W
   YANG N
   JIA J
   CAO R
AE UNIV HEILONGJIANG BAYI AGRIC (UYON-C)
GA 201953314S
AB    NOVELTY - Synergistically extracting seabuckthorn seed oil by using ultrasonic-microwave method comprises using seabuckthorn seed powder as raw material, then adding extracting solvent, and extracting the seabuckthorn seed oil by using ultrasonic-microwave synergistic extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for synergistically extracting seabuckthorn seed oil by using ultrasonic-microwave method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can extract high seabuckthorn seed oil; can shorten the extraction cycle; can fill the application blank on the seabuckthorn seed oil. The seabuckthorn seed oil: has composition and content of fatty acids extracted by ultrasonic, microwave and supercritical carbon dioxide were basically the same.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The extracting solvent is petroleum ether, n-hexane, or ethyl acetate, preferably n-hexane. Preferred Method: The extracting is carried out at 35-70 degrees C, preferably 57 degrees C for 5-30 minutes, preferably 22 minutes in a liquid to material ratio of 4-12:1, preferably 9:1 under the ultrasonic power of 400-900 W, preferably 628 W and microwave power of 100-600 W, preferably 200 W. The method specifically comprises (a) cleaning the selected seabuckthorn seeds, and drying in an drying oven at 45 degrees C to constant weight, (b) crushing the seabuckthorn seeds by a crusher, and then sieving the seabuckthorn seeds through 60 mesh sieve, (c) adding seabuckthorn seed sieved powder to an extracting solvent, then placing in an ultrasonic-microwave synergistic extractor for carrying out seabuckthorn seed oil extraction, (d) vacuum filtering the extract to obtain a filtrate, and (e) subjecting the collected filtrate to rotary evaporation for recovering the organic solvent, and then placing in a blast drying oven at 80-90 degrees C until the mass constant weight is constant.

   JU J
   CHEN B

AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201864253S

In step (ii), temperature of the pressed sheet is 170-200 degrees C, pressure is 10-30 MPa, pressing time is 5-10 minutes, and thickness of the sheet is 1-4 mm. In step (iii), placing sheet in an autoclave, sealing, passing supercritical carbon dioxide, heating at 150 degrees C, at a pressure of 12-20 MPa for 1-4 hours and then cooling to 100-130 degrees C for 5-15 minutes to obtain polystyrene foamed material. The rate of pressure relief is 3-10 MPa/second.

Preferred Components: The polymer substance is resorcinol or phenol. The acid treatment comprises biomass containing chitin. The biomass comprises chitin containing crustacean shells. PN WO2022044481-A1
TI Separation of carbon dioxide from mixed gas involves using module comprising separation membrane comprising treatment chamber, supplying mixed gas of specific gas, carbon dioxide and water vapor to chamber, and permeating carbon dioxide through membrane to separate carbon dioxide from mixed gas.
AU OSAMU O
   MASAAKI T
   NOBUAKI H
AE RENAISSANCE ENERGY RES CORP (RENA-Non-standard)
GA 2022398106
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of carbon dioxide from mixed gas (FG) involves (i) using modules (2) comprising a carbon dioxide separation membrane (3) comprising a carbon dioxide carrier, a treatment chamber (5) and a treatment chamber (6), (ii) supplying a mixed gas comprising a specific gas, carbon dioxide and water vapor that does not react with the carrier from one end side of the chamber (5) to the chamber (5), (iii) permeating carbon dioxide in the mixed gas to the chamber side (6) through the separation membrane to separate carbon dioxide from the mixed gas, (iv) discharging the non-permeable gas (EG) containing specific gas, carbon dioxide and water vapor remaining in chamber (5) without passing through the separation membrane from the other end side of the chamber (5), and (v) discharging the permeable gas (PG) that is passed through the separation membrane containing separated carbon dioxide to the outside from the same side as the other end side of the chamber (5) of the chamber (6).
   USE - Separation of carbon dioxide from mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains desired carbon dioxide permeance, and suppresses decrease in carbon dioxide permeance in downstream separation membrane module, due to decrease in relative humidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of carbon dioxide from mixed gas involves (i) using two or more stages of separation membrane modules comprising a carbon dioxide separation membrane comprising a carbon dioxide carrier that selectively react with carbon dioxide, and a treatment chamber (5) and a treatment chamber (6) which are separated by the separation membrane, connected in series, (ii) supplying a mixed gas comprising a specific gas, carbon dioxide and water vapor that does not react with the carrier from one end side of the chamber (5) to the chamber (5), (iii) permeating carbon dioxide in the mixed gas to the chamber side (6) through the separation membrane to separate carbon dioxide from the mixed gas, (iv) discharging the non-permeable gas containing the specific gas, carbon dioxide and water vapor remaining in the chamber (5) without passing through the separation membrane from the other end side of the chamber (5), (v) discharging the permeable gas that is passed through the separation membrane containing the separated carbon dioxide to the outside from the same side as the other end side of the chamber (5) of the chamber (6), and (vi) adding water vapor to the non-permeable gas discharged from the other end side of the chamber (5). The mixed gas of the separation membrane module connected to the rear stage side is supplied to the chamber (5) from the one end side of the chamber (5) of the separation membrane module on the rear stage side. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon dioxide separation device (1), which comprises the two or more stages of separation membrane modules, and a steam supply unit (11). The separation membrane module comprises a feed port (7) for supplying the mixed gas to the chamber (5), a discharge port (8) for discharging the non-permeable gas, and a discharge port (9) for discharging the permeable gas. The port (8) is interconnected to the feed port of the module connected to the rear stage side to form a connection unit (10). The steam supply section is configured to supply steam to each of the connection units. The non-permeable gas discharged from the port (8) and the water vapor supplied to the connection unit are used as the mixed gas of the separation membrane module connected to the rear stage side, and supplied into the chamber (5) from the feed port of the separation membrane module on the rear stage side; and
   (2) combustion system, which comprises the carbon dioxide separation device and a combustion device. The non-permeable gas discharged from the chamber (5) of the separation membrane module in the final stage of the separation device is supplied to the combustion device as fuel gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional view of a basic configuration of the carbon dioxide separation device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide separation device (1)
   Separation membrane module (2)
   Carbon dioxide separation membrane (3)
   Treatment chambers (5,6)
   Feed port (7)
   Discharge port (8,9)
   Connection unit (10)
   Steam supply unit (11)
   Non-permeable gas (EG)
   Mixed gas (FG)
   Permeable gas (PG)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: In each stage of the separation membrane module other than the final stage, water vapor is added to the non-permeable gas discharged from the other end side of the chamber (5), such that the relative humidity of the non-permeable gas increases by 20% or more. Preferred Composition: The mixed gas supplied to the chamber (5) of the separation membrane module in stage (s1) comprises a gas derived from biogas produced by methane fermentation of organic substance, and the specific gas is methane. The dry base of the non-permeable gas discharged from the chamber (5) of the separation membrane module in the stage (s1) has a methane concentration of 80 mol% or more. Preferred Component: The steam supply unit is connected to the connection unit, such that the relative humidity of the non-permeable gas discharged from the port (8) increases by 20% or more with respect to each stage of the separation membrane module other than the final stage. When the relative humidity of the mixed gas to be supplied to the chamber (5) of the separation membrane module of the stage (s1) is less than 70%, the steam supply unit has a relative humidity of 70% or more by supplying water vapor. The steam supply unit of the separation device uses waste heat from the combustion device to generate steam.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111704967-A
TI Nonflammable degreasing cleaning agent comprises halogenated hydrocarbon and fatty alcohol.
AU WU Y
   JIN Z
   WANG R
AE SHANGHAI WEISIDA IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202097571U
AB    NOVELTY - Nonflammable degreasing cleaning agent comprises 80-100 pts. wt. halogenated hydrocarbon and 1-10 pts. wt. fatty alcohol.
   USE - Nonflammable degreasing cleaning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaning agent has good cleaning effect on both polar and non-polar dirt, low toxicity, fast volatilization, no residue, higher safety, good compatibility with carbon dioxide gas in aerosol tank, high chemical stability in the process of storage and transportation and no corrosion to cleaned surface and is harmless to human body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halogenated hydrocarbon is chlorinated hydrocarbon, fluorohydrocarbon and/or chlorofluorohydrocarbon. The number ratio of fluorine and chlorine atoms in chlorofluorocarbon is 1:1-3. The number of carbon atoms in chlorofluorocarbons is 1-5. The fatty alcohol is saturated binary fatty alcohols of 2-5C, 7-13C, 15-18C and not greater than 20C. The fatty alcohol of 7-13C is n-heptanol, n-octanol, isooctanol, lauryl alcohol, n-decanol, octadecyl alcohol and/or n-undecanol. The mass ratio of octadecyl alcohol to n-undecanol is 1.2-1.8:1. The nonflammable degreasing cleaning agent further comprises 0.5-5 pts. wt. fatty ester. The fatty ester is myristate myristate, cetyl myristate, stearate myristate, myristicate palmitate, cetyl palmitate, stearic palmitate, myristica stearate, cetyl stearate, stearic acid stearate, behenate behenate and/or isocetyl myristate.

AE ANHUI TONGYI PURIFICATION TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - A nano-calcite volatile organic compounds                adsorbent is prepared using carbon dioxide                diffusion method by preparing carbon dioxide by                sodium bicarbonate and hydrochloric acid,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide into lime                water, and reacting in ultrasonic reactor.

   ADVANTAGE - The method observes adsorption phenomenon of                volatile organic pollutant (volatile organic                compounds, VOCs), uses constant temperature                adsorption experiment device, and performs                adsorption VOCs experimental test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  sodium bicarbonate solution into reaction                  container A, mounting stirring device, dropping                  funnel that is filled with hydrochloric acid                  solution and gas diffusion device, passing                  through other end of gas diffusion device into                  reaction container B that is filled with lime                  water, placing reaction container B in ultrasonic                  reactor that is provided with stirring device,                  dropping hydrochloric acid solution in dropping                  funnel into reaction container A under stirring                  condition, starting ultrasonic reactor for 0.1-1                  hours at 800 W, opening stirring device in                  reaction container B, filtering, washing, and                  drying at 100-200 degrees C for 1-2 hours. PT P
PN WO2022022808-A1
TI Separation and recovery of carbon dioxide from            ambient air involves contacting ambient air with basic            aqueous solution of alkali or alkaline earth metal            cation to absorb carbon dioxide into solution to form            hydrogen carbonate.
AU MAKARUK A
   SZIVACZ J
AE AXIOM ANGEWANDTE PROZESSTECHNIK GMBH (AXIO-Non-standard)
   MAKARUK A (MAKA-Individual)
GA 202218306C
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and recovery of carbon dioxide from                ambient air involves contacting ambient air with a                basic aqueous solution of at least one alkali or                alkaline earth metal cation to absorb the carbon                dioxide into the solution to form the bicarbonate                or carbonate of the at least one metal,                electrodialyzing the resulting solution using a                combination of bipolar and anion-selective ion                exchange membranes to obtain a (hydrogen) carbonate                ion-enriched and a depleted solution, each having a                pH of 7 or more, and recycling the (hydrogen)                carbonate ion-depleted solution to contacting                process, separating the carbon dioxide from the                solution enriched in (hydrogen) carbonate ions, and                recycling the solution depleted in carbon dioxide                to being obtained which is recycled to                electrodialysis, and drying and purifying the                separated carbon dioxide.
   USE - Separation and recovery of carbon dioxide from                ambient air used in beverage industry, greenhouses,                chemical synthesis e.g. to produce methane by                reacting with hydrogen, and synthetic fuel                production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can extract carbon dioxide from the                air with even greater purity than their previous                method and with even great energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation and recovery of carbon dioxide from                ambient air involves contacting ambient air with a                basic aqueous solution of at least one alkali or                alkaline earth metal cation to absorb the carbon                dioxide into the solution to form the bicarbonate                or carbonate of the at least one metal,                electrodialyzing the resulting solution using a                combination of bipolar and anion-selective ion                exchange membranes to obtain a (hydrogen) carbonate                ion-enriched and a depleted solution, each having a                pH of 7 or more, and recycling the (hydrogen)                carbonate ion-depleted solution to contacting                process, separating the carbon dioxide from the                solution enriched in (hydrogen) carbonate ions, and                recycling the solution depleted in carbon dioxide                to being obtained which is recycled to                electrodialysis, and drying and purifying the                separated carbon dioxide. The absorption is carried                out using at least one absorber and/or at least one                open basin. The separation of carbon dioxide from                the solution enriched in (hydrogen) carbonate ions                is carried out by thermal desorption of the carbon                dioxide by steam stripping to obtain a carbon                dioxide-water vapor mixture and/or by chemical                reaction of the (hydrogen) carbonate ions, in which                the carbon dioxide contained is converted into a                water-insoluble salt or a gas and is thereby                removed from the solution. The pH value of each of                the two solutions obtained by electrodialysis is                measured before recycling or before separating off                the carbon dioxide and in each case adjusted to a                predetermined value with the proviso that if the                absorption is not carried out in absorber and/or                basin, the carbon dioxide is separated by thermal                desorption of the carbon dioxide steam stripping.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for continuous                implementation plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating separation and recovery of carbon                dioxide.
   3Circulation pump
   5Electrodialyzer
   31Pump
   33Control valve
   35Water tank

PN JP2020196008-A
TI Isolation of mixture gas involves brining mixed gas containing at least carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon at a normal temperature into contact with the zeolite membrane composite having membrane formed on porous support forming mixture.
AU HAYASHI M
   KIKUCHI T
   KISHIMOTO Y
   TAKEWAKI T
   TAKATANI K
AE JAPAN OIL GAS & METALS NAT CORP (NIOI-C)
   CHIYODA CORP (CHIY-C)
   MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
GA 2020C3080L
AB    NOVELTY - Isolation of mixture gas involves brining mixed gas containing at least carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon and having a pressure of 1 megaPascal at a normal temperature or 35 degrees C into contact with the zeolite membrane composite having a zeolite membrane formed on the porous support and forming mixture, let the permeance of the carbon dioxide in the carbon-dioxide transparent test before performing the following propane contact-processing be P1 when the carbon-dioxide permeance in the carbon-dioxide transparent test after performing the following propane contact-processing is made into P2, the ratio of P2/P1 is 0.7. The carbon dioxide permeation test is performed when a single component gas of 100% carbon dioxide is supplied to the surface of the zeolite membrane under the conditions of a supply gas pressure of 0.2 megaPascal.
   USE - Method for isolation of mixture gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolation of mixture gas involves brining mixed gas containing at least carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon and having a pressure of 1 megaPascal at a normal temperature or 35 degrees C into contact with the zeolite membrane composite having a zeolite membrane formed on the porous support and forming mixture, let the permeance of the carbon dioxide in the carbon-dioxide transparent test before performing the following propane contact-processing be P1 when the carbon-dioxide permeance in the carbon-dioxide transparent test after performing the following propane contact-processing is made into P2, the ratio of P2/P1 is 0.7. The carbon dioxide permeation test is performed when a single component gas of 100% carbon dioxide is supplied to the surface of the zeolite membrane under the conditions of a supply gas pressure of 0.2 megaPascal, permeation gas pressure of 0.1 megaPascal and 90 degrees C temperature. The P1 be the permeence of carbon dioxide that permeates the zeolite membrane. After the carbon dioxide permeation test, the supply gas is changed from a single component gas of 100% carbon dioxide to propane. The supply gas pressure is 0.1 megaPascal, the permeation gas pressure is 0.1 megaPascal, and temperature is 90 degrees C. The propane is supplied to the surface of the zeolite membrane to replace the carbon dioxide with the propane, and then the zeolite is provided under the conditions of a supply gas pressure of 0.4 MPa, a permeation gas pressure of 0.1 megaPascal, and a temperature of 90 degrees C. The propane is supplied to the surface of the membrane for 30 minutes. The supply gas is changed from propane to a single component gas of 100% carbon dioxide, the supply gas pressure is 0.2 megaPascal, the permeation gas pressure is 0.1 megaPascal, and the temperature is 90 degrees C. The permeence of carbon dioxide that permeates the zeolite membrane when a single component gas of 100% carbon dioxide is supplied to the surface of the zeolite membrane is P2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The zeolite membrane composite has a supply gas pressure of 0.2 mgaPascal, permeation gas pressure of 0.1 megaPascal, and a temperature of 40 degrees C. When SF6 is set, PCH4 is 3.02 x PSF6 + 8.19 x 10-9. The zeolite constituting the zeolite membrane has a pore structure having an oxygen ring of 8 members.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Carbon dioxide trapping system using waste gas temperature difference to generate electricity comprises a temperature difference generating device and carbon dioxide capturing device connected with the each other.
AU FENG J
   WANG Z
   ZHAO C
AE UNIV SHANGHAI MARITIME (USHM-C)
GA 202164062X
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide trapping system using waste gas temperature difference to generate electricity comprises a temperature difference generating device (10) and carbon dioxide capturing device connected with each other, where the carbon dioxide capturing device comprises multiple carbon dioxide capturing units (20) connected in parallel. The carbon dioxide capturing unit comprises an electrolytic cell connected in turn, piston container and a gas cylinder. The high temperature waste gas is cooled down after passing through the temperature difference generating device, entered the electrolytic cell. The working electrode of the electrolytic cell is applied with reducing potential to absorb carbon dioxide in the waste gas, and then applied oxidation potential to release carbon dioxide to the piston container. The carbon dioxide is injected into the gas bottle by the piston container. The temperature difference generating device is used for supplying power to the electrolytic cell.
   USE - Carbon dioxide trapping system using waste gas temperature difference to generate electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides electric energy for the trapping device and the control system, and gives attention to the cooling requirement of the waste gas flow of the concentrated electrochemical process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system.

AU SORIMACHI K
AE SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO IND CO LTD (KOBM-C)
GA 202159147C
AB    NOVELTY - Fixing of carbon dioxide involves (a) forming a solution containing sodium hydroxide and a gas containing carbon dioxide are brought into contact with each other, and (b) adding 0.05-0.5 mol/L calcium chloride to the solution. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide in the mixed solution is 0.01-0.2 N.
   USE - Fixing of carbon dioxide in manufacture of fixed carbon dioxide (claimed) for treating combustion exhaust gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of fixed carbon dioxide, which involves immobilizing the carbon dioxide; and
   (2) manufacturing apparatus of fixed carbon dioxide, which consists of container comprising the solution containing sodium hydroxide and the gas containing carbon dioxide are brought into contact with each other, a solution charging unit can charge the solution into the container, a gas feeding unit can feed the gas into the container, and an adding unit for adding calcium chloride to the solution in the container after contact with the gas, where the container including several containers, and the solution can be brought into contact with the gas by sequentially flowing the solution in the several containers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves feeding the gas into the solution and the mixed solution after the adding the steps (a) and (b), and contacting the solution and the mixture with the gas. The method further involves cooling the mixed solution after the addition of step (b). Preferred Condition: The temperature of the solution and the mixed solution is performed at 70 degrees C or more.

PN US2019388868-A1; US10799849-B2
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from e.g. free air, involves passing gas mixture containing carbon dioxide into bed containing adsorbent comprising alumina, carbonate, and alkali metals, and withdrawing carbon dioxide-depleted gas from bed.
AU LAU G C
   SORENSEN E M
   TAYLOR F W
   GOLDEN T C
   QUINN R
   CASTEEL W J
AE AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
GA 2019A7409C
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide involves passing the gas mixture containing carbon dioxide into a bed containing an adsorbent, and withdrawing carbon dioxide-depleted gas from the bed. The adsorbent comprises 0.9-10 wt.% alkali metals, alumina, and a carbonate compound. The adsorbent has carbonate to alumina intensity ratio (R) of 0.0150 or less, determined by Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR) of a crushed sample of the adsorbent. The ratio (R) is the ratio of peak absorbance intensity for carbonate to peak absorbance intensity for alumina. Each peak absorbance intensity obtained after subtracting a baseline signal intensity, the peak absorbance intensity for alumina, is observed at an FTIR wavenumber in 420-520 cm-1, and the peak absorbance intensity for carbonate is observed at FTIR wavenumber of 1300-1400 cm-1.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide, ambient air, free air, synthesis gas, and natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables removal of carbon dioxide with adsorbent having excellent sorbing and desorbing characteristics, carbon dioxide removing performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for adsorption unit, which consists of a bed comprising an adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent comprises 1.0-8 wt.% alkali metals, 90-99 wt.% alumina. The gas mixture comprises 5 ppmv to 1 mol% carbon dioxide. Preferred Properties: The adsorbent has surface area of 220-400 m2/g. The intensity ratio of carbonate to alumina is 0.003-0.0150. Preferred Components: The alkali metals are sodium and/or potassium. The amount of the carbonate is lower than the amount of carbonate arithmetically necessary for stoichiometrically compensating the charge of the total amount of alkali metals. The gas mixture comprises oxygen, nitrogen, and water, and is a feed to a cryogenic air separation unit.

   CHENG S
   YAN X
   WANG S
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022A6485J
AB    NOVELTY - The co-production system comprises a heat                source (1), a carbon dioxide turbine (2), a CO2                regenerator (3), carbon dioxide pre-cooler, CO2                condenser (5), CO2 booster pump (6), a generator, a                heat pump compressor (B1), a heater (B2), a cooler,                a throttle valve, a first tee and a second tee. The CO2 regenerator and CO2 pre-                cooler are connected to CO2 condenser. PN US2022144634-A1; EP4000714-A1; CA3135894-A1; AU2021258013-A1
TI Producing hydrogen and separating carbon dioxide from synthesis gas by removing carbon dioxide from cooled synthesis gas via cooled absorption medium in physical absorption step at elevated pressure and separating hydrogen from the gas.
AU LINICUS M
   SCHMIDT S
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202266562A
AB    NOVELTY - A process for producing hydrogen and for separating carbon dioxide from synthesis gas, involves (a) providing a synthesis gas comprising at least hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; (b) providing a physical absorption medium; (c) cooling the synthesis gas and absorption medium; (d) removing carbon dioxide from cooled synthesis gas via the cooled absorption medium in a physical absorption step at elevated pressure; (e) treating the laden absorption medium in serially arranged flash stages; (f) separating hydrogen from the synthesis gas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide in a physical separation step; (g) compressing to absorption pressure and at least partially recycling to step (d) the offgas obtained in step (f) and the carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtained in step (e); and (h) discharging the product gas (hydrogen) obtained in step (f) and carbon dioxide obtained in step (e) from the process.
   USE - A process for producing hydrogen and for separating carbon dioxide from synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates the majority of the carbon dioxide generated during hydrogen production as a high purity carbon dioxide product. The efficiency of the process is further improved by an additional absorption step for the offgas, i.e. based on a defined time interval larger volume flows of synthesis gas may be treated, thus, increasing the yield of hydrogen as product gas and the yield of carbon dioxide discharged from the process according to step (h) (carbon dioxide product).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A process for producing hydrogen and for separating carbon dioxide from synthesis gas, involves (a) providing a synthesis gas comprising at least hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; (b) providing a physical absorption medium; (c) cooling the synthesis gas and absorption medium; (d) removing carbon dioxide from cooled synthesis gas via the cooled absorption medium in a physical absorption step at elevated pressure, where the cooled synthesis gas and the cooled absorption medium are run in countercurrent, where synthesis gas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide is obtained and absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtained; (e) treating the laden absorption medium in serially arranged flash stages, where co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is removed from the laden absorption medium in at least one first flash stage and carbon dioxide is removed from the laden absorption medium in a flash stage arranged downstream of the first flash stage; (f) separating hydrogen from the synthesis gas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide in a physical separation step, where hydrogen as product gas and offgas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide are obtained; (g) compressing to absorption pressure and at least partially recycling to step (d) the offgas obtained in step (f) and the carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtained in step (e); and (h) discharging the product gas (hydrogen) obtained in step (f) and carbon dioxide obtained in step (e) from the process.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant (100) for producing hydrogen and for separating carbon dioxide from synthesis gas, comprising (a) unit for providing synthesis gas comprising at least hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; (b) unit for providing a physical absorption medium; (c) unit for cooling the synthesis gas and absorption medium; (d) an absorption column (T-01) for running the cooled synthesis gas and the cooled absorption medium in countercurrent at elevated pressure for removal of carbon dioxide from the synthesis gas, where synthesis gas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide is obtainable and absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtainable; (e) serially arranged flash stages comprising a first flash stage for removal of the co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the laden absorption medium and comprising a flash stage arranged downstream of the first flash stage for removal of carbon dioxide from the laden absorption medium; (f) an apparatus for separating hydrogen from the synthesis gas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide in a physical separation step, where hydrogen as product gas and offgas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide are obtainable; (g) a compressor (C-01, C-02) for compressing the carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtainable in the first flash stage and offgas obtainable in the apparatus (f) and for recycling the carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtainable in the first flash stage and the offgas obtainable in the apparatus (f) to the absorption column (d); and (h) unit for discharging the product gas (hydrogen) obtainable in the apparatus (f) and the carbon dioxide obtainable in the downstream flash stage from the plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the process for producing hydrogen and for separating carbon dioxide from synthesis gas.
   Compressor (C-01, C-02)
   Separator (D-01, D-02)
   Heat exchanger (HX-01)
   Pump (P-01, P-02)
   Absorption column (T-01)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The physical separation step comprises a pressure swing adsorption. The offgas obtained in step (f) and carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtained in step (e) are in a common gas compressor compressed to absorption pressure and recycled to step (d). The offgas obtained in step (f) is partly utilized as fuel in the production of the synthesis gas. The offgas obtained in step (f) is subjected to a further absorption step with cooled absorption medium, where an offgas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide is obtained and a further absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtained, and the offgas partially freed of carbon dioxide is compressed to absorption pressure and recycled to step (d). The further absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is treated in serially arranged flash stages according to step (e). Water is removed from the absorption medium in a distillation step arranged downstream of step (e). Absorption medium vapors obtained in the distillation step are utilized as a stripping medium for removal of carbon dioxide in the downstream flash stage according to step (e). Compression heat obtained in step (g) is utilized for direct or indirect heating of the absorption medium in the distillation step. Water is at least partially removed from the synthesis gas by cooling the synthesis gas below the dew point of water and separating the condensed water. Compression heat obtained in step (g) is utilized to heat the laden absorption medium during treatment of the laden absorption medium in the serially arranged flash stages according to step (e). The synthesis gas is in a process step upstream of the process subjected to a water gas shift reaction to increase the hydrogen yield, thus enriching the synthesis gas with water and carbon dioxide.Preferred Components: The synthesis gas comprises water as a further component.Preferred Devices: The apparatus (f) comprises a unit for pressure swing adsorption. The plant comprises a further absorption column for running the offgas obtainable in the apparatus (f) and cooled absorption medium in countercurrent, where offgas at least partially freed of carbon dioxide is obtainable and a further absorption medium laden with carbon dioxide and partially co-absorbed carbon monoxide and hydrogen is obtainable.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The physical absorption medium is hygroscopic. The physical absorption medium comprises or consists of methanol. The physical absorption medium comprises or consists of N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP).

TI Supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor used to produce high heat resistant catalyst support and for recycling carbon fiber reinforced composite material, comprises e.g. injection unit including fluid container for injecting solvent.
AU GON K
   KWON S
   FELIX W
   BAEK S
   SU J
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2020140190
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor comprises an injection unit (100) including a fluid container for injecting a solvent and a carbon dioxide bomb; a pump unit (200) for supplying the solvent in uniform flow rate; a pre-heater (300) for pre-heating the solvent supplied from the pump; a reaction unit (400) for heating the solvent preheated in the pre-heater and accommodating a specimen; a cooling unit (500) for cooling the solvent discharged from the reaction unit; a back pressure control unit for adjusting the solvent through the cooling unit to atmospheric pressure; and a collecting unit for collecting the solvent passing through the back pressure control unit.
   USE - The supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor is useful for producing high heat resistant catalyst support and recycling carbon fiber reinforced composite material (all claimed), in electronic equipment, aircraft, and physical impact protection equipment in space environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor has high specific surface area, and heat resistance catalyst support, improves spacecraft's ability to perform missions due to reduced volume and mass of propulsion system, is environmentally friendly, provides supercritical fluid environment utilizing carbon dioxide, reduces additional cost and time required for handling by replacing existing toxic hydrazine thrusters, recycles composite material through solvation decomposition of epoxy-based matrix forming combination of expensive carbon fiber reinforced composite material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing high heat resistant catalyst support using supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor, comprising (a) placing a catalyst support in reactor for mounting a specimen, (b) fixing flange coupling part to the flange part to close the reaction chamber in which the catalyst support is located, (c) performing purging operation using nitrogen to remove oxygen in the reactor, (d) heating the reactor under carbon dioxide environment, (e) dissolving the carbon dioxide in the reactor, and adding a solvent to the reactor, (f) maintaining pressure, temperature, and flow rate of the reaction, (g) cooling the carbon dioxide in the reactor, (h) lowering pressure in the reactor to lower the pressure of reactor to atmospheric pressure, and (i) separating the flange coupling part to open the reactor to obtain dried catalyst support; and
   (2) recycling carbon fiber reinforced composite material using supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor, comprising (i) placing carbon fiber reinforced composite material into the reactor, recycling and mounting a specimen, (ii) fixing the flange coupling part to the flange part to close the reactor in which the carbon fiber reinforced composite material is located, (iii) performing purging operation using nitrogen, and removing oxygen in the reactor, (iv) heating the reactor under nitrogen environment, (v) dissolving the carbon dioxide into the reactor, and adding a solvent to the reactant, (vi) maintaining pressure, temperature of the reactor, and flow rate of the reaction, (vii) cooling the input carbon dioxide in the reactor, (viii) lowering the pressure of the reactor to lower the pressure in the reactor to atmospheric pressure, and (ix) separating the flange coupling part to open the reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the supercritical fluid semi-continuous reactor (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Injection unit (100)
   Pump unit (200)
   Pre-heater (300)
   Reaction unit (400)
   Cooling unit (500)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cooling unit further comprises a filter for filtering impurities and residues after the experiment through the solvent. The injection unit further comprises a carbon dioxide regulator for maintaining a constant pressure of the rear end from the high pressure carbon dioxide cylinder to inject a liquid carbon dioxide of a constant flow rate. The pump unit further comprises a pump, a manifold, and a reverse pressure regulator supplied with a predetermined level or more pressure and discharging the fluid when the pressure in the manifold is higher than set pressure through the fluid supplied from the pump. PN CN109762951-A
TI Degreasing animal skin involves mixing organic amine and water, adding into pre-processed animal skin, degreasing, processing waste liquid to separate oil phase, removing organic amine in skin, and heating to recover carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG Z
   ZHANG M
   GAO H
   WANG Y
   LIU G
   DAI H
   HAN W
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2019468803
AB    NOVELTY - Method for degreasing animal skin, involves (a) mixing organic amine and water, stirring, placing the pre-processed animal skin in a rotatory drum, adding 10-15 times emulsion into the drum, degreasing, and then taking out the animal skin, (b) discharging and collecting the degreased waste liquid, centrifuging, introducing carbon dioxide gas into liquid phase obtained by centrifugation, converting the organic phase to a hydrophilic state, and separating from the oil phase, (c) placing the treated animal skin in a sulfuric acid aqueous solution or a saturated aqueous solution of carbonic acid to remove the organic amine in the animal skin, (d) heating the aqueous phase obtained in the step (b) to remove carbon dioxide, separating the organic amine and returning the organic amine for recycling, and (e) heating the waste liquid obtained after the animal skin is taken out in the step (c) to remove the carbon dioxide, and recycling organic amine and liquid phase.
   USE - The method is useful for degreasing animal skin using hydrophilic controllable solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for degreasing animal skin using hydrophilic controllable solvent, involves (a) mixing organic amine and water, stirring until the organic amine is emulsified by water to form emulsion, where the concentration of the organic amine in the emulsion is 15-55 wt.%, placing the pre-processed animal skin in a rotatory drum, adding 10-15 times emulsion into the drum, controlling the emulsion temperature at 32-39 degrees C, rotating the rotatory drum for 10-30 minutes, stopping drum for 40-60 minutes, rotating rotatory drum for 10-30 minutes for degreasing, and then taking out the animal skin, where the pre-processed animal skin is dried animal skin or fresh animal skin that is subjected to water immersion, meat removal and re-immersion, (b) discharging and collecting the degreased waste liquid produced by the degreasing in the drum, centrifuging, introducing carbon dioxide gas into liquid phase obtained by centrifugation, converting the organic phase in the liquid phase from a hydrophobic state to a hydrophilic state, dissolving in water, and separating the resulting aqueous phase from the oil phase, (c) placing the treated animal skin in a sulfuric acid aqueous solution or a saturated aqueous solution of carbonic acid having a concentration of 0.08-1.2 wt.% to be immersed to remove the organic amine in the animal skin, and then taking the animal skin out, (d) heating the aqueous phase obtained in the step (b) to remove carbon dioxide and return organic amine to a hydrophobic state, separating the organic amine and returning the organic amine for recycling, and (e) heating the waste liquid obtained after the animal skin is taken out in the step (c) is heated to remove the carbon dioxide and restore the organic amine to a hydrophobic state, separating the organic amine, returning to the step (a) for recycling, and returning the liquid phase obtained after separating the organic amine to the step (c) for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves introducing the carbon dioxide gas into the liquid phase obtained by centrifugation until the relative content of the aqueous phase and the oil phase is no longer changed, and then stopping the introduction of the carbon dioxide gas. The step (d) involves heating the aqueous phase at 40-80 degrees C for 20-50 minutes to remove the carbon dioxide gas. The step (e) involves heating the waste liquid obtained after taking out the animal skin at 40-80 degrees C for 20-50 minutes to remove carbon dioxide gas. The step (c) involves immersing the treated animal skin in an aqueous solution of sulfuric acid for 20-30 minutes to remove the organic amine in the animal skin, or immersing the treated animal skin in a saturated aqueous solution of carbonic acid for 40-60 minutes to remove the organic amine in the animal skin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is dipropylamine, N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine or N-ethyl n-butylamine, where when the organic amine is dipropylamine, N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine or N-ethyl n-butylamine, the concentration of the organic amine in the emulsion is 15-30 wt.%, 30-55 wt.% or 30-45 wt.%, respectively.
