GA 2019A3798H
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture process (P1) for                capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas generated by                a host facility, comprises: (a) in an absorption                stage, directly contacting a flue gas stream with                an absorption solution comprising water and a                non-carbamate forming absorption compound to absorb                carbon dioxide and form a carbon dioxide-rich                solution and a carbon dioxide depleted gas; (b) in                a stripping stage, subjecting carbon dioxide-rich                solution to vacuum pressure conditions and                temperature conditions below 100 degrees C to cause                the release of carbon dioxide from carbon                dioxide-rich solution to form a regenerated                solution and an overhead gas stream comprising                carbon dioxide and steam; (c) recycling regenerated                solution back into absorption stage as at least                part of absorption solution; and (d) subjecting the                overhead gas stream to heat recovery, comprising                e.g. compressing the overhead gas stream or a gas                stream derived from it to increase a temperature of                it and produce a compressed gas stream.
   USE - The process is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide from flue gas generated by a host                facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes the equipment which is                easily available and less costly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide capture process (P1) for                capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas generated by                a host facility, comprises: (a) in an absorption                stage, directly contacting a flue gas stream with                an absorption solution comprising water and a                non-carbamate forming absorption compound to absorb                carbon dioxide and form a carbon dioxide-rich                solution and a carbon dioxide depleted gas; (b) in                a stripping stage, subjecting the carbon                dioxide-rich solution to vacuum pressure conditions                and temperature conditions below 100 degrees C to                cause the release of carbon dioxide from the carbon                dioxide-rich solution to form a regenerated                solution and an overhead gas stream comprising                carbon dioxide and steam; (c) recycling the                regenerated solution back into the absorption stage                as at least part of the absorption solution; and                (d) subjecting the overhead gas stream to heat                recovery, comprising compressing the overhead gas                stream or a gas stream derived from it to increase                a temperature of it and produce a compressed gas                stream, transferring heat from the compressed gas                stream to a heat transfer fluid to form a heated                fluid, and transferring heat from the heated fluid                to the stripping stage. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide capture system for                capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas generated by                a host facility, comprising an absorption stage                comprising an absorber configured to receive and                enable direct contact of a flue gas stream with an                absorption solution comprising water and a                non-carbamate forming absorption compound to absorb                carbon dioxide and form a carbon dioxide-rich                solution and a carbon dioxide depleted gas, a                stripping stage comprising a reboiler and a vacuum                stripper configured for receiving and subjecting                the carbon dioxide-rich solution to vacuum pressure                conditions and temperature conditions below 100                degrees C to cause release of carbon dioxide from                the carbon dioxide-rich solution to form a                regenerated solution and an overhead gas stream                comprising carbon dioxide and water vapor, an                overhead heat recovery assembly comprising a                compressor configured to receive and compress the                overhead gas stream or a gas stream derived from it                to increase temperature of it and produce a                compressed gas stream, a heat exchanger in fluid                communication with the compressor and configured to                transferring heat from the compressed gas stream to                a heat transfer fluid to form a heated fluid, and                an overhead recovery loop configured to circulate                the heat transfer fluid through the heat exchanger                to increase a temperature of it and then through                the reboiler of the stripper to transfer thermal                energy to the reboiler;
   (2) a heat integration system for heat                recovery and reuse in a carbon dioxide capture                operation employing an absorber and a vacuum                stripper operated at vacuum pressure conditions and                temperatures below 100 degrees C, comprising at                least one compressor configured to receive and                compress an overhead gas stream produced by the                vacuum stripper or a gas stream derived from it to                increase temperature of it and produce a compressed                gas stream, at least one heat exchanger each being                in fluid communication with a corresponding                compressor and configured to transfer thermal                energy from the compressed gas stream to a heat                transfer fluid to form a heated fluid, and an                overhead recovery loop configured to circulate the                heat transfer fluid through the heat exchanger to                form the heated fluid and through a reboiler of the                vacuum stripper to transfer thermal energy to a                solution in the reboiler;
   (3) a heat integration process (P2) for heat                recovery and integration in a carbon dioxide                capture operation employing an absorber and a                vacuum stripper operated at vacuum pressure                conditions and temperatures below 100 degrees C,                comprising compressing an overhead gas stream                generated by the vacuum stripper or a gas stream                derived from it to increase temperature of it and                produce a compressed gas stream, transferring heat                from the compressed gas stream to a heat transfer                fluid to form a heated fluid, and transferring heat                from the heated fluid to the vacuum stripper;
   (4) a carbon dioxide capture process (P3) for                capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas generated by                a host facility, comprising the step (a) as above                per se, in a stripping stage, subjecting the carbon                dioxide-rich solution to vacuum pressure conditions                and temperature conditions below 100 degrees C to                cause release of carbon dioxide from the carbon                dioxide-rich solution to form a regenerated                solution and an overhead gas stream comprising                carbon dioxide and steam, the step (c) as above per                se, and subjecting the overhead gas stream to heat                recovery comprising cooling the overhead gas stream                to produce a cooled overhead gas stream and a                condensate stream, recycling the condensate stream                back into the process, compressing the cooled                overhead gas stream to increase a temperature of it                and produce a compressed gas stream, transferring                heat from the compressed gas stream to a heat                transfer fluid to form a heated fluid and                transferring heat from the heated fluid to the                stripping stage; and
   (5) a carbon dioxide capture process (P4) for                capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas generated by                a host facility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P4) for                  capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas generated                  by a host facility, comprises the step (a) as                  above per se, in a stripping stage, subjecting                  the carbon dioxide-rich solution to vacuum                  pressure conditions and temperature conditions                  below 100 degrees C to cause release of carbon                  dioxide from the carbon dioxide-rich solution to                  form a regenerated solution and an overhead gas                  stream comprising carbon dioxide and steam, the                  step (c) as above per se, and subjecting the                  overhead gas stream to heat recovery comprising                  compressing the overhead gas stream directly                  after release from the stripping stage to                  increase a temperature of it and produce a                  compressed gas stream, transferring heat from the                  compressed gas stream to a heat transfer fluid                  and forming a cooled gas stream, compressing the                  cooled gas stream to increase a temperature of it                  and produce a further compressed gas stream,                  transferring heat from the further compressed gas                  stream to the heat transfer fluid, and                  transferring heat from the heated heat transfer                  fluid to the stripping stage. The process (P1)                  further comprises: subjecting at least a portion                  of the flue gas generated by the host facility to                  heat recovery and transferring the recovered heat                  to the stripping stage, where the portion of the                  flue gas is the flue gas stream supplied to the                  absorption stage; providing a first portion of                  the heat transfer fluid for heat recovery from                  the overhead gas stream, providing a second                  portion of the heat transfer fluid for heat                  recovery from the flue gas, combining the first                  and second portions of the heat transfer fluid                  after heating to form a combined heated fluid and                  supplying the combined heated fluid to the                  stripping stage for transferring heat to it;                  providing a third portion of the heat transfer                  fluid for heat recovery from a heat source                  provided by the host facility, combining the                  third portion of the heat transfer fluid with the                  first and second portions after heating to form                  the combined heated fluid and supplying the                  combined heated fluid to the stripping stage for                  transferring heat to it; controlling flow rates                  of the first, second and third portions to                  provide the combined heated fluid with thermal                  energy sufficient to meet thermal energy                  requirements of the stripping stage; removing the                  condensed water from the compressed gas stream                  and recycling the condensed water back into the                  process, optionally back into the regenerated                  solution; and after transferring heat from the                  compressed gas stream to the heat transfer fluid,                  subjecting the resulting gas stream to further                  cooling before subsequent compression and heat                  transfer. In the process (P1): subjecting the                  overhead gas stream to heat recovery comprises                  subjecting the overhead gas stream to multiple                  compression stages arranged in series to produce                  respective compressed gas streams, and for each                  of the compressed gas streams, indirectly                  transferring heat from the corresponding                  compressed gas stream to the heat transfer fluid                  to enable progressive heating of it to produce                  the heated fluid; transferring heat from the                  compressed gas stream to the heat transfer fluid                  comprises condensing water contained in the                  compressed gas stream; the absorption solution                  further comprises carbonic anhydrase for                  catalyzing hydration of carbon dioxide to form                  bicarbonate and hydrogen ions in the absorption                  stage, and optionally for catalyzing dehydration                  of the ions to form carbon dioxide in the                  stripping stage; transferring heat from the                  compressed gas stream to the heat transfer fluid                  is performed by indirect heat exchange;                  transferring heat from the heated fluid to the                  stripping stage is performed by indirect heat                  exchange; the indirect heat exchange is performed                  using at least one plate heat exchanger;                  transferring heat from the heated fluid to the                  stripping stage comprises transferring heat to a                  reboiler of the stripping stage; for a first                  compression stage, the overhead gas stream has an                  initial temperature of 55-75 degrees C, and the                  first compressed gas stream has a temperature of                  140-180 degrees C, and optionally, for subsequent                  compression stages, each cooled gas stream                  entering a corresponding compression stage has a                  temperature of 30-50 degrees C, or for a second                  compression stage, a cooled gas stream entering                  therein has a temperature of 70-90 degrees C, and                  then for subsequent compression stages each                  cooled gas stream entering a corresponding                  compression stage has a temperature of 30-50                  degrees C; the compressing is performed to                  provide a compression ratio of 2-4 or 2.5-3.5,                  and/or optionally where the overhead gas stream                  is compressed from an initial exit pressure of                  20-100 kPa to a final compressed carbon dioxide                  pressure of 10000-18000 kPa; the overhead gas                  stream is subjected to compression directly after                  being released from the stripping stage; and all                  of the overhead gas stream released from the                  stripping stage is subjected to the heat                  recovery. In the process (P4): the step of                  compressing the overhead gas stream directly                  after release from the stripping stage is                  performed by a vacuum fan; and the step of                  compressing the cooled gas stream and                  transferring heat from it is performed using a                  compression and heat recovery train that                  comprises multiple stages for progressively                  compressing and transferring heat to the heat                  transfer fluid. The process (P4) further                  comprises managing temperatures of the                  regenerated solution in the reboiler and of the                  heat transfer fluid used to transfer thermal                  energy to the regenerated solution to minimize                  denaturing of carbonic anhydrase circulating                  through the absorption and stripping stages,                  optionally where a maximum heat transfer fluid                  temperature providing the temperature to the                  reboiler is 85 degrees C. Preferred Components:                  In the process (P1): the stripping stage has                  thermal energy requirements and the heat                  transferred to it from the flue gas and the                  overhead gas stream meet at least 50%, 60%, 70%,                  80%, 90% 95% or 100% of the thermal energy                  requirements; the heat transfer fluid flows                  counter-currently with respect to the multiple                  compression stages and a flow of the compressed                  gas streams; the multiple compression stages                  comprises three to six stages or four to five                  stages, and optionally where a first compression                  stage is enabled by a vacuum fan and then                  compressors provide subsequent compression                  stages; the absorption compound comprises                  potassium carbonate (preferred),                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol,                  2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propenediol,                  N-methyldiethanolamine, dimethylmonoethanolamine,                  diethylmonoethanolamine, triisopropanolamine,                  triethanolamine, N-methyl-N-secondary butyl                  glycine, diethylglycine, dimethylglycine and/or                  sodium carbonate; the absorption solution further                  comprises at least one promoter, catalyst or                  biocatalyst; the promoter, catalyst or                  biocatalyst comprises piperazine, diethanolamine,                  diisopropanolamine, methylaminopropylamine,                  3-aminopropanol, 2,2-dimethyl-1,3-                  propanediamine, diglycolamine,                  2-amino-2-methylpropanol, 1-amino-2-propanol,                  2-methyl-methanolamine, piperidine, arsenite,                  hypochlorite, sulfite, carbonic anhydrase,                  glycine, sarcosine, alanine N-secondary butyl                  glycine and/or pipecolinic acid; the pressure                  conditions in the reboiler of the stripping stage                  are 0.1-0.9 (preferably 0.2-0.5) bara; and                  equipment used in the carbon dioxide capture                  process is sized, configured and operated based                  on a predetermined recoverable thermal energy                  from the flue gas and the overhead gas                  stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Circulation type carbon dioxide sensor for carbon dioxide underground storage facility, has fixing unit installed on outer wall of second container and coupled to one side of detection unit to fix position of detection unit.
AU CHUNG H
   KO K
   LEE J Y
AE UNIV KONKUK IND COOP CORP (UKUK-C)
GA 202093878M
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide sensor has a control unit (100) for calculating the concentration of carbon dioxide from the sensing data of the sensing unit. An LED plate (110) is connected to the control unit and provided with an LED light source to emit light. A first container (120) is coupled to the lower side of the LED plate and opens the lower side. A second container (180) is coupled to the outer side wall of the first container and has an upper side open. The indicator (130) changes color when a pH change occurs by the gas passing through the dialysis membrane (140). A detection unit transmits the sensing data to the control unit. A circulation unit (170) circulates the indicator from the first container to the second container or from the second container to the first container. A fixing unit is installed on an outer wall of the second container and coupled to one side of the detection unit to fix a position of the detection unit.
   USE - Circulation type carbon dioxide sensor for carbon dioxide underground storage facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide sensor can reduce the operating cost of the sensor by restoring the color-changed indicator to its original color and then reusing indicator. The control unit can accurately and quickly calculate the concentration of carbon dioxide leaking from the carbon dioxide underground storage area as the detection unit accurately and consistently detects the color change of the indicator using the LED light source. The carbon dioxide sensor can detect the concentration of carbon dioxide leaking from the carbon dioxide underground storage area of a large area. The carbon dioxide sensor can accurately and quickly detect the concentration of carbon dioxide leaking from industrial sites.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a circulation type carbon dioxide sensor for carbon dioxide underground storage facility.
   Control unit (100)
   LED plate (110)
   First container (120)
   Indicator (130)
   Dialysis membrane (140)
   Circulation unit (170)
   Second container (180)

TI Carbon dioxide emission change and emission            contribution assessment method, involves determining            carbon dioxide emission contribution degree            corresponding to social economic characteristic            attribute based on random forest model.
AU LIU M
   LIU F
   ZONG J
   LIAO T
   ZHOU L
   WANG Y
   WANG H
AE PIESAT INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (PIES-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1120T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S102) the                target data of the target area within a preset time                period. The target data includes remote sensing                image data, meteorological data and attribute data                of socio-economic attributes. The meteorological                data is normalized (S104) to obtain normalized                data, and using a multiple linear regression model                to process the remote sensing image data and the                normalized data to obtain carbon dioxide emission                change data. The annual carbon dioxide emission                factor change value of the target area is                calculated (S106) based on the target data and the                normalized data. A random forest model is                constructed (S108) based on the carbon dioxide                annual emission factor change value and the                attribute data. The carbon dioxide emission                contribution degree corresponding to the                socioeconomic characteristic attribute is                determined (S110) based on the random forest                model.
   USE - Carbon dioxide emission change and emission                contribution assessment method in electronic device                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining the carbon                dioxide emission contribution degree corresponding                to the social economic characteristic attribute                based on the random forest model, so as to realize                the technical effect of improving the accuracy of                the emission influence evaluation of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. a carbon dioxide emission change and                emission contribution assessment device; and
   2. a computer-readable storage medium storing                program for carbon dioxide emission change and                emission contribution assessment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the carbon dioxide emission change and emission                contribution assessment method.
   Step for obtaining target data of target area                within preset time period (S102)
   Step for normalizing meteorological data to                obtain normalized data, and using multiple linear                regression model to process remote sensing image                data and normalized data to obtain carbon dioxide                emission change data (S104)
   Step for calculating annual carbon dioxide                emission factor change value of target area                (S106)
   Step for constructing random forest model                based on carbon dioxide annual emission factor                change value and attribute data (S108)
   Step for determining carbon dioxide emission                contribution degree corresponding to socioeconomic                characteristic attribute (S110)

TI Desorbing carbon dioxide by alcohol amine pregnant            solution in development of industrial process and            maintenance of ecological environment of our country,            involves pumping alcohol amine rich in carbon dioxide            into ceramic membrane element.
AU FAN Y
   CHEN X
   QIU M
   GUO Y
   FU K
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2022104532
AB    NOVELTY - Desorbing carbon dioxide by alcohol amine                pregnant solution involves pumping the alcohol                amine rich in carbon dioxide into the ceramic                membrane element. The ceramic membrane element is                filled with catalyst for desorbing carbon dioxide                in the alcohol amine pregnant solution and alcohol                amine rich liquid under the action of the catalyst                carbon dioxide desorption, carbon dioxide after                desorption through the film layer of the ceramic                membrane element to obtain carbon dioxide and                discharged the desorbed alcohol amine barren                solution from the ceramic membrane element.
   USE - Method for desorbing carbon dioxide by alcohol                amine pregnant solution used in the development of                industrial process and maintenance of ecological                environment of our country.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the carbon dioxide absorbing                agent alcohol amine solution regeneration process                desorption temperature is high, energy consumption                is large, energy utilization rate is low and so on,                solving the severe system film material is not                resistant to high temperature. Using solid acid                type catalyst to reduce reaction activation energy,                using ceramic membrane pipe to provide filling                place of catalyst and providing large gas-liquid                mass transfer area, strengthening the coupling and                mass transfer of the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for desorbing carbon dioxide by alcohol amine                pregnant solution, comprises membrane contactor 4,                hydrophobic separation membrane, and the membrane                surface is provided with a catalyst for desorbing                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol amine                  compound used in the alcohol amine pregnant                  solution is selected from monoethanol amine, N,                  N-diethyl ethanolamine, methyl diethanol amine,                  1-dimethylamino-2-propanol,                  tetraethylenepentamine, triethylene tetramine,                  ethylenediamine, N-methyl diethanol amine,                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, piperazine,                  4-(diethylamino)-2-butanol, diethylenetriamine                  (DETA), diethylamino ethanol (DEAE). The                  concentration range of the alcohol amine compound                  is 2-6 mole/L. The catalyst is solid acid                  catalyst, the solid acid catalyst is molecular                  sieve catalyst or metal oxide catalyst, the                  molecular sieve catalyst is HZSM-5 and the metal                  oxide catalyst is selected from gamma aluminum                  oxide, titanium oxide or vanadium oxide. The                  ceramic film is tubular or multi-channel type,                  the diameter of the channel is 0.5-30 mm, the                  average aperture range of the ceramic film is                  2-500 nm. The film surface of the ceramic film                  has contact angle of greater than 130degrees                  Celsius to the water drop. Preferred Conditions:                  The temperature in the ceramic membrane tube is                  90-110degrees Celsius. The operation pressure                  range is 70-400 kiloPascal.

TI Carbon dioxide laser amplifier upper energy level testing device, has carbon dioxide laser amplifier started to excite multiple working particles to upwards level transition, where laser amplifier detects and analyzes transition light.
AU PAN Q
   GUO J
   LI D
   CHEN F
   XIE J
   YU D
   ZHANG K
   SUN J
   ZHANG L
AE CHANGCHUN INST OPTICS FINE MECHANICS & P (CAIO-C)
   CHANGCHUN INST OPTICS FINE MECHANICS & P (CAIO-C)
GA 202077089C
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide (CO2) laser amplifier receives a trigger signal. The carbon dioxide laser amplifier is started to excite multiple working particles to upwards level transition. The carbon dioxide laser amplifier detects and analyzes transition light that is generated by energy of the working particles from upper energy level transition to low energy level transition. A device main body determines upper energy level of the carbon dioxide laser amplifier according to energy and duration of the transition light. The device main body is formed with a resonant cavity. The device main body is provided with a laser guiding component and a laser detector. The resonant cavity generates laser by the laser detector by utilizing the transition light according to resonance of the resonant cavity.
   USE - Carbon dioxide laser amplifier upper energy level testing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is provided with the carbon dioxide laser amplifier to excite the working particles and detect the transition light to obtain the upper energy level by simulating actual operation of the carbon dioxide laser amplifier so as to increase upper energy level measuring accuracy and carbon dioxide laser amplifier data basis providing reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide laser amplifier upper energy level testing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon dioxide laser amplifier upper energy level testing device. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Carbon capture system for ultra-low concentration            carbon dioxide (CO2) flue gas, has booster pump fixed            between first-level absorption device and cooler, and            pregnant solution pump fixed on second pipeline, where            concentration of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide flue            gas is in specific range.
AU LIU G
   WANG L
   MU Z
   TONG D
AE CARBONLOCK HANGZHOU ENERGY ENVIRONMENTAL (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2022D4450F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a booster pump (4) fixed on a                first pipeline (6). A second pipeline (7) whose end                is connected with a bottom outlet of a secondary                absorption device (2). A cooler (3) is fixed on the                first pipeline for cooling a semi-rich liquid. The                booster pump is fixed between a first-level                absorption device and the cooler. A pregnant                solution pump (5) is fixed on the second pipeline.                The concentration of carbon dioxide in an ultra-low                concentration carbon dioxide flue gas is 1-10                mol.
   USE - Carbon capture system for ultra-low                concentration carbon dioxide (CO2) flue gas. Can                also be used for carbon dioxide tail gas.

   PARK H J
AE ADVANCED ENG INST (ADVG-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202041905W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has first electricity production unit (110) which produces electric energy by supplying a hydrocarbon-based fuel to a fuel cell (116). A second electricity production unit (120) produces electric energy using moisture separated from unreacted gas discharged from the anode of the fuel cell. A carbon dioxide capture unit (130) captures carbon dioxide from the unreacted gas from which moisture is removed in the second electricity production unit. After separating the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide capture portion, recirculation portion (140) burns off-gas discharged and supplied to the fuel cell.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture system using fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The irreversible loss of the cathode is minimized by utilizing unreacted gas discharged from the anode of the fuel cell. The electric energy is additionally recovered to improve overall system efficiency, by recovering the sensible heat of the gas discharged from the fuel cell anode as steam and supplies it to the steam turbine generator. The carbon dioxide is collected at a high concentration without an electrolyzer cell, thus the cost is reduced by reducing the facility investment cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide capturing method using fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of the carbon dioxide capture system using fuel cell.

PT P
PN CN113388241-A
TI Rubber-plastic blend composite of carbon dioxide-based plastic toughened by polyester elastomer comprises carbon dioxide-based plastic and polyester elastomer.
AU WANG C
   HAN Y
   ZHANG L
   WANG X
   ZHOU Q
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
   CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2021A86957
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber-plastic blend composite of carbon dioxide-based plastic toughened by polyester elastomer comprises 100 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based plastic, and 2-25 pts. wt., preferably 5-10 pts. wt. polyester elastomer.
   USE - Used as rubber-plastic blend composite of carbon dioxide-based plastic toughened by polyester elastomer.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite: ensure the good compatibility of raw materials; has good toughening effect on carbon dioxide-based plastics; is environmentally friendly degradable plastic; and has high value in terms of environmental protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the rubber-plastic blend composite of carbon dioxide-based plastic toughened by polyester elastomer, comprising blending the components including the polyester elastomer and the carbon dioxide-based plastic to obtain rubber-plastic blended composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-based plastic is carbon dioxide/propylene oxide copolymers, carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/ethylene oxide copolymer, carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/hexane oxide copolymer, preferably carbon dioxide/propylene oxide copolymers. The number average molecular weight of the polyester elastomer is 5000-80,000. The rubber-plastic blending composite material is further added with plasticizer, antioxidant, free radical inhibitor and/or stabilizer. The total weight of the carbon dioxide based plastic is 100 pts. wt.. The dosage of the polyester elastomer is 2-25 pts. wt., preferably 5-10 pts. wt.. The blending is further adding plasticizer, antioxidant, free radical inhibitor and/or stabilizer. The carbon dioxide-based plastic includes carbon dioxide/propylene oxide copolymers, carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/ethylene oxide copolymer and carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/hexane oxide copolymer, preferably carbon dioxide/propylene oxide copolymer. The number average molecular weight of the polyester elastomer is 5000-80,000. The blending is mechanical blending at 100-180 degrees C, preferably 120-150 degrees C. The polyester elastomer and carbon dioxide-based plastic are dried before being blended. The polyester elastomer is obtained by the reaction of a small molecule polyol and a small molecule polyacid. The small molecular polyol includes small molecular diols, small molecule triols, 2-16C saturated or unsaturated aliphatic diols or aliphatic triols, more preferably ethylene glycol, butylene glycol, propylene glycol, butene glycol, pentanediol, hexylene glycol , heptanediol, octanediol, nonanediol, decanediol, diethylene glycol, glycerol and/or triethylene glycol. The small molecule polybasic acid includes 4-16C saturated or unsaturated aliphatic dibasic acid or aromatic hydrocarbon dibasic acid, preferably succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, itaconic acid, fumaric acid, maleic acid, hydrogenated mucofuroic acid, terephthalic acid, and/or furandicarboxylic acid. The catalyst includes titanium-based metal catalysts, preferably titanate compounds, more preferably tetrabutyl titanate. Preferred Method: The polyester elastomer is prepared by first etherifying small molecular polyol and the small molecular polyacid, then polycondensing, and polymerizing to obtain polyester elastomer. The molar ratio of the amount of the small molecule polyol and the small molecule polyacid is 1:1-2:1, preferably 1.1:1-1.5:1. The esterification temperature is 160-200 degrees C, preferably 170-180 degrees C. The polycondensation temperature is 200-260 degrees C, preferably 200-220 degrees C. The preparation process of the polyester elastomer is carried out under inert gas conditions. The polycondensation reaction further adds catalyst. The amount of the catalyst is 0.1-0.2 wt.% of the total mass of the small molecule polyol and the small molecule polyacid.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A05-E01A2; A05-E06A; A07-A03A

PN CN114735701-A
TI Reinforcing geologic sealing by injecting            nano-particle stable carbon dioxide micro-bubble for            use in multi-phase flow technical field, involves            determining position of injection well, selecting            sealing position to install injection well, and            mounting monitoring device around injection well.
AU WANG D
   LIU Y
   YANG M
   WANG X
   WANG S
   JIANG L
   SONG Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2022969519
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforcing geologic sealing by injecting                nano-particle stable carbon dioxide micro-bubble                involves determining position of injection well;                selecting sealing position to install injection                well, and installing monitoring device (7) around                injection well; obtaining nanoparticle followed by                filling raw material collecting tank (1) with raw                materials for preparing nano particles enters nano                sand grinder (2) through pipeline (1a) for                grinding, and then introducing into nano particle                filter (4) through pipeline (2a) and valve (2b) to                filter out nano particles with nano particle size                of less than 500 nanometer (nm), where grinding                fineness of nano sand grinder (2) is &#8804;                200 nm, nanoparticle filter comprises nano filter                plate (5) with diameter less than 500 nm and                detection leakage sensor (3); opening of carbon                dioxide (CO2) valve for passing CO2, where                high-pressure pipeline continuously injects CO2into                mixer at constant flow rate.
   USE - Method for reinforcing geologic sealing by                injecting nano-particle stable carbon dioxide                micro-bubble for use in multi-phase flow technical                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps in improving CO2 sealing                amount and sealing safety; reduces reservoir gas                leakage risk; improves fluid property, dissolving                speed and efficiency of gas in salt water layer;                enhances mixing of fluid later; improves CO2                sealing stock and ensure sealing safety of salty                water layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reinforcing geologic sealing by injecting                nano-particle stable carbon dioxide micro-bubble                involves determining position of injection well;                selecting sealing position to install injection                well, and installing monitoring device (7) around                injection well; obtaining nanoparticle followed by                filling raw material collecting tank (1) with raw                materials for preparing nano particles enters nano                sand grinder (2) through pipeline (1a) for                grinding, and then introducing into nano particle                filter (4) through pipeline (2a) and valve (2b) to                filter out nano particles with nano particle size                of less than 500 nanometer (nm), where grinding                fineness of nano sand grinder (2) is &#8804;                200 nm, nanoparticle filter comprises nano filter                plate (5) with diameter less than 500 nm and                detection leakage sensor (3); opening of carbon                dioxide (CO2) valve for passing CO2, where                high-pressure pipeline continuously injects CO2into                mixer at constant flow rate, opening nanoparticle                valve (4a) to allow nanoparticles to flow through                nanoparticle pipeline (4b) uniformly and constantly                into mixing tank, obtaining mixed fluid after two                are mixed, where mixer comprises temperature and                pressure monitoring device (11) and high-pressure                magnetic stirrer (12); opening injection well                valve, injecting mixed fluid into fluid in                reservoir and maintaining reservoir pressure                stable, placing dense porous plate in injection                well cylinder (10), and passing fluid through dense                porous plate at bottom of well to produce                CO2containing nanoparticle bubbles, where compact                porous plate is made of corrosion-resistant                materials, and average pore diameter is 1 nm-1                &#181;m; arranging impurity filter plate in                front of dense porous plate in advance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of method for                reinforcing geologic sealing by injecting                nano-particle stable carbon dioxide                micro-bubble.
   1Raw material collecting tank
   1aPipeline
   2Nano-sand grinder
   2aPipeline
   2bValve
   3Detection leakage sensor
   4Nano-particle filter
   4aNano-particle valve
   4bNano-particle pipeline
   5Nano-filter plate
   7Monitoring device
   10Injection well cylinder
   11Temperature pressure monitoring                device
   12High pressure magnetic stirrer
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials for                  preparing nano particles are carbon nano                  particles, metal-based/metalloid nano particles                  with strong hydrophilicity or industrial waste                  residues with strong wettability. The strongly                  hydrophilic metal-based/metalloid nanoparticles                  are silica (SiO2) particles or ferric oxide                  (Fe2O3) granules. The strong wetting industrial                  waste residue is waste water sludge powder or                  blast furnace slag.

PT P
PN CN114613445-A
TI Prediction of copper alloy catalyst used for            carbon dioxide electroreduction, involves optimizing            structure of cell of different kinds of copper-metal            alloy, and constructing surface crystal face index of            structure using optimized cell.
AU MENG K
   FANG Z
   WANG H
   WANG Y
   XING M
   SUN S
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202282900P
AB    NOVELTY - Prediction of copper alloy catalyst, involves:                (1) optimizing the structure of the obtained unit                cells of different types of copper-metal alloys,                and constructing surface structures with different                crystal plane indices using optimized unit cells;                (2) taking carboxylic acid, carbon monoxide and                formate as key intermediates during reduction, and                considering that the hydrogen evolution reaction is                a competitive reaction of carbon dioxide reduction;                (3) taking the adsorption energy as target value,                and selecting 5 chemical and physical properties as                characteristics, evaluating the correlation of                characteristics, constructing carbon monoxide,                hydrogen, carboxylic acid and formate adsorption                energy data sets; (4) screening the sets; (5)                evaluating the prediction performance of different                models with evaluation indicators; (6) predicting                potential new materials; and (7) screening out                copper alloy catalyst for producing carbon monoxide                or formic acid.
   USE - Prediction of copper alloy catalyst used for                carbon dioxide electroreduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the problems of low                efficiency and poor selectivity, and reduces time                and cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Prediction of copper alloy catalyst, involves:                (1) optimizing the structure of the obtained unit                cells of different types of copper-metal alloys by                density functional theory method, and then                constructing surface structures with different                crystal plane indices using the optimized unit                cells; (2) taking carboxylic acid, carbon monoxide                and formate as key intermediates during                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide,                considering that the hydrogen evolution reaction is                a competitive reaction of the electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide, and hydrogen is the                key intermediate of hydrogen evolution reaction,                setting four adsorption intermediates and                calculating their adsorption energies at different                sites on different surfaces; (3) taking the                adsorption energy as the target value, and                selecting 5 chemical and physical properties as                characteristics, namely work function, atomic                number, interplanar spacing, electronegativity and                local electronegativity, evaluating the correlation                of characteristics using Pearson correlation                coefficient, constructing carbon monoxide                adsorption energy data set, hydrogen adsorption                energy data set, carboxylic acid adsorption energy                data set, and formate adsorption energy data set;                (4) screening the data set, and dividing the                training set and the test set; (5) applying the                machine learning model, training, and evaluating                the prediction performance of different models with                evaluation indicators; (6) selecting the model with                the best predictive performance to predict                potential new materials; and (7) screening out the                copper alloy catalyst for producing carbon monoxide                or formic acid according to the range of adsorption                energy of each intermediate. When the reduction                product of carbon dioxide is carbon monoxide, the                reaction intermediates involved are carboxylic acid                and carbon monoxide, When the reduction product is                formic acid, the reaction intermediate involved is                formate. The relevant intermediate of the hydrogen                evolution reaction is hydrogen. When the product is                carbon monoxide, carbon monoxide adsorption energy                is greater than 0.5 eV, and hydrogen adsorption                energy is 0.2&#177;0.1 eV. When carbon dioxide                reduction product is formic acid, formate                adsorption energy is 3.2&#177;0.1 eV and                hydrogen adsorption energy is 0.2&#177;0.1                eV.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The copper-metal alloy                  is copper-aluminum alloy, copper-zinc alloy,                  copper-tin alloy, copper-gallium alloy,                  copper-indium alloy, copper-gold alloy,                  copper-palladium alloy, copper-platinum alloy,                  copper-nickel alloy, copper-iron alloy,                  copper-iridium alloy or copper-rhodium                  alloy.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC E31-N05B1; E10-C04J1P; E11-N; J03-B; N07-G; T01-J13; T01-J04B2
IP C25B-001/23; C25B-011/089; C25B-003/07; C25B-003/26; G16C-020/10; G16C-020/70

AU TIAN D
   DU R
   WANG W
   WANG X
   ZUO C
   LI H
   DING C
AE SHANDONG DONGYUE CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022389047
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a raw material tank (12), a condensing separator (13), a middle tank (14), a carbon dioxide absorber (3), a fluorocarbon adsorber (5), a rectifying product absorber, a rectifying tower, a cooler, and a first finished product tank, second finished product tank, where the raw material tank is connected with the feeding port of the condensing separator, the discharging pipe of the condensing separator is divided into two paths one path is connected with the feeding port of the middle tank, the other path is connected with the feeding port of the carbon dioxide absorber, the discharging port of the carbon dioxide absorber is connected with the feeding port of the fluorine carbon compound absorber, the discharging pipe of the fluorine carbon compound absorber is divided into two paths, one path is connected with the first finished product tank.
   USE - Device for purifying electronic grade trifluoromethane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The purifying device and purifying technique of the effectively reduces the process flow, reduces the energy consumption the product trifluoromethane, purity reaches 99.999 %, which meets the use requirement of the semiconductor industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a process purification used by the purification device of electronic grade trifluoromethane which involves cooling and separating by firstly opening the vacuum pump to make the whole device in the vacuum state, then closing the vacuum pump the to- be-purified trifluoromethane into the condensing separator for cooling, after cooling, opening the vacuum pump to pump out the non-condensable gas, and then closing the vacuum pump, taking the condensed liquid analysis at the discharging port, if it is qualified introducing carbon dioxide absorber, if not qualified then discharging into the middle tank, re-introducing the condensing separator for cooling and separating until it is qualified, adsorption by cooling and separating the qualified condensed liquid into carbon dioxide absorber and fluorine carbon compound absorber to absorb, sampling and analyzing at the outlet of the fluorine carbon compound absorber, if the electronic grade product standard is introduced into the first finished product tank, if it does not reach the standard, introducing it into the rectifying tower, rectification by introducing the trifluoromethane material not reaching the electronic grade product standard into the rectifying tower for rectifying, sampling and analyzing at the tower top, discharging position, if it reaches the electronic grade product standard, introducing into the second finished product tank, if not reaching the standard, introducing into the rectifying product absorber, sampling and analyzing at the outlet of the rectifying product absorber, if the standard of the electronic grade product is reached, introducing into the secon finished product tank 16 if not discharging into the middle tank 14 returning to the condensing separator 13 to process again 4 filtering filtering the standard product in the 1 # finished product tank 15 and 2 # finished product tank 16 obtaining the purified electronic grade trifluoromethane
